════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #007: VISUAL REDESIGN
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 24
OVERLAPS WITH:   NONE (this IS the visual foundation)
BLOCKS:          Every other feature (all features render within this visual system)
BLOCKED BY:      NONE (this is the first thing built)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Visual Redesign transforms Athena's chat interface from a generic chatbot panel
into a full-screen, dot-matrix-aesthetic research companion that matches Arivu's
Nothing OS design language. The current chat panel is a narrow sidebar with basic
message bubbles. The redesigned Athena is a first-class interface with its own
visual identity: white background with subtle dot grid texture, monochrome
palette with gold accents, JetBrains Mono for data elements, Inter for prose,
and SVG dot matrix icons replacing all emoji.

This feature exists because visual coherence is not optional for a research tool
charging premium prices. When a user opens Athena, it must feel like an integral
part of Arivu, not a chatbot widget bolted onto the side. The dot matrix aesthetic
communicates precision and data-driven analysis. The monochrome palette keeps
focus on content rather than decorative chrome. Every pixel serves the researcher's
cognitive workflow.

The redesign implements a flexible layout system. Athena can operate in three
modes: Panel Mode (right sidebar, 380px wide, the default when the graph is the
primary focus), Split Mode (50/50 split with the graph, for extended conversations),
and Full Mode (Athena takes the full viewport, graph minimized to a thumbnail in
the corner). The user switches modes via a control in the Athena header bar. The
mode persists across page reloads via sessionStorage.

The message rendering system replaces basic text bubbles with structured message
containers. User messages appear in a minimal white card with subtle left border.
Assistant messages appear in a full-width container with no border (the content IS
the message, not a box around it). System messages use a thin horizontal bar with
icon and text. Every message container supports embedded blocks (Paper Cards, Stats,
Charts) as first-class citizens, not afterthoughts inserted into text.

The input area is redesigned from a single-line text field to a rich composition
area. It supports multi-line input (auto-expanding textarea), formatting shortcuts,
file attachment buttons, and a send button that transforms into a stop button during
streaming. The composition area has a subtle top border separator and sits fixed
at the bottom of the chat viewport.

Typography follows the Arivu design system: Inter 14px for prose, JetBrains Mono
12px for data values and paper IDs, Inter 600 weight for headings within messages.
Line height is 1.6 for readability of dense academic content. Paragraph spacing
is 12px between blocks within a single message.

The dot grid background texture is rendered via CSS repeating-radial-gradient:
1px circles at 20px intervals, color var(--text-muted) at 10% opacity. This is
subtle enough to read comfortably but establishes the Nothing OS aesthetic.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Automatic on first load. The visual redesign IS the Athena interface. There is
no "before" state to trigger from. The user opens Athena and sees the redesigned
interface immediately.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks the Athena icon in the tool page header (or it opens automatically
   on first graph load). The chat panel slides in from the right in Panel Mode
   (380px). The background shows a subtle dot grid texture. The header bar reads
   "ATHENA" in JetBrains Mono with a gold dot (●) icon.

2. A welcome message appears from Athena: a system-style thin bar that says
   "Ready to analyze [lineage name]. Ask me anything about the [N] papers in
   this ancestry." Below it, 3 template prompt pills (#123) offer starting points.

3. The user types in the rich input area. The textarea auto-expands as they type
   multi-line input. A character counter appears after 200 characters (subtle,
   right-aligned, monospace).

4. User sends a message. Their message appears as a white card with a subtle
   left border (var(--text-muted) at 20%). Athena's response streams below with
   no enclosing border, the content flows directly in the chat area. Structured
   blocks (Paper Card, Stats) render inline with full width.

5. User clicks the mode switcher (three dots icon in header). A dropdown offers
   Panel (current), Split, and Full. User selects Split. The layout animates:
   graph area shrinks to 50%, Athena panel expands to 50% over 300ms.

6. In Split Mode, Athena has more horizontal space. Stat Dashboard blocks render
   in a 2-column grid instead of single column. Paper Cards show more metadata.
   The rich input area is wider and more comfortable for long prompts.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The visual system is always active. Mode preference persists in sessionStorage.
All subsequent features render within this visual framework.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Complete stylesheet for the Athena interface.
  Layout modes, message containers, input area, header bar, scrollbar styling,
  dot grid background, responsive breakpoints.
- static/js/athena-layout.js: Layout mode manager. Panel/Split/Full mode
  switching. Resize handle for Split mode. SessionStorage persistence.
  CSS class toggling on parent container.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Updated to use new message container structure.
  Renders messages into the redesigned DOM layout.
- templates/tool.html: Updated grid template to support 3 Athena layout modes.
  Adds the Athena panel container with header, message area, and input area.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
The layout system uses CSS Grid with named areas:

  .tool-layout.athena-panel {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 380px;
  }
  .tool-layout.athena-split {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  }
  .tool-layout.athena-full {
    grid-template-columns: 0 1fr;
  }

Message containers use a flat list (no nested bubbles):

  <div class="athena-messages" role="log" aria-live="polite">
    <div class="msg msg-user">
      <div class="msg-content">User message text</div>
      <div class="msg-meta">10:30 AM</div>
    </div>
    <div class="msg msg-assistant">
      <div class="msg-content">
        <!-- Blocks render here as children -->
        <div class="block block-paper-card">...</div>
        <p>Prose text between blocks...</p>
        <div class="block block-stats">...</div>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="msg msg-system">
      <span class="msg-icon">●</span>
      <span class="msg-text">System notification</span>
    </div>
  </div>

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE: Visual Redesign is entirely frontend. No backend changes.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
// Layout mode manager
class AthenaLayout {
  constructor() {
    this.mode = sessionStorage.getItem('athena-mode') || 'panel';
    this.apply();
  }

  setMode(mode) {
    this.mode = mode;
    sessionStorage.setItem('athena-mode', mode);
    this.apply();
  }

  apply() {
    const layout = document.querySelector('.tool-layout');
    layout.classList.remove('athena-panel', 'athena-split', 'athena-full');
    layout.classList.add(`athena-${this.mode}`);

    // Notify graph to resize
    window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'));

    // Update mode indicator in header
    document.querySelectorAll('.mode-option').forEach(el => {
      el.classList.toggle('active', el.dataset.mode === this.mode);
    });
  }
}
```

```css
/* Dot grid background texture */
.athena-panel-container {
  background-color: #FFFFFF;
  background-image: radial-gradient(circle, var(--text-muted) 0.5px, transparent 0.5px);
  background-size: 20px 20px;
  background-position: 10px 10px;
  opacity: 1;
}
.athena-panel-container::before {
  /* Subtle fade overlay so dots don't overwhelm content */
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
  pointer-events: none;
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE: No API endpoints. Pure frontend feature.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Panel Mode (Default, 380px sidebar)

  ┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────┐
  │                              │ ● ATHENA    [≡]  │
  │     [Ancestry Graph]        │──────────────────│
  │                              │                  │
  │    ○───○───●───○             │ ○ Ready to       │
  │   /    \       \            │   analyze ResNet  │
  │  ○      ○       ○           │   lineage (601)   │

  │                              │ │ about key     │ │
  │                              │ │ papers        │ │
  │                              │ └──────────────┘ │
  │                              │                  │

  │                              │ in this lineage  │
  │                              │ include...       │
  │                              │                  │

  │                  │                              │
  │  ○──○──●──○      │ The key papers include:      │
  │  \     |  /     │                              │

  │                  │ │ He, Zhang, Ren, Sun 2016  │ │
  │                  │ │ Cited: 182,431 times      │ │
  │                  │ └──────────────────────────┘ │
  │                  │                              │

  │                  │ │ PageRank: #3 │ Impact 31%│ │
  │                  │ │ Cluster: DRL │ Desc: 23  │ │
  │                  │ └──────────────┴───────────┘ │

  └──────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Full Mode (graph minimized)

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ ● ATHENA                                     [≡]  │
  │────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
  │                                    ┌─────────┐    │
  │                                    │ [mini   │    │
  │  Full-width Athena content         │  graph] │    │
  │                                    └─────────┘    │
  │  ┌─ Paper Card ──────────────────────────────────┐│
  │  │ Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition  ││
  │  │ He, Zhang, Ren, Sun (2016) | CVPR             ││
  │  │ Citations: 182,431 | Fields: CS, CV           ││
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
  │                                                    │
  │  This paper represents a pivotal structural node   │
  │  in the computer vision lineage, ranking 3rd in    │
  │  PageRank importance out of 601 total papers...    │
  │                                                    │
  │────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                          [Send]│
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Panel → Split: Graph area shrinks, Athena expands. 300ms ease-out transition on
  grid-template-columns. Graph triggers resize event to refit nodes.
Split → Full: Graph shrinks to 120x80px thumbnail (absolute positioned, top-right
  corner with 8px border-radius). 300ms ease-out. Athena fills full width.
Full → Panel: Thumbnail expands back to full graph area. 300ms ease-out.
Mode button hover: Gold underline slides in from left (200ms).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks mode switcher
  → AthenaLayout.setMode(newMode)
  → CSS class updated on .tool-layout container
  → CSS Grid transition animates column widths
  → window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'))
  → Graph.js recalculates node positions for new viewport
  → sessionStorage stores mode preference

Page load:
  → AthenaLayout reads sessionStorage for saved mode
  → Applies mode class immediately (no animation on load)
  → Graph and Athena render in correct proportions

ERROR PATH:
  → sessionStorage unavailable → Default to 'panel' mode
  → Graph resize fails → Catch error, continue (Athena still works)

DATA STORED:
  → sessionStorage: 'athena-mode' = 'panel' | 'split' | 'full'

DATA CACHED:
  → Mode preference in sessionStorage only

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE: Visual Redesign is purely frontend. No LLM calls involved.
This feature provides the visual container within which LLM-generated content
is displayed by other features.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                   |
|----------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Mode switcher (≡)    | Click        | Dropdown with Panel/Split/Full options      |
| Mode option          | Click        | Transitions to selected layout mode         |
| Mode option          | Hover        | Gold underline slides in, cursor pointer    |
| Input textarea       | Type         | Auto-expands height up to 120px max         |
| Input textarea       | Enter        | Sends message (Shift+Enter for newline)     |
| Send button          | Click        | Sends message, transforms to stop button    |
| Message area         | Scroll       | Custom scrollbar (6px, rounded, muted)      |
| Resize handle (Split)| Drag         | Adjusts split ratio (min 30%, max 70%)      |
| Header area          | -            | Fixed at top, does not scroll               |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Layout mode transition: grid-template-columns 300ms ease-out
- Input textarea expand: height transition 100ms ease-out
- Send button → Stop: morph animation (circle maintains, icon rotates)
- Scrollbar: opacity 0 when idle, opacity 1 on hover/scroll, 300ms transition
- Mode dropdown: scaleY(0) to scaleY(1) from top, 150ms ease-out
- Message container entrance: see Feature #071 (Message Entrance Animation)

SOUND EFFECTS:
NOT APPLICABLE: Arivu does not use sound effects.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Athena panel has role="complementary" with aria-label="Athena Research Chat"
- Message area has role="log" and aria-live="polite"
- Mode switcher has aria-haspopup="true" and aria-expanded
- Input area has aria-label="Message Athena"
- All layout modes maintain minimum 320px width for Athena content
- Reduced motion preference disables layout transition animations

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Viewport width below 768px (mobile/tablet)
   Expected: Athena always renders in Full Mode. Mode switcher is hidden.
   Graph is accessible via a "Show Graph" button that switches to graph-only view.
   Implementation: CSS media query forces full mode below 768px.

2. Scenario: User switches from Full to Panel mode with a very long message
   Expected: Message content wraps correctly in narrower panel. No horizontal
   overflow. Tables and code blocks get horizontal scroll within their container.
   Implementation: All block containers have overflow-x: auto, max-width: 100%.

3. Scenario: Split mode resize handle dragged to extreme (95% Athena, 5% graph)
   Expected: Minimum graph width enforced at 30%. Handle snaps back to 30% if
   dragged further. Subtle resistance feedback at boundary.
   Implementation: CSS clamp() on grid-template-columns, min 30% for each side.

4. Scenario: User opens Athena while graph is still building (SSE in progress)
   Expected: Athena opens normally. Welcome message says "Graph is still building
   (47% complete). I can answer general questions, or wait for the build to finish
   for full analysis."
   Implementation: Check build job status, adjust welcome message.

5. Scenario: User has system dark mode preference
   Expected: Athena respects the light monochrome theme regardless of OS dark mode.
   The dot matrix aesthetic requires the light background. Dark mode is NOT supported
   for Athena (Arivu is light-themed by design).
   Implementation: No prefers-color-scheme media query. Fixed light theme.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON:
  NONE (this is the visual foundation)

ENABLES:
  Every other Athena feature (all render within this visual system)

ENHANCES:
  The existing tool page layout (templates/tool.html CSS grid)

CONFLICTS:
  NONE

EXTERNAL:
  Google Fonts: Inter and JetBrains Mono (already loaded by tool page).

PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The pessimistic agent MUST verify this feature by:

1. READ: athena-chat.css (all styles), athena-layout.js (mode manager),
   tool.html (grid template updates), athena-engine.js (message rendering)
2. COMPARE: Implementation against all Done Criteria checkboxes
3. TEST these specific scenarios:

   b. Switch to Split Mode, verify 50/50 split with animated transition
   c. Switch to Full Mode, verify mini graph thumbnail in corner

   f. Send a message with Enter, verify Shift+Enter creates newline
   g. Scroll through 50+ messages, verify 60fps smooth scrolling
   h. Resize viewport to 600px, verify forced Full Mode
4. CHECK for these failure modes:
   a. CSS Grid columns don't transition (jump instead of animate)
   b. Graph nodes overlap Athena panel in Panel Mode
   c. Custom scrollbar visible on mobile (should use native)
   d. Dot grid background too prominent (readability issue)
   e. JetBrains Mono not loaded (verify font fallback chain)
5. VERIFY no regression in: graph rendering, existing tool page layout
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN until proven otherwise
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #008: PATHFINDER INTEGRATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Graph Integration
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 18
OVERLAPS WITH:   #004 (Discuss Integration), #034 (Chat-Driven Graph Navigation)
BLOCKS:          NONE (Pathfinder integration is a terminal feature in its dependency chain)
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #005 (Graph-Aware Commands), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Pathfinder Integration bridges Arivu's existing Pathfinder system (the researcher
character with the 3-stage LLM pipeline) into Athena's chat interface. Currently,
Pathfinder operates in its own floating window with a separate input field and
response area. This feature makes Pathfinder's analytical capabilities accessible
through Athena's chat, so the user can ask Pathfinder-style questions ("position
my paper relative to this lineage," "compare these two approaches") directly in
the Athena chat without opening the Pathfinder window.

This feature exists because having two separate AI interfaces (Pathfinder window
AND Athena chat) creates cognitive fragmentation. The user should not have to
decide which AI to ask. Athena is the unified interface. When a user asks a
question that requires Pathfinder's deep analysis capabilities (3-stage pipeline,
structured blocks like stat grids, comparison cards, positioning analysis), Athena
routes the request to the Pathfinder pipeline transparently.

The integration works by detecting Pathfinder-class queries in Athena's input.
Questions about positioning ("where does my paper fit?"), deep comparison ("how
do these papers differ technically?"), and exploration ("give me a complete analysis
of this cluster") are classified as Pathfinder queries. The classification happens
at the same stage as graph command detection (Feature #005): before the regular
LLM pipeline, a fast classification determines if the query should go to the
Pathfinder 3-stage pipeline instead of the standard Athena response.

When a Pathfinder-class query is detected, Athena shows a subtle indicator:
"Activating deep analysis mode..." The researcher character (if visible in the
compact view) transitions to the "analyzing" pose. The response uses Pathfinder's
structured block format (stat grids, comparison cards, data-driven insights)
rather than Athena's simpler prose format. This gives the user the best of both
worlds: the convenience of a unified chat interface with the analytical depth
of Pathfinder's specialized pipeline.

The energy system from Pathfinder also integrates. Pathfinder-class queries in
Athena consume from the same energy pool (30 LLM calls per session). The energy
indicator appears briefly in Athena when a Pathfinder query is detected, showing
remaining energy. Regular Athena queries (simpler, single-stage LLM calls) do
NOT consume Pathfinder energy. This separation ensures users can have unlimited
casual conversations while rate-limiting expensive deep analysis queries.

The existing Pathfinder window remains available for users who prefer the dedicated
interface with the researcher character, chain physics, and separate workspace.
Athena integration is additive, not a replacement. Users who have used Pathfinder
before will find the same quality of analysis in Athena. Users who have never
opened Pathfinder benefit from its capabilities without needing to discover the
separate window.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User types a Pathfinder-class query in Athena's chat input. Examples: "position
my paper on transformers relative to this lineage," "give me a deep analysis of
the attention mechanism cluster," "compare ResNet and VGGNet's technical
contributions in detail." The detection is automatic; no prefix or mode switch
needed. Alternatively, user can type /pathfinder or /deep to force Pathfinder mode.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types "compare ResNet and VGGNet's technical approaches" in Athena chat
   and presses Enter. Message appears as a normal user bubble.

2. Within 500ms, Athena shows a system indicator: "Activating deep analysis..." with
   a gold border accent. If the researcher character is visible in the compact view
   on the right panel, it transitions to the "analyzing" pose (leaning forward with
   magnifying glass dot-matrix sprite).

3. An energy indicator briefly flashes: "Deep analysis (23/30 remaining)" then fades
   after 2 seconds. This alerts the user that this query uses the Pathfinder energy
   pool.

4. The Pathfinder 3-stage pipeline executes:
   - Stage 1 (0.5s): Groq fast classifies as "comparison" query
   - Stage 2 (1s): Groq fast selects blocks: stat_grid, comparison_card, prose
   - Stage 3 (3-5s): Groq 70b generates narratives for each block

5. The response streams into Athena's chat. First, a Comparison Card block renders
   showing ResNet vs VGGNet side-by-side with key metrics. Then a Stat Grid block
   shows the computed differences (citation count, PageRank, pruning impact, cluster
   overlap). Then prose analysis streams explaining the technical differences.

6. After the response completes, Pathfinder-specific quick actions appear:
   "Go deeper on ResNet," "Add AlexNet to comparison," "Show timeline of their
   influence." The researcher character returns to idle pose.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The conversation continues in Athena normally. The Pathfinder response is stored
in conversation memory like any other message. Future references to "the comparison"
or "the analysis" resolve to this Pathfinder response through memory.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Detection logic for Pathfinder-class queries.
  Routing to Pathfinder pipeline. Energy indicator display. Response rendering.
- static/js/pathfinder.js: Existing Pathfinder system. Exposes pipeline functions
  for Athena to call: classifyPrompt(), selectBlocks(), generateNarratives().
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Renders Pathfinder block types (stat_grid,
  comparison_card, positioning_card) within Athena's message containers.
- static/js/researcher-character.js: Compact view pose changes triggered by
  Athena Pathfinder queries (analyzing pose on query, idle on completion).
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Styles for Pathfinder blocks within Athena messages,
  energy indicator, deep analysis system message.
- app.py: /api/graph/<id>/pathfinder-prompt (existing endpoint, now also called
  from Athena context with source='athena' parameter).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
Pathfinder detection runs after graph command detection and before regular LLM:

  function isPathfinderQuery(message) {
    const patterns = [
      /\b(position|positioning|where.+fit|place.+in.+lineage)\b/i,
      /\b(deep analysis|thorough|comprehensive|detailed breakdown)\b/i,
      /\b(compare.+technical|versus.+approach|differ.+method)\b/i,
      /\b(cluster analysis|strand|dna|evolution of)\b/i,
    ];
    return patterns.some(p => p.test(message));
  }

When detected, the query routes to the existing Pathfinder pipeline:

  async function handlePathfinderInAthena(message, graphId) {
    showSystemMessage('Activating deep analysis...');
    showEnergyIndicator();

    // Use existing Pathfinder pipeline
    const response = await fetch(`/api/graph/${graphId}/pathfinder-prompt`, {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ prompt: message, source: 'athena' })
    });

    // Stream the response blocks into Athena chat
    const reader = response.body.getReader();
    await streamPathfinderBlocks(reader);
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
The existing /api/graph/<id>/pathfinder-prompt endpoint receives a source='athena'
parameter. This triggers the same 3-stage pipeline but returns the response in a
format compatible with Athena's streaming protocol (SSE events instead of JSON
blocks). The energy deduction is identical.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
// Integration point in athena-engine.js
async function processUserMessage(message) {
  // Priority 1: Graph commands (Feature #005)
  const command = recognizeCommand(message);
  if (command) return executeGraphCommand(command);

  // Priority 2: Pathfinder queries (this feature)
  if (isPathfinderQuery(message)) return handlePathfinderInAthena(message);

  // Priority 3: Regular Athena LLM response
  return sendToRegularPipeline(message);
}
```

API CONTRACTS:

POST /api/graph/<id>/pathfinder-prompt (existing, extended)
Request:
{
  "prompt": "compare ResNet and VGGNet's technical approaches",
  "source": "athena",  // NEW: indicates request from Athena chat
  "session_id": "..."
}

Response: SSE stream (same format as existing Pathfinder, but with source tag):
event: block
data: {"type": "comparison_card", "data": {...}}

event: prose
data: {"content": "The technical divergence between..."}

event: done
data: {"energy_remaining": 22}

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Pathfinder Query Detected

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ You: Compare ResNet and VGGNet            │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ SYSTEM (gold border) ──────────────┐  │
  │  │ ◐ Activating deep analysis...       │  │
  │  │   Energy: 23/30 remaining           │  │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Pathfinder Response Streaming in Athena

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ COMPARISON ────────────────────────┐  │
  │  │  ResNet (2016)   │  VGGNet (2014)   │  │
  │  │  PageRank: #3    │  PageRank: #5    │  │
  │  │  Impact: 31%     │  Impact: 18%     │  │
  │  │  Desc: 23        │  Desc: 15        │  │
  │  │  Cluster: DRL    │  Cluster: Deep   │  │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ STAT GRID ─────────────────────────┐  │
  │  │ Citation gap: 4.2x (ResNet leads)   │  │
  │  │ Structural: ResNet more critical    │  │
  │  │ Lineage depth: ResNet deeper (gen3) │  │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                           │
  │  The technical divergence between these   │
  │  two architectures represents a pivotal   │
  │  moment in deep learning history...█      │
  │                                           │
  │  ● Go deeper on ResNet                    │
  │  ● Add AlexNet to comparison              │
  │  ● Show timeline of influence             │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
User message → System indicator: 200ms fadeIn, gold left border
System indicator → Blocks: indicator stays visible, blocks render below
Blocks → Prose: blocks render instantly (not streamed), prose streams after
Energy indicator: fadeIn 200ms, holds for 2s, fadeOut 500ms
Researcher character: crossfade to "analyzing" pose over 300ms

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User sends message in Athena
  → athena-engine.js processUserMessage()
  → isPathfinderQuery() returns true
  → showSystemMessage('Activating deep analysis...')
  → showEnergyIndicator(remaining)
  → POST /api/graph/<id>/pathfinder-prompt { prompt, source: 'athena' }
  → Backend: 3-stage pipeline (classify → select blocks → generate)
  → SSE stream returns blocks + prose
  → athena-blocks.js renders comparison_card, stat_grid in Athena chat
  → Prose streams token-by-token below blocks
  → Energy counter decremented
  → Quick Action buttons appear
  → Response stored in conversation memory

ERROR PATH:
  → Energy depleted (0/30) → "Deep analysis limit reached for this session.
    You can still ask regular questions, or open a new session for more
    deep analyses." Regular Athena queries still work.
  → Pathfinder pipeline times out → "Deep analysis timed out. Let me try
    a simpler analysis..." Falls back to regular Athena response.
  → Graph not loaded → "Load a graph first to use deep analysis."

DATA STORED:
  → chat_history: Pathfinder response stored with metadata { source: 'pathfinder',
    block_types: ['comparison_card', 'stat_grid', 'prose'], energy_used: 1 }
  → pathfinder_prompts table: query stored (same as direct Pathfinder use)

DATA CACHED:
  → Pathfinder pipeline response cached per prompt+graph combination
  → Cache key: SHA256(prompt + graph_id), TTL: session duration

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: Uses existing Pathfinder 3-stage pipeline:
  Stage 1: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (classify query type)
  Stage 2: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (select response blocks)
  Stage 3: config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL (generate narratives)

STAGE: Pathfinder pipeline (3 stages), NOT the regular Athena pipeline.
The key difference: Pathfinder uses the 70b model for generation (richer output)
while regular Athena uses the 8b model (faster, simpler responses).

PROMPT: Uses existing Pathfinder prompts from pathfinder_pipeline.py. No new
prompts needed. The source='athena' parameter only affects response format
(SSE instead of JSON), not the prompt content.

OUTPUT FORMAT: SSE stream with typed events (block, prose, done). Each block
event contains a JSON payload that athena-blocks.js renders.

FALLBACK: If Pathfinder pipeline fails, falls back to regular Athena response
using the 8b model. Quality is lower but the user gets an answer.

TOKEN BUDGET:
- Stage 1 classification: ~100 tokens
- Stage 2 block selection: ~200 tokens
- Stage 3 narrative generation: ~1500-2000 tokens (70b model)
- Total per Pathfinder query: ~1800-2300 tokens
- Energy cost: 1 unit from the 30-unit session pool

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Chat input           | Send query  | Auto-detects Pathfinder-class, routes        |
| System indicator     | Visible     | Gold border, "deep analysis" label           |
| Energy indicator     | Auto        | Shows briefly (2s) then fades                |
| Comparison Card      | Hover cell  | Tooltip with full paper title                |
| Stat Grid cell       | Hover       | Explanation tooltip (what the metric means)  |
| Quick Action button  | Click       | Sends as new Pathfinder query                |
| /pathfinder prefix   | Type        | Forces Pathfinder mode regardless of content |
| /deep prefix         | Type        | Alias for /pathfinder                        |
| Researcher character | Auto pose   | analyzing → idle on completion               |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- System indicator: fadeIn 200ms, gold left border 3px solid var(--accent-gold)
- Energy counter: fadeIn 200ms, hold 2000ms, fadeOut 500ms
- Comparison Card: render instantly with opacity 0 → 1 (150ms)
- Stat Grid: render instantly with opacity 0 → 1 (150ms, 100ms delay after card)
- Prose: standard token streaming
- Researcher pose change: 300ms crossfade
- Quick Actions: stagger appear 100ms per button

SOUND EFFECTS:
NOT APPLICABLE: Arivu does not use sound effects.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- "Activating deep analysis" announced via aria-live
- Energy indicator: aria-label="23 of 30 deep analysis uses remaining"
- Comparison Card: table role with proper headers for screen readers
- Stat Grid: definition list semantics (term + definition pairs)
- /pathfinder command documented in slash command help (/help)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User types a Pathfinder-class query but energy is 0/30
   Expected: System message: "Deep analysis limit reached for this session.
   Let me answer with a simpler analysis instead." Falls back to regular
   Athena response using 8b model.
   Implementation: Check energy before pipeline dispatch. If 0, redirect.

2. Scenario: User types /pathfinder with a simple question ("what year was ResNet?")
   Expected: Even though the question is simple, /pathfinder forces the full
   3-stage pipeline. The response is richer than necessary but still correct.
   Energy is consumed. User chose this explicitly.
   Implementation: /pathfinder prefix bypasses detection, always uses pipeline.

3. Scenario: Pathfinder pipeline returns empty blocks (no comparison_card generated)
   Expected: Only prose renders. No empty card containers shown. Graceful
   degradation to prose-only response.
   Implementation: Check block array length before rendering each block type.

4. Scenario: User has Pathfinder window open AND uses Athena Pathfinder integration
   Expected: Both share the same energy pool. Energy indicator is consistent
   between both interfaces. Reducing energy in one updates the other.
   Implementation: Energy stored server-side in session, both UIs read from same.

5. Scenario: Regular Athena query misclassified as Pathfinder (false positive)
   Expected: User gets a richer-than-needed response (minor UX issue) and
   consumes 1 energy unit unnecessarily. Acceptable tradeoff for better coverage.
   Implementation: Pattern matching tuned for precision > recall to minimize
   false positives. /pathfinder available for forced mode.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON:
  #001 — Context-Aware Responses (graph context for Pathfinder queries)
  #002 — Rich Response Formatting (block rendering in Athena)
  #005 — Graph-Aware Commands (query classification priority chain)
  #037 — Streaming Text (streaming Pathfinder response in chat)

ENABLES:
  NONE (terminal feature in dependency chain)

ENHANCES:
  #003 — Quick Action Buttons (Pathfinder-specific quick actions)
  #004 — Discuss Integration (deep analysis follow-up to discussions)
  #016 — Paper Deep Dive (Pathfinder powers deep dives)

CONFLICTS:
  Potential UX conflict with Pathfinder window: user may be confused why
  "the same AI" exists in two places. Mitigated by shared energy pool and
  consistent response quality.

EXTERNAL:
  Depends on existing Pathfinder pipeline (pathfinder.js, pathfinder_pipeline.py).
  Energy system from existing EnergyManager class.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The pessimistic agent MUST verify this feature by:

1. READ: athena-engine.js (Pathfinder detection, routing), pathfinder.js
   (pipeline exposure for Athena), athena-blocks.js (block rendering),
   app.py (pathfinder-prompt endpoint with source='athena')
2. COMPARE: Implementation against all Done Criteria checkboxes
3. TEST these specific scenarios:
   a. Type "compare ResNet and VGGNet" — verify Pathfinder pipeline activates
   b. Type "what year was ResNet published?" — verify regular Athena responds
   c. Type /pathfinder what year — verify forced Pathfinder mode
   d. Use 30 Pathfinder queries, verify 31st falls back to regular Athena
   e. Open Pathfinder window, use 5 energy, then ask Pathfinder query in
      Athena. Verify energy shows 25/30 (shared pool).
   f. Verify researcher character pose changes during Athena Pathfinder query
4. CHECK for these failure modes:
   a. SSE stream format incompatible between Pathfinder window and Athena
   b. Energy race condition (two queries dispatched simultaneously)
   c. Block rendering breaks in narrow Panel Mode (380px)
   d. Classification false positive rate (test 10 regular questions)
5. VERIFY no regression in: regular Athena chat, Pathfinder window, energy system
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #009: PROACTIVE SUGGESTIONS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Proactive AI
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           F
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   #003 (Quick Action Buttons), #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups), #121 (Auto-Complete)
BLOCKS:          #032 (Paper Recommendation Engine)
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Proactive Suggestions transforms Athena from a reactive Q&A system into an
anticipatory research companion. Instead of waiting for the user to ask questions,
Athena observes the user's behavior in the graph (clicks, hovers, zoom patterns,
time spent on nodes, pruning actions) and proactively offers relevant insights
before the user even thinks to ask. When a user spends 10+ seconds hovering over
a cluster boundary, Athena gently suggests: "I notice you are exploring the
boundary between the attention mechanism and recurrent network clusters. Would
you like me to analyze the bridging papers that connect these two research
traditions?"

This feature exists because researchers often do not know the right question to
ask. A PhD student exploring a lineage for the first time may not realize that
a seemingly minor paper is actually a critical bottleneck until Athena points it
out. A senior researcher may overlook a contradiction that Athena detects from
the graph structure. Proactive suggestions are the difference between a tool
that answers questions and a tool that makes the researcher smarter.

The suggestion engine monitors six behavioral signals: (1) node click patterns
(which papers the user examines), (2) hover duration (what they linger on),
(3) zoom level changes (switching between overview and detail), (4) pruning
actions (what they are curious about removing), (5) time in session without
interaction (idle periods suggesting the user is thinking), and (6) conversation
topics already covered (to avoid repeating discussed insights).

Each behavioral signal maps to a suggestion type. Clicking three papers in the
same cluster triggers "I notice you are focused on the [cluster name] strand.
Want a deep dive?" Zooming out after detailed exploration triggers "You have
been looking at details. Here is a high-level summary of what you have explored
so far." An idle period of 30+ seconds after pruning triggers "Still thinking
about that pruning result? Here are the papers that would survive even if both
[X] and [Y] were removed."

Proactive suggestions appear as a subtle floating notification at the bottom of
the Athena chat panel, NOT as an intrusive popup. They use a muted gold accent
with a dismiss button (X). If the user ignores three consecutive suggestions,
the suggestion frequency decreases (adaptive throttling). If the user clicks
a suggestion, the system increases suggestion frequency for that signal type
(positive reinforcement). This behavioral tuning is stored per session.

The suggestion content is generated without an LLM call when possible. Most
suggestions use templated text with computed data injected: "Removing [paper]
collapses [N]% of the lineage" does not need an LLM. Only complex contextual
suggestions ("I notice a potential methodological gap between these clusters")
use a fast LLM call. This keeps the suggestion system lightweight and responsive.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Automatic based on user behavior. No explicit activation. The suggestion engine
runs continuously in the background, monitoring behavioral signals. Suggestions
appear when confidence exceeds a threshold (70%). The user can disable proactive
suggestions via a toggle in the Athena header settings.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has been clicking papers in the "attention mechanism" cluster for 45
   seconds (3 papers clicked, all in same cluster). The suggestion engine detects
   the cluster-focus pattern.

2. A subtle notification slides up from the bottom of the Athena panel:
   ┌ ● I notice you are exploring the Attention Mechanism cluster (12 papers).   X ┐
   │   Want a deep analysis of this research strand?                [Analyze]     │
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   The notification is a thin card with gold left border, muted background, and
   a dot matrix bullet. It slides up over 300ms with a subtle spring.

3. User clicks [Analyze]. The notification dismisses, and Athena automatically
   sends a message as if the user typed "Analyze the Attention Mechanism cluster."
   The response streams normally using the Pathfinder pipeline (#008) for depth.

4. Alternative: User clicks X to dismiss. The notification slides down and
   disappears. The suggestion engine notes the dismissal and reduces the
   frequency of cluster-focus suggestions (adaptive throttling).

5. Twenty seconds later, the user hovers over a paper for 8 seconds without
   clicking. A different suggestion appears: "Curious about [Paper Title]?
   It has the 5th highest structural impact in this lineage."

6. If the user ignores 3 consecutive suggestions (does not click or dismiss),
   the notification area goes quiet for 5 minutes. A subtle "Suggestions paused"
   note appears in the notification area, with a "Resume" link.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Suggestions continue throughout the session, adapting to user behavior. The
suggestion frequency, accepted/dismissed counts, and per-signal confidence
adjustments are stored in the session data.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-suggestions.js: Suggestion engine. Behavioral signal monitors.
  Template-based suggestion generator. Adaptive throttling. Notification UI.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Connects suggestion engine to chat (converts
  suggestion clicks into chat messages).
- static/js/graph.js: Exposes behavioral event emitters for node clicks, hovers,
  zoom changes, and pruning actions that the suggestion engine subscribes to.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Notification card styles, slide-up animation,
  dismiss button, gold accent.
- app.py: No new endpoints. Suggestions are computed client-side from cached
  graph data. Only complex suggestions trigger /api/athena/stream calls.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
The suggestion engine subscribes to graph events:

  class AthenaSuggestions {
    constructor(graphEvents, memory) {
      this.signals = {
        clusterFocus: new ClusterFocusMonitor(),
        hoverDuration: new HoverDurationMonitor(),
        zoomPattern: new ZoomPatternMonitor(),
        pruneAction: new PruneActionMonitor(),
        idlePeriod: new IdlePeriodMonitor(),
        topicCoverage: new TopicCoverageMonitor(memory),
      };
      this.throttle = { frequency: 30000, cooldown: 0, ignoreCount: 0 };

      graphEvents.on('nodeClick', (node) => this.signals.clusterFocus.observe(node));
      graphEvents.on('nodeHover', (node, duration) => this.signals.hoverDuration.observe(node, duration));
      graphEvents.on('zoom', (level) => this.signals.zoomPattern.observe(level));
      graphEvents.on('prune', (result) => this.signals.pruneAction.observe(result));
      setInterval(() => this.evaluate(), 5000); // Check every 5 seconds
    }

    evaluate() {
      if (this.throttle.cooldown > Date.now()) return;
      for (const [name, monitor] of Object.entries(this.signals)) {
        const suggestion = monitor.getSuggestion();
        if (suggestion && suggestion.confidence > 0.7) {
          this.show(suggestion);
          this.throttle.cooldown = Date.now() + this.throttle.frequency;
          return;
        }
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE for most suggestions (computed client-side from cached graph data).
Complex contextual suggestions that require LLM generate a prompt and send to
the existing /api/athena/stream endpoint.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
// Cluster focus detection
class ClusterFocusMonitor {
  constructor() { this.recentClicks = []; }

  observe(node) {
    this.recentClicks.push({ cluster: node.cluster, time: Date.now() });
    // Keep last 60 seconds of clicks
    const cutoff = Date.now() - 60000;
    this.recentClicks = this.recentClicks.filter(c => c.time > cutoff);
  }

  getSuggestion() {
    if (this.recentClicks.length < 3) return null;
    const clusterCounts = {};
    for (const c of this.recentClicks) {
      clusterCounts[c.cluster] = (clusterCounts[c.cluster] || 0) + 1;
    }
    const [topCluster, count] = Object.entries(clusterCounts)
      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0];
    if (count >= 3) {
      return {
        type: 'cluster_focus',
        confidence: Math.min(count / 5, 1.0),
        text: `I notice you are exploring the ${topCluster} cluster. Want a deep analysis?`,
        action: `Analyze the ${topCluster} cluster in detail`,
        cluster: topCluster
      };
    }
    return null;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
No new API endpoints. Suggestions are computed client-side. When a suggestion
is clicked, it converts to a regular chat message sent to existing endpoints.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Suggestion Notification Visible

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [Chat messages above...]                 │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ ● Suggestion ──────────────────── X ┐ │
  │  │ I notice you are exploring the       │ │
  │  │ Attention cluster (12 papers).       │ │
  │  │ Want a deep analysis?   [Analyze]    │ │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  │                                           │
  │ ─────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                [Send]│
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Suggestion Dismissed (Auto-throttle Active)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [Chat messages...]                       │
  │                                           │
  │  (no notification visible)                │
  │                                           │
  │ ─────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                [Send]│
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Suggestions Paused (After 3 ignores)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [Chat messages...]                       │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ ○ Suggestions paused ── [Resume] ──┐ │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  │                                           │
  │ ─────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                [Send]│
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Hidden → Visible: slideUp 300ms with ease-out-back spring from below input area
Visible → Dismissed: slideDown 200ms ease-in, opacity 0
After 3 ignores → Paused: "Suggestions paused" bar fades in 300ms
Paused → Resume: bar fades out, suggestion engine reactivates

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Graph interaction events (click, hover, zoom, prune)
  → Event emitters in graph.js
  → Suggestion engine monitors receive events
  → Each monitor accumulates behavioral signals
  → evaluate() runs every 5 seconds
  → Monitor with highest confidence > 0.7 wins
  → Suggestion notification rendered
  → User clicks action → suggestion text sent as chat message
  → User dismisses → throttle adjusted, counter incremented
  → 3 ignores → pause for 5 minutes

ERROR PATH:
  → Graph events not emitting (graph not loaded) → No suggestions shown
  → All monitors return null → No suggestion (normal state)
  → Suggestion action sends message but backend fails → Standard chat error

DATA STORED:
  → sessionStorage: suggestion_throttle (frequency, cooldown, ignoreCount)
  → sessionStorage: suggestion_history (last 20 suggestions shown + outcomes)
  → action_log: { action: 'suggestion_shown' | 'suggestion_clicked' | 'suggestion_dismissed' }

DATA CACHED:
  → Cluster membership data cached from graph JSON (no re-computation)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (only for complex contextual suggestions)

STAGE: Pre-generation (suggestions mostly use templates, not LLM)

PROMPT (for complex suggestions only):
```
Based on the user's behavior in this research lineage:
- They clicked these papers: {recent_clicks}
- They pruned: {recent_prunes}
- They have discussed: {topics_from_memory}
- Current graph has: {total_nodes} nodes, {cluster_count} clusters

Generate ONE proactive suggestion that would help this researcher.
The suggestion must reference specific data from the graph.
Maximum 2 sentences. No em dashes. No emojis.
```

OUTPUT FORMAT: Single suggestion text (1-2 sentences)

FALLBACK: If LLM unavailable, only template-based suggestions (no complex ones).
This is acceptable because most suggestions are template-based.

TOKEN BUDGET:
- Complex suggestion prompt: ~200 tokens
- Response: ~50 tokens
- Frequency: at most 1 complex suggestion per 5 minutes
- Total per hour: ~300 tokens for proactive suggestions

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Suggestion card      | Visible     | Slides up from bottom, gold left border     |
| [Action] button      | Click       | Sends suggestion text as chat message       |
| X dismiss button     | Click       | Card slides down, throttle adjusted         |
| Card itself          | Ignore      | Auto-dismisses after 15 seconds             |
| "Resume" link        | Click       | Reactivates suggestion engine               |
| Settings toggle      | Click       | Enables/disables proactive suggestions      |
| Suggestion card      | Hover       | Subtle shadow increase (elevation feedback) |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Slide up: translateY(100%) to translateY(0), 300ms ease-out-back
- Slide down: translateY(0) to translateY(100%), 200ms ease-in
- Auto-dismiss: opacity 1 to 0 over 500ms, then height collapse
- Resume link: text-decoration underline appears on hover
- Card hover: box-shadow 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1) → 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.15)

SOUND EFFECTS:
NOT APPLICABLE: Arivu does not use sound effects.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Suggestion card has role="alert" for screen reader announcement
- Dismiss button has aria-label="Dismiss suggestion"
- Action button has aria-label describing the suggestion
- Auto-dismiss respects prefers-reduced-motion (stays visible until dismissed)
- Keyboard: Tab to action button, then to dismiss. Escape dismisses.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User is typing in the input area when a suggestion appears
   Expected: Suggestion does NOT steal focus. Input cursor remains in textarea.
   Suggestion appears below the message area, above the input area.
   Implementation: tabIndex=-1 on suggestion card initially. Focus only on
   explicit Tab navigation.

2. Scenario: Suggestion appears during active streaming response
   Expected: Suggestion is queued until streaming completes. Showing a suggestion
   during streaming is distracting and competes for attention.
   Implementation: evaluate() checks isStreaming flag, defers if true.

3. Scenario: User has suggestions disabled in settings, re-enables mid-session
   Expected: Suggestion engine reactivates immediately. Previous behavioral
   signals are still stored (not cleared on disable).
   Implementation: disable flag prevents evaluate() from showing, does not
   clear monitor state.

4. Scenario: User rapidly interacts with many nodes (fast clicking through papers)
   Expected: Suggestion engine does not fire during rapid interaction. It waits
   for a 3-second pause after the last click before evaluating.
   Implementation: evaluate() debounced with 3-second idle requirement.

5. Scenario: Suggestion references a paper the user already discussed
   Expected: TopicCoverageMonitor filters out already-discussed topics.
   "You explored the Attention cluster" should not suggest if the user already
   asked about attention in the chat.
   Implementation: Monitor checks conversation memory for covered topics.

6. Scenario: Graph has only 5 nodes (very small lineage)
   Expected: Most behavioral signals do not trigger (not enough data for
   cluster focus, bottleneck, etc.). Only generic suggestions available.
   Implementation: Monitors have minimum thresholds (e.g., cluster_focus
   requires cluster with 5+ nodes).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON:
  #001 — Context-Aware Responses (graph data for suggestion templates)
  #006 — Conversation Memory (topic coverage tracking, avoid repeats)
  #037 — Streaming Text (queue suggestions during streaming)

ENABLES:
  #032 — Paper Recommendation Engine (proactive paper suggestions)

ENHANCES:
  #003 — Quick Action Buttons (suggestions are extended quick actions)
  #042 — Suggested Follow-Ups (proactive versions of follow-ups)
  #121 — Auto-Complete (proactive input suggestions)

CONFLICTS:
  NONE — proactive suggestions are additive.

EXTERNAL:
  Graph event system in graph.js (must emit behavioral events).
  SessionStorage for throttle persistence.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ Six behavioral monitors active (cluster, hover, zoom, prune, idle, coverage)
□ Suggestions appear when confidence exceeds 70%
□ Clicking suggestion action sends message to Athena chat
□ Dismiss button removes suggestion and adjusts throttle
□ Adaptive throttling: 3 ignores pauses for 5 minutes
□ "Suggestions paused" indicator with Resume link
□ Settings toggle to enable/disable suggestions
□ Suggestions reference specific graph data (not generic)

VISUAL:
□ Gold left border on suggestion card
□ Slide-up animation from below chat area
□ Dismiss X button visible with hover state
□ Muted background that does not overwhelm chat content
□ "Suggestions paused" bar is subtle and non-intrusive

PERFORMANCE:
□ evaluate() loop runs every 5 seconds without jank
□ Template suggestions render within 100ms (no LLM wait)
□ Complex LLM suggestions complete within 3 seconds
□ Behavioral monitors do not degrade graph interaction performance

ACCESSIBILITY:
□ role="alert" on suggestion card
□ Keyboard dismissible (Escape key)
□ Screen reader announces suggestion content
□ Auto-dismiss respects prefers-reduced-motion

EDGE CASES:
□ Suggestions do not steal focus from input area
□ Suggestions queued during streaming
□ Rapid interaction does not trigger false suggestions
□ Small graphs (< 10 nodes) do not flood with suggestions
□ Already-discussed topics filtered out

VERIFICATION:
□ PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 1 completed
□ PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 2 completed
□ PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Final round — ZERO issues
□ BROWSER TEST: Verified in Chrome
□ CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The pessimistic agent MUST verify this feature by:

1. READ: athena-suggestions.js (all monitors, evaluate loop, throttle logic),
   graph.js (event emitters), athena-chat.css (notification styles)
2. COMPARE: Implementation against all Done Criteria checkboxes
3. TEST these specific scenarios:
   a. Click 3 papers in same cluster, verify cluster-focus suggestion appears
   b. Hover on a paper for 10+ seconds, verify hover-duration suggestion appears
   c. Dismiss 3 suggestions, verify "Suggestions paused" appears
   d. Click Resume, verify suggestions reactivate
   e. Toggle suggestions off in settings, verify no suggestions appear
   f. Type in input area while suggestion appears, verify focus not stolen
4. CHECK for these failure modes:
   a. Suggestion engine fires during streaming (should be queued)
   b. evaluate() called too frequently (every 5s, not every frame)
   c. Throttle state lost on page navigation (sessionStorage check)
   d. Memory leak: behavioral data grows unbounded (verify pruning)
5. VERIFY no regression in: graph interaction performance, chat functionality
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
