════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #012: AI ANNOTATIONS ON GRAPH
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Graph Integration
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   #076 (Connection Animation), #105 (Click-Aware Chat)
BLOCKS:          #115 (Annotated Screenshots)
BLOCKED BY:      #004 (Discuss Integration), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

AI Annotations on Graph is the feature by which Athena places non-destructive visual
markers directly onto graph nodes and edges in response to its own analysis. When
Athena identifies a structural pattern in the lineage -- a bottleneck that collapses
38% of the graph if removed, a contradiction between two foundational papers, a bridge
paper that connects otherwise isolated clusters -- it does not merely describe the
finding in the chat panel. It marks the relevant node with a small SVG icon overlay
so the researcher can see it spatially within the citation graph itself.

This feature exists because the gap between textual analysis and visual graph is the
primary friction point in Arivu's current interface. A researcher reads Athena's
response: "VGGNet acts as a structural bottleneck connecting the detection cluster
to the object recognition cluster." This is valuable. But the researcher must then
manually scan the graph to find VGGNet, assess its position, and mentally verify the
claim. AI Annotations collapse that gap by marking VGGNet the moment Athena mentions
it. The annotation IS the spatial evidence for the textual claim.

The user experiences this as graph markup that appears in response to Athena's
analysis. Athena says "I have marked the three bottleneck nodes with warning triangles"
and three small triangle badges appear on the relevant graph nodes simultaneously.
The annotations are toggle-able: an "Annotations" toggle button in the graph controls
area shows/hides all Athena-generated markers without removing them. Individual
annotations can be dismissed by clicking the badge itself.

What makes this different from static graph styling: existing tools use fixed visual
encoding (node size for citation count, color for field). Athena annotations are
DYNAMIC and SEMANTIC. They reflect a specific analytical finding at a specific moment.
A warning triangle on a node does not mean "this node is old" -- it means "Athena
identified a contradiction between this paper and its cited ancestor in this session."
The annotation text (visible on hover) explains the specific finding that generated it.

The annotation system uses five icon types: warning triangle (contradiction detected),
star (bottleneck paper by pruning impact), bridge icon (paper connecting two clusters
with different field assignments), ghost icon (orphan idea -- paper with no methodological
descendants), and sparkle icon (high-potential research gap adjacent to this node as
identified by gap_finder.py). Each type has a distinct SVG shape, a distinct color
from the Arivu palette, and a distinct hover tooltip explaining what Athena found.

This feature connects directly to Feature #004 (Discuss Integration) as the input
mechanism: when the user selects a node and clicks "Discuss", Athena analyzes it and
may place annotations on it and its neighbors. It also connects to Feature #115
(Annotated Screenshots), which depends on this feature to provide the annotation
layer that gets baked into exported graph screenshots.

The implementation lives at the intersection of graph.js (the D3.js rendering layer)
and athena-annotations.js (the annotation lifecycle manager). The annotations layer
is an SVG group appended to the D3 container after the nodes group, so annotations
render above nodes but below tooltips. Each annotation is positioned relative to its
target node using the D3 force simulation's x/y coordinates, updated on every tick.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena produces an analysis response that identifies one or more specific structural
findings in the graph. The findings include paper IDs or edge pairs. The system
detects annotation-worthy content in the LLM response and dispatches annotation
events to the graph layer.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. You have the ResNet lineage open. You ask Athena: "Which papers are the most
   dangerous structural bottlenecks in this graph?" Athena begins streaming its
   analysis.

2. As Athena streams its response, the backend identifies the three bottleneck
   paper IDs from the pruning computation data. Before the prose response finishes,
   three star badges appear on the corresponding nodes in the graph: a filled star
   shape in --accent-gold color, 14x14 pixels, positioned at the top-right corner of
   the node circle. The badges fade in over 200ms. No animation jitter: they lock to
   the node and move with it as the force simulation settles.

3. The Athena response mentions "ResNet (2016), AlexNet (2012), and VGGNet (2014)
   are the three structural bottlenecks. I have marked them with stars in the graph."
   You see the marked nodes directly. You hover over the star badge on the ResNet
   node. A tooltip panel appears (not the standard node tooltip -- the annotation
   tooltip) showing: "BOTTLENECK -- Removing this paper collapses 38.2% of the
   lineage. Identified by Athena during bottleneck analysis, 14:23."

4. You ask "Are there any contradictions between these papers?" Athena identifies
   one contradiction edge between the AlexNet and VGGNet nodes. A warning triangle
   badge appears on both endpoints of that edge, and a red highlight overlays the
   edge line itself (the edge color changes from the default mutation-type color to
   --danger red during the annotation).

5. You decide you want to focus on the graph without annotation markers. You click
   the "Annotations" toggle in the graph control strip. All annotation badges
   immediately fade out (opacity 0, 200ms transition). The toggle shows "OFF" state.
   The underlying graph is unchanged: node sizes, colors, edge widths remain exactly
   as before. Annotations are truly non-destructive overlays.

6. You toggle annotations back ON. All badges reappear (opacity 1, 200ms). You click
   the star badge on AlexNet directly. A dismiss button appears on the badge: a small
   "x" inside the badge area. You click the x. That specific annotation is removed
   and does not reappear. The other annotations remain.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Annotations persist for the session duration unless explicitly dismissed. They
accumulate across multiple Athena interactions: by the end of a research session,
the graph may have 8-12 annotations marking the key findings from the conversation.
The full set is visible when the Annotations toggle is ON and cleared when the user
starts a new conversation or refreshes the page.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena/athena-annotations.js: AnnotationManager class (tracks all
  active annotations, CRUD operations), AnnotationBadge class (individual marker
  lifecycle: add, position, animate, dismiss), annotation tooltip rendering.
- static/js/graph.js: Modified to accept annotation layer. After nodes group
  creation, append an SVG group #annotation-layer. Expose updateAnnotationPositions()
  called on each simulation tick to keep badges locked to their nodes.
- static/js/athena/athena-engine.js: Modified to parse "annotation_event" SSE events
  from the backend and dispatch them to AnnotationManager.
- static/css/athena/athena-chat.css: .annotation-badge, .annotation-tooltip, and
  the five icon type styles (.ann-bottleneck, .ann-contradiction, .ann-bridge,
  .ann-orphan, .ann-gap). Toggle button styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
AnnotationManager: Module-level singleton. Maintains a Map of annotation_id to
AnnotationBadge. Listens for 'athena:annotation' custom events. On event, calls
add(annotation) which instantiates a badge, positions it, and appends to the
#annotation-layer SVG group. Provides toggle(visible) to show/hide all badges.
Provides dismiss(annotation_id) to remove a specific badge.

AnnotationBadge: Each badge is a D3 selection wrapping a <g> element containing
the icon SVG path and an invisible hover target <circle>. The badge's (x, y)
position is computed as: node.x + BADGE_OFFSET_X, node.y + BADGE_OFFSET_Y where
offsets position the badge at the node's top-right. graph.js calls
AnnotationManager.updatePositions(nodePositions) on each simulation tick, providing
current x/y for each paper_id. Badges translate to updated positions smoothly.

graph.js modification: The annotations layer is appended as the last child of the
main SVG <g> container, so it renders above all nodes and edges. The existing tick
function is extended to call AnnotationManager.updatePositions() after updating
node and link positions.

Annotation event protocol: The backend detects annotation-worthy content in LLM
responses by checking for paper_ids mentioned alongside bottleneck/contradiction/
bridge/orphan/gap keywords. It emits SSE events of type "annotation_event" alongside
the token stream. The frontend handles these without interrupting text streaming.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
class AnnotationManager {
    constructor() {
        this.annotations = new Map();
        this.visible = true;
        this.layer = null;
    }

    init(svgContainer) {
        this.layer = svgContainer.append('g').attr('id', 'annotation-layer');
        document.addEventListener('athena:annotation', e => this.add(e.detail));
    }

    add({ annotation_id, paper_id, annotation_type, tooltip_text }) {
        if (this.annotations.has(annotation_id)) return;
        const badge = new AnnotationBadge({
            annotation_id, paper_id, annotation_type, tooltip_text,
            layer: this.layer,
            onDismiss: id => this.dismiss(id)
        });
        badge.render();
        badge.setVisible(this.visible);
        this.annotations.set(annotation_id, badge);
    }

    updatePositions(nodePositions) {
        this.annotations.forEach(badge => {
            const pos = nodePositions.get(badge.paperId);
            if (pos) badge.moveTo(pos.x + 10, pos.y - 10);
        });
    }

    toggle(visible) {
        this.visible = visible;
        this.annotations.forEach(b => b.setVisible(visible));
    }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
```
SSE event type: "annotation_event"
Payload (JSON):
{
    "annotation_id": "ann_bottleneck_2c03df8b",
    "paper_id": "2c03df8b48bf3fa39054345bafabfeff15bfd11d",
    "annotation_type": "bottleneck" | "contradiction" | "bridge" | "orphan" | "gap",
    "tooltip_text": "BOTTLENECK -- Removing this paper collapses 38.2% of the lineage.",
    "edge_pair": null
}
For edge annotations (contradiction):
{
    "annotation_id": "ann_contradiction_abc_def",
    "paper_id": null,
    "annotation_type": "contradiction",
    "tooltip_text": "CONTRADICTION -- Methodology in AlexNet (2012) is directly contradicted by VGGNet (2014).",
    "edge_pair": ["abd1c342495432171beb7ca8fd9551ef13cbd0ff", "54e325aee6b2d476bbbb88615ac15e251c6e8214"]
}
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: No Annotations (initial state before any Athena analysis)
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [graph area -- standard Arivu visualization]    |
|                                                  |
|   o -- ResNet ---- o -- VGGNet ---- o AlexNet    |
|   |                                              |
|   o -- BatchNorm -- o -- Dropout                 |
|                                                  |
|  [Annotations: OFF]  (toggle, greyed out)        |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

STATE: Annotations Active (bottleneck stars placed on 3 nodes)
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [graph area]                                    |
|                                                  |
|   o -- ResNet[*] -- o -- VGGNet[*] -- o AlexNet[*]|
|   |                                              |
|   o -- BatchNorm -- o -- Dropout                 |
|                                                  |
|  [Annotations: ON]  (toggle, active)             |
+--------------------------------------------------+
[*] = 14x14 star badge in --accent-gold, top-right of node circle
Nodes otherwise unchanged (size, color, edge widths identical)
```

STATE: Annotation Tooltip Hover
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|   o -- ResNet[*]                                 |
|              |                                   |
|   +-- BOTTLENECK --------------------------+     |
|   | Removing this paper collapses 38.2%    |     |
|   | of the lineage (189 papers affected).  |     |
|   | Identified by Athena -- 14:23          |     |
|   | [Dismiss this annotation]              |     |
|   +----------------------------------------+     |
+--------------------------------------------------+
Tooltip appears above the badge, 8px gap.
Border: 1px solid --accent-gold. Background: --bg-elevated.
Font: JetBrains Mono, 12px. No emojis. All text, dot matrix icons.
```

STATE: Contradiction Annotation (edge highlighted + node badges)
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                  |
|   o AlexNet[!] ===========================       |
|                [CONTRADICTION EDGE -- red]       |
|                ==========================        |
|                             o VGGNet[!]          |
|                                                  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
[!] = warning triangle badge in --danger red
=== = edge rendered in --danger red (overrides mutation_type color)
Standard edges remain their normal colors. Only annotated edges change.
```

STATE: Annotations Hidden (toggle OFF)
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [graph area -- identical to No Annotations]     |
|                                                  |
|   o -- ResNet ----- o -- VGGNet ----- o AlexNet  |
|   |                                              |
|   o -- BatchNorm -- o -- Dropout                 |
|                                                  |
|  [Annotations: OFF]  (toggle, inactive)          |
+--------------------------------------------------+
All badges have opacity: 0. Edge annotation colors revert. Toggle shows "OFF".
```

TRANSITIONS:
Badge creation: opacity 0 + scale(0.5) to opacity 1 + scale(1.0), 200ms, ease-out.
Toggle OFF: all badges opacity 1 to 0, 200ms, ease-out. Edge colors revert simultaneously.
Toggle ON: all badges opacity 0 to 1, 200ms, ease-in.
Dismiss: scale(1.0) to scale(0) + opacity 0, 150ms, ease-in. Badge removed from DOM after.
Tooltip: opacity 0 + translateY(4px) to opacity 1 + translateY(0), 120ms ease-out.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User asks Athena about structural properties of the graph
    |
POST /api/athena/message [app.py]
    |--- LLM identifies bottleneck/contradiction/bridge/orphan/gap findings
    |--- Backend detects paper_ids in LLM response matching known structural data
    |--- For each annotatable finding, emits "annotation_event" before token stream ends
    |
SSE stream received by athena-engine.js
    |--- "token" events -> stream into message bubble (parallel to annotation events)
    |--- "annotation_event" event -> dispatch 'athena:annotation' custom event
    |
AnnotationManager receives 'athena:annotation' [athena-annotations.js]
    |--- Instantiate AnnotationBadge for each annotation_event payload
    |--- Append badge <g> elements to #annotation-layer SVG group
    |--- Badges initially positioned at paper node's current x/y + offset
    |
D3 simulation tick [graph.js]
    |--- AnnotationManager.updatePositions(nodePositions) called each tick
    |--- Badges translate to follow their target nodes
    |
User hovers badge
    |--- AnnotationBadge renders tooltip DOM element with tooltip_text
    |--- Tooltip positioned above badge
    |
User clicks toggle button
    |--- AnnotationManager.toggle(false/true) called
    |--- All badge opacities updated
    |--- Edge annotation colors reverted/applied via graph.js edge color update
    |
User clicks dismiss on badge
    |--- AnnotationBadge.dismiss() called
    |--- Badge animates out (scale 0, opacity 0), removed from DOM after 150ms
    |--- AnnotationManager.annotations.delete(annotation_id)

ERROR PATH:
Backend emits annotation_event with paper_id not found in current graph nodes
    |
AnnotationBadge.render() called with unknown paper_id
    |--- nodePositions.get(paper_id) returns undefined
    |--- Badge not appended (silent skip)
    |--- Console warning: "Annotation target paper_id not found in graph: [id]"

DATA STORED:
Annotations: In-memory only (AnnotationManager.annotations Map). Not persisted.
Cleared on page refresh or new graph load.

DATA CACHED:
Annotation positions computed from D3 simulation state, no separate cache.
Structural finding data (impact percentages) read from graphData already in memory.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Yes

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile
STAGE: Generation (annotation signals emitted during generation, not post-processing)

PROMPT SECTION: "ANNOTATION SIGNAL RULES" added to system prompt for analysis queries.

ADDITIONAL PROMPT (literal text):
```
ANNOTATION SIGNAL RULES:
When your analysis identifies specific papers with structural significance, you must
include annotation signals in your response so the graph can be marked visually.
For each annotatable finding, output a line in this format after your analysis paragraph:

<ANNOTATE paper_id="[40-char S2 corpus ID]" type="[bottleneck|contradiction|bridge|orphan|gap]" text="[tooltip text, max 120 chars]" />

For contradiction annotations involving an edge between two papers:
<ANNOTATE paper_id="[citing_paper_id]" type="contradiction" text="[tooltip]" edge_target="[cited_paper_id]" />

ANNOTATION TYPE RULES:
- bottleneck: Use only for papers whose computed pruning_impact exceeds 20%.
  The tooltip text MUST include the exact impact percentage from the lineage context.
- contradiction: Use only for edges where mutation_type = "contradiction" in the
  edge_analysis data from the lineage context.
- bridge: Use for papers whose fields_of_study spans two or more distinct clusters.
- orphan: Use for papers that appear in the orphan_detector results in context.
- gap: Use for papers adjacent to a research gap identified by gap_finder.

CRITICAL: Only annotate papers that exist in the LINEAGE CONTEXT by their S2 paper_id.
Do not annotate papers not present in the current graph. Annotation lines are parsed
by the backend and must conform exactly to the XML-style format above.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
The LLM response streams normally. Annotation lines (<ANNOTATE .../>) are stripped
from the visible text stream by the SSE emitter and converted to "annotation_event"
SSE events. The user sees clean prose; the system receives annotation data.

FALLBACK IF LLM FAILS:
If the LLM does not include any ANNOTATE lines (model ignores instructions):
no annotations are placed. The response is still useful as text. No error shown.
If the LLM includes ANNOTATE lines with malformed attributes: the backend regex
parser catches the error, logs a warning, and skips that annotation. Partial
annotations (those that parsed correctly) still appear.

TOKEN BUDGET:
Input: annotation rules add ~150 tokens to the system prompt.
Output: each ANNOTATE line is ~80 tokens. 5 annotations = ~400 extra output tokens.
Total overhead: well within Groq 70b's 32,768 token context window.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Trigger                      | Element                   | Action                                              |
|------------------------------|---------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| Athena emits annotation_event| AnnotationManager         | Instantiate and render badge on target node         |
| Hover over annotation badge  | AnnotationBadge hover area| Show annotation tooltip with finding text           |
| Move mouse off badge         | AnnotationBadge hover area| Hide tooltip, 200ms fade-out                        |
| Click dismiss in tooltip     | .annotation-dismiss btn   | Animate badge out, remove from DOM and Map          |
| Click Annotations toggle ON  | #annotations-toggle       | Show all badges, reapply edge colors, 200ms fade-in |
| Click Annotations toggle OFF | #annotations-toggle       | Hide all badges, revert edge colors, 200ms fade-out |
| Graph simulation tick        | D3 tick function          | updatePositions() locks badges to their nodes       |
| New graph loaded             | arivu:graph-loaded event  | AnnotationManager.clear() removes all annotations  |
| New conversation started     | athena:conversation-reset | AnnotationManager.clear() removes all annotations  |

KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS:
- Tab navigation reaches annotation badges. Enter/Space opens tooltip.
- Within tooltip, Tab reaches dismiss button. Enter/Space dismisses.
- Escape closes tooltip without dismissing.

SOUND EFFECTS:
NOT APPLICABLE (Athena uses no audio)

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Each annotation badge: role="img", aria-label="[annotation_type] annotation on [paper title]"
- Tooltip: role="tooltip", aria-live="polite" for screen reader announcement on hover
- Dismiss button inside tooltip: aria-label="Dismiss this annotation"
- Annotations toggle: role="switch", aria-checked="true|false"
- Hidden annotations: aria-hidden="true" on badge elements when toggle is OFF

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

EDGE CASE 1: Target node not visible in current viewport
Scenario: Athena annotates a paper that is currently off-screen due to graph pan.
Expected behavior: The badge is placed at the correct D3 coordinates and will be
visible when the user pans to that area. A small "Jump to annotation" notification
appears in the bottom-left of the graph area, listing the annotated paper names.
Clicking "Jump to" calls graph.js panToNode(paper_id).
Implementation: After annotation placement, check if node position is within
current SVG viewport bounds. If not, render jump notification.

EDGE CASE 2: Duplicate annotation on same node from different Athena messages
Scenario: User asks about bottlenecks twice. ResNet gets a star annotation on both
responses.
Expected behavior: The second annotation replaces the first (same paper_id, same
annotation_type results in an update, not a duplicate). Tooltip text updates to the
most recent finding. The badge does not double-render.
Implementation: AnnotationManager checks for existing annotation with matching
paper_id AND annotation_type before adding. If found, calls existing badge's
updateTooltip(new_text) rather than creating a new badge.

EDGE CASE 3: Graph force simulation still running when annotation placed
Scenario: The graph is still settling (nodes moving) when an annotation arrives.
Expected behavior: Badge is placed and moves with the node during simulation.
No jitter or lag. Badge appears immediately at current position, then follows.
Implementation: updatePositions() runs on every tick regardless of simulation
alpha. The badge always reflects current node position. No waiting for simulation
to settle.

EDGE CASE 4: More than 15 annotations accumulated in one session
Scenario: User has an extended conversation. 20+ annotations accumulate.
Expected behavior: After 15 annotations, a counter "15+ annotations" appears on
the toggle button. An "Annotations" management panel is offered: a small popover
listing all annotations with dismiss buttons. Visual clutter is managed: badges
scale to 10px (from 14px) when count exceeds 15 to reduce overlap.
Implementation: AnnotationManager tracks count. At count > 15, call
this.enableCompactMode() which applies .annotation-compact class reducing badge size.

EDGE CASE 5: Annotation placed on a node during pruning simulation
Scenario: User starts a pruning animation. Some nodes are collapsing. Athena
simultaneously places an annotation on a collapsing node.
Expected behavior: The annotation badge follows the node as it collapses and fades.
When the node reaches opacity 0 (collapsed), the badge also reaches opacity 0.
When the node is removed from the DOM (pruning complete), the badge is also removed.
Implementation: The pruning collapse animation (in pruning.js) dispatches
'arivu:node-collapsing' events for each affected node. AnnotationManager listens
and marks those badges for co-animation, binding their opacity to the node's opacity.

EDGE CASE 6: No paper_id in annotation event (malformed backend response)
Scenario: Backend emits annotation_event with null paper_id.
Expected behavior: No badge rendered. Console warning logged. The prose response
still displays normally. No visible error to user.
Implementation: AnnotationManager.add() validates annotation_id and paper_id before
proceeding. Missing required fields result in a console.warn and early return.

EDGE CASE 7: User has accessibility "reduce motion" preference enabled
Scenario: OS/browser has prefers-reduced-motion: reduce set.
Expected behavior: All badge animations are disabled. Badges appear instantly (no
translateY or scale animation). Toggle shows/hides instantly. The visual states
are identical; only the transitions are removed.
Implementation: athena-chat.css already has the global @media (prefers-reduced-motion)
rule from the design system. Annotation badge transitions are declared in that
block with duration 0.01ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON (must be built first):
- Feature #004 (Discuss Integration): The primary trigger for annotations is an
  Athena analysis response. Feature #004 establishes the "discuss a node" workflow
  that produces structural analysis responses. Without #004, there is no reliable
  trigger for annotation generation (user could ask in free text, but the structured
  annotation signal pipeline requires the context established by #004).
- Feature #001 (Context-Aware Responses): The LLM must know the paper IDs and their
  structural properties to produce correct ANNOTATE signals. Context-aware responses
  inject the required lineage data (paper IDs, pruning impacts, mutation types) into
  the prompt. Without context, the LLM cannot produce accurate annotations.

ENABLES (can be built after this):
- Feature #115 (Annotated Screenshots): Depends on the annotation layer existing in
  the graph SVG. Screenshot capture includes the #annotation-layer group, producing
  screenshots with Athena's analysis markings baked in.

ENHANCES (improves but does not require):
- Feature #016 (Paper Deep Dive): Deep dive responses can place annotations on the
  analyzed paper and its immediate neighbors.
- Feature #108 (Prune-Aware Chat): After a pruning simulation, Athena can annotate
  surviving nodes as "survival path" and collapsed nodes as "dependency casualties."
- Feature #076 (Connection Animation): Connection animations showing lineage paths
  complement annotation badges on the same nodes. Both are non-destructive overlays.

CONFLICTS WITH:
- Feature #005 (Graph-Aware Commands): Both features modify the visual state of the
  graph in response to chat commands. Resolution: commands from #005 operate on
  graph DATA (filters, layouts); annotations from this feature operate on a separate
  OVERLAY layer. No conflict in data or rendering paths.

EXTERNAL DEPENDENCIES:
- D3.js (v7, already loaded by graph.js): Annotation badges use D3 selections for
  SVG manipulation and position binding. No additional library needed.
- graph.js simulation tick: The annotation position update hook requires access to
  the D3 force simulation tick event. This is available in the existing graph.js.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Bottleneck annotations (star badge) appear on correct nodes after analysis
[] Contradiction annotations (triangle badge) appear on both edge endpoints
[] Bridge annotations (bridge icon) appear on cluster-spanning papers
[] Orphan annotations (ghost icon) appear on papers from orphan_detector data
[] Gap annotations (sparkle icon) appear on papers adjacent to gap_finder gaps
[] All badges lock to their target nodes and move with force simulation
[] Annotations toggle button hides/shows all badges simultaneously
[] Individual badge dismiss removes that badge permanently for the session
[] AnnotationManager.clear() fires on arivu:graph-loaded event
[] Duplicate annotation (same paper_id + type) updates tooltip instead of double-rendering

VISUAL:
[] Star badge: 14x14px, --accent-gold, top-right corner of node
[] Triangle badge: 14x14px, --danger red, top-right corner of node
[] Edge annotation: target edge renders in --danger red while annotation active
[] Tooltip: JetBrains Mono 12px, --bg-elevated background, --accent-gold border
[] Badge entrance: scale(0.5)+opacity(0) to scale(1)+opacity(1), 200ms ease-out
[] Toggle OFF: all badges fade out 200ms simultaneously

QUALITY:
[] No console errors during badge creation, position update, or dismissal
[] Works correctly when graph has 0, 1, and 600+ nodes
[] Graceful handling of annotation_event with unknown paper_id (warning, no crash)
[] No performance degradation during force simulation with 15 active badges

ACCESSIBILITY:
[] Each badge has role="img" with descriptive aria-label
[] Tooltip has role="tooltip" with aria-live="polite"
[] Dismiss button keyboard accessible (Enter/Space)
[] Annotations toggle has role="switch" with aria-checked

INTEGRATION:
[] Does not modify node color, size, or edge width (non-destructive overlay)
[] Badges correctly follow nodes during pruning animation opacity transitions
[] No conflict with standard node hover tooltip (#001 tooltip system)
[] graph.js updateAnnotationPositions() called without breaking existing tick logic

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 1 completed -- all findings addressed
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 2 completed -- all findings addressed
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Final round -- agent confirms ZERO remaining issues
[] BROWSER TEST: Feature verified in Chrome via browsermcp
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Implementation matches this spec document exactly

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The pessimistic agent MUST verify this feature by:

1. READ: athena-annotations.js (AnnotationManager, AnnotationBadge), graph.js
   (annotation layer initialization, tick function modification),
   athena-engine.js (annotation_event handling), athena-chat.css (badge styles),
   app.py (ANNOTATE signal parsing logic)
2. COMPARE: Implementation against all Done Criteria checkboxes above
3. TEST these specific scenarios:
   a. Ask "what are the bottlenecks?" -- verify star badges appear on correct nodes
      with impact percentages matching graph data (not LLM estimates)
   b. Ask about contradictions -- verify triangle badges appear on edge endpoints
      and edge line changes color to --danger red
   c. Toggle annotations OFF -- verify all badges hidden, edge colors reverted
   d. Toggle annotations ON -- verify all badges reappear
   e. Ask about bottlenecks twice (same graph) -- verify ResNet node has ONE badge,
      not two stacked
   f. Load a new paper lineage (navigate away) -- verify all badges cleared
   g. Ask about a paper not in the graph -- verify no badge appears, no crash
4. CHECK for these failure modes:
   a. Badge positions drift from node positions during force simulation (position
      update not called on tick, or wrong offset calculation)
   b. Edge annotation color persists after the annotation is dismissed (edge color
      not reverted on dismiss)
   c. LLM produces ANNOTATE lines with fabricated paper_ids not in the graph
      (hallucinated IDs should be silently skipped)
   d. AnnotationManager event listeners accumulate on re-initialization (memory leak)
   e. Tooltip renders outside SVG viewport bounds on edge nodes
5. VERIFY no regression in: #004 (Discuss Integration), graph.js node interaction
6. The agent assumes the implementation is BROKEN until proven otherwise
7. The agent must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round or state with evidence why none
8. The agent records ALL findings in the Pessimistic Agent Log section below

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24

                  ensure annotation layer is appended after nodes group and tick

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #013: RESEARCH QUESTION GENERATOR
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Proactive AI
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           F
ESTIMATED HOURS: 20
OVERLAPS WITH:   #009 (Proactive Suggestions), #019 (Hypothesis Tester)
BLOCKS:          #019 (Hypothesis Tester)
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Research Question Generator is an active intelligence feature that analyzes the
complete structure of a citation lineage to produce novel, well-grounded research
questions. It does not generate generic research questions about a topic. It generates
questions that are structurally derived from the specific gaps, orphan ideas, and
blind spots identified by Arivu's backend intelligence modules -- gap_finder.py and
orphan_detector.py -- and then reasons about those findings using the LLM to produce
questions that a researcher could realistically pursue. Every question generated is
traceable to specific graph evidence.

This feature exists because identifying gaps and orphan nodes in a citation graph is
only the first half of the intellectual task. The second half is formulating those
structural observations into actionable research questions. A researcher looking at a
graph can see that Paper X has no descendants in the methods cluster, but they may
not immediately understand what research question that absence implies. The generator
bridges the gap between structural observation and research framing. It translates
"there is a gap adjacent to node 2c03df8b in the embeddings cluster" into "Why has no
subsequent work applied deep residual architectures to few-shot learning benchmarks
despite the architectural compatibility demonstrated in He et al. (2016)?"

The user experiences this feature through either a proactive trigger (Athena surfaces
the generator after detecting significant gap_finder output in the current lineage)
or an explicit request ("Generate research questions for this lineage"). In both cases,
the output is a structured list of questions rendered as a specialized response block.
Each question appears as its own card within the response, showing the question text,
a confidence score, a justification paragraph, and a list of linked evidence papers
from the lineage. The questions are ranked by a combination of structural novelty and
estimated tractability.

What makes these questions different from questions a researcher could generate
by reading the literature: they are grounded in STRUCTURAL properties of the citation
network that are invisible to a reader of individual papers. A question about a gap
at the intersection of two clusters emerges from the observation that papers from
those clusters never cite each other, despite semantic similarity computed by the
NLP worker's embedding space. A question about an orphan idea emerges from the
computed trajectory of a paper that produced no methodological descendants. These
are insights that require computational analysis of the full graph; the LLM alone
cannot produce them.

The confidence score for each question is computed from three signals: gap_finder.py
proximity score (how semantically distinct is the gap from the nearest cluster
centroid), the orphan_detector.py trajectory confidence (how definitively is the
paper's orphan status established), and the LLM's self-assessed tractability score
(how feasible does the question appear given current methodological state of the
field). The final score is a weighted average: 40% structural signal + 60% LLM
tractability assessment.

The justification paragraph for each question explicitly names the specific papers
and structural observations that motivated the question. It does not use vague
academic language. It says "This question arises because the gap_finder identified
a 0.73 semantic distance between the detection cluster (42 papers) and the
generative modeling cluster (38 papers) with no bridging papers published after 2018."
This specificity is what distinguishes Athena-generated questions from generic
literature review boilerplate.

The connection to Feature #019 (Hypothesis Tester) is foundational: the Hypothesis
Tester takes a research question as input and evaluates it against the lineage
structure. Research questions generated by this feature are designed to be directly
testable by the Hypothesis Tester, creating a closed loop of question generation,
evaluation, and refinement. The output format of the generator (question text +
evidence papers + confidence score) matches the expected input format of the tester.

The backend endpoint /api/athena/research-questions computes the raw structural data
from gap_finder.py results stored in the graph cache (the graphs table already stores
gap data via the graph build pipeline) and orphan_detector.py output (stored in the
leaderboard_json column of the graphs table from Phase 3). This data is passed to the
LLM for question formulation. No new database queries are needed beyond fetching the
existing cached graph analysis data.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Two paths: (1) Proactive -- Athena detects that gap_finder produced 3 or more gaps
with proximity score above 0.65, and surfaces a suggestion card: "I have identified
structural gaps in this lineage that suggest research opportunities. Want me to
generate specific research questions?" User clicks "Yes, generate questions."
(2) Explicit -- User types a command: "Generate research questions for this lineage"
or uses the slash command /research-questions.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. You click "Yes, generate questions" in the Athena proactive suggestion. Athena
   responds with "Analyzing lineage structure..." and a dot matrix progress indicator
   showing three phases: "Reading gap analysis", "Evaluating orphan trajectories",
   "Formulating questions with evidence."

2. The backend POST /api/athena/research-questions fetches the graph's gap_finder
   results and orphan_detector data from the graphs table. This takes approximately
   2-4 seconds for a 600-paper lineage. Progress events stream back via SSE.

3. After the computation phase, the LLM begins generating the questions. The response
   streams in as a specially formatted block. The first question card materializes:
   a bordered container with the question text in large type, a confidence meter bar
   below it, then the justification paragraph, then a "Evidence Papers" section
   listing the specific papers from the lineage that ground this question.

4. Four more question cards appear in sequence, each streaming in as the LLM
   produces them. Questions are labeled Q1 through Q5. Each confidence score is
   rendered as a horizontal bar using the dot matrix fill style: 0 to 10 filled
   circles representing 0% to 100% confidence. The confidence score is numerical
   text next to the bar: "Confidence: 74%".

5. After all five questions are rendered, an action strip appears below the list:
   [Refine Questions] [Save as Artifact] [Test Q1 in Hypothesis Tester] [Export].
   You hover over Q2 -- "Why has no work applied transformer attention mechanisms
   to citation graph structure despite the architectural parallel?" -- and see its
   evidence panel expanding: it lists 4 papers from the lineage with their relevance
   to this question, and cites the specific gap_finder cluster pair (attention
   cluster vs. graph neural network cluster, semantic distance 0.81, zero bridging
   papers post-2020).

6. You click "Test Q1 in Hypothesis Tester." Feature #019 activates with Q1's text
   and evidence papers pre-loaded as the hypothesis to evaluate.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The five question cards remain in the chat as a response block. You can scroll past
them and return. The "Save as Artifact" button creates an Artifact Card (Feature #011)
containing all five questions in markdown format with their justifications. Questions
can be individually dismissed from the list if not relevant to your research direction.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena/athena-questions.js: ResearchQuestionBlock class (renders the
  five-question response block with confidence bars, justification panels, evidence
  linking), QuestionCard class (individual question card with expand/collapse),
  QuestionActionStrip (Refine, Save, Test, Export buttons below the list).
- static/js/athena/athena-engine.js: Modified to detect "research_questions_block"
  SSE event type and instantiate ResearchQuestionBlock instead of standard markdown.
- static/js/athena/athena-blocks.js: The ResearchQuestionBlock is a response block
  type registered in the block registry alongside stat cards, paper cards, etc.
- app.py: POST /api/athena/research-questions endpoint. Fetches gap_finder and
  orphan_detector data from graphs table cache. Builds structured context for LLM.
  Streams back research_questions_block SSE events plus token events for any prose.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
ResearchQuestionBlock: Rendered from a JSON payload containing an array of up to 7
question objects. Each question object: {question_id, text, confidence, justification,
evidence_papers, gap_metadata}. The block renders a header "RESEARCH QUESTIONS -- [N]
Generated from Lineage Structure" followed by N QuestionCard elements.

QuestionCard: A bordered container. Header row: question number label + confidence bar.
Body: question text in 16px Inter semibold. Collapsed state shows only the first
sentence of justification. Expanded state shows full justification + evidence paper list.
Evidence papers are clickable links that dispatch arivu:focus-paper to graph.js.

Confidence bar: Rendered as a row of 10 dot matrix circles. Number of filled circles =
Math.round(confidence * 10). Filled circle: SVG circle with fill: --accent-gold.
Empty circle: SVG circle with fill: none, stroke: --text-muted.

athena-blocks.js registration:
```javascript
BlockRegistry.register('research_questions_block', (payload, container) => {
    const block = new ResearchQuestionBlock(payload);
    container.appendChild(block.render());
});
```

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
POST /api/athena/research-questions:
1. Validate request: {graph_id, session_id, max_questions (optional, default 5)}.
2. Fetch graph record from graphs table: SELECT leaderboard_json, dna_json, diversity_json
   WHERE graph_id = ? (leaderboard_json contains orphan data from orphan_detector.py,
   dna_json contains cluster data used by gap_finder.py context).
3. Fetch gap_finder results: gap_finder.py was run during graph build. Results are
   embedded in the graph JSON stored in R2 under graphs/{graph_id}.json. Fetch and
   parse the relevant section.
4. Build structured question_context dict: {gaps: [...], orphans: [...], clusters: [...],
   seed_paper: {...}, total_papers: N}.
5. Call Groq 70b with question_context + prompt. Stream response back as SSE.
6. Parse research question blocks from LLM response. Emit research_questions_block
   SSE event with parsed JSON payload.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```python
@app.route("/api/athena/research-questions", methods=["POST"])
def api_athena_research_questions():
    from backend.config import config
    from backend.db import fetchone
    from backend.r2_client import R2Client
    import json

    data = request.get_json()
    graph_id = data.get("graph_id")
    session_id = data.get("session_id")
    max_questions = min(int(data.get("max_questions", 5)), 7)

    graph_row = fetchone(
        "SELECT leaderboard_json, dna_json, graph_id FROM graphs WHERE graph_id = %s",
        (graph_id,)
    )
    if not graph_row:
        return jsonify({"error": "Graph not found"}), 404

    r2 = R2Client()
    graph_json = r2.get_json(f"graphs/{graph_id}.json") or {}
    gaps = graph_json.get("gap_analysis", {}).get("gaps", [])
    orphans = (graph_row.get("leaderboard_json") or {}).get("orphans", [])

    def generate():
        yield from _stream_research_questions(gaps, orphans, graph_row, max_questions)

    return Response(stream_with_context(generate()), mimetype="text/event-stream")
```

API CONTRACTS:
```
POST /api/athena/research-questions
Request: {
    "graph_id": "a1b2c3d4e5f6...",
    "session_id": "sess_xyz",
    "max_questions": 5
}
Response: SSE stream
  event: progress
  data: {"stage": "reading_gaps", "message": "Reading gap analysis..."}

  event: progress
  data: {"stage": "evaluating_orphans", "message": "Evaluating orphan trajectories..."}

  event: research_questions_block
  data: {
      "questions": [
          {
              "question_id": "q_001",
              "text": "Why has no subsequent work applied deep residual architectures...",
              "confidence": 0.74,
              "justification": "This question arises because gap_finder identified...",
              "evidence_papers": [
                  {"paper_id": "2c03df8b...", "title": "Deep Residual Learning...", "relevance": "primary gap anchor"}
              ],
              "gap_metadata": {"cluster_pair": ["detection", "embeddings"], "semantic_distance": 0.73}
          }
      ],
      "generation_metadata": {
          "gaps_analyzed": 6,
          "orphans_analyzed": 4,
          "lineage_size": 601
      }
  }
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Generating (progress indicators streaming)
```
+--------------------------------------------------------+
|  ATHENA                                                |
|                                                        |
|  Analyzing your lineage for research opportunities...  |
|                                                        |
|  +-- GENERATING RESEARCH QUESTIONS ------------------+ |
|  |  [o] Reading gap analysis (6 gaps found)          | |
|  |  [o] Evaluating orphan trajectories (4 orphans)   | |
|  |  [ ] Formulating questions with evidence...       | |
|  +---------------------------------------------------+ |
+--------------------------------------------------------+
[o] = filled dot (complete), [ ] = empty dot (in progress)
```

STATE: Questions Rendered (all 5 questions visible)
```
+--------------------------------------------------------+
|  RESEARCH QUESTIONS -- 5 Generated from Lineage        |
|  601 papers analyzed | 6 gaps | 4 orphans              |
|                                                        |
|  +-- Q1  Confidence: oooooooo.. 74% -----------------+ |
|  |  Why has no subsequent work applied deep residual  | |
|  |  architectures to few-shot learning benchmarks     | |
|  |  despite the architectural compatibility in        | |
|  |  He et al. (2016)?                                 | |
|  |  [Expand justification v]                         | |
|  +---------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                        |
|  +-- Q2  Confidence: ooooooooo. 88% -----------------+ |
|  |  Why has transformer attention not been applied to | |
|  |  citation graph structure despite the parallel     | |
|  |  with graph attention networks?                    | |
|  |  [Expand justification v]                         | |
|  +---------------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                        |
|  ... (Q3, Q4, Q5 below)                               |
|                                                        |
|  [Refine Questions] [Save as Artifact] [Test Q1] [Export]|
+--------------------------------------------------------+
"o" in confidence bar = filled dot matrix circle (--accent-gold)
"." in confidence bar = empty dot matrix circle (--text-muted stroke)
```

STATE: Question Card Expanded (justification and evidence visible)
```
+--------------------------------------------------------+
|  +-- Q1  Confidence: oooooooo.. 74% -----------------+ |
|  |  Why has no subsequent work applied deep residual  | |
|  |  architectures to few-shot learning benchmarks...  | |
|  |                                                    | |
|  |  JUSTIFICATION:                                    | |
|  |  Gap finder identified semantic distance 0.73      | |
|  |  between the residual learning cluster (84 papers) | |
|  |  and the few-shot learning cluster (29 papers).    | |
|  |  No bridging papers exist post-2018. Orphan        | |
|  |  detector confirms He et al. (2016) has zero       | |
|  |  descendants in the meta-learning sub-cluster.     | |
|  |                                                    | |
|  |  EVIDENCE PAPERS:                                  | |
|  |  > Deep Residual Learning (He, 2016) [primary]     | |
|  |  > MAML (Finn, 2017) [gap anchor]                  | |
|  |  > Prototypical Networks (Snell, 2017) [gap anchor]| |
|  |                                                    | |
|  |  [Test this question]  [Dismiss]  [^ Collapse]    | |
|  +---------------------------------------------------+ |
+--------------------------------------------------------+
Evidence paper names are underlined clickable links to graph nodes.
">" prefix is plain ASCII, not bullet emojis.
```

STATE: Empty (generator runs but no significant gaps found)
```
+--------------------------------------------------------+
|  ATHENA                                                |
|                                                        |
|  I analyzed this lineage for structural gaps and       |
|  orphan ideas. The lineage appears well-connected      |
|  with no significant unexplored gaps above the         |
|  detection threshold (proximity score < 0.50).         |
|                                                        |
|  This suggests the field covered by this lineage       |
|  is relatively mature. You may want to explore the     |
|  edges of the graph -- papers with low citation        |
|  counts and recent publication dates -- for emerging   |
|  research directions.                                  |
+--------------------------------------------------------+
```

TRANSITIONS:
Progress indicator: dots fill left to right as stages complete, 200ms per dot.
Question card entrance: each card translates in from below (translateY(16px) to 0),
staggered by 100ms per card. 300ms ease-out per card.
Expand/collapse: max-height transition, 250ms ease-in-out.
Action strip entrance: fades in after last card renders, 400ms delay + 200ms fade.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User triggers generator (proactive button or explicit request)
    |
POST /api/athena/research-questions [app.py]
    |--- Validate session and graph_id from request body
    |--- fetchone(graphs table) to get leaderboard_json, dna_json
    |--- R2Client.get_json("graphs/{graph_id}.json") to get gap_analysis section
    |--- Extract gaps list (max 10) and orphans list (max 10)
    |
SSE stream starts
    |--- emit "progress" event: stage="reading_gaps"
    |--- emit "progress" event: stage="evaluating_orphans"
    |
Build question_context dict (gap data + orphan data + cluster data + seed paper)
    |
Call Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile with question generation prompt
    |--- Streaming: yield token events for any prose preamble
    |--- Parse <RESEARCH_QUESTION>...</RESEARCH_QUESTION> blocks from response
    |
For each parsed question block
    |--- Compute confidence: 0.4 * structural_score + 0.6 * llm_tractability_score
    |--- Link evidence papers to graph node IDs from lineage context
    |--- Accumulate into questions array
    |
After all questions parsed
    |--- emit "research_questions_block" SSE event with questions array JSON
    |--- SSE stream closes
    |
Frontend: athena-engine.js receives research_questions_block
    |--- BlockRegistry.render('research_questions_block', payload, container)
    |--- ResearchQuestionBlock.render() -> staggered card entrance animation
    |--- QuestionActionStrip renders after last card
    |
User clicks "Test Q1"
    |--- Dispatch 'athena:hypothesis-test' custom event with Q1 data
    |--- Feature #019 (Hypothesis Tester) receives event and activates
    |
User clicks "Save as Artifact"
    |--- ArtifactCard created with type="research_questions", content=formatted markdown
    |--- Feature #011 (Artifact Cards) handles the card lifecycle

ERROR PATH:
R2 fetch fails (graph JSON not in R2)
    |
Backend falls back to computing questions from leaderboard_json alone (no gap data)
    |--- Questions generated from orphan data only
    |--- question_metadata notes "Gap analysis unavailable. Questions based on orphan data."
    |--- Confidence scores reduced by 0.2 to reflect reduced evidence

LLM fails (Groq unavailable)
    |
Backend generates template questions directly from structural data (no LLM)
    |--- Template: "What would happen if [orphan_paper] had been applied in [adjacent_cluster]?"
    |--- confidence = structural_score only (no LLM tractability component)
    |--- Prose note: "LLM unavailable. Questions generated from structural data only."

DATA STORED:
Generated questions: Not persisted to DB in Phase F.
If user saves as artifact (Feature #011): stored in ArtifactRegistry (in-memory).
Phase 6 auth adds DB persistence for saved artifacts.

DATA CACHED:
Gap analysis data: already cached in graphs table (dna_json, leaderboard_json)
and in R2 graph JSON from graph build pipeline. No re-computation needed.
TTL: GRAPH_CACHE_TTL_DAYS (default 7) from config.GRAPH_CACHE_TTL_DAYS.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Yes

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile
STAGE: Standalone (this endpoint calls the LLM directly, outside the main chat pipeline)

PROMPT SECTION: Standalone system prompt specific to research question generation.
This is NOT appended to the main Athena system prompt; it is a dedicated prompt.

ADDITIONAL PROMPT (literal text):
```
You are a research question generator for an academic citation analysis tool.
You have been provided with structural analysis of a citation lineage containing
[total_papers] papers spanning [year_min]-[year_max].

STRUCTURAL DATA PROVIDED:
GAPS: [gap_list -- each gap has: cluster_pair, semantic_distance, last_bridge_year, paper_count]
ORPHANS: [orphan_list -- each orphan has: paper_id, title, year, trajectory_score, reason]
CLUSTERS: [cluster_list -- each cluster has: name, paper_count, centroid_year]
SEED PAPER: [title, year, citation_count]

YOUR TASK:
Generate [max_questions] research questions that are STRUCTURALLY GROUNDED.
Each question must be traceable to specific gaps or orphans in the data above.
Do NOT generate generic questions about the research topic.
Do NOT fabricate structural observations not present in the data above.

For each question, output a block in this exact format:
<RESEARCH_QUESTION>
text: [The question, written as a specific, answerable research question. Max 150 chars.]
tractability: [Your assessment of how tractable this question is: 0.0 to 1.0. Be honest.]
justification: [2-3 sentences explaining which specific gap or orphan motivated this question.
                Name exact papers by title and year. Include the numerical structural evidence.]
evidence_papers: [Comma-separated list of exact paper titles from the provided data that are
                  most relevant to this question. Max 4 papers.]
gap_type: [gap | orphan | both]
</RESEARCH_QUESTION>

QUALITY STANDARDS:
- Questions should be novel: they should NOT be answerable by reading existing papers in the list.
- Questions should be specific: "How does X method apply to Y context?" not "What is the future of X?"
- Justification must cite numerical evidence: "semantic distance 0.73", "zero descendants post-2019."
- Tractability should reflect honest assessment of methodological feasibility given current literature.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
LLM streams text normally. RESEARCH_QUESTION blocks are parsed by a regex in the
backend SSE emitter. Each completed block is added to the questions array. After
all blocks are detected (or max_questions reached), the full array is emitted as
a single "research_questions_block" SSE event.

FALLBACK IF LLM FAILS:
Generate 3 template questions from structural data without LLM:
- "What would happen if the methods of [orphan_paper] were applied in [adjacent_cluster]?"
- "Why has no work bridged [cluster_A] (N papers) and [cluster_B] (M papers) since [year]?"
- "What prevented [orphan_paper]'s approach from influencing [descendant_cluster]?"
Questions flagged with confidence: 0.4 and a note "Generated from structure only."

TOKEN BUDGET:
Input: structural data (10 gaps + 10 orphans + clusters) = 600-900 tokens.
       System prompt = ~350 tokens. Total input: ~1200 tokens.
Output: 5 questions * ~200 tokens each = ~1000 tokens.
Total: ~2200 tokens. Well within Groq 70b's 32,768 context window.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Trigger                       | Element                    | Action                                               |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| Proactive suggestion button   | .athena-suggestion-btn     | POST /api/athena/research-questions, show progress   |
| /research-questions command   | Slash command parser       | Same as above                                        |
| Click "Expand justification"  | .question-expand           | Expand card, show justification + evidence, 250ms    |
| Click "Collapse"              | .question-collapse         | Collapse card to question text only, 250ms           |
| Click evidence paper link     | [data-paper-id] in card    | Dispatch arivu:focus-paper event to graph.js         |
| Click "Test this question"    | .question-test-btn         | Dispatch 'athena:hypothesis-test' with question data |
| Click "Dismiss" on card       | .question-dismiss          | Fade out and remove that question card, 200ms        |
| Click "Refine Questions"      | .action-refine             | Send follow-up message requesting refinement         |
| Click "Save as Artifact"      | .action-save-artifact      | Create ArtifactCard with all questions as markdown   |
| Click "Export"                | .action-export             | Download questions as markdown file                  |
| Click "Test Q1" in action bar | .action-test-q1            | Dispatch 'athena:hypothesis-test' with Q1 data       |

KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS:
- Tab through question cards in order. Enter/Space expands/collapses.
- Within expanded card: Tab to evidence paper links, Enter navigates to paper in graph.
- "Test this question" button: Tab to reach, Enter to activate.
- "Dismiss" button: Tab to reach, Enter to dismiss, Escape cancels.

SOUND EFFECTS:
NOT APPLICABLE (Athena uses no audio)

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Question block container: role="list", aria-label="Generated research questions"
- Each question card: role="listitem", aria-expanded="true|false"
- Confidence bar: role="progressbar", aria-valuenow=[0-100], aria-label="Confidence [N]%"
- Expand toggle: aria-expanded="false|true", aria-controls=[content-id]
- Evidence paper links: aria-label="Focus [paper title] in graph"
- "Test this question" button: aria-label="Test question [N] in hypothesis tester"
- Progress indicators during generation: role="status", aria-live="polite"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

EDGE CASE 1: Graph has no significant gaps (gap_finder returns no results above threshold)
Scenario: A lineage with dense interconnection between all clusters produces no gaps.
Expected behavior: The empty state is shown (see Visual Specification). Athena
explains that the lineage is well-connected. No question cards rendered. The
endpoint still returns 200 with an empty questions array and a message field.
Implementation: If gaps.length === 0 and orphans.length === 0, emit a
"research_questions_empty" SSE event. Frontend renders the empty state prose block.

EDGE CASE 2: User requests more than the allowed maximum (max_questions > 7)
Scenario: API call includes max_questions: 20.
Expected behavior: Silently capped at 7. The response includes at most 7 questions.
No error raised. This prevents excessive LLM token usage.
Implementation: max_questions = min(int(data.get("max_questions", 5)), 7) in the route.

EDGE CASE 3: LLM generates a question referencing a paper not in the lineage
Scenario: LLM halluccinates a paper title in the evidence_papers field.
Expected behavior: Unmatched paper names are rendered as plain text (not hyperlinks).
A small annotation "(not in current lineage)" appears next to the paper name.
The question itself is still shown. Evidence linking degrades gracefully.
Implementation: Frontend cross-references evidence_papers titles against
graphData.nodes titles. Unmatched entries get class "evidence-unverified".

EDGE CASE 4: User clicks "Test Q1" before Feature #019 is implemented
Scenario: Feature #013 is built in Phase F but #019 is not yet implemented.
Expected behavior: The "Test Q[N]" buttons are present but show a tooltip on hover:
"Hypothesis Tester not yet available. Coming in Phase F." Button is grayed out but
not hidden (preserves intended interface).
Implementation: Before dispatching 'athena:hypothesis-test', check if the event
listener exists (typeof window.AthenaHypothesisTester !== 'undefined'). If not,
show the "not available" tooltip.

EDGE CASE 5: R2 graph JSON not available (graph not stored in R2)
Scenario: Local development without R2 credentials, or R2 is unavailable.
Expected behavior: Backend falls back to generating questions from leaderboard_json
and dna_json alone (no gap data). Questions are generated from orphan analysis only.
A metadata note is included in the response: "Gap analysis unavailable. Questions
reflect orphan trajectory analysis only." Confidence scores reduced by 0.2.
Implementation: If R2Client.get_json() raises StorageError or returns None, set
gaps = [] and proceed. Log the fallback. Do not return a 500 error.

EDGE CASE 6: Session ends mid-generation (SSE connection drops)
Scenario: User closes the browser tab while the LLM is generating question 3 of 5.
Expected behavior: The backend LLM call is cancelled by the GeneratorExit exception
from the broken SSE connection. Partial results are not stored. When the user returns
(new session), no partial state is shown. The generator can be re-triggered fresh.
Implementation: The generator function wraps the LLM streaming call in a try/finally
block. GeneratorExit caught in finally cancels the Groq streaming call via the Groq
SDK's stream cancellation API.

EDGE CASE 7: Two rapid successive requests for research questions on the same graph
Scenario: User double-clicks the proactive suggestion button before the first
request returns.
Expected behavior: The second request is debounced/blocked. The first request
proceeds normally. The button becomes disabled after the first click and re-enables
after the first request completes or fails.
Implementation: athena-questions.js sets a module-level isGenerating flag to true
on the first click. The button gains disabled attribute. Flag cleared and button
re-enabled on stream close (research_questions_block received or error event).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON (must be built first):
- Feature #001 (Context-Aware Responses): The LLM prompt for question generation
  requires the lineage context (cluster names, paper counts, year range, seed paper).
  Without #001's StaticContextBuilder, this structural data is not available to the
  question generation prompt.
- Feature #002 (Rich Response Formatting): The question cards use the AthenaMarkdown
  renderer for justification text and the rich formatting system for the progress
  indicator block. Without #002, the question block renders as raw text.
- Feature #037 (Streaming Text): The question generation endpoint streams progress
  events and the final question block via SSE. Without the streaming infrastructure
  established by #037, the endpoint would need to block for the full LLM response
  (poor UX for a 15-30 second operation).

ENABLES (can be built after this):
- Feature #019 (Hypothesis Tester): Takes a research question (from this feature or
  manually entered) and evaluates it against the lineage structure. The data contract
  established here (question text + evidence_papers + gap_metadata) is the expected
  input format of the Hypothesis Tester.

ENHANCES (improves but does not require):
- Feature #009 (Proactive Suggestions): The proactive suggestion that triggers this
  feature is an instance of the broader proactive suggestion system from #009.
  Feature #013 adds a specific suggestion type to the proactive catalog.
- Feature #011 (Artifact Cards): Generated questions can be saved as Artifact Cards
  via the "Save as Artifact" button. Artifacts provide persistence and export. Without
  #011, the questions exist only in the conversation and cannot be exported.

CONFLICTS WITH:
None. This feature uses a dedicated endpoint and response block type that does not
overlap with the main chat pipeline or any other feature's data paths.

EXTERNAL DEPENDENCIES:
- gap_finder.py (backend/gap_finder.py): GapFinder results must be available in
  the graph JSON stored in R2. If graph was built without gap_finder data (Phase 2
  graphs without Phase 3 upgrades), the endpoint falls back to orphan-only questions.
- orphan_detector.py (backend/orphan_detector.py): Orphan data in leaderboard_json
  column of graphs table. Set during Phase 3 graph build.
- Groq API (groq==0.8.0): Primary LLM for question generation. Fallback to template
  questions if unavailable.
- R2Client (backend/r2_client.py): Fetch full graph JSON for gap_analysis section.
  If R2 unavailable, falls back to DB-cached data (leaderboard_json, dna_json).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[] POST /api/athena/research-questions returns SSE stream within 500ms of request
[] Progress events emit for all three stages (reading_gaps, evaluating_orphans, formulating)
[] Between 1 and 7 question cards rendered from LLM output
[] Each question card shows text, confidence bar (dot matrix), and justification
[] Confidence bar fills correct number of dots (Math.round(confidence * 10))
[] Evidence papers linked to graph nodes via arivu:focus-paper dispatch
[] "Test this question" dispatches 'athena:hypothesis-test' event with correct data
[] "Save as Artifact" creates Artifact Card (Feature #011) with all questions
[] "Export" downloads markdown file with all questions and justifications
[] Empty state renders when no significant gaps or orphans found
[] max_questions parameter capped at 7 regardless of request value
[] Fallback (LLM unavailable) generates template questions from structural data

VISUAL:
[] Question cards enter with staggered translateY animation (100ms delay per card)
[] Confidence bar uses dot matrix circles (filled = --accent-gold, empty = --text-muted)
[] Progress indicator uses dot matrix fill dots (empty, then filled as stages complete)
[] Expand/collapse animation uses max-height transition 250ms ease-in-out
[] Action strip fades in after last card with 400ms delay + 200ms duration

QUALITY:
[] No console errors during generation, rendering, or user interactions
[] Unverified evidence papers (not in graph) show "(not in current lineage)" label
[] Double-click on trigger button does not fire two simultaneous requests
[] GeneratorExit on connection drop cancels Groq streaming call cleanly

ACCESSIBILITY:
[] Question block has role="list" with aria-label
[] Each card has role="listitem" with aria-expanded
[] Confidence bar has role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow
[] Progress indicators have role="status" with aria-live="polite"
[] All interactive buttons keyboard accessible (Tab + Enter/Space)

INTEGRATION:
[] /api/athena/research-questions does not conflict with /api/athena/message route
[] Feature #011 Artifact Card creation works from this feature's Save button
[] gap_finder.py results correctly read from R2 graph JSON (field name verified)
[] orphan_detector.py results correctly read from leaderboard_json column

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 1 completed -- all findings addressed
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Round 2 completed -- all findings addressed
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: Final round -- agent confirms ZERO remaining issues
[] BROWSER TEST: Feature verified in Chrome via browsermcp
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Implementation matches this spec document exactly

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The pessimistic agent MUST verify this feature by:

1. READ: athena-questions.js (ResearchQuestionBlock, QuestionCard, QuestionActionStrip),
   athena-blocks.js (block registry entry for research_questions_block),
   athena-engine.js (research_questions_block SSE event handling),
   app.py (POST /api/athena/research-questions route),
   backend/gap_finder.py (verify field names for gap data structure),
   backend/orphan_detector.py (verify field names for orphan data structure)
2. COMPARE: Implementation against all Done Criteria checkboxes above
3. TEST these specific scenarios:
   a. Request questions on a 600-paper lineage with 6 gaps: verify 5 cards render
      with confidence bars showing correct dot fills
   b. Click an evidence paper link: verify arivu:focus-paper fires with correct paper_id
   c. Request questions on a small graph (< 30 papers, no gaps): verify empty state
   d. Trigger with max_questions: 20 in request: verify only 7 returned
   e. Test fallback: disable Groq in config, request questions: verify template
      questions appear with confidence 0.4
   f. Verify confidence calculation: check that it is 40% structural + 60% LLM
      tractability, not purely LLM-generated
   g. Save as Artifact: verify Artifact Card appears with correct content
4. CHECK for these failure modes:
   a. LLM confidence field may be outside 0.0-1.0 range (no validation in backend)
   b. gap_analysis field name in graph JSON may differ from expected ("gap_analysis"
      vs "gaps" vs "research_gaps") -- verify against actual gap_finder.py output
   c. Evidence paper matching by title is fragile (title case differences, subtitle
      truncation) -- verify the matching algorithm handles these cases
   d. The isGenerating debounce flag may not be cleared if SSE stream errors out
      before emitting research_questions_block (user gets stuck, button disabled)
   e. leaderboard_json may be None for graphs built before Phase 3 -- null check needed
5. VERIFY no regression in: #001 (Context), #002 (Formatting), #037 (Streaming),
   #011 (Artifact Cards if Save button is tested)
6. The agent assumes the implementation is BROKEN until proven otherwise
7. The agent must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round or state with evidence why none
8. The agent records ALL findings in the Pessimistic Agent Log section below
