════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #028: SPEED RUN MODE
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Mode Selector
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           E
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #024 (Teach Me Mode), #015 (Timeline Narration)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001, #002, #006, #037, #049 (Mode Selector)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Speed Run Mode is an accelerated lineage walkthrough that gives the user a
complete understanding of the research lineage in under 5 minutes. Instead of
exploring paper by paper, Athena presents a curated, auto-advancing sequence of
the most important insights: the seed paper, the top 5 most structural papers,
the main clusters, the biggest contradiction, the most critical bottleneck, and
the key gap. Each insight is presented as a compact card with 2-3 sentences and
a graph highlight, advancing automatically every 15-30 seconds.

This feature exists because not every researcher has time for deep exploration.
A reviewer needs to quickly understand a field's structure. A student needs a
fast overview before diving deep. Speed Run gives them "the essential story" of
the lineage in a compressed, well-structured format. It is the TL;DR of the
entire lineage, but computed and data-driven rather than summarized.

The speed run sequence is pre-computed from graph metrics:
1. Seed paper introduction (the starting point)
2. Top 3 structural papers by PageRank (the foundations)
3. Main clusters overview (the research strands)
4. Biggest pruning impact (the critical dependency)
5. Key contradiction (the active debate)
6. White space / gap (the opportunity)
7. Summary statement (the field in one paragraph)

Each step is a compact card with: paper/concept title, 2-3 sentences of
computed-data-driven explanation, a "Go deeper" button that pauses auto-advance
and opens a detailed discussion, and a progress indicator (step 3 of 7).

The auto-advance timer can be paused, and the user can manually navigate between
steps. The speed run uses only pre-computed data for steps 1-6 (no LLM calls).
Step 7 (summary) uses a fast LLM call. This means the speed run loads almost
instantly and does not consume significant resources.

What makes this unique: the speed run is COMPUTED, not curated. The top papers
are determined by PageRank and pruning impact. The clusters are from the DNA
profiler. The contradiction is from edge_analysis citation_intent. No human
pre-selected these highlights. The data determines what is important.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User activates via mode selector (#049), types /speedrun, or Athena suggests it
for first-time users: "New to this lineage? Try Speed Run for a 5-minute overview."

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /speedrun. A full-width card appears: "SPEED RUN: ResNet Lineage
   Overview (601 papers). 7 steps. ~5 minutes. [Start] [Skip to end]"
2. User clicks Start. Step 1 card appears with auto-advance timer (20s countdown
   bar at bottom): "This lineage traces the ancestry of Deep Residual Learning
   (He 2016). It contains 601 papers spanning 1989-2024 across 5 main clusters."
   Graph zooms to show overview.
3. After 20 seconds (or user clicks "Next"), Step 2 appears: "Top structural
   papers: #1 ResNet (PageRank 0.043), #2 VGGNet (0.031), #3 AlexNet (0.028)."
   Graph highlights these three nodes.
4. Steps 3-6 advance automatically, each highlighting relevant graph elements.
5. Step 7: LLM-generated one-paragraph summary of the entire lineage.
6. Final card: "Speed Run complete. [Explore further] [Session Summary] [Exit]"

AFTER COMPLETION:
User has a complete mental model of the lineage. Can dive deeper into any
highlighted element. Speed run stored in chat history.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-speedrun.js: Speed run orchestrator, step sequencer,
  auto-advance timer, graph highlight coordination.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: /speedrun command routing.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: SpeedRunCard block renderer.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Speed run card styles, progress bar, timer.
- app.py: GET /api/athena/speedrun/<graph_id> returns pre-computed steps.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/speedrun/<graph_id>')
  def athena_speedrun(graph_id):
      graph_data = load_graph(graph_id)
      nodes = graph_data['nodes']
      edges = graph_data['edges']
      seed = graph_data['metadata']
      # Compute steps from graph data (no LLM)
      steps = [
          {'type': 'intro', 'title': seed['seed_paper_title'], 'total_nodes': len(nodes)},
          {'type': 'top_papers', 'papers': sorted(nodes, key=lambda n: n.get('pagerank',0), reverse=True)[:3]},
          {'type': 'clusters', 'clusters': get_cluster_summary(nodes)},
          {'type': 'bottleneck', 'paper': max(nodes, key=lambda n: n.get('pruning_impact',0))},
          {'type': 'contradiction', 'edges': [e for e in edges if e.get('citation_intent')=='direct_contradiction'][:3]},
          {'type': 'gap', 'gaps': get_research_gaps(graph_data)},
      ]
      return jsonify({'steps': steps, 'total_steps': 7})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
class AthenaSpeedRun {
  constructor(steps) {
    this.steps = steps;
    this.current = 0;
    this.timer = null;
    this.paused = false;
  }
  start() {
    this.renderStep(0);
    this.startTimer();
  }
  startTimer() {
    this.timer = setTimeout(() => {
      if (!this.paused && this.current < this.steps.length - 1) {
        this.next();
      }
    }, 20000); // 20 seconds per step
  }
  next() {
    this.current++;
    this.renderStep(this.current);
    if (this.current < this.steps.length - 1) this.startTimer();
    else this.showCompletion();
  }
  renderStep(index) {
    const step = this.steps[index];
    appendSpeedRunCard(step, index + 1, this.steps.length + 1);
    highlightGraphForStep(step); // Zoom/highlight relevant nodes
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
GET /api/athena/speedrun/<graph_id>
Response: { "steps": [...], "total_steps": 7 }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Speed Run Step

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ SPEED RUN ● Step 2 of 7 ─────────┐   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  TOP STRUCTURAL PAPERS              │   │
  │  │  1. ResNet (PageRank: 0.043)        │   │
  │  │  2. VGGNet (PageRank: 0.031)        │   │
  │  │  3. AlexNet (PageRank: 0.028)       │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  These 3 papers control 42% of the  │   │
  │  │  structural influence in this       │   │
  │  │  lineage.                           │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  [Go deeper]          [Next →]      │   │
  │  │  ████████░░░░░░░░░░░ 12s remaining  │   │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Cards slide in from right (200ms). Timer bar decreases linearly. Graph smoothly zooms to relevant area (500ms).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User types /speedrun → GET /api/athena/speedrun/<graph_id>
  → Backend computes steps from graph data (no LLM, instant)
  → Returns 6 pre-computed steps
  → Frontend: SpeedRun orchestrator starts
  → Each step renders card + highlights graph
  → Auto-advance every 20 seconds
  → Step 7: POST /api/athena/stream for LLM summary
  → Completion card shown

ERROR PATH: Graph not loaded → "Load a graph first to start a Speed Run."
DATA STORED: action_log: { action: 'speedrun', completed_steps: N }
DATA CACHED: Speed run steps cached per graph_id

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (only for step 7 summary)
STAGE: Final step only. Steps 1-6 use pre-computed data (no LLM).
PROMPT: "Summarize this research lineage in one paragraph: {steps_data}. Use specific numbers."
OUTPUT FORMAT: One paragraph (3-4 sentences)
FALLBACK: Rule-based summary from step data: "This lineage of N papers has M clusters..."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~300 context + ~100 output = ~400 total for entire speed run

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| [Start]              | Click       | Begins speed run sequence                    |
| [Next →]             | Click       | Advances to next step immediately            |
| [Go deeper]          | Click       | Pauses auto-advance, opens detailed discuss  |
| Timer bar            | Click       | Pauses/resumes auto-advance                 |
| Progress (Step N/7)  | Visible     | Shows current position in sequence           |
| Paper name           | Click       | Highlights paper in graph                   |
| [Skip to end]        | Click       | Jumps to summary step                       |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Cards slide in from right 200ms. Timer bar linear decrease. Graph zoom 500ms ease-out.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Progress as aria-valuenow/aria-valuemax, auto-advance pause announced, step content readable by screen reader

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Graph has no contradictions (step 5 empty)
   Expected: Step 5 becomes "No contradictions detected. This lineage shows
   strong consensus." Implementation: Check data, adjust step content.

2. Scenario: User clicks "Go deeper" on step 3, then returns to speed run
   Expected: Speed run resumes from step 4 with timer restarted.
   Implementation: Store current step, resume on return.

3. Scenario: Very small graph (10 nodes)
   Expected: Steps adapt to available data. Top papers may only be 2. Clusters
   may be 1. Speed run still runs but with fewer insights per step.
   Implementation: Steps degrade gracefully with small data.

4. Scenario: User starts speed run then builds a new graph
   Expected: Speed run cancelled. "Speed Run cancelled. New graph building."
   Implementation: Cancel on graph build event.

5. Scenario: Auto-advance while user is scrolled up reading previous step
   Expected: New step appends but does NOT auto-scroll. User controls scroll.
   Implementation: Only auto-scroll if user is at bottom of chat.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #006, #037, #049
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #015 (Timeline Narration as deeper version), #024 (Teach Me as complementary)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: graph_data (PageRank, clusters, pruning impact, contradictions)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ 7-step sequence covering seed, top papers, clusters, bottleneck, contradiction, gap, summary
□ Auto-advance every 20 seconds with visible timer bar
□ Manual next/back navigation
□ "Go deeper" pauses and opens detailed discussion
□ Graph highlights synchronized with each step
□ Steps 1-6 use pre-computed data (no LLM), step 7 uses LLM

VISUAL: □ Full-width cards, progress indicator, timer bar
PERFORMANCE: □ Steps load instantly (pre-computed), total < 5 min
ACCESSIBILITY: □ Progress tracked, auto-advance pausable, content readable
EDGE CASES: □ Empty steps handled, small graphs adapted, scroll-aware advance

VERIFICATION: □ PESSIMISTIC AGENT complete □ BROWSER TEST verified □ CROSS-REFERENCE matches

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ files 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST all 7 steps 4. CHECK timer accuracy 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty — feature not yet implemented]

STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            Phase E. Mostly pre-computed. Only 1 LLM call total.


════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #029: SOCRATIC MODE
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Special Modes
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           E
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #024 (Teach Me Mode), #113 (Quiz Mode)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001, #002, #006, #037, #049 (Mode Selector)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Socratic Mode reverses the typical chat dynamic: instead of the user asking
questions and Athena answering, Athena asks questions and guides the user to
discover insights themselves. Athena becomes a research mentor who leads through
inquiry rather than exposition. "Before I tell you why ResNet matters, let me
ask: what do you think happens to the lineage if we remove it? What percentage
of papers do you think would collapse?"

This feature exists because the deepest learning comes from active reasoning,
not passive consumption. When a user predicts "maybe 10% would collapse" and
then sees the actual 31%, the surprise creates a memorable learning moment.
Socratic Mode leverages Arivu's computed data to create these moments of
productive surprise.

The mode operates through a question-answer-reveal cycle:
1. Athena asks a question about the lineage (based on graph data)
2. User provides their answer or prediction
3. Athena reveals the computed answer and explains the gap between prediction
   and reality

Questions are generated from graph metrics and tailored to what the user has NOT
yet explored. If they have not looked at pruning impacts, Athena asks about
structural importance. If they have not examined clusters, Athena asks about
research groupings. The question generation uses a combination of template
questions (filled with graph data) and LLM-generated questions for more
nuanced topics.

What makes this unique: the reveal step uses COMPUTED DATA, not opinions.
"You predicted 10% collapse. The actual simulation shows 31%. Here is why:
ResNet has 23 direct descendants and 3 of them are themselves critical
structural nodes (DenseNet, ResNeXt, SENet)." The gap between prediction and
reality is measured against actual graph computations.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User activates via mode selector (#049) or types /socratic.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /socratic. Athena: "Let us explore this lineage together through
   questions. I will ask, you predict, and then we will see what the data shows."
2. Athena asks: "Looking at this lineage of 601 papers, how many distinct research
   clusters do you think exist? (a) 2-3 (b) 4-5 (c) 6-8 (d) 9+"
3. User selects (b) 4-5.
4. Athena reveals: "The DNA profiler identified 7 distinct clusters. You were
   close! Here are the clusters: [list]. The largest is Deep Residual Learning
   with 89 papers (15% of the lineage)."
5. Athena asks next question: "Which single paper, if removed, would collapse
   the highest percentage of this lineage? Take a guess."
6. User types: "Probably the attention paper?"
7. Athena reveals: "Interesting guess! The attention paper collapses 22%.
   But ResNet actually collapses 31%. ResNet's higher impact comes from
   its position as a structural hub with 23 direct dependents."
8. After 5-7 questions, Athena summarizes: "You correctly predicted 3 out of 6.
   Your strongest intuition: cluster structure. Biggest surprise: pruning impact."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Summary of predictions vs reality. User can continue in normal mode or another round.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-socratic.js: Question generator, prediction tracker, reveal
  system, score tracking.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: /socratic command routing, mode activation.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: SocraticQuestionBlock, PredictionInputBlock, RevealBlock.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Question styling, prediction input, reveal animation.
- app.py: POST /api/athena/socratic/question (get next question),
  POST /api/athena/socratic/reveal (compare prediction to data).

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/socratic/question', methods=['POST'])
  def socratic_question():
      graph_data = load_graph(request.json['graph_id'])
      explored = request.json.get('explored_topics', [])
      # Generate question from unexplored graph aspects
      question = generate_socratic_question(graph_data, explored)
      return jsonify(question)

  def generate_socratic_question(graph_data, explored):
      questions = []
      if 'clusters' not in explored:
          cluster_count = len(set(n.get('cluster','') for n in graph_data['nodes'] if n.get('cluster')))
          questions.append({
              'topic': 'clusters',
              'question': f'How many distinct research clusters do you think exist in this lineage of {len(graph_data["nodes"])} papers?',
              'answer_type': 'multiple_choice',
              'options': ['2-3', '4-5', '6-8', '9+'],
              'correct_value': cluster_count,
              'correct_option': '2-3' if cluster_count <= 3 else '4-5' if cluster_count <= 5 else '6-8' if cluster_count <= 8 else '9+'
          })
      if 'pruning' not in explored:
          top_impact = max(graph_data['nodes'], key=lambda n: n.get('pruning_impact', 0))
          questions.append({
              'topic': 'pruning',
              'question': 'Which paper has the highest pruning impact? Take a guess.',
              'answer_type': 'free_text',
              'correct_value': {'title': top_impact['title'], 'impact': top_impact.get('pruning_impact', 0)}
          })
      return questions[0] if questions else None

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
class AthenaSocratic {
  constructor() {
    this.score = { correct: 0, total: 0 };
    this.explored = [];
  }
  async askNextQuestion() {
    const q = await fetch('/api/athena/socratic/question', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ graph_id: currentGraphId, explored_topics: this.explored })
    }).then(r => r.json());
    if (!q) { this.showSummary(); return; }
    renderSocraticQuestion(q);
    this.explored.push(q.topic);
  }
  async submitPrediction(question, userAnswer) {
    this.score.total++;
    const isCorrect = evaluatePrediction(question, userAnswer);
    if (isCorrect) this.score.correct++;
    renderReveal(question, userAnswer, isCorrect);
    setTimeout(() => this.askNextQuestion(), 3000);
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/socratic/question
Request: { "graph_id": "...", "explored_topics": ["clusters"] }
Response: { "topic": "pruning", "question": "Which paper...", "answer_type": "free_text", "correct_value": {...} }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Socratic Question (Multiple Choice)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ ATHENA ASKS ──────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ How many distinct research clusters│   │
  │  │ exist in this lineage of 601       │   │
  │  │ papers?                            │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  (a) 2-3                           │   │
  │  │  (b) 4-5                           │   │
  │  │  (c) 6-8                           │   │
  │  │  (d) 9+                            │   │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Reveal

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  You answered: (b) 4-5                    │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ REVEAL ───────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ ● Close! The answer is 7 clusters. │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │ 1. Deep Residual Learning (89)     │   │
  │  │ 2. Attention Mechanisms (67)       │   │
  │  │ 3. Object Detection (45)           │   │
  │  │ ... (4 more)                       │   │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Question fadeIn 300ms. Options stagger 100ms. Reveal: answer highlight (green correct, gold close, red far off) 200ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/socratic activated → first question fetched
  → Question rendered in chat → User submits prediction
  → Prediction compared to computed answer
  → Reveal rendered with explanation
  → Score updated → Next question after 3s delay
  → After 5-7 questions: summary of predictions vs reality

ERROR PATH: No more questions available → Show summary early
DATA STORED: action_log with predictions and scores, chat_history
DATA CACHED: Questions pre-fetched in batch (all 6-7 at once)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (for reveal explanations and final summary only)
STAGE: Post-prediction (explains gap between prediction and reality)
PROMPT: "The user predicted {prediction} for {question}. The actual answer is {correct}. Explain why the answer is what it is, using graph data. 2-3 sentences."
OUTPUT FORMAT: Short prose explanation
FALLBACK: Template-based reveal: "The actual answer is {correct}. {reason}."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~150 per reveal x 7 questions = ~1050 total for full Socratic session

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Multiple choice opt  | Click       | Submits prediction, triggers reveal          |
| Free text input      | Enter       | Submits prediction                          |
| Reveal block         | Visible     | Shows correct answer with explanation        |
| Score display        | Visible     | "3/6 correct" running total                 |
| [Skip question]      | Click       | Moves to next without scoring               |
| Graph highlight      | Auto        | Relevant nodes highlighted during reveal     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Options stagger 100ms. Reveal answer highlight pulse 300ms. Score counter animation.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Options as radio group, reveal as aria-live, score as aria-valuenow

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: All topics already explored (user explored everything before Socratic)
   Expected: "You have already explored all aspects of this lineage! Let me test
   your memory instead..." (quiz-style recall questions)
   Implementation: Fallback to memory-based questions.

2. Scenario: User's free-text prediction cannot be matched to any paper
   Expected: "I could not find that paper. Did you mean [suggestions]?"
   Implementation: Fuzzy matching with clarification.

3. Scenario: User types /socratic on a graph with < 20 nodes
   Expected: Reduced question set (3-4 questions instead of 7). Some questions
   skipped (e.g., cluster question if only 1 cluster).
   Implementation: Filter questions by minimum data requirements.

4. Scenario: User wants to skip to the answers without predicting
   Expected: Allowed but logged differently. Reveals still educational without scores.
   Implementation: [Skip] button on each question.

5. Scenario: Two questions have the same correct answer paper
   Expected: Deduplicate. Different aspects of the same paper are valid.
   Implementation: Questions reference different metrics of the same paper.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #006, #037, #049
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #024 (Teach Me), #113 (Quiz Mode)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: graph_data metrics, GROQ_FAST_MODEL for reveals

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ 5-7 questions generated from unexplored graph aspects
□ Multiple choice and free-text answer types
□ Prediction compared to computed data
□ Reveal with explanation and graph highlight
□ Running score tracker
□ Final summary of predictions vs reality
□ Questions adapt to what user has already explored

VISUAL: □ Question blocks, option styling, reveal animation
PERFORMANCE: □ Questions pre-computed (instant), reveals < 2s
ACCESSIBILITY: □ Options as radio, reveals as aria-live, score tracked
EDGE CASES: □ Small graphs adapted, free-text fuzzy matching

VERIFICATION: □ PESSIMISTIC AGENT complete □ BROWSER TEST verified □ CROSS-REFERENCE matches

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ files 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST full Socratic session 4. CHECK score accuracy 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #030: CONSPIRACY BOARD MODE
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Special Modes
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           E
ESTIMATED HOURS: 18
OVERLAPS WITH:   #034 (Chat-Driven Graph Navigation), #012 (AI Annotations)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001, #002, #006, #037, #049, #012 (AI Annotations)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Conspiracy Board Mode transforms the graph visualization into a "detective's
evidence board" aesthetic where Athena helps the user trace hidden connections,
find suspicious patterns, and uncover non-obvious relationships in the research
lineage. Nodes become "evidence cards" pinned to the board. Edges become "red
strings" connecting evidence. Athena acts as a detective's partner: "Notice how
these three papers all cite the same obscure 1994 paper? That is suspicious.
Let me dig deeper into what connects them."

This feature exists because research lineages contain non-obvious patterns that
are difficult to spot in a standard graph view. Papers that share unusual
ancestors, clusters that formed suspiciously quickly (many papers in a short
time window citing each other), and citation patterns that suggest coordinated
influence are all "detective-worthy" observations. Conspiracy Board Mode makes
finding these patterns fun and intuitive.

The mode overlays the graph with a visual transformation: background becomes
a cork-board texture (dot matrix interpretation: dense dot grid with warm tint),
nodes get paper-card styling (white cards with slight rotation), edges become
red connecting lines (thicker, slightly curved). Annotations (#012) appear as
"sticky notes" with handwriting-style text.

Athena's behavior changes in this mode. Instead of neutral analysis, it adopts
a detective persona: "Interesting. These 5 papers all emerged within 6 months
of each other, all citing the same foundational work. That is an unusually
tight cluster. Could indicate a coordinated research push or a breakthrough
that multiple groups discovered simultaneously."

The detective analysis is computed, not fabricated: temporal clustering uses
actual publication years, citation patterns use real edge data, and "suspicious"
connections are identified by statistical outliers (papers with unusually high
citation overlap or papers cited together far more often than expected).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User activates via mode selector or types /conspiracy or /detective.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /conspiracy. Graph transforms: cork-board background, cards for
   nodes, red strings for edges. Athena: "Detective mode activated. Let me
   search for hidden patterns in this lineage..."
2. Athena highlights the first "finding": "Finding #1: Temporal cluster. Papers
   [A], [B], [C] all published 2017-2018, all citing [D]. Publication density:
   3 papers in 14 months, 4x the lineage average."
3. Graph zooms to the identified cluster. Red strings pulse between the papers.
4. Athena asks: "Want me to investigate this further, or should I look for
   another pattern?"
5. User: "Look for more patterns."
6. Athena: "Finding #2: Citation anomaly. Paper [E] has 12 incoming citations
   but 0 outgoing in this lineage. It is a dead end. All 12 citing papers
   are from the same research group."
7. After 3-5 findings, Athena produces a "Detective Report" summarizing all
   discovered patterns.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Graph shows all findings highlighted. Report in chat. User can discuss any finding.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-conspiracy.js: Pattern detector, finding generator, graph
  overlay manager, detective persona.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: /conspiracy command routing.
- static/js/graph.js: Cork-board visual overlay, card node styling, red string edges.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Detective-style message blocks, finding cards.
- app.py: POST /api/athena/conspiracy-findings endpoint.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/conspiracy-findings', methods=['POST'])
  def conspiracy_findings():
      graph_data = load_graph(request.json['graph_id'])
      findings = []
      # Pattern 1: Temporal clusters
      temporal = find_temporal_clusters(graph_data['nodes'])
      if temporal: findings.extend(temporal)
      # Pattern 2: Citation anomalies
      anomalies = find_citation_anomalies(graph_data['nodes'], graph_data['edges'])
      if anomalies: findings.extend(anomalies)
      # Pattern 3: Author concentration
      author_conc = find_author_concentration(graph_data['nodes'])
      if author_conc: findings.extend(author_conc)
      # Pattern 4: Orphan chains
      orphans = find_orphan_chains(graph_data['nodes'], graph_data['edges'])
      if orphans: findings.extend(orphans)
      return jsonify({'findings': findings[:5]})  # Top 5

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```python
def find_temporal_clusters(nodes):
    """Find groups of 3+ papers published within 18 months of each other
    that share common citations (potential coordinated research)."""
    findings = []
    by_year = defaultdict(list)
    for n in nodes:
        if n.get('year'): by_year[n['year']].append(n)
    for year in sorted(by_year.keys()):
        window = by_year.get(year, []) + by_year.get(year+1, [])
        if len(window) >= 3:
            # Check if they share common ancestors
            # ... (detailed analysis)
            findings.append({
                'type': 'temporal_cluster',
                'papers': [n['title'] for n in window[:5]],
                'year_range': f'{year}-{year+1}',
                'density': len(window) / 2  # papers per year in window
            })
    return findings
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/conspiracy-findings
Request: { "graph_id": "..." }
Response: { "findings": [
  { "type": "temporal_cluster", "papers": [...], "year_range": "2017-2018", "description": "..." },
  { "type": "citation_anomaly", "paper": "...", "incoming": 12, "outgoing": 0, "description": "..." }
]}

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Conspiracy Board Active

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ [Cork-board dot matrix background]        │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌──────┐  red string  ┌──────┐          │
  │  │Paper │──────────────│Paper │          │
  │  │Card A│              │Card B│          │
  │  └──────┘     \        └──────┘          │
  │                \                         │
  │                ┌──────┐                   │
  │                │Paper │                   │
  │                │Card C│                   │
  │                └──────┘                   │
  │                                           │
  │  FINDING #1: Temporal Cluster             │
  │  Papers A, B, C all published 2017-2018   │
  │  citing the same foundational work.       │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Board transformation 500ms (background + node styling). Findings fade in 300ms. Red strings pulse animation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/conspiracy activated → Graph overlay applied
  → POST /api/athena/conspiracy-findings { graph_id }
  → Backend: runs 4 pattern detectors on graph data
  → Returns top 5 findings
  → Frontend: renders findings sequentially
  → Graph highlights relevant nodes for each finding
  → Detective persona narrates findings
  → After all findings: "Detective Report" summary

ERROR PATH: No patterns found → "This lineage appears clean. No unusual patterns detected."
DATA STORED: action_log, findings cached per graph
DATA CACHED: Findings per graph_id in sessionStorage

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (detective-persona narration of pre-computed findings)
STAGE: Post-computation (narrates each finding in detective voice)
PROMPT: "You are a research detective. Narrate this finding in 2-3 sentences. Be intriguing but factual: {finding_data}. No em dashes. No emojis."
OUTPUT FORMAT: 2-3 sentence narrative per finding
FALLBACK: Template-based narration: "Finding #{n}: {type}. Papers {papers}."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~150 per finding x 5 = ~750 per conspiracy session

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Finding card         | Click       | Expands with detailed analysis               |
| Paper card (node)    | Click       | Shows paper details in detective style       |
| Red string (edge)    | Hover       | Shows edge classification                   |
| [Investigate further]| Click       | Deeper analysis of specific finding          |
| [Next finding]       | Click       | Reveals next pattern                        |
| [Exit detective mode]| Click       | Restores normal graph view                  |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Board transform 500ms. Red strings pulse (opacity 0.5 to 1.0, 1.5s loop). Finding cards slide in from right 300ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Findings numbered, graph overlay announced, red strings described in aria-labels

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: No suspicious patterns found in graph
   Expected: "This lineage is clean. No temporal clusters, citation anomalies,
   or author concentrations detected. That itself is interesting: it suggests
   organic, decentralized research evolution."
   Implementation: Handle empty findings with informational message.

2. Scenario: Very large graph (600+ nodes) slows pattern detection
   Expected: Pattern detection runs on top 100 nodes by PageRank.
   Implementation: Sample high-impact nodes for efficiency.

3. Scenario: Finding is a false positive (e.g., papers in same year is coincidence)
   Expected: Findings include confidence score. Low-confidence findings labeled
   "Weak signal." User can dismiss.
   Implementation: Score each finding by statistical significance.

4. Scenario: Cork-board overlay conflicts with accessibility requirements
   Expected: High-contrast mode available. Red strings become thick black lines.
   Cards get stronger borders.
   Implementation: Detect prefers-contrast, adjust overlay.

5. Scenario: User exits detective mode with findings still highlighted
   Expected: All overlay effects removed cleanly. Graph returns to normal.
   Implementation: removeOverlay() restores original node/edge styles.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #006, #037, #049, #012 (AI Annotations for overlay)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: Graph exploration, #034 (Chat-Driven Navigation)
CONFLICTS: NONE (overlays are removable)
EXTERNAL: graph_data, temporal analysis

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ Cork-board visual overlay on graph
□ 4 pattern types detected (temporal, citation anomaly, author concentration, orphan chains)
□ Top 5 findings presented sequentially
□ Detective persona narration for each finding
□ Graph highlights relevant nodes/edges per finding
□ Exit mode restores normal graph
□ Findings include confidence scores

VISUAL: □ Cork-board background, card nodes, red string edges, pulsing animation
PERFORMANCE: □ Pattern detection < 3s, overlay transition 500ms
ACCESSIBILITY: □ High-contrast mode, findings numbered, overlay announced
EDGE CASES: □ No patterns handled, large graphs sampled, false positives labeled

VERIFICATION: □ PESSIMISTIC AGENT complete □ BROWSER TEST verified □ CROSS-REFERENCE matches

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ files 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST pattern detection 4. CHECK overlay removal 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Implementation:   Not started

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #031: EXPLAIN FOR ANOTHER FIELD
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Mode Selector
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           E
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #024 (Teach Me Mode)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001, #002, #037, #049 (Mode Selector), #024 (Teach Me Mode)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Explain for Another Field is a specialized mode that translates Athena's
explanations into the terminology and conceptual framework of a different
academic discipline. A biologist exploring a computer science lineage gets
explanations using biological analogies. A physicist gets physics analogies.
An economist gets economic analogies. This goes beyond Teach Me Mode's level
adjustment: it fundamentally reframes the concepts.

This feature exists because interdisciplinary research is the norm, not the
exception. A neuroscientist studying attention mechanisms (biological) naturally
explores the transformer lineage (computational). Without field translation,
every explanation uses CS jargon that creates unnecessary cognitive overhead.
With field translation, "residual connections" becomes "feedback loops that
prevent signal degradation, similar to homeostatic mechanisms in biological
neural circuits."

The mode is activated by selecting a target field from a dropdown. Available
fields: Biology, Physics, Mathematics, Economics, Psychology, Chemistry,
Medicine, Engineering, Social Sciences, Humanities, and Custom (user specifies).
Each field has a pre-built analogy bank and a system prompt modifier that
instructs the LLM to use field-appropriate language.

The system prompt for field translation is: "Translate all computer science
and machine learning concepts into {field} analogies and terminology. When
discussing 'neural networks,' compare to {field_specific_analog}. When
discussing 'optimization,' compare to {field_specific_analog}. Use terminology
that a {field} researcher would naturally understand."

What makes this unique: the analogies reference COMPUTED GRAPH DATA. "In this
lineage, ResNet plays a role similar to DNA polymerase in molecular biology:
it is the foundational mechanism that 31% of all subsequent research depends on.
Removing it is like knocking out a essential gene: the cascade of effects is
massive." The analogy is dynamic (31% is computed), not a static mapping.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User selects from mode selector (#049) or types /explain-as [field].

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /explain-as biology. Athena: "I will now explain this computer
   science lineage using biology analogies and terminology."
2. User asks: "What is ResNet?"
3. Athena: "ResNet introduced residual connections, which function like feedback
   loops in biological regulatory networks. Just as homeostatic mechanisms
   prevent signal degradation in neural pathways, residual connections prevent
   gradient degradation in computational networks. In this lineage, ResNet is
   the equivalent of a keystone species: remove it and 31% of the ecosystem
   (47 of 152 dependent papers) collapses."
4. User asks: "What about the clusters?"
5. Athena: "The 7 research clusters in this lineage map well to ecological
   niches. The largest niche (Deep Residual Learning, 89 papers) is like a
   dominant species in a mature ecosystem..."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Field translation stays active. All responses use field analogies. Deactivate
via mode selector or /explain-as default.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-fieldtranslate.js: Field selection, analogy bank, system
  prompt modifier.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: /explain-as routing, prompt modification.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Field indicator badge in header.
- app.py: Field translation metadata passed with chat requests.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  const FIELD_ANALOGIES = {
    biology: {
      neural_network: 'biological neural circuit',
      optimization: 'evolutionary fitness optimization',
      residual_connection: 'homeostatic feedback loop',
      cluster: 'ecological niche',
      pruning: 'gene knockout experiment',
      pagerank: 'keystone species importance',
    },
    physics: {
      neural_network: 'coupled oscillator system',
      optimization: 'energy minimization',
      residual_connection: 'quantum tunneling shortcut',
      cluster: 'phase of matter',
      pruning: 'removing a fundamental force',
      pagerank: 'gravitational center of mass',
    },
    // ... more fields
  };

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
No new endpoint. System prompt modified when field translation is active:
  if teach_mode.field_translation:
      system_prompt = FIELD_TRANSLATION_PROMPT.format(
          field=teach_mode.target_field,
          analogies=json.dumps(FIELD_ANALOGIES.get(teach_mode.target_field, {}))
      ) + system_prompt

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```python
FIELD_TRANSLATION_PROMPT = """You are explaining a computer science research lineage
to a {field} researcher. Translate ALL technical concepts using {field} analogies:
{analogies}
When mentioning computed metrics, wrap them in field-appropriate context.
For example, instead of "PageRank 0.043", say "keystone importance score of 0.043."
Use {field} terminology naturally. No em dashes. No emojis."""
```

API CONTRACTS: No new endpoints. Metadata added to existing /api/athena/stream calls.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Field Translation Active

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ ● ATHENA [Explain as: Biology] [≡]       │
  │───────────────────────────────────────────│
  │                                           │
  │  ResNet introduced residual connections,  │
  │  which function like **feedback loops**   │
  │  in biological regulatory networks...     │
  │                                           │
  │  In this lineage, ResNet is the           │
  │  equivalent of a **keystone species**:    │
  │  remove it and 31% of the ecosystem       │
  │  collapses.                               │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Field badge appears in header (200ms fadeIn). Responses immediately use new field.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/explain-as [field] → field stored in session state
  → System prompt modified with field translation prefix + analogy bank
  → All subsequent LLM calls include field context
  → Responses use field-appropriate language
  → Header shows active field badge

ERROR PATH: Unknown field → "I do not have analogies for [field]. I will try my best with general cross-disciplinary language."
DATA STORED: session state: target_field
DATA CACHED: Analogy banks are static (no caching needed)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (regular chat with modified system prompt)
STAGE: System prompt modification (always active when field selected)
PROMPT: FIELD_TRANSLATION_PROMPT prefix with field and analogies
OUTPUT FORMAT: Standard prose with field-appropriate language
FALLBACK: If LLM ignores field instructions, standard response (no degradation)
TOKEN BUDGET: ~150 extra tokens for translation prefix per message

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Field dropdown       | Select      | Activates field translation                 |
| Header badge         | Click       | Opens field dropdown to change/deactivate   |
| Field-specific terms | Hover       | Tooltip showing original CS term             |
| /explain-as default  | Type        | Deactivates field translation                |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Badge fadeIn 200ms. Field switch immediate (no animation needed).
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Field badge as aria-label, terms with both field and original accessible

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User selects "Custom" field and types "Culinary Arts"
   Expected: LLM does its best with novel field. No pre-built analogy bank.
   Implementation: Custom fields use generic cross-disciplinary prompt.

2. Scenario: Concept has no good analogy in target field
   Expected: LLM explains in plain language: "There is no direct equivalent in
   biology, but think of it as..."
   Implementation: Prompt allows for "no direct equivalent" responses.

3. Scenario: User is actually FROM the displayed field (CS person exploring CS lineage)
   Expected: /explain-as cs uses native terminology. Essentially a no-op.
   Implementation: CS field uses standard CS vocabulary (identity translation).

4. Scenario: Combined with Teach Me Mode (beginner + biology)
   Expected: Both modifiers applied. Simple biology analogies.
   Implementation: Both prompts concatenated in system message.

5. Scenario: Field-specific tooltip shows original term
   Expected: Hovering "keystone species" shows tooltip "Original: PageRank importance."
   Implementation: LLM wraps translated terms in ** for tooltip detection.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #037, #049, #024 (Teach Me Mode for level system)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #024 (Teach Me Mode), #008 (Pathfinder with field context)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: GROQ_FAST_MODEL system prompt modification

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ 10+ pre-built fields with analogy banks
□ Custom field option
□ System prompt modified with field-appropriate language
□ Responses use field analogies for technical concepts
□ Field badge in Athena header
□ Deactivatable via /explain-as default
□ Combinable with Teach Me Mode levels

VISUAL: □ Field badge, tooltip for original terms
PERFORMANCE: □ No additional latency (prompt modification only)
ACCESSIBILITY: □ Badge labeled, tooltips accessible
EDGE CASES: □ Custom fields handled, no-analogy graceful, CS identity translation

VERIFICATION: □ PESSIMISTIC AGENT complete □ BROWSER TEST verified □ CROSS-REFERENCE matches

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ files 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST multiple fields 4. CHECK analogy quality 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #037: STREAMING TEXT
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   NONE (this IS the streaming foundation)
BLOCKS:          #041 (Stop Generation), #046 (Typing Indicator), #073 (Streaming Cursor), #075 (Response Reveal)
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Streaming Text is the real-time token delivery system that makes Athena's
responses appear word-by-word as the LLM generates them, rather than waiting
for the complete response and rendering it all at once. This is the most
fundamental infrastructure feature in Athena because it transforms the
experience from "waiting for a wall of text" to "watching the AI think."

Without streaming, the user sends a message and stares at a blank space for
5-8 seconds (Groq 70b inference time). Then a complete paragraph appears
instantaneously. This feels broken. With streaming, text begins appearing
within 1-3 seconds, flowing word by word. The user can start reading while
the AI is still generating. They can decide to stop if the response is going
in the wrong direction. They feel in control.

The streaming implementation uses Server-Sent Events (SSE) over HTTP, not
WebSockets. SSE is simpler, works through proxies (critical for Koyeb
deployment), supports automatic reconnection, and is sufficient for one-way
streaming (server to client). The backend sends events for each token batch
(5-10 tokens per event to reduce overhead while maintaining perceived fluidity).

Athena's streaming is more complex than a generic chat because it streams
STRUCTURED content. A response might contain: a heading (rendered as h3),
then prose (streamed token by token), then a Paper Card block (rendered
complete, not streamed character by character), then more prose (streamed),
then a Stat Dashboard (rendered complete). Block boundaries must be detected
during streaming so that blocks render atomically while prose streams smoothly.

The detection works via delimiter tokens in the LLM output. When the stream
encounters a block start marker (e.g., `<PAPER_CARD>` or `<STAT_GRID>`), it
buffers until the corresponding end marker, then renders the complete block.
Prose between markers streams normally. This creates a smooth experience where
text flows naturally and structured blocks "pop in" at the right moments.

The SSE connection also carries metadata events: progress indicators (for
multi-stage queries), block type announcements (so the frontend can prepare
layout space), and completion signals (to trigger post-response actions like
quick action buttons). The event types are: 'token' (text chunk), 'block'
(complete structured block), 'meta' (progress/status), 'done' (stream complete),
and 'error' (generation failed).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User sends any message in Athena chat. Streaming begins automatically for
every response.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User sends a message and presses Enter. Message appears as user bubble.
2. Within 200ms, a typing indicator (#046) appears below the user message,
   showing Athena is processing.
3. Within 1-3 seconds, the first tokens arrive. The typing indicator is replaced
   by actual text. Words appear 3-5 at a time, creating a smooth reading pace.
4. If the response includes a block (Paper Card, Stats), the prose streams up to
   the block boundary, then the block renders complete (200ms fade-in), then
   prose continues streaming after the block.
5. A blinking cursor (#073) marks the current streaming position. As text appears,
   the cursor advances. The chat auto-scrolls to keep the cursor visible (unless
   the user has manually scrolled up).
6. A stop button (#041) appears during streaming, allowing the user to halt
   generation.
7. When streaming completes, the 'done' event fires. Quick action buttons (#003)
   appear. The stop button disappears. The cursor disappears.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Full response visible in chat. Auto-scroll position at bottom. Quick actions
available. Response stored in conversation memory (#006).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-stream.js: SSE connection manager, token buffer, block
  detection, auto-scroll, reconnection logic.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Initiates stream on message send, routes events
  to appropriate handlers.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Block rendering triggered by 'block' events.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Streaming text styles, cursor animation.
- app.py: GET /api/athena/stream SSE endpoint that wraps Groq streaming API.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaStream {
    constructor() {
      this.eventSource = null;
      this.currentMessageEl = null;
      this.blockBuffer = '';
      this.isBufferingBlock = false;
    }
    start(sessionId, message, graphId) {
      const url = `/api/athena/stream?session_id=${sessionId}&message=${encodeURIComponent(message)}&graph_id=${graphId}`;
      this.eventSource = new EventSource(url);
      this.currentMessageEl = createAssistantMessage();

      this.eventSource.addEventListener('token', (e) => {
        const text = e.data;
        if (this.isBufferingBlock) {
          this.blockBuffer += text;
          if (this.blockBuffer.includes('</BLOCK>')) {
            this.renderBlock(this.blockBuffer);
            this.blockBuffer = '';
            this.isBufferingBlock = false;
          }
        } else if (text.includes('<BLOCK')) {
          this.isBufferingBlock = true;
          this.blockBuffer = text;
        } else {
          this.appendText(text);
        }
      });

      this.eventSource.addEventListener('block', (e) => {
        const block = JSON.parse(e.data);
        this.renderBlock(block);
      });

      this.eventSource.addEventListener('done', () => {
        this.eventSource.close();
        this.onComplete();
      });

      this.eventSource.addEventListener('error', (e) => {
        this.handleError(e);
      });
    }
    appendText(text) {
      const textNode = document.createTextNode(text);
      this.currentMessageEl.querySelector('.msg-content').appendChild(textNode);
      this.autoScroll();
    }
    stop() {
      if (this.eventSource) {
        this.eventSource.close();
        this.appendText('\n\n[Generation stopped]');
        this.onComplete();
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/stream')
  def athena_stream():
      session_id = request.args.get('session_id')
      message = request.args.get('message')
      graph_id = request.args.get('graph_id')

      def generate():
          # Build context
          memory = load_conversation_memory(session_id)
          graph_context = get_graph_summary_for_chat(load_graph(graph_id))
          messages = build_llm_context(memory, message, graph_context)

          # Stream from Groq
          stream = groq_client.chat.completions.create(
              model=config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL,
              messages=messages,
              stream=True
          )
          token_buffer = []
          for chunk in stream:
              delta = chunk.choices[0].delta.content
              if delta:
                  token_buffer.append(delta)
                  if len(token_buffer) >= 5:  # Batch 5 tokens per event
                      yield f"event: token\ndata: {''.join(token_buffer)}\n\n"
                      token_buffer = []
          if token_buffer:
              yield f"event: token\ndata: {''.join(token_buffer)}\n\n"
          yield f"event: done\ndata: {{}}\n\n"

      return Response(generate(), mimetype='text/event-stream',
                      headers={'Cache-Control': 'no-cache', 'X-Accel-Buffering': 'no'})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
// Auto-scroll logic: only auto-scroll if user is near bottom
autoScroll() {
  const container = document.querySelector('.athena-messages');
  const isNearBottom = container.scrollHeight - container.scrollTop - container.clientHeight < 100;
  if (isNearBottom) {
    container.scrollTop = container.scrollHeight;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
GET /api/athena/stream?session_id=...&message=...&graph_id=...
SSE Events:
  event: token
  data: "text chunk here"

  event: block
  data: {"type": "paper_card", "data": {...}}

  event: meta
  data: {"stage": "analyzing", "progress": 0.5}

  event: done
  data: {}

  event: error
  data: {"message": "Generation failed", "code": "LLM_ERROR"}

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Streaming in Progress

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  You: Tell me about key papers            │
  │                                           │
  │  Athena: The key papers in this           │
  │  lineage include several foundational     │
  │  works that shaped the field of deep█     │
  │                                           │
  │                              [■ Stop]     │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Block Rendering Mid-Stream

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena: The most important paper is:     │
  │                                           │
  │  ┌─ Paper Card ──────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ Deep Residual Learning...          │    │
  │  │ He et al., 2016                   │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────┘    │
  │                                           │
  │  This paper's significance stems from█    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Stream Complete

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena: The key papers include...        │
  │  (full response visible)                  │
  │                                           │
  │  ● Show blind spots                       │
  │  ● Compare ResNet and VGGNet              │
  │  ● Go deeper on attention mechanism       │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Typing indicator → First token: indicator fades out (100ms), text starts
Block boundary: prose pauses, block fades in (200ms), prose resumes
Stream end: cursor disappears (100ms), quick actions fade in (200ms)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User sends message
  → athena-engine.js stores user message in memory
  → Creates EventSource connection to /api/athena/stream
  → Backend: builds LLM context (memory + graph + system prompt)
  → Backend: calls Groq streaming API
  → Groq: generates tokens
  → Backend: batches 5 tokens per SSE event
  → Frontend: 'token' events append text to message element
  → Frontend: 'block' events render structured blocks
  → Frontend: 'done' event closes connection, triggers post-stream actions
  → athena-engine.js stores complete response in memory

ERROR PATH:
  → SSE connection fails → Retry with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 3 retries)
  → LLM generation fails → 'error' event with message, show "Generation failed. Retry?"
  → Network drops mid-stream → EventSource auto-reconnects (built-in SSE behavior)
  → Groq rate limited → 'error' event, show "Rate limited. Try again in a moment."

DATA STORED:
  → Complete response stored in chat_history AFTER stream completes
  → Partial responses NOT stored (only complete ones)

DATA CACHED:
  → No caching for stream itself (always fresh)
  → Response cached in memory after completion

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (default) or config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL (for deep queries)
STAGE: Direct streaming from Groq API
PROMPT: Context-dependent (system prompt + memory context + graph context + user message)
OUTPUT FORMAT: Raw text stream with block delimiters
FALLBACK: If streaming unavailable, fall back to non-streaming (wait for full response)
TOKEN BUDGET: Varies per query. Typical: 500-1500 tokens output.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Streaming text       | Read        | Auto-scrolls to keep cursor visible          |
| User scrolls up      | Scroll      | Auto-scroll pauses (user reading earlier text)|
| User scrolls down    | Scroll      | Auto-scroll resumes when near bottom         |
| [Stop] button        | Click       | Closes EventSource, appends "[stopped]"      |
| Error retry          | Click       | Re-initiates stream with same message        |
| Block render         | Auto        | Blocks fade in 200ms at insertion point      |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Text appearance: instant (no per-character animation, text appears in batches)
- Streaming cursor: blinking 1s period (see #073)
- Block insertion: opacity 0 to 1, 200ms ease-in
- Auto-scroll: smooth scrollBehavior for 50ms

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Message area has aria-live="polite" (announces new content)
- Streaming state announced: "Athena is responding"
- Stop button has aria-label="Stop generating response"
- Complete announcement: "Response complete"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User sends new message while previous response is still streaming
   Expected: Previous stream stops (EventSource closed). New stream begins.
   Partial previous response preserved with "[interrupted]" suffix.
   Implementation: stop() current stream before starting new one.

2. Scenario: SSE connection drops after 30 seconds of streaming
   Expected: EventSource auto-reconnects (SSE built-in). Text continues from
   where it left off. If reconnection fails 3 times, show error with retry button.
   Implementation: EventSource.onerror handles reconnection, count retries.

3. Scenario: LLM outputs malformed block delimiter (unclosed `<BLOCK>` tag)
   Expected: After 5 seconds of buffering without close tag, flush buffer as
   plain text. Log the malformed output.
   Implementation: Timeout on block buffer (5s max).

4. Scenario: Very long response (2000+ tokens, takes 20+ seconds)
   Expected: User can scroll up during streaming without losing auto-scroll.
   When they scroll back to bottom, auto-scroll resumes.
   Implementation: isNearBottom check (within 100px of bottom).

5. Scenario: Multiple concurrent streams (rare, from race condition)
   Expected: Only one stream active at a time. Second start() calls stop() on first.
   Implementation: Singleton stream manager, abort previous on new.

6. Scenario: Groq returns empty stream (0 tokens)
   Expected: "I could not generate a response. Please try rephrasing your question."
   Implementation: If 'done' received with 0 tokens appended, show error.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #007 (Visual Redesign for message containers)
ENABLES: #041 (Stop), #046 (Typing Indicator), #073 (Streaming Cursor), #075 (Response Reveal)
ENHANCES: Every feature that generates LLM responses
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Groq streaming API, SSE protocol, Koyeb proxy (X-Accel-Buffering: no)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ SSE connection established on message send
□ Tokens stream in batches of 5 (configurable)
□ Text appears word-by-word in chat
□ Block delimiters detected and blocks rendered atomically
□ 'done' event triggers post-stream actions
□ 'error' event shows user-friendly error with retry
□ Auto-scroll while streaming (pauses if user scrolls up)
□ Concurrent stream prevention (singleton)
□ Partial response preserved on stop/interrupt

VISUAL:
□ Text appears smoothly without jank
□ Blocks "pop in" at correct position
□ No layout shift during streaming (pre-allocated space not needed but no CLS)

PERFORMANCE:
□ First token appears within 1-3 seconds of message send
□ Token batching reduces event overhead (not per-character)
□ Auto-scroll maintains 60fps during streaming
□ X-Accel-Buffering: no header prevents proxy buffering

ACCESSIBILITY:
□ aria-live="polite" on message area
□ Streaming state announced
□ Stop button accessible
□ Completion announced

EDGE CASES:
□ New message interrupts current stream
□ SSE reconnection on drop (max 3 retries)
□ Malformed block delimiters flushed after timeout
□ Empty stream handled with error message
□ Very long responses (2000+ tokens) stable

VERIFICATION:
□ PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
□ BROWSER TEST: Verified
□ CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ all files (athena-stream.js, athena-engine.js, app.py SSE endpoint) 2. COMPARE against Done Criteria 3. TEST: send message and verify tokens stream, send during stream, network disconnect simulation, long response 4. CHECK: X-Accel-Buffering header, EventSource cleanup on close, memory leak from unclosed connections 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below
