════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #038: MARKDOWN RENDERING
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
BLOCKS:          #039 (Code Blocks), #064 (Equations), #136 (Footnotes)
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Markdown Rendering converts the raw Markdown text from Athena's LLM responses
into properly formatted HTML within the chat messages. Headers become styled
headings, lists become proper HTML lists, bold and italic text get emphasis,
links become clickable, and code blocks get syntax highlighting. This is the
rendering layer that transforms raw text into readable, structured content.

This feature exists because LLMs naturally output Markdown. Groq's output
includes ## headers, **bold**, *italic*, `inline code`, numbered lists, and
fenced code blocks. Without Markdown rendering, the user sees raw Markdown
syntax characters. With rendering, they see properly formatted academic content
that matches the quality of a research paper or documentation site.

The rendering must work DURING STREAMING. As tokens arrive, partially formed
Markdown must be handled gracefully. A heading "## Key F" should be rendered
as a heading-in-progress (styled but incomplete), not as plain text with "##"
visible. Bold markers "**imp" should buffer until the closing ** arrives. This
incremental rendering is the hard part of the implementation.

The Markdown library used is marked.js (lightweight, 35KB, streaming-compatible)
with DOMPurify for XSS sanitization. Every rendered Markdown output passes
through DOMPurify before insertion into the DOM. This prevents injection attacks
from LLM output that might contain script tags or event handlers.

What makes this unique to Athena: Markdown rendering integrates with the block
system. When the LLM outputs a block delimiter within Markdown (e.g., a Paper
Card block inside a bulleted list), the Markdown renderer pauses, inserts the
block, and resumes Markdown rendering after the block. This hybrid rendering
is specific to Athena's structured response format.

Typography follows the design system: headers use Inter 600 weight, body text
is Inter 400 at 14px, code uses JetBrains Mono 12px, and academic-style
indented blockquotes have a left border in var(--text-muted).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Automatic. Every Athena response is Markdown-rendered. No user activation needed.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena streams a response. Raw tokens arrive: "## Key Papers\n\nThe following papers are **critical** to this lineage:\n\n1. **ResNet** (2016) - deepest..."
2. The Markdown renderer processes incrementally:
   - "## Key Papers" renders as an h3 heading (not h2, we cap at h3 inside chat)
   - "**critical**" renders as bold text
   - "1. **ResNet**" renders as a numbered list item with bold
3. Inline code (`similarity_score`) renders in JetBrains Mono with a subtle
   background highlight.
4. Links render as clickable with appropriate styling (gold accent underline).
5. Tables render as formatted HTML tables with alternating row backgrounds.
6. After streaming completes, the full Markdown is re-rendered for consistency
   (in case incremental rendering missed any edge cases).

AFTER COMPLETION:
Fully rendered, formatted response visible. All Markdown elements properly styled.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-markdown.js: Markdown renderer wrapper, incremental rendering,
  DOMPurify integration, block-aware rendering.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Passes tokens through Markdown renderer.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Markdown element styles (headings, lists, code,
  tables, blockquotes, links).
- Third-party: marked.js (CDN or bundled), DOMPurify (CDN or bundled).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaMarkdown {
    constructor() {
      this.buffer = '';
      this.renderer = new marked.Renderer();
      // Cap headings at h3 inside chat messages
      this.renderer.heading = (text, level) =>
        `<h${Math.max(level + 1, 3)} class="athena-heading">${text}</h${Math.max(level + 1, 3)}>`;
      marked.setOptions({
        renderer: this.renderer,
        breaks: true,  // GFM line breaks
        gfm: true,     // GitHub Flavored Markdown
      });
    }
    renderIncremental(newText) {
      this.buffer += newText;
      // Only render complete blocks to avoid partial Markdown artifacts
      const safeHtml = DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(this.buffer));
      return safeHtml;
    }
    renderFinal(fullText) {
      const safeHtml = DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(fullText));
      return safeHtml;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. Markdown rendering is entirely frontend.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
/* Markdown styles within Athena messages */
.msg-assistant .athena-heading {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin: 12px 0 8px 0;
}
.msg-assistant h3.athena-heading { font-size: 15px; }
.msg-assistant h4.athena-heading { font-size: 14px; }
.msg-assistant code {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 12px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  padding: 2px 6px;
  border-radius: 3px;
}
.msg-assistant blockquote {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--text-muted);
  padding-left: 12px;
  margin: 8px 0;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
}
.msg-assistant table {
  border-collapse: collapse;
  width: 100%;
  font-size: 13px;
}
.msg-assistant th, .msg-assistant td {
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  padding: 6px 10px;
  text-align: left;
}
.msg-assistant tr:nth-child(even) {
  background: var(--bg-surface);
}
```

API CONTRACTS: NOT APPLICABLE (frontend only).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Rendered Markdown Response

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                           │
  │  Key Papers                               │
  │  ─────────                                │
  │                                           │
  │  The following papers are critical:       │
  │                                           │
  │  1. ResNet (2016) - deepest architecture  │
  │  2. VGGNet (2014) - depth validation      │
  │  3. AlexNet (2012) - CNN revival          │
  │                                           │
  │  > "The introduction of residual          │
  │  > connections fundamentally changed      │
  │  > the depth problem." - He et al.        │
  │                                           │
  │  The similarity_score between these       │
  │  papers exceeds 0.8 in all cases.         │
  │                                           │
  │  │ Paper  │ PageRank │ Impact │           │
  │  │ ResNet │ 0.043    │ 31%    │           │
  │  │ VGGNet │ 0.031    │ 18%    │           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Text renders incrementally during streaming. Final re-render on completion (no visible change if incremental was correct).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tokens from SSE stream
  → AthenaStream appends to buffer
  → AthenaMarkdown.renderIncremental() processes buffer
  → DOMPurify sanitizes HTML
  → innerHTML updated on message element
  → On stream complete: renderFinal() re-renders for consistency

ERROR PATH: Malformed Markdown → rendered as plain text (safe fallback)
  DOMPurify strips dangerous HTML → safe output guaranteed
DATA STORED: Raw Markdown stored in chat_history (not rendered HTML)
DATA CACHED: No caching needed

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. This feature renders LLM output, does not generate it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Link in Markdown     | Click       | Opens URL in new tab (target="_blank")       |
| Code block           | Hover       | Copy button appears in top-right             |
| Table                | Visible     | Horizontal scroll if wider than container    |
| Heading              | Visible     | Styled with Inter 600 weight                |
| Inline code          | Visible     | JetBrains Mono with subtle background       |

ANIMATION DETAILS: No animations for Markdown rendering (content appears as part of streaming)
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Semantic HTML (h3, ul, ol, blockquote, table, code), links with proper href, images with alt text (if any)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: LLM outputs raw HTML (script tags, onclick handlers)
   Expected: DOMPurify strips all dangerous HTML. Safe output guaranteed.
   Implementation: DOMPurify.sanitize() on all rendered output.

2. Scenario: Markdown table wider than chat panel
   Expected: Horizontal scroll within the table container.
   Implementation: overflow-x: auto on table wrapper div.

3. Scenario: Incremental render produces temporary artifact (e.g., unclosed bold)
   Expected: Minor visual flicker acceptable during streaming. Final re-render
   corrects any artifacts.
   Implementation: Re-render full buffer on each batch (not just append).

4. Scenario: Extremely nested Markdown (list inside blockquote inside list)
   Expected: Rendered correctly up to 3 levels of nesting. Beyond that, flat.
   Implementation: marked.js handles nesting natively.

5. Scenario: LLM outputs a very large table (20+ rows)
   Expected: Table renders fully. If > 10 rows, collapsed with "Show all" toggle.
   Implementation: Post-render check on table row count, collapse if needed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENABLES: #039 (Code Blocks), #064 (Equations), #136 (Footnotes)
ENHANCES: Every feature that displays LLM text output
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: marked.js, DOMPurify (CDN or bundled)

□ Ordered and unordered lists rendered
□ Code blocks with syntax highlighting

□ Tables with borders and alternating rows
□ Blockquotes with left border
□ Links clickable (target="_blank")
□ DOMPurify sanitizes all output (XSS prevention)

□ Final re-render on stream complete

VISUAL: □ Typography matches design system, code in JetBrains Mono
PERFORMANCE: □ Render does not jank during streaming

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #039: CODE BLOCKS WITH COPY
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 6
OVERLAPS WITH:   #101 (Python Code Block), #103 (JSON Data Block)
BLOCKS:          #101, #103, #102, #104
BLOCKED BY:      #038 (Markdown Rendering), #037 (Streaming)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Code Blocks with Copy renders fenced code blocks from Athena's responses with
syntax highlighting, a language label, and a one-click copy button. When Athena
provides code examples (Python scripts for graph analysis, SQL queries for
custom data extraction, API endpoint documentation, or BibTeX entries), they
render as distinct, visually separated blocks with proper formatting.

This feature exists because researchers frequently need to use code or data
formats that Athena generates. A BibTeX citation needs to be copied into a
.bib file. A Python snippet for analyzing graph data needs to be pasted into
a notebook. A SQL query for custom analysis needs to go into a query tool.
The copy button eliminates the friction of manual text selection in rendered
code blocks (which is notoriously unreliable with line numbers and decorations).

Code blocks use a dark background (var(--bg-primary) = #0a0e17) to visually
distinguish them from prose. This inverted color scheme for code blocks is
standard in documentation tools and creates a clear boundary between natural
language and formal language. The font is JetBrains Mono at 13px with
1.5 line height. Syntax highlighting uses a subset of Prism.js or highlight.js
colors appropriate for the dot matrix aesthetic: gold for keywords, teal for
strings, blue for functions, muted for comments.

The copy button appears on hover in the top-right corner of the code block.
Clicking it copies the raw code (without line numbers or decorations) to the
clipboard. A brief toast confirms: "Copied to clipboard." The button icon
transitions from a copy icon (dot matrix squares) to a checkmark for 2 seconds
after copying.

Language detection: If the LLM specifies a language in the fenced block
(```python, ```bibtex, ```sql), that language is used. If no language is
specified, the system attempts auto-detection based on content heuristics.
The detected language appears as a small label in the top-left corner.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Automatic when Athena's response contains a fenced code block (```...```).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena streams a response that includes a code block. When the opening
   ``` delimiter is detected, the Markdown renderer creates a code block element.
2. Code content streams into the block with syntax highlighting applied.
3. Once complete, the block shows: language label (top-left), copy button
   (top-right, visible on hover), and properly highlighted code.
4. User hovers over the block. Copy button appears with a subtle fade-in.
5. User clicks copy. Button icon changes to checkmark. Toast: "Copied to clipboard."
   The raw code (without HTML decorations) is in the clipboard.
6. User pastes into their IDE/notebook. Code is clean and properly formatted.

AFTER COMPLETION: Code block remains in chat. Copy button always available.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-markdown.js: Code block rendering with syntax highlighting.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: CodeBlock class with copy button and language label.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Code block styles, syntax highlighting colors.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class CodeBlock {
    render(code, language) {
      const highlighted = highlightCode(code, language);
      return `
        <div class="block block-code" data-language="${language}">
          <div class="code-header">
            <span class="code-language">${language}</span>
            <button class="code-copy" onclick="copyCode(this)">
              <span class="copy-icon">◫</span>
            </button>
          </div>
          <pre><code class="language-${language}">${highlighted}</code></pre>
        </div>`;
    }
  }
  function copyCode(btn) {
    const code = btn.closest('.block-code').querySelector('code').textContent;
    navigator.clipboard.writeText(code);
    btn.querySelector('.copy-icon').textContent = '✓';
    setTimeout(() => btn.querySelector('.copy-icon').textContent = '◫', 2000);
    showToast('Copied to clipboard');
  }

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.block-code {
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  border-radius: 6px;
  margin: 8px 0;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.code-header {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  padding: 6px 12px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05);
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
}
.block-code pre {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 12px;
  overflow-x: auto;
}
.block-code code {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 13px;
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: var(--text-primary);
}
/* Syntax colors (dot matrix palette) */
.token.keyword { color: var(--accent-gold); }
.token.string { color: var(--accent-teal); }
.token.function { color: var(--accent-blue); }
.token.comment { color: var(--text-muted); font-style: italic; }
```

API CONTRACTS: NOT APPLICABLE (frontend only).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Code Block Rendered

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ python ──────────────────── [◫] ──┐   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  import networkx as nx              │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  │  G = nx.from_dict_of_lists(         │   │
  │  │      graph_data['adjacency']        │   │
  │  │  )                                  │   │
  │  │  pr = nx.pagerank(G, alpha=0.85)    │   │
  │  │  top = sorted(pr.items(),           │   │
  │  │      key=lambda x: x[1],           │   │
  │  │      reverse=True)[:5]              │   │
  │  │                                     │   │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: After Copy

  │  ┌─ python ──────────────────── [✓] ──┐   │
  │  │  (same code, copy icon changed)     │   │

TRANSITIONS: Copy icon → checkmark 100ms, reverts after 2s. Hover: copy button fadeIn 150ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM outputs fenced code block → Markdown renderer detects ``` delimiter
  → Creates CodeBlock element with syntax highlighting
  → Language detected from delimiter or auto-detected
  → Copy button added to header
  → User clicks copy → navigator.clipboard.writeText(raw code)
  → Toast notification → icon change for 2s

ERROR PATH: Clipboard API unavailable (HTTP) → fallback to textarea selection method
DATA STORED: None (copy is ephemeral)
DATA CACHED: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. This feature renders LLM output, does not generate it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Code block           | Hover       | Copy button fades in                        |
| Copy button (◫)      | Click       | Copies code to clipboard, icon → ✓          |
| Language label       | Visible     | Shows detected language                     |
| Code text            | Select      | Standard text selection (for partial copy)  |
| Long code            | Scroll      | Horizontal scroll for wide lines            |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Copy button 150ms fadeIn on hover. Icon change instant. Toast 200ms fadeIn, 2s hold, 200ms fadeOut.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Code block as pre/code with language attribute, copy button with aria-label="Copy code to clipboard", toast as aria-live

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Code block without language specifier (just ```)
   Expected: Auto-detect or show "text" as language. No highlighting.
   Implementation: Language detection heuristic, fallback to plain text.

2. Scenario: Code block during streaming (incomplete)
   Expected: Block renders progressively. Copy button appears only after block
   is complete (closing ``` received).
   Implementation: Buffer code block content until closing delimiter.

3. Scenario: Very long code block (50+ lines)
   Expected: Block has max-height with internal scroll. Expand button: "Show all."
   Implementation: max-height: 300px, overflow-y: auto, expand toggle.

4. Scenario: Clipboard API blocked (non-HTTPS context)
   Expected: Fallback to creating a hidden textarea, selecting, and document.execCommand('copy').
   Implementation: Try navigator.clipboard first, fallback to textarea method.

5. Scenario: Code contains HTML-like tags (e.g., JSX)
   Expected: Tags rendered as text, not interpreted as HTML. Entity encoding.
   Implementation: DOMPurify handles this, or pre-encode < > characters.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #038 (Markdown Rendering), #037 (Streaming)
ENABLES: #101 (Python Code Block), #102 (SQL), #103 (JSON), #104 (API Endpoint)
ENHANCES: All features that output code or data formats
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Syntax highlighting library (Prism.js or highlight.js)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ Fenced code blocks render with syntax highlighting
□ Language label displayed (from delimiter or auto-detect)
□ Copy button copies raw code to clipboard
□ Copy icon transitions to checkmark for 2s
□ Toast confirmation on copy
□ Code blocks visually distinct (dark background)
□ Horizontal scroll for wide lines

VISUAL: □ JetBrains Mono font, dot-matrix-palette syntax colors
PERFORMANCE: □ Copy completes in < 50ms
ACCESSIBILITY: □ pre/code semantic elements, copy button labeled
EDGE CASES: □ No language handled, streaming blocks buffered, long blocks scrollable

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #040: USER MESSAGE EDITING
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Message Actions
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   NONE
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming), #002 (Rich Formatting)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User Message Editing allows users to modify a previously sent message and
regenerate Athena's response from that point. This is the "edit and retry"
pattern used by ChatGPT and Claude. When a user realizes they asked the wrong
question or wants to refine their query, they can click the edit icon on their
message, modify the text, and resubmit. Athena regenerates a new response
for the edited message, and all subsequent messages in the conversation are
removed (the conversation forks from the edit point).

This feature exists because researchers iterate on their questions. A first
attempt like "tell me about the papers" might produce an overly broad response.
The user edits to "tell me about the top 3 papers by structural impact" and
gets a more focused answer. Without edit, they would need to send a new message
that says "I meant the top 3 by structural impact," which pollutes the
conversation with correction messages.

The edit flow: user hovers over their message, clicks the edit icon (pencil/dot
matrix icon). The message transforms into an editable textarea pre-filled with
the original text. The user modifies and presses Enter (or clicks "Resubmit").
Athena's response to the original message is removed (with a subtle slide-out
animation). All messages AFTER the edited message are also removed (the
conversation forks). A new response is generated for the edited message.

Conversation memory (#006) is updated: the edited message replaces the original
in chat_history. Messages after the edit point are marked as superseded (not
deleted, for audit purposes). This means the conversation is always coherent
from the edit point forward.

What makes this unique to Athena: when editing a message that referenced a
specific paper or graph state, the graph context is recaptured at the time of
re-generation. If the user originally asked about "the top paper" when ResNet
was highlighted but now edits to "compare the top 2 papers," Athena uses the
current graph state for the new response, not the state at the time of the
original message.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User hovers over their own message and clicks the edit icon (pencil). Only user
messages are editable. Athena messages are not directly editable (use Challenge
#023 instead).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User hovers over their message "tell me about the papers." An action bar
   appears: [Edit] [Copy].
2. User clicks Edit. The message transforms into a textarea with the original
   text, a "Resubmit" button, and a "Cancel" link.
3. User changes text to "tell me about the top 3 papers by structural impact."
4. User clicks Resubmit (or Ctrl+Enter). The textarea converts back to a message
   bubble with the new text. Athena's previous response slides out (300ms).
   All subsequent messages also slide out.
5. A new streaming response begins for the edited message. Quick actions appear
   after completion.
6. If user clicks Cancel, the textarea reverts to the original message. Nothing
   is modified.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Conversation forks from the edit point. Original response gone (but preserved in
DB with superseded flag). New response is the current state.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Edit action handler, conversation fork logic,
  re-generation trigger.
- static/js/athena-memory.js: Message editing in memory (supersede pattern).
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Edit mode textarea styles, slide-out animation.
- app.py: PUT /api/athena/history/<message_id> for editing stored messages.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/history/<message_id>', methods=['PUT'])
  def edit_message(message_id):
      new_content = request.json['content']
      session_id = get_session_id()
      # Mark original as superseded
      db.execute("UPDATE chat_history SET metadata = metadata || '{\"superseded\": true}' WHERE id=%s AND session_id=%s", (message_id, session_id))
      # Mark all subsequent messages as superseded
      db.execute("UPDATE chat_history SET metadata = metadata || '{\"superseded\": true}' WHERE session_id=%s AND created_at > (SELECT created_at FROM chat_history WHERE id=%s)", (session_id, message_id))
      # Insert edited message
      db.execute("INSERT INTO chat_history (session_id, role, content, metadata) VALUES (%s, 'user', %s, %s)",
                  (session_id, new_content, json.dumps({'edited_from': message_id})))
      return jsonify({'success': True})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
function editMessage(messageEl) {
  const originalText = messageEl.dataset.content;
  const msgContent = messageEl.querySelector('.msg-content');
  msgContent.innerHTML = `
    <textarea class="edit-textarea">${originalText}</textarea>
    <div class="edit-actions">
      <button class="btn-resubmit" onclick="resubmitEdit(this)">Resubmit</button>
      <a class="btn-cancel" onclick="cancelEdit(this)">Cancel</a>
    </div>`;
  msgContent.querySelector('.edit-textarea').focus();
}

async function resubmitEdit(btn) {
  const messageEl = btn.closest('.msg');
  const newText = messageEl.querySelector('.edit-textarea').value;
  // Remove all messages after this one
  let next = messageEl.nextElementSibling;
  while (next) {
    const toRemove = next;
    next = next.nextElementSibling;
    toRemove.classList.add('slide-out');
    setTimeout(() => toRemove.remove(), 300);
  }
  // Update message content
  messageEl.querySelector('.msg-content').textContent = newText;
  messageEl.dataset.content = newText;
  // Update server
  await fetch(`/api/athena/history/${messageEl.dataset.id}`, {
    method: 'PUT', body: JSON.stringify({ content: newText })
  });
  // Generate new response
  athenaEngine.sendMessage(newText);
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
PUT /api/athena/history/<message_id>
Request: { "content": "edited message text" }
Response: { "success": true }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Edit Mode Active

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ You (editing) ────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
  │  │ │ tell me about the top 3 papers  │ │  │
  │  │ │ by structural impact█           │ │  │
  │  │ └─────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
  │  │        [Cancel]  [Resubmit]         │  │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                           │
  │  (Previous Athena response sliding out)   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Message → textarea 200ms. Subsequent messages slide out 300ms. New response streams in normally.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks Edit → textarea opens with original text
  → User modifies text → clicks Resubmit
  → PUT /api/athena/history/<id> marks original + subsequent as superseded
  → DOM: subsequent messages slide out and removed
  → Message bubble updates with new text
  → New SSE stream initiated for edited message
  → New response generated and streamed
  → Memory updated with fork point

ERROR PATH: Edit fails (DB error) → "Could not save edit. Your changes are preserved locally."
DATA STORED: Original marked superseded (not deleted), edited message as new entry
DATA CACHED: Memory cache updated to reflect fork

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE directly. Editing triggers a new streaming response (same as any new message). No special LLM integration for the edit itself.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Edit icon (hover)    | Click       | Transforms message into editable textarea   |
| Textarea             | Type        | Edit message content                        |
| Resubmit button      | Click       | Saves edit, removes subsequent, regenerates |
| Cancel link          | Click       | Reverts to original message, no changes     |
| Ctrl+Enter in edit   | Keypress    | Same as Resubmit click                     |
| Escape in edit       | Keypress    | Same as Cancel                              |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Textarea expansion 200ms. Subsequent messages slide left and fade (300ms). New response streams normally.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Edit button aria-label="Edit message", textarea aria-label="Edit your message", resubmit as primary action

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User tries to edit while Athena is streaming
   Expected: Edit disabled during streaming. Hover shows "Wait for response to complete."
   Implementation: Check isStreaming flag, disable edit icon during stream.

2. Scenario: User edits the very first message in the conversation
   Expected: Only Athena's first response is removed. No "subsequent" messages issue.
   Implementation: Fork logic starts from the first message correctly.

3. Scenario: User edits a message that triggered a Pathfinder response (#008)
   Expected: Pathfinder response also removed. Re-generation may or may not
   trigger Pathfinder depending on the edited text.
   Implementation: All subsequent messages removed regardless of type.

4. Scenario: User submits an empty edit (clears the textarea)
   Expected: "Message cannot be empty." Edit not submitted.
   Implementation: Validate non-empty before resubmit.

5. Scenario: Edit the same message twice
   Expected: Both edits create new superseded chains. The conversation always
   reflects the latest edit.
   Implementation: Each edit creates a new supersede entry.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #006 (Memory), #037 (Streaming), #002 (Rich Formatting)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: Conversation quality (fewer correction messages)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: chat_history table (needs id column on messages for editing)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ Edit icon on user messages (hover)
□ Click transforms to textarea with original text
□ Resubmit saves edit, removes subsequent messages, regenerates
□ Cancel reverts to original without changes
□ Ctrl+Enter and Escape keyboard shortcuts
□ Edited messages marked in DB (superseded chain)
□ Empty edits rejected

VISUAL: □ Textarea styled to match input area, slide-out animation on removed messages
PERFORMANCE: □ Edit transition < 200ms
ACCESSIBILITY: □ Edit button labeled, textarea labeled, keyboard shortcuts documented
EDGE CASES: □ Disabled during streaming, first message handled, empty edits blocked

VERIFICATION: □ PESSIMISTIC AGENT complete □ BROWSER TEST verified □ CROSS-REFERENCE matches

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ files 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST edit flow end-to-end 4. CHECK DB state after edit 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FEATURE #041: STOP GENERATION BUTTON
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 4
OVERLAPS WITH:   NONE
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Stop Generation Button allows users to halt Athena's response mid-stream.
This is one of the most critical UX features in any chat interface because it
gives the user immediate control over a process that otherwise runs to completion
regardless of relevance. When Athena starts generating a response that goes in
the wrong direction, the user can stop it instantly rather than waiting 5-10
seconds for a useless response to finish.

This feature exists because LLM responses are not always on target. The user
might realize from the first few sentences that Athena misunderstood their
question. Or the response might be too detailed when they wanted a brief answer.
Or they might want to rephrase and try again. Without a stop button, they must
wait for the full response, wasting time and tokens.

The button appears in the input area, replacing the send button during streaming.
It is a square icon (■) that replaces the send arrow. Clicking it closes the
EventSource connection, appends "[Generation stopped]" to the partial response,
and re-enables the input area for a new message. The partial response is
preserved in the chat (not deleted) so the user can see what was generated
before they stopped.

The implementation is straightforward: the AthenaStream.stop() method calls
EventSource.close() and triggers the completion flow (store partial response in
memory, show quick actions, restore input area). The backend does not need to
know that the client disconnected: the Groq streaming API will stop automatically
when the SSE connection closes (no wasted API tokens after disconnection).

What makes this unique to Athena: the stop button is context-aware. After
stopping, Athena offers intelligent follow-up options based on what was
generated before the stop: "I stopped that response. Would you like me to:
[Be more concise] [Focus on a different aspect] [Try a different approach]?"
These options use the partial response context to suggest useful next steps.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks the stop button (■) that appears during streaming, or presses
Escape key while streaming is active.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User sends a message. Streaming begins. The send button transforms into a
   square stop button (■) with a 200ms morph animation.
2. Text streams into the response. User reads the first few sentences and
   realizes the response is not what they wanted.
3. User clicks ■ (or presses Escape). The streaming stops immediately (within
   50ms). The text "...  [Generation stopped]" is appended to the partial
   response in a muted style.
4. The stop button transforms back into the send button (200ms morph). The
   input area is re-enabled (was disabled during streaming by default).
5. Below the partial response, quick actions appear: "Be more concise," "Focus
   on [different topic]," "Try again."
6. User types a new message or clicks a quick action.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Partial response visible in chat with "[Generation stopped]" marker. Input
area active. User can send new message.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-stream.js: stop() method closes EventSource.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Stop button state management, Escape key handler.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Stop button styles, morph animation.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-engine.js
  function onStreamStart() {
    document.querySelector('.btn-send').classList.add('is-stop');
    document.querySelector('.btn-send').onclick = stopGeneration;
    document.addEventListener('keydown', escapeHandler);
  }

  function stopGeneration() {
    athenaStream.stop();
    document.querySelector('.btn-send').classList.remove('is-stop');
    document.querySelector('.btn-send').onclick = sendMessage;
    document.removeEventListener('keydown', escapeHandler);
  }

  function escapeHandler(e) {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') stopGeneration();
  }

  // In athena-stream.js
  stop() {
    if (this.eventSource) {
      this.eventSource.close();
      this.appendText('\n\n[Generation stopped]');
      this.onComplete({ stopped: true });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. The backend simply stops generating when the SSE connection
closes. No explicit stop endpoint needed.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.btn-send {
  width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--accent-gold); border: none; cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-radius 200ms ease-out;
}
.btn-send.is-stop {
  border-radius: 4px; /* Square during stop mode */
  background: var(--danger);
}
.btn-send .icon-send { /* Arrow icon */ }
.btn-send.is-stop .icon-send { display: none; }
.btn-send.is-stop::after {
  content: '■'; font-size: 14px; color: white;
}
```

API CONTRACTS: No API needed. Client-side EventSource.close() suffices.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Streaming (Stop Button Visible)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena: The key papers in this lineage   │
  │  include several foundational works█      │
  │                                           │
  │ ─────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                  [■] │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: After Stop

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena: The key papers in this lineage   │
  │  include several foundational works       │
  │                                           │
  │  [Generation stopped]                     │
  │                                           │
  │  ● Be more concise                       │
  │  ● Focus on structural impact             │
  │  ● Try again                              │
  │                                           │
  │ ─────────────────────────────────────────│
  │ [Type a message...]                  [→] │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Send → Stop: border-radius 50% to 4px over 200ms, color gold to red
Stop → Send: border-radius 4px to 50% over 200ms, color red to gold
"[Generation stopped]": text-secondary color, italic

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks ■ or presses Escape
  → athenaStream.stop() called
  → EventSource.close() terminates SSE connection
  → "[Generation stopped]" appended to partial response
  → onComplete({ stopped: true }) triggered
  → Partial response stored in chat_history with stopped=true metadata
  → Quick actions shown based on partial content
  → Send button restored

ERROR PATH: EventSource already closed → stop() is a no-op (safe to call multiple times)
DATA STORED: Partial response in chat_history with metadata.stopped=true
DATA CACHED: Partial response in memory cache

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. This feature stops LLM generation, does not use it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Stop button (■)      | Click       | Stops streaming immediately                 |
| Escape key           | Keydown     | Same as stop button click                   |
| Quick actions (post) | Click       | Sends new message based on partial context  |
| Send button (after)  | Click       | Normal message send (streaming stopped)     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Send→Stop morph 200ms (border-radius + color). Stop text italic, muted.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Stop button aria-label="Stop generating response", Escape key documented, state change announced

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User clicks stop immediately after sending (before any tokens arrive)
   Expected: Empty partial response. "I stopped before generating anything. Try again?"
   Implementation: Check if any tokens were appended.

2. Scenario: User clicks stop, then clicks stop again (double-click)
   Expected: Second click is a no-op. No error.
   Implementation: stop() checks if EventSource exists and is open.

3. Scenario: Stream naturally completes at the exact moment user clicks stop
   Expected: "done" event may fire simultaneously. No error. Response treated
   as complete (not stopped).
   Implementation: Check eventSource.readyState before stop actions.

4. Scenario: Stop during a block render (block buffer incomplete)
   Expected: Partial block buffer flushed as plain text. No broken HTML.
   Implementation: On stop, flush blockBuffer as text if isBufferingBlock.

5. Scenario: Accessibility: screen reader user cannot see visual stop button
   Expected: Escape key always works. aria-live announces "Generating response,
   press Escape to stop."
   Implementation: Announce streaming state and escape option.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE (but used by every feature that streams responses)
ENHANCES: User control over all streaming features
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: EventSource API

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
□ Stop button appears during streaming (replaces send button)
□ Click stops streaming immediately (< 50ms)
□ Escape key stops streaming
□ "[Generation stopped]" appended to partial response
□ Partial response preserved in chat
□ Send button restored after stop
□ Quick actions appear after stop
□ Double-click safe (no errors)

VISUAL:
□ Send → Stop morph animation (circle to square, gold to red)
□ Stop → Send morph on completion
□ "[Generation stopped]" in muted italic style

PERFORMANCE: □ Stop takes effect within 50ms of click
ACCESSIBILITY: □ Stop button labeled, Escape documented, state announced
EDGE CASES: □ Stop before first token, stop during block buffer, double-click safe

VERIFICATION:
□ PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
□ BROWSER TEST: Verified
□ CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-stream.js, athena-engine.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST stop at various points during stream 4. CHECK memory state after stop 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #042 - SUGGESTED FOLLOW-UPS                                       ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       042
Feature Name:     Suggested Follow-Ups
Category:         Proactive AI
Priority:         P1
Phase:            B
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 12 hours
Dependencies:     #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After every AI response in the Athena chat panel, the system generates a set of
contextually relevant follow-up questions that the researcher can click to continue
the conversation. These suggestions are not random or generic. They are derived
from the current graph state, the response content, and the Arivu-specific data
the researcher is exploring - PageRank centrality scores, mutation type distributions,
pruning impact percentages, edge_analysis confidence tiers, and cluster membership.

The follow-up suggestions appear as a row of clickable pill-shaped buttons below the
AI response. Each pill contains a concise question or action prompt. When the
researcher clicks one, it is injected into the input area as if they typed it,
and the send action fires automatically. This creates a guided exploration flow
where the researcher can drill into the intellectual ancestry graph without
formulating every query manually.

The generation of follow-ups happens in two modes. The primary mode extracts
candidates from the LLM response itself, using a structured output format where
the model is instructed to append a JSON block of suggested follow-ups. The
fallback mode uses heuristic rules based on graph topology - if the response
mentions a high-PageRank node, suggest "What happens if we prune [paper]?" If
the response discusses a mutation type like hybridization, suggest "Show me all
hybridization edges in this graph." If the response references a low-confidence
edge, suggest "What evidence supports this classification?"

The system caps suggestions at 3 per response. Suggestions are deduplicated
against the current conversation history so the researcher never sees a question
they already asked. The pills use the dot matrix aesthetic with a subtle gold
border and muted text, matching the Arivu visual language. On hover, the pill
elevates slightly and the border brightens. On click, the pill pulses once
before the text transfers to the input area.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: An AI response finishes streaming in the chat panel.

STEP 1: The streaming completes and the response content finalizes. After a
200ms delay (to let the researcher's eye settle), three pill-shaped suggestion
buttons fade in below the response, left-aligned.

STEP 2: The researcher reads the response about how "Attention Is All You Need"
generalized the sequence-to-sequence paradigm. Below the response they see:
  [What papers adopted this generalization?]
  [Show pruning impact for Vaswani 2017]
  [Which edges have LOW confidence here?]

STEP 3: The researcher clicks "Show pruning impact for Vaswani 2017". The pill
pulses with a gold flash, then the text appears in the input area.

STEP 4: The message sends automatically. The previous suggestions fade out.
The new response streams in.

STEP 5: New suggestions appear after the new response completes, reflecting
the updated conversational context. The researcher continues this guided
exploration pattern, clicking through suggestions to build understanding of
the graph's intellectual structure.

STEP 6: If the researcher types their own question instead of clicking a
suggestion, the existing suggestions fade out gracefully. No conflict between
typed input and suggested input.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-suggestions.js    Suggestion pill renderer and click handler
    static/css/athena-suggestions.css  Pill styling, animations, hover states

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-engine.js         Parse structured follow-ups from LLM output
    static/js/athena-stream.js         Trigger suggestion generation after stream end
    static/js/athena-chat.js           Inject clicked suggestion into input + send
    backend/chat_guide.py              Add follow-up generation to system prompt
    backend/prompt_sanitizer.py        Sanitize generated suggestions before display

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The suggestion system lives in athena-suggestions.js as a SuggestionManager
  class. It listens for the "athena:stream:complete" custom event dispatched
  by the streaming module. On receipt, it extracts the structured follow-up
  block from the response (a JSON array appended by the LLM), validates each
  suggestion string, deduplicates against conversation history, and renders
  up to 3 pills in a flex container below the response bubble.

  Each pill is a <button> element with class "athena-suggestion-pill". The
  click handler calls athena-chat.js setInput(text) followed by send(). The
  pills container is absolutely positioned relative to the response bubble
  so it scrolls naturally with chat history.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The LLM system prompt in chat_guide.py is augmented with an instruction block
  telling the model to append a structured JSON block to every response:

    [FOLLOW_UPS]
    ["question one", "question two", "question three"]
    [/FOLLOW_UPS]

  The engine.js parser strips this block from the displayed response and passes
  it to SuggestionManager. If the LLM fails to produce the block (or the parse
  fails), the fallback heuristic engine kicks in, scanning the response for
  Arivu-specific patterns:

    - Paper titles with high PageRank -> "What is the pruning impact of [title]?"
    - Mutation type mentions -> "Show all [type] edges in this lineage"
    - Confidence tier mentions -> "What evidence supports the [tier] edges?"
    - Cluster references -> "Explore the [cluster] research thread"
    - Orphan idea mentions -> "Why did [concept] become an orphan idea?"

CODE PATTERNS:

  class SuggestionManager {
    constructor(chatInterface, conversationHistory) { ... }
    onStreamComplete(responseText, graphContext) { ... }
    parseStructuredSuggestions(text) { ... }
    generateHeuristicSuggestions(text, graphContext) { ... }
    deduplicateAgainstHistory(suggestions) { ... }
    renderPills(suggestions, responseElement) { ... }
    onPillClick(text) { ... }
    clearSuggestions() { ... }
  }

API:

  No new API endpoints. Suggestions are generated client-side from LLM output
  and graph context already available in the frontend. The LLM prompt modification
  happens server-side in chat_guide.py but requires no new routes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SUGGESTION PILLS BELOW RESPONSE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  AI Response                                                        │
  │                                                                     │
  │  The Vaswani 2017 paper has a pruning impact of 31.6%, meaning     │
  │  removing it would collapse 47 dependent papers in this lineage.   │
  │  The primary mutation type from this paper is "generalization" -    │
  │  subsequent work took the attention mechanism and applied it to     │
  │  domains beyond machine translation...                              │
  │                                                                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
    │ ○ What papers adopted this  │  │ ○ Show all generalization    │
    │   generalization?           │  │   edges in this lineage      │
    └─────────────────────────────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
    ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
    │ ○ Which edges have LOW          │
    │   confidence here?              │
    └─────────────────────────────────┘

PILL STATES:

  Default:    border: 1px solid var(--text-muted)
              background: transparent
              color: var(--text-secondary)
              ○ prefix icon (open dot)

  Hover:      border: 1px solid var(--accent-gold)
              background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.08)
              transform: translateY(-1px)
              ● prefix icon (filled dot)

  Click:      gold pulse animation (150ms)
              pill fades out, text transfers to input

  Container:  display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px;
              margin-top: 8px; padding: 4px 0;

ANIMATION SEQUENCE (after stream complete):

  t=0ms       Stream ends
  t=200ms     Pills container opacity 0 -> 1 (200ms ease-in)
  t=200ms     Each pill staggers in: pill1 at t=200, pill2 at t=280, pill3 at t=360

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  LLM Response (with [FOLLOW_UPS] block)
       |
       v
  athena-engine.js: parseResponse()
       |
       ├── Display text (without [FOLLOW_UPS] block)
       |
       └── Extracted suggestions JSON array
              |
              v
       SuggestionManager.onStreamComplete()
              |
              ├── parseStructuredSuggestions() -- primary path
              |        |
              |        └── (on parse failure) --> generateHeuristicSuggestions()
              |
              v
       deduplicateAgainstHistory()
              |
              v
       renderPills() -- max 3 pills
              |
              v
       User clicks pill
              |
              v
       onPillClick() -> chatInterface.setInput(text) -> chatInterface.send()
              |
              v
       clearSuggestions() -- old pills fade out
              |
              v
       New stream begins -> cycle repeats

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: Groq (via existing chat_guide.py pipeline)
MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
  "After your response, append exactly 3 follow-up questions the researcher
  might want to ask next, formatted as:
  [FOLLOW_UPS]
  ["question 1", "question 2", "question 3"]
  [/FOLLOW_UPS]
  Make questions specific to the graph data discussed. Reference paper titles,
  mutation types, confidence tiers, or pruning impacts when relevant."

INPUT: The existing chat context (conversation history + graph summary)
OUTPUT: Response text + structured [FOLLOW_UPS] block
FALLBACK: If LLM omits the block, heuristic suggestions from response text
CACHE: Follow-ups are ephemeral, not cached. Each response generates fresh ones.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Stream complete        Generate + show pills            Staggered fade-in
 Pill hover             Highlight pill                   Gold border, elevation
 Pill click             Inject text + auto-send          Gold pulse, fade out
 User types own input   Dismiss existing suggestions     Suggestions fade out
 New stream starts      Clear previous suggestions       Instant removal
 LLM parse failure      Fallback to heuristic pills      Same visual, different source
 No suggestions found   Show nothing                     No empty container
 Pill text too long     Truncate at 80 chars + "..."     Ellipsis, full on hover tooltip

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: LLM returns malformed JSON in the [FOLLOW_UPS] block
   Expected: Fallback to heuristic suggestion generation. No error visible.
   Implementation: try/catch around JSON.parse, log warning, call heuristic path.

2. Scenario: All 3 LLM suggestions duplicate questions already asked
   Expected: Show 0 pills. No empty container visible.
   Implementation: deduplicateAgainstHistory filters all, renderPills checks length.

3. Scenario: LLM returns suggestions that contain prompt injection attempts
   Expected: Suggestions sanitized. HTML stripped. Script tags removed.
   Implementation: Each suggestion string passes through DOMPurify + length cap.

4. Scenario: User clicks pill at exact moment a new stream starts
   Expected: Pill click wins. The clicked suggestion becomes the next message.
   Implementation: Pill click handler checks if input is already sending.

5. Scenario: Response has no Arivu-specific content (generic greeting)
   Expected: Generic follow-ups like "What paper should I analyze?" or
   "Tell me about a paper in my graph."
   Implementation: Heuristic engine has a generic fallback set for non-graph responses.

6. Scenario: Graph context is empty (no graph built yet)
   Expected: Suggestions focus on getting started: "Search for a paper to analyze"
   Implementation: SuggestionManager detects empty graphContext and uses starter set.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: Enhanced guided exploration workflows
ENHANCES: #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (uses existing Groq LLM pipeline)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Up to 3 suggestion pills appear after each AI response
[ ] Clicking a pill injects text into input and sends automatically
[ ] Suggestions are deduplicated against conversation history
[ ] LLM structured output parsed correctly
[ ] Heuristic fallback generates Arivu-specific suggestions
[ ] Suggestions cleared when user types own message
[ ] Suggestions cleared when new stream begins

VISUAL:
[ ] Pills use dot matrix aesthetic (open dot prefix, gold accent)
[ ] Staggered fade-in animation (200ms base + 80ms per pill)
[ ] Hover state with gold border and slight elevation
[ ] Click pulse animation before fade-out
[ ] No empty container when 0 suggestions

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Suggestion generation completes within 100ms of stream end
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Pills are focusable buttons with descriptive aria-labels
EDGE CASES: [ ] Malformed JSON handled, injection sanitized, empty graph handled

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches Arivu chat_guide.py integration

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-suggestions.js, athena-engine.js, chat_guide.py system prompt
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Click each pill, verify injection + auto-send
4. TEST: Force LLM parse failure, verify heuristic fallback
5. TEST: Repeat same question, verify deduplication
6. CHECK: No empty containers, no broken pills on resize
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
