================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #049 - MODEL/MODE SELECTOR                                        ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       049
Feature Name:     Model/Mode Selector
Category:         Chat Controls
Priority:         P1
Phase:            B
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 10 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Model/Mode Selector allows the researcher to switch between different AI
analysis modes in the Athena chat panel. These modes are not different LLM
models exposed directly (the researcher never sees "llama-3.1-8b" vs "llama-
3.3-70b" as a choice). Instead, they are curated analysis personas that change
how the AI interprets questions and formulates responses about the citation
ancestry graph.

Arivu defines three core analysis modes that map to the existing persona system
(sessions.persona column) and the Groq model selection:

  1. EXPLORER mode (default) - Uses GROQ_FAST_MODEL. Responds quickly with
     concise overviews. Best for browsing the graph, getting quick summaries,
     asking about individual papers. The AI acts as a knowledgeable guide who
     gives direct answers about PageRank scores, mutation types, and graph
     topology.

  2. ANALYST mode - Uses GROQ_SMART_MODEL. Responds with deeper, more nuanced
     analysis. Best for understanding complex relationships, comparing multiple
     papers, tracing intellectual lineages across generations. The AI produces
     longer responses with evidence from edge_analysis data, confidence tiers,
     and structural patterns.

  3. STORYTELLER mode - Uses GROQ_SMART_MODEL with a different system prompt
     focused on narrative. Best for understanding the "story" of a research
     field - how ideas evolved, why certain paradigms won, what the turning
     points were. The AI produces engaging narrative-style responses that
     connect the graph data to the broader arc of scientific progress.

The mode selector appears as a segmented control in the chat panel header,
showing the three mode names. The active mode is indicated by a filled dot
(●) and gold underline. Inactive modes show open dots (○). Switching modes
does not clear the conversation history - the AI retains the same context
but changes its response style and depth.

Each mode has a distinct visual accent. Explorer uses the default gold accent.
Analyst uses the blue accent (var(--accent-blue)). Storyteller uses the teal
accent (var(--accent-teal)). When a mode is active, the chat panel header
border-bottom color subtly shifts to match the mode accent, providing a
persistent visual cue about the current analysis depth.

The mode selection is stored in the sessions table (persona column) and
persists across page reloads. It is also sent with each /api/chat request
so the backend can select the appropriate Groq model and system prompt.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher wants to change the AI's analysis depth and style.

STEP 1: The researcher is in EXPLORER mode (default). They ask "What are the
most important papers in this graph?" and receive a concise list of top 5
papers by PageRank with brief descriptions.

STEP 2: The researcher wants deeper analysis. They click "ANALYST" in the
mode selector. The selector animates: the gold underline slides from Explorer
to Analyst, the header border-bottom shifts to blue.

STEP 3: The researcher asks the same question: "What are the most important
papers in this graph?" This time, the AI responds with a detailed multi-
paragraph analysis covering PageRank methodology, structural bottleneck
analysis, pruning impact for each top paper, confidence tiers of the
supporting edge classifications, and a comparison of importance metrics.

STEP 4: The researcher switches to STORYTELLER mode. They ask "Tell me the
story of how attention mechanisms evolved." The AI responds with a narrative
arc: the precursors, the breakthrough moment of Vaswani 2017, the adoption
wave, the generalization into new domains, the contradiction papers that
challenged the paradigm, and the current frontier.

STEP 5: The researcher switches back to EXPLORER for quick questions. The
conversation history from all three modes is preserved and the AI can
reference insights from earlier exchanges regardless of which mode generated
them.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-mode-selector.js   Mode selector UI, state management
    static/css/athena-mode-selector.css Segmented control, mode accents, transitions

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Include mode in message payload
    app.py                              /api/chat reads mode, selects model + prompt
    backend/chat_guide.py               Mode-specific system prompts and model selection
    backend/session_manager.py          Persist mode in sessions.persona

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  ModeSelector class renders a segmented control with three buttons:
  Explorer, Analyst, Storyteller. The active mode is stored in local state
  and synced to the backend via a PATCH /api/session/mode endpoint.

  When mode changes:
  1. Update visual state (slide underline, shift accent color)
  2. PATCH /api/session/mode to persist selection
  3. Dispatch "athena:mode:changed" event for other components to react
  4. The next chat message will use the new mode's model and system prompt

  The segmented control uses CSS transitions for smooth underline sliding.
  The underline width matches the active segment's text width. Mode accent
  colors are applied via CSS custom properties set on the chat panel container.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The /api/chat endpoint reads the mode from the request payload and uses it
  to select:
  1. The Groq model: Explorer -> GROQ_FAST_MODEL, Analyst/Storyteller -> GROQ_SMART_MODEL
  2. The system prompt: each mode has a distinct prompt in chat_guide.py

  Mode-specific system prompts:

  EXPLORER: "You are a concise research guide. Give direct, factual answers
  about the citation graph. Use specific numbers (PageRank scores, pruning
  percentages, confidence tiers). Keep responses under 200 words."

  ANALYST: "You are a thorough research analyst. Provide detailed, evidence-
  based analysis of the citation graph. Cross-reference multiple data sources:
  edge_analysis classifications, structural metrics, temporal patterns. Support
  every claim with specific data from the graph."

  STORYTELLER: "You are a research storyteller. Weave the data from the citation
  graph into a compelling narrative about how ideas evolved. Connect the dots
  between papers, explain why certain innovations mattered, and make the
  intellectual history come alive. Use the graph data as evidence but present
  it as narrative, not statistics."

CODE PATTERNS:

  class ModeSelector {
    constructor(chatPanel, sessionId) { ... }
    render() { ... }
    setMode(mode) { ... }
    getMode() { ... }
    onModeChange(mode) { ... }
    updateVisuals(mode) { ... }
    persistMode(mode) { ... }  // PATCH /api/session/mode
  }

  MODE_CONFIG = {
    explorer: { model: 'fast', accent: '--accent-gold', icon: '○' },
    analyst:  { model: 'smart', accent: '--accent-blue', icon: '◐' },
    storyteller: { model: 'smart', accent: '--accent-teal', icon: '●' }
  }

API:

  PATCH /api/session/mode
    Request: { session_id, mode: "explorer"|"analyst"|"storyteller" }
    Response: { updated: true, mode: "analyst" }

  POST /api/chat (modified)
    Request: { session_id, message, graph_id, mode }
    (mode parameter added to existing endpoint)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODE SELECTOR IN CHAT HEADER:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena Research Guide                                              │
  │                                                                     │
  │  ┌──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────┐                │
  │  │ ● Explorer   │ ◐ Analyst    │ ○ Storyteller    │                │
  │  └──────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────────┘                │
  │  ═══════════                                           [+] [H] [...│
  │  (gold underline under active mode)                                │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

MODE ACCENT STATES:

  Explorer active:     Header border-bottom: var(--accent-gold)
                       Underline: var(--accent-gold)
                       Mode icon: ● (filled)

  Analyst active:      Header border-bottom: var(--accent-blue)
                       Underline: var(--accent-blue)
                       Mode icon: ◐ (half-filled)

  Storyteller active:  Header border-bottom: var(--accent-teal)
                       Underline: var(--accent-teal)
                       Mode icon: ● (filled, teal)

SEGMENTED CONTROL DETAILS:

  Background: var(--bg-surface)
  Active segment: text var(--text-primary), underline 2px solid accent
  Inactive segment: text var(--text-muted), no underline
  Hover: text var(--text-secondary)
  Transition: underline slides via transform translateX (200ms ease)
  Height: 32px
  Font: 13px Inter, font-weight 500

UNDERLINE SLIDE ANIMATION:

  Explorer -> Analyst:
    Underline translateX: 0 -> segmentWidth (200ms)
    Border-bottom color: gold -> blue (200ms)

  Analyst -> Storyteller:
    Underline translateX: segmentWidth -> 2*segmentWidth (200ms)
    Border-bottom color: blue -> teal (200ms)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User clicks mode segment (e.g., "Analyst")
       |
       v
  ModeSelector.setMode("analyst")
       |
       ├── updateVisuals("analyst")
       |        |
       |        ├── Slide underline to Analyst segment
       |        ├── Shift header border-bottom to blue
       |        └── Update icon states
       |
       ├── persistMode("analyst")
       |        |
       |        └── PATCH /api/session/mode { session_id, mode: "analyst" }
       |                  |
       |                  v
       |             Backend: UPDATE sessions SET persona = 'analyst'
       |
       └── dispatch("athena:mode:changed", { mode: "analyst" })
       |
       v
  Next message sent includes mode: "analyst"
       |
       v
  /api/chat handler:
       |
       ├── mode == "analyst" -> model = config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL
       |
       └── chat_guide.py uses ANALYST system prompt
       |
       v
  Deeper, more analytical response streams back

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: Groq
MODEL: Varies by mode
  Explorer:    config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)
  Analyst:     config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL (llama-3.3-70b-versatile)
  Storyteller: config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL (llama-3.3-70b-versatile)

SYSTEM PROMPTS: Three distinct prompts (see Backend Architecture above)
INPUT: Same context assembly as #044, but model and prompt vary by mode
OUTPUT: Response style and depth varies by mode
FALLBACK: If GROQ_SMART_MODEL is unavailable, all modes fall back to GROQ_FAST_MODEL
CACHE: Responses cached per normal llm_cache key (model included in hash key)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Click mode segment     Switch active mode               Underline slide + color shift
 Mode changed           Persist to session               No visible feedback
 Send message           Mode included in payload         Response reflects mode
 Page reload            Mode restored from session       Correct segment highlighted
 During streaming       Mode switch disabled             Segments grayed out
 GROQ_SMART unavailable Analyst/Storyteller fall back    Toast: "Using standard model"
 Hover inactive mode    Highlight segment                Text brightens

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User switches mode mid-conversation
   Expected: Conversation history preserved. Only future responses use new mode.
   Previous responses retain their original style.
   Implementation: Mode is per-request, not retroactively applied.

2. Scenario: GROQ_SMART_MODEL rate limited or unavailable
   Expected: Analyst and Storyteller modes fall back to GROQ_FAST_MODEL.
   User warned via toast notification.
   Implementation: chat_guide.py catches Groq errors and falls back.

3. Scenario: User switches mode while AI is streaming
   Expected: Mode switch is disabled during streaming. Segments are visually
   dimmed and non-clickable until stream completes.
   Implementation: ModeSelector checks streaming state, disables interactions.

4. Scenario: Session persona column has unexpected value (e.g., from Phase 7 persona engine)
   Expected: Unknown persona values map to "explorer" mode as default.
   Implementation: ModeSelector validates against known modes, defaults to explorer.

5. Scenario: GROQ_API_KEY not set (no LLM available)
   Expected: Mode selector hidden entirely. Chat panel shows "AI features require
   configuration" message.
   Implementation: ModeSelector checks config.GROQ_ENABLED before rendering.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: Differentiated analysis workflows, Phase 7 persona system integration
ENHANCES: AI response quality and relevance
CONFLICTS: NONE (complements Phase 7 PersonaEngine)
EXTERNAL: Groq API (FAST + SMART models)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Three modes selectable: Explorer, Analyst, Storyteller
[ ] Mode persisted in sessions.persona column
[ ] Explorer uses GROQ_FAST_MODEL
[ ] Analyst and Storyteller use GROQ_SMART_MODEL
[ ] Each mode has distinct system prompt
[ ] Conversation history preserved across mode switches
[ ] Mode restored on page reload
[ ] Disabled during streaming

VISUAL:
[ ] Segmented control with sliding underline
[ ] Mode-specific accent colors (gold, blue, teal)
[ ] Header border-bottom shifts with mode
[ ] Dot matrix icons (filled, half, open) per mode
[ ] Smooth 200ms slide transitions

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Mode switch under 100ms (visual), persist under 500ms (API)
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Segmented control is a radio group with aria-labels
EDGE CASES: [ ] GROQ_SMART fallback, unknown persona default, no API key handled

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: All three modes tested with same question
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Compatible with sessions.persona, chat_guide.py

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-mode-selector.js, chat_guide.py mode prompts
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Switch each mode, send same question, verify response style differs
4. TEST: Reload page, verify mode persisted
5. TEST: Switch during streaming, verify disabled
6. CHECK: Correct Groq model used per mode (inspect API logs)
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty - feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P1 Phase B. Key differentiator for analysis depth. 10 hours.


================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #050 - RICH INPUT AREA                                            ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       050
Feature Name:     Rich Input Area
Category:         Core Infrastructure
Priority:         P0
Phase:            A
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 12 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Rich Input Area is the primary text entry component in the Athena chat
panel where the researcher composes messages to the AI. It goes beyond a
simple single-line text input by providing a multi-line, auto-expanding
textarea with intelligent features: keyboard shortcuts, character counting,
slash command support, mention autocomplete for paper titles, and visual
integration with the Arivu dot matrix aesthetic.

The input area uses a contenteditable div (not a <textarea>) to enable future
inline formatting, @-mentions with rich previews, and embedded graph node
references. However, for Phase A, the input remains plain text only - the
contenteditable foundation is laid but rich formatting is deferred to later
phases. The submitted text is always extracted as plain text via innerText
for processing.

Auto-expansion is the most visible feature. The input area starts at a single
line height (44px) and grows as the researcher types, up to a maximum of 6
lines (approximately 132px). After 6 lines, a scrollbar appears within the
input area. The expansion is smooth, animated with CSS transitions, and does
not cause the chat message history above to jump or re-layout unpredictably.

The send button sits to the right of the input area. It is enabled only when
the input contains non-whitespace text. The send action is triggered by
clicking the button or pressing Enter (without Shift). Shift+Enter inserts
a newline. This is the standard convention researchers expect from chat
interfaces. The send button uses the Arivu gold accent with a right-pointing
arrow icon rendered in the dot matrix style.

The input area includes a character counter that appears when the text
approaches the maximum length (2000 characters, matching the ChatRequest
Pydantic schema validation). The counter appears at 80% of the limit (1600
chars) and changes color as it approaches the max: text-secondary at 80%,
warning color at 95%, danger color at 100%. At 2000 characters, further
input is blocked.

A subtle placeholder text reads "Ask about the ancestry graph..." when the
input is empty. The placeholder fades out as the researcher begins typing and
fades back in when the input is cleared. The placeholder text is context-aware:
if a graph is loaded, it says "Ask about [graph title]...". If no graph is
loaded, it says "Search for a paper to begin...".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher focuses the input area to compose a message.

STEP 1: The researcher clicks into the input area. The placeholder text
"Ask about Attention Is All You Need..." fades out. The input border subtly
brightens from muted to the secondary text color, indicating focus.

STEP 2: The researcher types a multi-line question:
  "Can you compare the pruning impact of these three papers:
   1. Vaswani 2017 (Attention)
   2. Devlin 2018 (BERT)
   3. He 2016 (ResNet)"
The input area auto-expands to accommodate all 4 lines. Each line causes a
smooth height increase.

STEP 3: As the researcher types, the send button transitions from disabled
(dim, non-interactive) to enabled (gold, interactive). The character counter
is not visible yet because the text is well under 1600 characters.

STEP 4: The researcher presses Enter (without Shift). The message sends, the
input area collapses back to single-line height, the placeholder reappears,
and the send button returns to its disabled state.

STEP 5: For their next message, the researcher presses Shift+Enter to insert
a deliberate newline within their message, then continues typing on the new
line without triggering a send.

STEP 6: The researcher pastes a long text block from a paper abstract (1800
characters). The character counter appears: "1800 / 2000" in the text-secondary
color. They add more text and at 1900 characters the counter shifts to warning
color. At 2000, the input stops accepting characters and the counter turns red.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-input.js           Input area logic, auto-expand, shortcuts
    static/css/athena-input.css         Input styling, expansion, counter, button

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Wire input to message send pipeline
    static/css/athena-chat.css          Layout integration for input area

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  InputArea class manages the contenteditable div and associated controls:

  1. The main input element is a <div contenteditable="true"> with a
     placeholder overlay positioned behind it
  2. An input event listener triggers auto-expansion by comparing scrollHeight
     to clientHeight. If scrollHeight exceeds clientHeight, height is increased
     up to max-height (132px for ~6 lines)
  3. A keydown event listener handles:
     - Enter (no modifier): prevent default, call send()
     - Shift+Enter: allow default (insert newline)
     - Escape: blur input (if no dialog open)
     - Ctrl+A / Cmd+A: select all text in input
  4. An input event listener updates the character counter and enforces the
     2000-character limit by truncating excess text
  5. The send button listens for clicks and calls the same send() function

  The input area dispatches custom events:
  - "athena:input:send" with { text } when send is triggered
  - "athena:input:change" with { text, length } on every input change
  - "athena:input:focus" and "athena:input:blur" for focus tracking

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  No backend changes. The input area is a pure frontend component. The
  text is sent to the existing POST /api/chat endpoint as the message field.
  Server-side validation via ChatRequest schema enforces the same 2000-char
  limit.

CODE PATTERNS:

  class InputArea {
    constructor(container, options = {}) { ... }
    render() { ... }
    getText() { ... }          // Returns plain text from contenteditable
    setText(text) { ... }      // Sets text programmatically (for #042 pill injection)
    clear() { ... }            // Clears input and resets height
    focus() { ... }
    blur() { ... }
    send() { ... }             // Extract text, validate, dispatch send event, clear
    onInput(event) { ... }     // Auto-expand + char counter update
    onKeyDown(event) { ... }   // Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape handling
    updateCharCounter() { ... }
    updatePlaceholder() { ... }
    setEnabled(enabled) { ... } // Disable during streaming
    autoExpand() { ... }
    collapse() { ... }
  }

API:

  No new API endpoints. Input text is sent via existing POST /api/chat.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

INPUT AREA - EMPTY STATE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ┌─┐ │
  │  │  Ask about Attention Is All You Need...                    │ │○│ │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ └─┘ │
  │                                                                  ^   │
  │                                                            Send btn  │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Input: bg var(--bg-surface), border 1px solid var(--text-muted)
  Placeholder: color var(--text-muted), italic
  Send button: 36x36px, bg transparent, border none, color var(--text-muted)

INPUT AREA - FOCUSED WITH TEXT:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ┌─┐ │
  │  │  Can you compare the pruning impact of these three papers:│ │●│ │
  │  │  1. Vaswani 2017 (Attention)                              │ │ │ │
  │  │  2. Devlin 2018 (BERT)                                    │ │ │ │
  │  │  3. He 2016 (ResNet)                                      │ └─┘ │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Input: border 1px solid var(--text-secondary) (brightened on focus)
  Send button: bg var(--accent-gold), color var(--bg-primary), ● filled

INPUT AREA - NEAR CHARACTER LIMIT:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ┌─┐ │
  │  │  (long text content, 6 lines max, scrollbar inside)       │ │●│ │
  │  │  ...                                                      │ │ │ │
  │  │  ...                                                      │ │ │ │
  │  │  ...                                                      │ │ │ │
  │  │  ...                                                      │ │ │ │
  │  │  ...                                                      │ └─┘ │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
  │                                              1850 / 2000           │
  │                                              ^^^^^^^^^^^^^          │
  │                                              warning color          │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SEND BUTTON STATES:

  Disabled (no text):   ○  color: var(--text-muted)
                           background: transparent
                           cursor: not-allowed

  Enabled (has text):   ●  color: var(--bg-primary)
                           background: var(--accent-gold)
                           cursor: pointer
                           border-radius: 50%

  Hover:                ●  background: lighten(var(--accent-gold), 10%)
                           transform: scale(1.05)

  Active (pressed):     ●  transform: scale(0.95)

CHARACTER COUNTER THRESHOLDS:

  < 80% (< 1600):      Hidden
  80-95% (1600-1900):   color: var(--text-secondary)    "1750 / 2000"
  95-100% (1900-2000):  color: var(--warning)            "1950 / 2000"
  100% (2000):          color: var(--danger)             "2000 / 2000"

AUTO-EXPAND HEIGHTS:

  Min height:   44px (1 line)
  Per line:     ~22px (at 16px font, 1.375 line-height)
  Max height:   132px (6 lines)
  Transition:   height 100ms ease

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User types in input area
       |
       v
  onInput(event)
       |
       ├── autoExpand() -- compare scrollHeight vs clientHeight, grow if needed
       ├── updateCharCounter() -- show/hide counter, update color
       ├── updatePlaceholder() -- hide if text present, show if empty
       └── dispatch("athena:input:change", { text, length })
       |
       v
  User presses Enter
       |
       v
  onKeyDown(event)
       |
       ├── Shift held? -> allow default (newline), return
       |
       └── No shift -> prevent default
              |
              v
         send()
              |
              ├── getText() -- extract plain text from contenteditable
              ├── Validate: non-empty, <= 2000 chars
              ├── dispatch("athena:input:send", { text })
              ├── clear() -- reset input to empty state
              └── collapse() -- return to min height
              |
              v
         athena-chat.js receives "athena:input:send" event
              |
              v
         Constructs payload { session_id, message: text, graph_id, mode }
              |
              v
         POST /api/chat

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: None directly. Input text is passed to LLM via existing chat pipeline.
MODEL: N/A (determined by mode selector #049)
INPUT: User-composed plain text (max 2000 chars)
OUTPUT: N/A (input component only)
FALLBACK: N/A
CACHE: N/A

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Focus input            Brighten border                  Border color shift
 Type text              Auto-expand height               Smooth height growth
 Enter (no shift)       Send message                     Input clears, collapses
 Shift+Enter            Insert newline                   Height grows
 Escape                 Blur input                       Border dims
 Text > 1600 chars      Show character counter           Counter appears
 Text > 1900 chars      Counter turns warning            Yellow counter
 Text = 2000 chars      Block further input              Red counter
 Paste long text        Truncate to 2000 chars           Counter shows immediately
 Clear input            Show placeholder                 Placeholder fades in
 Click send button      Send message (same as Enter)     Button depresses
 During streaming       Input disabled                   Dimmed, non-editable

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User pastes text exceeding 2000 characters
   Expected: Text truncated to 2000 characters. Character counter shows
   "2000 / 2000" in red. User notified that text was truncated.
   Implementation: onInput handler checks length, truncates if over limit.

2. Scenario: User types only whitespace and presses Enter
   Expected: Send does not fire. Input treated as empty.
   Implementation: getText().trim() check before dispatching send event.

3. Scenario: ContentEditable produces HTML on paste (e.g., from a webpage)
   Expected: HTML stripped. Only plain text preserved.
   Implementation: "paste" event handler strips HTML via clipboard API or
   div.innerText extraction.

4. Scenario: User types very quickly (fast typist, 120 WPM)
   Expected: Auto-expand does not lag or cause visual jitter.
   Implementation: Auto-expand uses requestAnimationFrame debounce.

5. Scenario: Input contains special characters (angle brackets, ampersands)
   Expected: Characters preserved as-is. No HTML interpretation.
   Implementation: getText() uses innerText, not innerHTML.

6. Scenario: Mobile browser with virtual keyboard
   Expected: Input area visible above keyboard. Chat panel adjusts viewport.
   Implementation: CSS uses position: sticky for input, or viewport height
   calculation accounts for keyboard.

7. Scenario: Screen reader user navigates to input area
   Expected: Input announced as "Message input, ask about [graph title]".
   Role="textbox", aria-label set, aria-describedby points to counter.
   Implementation: Proper ARIA attributes on contenteditable div.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #033 (Chat Panel)
ENABLES: #042 (Suggestions - pill text injection), #043 (Attachments - paperclip button), #049 (Mode Selector - layout integration)
ENHANCES: Every chat interaction in Athena
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (pure HTML/CSS/JS)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] ContentEditable input area renders in chat panel
[ ] Auto-expand from 1 line to 6 lines as text grows
[ ] Scrollbar appears after 6 lines
[ ] Enter sends message
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline
[ ] Send button enabled only when non-empty text present
[ ] Character counter appears at 1600 characters
[ ] Input blocked at 2000 characters
[ ] Paste strips HTML, preserves plain text
[ ] setText() API for programmatic text injection (#042)
[ ] Input disabled during streaming

VISUAL:
[ ] Dot matrix send button (open dot disabled, filled dot enabled)
[ ] Gold accent send button when enabled
[ ] Focus state with brightened border
[ ] Smooth height transition on auto-expand
[ ] Context-aware placeholder text
[ ] Character counter with color thresholds

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Auto-expand under 16ms (one animation frame)
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] role="textbox", aria-label, aria-describedby counter, keyboard nav
EDGE CASES: [ ] Paste truncation, whitespace-only blocked, HTML stripped

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, mobile Safari
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: ChatRequest 2000-char limit matches, send event wired

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-input.js, athena-input.css
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Type multi-line text, verify auto-expand
4. TEST: Enter sends, Shift+Enter newline
5. TEST: Paste HTML, verify plain text extraction
6. TEST: Character limit at 2000, verify block
7. CHECK: Placeholder updates with graph context
8. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
9. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty - feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P0 Phase A. Foundation for all chat input. 12 hours estimated.


================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #042 — SUGGESTED FOLLOW-UPS                                       ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       042
Feature Name:     Suggested Follow-Ups
Category:         Proactive AI
Priority:         P1
Phase:            B
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 12 hours
Dependencies:     #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After every AI response in the Athena chat panel, the system generates a set of
contextually relevant follow-up questions that the researcher can click to continue
the conversation. These suggestions are not random or generic. They are derived
from the current graph state, the response content, and the Arivu-specific data
the researcher is exploring - PageRank centrality scores, mutation type distributions,
pruning impact percentages, edge_analysis confidence tiers, and cluster membership.

The follow-up suggestions appear as a row of clickable pill-shaped buttons below the
AI response. Each pill contains a concise question or action prompt. When the
researcher clicks one, it is injected into the input area as if they typed it,
and the send action fires automatically. This creates a guided exploration flow
where the researcher can drill into the intellectual ancestry graph without
formulating every query manually.

The generation of follow-ups happens in two modes. The primary mode extracts
candidates from the LLM response itself, using a structured output format where
the model is instructed to append a JSON block of suggested follow-ups. The
fallback mode uses heuristic rules based on graph topology - if the response
mentions a high-PageRank node, suggest "What happens if we prune [paper]?" If
the response discusses a mutation type like hybridization, suggest "Show me all
hybridization edges in this graph." If the response references a low-confidence
edge, suggest "What evidence supports this classification?"

The system caps suggestions at 3 per response. Suggestions are deduplicated
against the current conversation history so the researcher never sees a question
they already asked. The pills use the dot matrix aesthetic with a subtle gold
border and muted text, matching the Arivu visual language. On hover, the pill
elevates slightly and the border brightens. On click, the pill pulses once
before the text transfers to the input area.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: An AI response finishes streaming in the chat panel.

STEP 1: The streaming completes and the response content finalizes. After a
200ms delay (to let the researcher's eye settle), three pill-shaped suggestion
buttons fade in below the response, left-aligned.

STEP 2: The researcher reads the response about how "Attention Is All You Need"
generalized the sequence-to-sequence paradigm. Below the response they see:
  [What papers adopted this generalization?]
  [Show pruning impact for Vaswani 2017]
  [Which edges have LOW confidence here?]

STEP 3: The researcher clicks "Show pruning impact for Vaswani 2017". The pill
pulses with a gold flash, then the text appears in the input area.

STEP 4: The message sends automatically. The previous suggestions fade out.
The new response streams in.

STEP 5: New suggestions appear after the new response completes, reflecting
the updated conversational context. The researcher continues this guided
exploration pattern, clicking through suggestions to build understanding of
the graph's intellectual structure.

STEP 6: If the researcher types their own question instead of clicking a
suggestion, the existing suggestions fade out gracefully. No conflict between
typed input and suggested input.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-suggestions.js    Suggestion pill renderer and click handler
    static/css/athena-suggestions.css  Pill styling, animations, hover states

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-engine.js         Parse structured follow-ups from LLM output
    static/js/athena-stream.js         Trigger suggestion generation after stream end
    static/js/athena-chat.js           Inject clicked suggestion into input + send
    backend/chat_guide.py              Add follow-up generation to system prompt
    backend/prompt_sanitizer.py        Sanitize generated suggestions before display

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The suggestion system lives in athena-suggestions.js as a SuggestionManager
  class. It listens for the "athena:stream:complete" custom event dispatched
  by the streaming module. On receipt, it extracts the structured follow-up
  block from the response (a JSON array appended by the LLM), validates each
  suggestion string, deduplicates against conversation history, and renders
  up to 3 pills in a flex container below the response bubble.

  Each pill is a <button> element with class "athena-suggestion-pill". The
  click handler calls athena-chat.js setInput(text) followed by send(). The
  pills container is absolutely positioned relative to the response bubble
  so it scrolls naturally with chat history.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The LLM system prompt in chat_guide.py is augmented with an instruction block
  telling the model to append a structured JSON block to every response:

    [FOLLOW_UPS]
    ["question one", "question two", "question three"]
    [/FOLLOW_UPS]

  The engine.js parser strips this block from the displayed response and passes
  it to SuggestionManager. If the LLM fails to produce the block (or the parse
  fails), the fallback heuristic engine kicks in, scanning the response for
  Arivu-specific patterns:

    - Paper titles with high PageRank -> "What is the pruning impact of [title]?"
    - Mutation type mentions -> "Show all [type] edges in this lineage"
    - Confidence tier mentions -> "What evidence supports the [tier] edges?"
    - Cluster references -> "Explore the [cluster] research thread"
    - Orphan idea mentions -> "Why did [concept] become an orphan idea?"

CODE PATTERNS:

  class SuggestionManager {
    constructor(chatInterface, conversationHistory) { ... }
    onStreamComplete(responseText, graphContext) { ... }
    parseStructuredSuggestions(text) { ... }
    generateHeuristicSuggestions(text, graphContext) { ... }
    deduplicateAgainstHistory(suggestions) { ... }
    renderPills(suggestions, responseElement) { ... }
    onPillClick(text) { ... }
    clearSuggestions() { ... }
  }

API:

  No new API endpoints. Suggestions are generated client-side from LLM output
  and graph context already available in the frontend. The LLM prompt modification
  happens server-side in chat_guide.py but requires no new routes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SUGGESTION PILLS BELOW RESPONSE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  AI Response                                                        │
  │                                                                     │
  │  The Vaswani 2017 paper has a pruning impact of 31.6%, meaning     │
  │  removing it would collapse 47 dependent papers in this lineage.   │
  │  The primary mutation type from this paper is "generalization" -    │
  │  subsequent work took the attention mechanism and applied it to     │
  │  domains beyond machine translation...                              │
  │                                                                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ┌─────────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
    │ ○ What papers adopted this  │  │ ○ Show all generalization    │
    │   generalization?           │  │   edges in this lineage      │
    └─────────────────────────────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
    ┌─────────────────────────────────┐
    │ ○ Which edges have LOW          │
    │   confidence here?              │
    └─────────────────────────────────┘

PILL STATES:

  Default:    border: 1px solid var(--text-muted)
              background: transparent
              color: var(--text-secondary)
              ○ prefix icon (open dot)

  Hover:      border: 1px solid var(--accent-gold)
              background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.08)
              transform: translateY(-1px)
              ● prefix icon (filled dot)

  Click:      gold pulse animation (150ms)
              pill fades out, text transfers to input

  Container:  display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px;
              margin-top: 8px; padding: 4px 0;

ANIMATION SEQUENCE (after stream complete):

  t=0ms       Stream ends
  t=200ms     Pills container opacity 0 -> 1 (200ms ease-in)
  t=200ms     Each pill staggers in: pill1 at t=200, pill2 at t=280, pill3 at t=360

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  LLM Response (with [FOLLOW_UPS] block)
       |
       v
  athena-engine.js: parseResponse()
       |
       ├── Display text (without [FOLLOW_UPS] block)
       |
       └── Extracted suggestions JSON array
              |
              v
       SuggestionManager.onStreamComplete()
              |
              ├── parseStructuredSuggestions() -- primary path
              |        |
              |        └── (on parse failure) --> generateHeuristicSuggestions()
              |
              v
       deduplicateAgainstHistory()
              |
              v
       renderPills() -- max 3 pills
              |
              v
       User clicks pill
              |
              v
       onPillClick() -> chatInterface.setInput(text) -> chatInterface.send()
              |
              v
       clearSuggestions() -- old pills fade out
              |
              v
       New stream begins -> cycle repeats

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: Groq (via existing chat_guide.py pipeline)
MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
  "After your response, append exactly 3 follow-up questions the researcher
  might want to ask next, formatted as:
  [FOLLOW_UPS]
  ["question 1", "question 2", "question 3"]
  [/FOLLOW_UPS]
  Make questions specific to the graph data discussed. Reference paper titles,
  mutation types, confidence tiers, or pruning impacts when relevant."

INPUT: The existing chat context (conversation history + graph summary)
OUTPUT: Response text + structured [FOLLOW_UPS] block
FALLBACK: If LLM omits the block, heuristic suggestions from response text
CACHE: Follow-ups are ephemeral, not cached. Each response generates fresh ones.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Stream complete        Generate + show pills            Staggered fade-in
 Pill hover             Highlight pill                   Gold border, elevation
 Pill click             Inject text + auto-send          Gold pulse, fade out
 User types own input   Dismiss existing suggestions     Suggestions fade out
 New stream starts      Clear previous suggestions       Instant removal
 LLM parse failure      Fallback to heuristic pills      Same visual, different source
 No suggestions found   Show nothing                     No empty container
 Pill text too long     Truncate at 80 chars + "..."     Ellipsis, full on hover tooltip

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: LLM returns malformed JSON in the [FOLLOW_UPS] block
   Expected: Fallback to heuristic suggestion generation. No error visible.
   Implementation: try/catch around JSON.parse, log warning, call heuristic path.

2. Scenario: All 3 LLM suggestions duplicate questions already asked
   Expected: Show 0 pills. No empty container visible.
   Implementation: deduplicateAgainstHistory filters all, renderPills checks length.

3. Scenario: LLM returns suggestions that contain prompt injection attempts
   Expected: Suggestions sanitized. HTML stripped. Script tags removed.
   Implementation: Each suggestion string passes through DOMPurify + length cap.

4. Scenario: User clicks pill at exact moment a new stream starts
   Expected: Pill click wins. The clicked suggestion becomes the next message.
   Implementation: Pill click handler checks if input is already sending.

5. Scenario: Response has no Arivu-specific content (generic greeting)
   Expected: Generic follow-ups like "What paper should I analyze?" or
   "Tell me about a paper in my graph."
   Implementation: Heuristic engine has a generic fallback set for non-graph responses.

6. Scenario: Graph context is empty (no graph built yet)
   Expected: Suggestions focus on getting started: "Search for a paper to analyze"
   Implementation: SuggestionManager detects empty graphContext and uses starter set.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: Enhanced guided exploration workflows
ENHANCES: #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (uses existing Groq LLM pipeline)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Up to 3 suggestion pills appear after each AI response
[ ] Clicking a pill injects text into input and sends automatically
[ ] Suggestions are deduplicated against conversation history
[ ] LLM structured output parsed correctly
[ ] Heuristic fallback generates Arivu-specific suggestions
[ ] Suggestions cleared when user types own message
[ ] Suggestions cleared when new stream begins

VISUAL:
[ ] Pills use dot matrix aesthetic (open dot prefix, gold accent)
[ ] Staggered fade-in animation (200ms base + 80ms per pill)
[ ] Hover state with gold border and slight elevation
[ ] Click pulse animation before fade-out
[ ] No empty container when 0 suggestions

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Suggestion generation completes within 100ms of stream end
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Pills are focusable buttons with descriptive aria-labels
EDGE CASES: [ ] Malformed JSON handled, injection sanitized, empty graph handled

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches Arivu chat_guide.py integration

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-suggestions.js, athena-engine.js, chat_guide.py system prompt
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Click each pill, verify injection + auto-send
4. TEST: Force LLM parse failure, verify heuristic fallback
5. TEST: Repeat same question, verify deduplication
6. CHECK: No empty containers, no broken pills on resize
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty - feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24

================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #043 — FILE/IMAGE ATTACHMENTS                                     ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       043
Feature Name:     File/Image Attachments
Category:         Input Methods
Priority:         P1
Phase:            D
Complexity:       High
Estimated Effort: 20 hours
Dependencies:     #050 (Rich Input Area), #037 (Streaming Text), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Athena chat panel allows researchers to attach files and images to their
messages. This enables workflows where a researcher drags a PDF paper, a
screenshot of a citation graph, or a CSV of paper metadata directly into the
chat and asks the AI to analyze it in the context of the current Arivu graph.

The attachment system supports three file types relevant to the research
intelligence domain: PDF papers (extracted via PyMuPDF on the backend for
text content), images (screenshots of graphs, figures from papers, hand-drawn
diagrams of research relationships), and plain text files (BibTeX entries,
CSV exports, text notes). Each type has a distinct processing pipeline.

PDFs are uploaded to the backend via a multipart POST endpoint. The backend
extracts text using PyMuPDF (import fitz), structures it into sections using
the existing section_parser.py module, and returns a text summary that becomes
part of the chat context. The original PDF is stored temporarily in memory
(not on disk, not in R2) and discarded after the chat session ends.

Images are handled client-side when possible. Small images (under 500KB) are
base64-encoded and included in the message payload. The LLM receives a text
description generated by a lightweight image captioning prompt. For the Arivu
use case, image analysis focuses on extracting paper titles, citation counts,
and structural patterns visible in graph screenshots.

The attachment UI consists of a paperclip icon button in the input area that
opens a file picker, plus a drag-and-drop zone that activates when the user
drags a file over the chat panel. Attached files appear as compact preview
cards above the input area showing the filename, file size, file type icon,
and a remove button. Multiple attachments per message are supported up to a
limit of 3 files and 10MB total.

The visual design uses the dot matrix aesthetic. File type icons are rendered
as small dot matrix patterns - a grid of dots for PDF, a frame of dots for
images, a column of dots for text files. The preview card has a dark surface
background with a subtle gold accent border on the left edge.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher wants to share a file with the AI in the chat context.

STEP 1: The researcher clicks the paperclip icon (bottom-left of input area)
or drags a PDF file from their desktop onto the chat panel.

STEP 2: A drag-over visual cue activates - the chat panel border glows gold
and a translucent overlay says "Drop file to attach". The researcher drops
the file.

STEP 3: A compact preview card appears above the input area:
  [PDF icon] attention_is_all_you_need.pdf  2.3 MB  [x remove]

STEP 4: The researcher types "Compare this paper's methodology section to
the methods used by papers that adopted the attention mechanism" and presses
Enter.

STEP 5: The backend receives the PDF, extracts text via PyMuPDF, identifies
the methodology section via section_parser.py, and passes the extracted text
along with the chat message to the LLM. The response streams back, referencing
specific content from the uploaded paper alongside edge_analysis data from
the current graph.

STEP 6: After the response completes, the attachment preview card is removed
from the input area. The attached content remains in the conversation context
for follow-up questions within the same session.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-attachments.js     Attachment manager, drag-drop, file picker
    static/css/athena-attachments.css   Preview cards, drag overlay, file icons

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Integrate attachment data into message send
    static/js/athena-input.js           Add paperclip button to input area
    app.py                              POST /api/chat/upload endpoint
    backend/chat_guide.py               Include attachment text in LLM context
    backend/section_parser.py           Expose extract_text_from_pdf() for chat use
    backend/prompt_sanitizer.py         Sanitize extracted PDF text

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  AttachmentManager class in athena-attachments.js handles:
  - File picker dialog (triggered by paperclip button click)
  - Drag-and-drop event handling (dragover, dragleave, drop on chat panel)
  - File validation (type check, size check, count check)
  - Preview card rendering (filename, size, type icon, remove button)
  - File data preparation for upload (FormData for PDFs, base64 for images)

  The manager maintains an attachments array that is read by athena-chat.js
  when constructing the message payload. After send, attachments are cleared
  from the input area but their extracted content persists in conversation
  memory on the backend.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  POST /api/chat/upload receives multipart form data with the file and
  session_id. Processing pipeline:

  1. Validate file type (application/pdf, image/png, image/jpeg, text/plain)
  2. Validate file size (max 10MB per file via config.MAX_UPLOAD_MB)
  3. For PDFs: extract text via fitz (PyMuPDF), structure via section_parser
  4. For images: store base64, generate description prompt for LLM
  5. For text: read content directly
  6. Return extracted text summary + attachment_id to frontend
  7. Store extracted text in session memory (not persisted to disk)

  The extracted text is appended to the chat context in chat_guide.py when
  building the LLM prompt for the next message.

CODE PATTERNS:

  class AttachmentManager {
    constructor(inputArea, chatInterface) { ... }
    openFilePicker() { ... }
    onDragOver(event) { ... }
    onDrop(event) { ... }
    validateFile(file) { ... }
    addAttachment(file) { ... }
    removeAttachment(attachmentId) { ... }
    renderPreviewCard(attachment) { ... }
    prepareForSend() { ... }  // Returns FormData or base64 payloads
    clearAttachments() { ... }
    getAttachmentContext() { ... }  // Returns text for LLM context
  }

API:

  POST /api/chat/upload
    Request: multipart/form-data { file, session_id }
    Response: { attachment_id, filename, type, extracted_text_preview, size_bytes }
    Errors: 400 (invalid type), 413 (too large), 422 (extraction failed)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ATTACHMENT PREVIEW CARD (above input area):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┐   │
  │  │ ● ● ●   attention_is_all_you_need.pdf   2.3 MB     [x]     │   │
  │  │ ● ● ●                                                       │   │
  │  └─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┘   │
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ Compare this paper's methodology to the attention adopters  │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │                                                          [clip] [>] │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

DRAG-AND-DROP OVERLAY:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │                                                                      │
  │                    ┌─────────────────────────┐                       │
  │                    │                         │                       │
  │                    │    ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○      │                       │
  │                    │    ○             ○      │                       │
  │                    │    ○   Drop to   ○      │                       │
  │                    │    ○   attach    ○      │                       │
  │                    │    ○             ○      │                       │
  │                    │    ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○ ○      │                       │
  │                    │                         │                       │
  │                    └─────────────────────────┘                       │
  │                                                                      │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Overlay: background rgba(10, 14, 23, 0.85) with gold dashed border

FILE TYPE DOT MATRIX ICONS:

  PDF:        IMAGE:      TEXT:
  ● ● ●      ○ ○ ○ ○     ● ● ● ●
  ● ● ●      ○       ○     ● ● ● ●
  ● ● ●      ○       ○     ● ● ● ●
  ● ● ●      ○ ○ ○ ○     ● ● ● ●

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User drops/selects file
       |
       v
  AttachmentManager.addAttachment(file)
       |
       ├── validateFile() -- type + size check
       |        |
       |        └── (invalid) --> show error toast, reject file
       |
       v
  renderPreviewCard() -- show in input area
       |
       v
  User types message + clicks send
       |
       v
  prepareForSend() -- build FormData with file + message
       |
       v
  POST /api/chat/upload
       |
       ├── PDF path: fitz.open(stream) -> section_parser -> extracted text
       ├── Image path: base64 encode -> description prompt
       └── Text path: read content directly
       |
       v
  Return { attachment_id, extracted_text_preview }
       |
       v
  chat_guide.py builds LLM prompt with attachment text + graph context
       |
       v
  LLM response streams back referencing attachment content
       |
       v
  clearAttachments() -- preview cards removed from input area

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: Groq (via existing chat_guide.py pipeline)
MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL for text analysis, config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL for
       complex cross-referencing between attachment and graph data

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
  "The user has attached a file. The extracted content is provided below.
  Analyze it in the context of the current citation ancestry graph. Reference
  specific papers, mutation types, PageRank scores, and edge classifications
  when drawing connections between the attachment and the graph."

INPUT: Extracted text from attachment + existing chat context + graph summary
OUTPUT: Response referencing both attachment content and graph data
FALLBACK: If extraction fails, inform user and proceed with message text only
CACHE: Attachment-derived context not cached (session-scoped, ephemeral)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Click paperclip        Open file picker dialog          Button highlight
 Drag file over panel   Show drop overlay                Gold dashed border
 Drag leave panel       Hide drop overlay                Overlay fades out
 Drop file              Validate + show preview card     Card slides in from bottom
 Click remove [x]       Remove attachment                Card slides out
 File too large         Reject with error                Toast: "File exceeds 10MB"
 Wrong file type        Reject with error                Toast: "Unsupported type"
 3+ files attached      Reject additional                Toast: "Max 3 attachments"
 Send with attachment   Upload + process + send          Progress bar on card
 Upload fails           Show error on card               Red border on card
 PDF extraction fails   Inform user, send text only      Warning toast

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User drops a password-protected PDF
   Expected: Backend extraction fails gracefully. User sees "Could not extract
   text from this PDF. It may be password-protected." Message sends without
   attachment context.
   Implementation: fitz.open() catches encryption error, returns empty text.

2. Scenario: User drops a 50MB file (exceeds 10MB limit)
   Expected: File rejected client-side before upload. Toast shows size error.
   Implementation: validateFile() checks file.size before adding to attachments.

3. Scenario: User drops an executable (.exe) disguised as .pdf
   Expected: Backend validates MIME type from file header, not extension.
   Implementation: python-magic checks actual file type, rejects non-PDF.

4. Scenario: User attaches a file then navigates away before sending
   Expected: Attachments cleared. No orphaned uploads on backend.
   Implementation: Attachments are client-side only until send. No pre-upload.

5. Scenario: Network fails during file upload
   Expected: Upload error shown on preview card. User can retry or remove.
   Implementation: Upload wrapped in try/catch with retry button on card.

6. Scenario: Extracted PDF text is extremely long (100+ pages)
   Expected: Text truncated to first 8000 tokens for LLM context window.
   Implementation: Backend truncates with "[...content truncated for length]".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #050 (Rich Input Area), #037 (Streaming Text), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: PDF-based research workflows, graph-to-paper comparison
ENHANCES: #033 (Chat Panel), #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: PyMuPDF (fitz) for PDF extraction, python-magic for MIME validation

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Paperclip button opens file picker
[ ] Drag-and-drop onto chat panel works
[ ] PDF text extraction via PyMuPDF on backend
[ ] Image base64 encoding for small images
[ ] Text file content read directly
[ ] Preview cards show filename, size, type icon, remove button
[ ] Max 3 files, max 10MB total enforced
[ ] Extracted content included in LLM context
[ ] Attachments cleared after send

VISUAL:
[ ] Dot matrix file type icons
[ ] Gold accent drag-and-drop overlay
[ ] Preview cards with dark surface background
[ ] Upload progress indicator on cards
[ ] Smooth slide-in/slide-out animations

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Client-side validation under 50ms, upload under 5s for 10MB
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Paperclip button labeled, drag-drop announced, preview cards readable
EDGE CASES: [ ] Protected PDFs handled, oversized files rejected, MIME validated

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: PDF, image, text uploads verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Uses existing section_parser.py, matches secure_upload.py patterns

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-attachments.js, upload endpoint, section_parser.py
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Upload each file type (PDF, PNG, TXT)
4. TEST: Drag-drop interaction on all browsers
5. TEST: Oversized file, wrong type, protected PDF
6. CHECK: MIME validation, no disk persistence, context truncation
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
