================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #044 — MULTI-TURN CONTEXT                                         ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       044
Feature Name:     Multi-Turn Context
Category:         Core Infrastructure
Priority:         P0
Phase:            A
Complexity:       High
Estimated Effort: 16 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Multi-turn context is the memory system that allows the Athena chat panel to
maintain coherent conversations across multiple exchanges. Without this feature,
every message the researcher sends would be treated in isolation - the AI would
have no memory of what was discussed two messages ago. With it, the AI can
reference earlier parts of the conversation, build on previous analysis, and
maintain a consistent understanding of what the researcher is exploring.

The context system operates on three layers. The first layer is conversation
history: an ordered list of message pairs (user message + AI response) stored
in the chat_history table on the backend, keyed by session_id. This history is
never accepted from the client payload (security requirement from CLAUDE.md).
The server loads it from the database on every /api/chat request.

The second layer is graph context: a compact summary of the current ancestry
graph being explored. This includes the top 15 papers by PageRank, the top 5
bottleneck nodes (papers whose removal would cause maximum cascade), the top
20 edges by mutation_confidence, and aggregate statistics like total nodes,
total edges, dominant mutation types, and field distribution. This summary is
generated by get_graph_summary_for_chat() from backend/utils.py and is kept
under 1500 tokens to preserve LLM context window budget.

The third layer is session context: metadata about the researcher's exploration
session including which papers they have examined, which pruning simulations
they have run, which clusters they have expanded, and which edges they have
flagged for disagreement. This is read from the sessions table (graph_memory
JSONB column) and provides behavioral context the LLM can use to personalize
responses.

The context window management is critical. The LLM (Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant)
has a limited context window. The system allocates the budget as follows:
system prompt (500 tokens), graph summary (1500 tokens), conversation history
(variable, up to 4000 tokens), and current message (up to 500 tokens). When
conversation history exceeds its budget, older messages are summarized into a
single "conversation so far" block and the most recent 5 exchanges are kept
verbatim. This sliding window with summarization ensures the AI never loses
important context while staying within token limits.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher sends any message in the Athena chat panel.

STEP 1: The researcher asks "What is the most influential paper in this graph?"
The AI responds with the highest-PageRank paper and its pruning impact.

STEP 2: The researcher follows up with "What mutation types does it produce?"
The AI understands "it" refers to the paper from the previous answer. It queries
the edge_analysis data for edges originating from that paper and lists the
mutation type distribution.

STEP 3: The researcher asks "Are any of those LOW confidence?" The AI
understands "those" refers to the edges discussed in step 2 and "LOW confidence"
refers to the confidence_tier classification. It filters and responds.

STEP 4: The researcher says "Flag the hybridization one for review." The AI
understands which specific edge is being referenced from the entire conversation
history and executes the flag action.

STEP 5: Twenty messages later, the researcher asks "Going back to what we
discussed about the most influential paper - has anything changed if I prune
paper X instead?" The AI retrieves the summarized early conversation context
and connects it to the current question, maintaining coherent understanding
across the entire session.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-context.js         Client-side context state manager
    backend/context_manager.py          Server-side context assembly + window management

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Send session_id with every message
    static/js/athena-engine.js          Track conversation state on client
    app.py                              /api/chat route loads full context
    backend/chat_guide.py               Accept assembled context, build LLM prompt
    backend/utils.py                    get_graph_summary_for_chat() optimization
    backend/db.py                       Queries for chat_history retrieval

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The client-side context manager (athena-context.js) maintains a local mirror
  of the conversation for display purposes. It tracks message pairs with
  timestamps and message IDs. However, it never sends conversation history to
  the server - the server loads history from the database independently. The
  client sends only: session_id, current message text, and graph_id (to identify
  which graph context to load).

  The client also tracks "active references" - when the AI mentions a specific
  paper, edge, or cluster, the client stores that reference locally so the UI
  can highlight the referenced element in the graph visualization. This is a
  display-only feature that does not affect the context sent to the LLM.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  The context_manager.py module assembles the complete LLM prompt from three
  sources:

  1. load_conversation_history(session_id) - fetches from chat_history table
  2. get_graph_summary_for_chat(graph) - compact graph representation
  3. load_session_context(session_id) - exploration metadata from sessions table

  The context window manager implements a sliding window with summarization:

  - Total budget: ~6500 tokens (leaves room for LLM response)
  - System prompt: 500 tokens (fixed)
  - Graph summary: 1500 tokens (fixed, regenerated per request)
  - History: 4000 tokens (variable)
  - Current message: 500 tokens (variable)

  When history exceeds 4000 tokens:
  1. Keep the most recent 5 message pairs verbatim
  2. Summarize all older messages into a single "Context from earlier" block
  3. The summary is generated by a fast LLM call (GROQ_FAST_MODEL)
  4. The summary is cached in llm_cache to avoid re-summarizing on every turn

CODE PATTERNS:

  class ContextManager:
    def __init__(self, session_id, graph_id): ...
    def load_conversation_history(self): ...
    def load_graph_context(self): ...
    def load_session_context(self): ...
    def estimate_tokens(self, text): ...
    def summarize_old_history(self, messages): ...
    def assemble_prompt(self, current_message): ...
    def save_exchange(self, user_message, ai_response): ...

API:

  No new endpoints. The existing POST /api/chat endpoint is modified to use
  ContextManager internally. The request payload remains:
    { session_id, message, graph_id }
  The response format is unchanged. Context management is entirely server-side.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CONTEXT INDICATOR (subtle, bottom of chat panel):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ... (chat messages above) ...                                       │
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │
  │  │  ◐ Context: 12 turns  ●  Graph: attention (152 nodes)     │      │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │
  │  │  Type your message...                                [>]  │      │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

CONTEXT BUDGET VISUALIZATION (debug/dev mode only):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Context Budget         Used / Total             │
  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │
  │  System prompt   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░   500 / 500   │
  │  Graph summary   ████████░░░░░░░░  1200 / 1500  │
  │  History         ██████████████░░  3800 / 4000   │
  │  Current msg     ██░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   120 / 500   │
  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────── │
  │  Total           ████████████████  5620 / 6500   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

SUMMARIZATION INDICATOR (when old history gets summarized):

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┐   │
  │  │  ◑ Earlier messages summarized (7 turns compressed)          │   │
  │  └─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ┘   │
  │                                                                      │
  │  (recent messages shown verbatim below)                              │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User sends message (with session_id, graph_id)
       |
       v
  POST /api/chat
       |
       v
  ContextManager(session_id, graph_id)
       |
       ├── load_conversation_history()
       |        |
       |        └── SELECT * FROM chat_history WHERE session_id = ?
       |             ORDER BY created_at ASC
       |
       ├── load_graph_context()
       |        |
       |        └── get_graph_summary_for_chat(graph)
       |             Top 15 PageRank, 5 bottlenecks, 20 edges
       |
       └── load_session_context()
                |
                └── SELECT graph_memory FROM sessions WHERE session_id = ?
       |
       v
  assemble_prompt(current_message)
       |
       ├── estimate_tokens(history) > 4000?
       |        |
       |        ├── YES: summarize_old_history(older_messages)
       |        |        Cache summary in llm_cache
       |        |        Keep recent 5 pairs verbatim
       |        |
       |        └── NO: Use full history
       |
       v
  Complete prompt = system + graph_context + history + session_context + message
       |
       v
  chat_guide.py -> Groq LLM call
       |
       v
  save_exchange(user_message, ai_response) -> INSERT INTO chat_history
       |
       v
  Stream response to client

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: Groq
MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL for chat responses and history summarization

CONTEXT BUDGET ALLOCATION:
  System prompt:     500 tokens (fixed)
  Graph summary:    1500 tokens (regenerated per request)
  History:          4000 tokens (sliding window)
  Current message:   500 tokens (variable)
  Total budget:     6500 tokens (leaves ~1500 for response)

SUMMARIZATION PROMPT:
  "Summarize the following conversation between a researcher and an AI research
  assistant. Preserve all paper titles, mutation types, confidence assessments,
  pruning results, and specific analytical conclusions. Be concise but do not
  lose any factual claims about the citation graph."

INPUT: Assembled multi-layer context + current message
OUTPUT: Streaming response with full conversational coherence
FALLBACK: If context assembly fails, fall back to graph context + current message only
CACHE: History summaries cached in llm_cache (key = SHA256 of summarized messages)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Send message           Load full context server-side    Context indicator updates
 10+ turns reached      Summarize old history            Summarization indicator shown
 Graph changes          Regenerate graph context          Context indicator refreshes
 Session timeout        Context persists in DB           No visible change
 Page reload            History reloaded from DB         Chat history restored
 Clear conversation     Reset all context layers         #047 handles this
 Switch graph           Replace graph context layer      Context indicator updates
 Token budget exceeded  Aggressive summarization         Warning in dev mode

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Conversation reaches 50+ turns (very long session)
   Expected: Multiple rounds of summarization. Oldest summaries get re-summarized.
   No degradation in response quality for recent context.
   Implementation: Recursive summarization with cached intermediate summaries.

2. Scenario: Graph is rebuilt mid-conversation (different graph_id)
   Expected: Graph context layer updates. Conversation history preserved.
   AI acknowledges the graph change if relevant.
   Implementation: ContextManager detects graph_id change, regenerates graph layer.

3. Scenario: Database connection fails when loading history
   Expected: Graceful degradation - respond with current message + graph context only.
   Log error but do not fail the request.
   Implementation: try/except around history load, fall back to minimal context.

4. Scenario: Session_id is missing or invalid
   Expected: Create new session. Respond with empty history context.
   Implementation: ContextManager creates session if not found.

5. Scenario: History summarization LLM call fails
   Expected: Use truncated raw history (most recent messages that fit budget).
   Implementation: Summarization failure falls back to simple truncation.

6. Scenario: Two browser tabs share the same session_id
   Expected: Both tabs see the same conversation history. Messages from either
   tab are interleaved in chronological order.
   Implementation: History is DB-authoritative. No client-side history conflicts.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups), #043 (File Attachments), #048 (History Sidebar)
ENHANCES: Every feature that uses the chat panel
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Groq API (for summarization calls), chat_history table, sessions table

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Conversation history loaded from DB on every request
[ ] History never accepted from client payload
[ ] Graph context generated via get_graph_summary_for_chat()
[ ] Session context loaded from sessions.graph_memory
[ ] Sliding window with summarization at 4000-token history budget
[ ] Summarized history cached in llm_cache
[ ] AI maintains coherent references across 10+ turns
[ ] Page reload preserves full conversation context

VISUAL:
[ ] Context indicator shows turn count and active graph
[ ] Summarization indicator shown when old messages compressed
[ ] Context budget visualization available in dev mode

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Context assembly under 200ms (excluding summarization LLM call)
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Context state announced to screen readers
EDGE CASES: [ ] DB failure handled, graph change handled, 50+ turns handled

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: 10+ turn conversation tested for coherence
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches chat_guide.py, chat_history security model

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ context_manager.py, chat_guide.py, athena-context.js
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: 10-turn conversation, verify pronoun resolution ("it", "those", "that paper")
4. TEST: 20-turn conversation, verify summarization triggers
5. TEST: Reload page, verify history restoration
6. CHECK: Client never sends history array, server always loads from DB
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty - feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P0 Phase A. Critical infrastructure. 16 hours estimated.


================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #045 — COLLAPSIBLE LONG RESPONSES                                ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       045
Feature Name:     Collapsible Long Responses
Category:         Response Blocks
Priority:         P1
Phase:            B
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 8 hours
Dependencies:     #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When the AI produces a long response in the Athena chat panel - more than a
configurable threshold of visible lines - the response is automatically
collapsed to show only the first portion with a "Show more" control at the
bottom. This prevents long analytical responses from dominating the chat
viewport and pushing earlier context out of view.

The collapsing system is intelligent about where it cuts. Rather than slicing
at an arbitrary character count, it identifies natural break points: paragraph
boundaries, section headings (rendered from markdown), or the end of a list
item. The cut never splits a code block, a table, or a citation reference
mid-element. This ensures the collapsed preview is always a coherent,
readable fragment.

The threshold is set at 15 visible lines (approximately 300px at standard
line height). Responses shorter than this are never collapsed. The threshold
is measured after markdown rendering, not on raw text length, because a
single markdown table might render to many visual lines while being few
characters of source text.

The "Show more" control is a subtle button that sits at the bottom of the
collapsed response, centered, with a downward-pointing dot matrix chevron.
Clicking it smoothly expands the response to full height with a CSS transition.
The button text changes to "Show less" with an upward chevron. The expansion
is animated at a rate that keeps the researcher's reading position stable -
content above the fold does not jump.

When multiple long responses exist in the conversation, each has independent
collapse state. Collapsing one does not affect others. The collapse state
is ephemeral - it resets on page reload (all responses start expanded after
reload since the researcher is re-reading the conversation anyway).

The collapsed view includes a gradient fade at the bottom edge, transitioning
from the response background color to transparent, indicating that more
content exists below. This is a common UI pattern that researchers will
recognize immediately. The gradient uses the dark surface color of the chat
bubble background (#1E293B to transparent).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: AI produces a response exceeding 15 rendered lines.

STEP 1: The AI streams a detailed analysis of the mutation type distribution
across 47 edges in the current graph. The response includes a markdown table,
three paragraphs of analysis, and a bulleted list of recommendations. Total
rendered height: approximately 600px (40+ lines).

STEP 2: As the stream completes, the response smoothly collapses to show
only the first 15 lines. A gradient fade appears at the bottom edge, and a
"Show more" button with a downward ○ chevron appears below the fade.

STEP 3: The researcher reads the preview. They want the full analysis, so
they click "Show more". The response expands with a smooth 300ms animation.
The button changes to "Show less" with an upward chevron.

STEP 4: The researcher scrolls through the full response, then clicks
"Show less" to re-collapse it, keeping the chat history compact.

STEP 5: The next AI response is short (5 lines). It appears normally with
no collapse controls. The system only activates for responses that actually
need it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-collapse.js        Collapse manager, height measurement, animation
    static/css/athena-collapse.css      Collapse states, gradient fade, button styles

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-stream.js          Trigger collapse check after stream complete
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Apply collapse to message bubbles
    static/css/athena-chat.css          Overflow handling on collapsed bubbles

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  CollapseManager class observes each AI response bubble after rendering
  completes. It measures the rendered height using getBoundingClientRect().
  If height exceeds the threshold (configurable, default 300px which is
  approximately 15 lines at 20px line-height), it:

  1. Records the full natural height
  2. Sets max-height to threshold with overflow: hidden
  3. Appends the gradient fade overlay (CSS pseudo-element)
  4. Appends the "Show more" button

  The expand/collapse toggle uses CSS transitions on max-height. The
  max-height transitions from threshold to the recorded natural height
  (not "auto", because CSS cannot transition to auto). On content changes
  (e.g., streaming updates), the natural height is re-measured.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  No backend changes. Collapsing is entirely a frontend presentation concern.
  The full response is always stored in chat_history regardless of collapse
  state.

CODE PATTERNS:

  class CollapseManager {
    constructor(options = { threshold: 300 }) { ... }
    checkAndCollapse(responseBubble) { ... }
    measureNaturalHeight(element) { ... }
    findCutPoint(element, threshold) { ... }
    applyCollapse(element, naturalHeight) { ... }
    toggleExpand(element) { ... }
    renderToggleButton(element, isCollapsed) { ... }
    renderGradientFade(element) { ... }
    onStreamComplete(responseBubble) { ... }
  }

API:

  No new API endpoints. Entirely client-side feature.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

COLLAPSED RESPONSE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  AI Response                                                        │
  │                                                                     │
  │  The mutation type distribution across this lineage shows a         │
  │  clear pattern of adoption (42%) and generalization (28%) as        │
  │  the dominant inheritance mechanisms. The remaining edges split     │
  │  between specialization (15%), hybridization (8%), and              │
  │  contradiction (4%). Three incidental edges were excluded from      │
  │  this analysis due to LOW confidence tiers.                         │
  │                                                                     │
  │  The high concentration of adoption edges suggests this is a        │
  │  mature research lineage where the core technique has been...       │
  │                                                                     │
  │  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  (gradient fade)  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  │
  │                                                                     │
  │                    ○  Show more  ○                                   │
  │                                                                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

EXPANDED RESPONSE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  AI Response                                                        │
  │                                                                     │
  │  (full content visible, no gradient fade)                           │
  │                                                                     │
  │  ... (all paragraphs, tables, lists shown) ...                      │
  │                                                                     │
  │                    ○  Show less  ○                                   │
  │                                                                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TOGGLE BUTTON STATES:

  Show more:   ○ Show more ○     color: var(--text-muted)
               (down chevron)    hover: color var(--accent-gold)

  Show less:   ○ Show less ○     color: var(--text-muted)
               (up chevron)      hover: color var(--accent-gold)

GRADIENT FADE:

  background: linear-gradient(
    to bottom,
    transparent 0%,
    var(--bg-surface) 100%
  );
  height: 60px;
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 40px (above button);
  width: 100%;

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  AI response finishes streaming
       |
       v
  athena-stream.js dispatches "athena:stream:complete"
       |
       v
  CollapseManager.onStreamComplete(responseBubble)
       |
       v
  measureNaturalHeight(responseBubble)
       |
       ├── height <= threshold (300px)
       |        |
       |        └── Do nothing. Response stays fully visible.
       |
       └── height > threshold
                |
                v
           findCutPoint(element, threshold)
                |
                v
           applyCollapse(element, naturalHeight)
                |
                ├── Set max-height: threshold
                ├── Add overflow: hidden
                ├── Render gradient fade
                └── Render "Show more" button
                     |
                     v
                User clicks "Show more"
                     |
                     v
                toggleExpand(element)
                     |
                     ├── Animate max-height: threshold -> naturalHeight
                     ├── Remove gradient fade
                     └── Change button to "Show less"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: None. This feature is entirely client-side.
MODEL: N/A
INPUT: Rendered AI response HTML
OUTPUT: Collapsed/expanded presentation of the same content
FALLBACK: If height measurement fails, show full response (no collapse)
CACHE: N/A

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Stream completes       Measure + collapse if needed     Smooth collapse animation
 Click "Show more"      Expand to full height            300ms height transition
 Click "Show less"      Collapse to threshold            300ms height transition
 Short response         No collapse applied              Normal display
 Window resize          Re-measure natural height        Threshold re-evaluated
 Page reload            All responses start expanded     No collapse persisted
 During streaming       No collapse (wait for complete)  Collapse after stream ends

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Response is exactly at the threshold (15 lines exactly)
   Expected: Do not collapse. Only collapse when strictly exceeding threshold.
   Implementation: Strict > comparison, not >=.

2. Scenario: Response contains a large markdown table that renders tall
   Expected: Table is not split mid-row. Cut point moves to before the table
   if the table starts within the threshold zone.
   Implementation: findCutPoint() checks for <table> elements near the boundary.

3. Scenario: Response contains a code block near the threshold boundary
   Expected: Code block is not split. Cut point moves to before the code block.
   Implementation: findCutPoint() checks for <pre> and <code> elements.

4. Scenario: User expands a response, then the chat scrolls due to new message
   Expected: Expanded state preserved. Scroll position adjusts naturally.
   Implementation: Collapse state is per-element, independent of scroll.

5. Scenario: Images or embedded content in response change rendered height
   Expected: Natural height re-measured after images load.
   Implementation: MutationObserver or image onload triggers re-measurement.

6. Scenario: Very long response (200+ lines) expanded, then user scrolls up
   Expected: Smooth scrolling. No jank. The response element handles overflow
   properly even at extreme heights.
   Implementation: No max-height when expanded (set to naturalHeight or "none").

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering)
ENABLES: Compact chat history for long sessions
ENHANCES: #033 (Chat Panel), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (pure CSS + JS)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Responses exceeding 15 rendered lines are collapsed
[ ] "Show more" button expands to full height
[ ] "Show less" button re-collapses
[ ] Cut point respects paragraph, table, and code block boundaries
[ ] Short responses are never collapsed
[ ] Multiple collapsed responses have independent state
[ ] Collapse state is ephemeral (resets on reload)

VISUAL:
[ ] Gradient fade at bottom of collapsed response
[ ] Smooth 300ms height transition on toggle
[ ] Toggle button centered with dot matrix chevron
[ ] Hover state with gold accent on button

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Height measurement under 5ms per response
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Toggle button labeled, expanded state announced, keyboard accessible
EDGE CASES: [ ] Tables not split, code blocks not split, images re-measured

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified with various response lengths
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Compatible with streaming and markdown rendering

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-collapse.js, athena-collapse.css
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Generate long response, verify collapse triggers
4. TEST: Expand/collapse multiple times rapidly
5. TEST: Response with table near threshold boundary
6. CHECK: No layout shift on expand, no jank on scroll
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty - feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P1 Phase B. Improves chat usability for long sessions. 8 hours.


================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #046 — TYPING INDICATOR                                           ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       046
Feature Name:     Typing Indicator
Category:         Core Infrastructure
Priority:         P0
Phase:            A
Complexity:       Low
Estimated Effort: 4 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The typing indicator provides visual feedback to the researcher while the AI
is processing their message and before the first token of the response arrives.
This covers the latency gap between when the user sends a message and when the
streaming response begins - a period that can be 1 to 5 seconds depending on
graph complexity, context assembly time, and Groq API response latency.

Without a typing indicator, the researcher sees nothing after clicking send.
They wonder if the message was received, if the system is broken, or if they
should resend. The typing indicator eliminates this uncertainty by showing
an animated element in the chat panel that communicates "the AI is thinking."

The indicator uses the Arivu dot matrix visual language. Three dots arranged
horizontally animate in a staggered wave pattern - each dot scales up and
brightens in sequence, creating a flowing "thinking" animation. The dots use
the accent-gold color (var(--accent-gold), #D4A843) at peak brightness and
the muted text color (var(--text-muted), #64748B) at rest. The animation
cycle is 1.2 seconds with each dot offset by 0.2 seconds.

The indicator appears inside a chat bubble styled identically to an AI response
bubble, positioned at the bottom of the chat history where the next AI message
will appear. This primes the researcher's visual attention for where the
response will materialize. When the first token of the response arrives, the
typing indicator morphs into the beginning of the response bubble - the dots
fade out and the text fades in at the same position, creating a seamless
transition.

The indicator also serves as an implicit promise that the system is healthy.
If the indicator appears but no response follows within 30 seconds, the system
should automatically show a "This is taking longer than expected..." message.
This prevents the researcher from staring at animated dots indefinitely.

The typing indicator respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query. When
reduced motion is active, the dots do not animate but instead display as a
static ellipsis "..." with a subtle opacity pulse (opacity 0.5 to 1.0 over
2 seconds) that conveys activity without motion.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher sends a message in the Athena chat panel.

STEP 1: The researcher types "What is the pruning impact of removing the
BERT paper from this lineage?" and presses Enter. The message appears in
the chat as a user bubble.

STEP 2: Immediately (within 50ms of send), a typing indicator bubble appears
below the user message. Three gold dots animate in a wave pattern:
  ● ○ ○  ->  ○ ● ○  ->  ○ ○ ●  ->  ● ○ ○  (repeating)

STEP 3: The backend assembles the context (loading conversation history,
graph summary, session context), calls the Groq API, and begins streaming.
This takes approximately 2 seconds.

STEP 4: The first token arrives via SSE. The typing indicator dots fade out
(150ms) and the response text fades in at the same position. The transition
is seamless - the chat bubble remains in place, only its content changes.

STEP 5: If the response had taken more than 30 seconds (e.g., due to API
slowness), the dots would have been replaced by "This is taking longer than
expected. Still working..." in muted italic text, reassuring the researcher
that the system has not frozen.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-typing.js          Typing indicator show/hide/timeout logic
    static/css/athena-typing.css        Dot animation, reduced-motion, transition

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Show indicator on send, hide on first token
    static/js/athena-stream.js          Trigger indicator hide when stream starts

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  TypingIndicator class manages the indicator lifecycle:

  1. show() - called by athena-chat.js immediately after message send
  2. The indicator is a <div> with class "athena-typing-indicator" containing
     three <span> elements with class "athena-typing-dot"
  3. The indicator is inserted into the chat messages container at the bottom
  4. Chat auto-scrolls to show the indicator
  5. hide() - called by athena-stream.js when the first SSE event arrives
  6. The hide transition fades dots out and returns the container element
     so the stream renderer can reuse it for the response bubble
  7. setTimeout(30000) starts on show(). If not hidden within 30s, the
     "taking longer" message replaces the dots.

  CSS animations use @keyframes with animation-delay for staggered dots.
  The reduced-motion query replaces keyframe animation with opacity pulse.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  No backend changes. The typing indicator is entirely a frontend timing concern.

CODE PATTERNS:

  class TypingIndicator {
    constructor(chatContainer) { ... }
    show() { ... }
    hide() { ... }          // Returns the bubble element for reuse
    showTimeout() { ... }   // "Taking longer than expected..."
    destroy() { ... }       // Full cleanup
    isVisible() { ... }
  }

API:

  No new API endpoints. Entirely client-side.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TYPING INDICATOR BUBBLE:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │   User: What is the pruning impact of removing BERT?                │
  │                                                                      │
  │   ┌─────────────────┐                                               │
  │   │  ● ○ ○          │  <- AI typing indicator bubble                │
  │   └─────────────────┘                                               │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

DOT ANIMATION SEQUENCE (1.2s cycle):

  Frame 1 (0.0s):   ●  ○  ○     dot1 bright, dot2 dim, dot3 dim
  Frame 2 (0.2s):   ○  ●  ○     dot1 dim, dot2 bright, dot3 dim
  Frame 3 (0.4s):   ○  ○  ●     dot1 dim, dot2 dim, dot3 bright
  Frame 4 (0.6s):   ○  ○  ○     all dim (brief pause)
  Frame 5 (0.8s):   (repeat from frame 1)

DOT SPECIFICATIONS:

  Bright state:  color: var(--accent-gold)  transform: scale(1.2)
  Dim state:     color: var(--text-muted)   transform: scale(1.0)
  Size:          8px diameter
  Spacing:       6px gap between dots
  Bubble:        Same style as AI response bubble (bg-surface, rounded)
  Padding:       12px 16px

TRANSITION TO RESPONSE:

  t=0ms      First token arrives
  t=0-150ms  Dots fade out (opacity 1 -> 0)
  t=100ms    Response text begins fade in (opacity 0 -> 1)
  t=150ms    Dots removed from DOM, response text fully visible

REDUCED MOTION:

  No dot animation. Static "..." text with opacity pulse:
  opacity: 0.5 -> 1.0 -> 0.5 (2s cycle, ease-in-out)

TIMEOUT STATE (after 30 seconds):

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  This is taking longer than expected. Still working...│
  │  ◐                                                    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Text: var(--text-muted), font-style: italic
  ◐ symbol: half-filled dot, pulses slowly

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User clicks send
       |
       v
  athena-chat.js: sendMessage()
       |
       ├── Append user message bubble to chat
       |
       └── typingIndicator.show()
                |
                ├── Create indicator bubble in chat container
                ├── Start dot animation
                ├── Start 30s timeout timer
                └── Auto-scroll to indicator
       |
       v
  (Backend processes: context assembly, Groq API call)
       |
       v
  athena-stream.js: first SSE event received
       |
       v
  typingIndicator.hide()
       |
       ├── Fade out dots (150ms)
       ├── Clear timeout timer
       ├── Return bubble element for reuse by stream renderer
       └── Stream renderer begins appending tokens to the same bubble

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: None. This feature is entirely client-side.
MODEL: N/A
INPUT: Chat send event (trigger), first SSE event (dismiss)
OUTPUT: Visual indicator element in chat container
FALLBACK: If indicator fails to render, no visible harm - response still streams
CACHE: N/A

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Message sent           Show typing indicator            Dots appear + animate
 First token arrives    Hide indicator, show response    Dots fade, text fades in
 30s timeout            Show "taking longer" message     Dots -> italic text
 Error before response  Hide indicator, show error       Dots -> error message
 User sends stop (#041) Hide indicator if no response    Dots removed
 Reduced motion pref    Static dots with opacity pulse   No animation, pulse only
 Multiple rapid sends   Only one indicator at a time     Previous hides first

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: First token arrives in under 100ms (very fast response)
   Expected: Indicator appears briefly then transitions. No visual glitch.
   Implementation: show() and hide() are safe to call in rapid succession.

2. Scenario: Network error before any response
   Expected: Indicator replaced with error message after connection timeout.
   Implementation: Stream error handler calls typingIndicator.destroy() and
   shows error bubble in its place.

3. Scenario: User sends another message while indicator is showing
   Expected: Should not happen (send button disabled during streaming).
   But if it does, current indicator is destroyed and a new one appears.
   Implementation: show() calls destroy() first if indicator already visible.

4. Scenario: Chat panel is scrolled up when indicator appears
   Expected: Auto-scroll to bottom to show indicator. User can scroll up
   during the wait, but indicator remains at the bottom.
   Implementation: show() calls scrollToBottom() on chat container.

5. Scenario: Browser tab is backgrounded during indicator display
   Expected: Animation pauses (browser behavior). Resumes when tab returns.
   No accumulated animation frames or glitches.
   Implementation: CSS animations naturally pause in background tabs.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #033 (Chat Panel), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: Visual feedback for all AI interactions
ENHANCES: User confidence during wait times
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (pure CSS animation + JS timing)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Typing indicator appears within 50ms of message send
[ ] Indicator hides when first streaming token arrives
[ ] Seamless transition from dots to response text
[ ] 30-second timeout shows "taking longer" message
[ ] Only one indicator visible at a time
[ ] Indicator destroyed on stream error

VISUAL:
[ ] Three gold dots with staggered wave animation
[ ] 1.2s animation cycle, 0.2s stagger per dot
[ ] Indicator bubble matches AI response bubble style
[ ] Gradient transition from dots to response text
[ ] Reduced motion: static dots with opacity pulse

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Indicator render under 5ms, zero layout thrash
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] aria-live="polite" on indicator, "AI is thinking" label
EDGE CASES: [ ] Fast response, network error, rapid send all handled

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Compatible with streaming and stop button

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-typing.js, athena-typing.css
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: Send message, verify indicator appears within 50ms
4. TEST: Verify smooth transition to response text
5. TEST: Force 30s timeout, verify timeout message
6. CHECK: Reduced motion fallback, screen reader announcement
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24

================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #047 — NEW CHAT / CLEAR CONVERSATION                              ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       047
Feature Name:     New Chat / Clear Conversation
Category:         Chat Controls
Priority:         P1
Phase:            A
Complexity:       Medium
Estimated Effort: 8 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The New Chat / Clear Conversation feature allows a researcher to reset the
Athena chat panel to a fresh state, discarding the current conversation history
and starting a new dialogue. This is essential for researchers who want to
begin a new line of inquiry without the AI's responses being influenced by
previous conversational context about a different topic or graph.

The feature provides two distinct actions with different scopes. "New Chat"
creates a fresh conversation context while keeping the same session and graph.
The chat history is archived (not deleted) and the AI starts with no prior
conversation memory, but the graph context and session metadata remain intact.
"Clear Conversation" is more aggressive - it discards the conversation without
archiving and resets the chat panel to its initial welcome state.

The New Chat action is triggered by a button in the chat panel header - a
minimal icon (dot matrix pattern suggesting a fresh page) positioned at the
top-right corner of the panel. Clicking it opens a confirmation dialog because
the action is destructive to current context. The dialog uses the Arivu visual
language: dark surface background, gold accent on the confirm button, clear
description of what will be lost.

On confirmation, the current chat_history records for this session are marked
with an archived_at timestamp (not deleted, preserving audit trail). The chat
panel scrolls to top and shows the initial greeting message: "Welcome back.
You are exploring [graph title]. What would you like to understand?" The
multi-turn context manager resets its state, and the next message will be
processed with fresh history.

The conversation that was just cleared is saved to the conversation history
sidebar (#048) if that feature is implemented, allowing the researcher to
review or resume previous conversations. If #048 is not yet available, the
archived conversation is simply preserved in the database for future retrieval.

The Clear Conversation action is available in a dropdown menu (three-dot menu)
next to the New Chat button. It shows a more prominent warning dialog since
it does not archive. This action permanently removes chat_history rows for
the current session. It is intended for cases where the researcher wants to
truly start fresh with no trace of the previous conversation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher wants to start a fresh conversation.

STEP 1: The researcher has been discussing the pruning impact of various papers
in the Attention graph for 15 turns. Now they want to ask about mutation type
distributions without the AI referencing the pruning discussion.

STEP 2: The researcher clicks the "New Chat" icon (fresh-page dot matrix icon)
in the top-right corner of the chat panel header.

STEP 3: A confirmation dialog slides down from the panel header:
  "Start a new conversation? Your current conversation (15 messages) will be
  saved and accessible from the conversation history. The AI will start fresh
  with no memory of this discussion."
  [Cancel]  [New Chat]

STEP 4: The researcher clicks "New Chat". The chat panel smoothly transitions:
the existing messages fade out (200ms), the panel scrolls to top, and the
welcome message fades in: "Welcome back. You are exploring 'Attention Is All
You Need' (152 nodes). What would you like to understand?"

STEP 5: The researcher types their new question. The AI responds with no
reference to the previous pruning discussion. Context is fresh.

STEP 6: Later, the researcher accesses the conversation history sidebar (#048)
and can see their archived pruning discussion available for review or resumption.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-new-chat.js        New Chat/Clear logic, confirmation dialog
    static/css/athena-new-chat.css      Dialog styles, button styles, transitions

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            Reset chat container, show welcome message
    static/js/athena-context.js         Reset context state on new chat
    app.py                              POST /api/chat/archive, DELETE /api/chat/history
    backend/chat_guide.py               Reset conversation context on archive
    backend/db.py                       Archive and delete queries for chat_history

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  NewChatManager class handles both "New Chat" and "Clear Conversation" flows:

  1. New Chat button click -> show confirmation dialog
  2. On confirm: POST /api/chat/archive (archives current history)
  3. Reset client-side state: clear message container, reset context manager
  4. Show welcome message with current graph context
  5. Clear Conversation (from dropdown): more aggressive warning dialog
  6. On confirm: DELETE /api/chat/history (permanently removes)
  7. Same client-side reset as New Chat

  The confirmation dialog is a custom component (not browser confirm()) to
  maintain the Arivu visual language. It overlays the chat panel, not the
  full page.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  POST /api/chat/archive
    - Sets archived_at = NOW() on all chat_history rows for session_id
    - Creates a conversation_archive record with summary metadata
    - Returns { archived: true, message_count: N }

  DELETE /api/chat/history
    - Permanently deletes chat_history rows for session_id
    - Returns { cleared: true }

  Both endpoints validate session_id ownership. Neither affects the graph
  state, session metadata, or action_log entries.

CODE PATTERNS:

  class NewChatManager {
    constructor(chatInterface, contextManager) { ... }
    showNewChatDialog() { ... }
    showClearDialog() { ... }
    onNewChatConfirm() { ... }
    onClearConfirm() { ... }
    archiveConversation() { ... }  // POST /api/chat/archive
    clearConversation() { ... }    // DELETE /api/chat/history
    resetChatPanel() { ... }
    showWelcomeMessage() { ... }
  }

API:

  POST /api/chat/archive
    Request: { session_id }
    Response: { archived: true, message_count: 15, archive_id: "abc123" }

  DELETE /api/chat/history
    Request: { session_id }
    Response: { cleared: true }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CHAT PANEL HEADER WITH NEW CHAT BUTTON:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena Research Guide                          [+]  [...]          │
  │                                                  ^     ^            │
  │                                            New Chat  Menu           │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

NEW CHAT ICON (dot matrix fresh page):

  ○ ○ ○
  ○   ○     16x16px, color: var(--text-secondary)
  ○ ○ ○     hover: var(--accent-gold)

CONFIRMATION DIALOG:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                      │
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
  │  │                                                                │  │
  │  │  ○ Start a new conversation?                                  │  │
  │  │                                                                │  │
  │  │  Your current conversation (15 messages) will be saved and    │  │
  │  │  accessible from the conversation history. The AI will start  │  │
  │  │  fresh with no memory of this discussion.                     │  │
  │  │                                                                │  │
  │  │  Graph context (Attention Is All You Need, 152 nodes) will    │  │
  │  │  be preserved.                                                │  │
  │  │                                                                │  │
  │  │                              ┌────────┐  ┌──────────────┐     │  │
  │  │                              │ Cancel │  │  ● New Chat  │     │  │
  │  │                              └────────┘  └──────────────┘     │  │
  │  │                                                                │  │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Dialog: bg var(--bg-elevated), border 1px solid var(--bg-surface)
  Cancel: ghost button, text-secondary
  New Chat: solid button, bg var(--accent-gold), text var(--bg-primary)

WELCOME STATE AFTER RESET:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Athena Research Guide                          [+]  [...]          │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │                                                                      │
  │                                                                      │
  │         ○ ○ ○ ○ ○                                                    │
  │         ○       ○                                                    │
  │         ○ ○ ○ ○ ○   Welcome back.                                    │
  │                                                                      │
  │         You are exploring "Attention Is All You Need"                │
  │         (152 nodes, 203 edges)                                       │
  │                                                                      │
  │         What would you like to understand?                           │
  │                                                                      │
  │                                                                      │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  User clicks "New Chat" button
       |
       v
  NewChatManager.showNewChatDialog()
       |
       v
  User clicks "New Chat" in dialog
       |
       v
  archiveConversation()
       |
       v
  POST /api/chat/archive { session_id }
       |
       v
  Backend: UPDATE chat_history SET archived_at = NOW()
           WHERE session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL
       |
       v
  Response: { archived: true, message_count: 15 }
       |
       v
  resetChatPanel()
       |
       ├── Clear message container DOM
       ├── contextManager.reset() -- clear conversation history state
       ├── Fade out old messages (200ms)
       └── showWelcomeMessage() -- fade in welcome state
              |
              v
  Chat panel ready for new conversation
  (Graph context preserved, history context cleared)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROVIDER: None directly. Affects context sent to Groq via ContextManager.
MODEL: N/A
INPUT: Archive/clear triggers reset of conversation history layer in context
OUTPUT: Next LLM call receives empty history (fresh start) but same graph context
FALLBACK: If archive API fails, show error and do not reset chat panel
CACHE: Archived conversations remain in DB for #048 retrieval

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 Trigger               Action                           Visual Feedback
 ───────────────────── ──────────────────────────────── ─────────────────────────
 Click New Chat icon    Show confirmation dialog         Dialog slides down
 Confirm New Chat       Archive + reset panel            Messages fade, welcome in
 Cancel dialog          Close dialog, no change          Dialog slides up
 Click Clear (menu)     Show clear warning dialog        Red-accented warning
 Confirm Clear          Delete + reset panel             Messages fade, welcome in
 Escape key on dialog   Close dialog                     Same as Cancel
 During streaming       New Chat disabled                Button grayed out
 Empty conversation     New Chat is a no-op              Button still clickable but dialog says "No conversation to archive"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User clicks New Chat while AI is streaming a response
   Expected: New Chat button is disabled during streaming. User must wait
   for stream to complete or stop it first (#041).
   Implementation: NewChatManager checks streaming state, disables button.

2. Scenario: Archive API call fails (network error)
   Expected: Error toast shown. Chat panel NOT reset. Conversation preserved.
   Implementation: archiveConversation() wrapped in try/catch, rejects on error.

3. Scenario: User clicks New Chat on an already-empty conversation (just opened)
   Expected: No-op with friendly message: "No conversation to archive."
   Implementation: Check message count before showing confirmation dialog.

4. Scenario: User rapidly clicks New Chat, Cancel, New Chat
   Expected: Each click is handled correctly. No double-archive.
   Implementation: Dialog has a debounce. Confirm button disabled during API call.

5. Scenario: Two tabs share session, one archives conversation
   Expected: Other tab continues showing old messages but next send will
   use fresh context. Tab refresh will show welcome state.
   Implementation: Archive is server-side. Client state diverges until refresh.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #033 (Chat Panel), #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: #048 (Conversation History Sidebar)
ENHANCES: Fresh exploration workflows, context management
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] New Chat button visible in chat panel header
[ ] Confirmation dialog shows message count and graph context
[ ] Archive preserves conversation in DB with archived_at timestamp
[ ] Chat panel resets to welcome state after confirm
[ ] AI has no memory of previous conversation after reset
[ ] Graph context preserved after reset
[ ] Clear Conversation permanently deletes history
[ ] Disabled during active streaming

VISUAL:
[ ] Dot matrix fresh-page icon for New Chat button
[ ] Confirmation dialog with Arivu visual language
[ ] Smooth fade-out of old messages, fade-in of welcome
[ ] Welcome state shows current graph info
[ ] Gold accent on confirm button

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Archive API call under 500ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Dialog focus-trapped, Escape closes, confirm button labeled
EDGE CASES: [ ] API failure handled, empty conversation handled, streaming blocked

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Archive + reset verified, Clear verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Compatible with #044 context reset, #048 history retrieval

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-new-chat.js, archive endpoint, context_manager.py
2. COMPARE criteria against implementation
3. TEST: New Chat flow end-to-end (archive + reset + fresh AI response)
4. TEST: Clear flow end-to-end (delete + reset)
5. TEST: API failure during archive
6. CHECK: Chat history DB state after each action
7. ASSUME BROKEN until all criteria green
8. Minimum 1 fix per round. Log below.

================================================================================
================================================================================
==  FEATURE #048 — CONVERSATION HISTORY SIDEBAR                               ==
================================================================================
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METADATA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Feature ID:       048
Feature Name:     Conversation History Sidebar
Category:         Conversation Mgmt
Priority:         P1
Phase:            B
Complexity:       High
Estimated Effort: 16 hours
Dependencies:     #033 (Chat Panel), #044 (Multi-Turn Context), #047 (New Chat)
Introduced:       2026-03-24
Status:           NOT_STARTED
Author:           Athena Core Team

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Conversation History Sidebar provides a navigable list of all previous
conversations the researcher has had in the Athena chat panel within their
current session and across sessions (if authenticated). It slides out from
the left edge of the chat panel when activated, overlaying the chat content
temporarily, and allows the researcher to browse, search, resume, or delete
past conversations.

Each conversation entry in the sidebar displays: a title (auto-generated from
the first user message, truncated to 50 characters), the date and time of the
conversation, the number of message turns, the graph that was active during
the conversation (e.g., "Attention Is All You Need"), and a preview of the
last exchange. The entries are sorted by most recent first.

Conversations are grouped by time period: "Today", "Yesterday", "This Week",
"Earlier". Each group has a subtle heading using the muted text color. The
sidebar includes a search field at the top that filters conversations by
keyword match against message content, allowing the researcher to find a
specific discussion about a particular paper, mutation type, or analysis result.

Clicking a conversation entry loads that conversation's history back into the
chat panel. If the conversation was archived (via #047 New Chat), it is
restored as the active conversation. The graph context associated with that
conversation is also restored if it still exists. If the researcher is in
the middle of a current conversation, loading a historical one triggers the
same archive flow as #047 (with confirmation).

The sidebar has a dot matrix visual identity. Each conversation entry has a
small dot matrix icon indicating the graph type: a filled dot pattern for
conversations with active graphs, an open dot pattern for general conversations
without graph context. The sidebar background uses the elevated surface color
with a subtle left border accent in gold.

For anonymous users, conversation history is limited to the current browser
session (stored via session_id in the database). For authenticated users
(Phase 6+), history persists across sessions, devices, and browsers, tied
to the user account.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER: Researcher wants to access a previous conversation.

STEP 1: The researcher clicks the history icon (stacked lines dot matrix icon)
in the chat panel header, to the left of the New Chat button.

STEP 2: The sidebar slides in from the left, overlaying the chat content. It
shows a search field at the top and a list of previous conversations grouped
by time period:

  Today
    "What is the pruning impact of BERT?" - 15 turns - Attention graph
    "Show mutation type distribution" - 8 turns - BERT graph

  Yesterday
    "Explain the hybridization edges" - 12 turns - AlexNet graph

STEP 3: The researcher types "pruning" in the search field. The list filters
to show only conversations containing the word "pruning" in their messages.

STEP 4: The researcher clicks the "What is the pruning impact of BERT?"
conversation. A confirmation dialog asks if they want to archive the current
conversation and switch. They confirm.

STEP 5: The current conversation is archived. The selected historical
conversation loads into the chat panel with all its messages visible. The
sidebar slides closed. The researcher can continue the conversation where
they left off, with full multi-turn context restored.

STEP 6: The researcher clicks the history icon again to close the sidebar
without selecting anything. The sidebar slides back out.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:

  NEW:
    static/js/athena-history.js         History sidebar logic, search, load
    static/css/athena-history.css       Sidebar slide, entry cards, search, groups

  MODIFIED:
    static/js/athena-chat.js            History button in header, load conversation
    static/js/athena-context.js         Restore context from historical conversation
    app.py                              GET /api/chat/conversations, GET /api/chat/conversation/:id
    backend/db.py                       Conversation listing and retrieval queries

FRONTEND ARCHITECTURE:

  HistorySidebar class manages the sidebar lifecycle:

  1. Toggle button in chat header opens/closes sidebar
  2. On open: fetch conversation list from GET /api/chat/conversations
  3. Render grouped entries with metadata
  4. Search field filters entries client-side (no API call per keystroke)
  5. Click entry: confirm if current conversation exists, archive, then load
  6. Load: fetch full conversation from GET /api/chat/conversation/:id
  7. Replace chat panel content with historical messages
  8. Restore context manager state for the loaded conversation

  The sidebar is a <div> that slides in via CSS transform: translateX().
  It sits inside the chat panel container and overlays the message area.
  A semi-transparent backdrop appears behind the sidebar to indicate it
  is a modal overlay.

BACKEND ARCHITECTURE:

  GET /api/chat/conversations?session_id=X
    Returns a list of conversation summaries for the session:
    [
      {
        conversation_id: "abc",
        title: "What is the pruning impact...",
        message_count: 15,
        graph_title: "Attention Is All You Need",
        graph_id: "...",
        first_message_at: "2026-03-24T10:00:00Z",
        last_message_at: "2026-03-24T10:30:00Z",
        preview: "Last exchange preview text..."
      }
    ]

  Conversations are derived from chat_history grouped by conversation
  boundaries (marked by archived_at timestamps from #047). The current
  active conversation (archived_at IS NULL) is included as the first entry.

  GET /api/chat/conversation/:conversation_id
    Returns all messages for a specific conversation:
    { messages: [ { role: "user"|"assistant", content: "...", timestamp: "..." } ] }

CODE PATTERNS:

  class HistorySidebar {
    constructor(chatInterface, contextManager) { ... }
    toggle() { ... }
    open() { ... }
    close() { ... }
    fetchConversations() { ... }
    renderConversationList(conversations) { ... }
    groupByTimePeriod(conversations) { ... }
    searchConversations(query) { ... }
    onConversationClick(conversationId) { ... }
    loadConversation(conversationId) { ... }
    deleteConversation(conversationId) { ... }
  }

API:

  GET /api/chat/conversations?session_id=X
    Response: { conversations: [ ... ] }

  GET /api/chat/conversation/:id?session_id=X
    Response: { messages: [ ... ], graph_id: "...", graph_title: "..." }

  DELETE /api/chat/conversation/:id?session_id=X
    Response: { deleted: true }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

HISTORY SIDEBAR (slid open):

  │  ┌───────────────────┐  │                                            │
  │  │ Search...         │  │  (chat content behind, dimmed)             │
  │  └───────────────────┘  │                                            │
  │                         │                                            │
  │  TODAY                  │                                            │

  │  ┌───────────────────┐  │                                            │
  │  │ ● What is the     │  │                                            │

  │                         │                                            │

  │  ─────────────────────  │                                            │
  │  ┌───────────────────┐  │                                            │

  │  │   hybridization   │  │                                            │
  │  │   edges           │  │                                            │

  Sidebar width: 280px
