FEATURE #054: THINKING/REASONING DISPLAY
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #057 (Progress Indicators)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Thinking/Reasoning Display makes Athena's internal process visible to the
researcher. When Athena receives a complex question like "How did attention
mechanisms evolve from Bahdanau to Vaswani?" it does not simply generate an
answer. It performs multiple internal steps: identifying relevant papers in the
graph, computing relationship paths between nodes, evaluating mutation_type
chains along those paths, checking PageRank importance scores, and constructing
a narrative. The Thinking Display shows these intermediate steps to the user
in real time, creating transparency and building trust.

This is not a cosmetic feature. Arivu's core promise is making research
intelligence legible. If Athena claims "The path from Bahdanau (2014) to
Vaswani (2017) passes through three generalization mutations," the researcher
needs to see how that conclusion was reached. The Thinking Display shows:
"Searching graph for path between paper_id_A and paper_id_B... Found 2 paths
of length 3... Evaluating mutation_type chain: adoption, generalization,
generalization... Computing confidence: 0.72 (MEDIUM tier)..."

The display appears as a collapsible panel above the response. By default it
is collapsed after the response completes (showing a summary like "Analyzed
12 papers, 3 paths, 2 mutation chains"). The user can expand it to see the
full reasoning trace. During streaming, the thinking panel is open and
actively updating, giving the researcher the experience of watching an expert
work through a problem.

Thinking steps are categorized into types: SEARCH (querying the graph),
COMPUTE (running algorithms like PageRank or BFS pruning), CLASSIFY
(evaluating mutation_type or citation_intent), REASON (LLM-based inference),
and VALIDATE (cross-checking claims against data). Each step type has a
distinct icon and color: SEARCH uses accent-blue, COMPUTE uses accent-teal,
CLASSIFY uses accent-gold, REASON uses text-primary, VALIDATE uses success
green.

The backend sends thinking events through the SSE stream using the 'meta'
event type. Each thinking event includes a step_type, description, duration,
and optional data payload. The frontend renders these in the thinking panel
as they arrive, creating a live feed of Athena's reasoning process.

The feature draws from the Arivu philosophy that research tools should not be
black boxes. A DNA cluster analysis involves hierarchical clustering with
average and complete linkage methods across five thresholds. The researcher
should be able to see "Running consensus clustering... 2 methods x 5
thresholds = 10 runs... Building co-occurrence matrix... Final cluster count:
3..." rather than just receiving a result.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena begins processing any query that involves multi-step reasoning. The
thinking panel opens automatically at the start of processing.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What would happen if we removed BERT from this lineage?" Athena
   begins processing. The thinking panel opens above the response area.
2. Thinking step 1 appears: "[SEARCH] Locating BERT (paper_id: df2b0e26...) in
   graph... found. 23 outgoing edges, 8 incoming."
3. Thinking step 2: "[COMPUTE] Running BFS pruning from BERT node... 47 papers
   depend on BERT... computing cascade levels..."
4. Thinking step 3: "[COMPUTE] Pruning impact: 31.0% of graph (47/152 nodes
   would collapse). 3 BFS levels affected."
5. Thinking step 4: "[REASON] Generating narrative for pruning result... using
   Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile..."
6. Response text begins streaming below the thinking panel. The thinking panel
   collapses to a summary: "Analyzed 152 nodes, pruned 47, 3 BFS levels."
7. User clicks the collapsed thinking summary to expand the full trace.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Response visible below collapsed thinking panel. User can expand/collapse the
thinking trace at any time. Thinking data stored with message in chat_history.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-thinking.js: Thinking panel renderer, step categorization, collapse logic.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Routes 'meta' SSE events to thinking panel.
- static/css/athena-thinking.css: Thinking panel styles, step type colors, collapse animation.
- app.py: Thinking events emitted during graph queries.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class ThinkingDisplay {
    constructor(containerEl) {
      this.container = containerEl;
      this.steps = [];
      this.collapsed = false;
    }

    addStep(step) {
      this.steps.push(step);
      const stepEl = document.createElement('div');
      stepEl.className = `thinking-step thinking-${step.type.toLowerCase()}`;
      stepEl.innerHTML = `
        <span class="thinking-icon">${this.iconFor(step.type)}</span>
        <span class="thinking-text">${step.description}</span>
        <span class="thinking-duration">${step.duration_ms}ms</span>
      `;
      this.container.appendChild(stepEl);
      stepEl.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' });
    }

    collapse() {
      this.collapsed = true;
      this.container.classList.add('collapsed');
      this.summaryEl.textContent = this.buildSummary();
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In streaming response generator
  def generate_with_thinking(query, graph_data, session_id):
      yield f"event: meta\ndata: {json.dumps({'type': 'thinking', 'step_type': 'SEARCH', 'description': 'Locating papers in graph...', 'duration_ms': 0})}\n\n"

      papers = find_relevant_papers(query, graph_data)
      yield f"event: meta\ndata: {json.dumps({'type': 'thinking', 'step_type': 'SEARCH', 'description': f'Found {len(papers)} relevant papers', 'duration_ms': elapsed})}\n\n"

      if needs_pruning(query):
          yield f"event: meta\ndata: {json.dumps({'type': 'thinking', 'step_type': 'COMPUTE', 'description': 'Computing pruning impact via BFS...', 'duration_ms': 0})}\n\n"
          result = compute_pruning_result(graph_data, target_id)
          yield f"event: meta\ndata: {json.dumps({'type': 'thinking', 'step_type': 'COMPUTE', 'description': f'Pruning impact: {result.impact_pct}%', 'duration_ms': elapsed})}\n\n"

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.thinking-panel {
  background: var(--bg-surface); border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-teal);
  padding: 8px 12px; margin-bottom: 8px; border-radius: 4px;
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px;
  max-height: 200px; overflow-y: auto;
  transition: max-height 300ms ease;
}
.thinking-panel.collapsed { max-height: 32px; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; }
.thinking-step { display: flex; gap: 8px; padding: 2px 0; align-items: center; }
.thinking-SEARCH .thinking-icon { color: var(--accent-blue); }
.thinking-COMPUTE .thinking-icon { color: var(--accent-teal); }
.thinking-CLASSIFY .thinking-icon { color: var(--accent-gold); }
.thinking-REASON .thinking-icon { color: var(--text-primary); }
.thinking-VALIDATE .thinking-icon { color: var(--success); }
```

API CONTRACTS:
  SSE event type: meta
  Data: { "type": "thinking", "step_type": "SEARCH|COMPUTE|CLASSIFY|REASON|VALIDATE",
           "description": "...", "duration_ms": 42, "data": {} }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Thinking Panel Active (During Processing)

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  [SEARCH] Locating BERT in graph...    2ms|
  |  [SEARCH] Found. 23 out, 8 in edges   5ms|
  |  [COMPUTE] Running BFS pruning...     0ms |
  |  [COMPUTE] Impact: 31.0% (47 nodes) 120ms|
  |  [REASON] Generating narrative...      0ms|
  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena: If BERT were removed from     |
  |  this lineage, 31% of the graph would  |
  |  collapse...                            |
  +-------------------------------------------+

STATE: Thinking Panel Collapsed (After Completion)

  +-------------------------------------------+
  | ○ Analyzed 152 nodes, pruned 47, 3 levels |
  | ----------------------------------------- |
  |  Athena: If BERT were removed from this   |
  |  lineage, 31% of the graph would collapse.|
  |  The cascade affects 47 papers across 3   |
  |  BFS levels...                            |
  +-------------------------------------------+

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User sends complex query
  -> Backend begins processing
  -> Each processing step emits SSE 'meta' event with thinking data
  -> Frontend ThinkingDisplay.addStep() renders each step in real time
  -> Steps scroll into view as they arrive
  -> When response streaming begins, thinking panel auto-collapses
  -> Summary line shows: papers analyzed, computations run, time elapsed
  -> User can click to expand full trace
  -> Thinking data stored in chat_history message metadata

DATA STORED: Thinking steps array in chat_history message metadata
DATA CACHED: NONE (steps are transient, stored once with message)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (REASON steps use LLM, others do not)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant (for step summaries), llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for actual reasoning)
STAGE: Pre-generation (thinking steps emitted before main response generation)

PROMPT SECTION: NOT APPLICABLE (thinking steps are generated by the backend pipeline, not by the LLM)

The LLM is used only for the REASON step type, where it generates the actual
response content. The SEARCH, COMPUTE, CLASSIFY, and VALIDATE steps are
produced by deterministic code (graph traversal, PageRank computation,
mutation_type lookup, data validation).

FALLBACK: If thinking steps fail to emit, response still generates normally.
Thinking panel simply does not appear.

TOKEN BUDGET: 0 extra tokens (thinking steps are metadata, not LLM output)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Thinking panel       | Auto-open   | Opens when first thinking step arrives      |
| Thinking panel       | Auto-close  | Collapses when response streaming starts    |
| Collapsed summary    | Click       | Expands to show full thinking trace         |
| Expanded panel       | Click header| Collapses back to summary                   |
| Step with data       | Hover       | Shows additional data payload in tooltip    |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Panel expand/collapse 300ms ease. Steps fade-in 100ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Panel aria-expanded state, steps role="log", step descriptions readable by screen reader.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Simple query that needs no multi-step reasoning
   Expected: No thinking panel. Response streams directly.
   Implementation: Only emit thinking events for complex query types.

2. Scenario: Thinking step takes very long (10+ seconds for BFS on large graph)
   Expected: Step shows elapsed timer updating every second.
   Implementation: Duration_ms updates via subsequent meta events.

3. Scenario: 50+ thinking steps (very complex query)
   Expected: Panel scrolls. Oldest steps scroll up. Maximum 200px height.
   Implementation: overflow-y: auto with max-height.

4. Scenario: Thinking step contains an error (computation failed)
   Expected: Step shown in danger-red with error description. Processing continues.
   Implementation: step_type: 'ERROR' with red color.

5. Scenario: User collapses thinking panel during active thinking
   Expected: Steps continue accumulating. Summary updates. Panel stays collapsed.
   Implementation: Track collapsed state, update summary text.

6. Scenario: Screen reader user
   Expected: Steps announced as they arrive via aria-live="polite" region.
   Implementation: Thinking panel is an aria-live region.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering)
ENABLES: #057 (Progress Indicators -- shares meta event infrastructure)
ENHANCES: All complex query features (pruning, DNA analysis, gap finding)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

[ ] Steps appear in real time during processing
[ ] Panel auto-collapses when response streaming begins
[ ] Collapsed summary shows accurate counts
[ ] Expand/collapse toggle works
[ ] Thinking data stored in chat_history metadata

VISUAL:

[ ] JetBrains Mono font for thinking text
[ ] Panel border-left teal accent
[ ] Smooth expand/collapse animation

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Thinking step rendering < 5ms per step
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] aria-expanded, aria-live region, screen reader announces steps
EDGE CASES: [ ] No panel for simple queries, long steps, 50+ steps, error steps

VERIFICATION:

[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

FEATURE #055: MULTI-MODAL RESPONSES
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 20
OVERLAPS WITH:   #058 (Paper Citation Card), #059 (Mini-Graph), #060 (Data Table)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Multi-Modal Responses is the orchestration layer that allows Athena to compose
responses from multiple content types within a single message. Rather than
being limited to plain text, Athena can interleave prose paragraphs with
structured blocks: Paper Citation Cards showing paper metadata with PageRank
scores, inline mini-graphs highlighting specific subgraphs, data tables
comparing mutation_type distributions, timeline visualizations of publication
dates, and statistical dashboards with pruning impact metrics.

This is the architectural foundation that makes Athena a research intelligence
interface rather than a text chatbot. When a researcher asks "Compare the
influence of BERT and GPT-2," Athena's response should contain: a prose
paragraph explaining the comparison, a Comparison Card block showing key
metrics side-by-side (citation_count, PageRank, pruning_impact, DNA cluster
membership), a mini-graph highlighting the two nodes and their shared
ancestors, and a data table listing the mutation_type distribution of each
paper's outgoing edges.

The block system works through structured delimiters in the LLM output. The
LLM is instructed to use XML-style markers: <PAPER_CARD paper_id="..."/>,
<DATA_TABLE columns="..." rows="..."/>, <MINI_GRAPH nodes="..." highlight="..."/>,
<TIMELINE events="..."/>, <COMPARISON left="..." right="..."/>, and
<STAT_GRID metrics="..."/>. The streaming parser detects these markers,
buffers content until the closing tag, and renders the complete block using
the appropriate block renderer.

Each block type has its own renderer class in athena-blocks.js. Renderers
receive structured data (parsed from the block marker attributes) and produce
DOM elements matching Arivu's design system. Blocks render atomically: the
user sees the block appear complete, not character by character. Between
blocks, prose text continues to stream normally.

The block detection system must handle edge cases: blocks that span multiple
SSE events (buffering required), malformed block markers (render as plain
text), nested blocks (not supported, render outer block only), and blocks
with missing data attributes (render degraded version with available data).

The visual design of blocks follows the dot matrix aesthetic. Each block has
a subtle border using bg-elevated, rounded corners (8px), and a category
label in text-muted at the top-left. Blocks are full-width within the
response column, creating visual anchors that break up the text flow and
draw the eye to structured data.

This feature is the gateway to all other block features (#058 through #067).
It provides the detection, buffering, rendering, and layout infrastructure
that every specific block type depends on. Without this feature, Athena
can only produce text.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena determines that the response would benefit from structured content
blocks based on the query type and available graph data.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Give me a comprehensive overview of this lineage." Athena
   begins streaming text.
2. Prose streams: "This lineage spans 152 papers across 24 years..."
3. Streaming pauses briefly as a block marker is detected. A Paper Card block
   renders showing the seed paper with PageRank, citation_count, and fields.
4. Prose resumes streaming after the block: "The most critical relationship..."
5. A Data Table block renders showing the top 10 edges by mutation_confidence,
   with columns for citing paper, cited paper, mutation_type, and confidence.
6. More prose, then a mini-graph block highlighting the three DNA cluster
   centers with their interconnections.
7. Response completes with a final prose paragraph and quick action buttons.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Full multi-modal response visible. Blocks are interactive (hoverable, clickable
where applicable). All blocks match the Arivu visual design system.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Block registry, detection, buffering, rendering orchestration.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Block marker detection in token stream.
- static/js/blocks/paper-card.js: Paper Card block renderer.
- static/js/blocks/data-table.js: Data Table block renderer.
- static/js/blocks/mini-graph.js: Mini-Graph block renderer.
- static/js/blocks/timeline.js: Timeline block renderer.
- static/js/blocks/comparison.js: Comparison Card block renderer.
- static/js/blocks/stat-grid.js: Stat Dashboard block renderer.
- static/css/athena-blocks.css: Shared block styles, layout.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class BlockRegistry {
    constructor() {
      this.renderers = new Map();
      this.register('PAPER_CARD', PaperCardRenderer);
      this.register('DATA_TABLE', DataTableRenderer);
      this.register('MINI_GRAPH', MiniGraphRenderer);
      this.register('TIMELINE', TimelineRenderer);
      this.register('COMPARISON', ComparisonRenderer);
      this.register('STAT_GRID', StatGridRenderer);
    }

    detect(token) {
      const match = token.match(/<([A-Z_]+)\s/);
      if (match && this.renderers.has(match[1])) return match[1];
      return null;
    }

    render(blockType, data, container) {
      const Renderer = this.renderers.get(blockType);
      if (!Renderer) return this.renderFallback(data, container);
      const renderer = new Renderer(data);
      container.appendChild(renderer.render());
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # Block data is embedded in the LLM prompt context
  # The LLM produces block markers with paper_ids and metric names
  # The backend resolves these to full data before streaming
  # In practice: LLM says <PAPER_CARD paper_id="abc123"/>
  # Backend intercepts, resolves abc123 to full paper data,
  # and emits the block event with resolved data

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-block {
  background: var(--bg-elevated); border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 16px; margin: 12px 0; width: 100%;
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.1);
}
.athena-block .block-label {
  font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted); text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.05em; margin-bottom: 8px;
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
}
.athena-block:hover { border-color: rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.2); }
```

API CONTRACTS:
  SSE event type: block
  Data: { "block_type": "PAPER_CARD", "data": { "paper_id": "...", "title": "...", ... } }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Multi-Modal Response with Blocks

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena: This lineage spans 152 papers    |
  |  across 24 years of research...           |
  |                                           |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  | PAPER CARD                            ||
  |  | Attention Is All You Need (2017)      ||
  |  | PageRank: 0.0847  Cites: 216,247     ||
  |  | Fields: CS, ML   Impact: 31.0%       ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |                                           |
  |  The most critical edges show a pattern   |
  |  of generalization mutations...           |
  |                                           |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  | DATA TABLE                            ||
  |  | Paper A     | Paper B    | Type       ||
  |  | ----------- | ---------- | ---------- ||
  |  | Vaswani '17 | BERT '18   | general.   ||
  |  | Vaswani '17 | GPT-2 '19  | adoption   ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |                                           |
  |  These patterns suggest...                |
  +-------------------------------------------+

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM generates text with block markers interspersed
  -> SSE stream delivers tokens to frontend
  -> athena-stream.js detects block start marker (<BLOCK_TYPE ...)
  -> Buffering mode activates: tokens collected until closing marker
  -> On closing marker: parse attributes into structured data
  -> BlockRegistry.render() called with block type and data
  -> Block DOM element inserted into response container
  -> Streaming resumes for subsequent prose text
  -> On error (malformed marker): buffer flushed as plain text

BLOCK DATA RESOLUTION:
  LLM output: <PAPER_CARD paper_id="abc123"/>
  -> Backend intercepts marker in stream
  -> Resolves paper_id to full paper data from graph
  -> Emits 'block' SSE event with complete data
  -> Frontend renders block with resolved data

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Yes (LLM decides when to insert blocks)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (needs structured output capability)
STAGE: Generation (block markers embedded in response)

PROMPT SECTION: "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" appended to system prompt

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
BLOCK FORMAT RULES:
You may include structured blocks in your response. Available blocks:
- <PAPER_CARD paper_id="..."/> : Show a paper's key stats
- <DATA_TABLE columns="col1,col2" rows="val1,val2;val3,val4"/> : Tabular data
- <MINI_GRAPH nodes="id1,id2,id3" highlight="id1"/> : Subgraph visualization
- <TIMELINE events="year1:event1,year2:event2"/> : Chronological events
- <COMPARISON left="paper_id_1" right="paper_id_2"/> : Side-by-side comparison
- <STAT_GRID metrics="pagerank:0.08,impact:31.0"/> : Key metrics dashboard

Rules:
- Use blocks to present structured data, not as decoration
- Always include prose context before and after blocks
- Use paper_ids from the graph data, never fabricate them
- Maximum 4 blocks per response to maintain readability
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
Prose text interspersed with block markers. Example:
"The lineage centers on <PAPER_CARD paper_id="204e3073..."/> which established
the transformer architecture. Key relationships include:
<DATA_TABLE columns="Source,Target,Type" rows="Vaswani,BERT,generalization;..."/>"

FALLBACK: If LLM produces malformed blocks, render as plain text. No error.

TOKEN BUDGET: ~100 tokens per block marker (6 block types max = 600 tokens)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Block container      | Hover       | Subtle border highlight                     |
| Paper Card           | Click       | Navigate to paper in graph                  |
| Data Table cell      | Click       | Highlight corresponding node/edge in graph  |
| Mini-Graph node      | Click       | Focus node in main graph                    |
| Timeline event       | Click       | Filter graph by year range                  |
| Stat Grid metric     | Hover       | Tooltip with metric explanation             |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Blocks fade-in 200ms on render. Hover border transition 150ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Each block has role="region" with aria-label describing type.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Block marker spans two SSE events (split across chunks)
   Expected: Buffer collects tokens until closing marker in next chunk.
   Implementation: Stateful buffer in stream parser.

2. Scenario: LLM produces nested blocks (block inside block)
   Expected: Outer block rendered. Inner marker treated as plain text.
   Implementation: No recursive block parsing.

3. Scenario: Block references paper_id not in current graph
   Expected: Degraded block with available data. Missing fields show "N/A."
   Implementation: Renderer handles partial data gracefully.

4. Scenario: 5+ blocks in one response (exceeds recommended max of 4)
   Expected: All render correctly. No artificial limit enforced client-side.
   Implementation: Recommendation is for the LLM, not a hard limit.

5. Scenario: Block marker is malformed (missing closing bracket)
   Expected: Buffer timeout after 500ms, flush as plain text.
   Implementation: Buffer timeout prevents infinite buffering.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #038 (Markdown Rendering), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: #058 (Paper Citation Card), #059 (Mini-Graph), #060 (Data Table), #061 (Comparison Card), #062 (Timeline), #063 (Stat Dashboard), #064 (Equation), #065 (Quote)
ENHANCES: Every feature that produces structured output
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Block registry supports all 6 block types
[ ] Block markers detected during streaming
[ ] Buffering works across SSE event boundaries
[ ] Blocks render atomically (complete, not streamed)
[ ] Prose streams normally between blocks
[ ] Malformed markers render as plain text
[ ] Block data resolved from graph before rendering

VISUAL:
[ ] Blocks use bg-elevated background with rounded corners
[ ] Block labels in JetBrains Mono uppercase
[ ] Hover highlights block border
[ ] Consistent 12px vertical margins

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Block rendering < 50ms per block
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Blocks have role="region" and aria-label
EDGE CASES: [ ] Split markers, nested blocks, missing data, buffer timeout

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-blocks.js, all block renderers, stream parser, CSS. 2. COMPARE block marker format against LLM prompt instructions. 3. TEST each block type with real graph data. 4. CHECK buffer behavior when markers span SSE events. 5. VERIFY malformed markers degrade gracefully. 6. BROKEN until all 6 block types render correctly. 7. At least 1 fix per round. 8. Log below.

FEATURE #056: SLASH COMMANDS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Slash Commands
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   #005 (Graph-Aware Commands)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #050 (Rich Input Area), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Slash Commands give researchers direct command access to Athena's capabilities
through a structured input syntax. Instead of typing natural language like
"Show me the pruning impact of removing Vaswani 2017," the researcher can type
"/prune Vaswani 2017" and get the same result with less ambiguity and faster
execution. Slash commands are not a replacement for natural language. They are
a power-user shortcut for researchers who know exactly what they want.

The command system is modeled after Discord and Slack: typing "/" in an empty
input field opens a command palette showing all available commands with
descriptions. As the user types after the slash, the palette filters to
matching commands. Pressing Tab or Enter selects a command. Some commands
accept arguments (paper names, node IDs, metric names) with autocomplete
powered by the current graph data.

The initial command set covers core Arivu operations:

/prune <paper>        Remove paper from history, show cascade animation
/compare <p1> <p2>    Side-by-side comparison of two papers
/dna                  Show DNA cluster analysis for current lineage
/diversity            Show diversity radar for current lineage
/pagerank [paper]     Show PageRank scores (optionally for one paper)
/path <p1> <p2>       Find intellectual path between two papers
/orphans              List orphan ideas with declining trajectories
/gaps                 Show research gap analysis via pgvector search
/export <format>      Export current graph in specified format
/help                 Show all available commands
/clear                Clear current conversation (preserves graph)
/mode <name>          Switch to named mode (teach, debate, socratic)

Each command maps to a backend handler that produces a structured response.
The /prune command, for example, triggers the cascading pruning animation
on the graph and produces a response with pruning statistics. The /dna
command generates a DNA cluster analysis and renders it as a block with a
donut chart. These are not just text responses; they are rich multi-modal
outputs that leverage the block system (#055).

Command autocomplete is graph-aware. When the user types "/prune V", the
autocomplete suggests papers in the current graph starting with "V":
"Vaswani et al. (2017) - Attention Is All You Need." The autocomplete
uses fuzzy matching via pg_trgm on paper titles within the active graph,
ensuring only relevant papers are suggested.

The command palette uses Arivu's dark aesthetic: bg-surface background,
accent-gold for the selected command, JetBrains Mono for command names,
Inter for descriptions. The palette appears above the input area, anchored
to the bottom of the chat, growing upward as more commands match.

Error handling for commands is explicit. If /prune is called without an
argument, Athena responds: "Missing paper argument. Usage: /prune <paper
name or ID>. Available papers in this graph: [list top 5 by PageRank]."
If the argument does not match a paper, fuzzy matching suggests the closest:
"Did you mean: Vaswani et al. (2017)?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User types "/" as the first character in the input area. The command palette
opens immediately.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types "/" in the input area. The command palette opens showing all
   available commands in alphabetical order.
2. User types "pr". The palette filters to show "/prune" and "/path" (both
   start with "p", but "prune" matches "pr").
3. User presses Tab or clicks "/prune". The input area now shows "/prune "
   with a cursor ready for the argument.
4. User types "Vas". Autocomplete shows "Vaswani et al. (2017) - Attention
   Is All You Need" as a suggestion from the current graph.
5. User presses Tab to accept the suggestion. Input area shows:
   "/prune Vaswani et al. (2017)"
6. User presses Enter. The command executes. The cascading pruning animation
   plays on the graph. Athena responds with pruning statistics in a Stat Grid
   block: "Removing Vaswani (2017) collapses 47/152 nodes (31.0%)..."
7. The response includes quick actions: "/compare BERT GPT-2" as a natural
   follow-up.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Command executed. Response visible with structured output. Command stored in
chat_history as a user message with type="command".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-commands.js: Command registry, palette UI, autocomplete, execution.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Intercepts "/" input, routes to command system.
- static/css/athena-commands.css: Palette styles, autocomplete dropdown.
- app.py: POST /api/athena/command endpoint for server-side execution.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class CommandRegistry {
    constructor() {
      this.commands = new Map();
      this.register('prune', {
        description: 'Remove paper from history, show cascade',
        args: [{ name: 'paper', type: 'paper', required: true }],
        handler: this.handlePrune
      });
      this.register('compare', {
        description: 'Side-by-side paper comparison',
        args: [
          { name: 'paper1', type: 'paper', required: true },
          { name: 'paper2', type: 'paper', required: true }
        ],
        handler: this.handleCompare
      });
      // ... all commands registered
    }

    autocomplete(input, graphData) {
      const parts = input.slice(1).split(' ');
      const cmd = parts[0];
      if (parts.length === 1) {
        return this.filterCommands(cmd);
      }
      const command = this.commands.get(cmd);
      if (!command) return [];
      const argIndex = parts.length - 2;
      const arg = command.args[argIndex];
      if (arg && arg.type === 'paper') {
        return this.fuzzyMatchPapers(parts.slice(1).join(' '), graphData);
      }
      return [];
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/command', methods=['POST'])
  def execute_command():
      data = request.json
      cmd = data['command']
      args = data.get('args', {})
      session_id = session.get('session_id')

      if cmd == 'prune':
          result = compute_pruning_result(graph_data, args['paper_id'])
          return jsonify({
              'type': 'prune_result',
              'data': result,
              'response': f"Removing {args['paper_title']} collapses "
                         f"{result['collapsed_count']}/{result['total']} nodes "
                         f"({result['impact_percentage']}%)"
          })
      elif cmd == 'dna':
          profiler = DNAProfiler(graph_data)
          clusters = profiler.analyze()
          return jsonify({'type': 'dna_result', 'data': clusters})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.command-palette {
  position: absolute; bottom: 100%; left: 0; right: 0;
  background: var(--bg-surface); border-radius: 8px 8px 0 0;
  max-height: 300px; overflow-y: auto;
  border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.15);
  box-shadow: 0 -4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
}
.command-item {
  display: flex; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 12px; cursor: pointer;
}
.command-item.selected { background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.1); }
.command-name {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; color: var(--accent-gold);
  min-width: 100px;
}
.command-desc { color: var(--text-secondary); font-size: 13px; }
```

API CONTRACTS:
  POST /api/athena/command
  Body: { "command": "prune", "args": { "paper_id": "...", "paper_title": "..." }, "graph_id": "..." }
  Response 200: { "type": "prune_result", "data": {...}, "response": "..." }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Command Palette Open

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  /compare  Side-by-side paper comparison  |
  |  /dna      DNA cluster analysis           |
  |  /diversity Diversity radar               |
  |  /export   Export graph in format          |
  |  /gaps     Research gap analysis           |
  |  /help     Show all commands               |
  |  /mode     Switch interaction mode         |
  |  /orphans  Orphan ideas analysis           |
  |  /pagerank PageRank scores                 |
  |  /path     Find path between papers        |
  | >/prune    Remove paper, show cascade      |
  | ----------------------------------------- |
  | [/pr█                              ] [->] |
  +-------------------------------------------+

STATE: Paper Autocomplete

  +-------------------------------------------+
  | ○ Vaswani et al. (2017) - Attention Is    |
  |   All You Need    PageRank: 0.0847        |
  | ----------------------------------------- |
  | [/prune Vas█                       ] [->] |
  +-------------------------------------------+

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User types "/" in input area
  -> CommandRegistry detects slash prefix
  -> Command palette opens with all commands
  -> User types command name -> palette filters
  -> Tab/Enter selects command -> input shows command with cursor
  -> User types argument -> autocomplete queries graph data
  -> Tab/Enter accepts suggestion
  -> Enter submits command
  -> POST /api/athena/command with command name and args
  -> Backend executes command handler
  -> Response returned with type and data
  -> Frontend renders appropriate block type for result
  -> Command stored in chat_history as type="command"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (some commands use LLM for narrative, most do not)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant (for command response narratives)
STAGE: Post-computation (LLM wraps computed results in natural language)

PROMPT SECTION: "COMMAND RESULT CONTEXT"

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
COMMAND RESULT CONTEXT:
The user executed the command: /<command> <args>
Result data: <json_result>

Write a brief (2-3 sentence) natural language summary of this result.
Reference specific numbers from the data. Do not add information not in
the result data. Keep the tone of a research assistant reporting findings.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"Removing Vaswani (2017) collapses 47 of 152 nodes (31.0% impact). The
cascade affects 3 BFS levels, with BERT and GPT-2 among the first to fall."

FALLBACK: If LLM unavailable, show raw data with labels (no narrative).
TOKEN BUDGET: ~200 tokens per command response narrative

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| "/" in input         | Type        | Opens command palette                       |
| Command item         | Click       | Selects command, populates input            |
| Command item         | Hover       | Highlights with gold accent                 |
| Autocomplete item    | Tab/Enter   | Accepts suggestion                          |
| Palette              | Escape      | Closes palette, clears input                |
| Arrow keys           | Keydown     | Navigate palette/autocomplete items         |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Palette slides up 200ms. Autocomplete fades in 100ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Palette role="listbox", items role="option", aria-activedescendant tracks selection.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User types "/" in the middle of a sentence (not at start)
   Expected: Palette does not open. "/" treated as regular character.
   Implementation: Only trigger on "/" as first character in input.

2. Scenario: Command argument does not match any paper in graph
   Expected: "No paper matching 'xyz' in current graph. Did you mean: [suggestions]?"
   Implementation: Fuzzy match with pg_trgm, return top 3 closest.

3. Scenario: Command executed with no active graph
   Expected: "No graph loaded. Search for a paper first to build a lineage graph."
   Implementation: Check graphData before executing graph-dependent commands.

4. Scenario: User types unknown command (e.g., "/foo")
   Expected: "Unknown command '/foo'. Type /help to see available commands."
   Implementation: CommandRegistry.get() returns null, show error response.

5. Scenario: Paper name argument contains special characters
   Expected: Sanitized before query. No injection.
   Implementation: Arguments passed through sanitizer before use.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #050 (Rich Input Area), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: All graph command features (#005 Graph-Aware Commands)
ENHANCES: #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- commands produce block output)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

[ ] "/" opens command palette
[ ] Palette filters as user types
[ ] Tab/Enter selects command
[ ] Paper autocomplete works from graph data
[ ] All 12 commands execute correctly

[ ] Command results render as appropriate blocks
[ ] Commands stored in chat_history

VISUAL:

[ ] JetBrains Mono for command names
[ ] Palette grows upward from input area
[ ] Autocomplete shows paper metadata (title, year, PageRank)

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Palette opens < 50ms, autocomplete filters < 100ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Listbox/option roles, aria-activedescendant, keyboard navigation

VERIFICATION:

[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

FEATURE #057: PROGRESS INDICATORS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #054 (Thinking/Reasoning Display), #046 (Typing Indicator)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Progress Indicators show the researcher where Athena is in a multi-step
operation. While the Thinking Display (#054) shows detailed reasoning steps,
Progress Indicators are the high-level status: "Analyzing graph (step 2 of 5)"
or "Building lineage: 47/152 papers processed." They appear as compact bars
or labels that communicate progress without requiring the user to read
detailed logs.

This feature is critical because Arivu operations can take significant time.
Building a graph from a paper DOI involves BFS traversal across Semantic
Scholar and OpenAlex APIs, rate-limited to respect provider limits. A full
graph build can take 30-90 seconds. Without progress indicators, the user
stares at a blank screen wondering if anything is happening. With indicators,
they see "Fetching references (depth 1/2)... 23/50 papers... Running NLP
pipeline (12/47 edges)..." and can estimate completion time.

Three types of progress indicators are supported. Determinate progress bars
show a percentage-based bar for operations with known total counts (e.g.,
"Processing edges: 12/47"). Indeterminate indicators show a pulsing animation
for operations with unknown duration (e.g., "Waiting for Groq API response").
Step indicators show the current step in a numbered sequence (e.g., "Step 3
of 5: NLP Classification").

Progress data is delivered through the SSE 'meta' event channel, the same
infrastructure used by the Thinking Display. The difference is in the event
subtype: 'progress' events carry a progress_type (determinate, indeterminate,
step), current value, total value (if determinate), and a label string.

The visual design uses a thin progress bar (3px height) at the top of the
response area for determinate progress, and a subtle pulse animation for
indeterminate progress. Step indicators render as a horizontal row of
numbered dots: filled dots for completed steps, a pulsing dot for the current
step, and hollow dots for pending steps. Colors follow the palette: completed
steps use success green, current step uses accent-gold with a pulse, pending
steps use text-muted.

Progress indicators integrate with the graph build SSE stream. During graph
building (the core Arivu flow), the build_jobs and job_events tables already
track build progress. The Progress Indicator system reads these events and
renders them as visual progress in the Athena chat area, creating a unified
experience where the graph build and the chat interface share the same
progress visualization.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena begins any multi-step operation: graph build, pruning computation,
DNA analysis, export generation, or complex LLM query.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Build the lineage for this paper." Athena acknowledges and
   starts the build. A step indicator appears: "Step 1 of 5: Resolving paper."
2. Step 1 completes. The dot fills green. Step 2 activates: "Fetching
   references (depth 1)." A determinate bar appears: 0/50.
3. As papers are fetched, the bar fills: 12/50... 25/50... 47/50... 50/50.
4. Step 3: "Running NLP pipeline." Another determinate bar: "Processing
   edges: 0/203."
5. Edges process in batches. Bar fills incrementally. The researcher can see
   real-time progress and estimate remaining time.
6. Step 4: "Computing metrics." Indeterminate pulse (PageRank computation has
   no meaningful progress metric).
7. Step 5: "Preparing visualization." Quick step, then "Complete."
8. All dots filled green. Progress indicators fade out. Response with the
   completed graph description appears.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Progress indicators removed. Graph loaded. Response visible. The researcher
experienced continuous feedback during a 30-90 second operation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-progress.js: Progress bar, step indicator, pulse animation.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Routes 'meta' events with subtype 'progress' to progress system.
- static/css/athena-progress.css: Progress bar styles, step dots, pulse keyframes.
- app.py: Progress events emitted during graph build and analysis operations.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class ProgressIndicator {
    constructor(containerEl) {
      this.container = containerEl;
      this.steps = [];
      this.currentStep = 0;
      this.barEl = null;
    }

    setSteps(stepLabels) {
      this.steps = stepLabels;
      this.renderStepDots();
    }

    updateDeterminate(current, total, label) {
      if (!this.barEl) this.createBar();
      const pct = Math.round((current / total) * 100);
      this.barEl.style.width = `${pct}%`;
      this.barEl.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', current);
      this.barEl.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', total);
      this.labelEl.textContent = `${label}: ${current}/${total}`;
    }

    setIndeterminate(label) {
      if (!this.barEl) this.createBar();
      this.barEl.classList.add('indeterminate');
      this.labelEl.textContent = label;
    }

    completeStep(stepIndex) {
      this.stepDots[stepIndex].classList.add('complete');
      this.stepDots[stepIndex].classList.remove('active');
      if (stepIndex + 1 < this.steps.length) {
        this.stepDots[stepIndex + 1].classList.add('active');
        this.currentStep = stepIndex + 1;
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In graph build streaming generator
  def stream_graph_build(paper_id, session_id):
      steps = ['Resolving paper', 'Fetching references', 'NLP pipeline',
               'Computing metrics', 'Preparing visualization']
      yield sse_event('meta', {
          'type': 'progress', 'progress_type': 'step',
          'steps': steps, 'current': 0
      })

      # Step 1: Resolve
      paper = resolve_paper(paper_id)
      yield sse_event('meta', {
          'type': 'progress', 'progress_type': 'step_complete', 'step': 0
      })

      # Step 2: Fetch refs with determinate progress
      refs = []
      for i, batch in enumerate(fetch_references_batched(paper)):
          refs.extend(batch)
          yield sse_event('meta', {
              'type': 'progress', 'progress_type': 'determinate',
              'current': len(refs), 'total': estimated_total,
              'label': 'Fetching references'
          })

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.progress-bar-container {
  width: 100%; height: 3px; background: var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 2px; overflow: hidden; margin: 4px 0;
}
.progress-bar-fill {
  height: 100%; background: var(--accent-gold);
  transition: width 200ms ease-out; border-radius: 2px;
}
.progress-bar-fill.indeterminate {
  width: 30%; animation: indeterminate-slide 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes indeterminate-slide {
  0% { transform: translateX(-100%); }
  100% { transform: translateX(400%); }
}
.step-dot {
  width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%;
  border: 2px solid var(--text-muted); background: transparent;
}
.step-dot.active { border-color: var(--accent-gold);
  animation: dot-pulse 1s ease-in-out infinite; }
.step-dot.complete { background: var(--success); border-color: var(--success); }
```

API CONTRACTS:
  SSE event type: meta
  Subtypes:
    { "type": "progress", "progress_type": "step", "steps": [...], "current": 0 }
    { "type": "progress", "progress_type": "determinate", "current": 12, "total": 47, "label": "..." }
    { "type": "progress", "progress_type": "indeterminate", "label": "..." }
    { "type": "progress", "progress_type": "step_complete", "step": 0 }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Step Progress with Determinate Bar

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  ● ---- ● ---- ◐ ---- ○ ---- ○           |
  |  Resolve  Fetch   NLP   Metrics  Viz      |
  |                                           |
  |  Fetching references: 25/50               |
  |  [==================------] 50%           |
  +-------------------------------------------+

STATE: Indeterminate Progress

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  ● ---- ● ---- ● ---- ◐ ---- ○           |
  |  Resolve  Fetch   NLP   Metrics  Viz      |
  |                                           |
  |  Computing PageRank...                    |
  |  [   =====>                    ]          |
  +-------------------------------------------+

STATE: All Steps Complete

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  ● ---- ● ---- ● ---- ● ---- ●           |
  |  All steps complete                       |
  +-------------------------------------------+

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Backend begins multi-step operation
  -> Emits SSE 'meta' event with progress_type='step' and step labels
  -> Frontend ProgressIndicator.setSteps() renders step dots
  -> Each sub-operation emits determinate or indeterminate progress events
  -> Frontend updates bar position and label in real time
  -> On step completion: SSE 'meta' with step_complete
  -> Frontend fills dot green, activates next dot
  -> All steps complete: progress indicators fade out over 300ms
  -> Response content begins streaming

DATA STORED: Progress events in job_events table (for SSE replay)
DATA CACHED: Current progress state in frontend ProgressIndicator instance

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: No (progress indicators are metadata, not LLM output)

MODEL: NOT APPLICABLE
STAGE: NOT APPLICABLE
PROMPT SECTION: NOT APPLICABLE
FALLBACK: NOT APPLICABLE
TOKEN BUDGET: NOT APPLICABLE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Step dots            | Hover       | Tooltip with step label and status          |
| Progress bar         | None        | Auto-updates from SSE events                |
| Completed steps      | Visual only | Green filled dot, no interaction            |
| Progress area        | None        | Auto-removes when operation completes       |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Bar fill 200ms ease-out. Dot pulse 1s infinite. Indeterminate slide 1.5s. Fade-out 300ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Progress bar role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow/max. Steps role="list" with status announced.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Operation fails mid-progress (API error during fetch)
   Expected: Current step dot turns red. Error message shown. Bar stops.
   Implementation: Error events set step state to 'error'.

2. Scenario: Total count changes during operation (more refs found than estimated)
   Expected: Bar adjusts. "25/75" replaces "25/50." No visual glitch.
   Implementation: updateDeterminate() handles total changes gracefully.

3. Scenario: Very fast operation (completes in < 500ms)
   Expected: Progress indicators may flash briefly. Not disorienting.
   Implementation: Minimum display time of 300ms before auto-remove.

4. Scenario: SSE connection drops during progress
   Expected: Progress freezes. Reconnection resumes from last known state.
   Implementation: Last-Event-ID replay includes progress events.

5. Scenario: Screen reader user
   Expected: Progress percentage announced periodically (every 25% increment).
   Implementation: aria-valuenow updates trigger announcements.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE (but used by every multi-step operation)
ENHANCES: #054 (Thinking Display -- complementary views of progress)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Step dots render with correct states (pending, active, complete, error)
[ ] Determinate bar fills proportionally
[ ] Indeterminate bar animates correctly
[ ] Progress label updates in real time
[ ] Progress auto-removes on operation completion
[ ] Error state renders red on failure

VISUAL:
[ ] Gold pulse on active step dot
[ ] Green fill on completed step dot
[ ] 3px gold progress bar
[ ] Indeterminate slide animation

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Progress update rendering < 5ms per event
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] progressbar role, aria-valuenow/max, announcements at 25% intervals
EDGE CASES: [ ] Mid-operation failure, total change, fast operations, SSE reconnect

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

CHECK error state rendering on simulated failures. 5. VERIFY progress removal timing and animation. 6. BROKEN until all progress types render correctly. 7. At least 1 fix per round. 8. Log below.

FEATURE #058: PAPER CITATION CARD BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #051 (Inline Citations), #055 (Multi-Modal Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- requires block infrastructure)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Paper Citation Card Block is a rich, structured component that renders
inside Athena's responses whenever a specific paper needs to be showcased with
its full metadata. Unlike inline citations (#051) which are small numbered
chips, the Paper Citation Card is a full-width block that displays a paper's
title, authors, year, venue, citation count, PageRank score, pruning impact,
fields of study, DNA cluster membership, and a mini-sparkline of its citation
trajectory over time.

This block exists because Arivu's value proposition is deeply tied to paper
metadata. When Athena says "The most influential paper in this lineage is
Attention Is All You Need," the researcher needs more than a name. They need
to see the numbers: 216,247 citations, PageRank 0.0847 (top 1% in graph),
pruning impact 31.0% (removing it collapses nearly a third of the lineage),
fields CS and ML, DNA cluster "Attention Mechanisms." These numbers are the
evidence that supports the claim of influence.

The card renders from graph data, not from the LLM output. The LLM produces
a <PAPER_CARD paper_id="..."/> marker, and the block system resolves the
paper_id against the in-memory graph to produce the full card. This ensures
data accuracy: the card always shows the real numbers from Arivu's computation,
never hallucinated values from the LLM.

The visual design uses the elevated surface background with a left border
colored by the paper's primary field of study (using the field color palette:
CS=field-cs blue, Biology=field-bio purple, Physics=field-physics pink, etc.).
The title is large (16px Inter semibold), authors are truncated after three
with "+N more," and metrics are displayed in a 2x3 grid of labeled values
using JetBrains Mono for numbers.

The card is interactive. Clicking the title navigates to the paper node in the
graph and opens the right panel with full details. Hovering the PageRank value
shows a tooltip explaining what the score means relative to the graph.
Hovering the pruning impact shows a mini-preview of which nodes would collapse.
The citation sparkline in the card shows the paper's citation count by year
for the last 10 years, rendered as a tiny SVG line chart (60px wide, 20px
tall) providing a visual sense of the paper's citation trajectory.

The confidence tier of the paper's relationships is indicated by a small badge
in the top-right corner of the card. If the paper has primarily HIGH confidence
edges, the badge is green. MEDIUM is blue. LOW is amber. SPECULATIVE is purple.
This gives the researcher an immediate sense of how well-characterized this
paper's role in the lineage is.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena decides a paper needs to be highlighted in a response. The LLM emits
a <PAPER_CARD paper_id="..."/> marker in the stream.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What is the most important paper here?" Athena streams prose:
   "The most influential paper in this lineage is..."
2. A Paper Card block renders, showing the full metadata for Vaswani (2017).
3. The card shows: title, 3 authors + "+4 more", year 2017, venue NeurIPS,
   216,247 citations, PageRank 0.0847, pruning impact 31.0%, fields CS/ML,
   DNA cluster "Attention Mechanisms."
4. A tiny sparkline shows citations rising sharply from 2018 onward.
5. A green "HIGH" confidence badge sits in the top-right corner.
6. User hovers the pruning impact value (31.0%). A tooltip shows: "Removing
   this paper collapses 47 of 152 nodes across 3 BFS levels."
7. User clicks the paper title. The graph pans to the Vaswani node, which
   pulses gold. The right panel opens with full paper details.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Card rendered in response. All metrics accurate from graph data. Card
interactive with hover tooltips and click navigation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/blocks/paper-card.js: PaperCardRenderer class.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Registers PaperCardRenderer for PAPER_CARD type.
- static/css/blocks/paper-card.css: Card layout, field color borders, sparkline.
- app.py: Paper data resolution for block events.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class PaperCardRenderer {
    constructor(data) {
      this.paper = data;
    }

    render() {
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.className = 'athena-block paper-card';
      card.style.borderLeftColor = this.fieldColor(this.paper.fields_of_study[0]);
      card.innerHTML = `
        <div class="block-label">PAPER CARD</div>
        <div class="pc-confidence ${this.paper.confidence_tier.toLowerCase()}">
          ${this.paper.confidence_tier}</div>
        <h4 class="pc-title" data-paper-id="${this.paper.paper_id}">
          ${this.paper.title}</h4>
        <div class="pc-authors">${this.formatAuthors(this.paper.authors)}</div>
        <div class="pc-venue">${this.paper.venue} (${this.paper.year})</div>
        <div class="pc-metrics">
          <div class="pc-metric">
            <span class="pc-metric-value">${this.paper.citation_count.toLocaleString()}</span>
            <span class="pc-metric-label">Citations</span>
          </div>
          <div class="pc-metric">
            <span class="pc-metric-value">${this.paper.pagerank.toFixed(4)}</span>
            <span class="pc-metric-label">PageRank</span>
          </div>
          <div class="pc-metric">
            <span class="pc-metric-value">${this.paper.pruning_impact}%</span>
            <span class="pc-metric-label">Pruning Impact</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        <svg class="pc-sparkline" width="60" height="20"></svg>
      `;
      this.renderSparkline(card.querySelector('.pc-sparkline'), this.paper.citation_history);
      return card;
    }

    fieldColor(field) {
      const colors = {
        'Computer Science': 'var(--field-cs)',
        'Biology': 'var(--field-bio)',
        'Physics': 'var(--field-physics)',
        'Chemistry': 'var(--field-chem)',
        'Economics': 'var(--field-econ)',
        'Mathematics': 'var(--field-math)'
      };
      return colors[field] || 'var(--field-other)';
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # Paper card data resolution (called when block event emitted)
  def resolve_paper_card(paper_id, graph_data):
      paper = graph_data['nodes_by_id'].get(paper_id)
      if not paper: return None
      pagerank_scores = graph_data.get('pagerank', {})
      pruning = graph_data.get('precomputed_pruning', {}).get(paper_id, {})
      return {
          'paper_id': paper_id,
          'title': paper['title'],
          'authors': paper.get('authors', []),
          'year': paper.get('year'),
          'venue': paper.get('venue'),
          'citation_count': paper.get('citation_count', 0),
          'pagerank': pagerank_scores.get(paper_id, 0.0),
          'pruning_impact': pruning.get('impact_percentage', 0.0),
          'fields_of_study': paper.get('fields_of_study', []),
          'confidence_tier': compute_avg_confidence(paper_id, graph_data),
          'citation_history': get_citation_history(paper_id)
      }

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.paper-card {
  border-left: 4px solid var(--field-cs);
  padding: 16px; position: relative;
}
.pc-title {
  font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary);
  cursor: pointer; margin: 4px 0;
}
.pc-title:hover { color: var(--accent-gold); }
.pc-metrics {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 8px; margin-top: 12px;
}
.pc-metric-value {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-primary);
}
.pc-metric-label {
  font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted); text-transform: uppercase;
}
.pc-confidence {
  position: absolute; top: 12px; right: 12px;
  font-size: 10px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px;
}
.pc-confidence.high { background: var(--conf-high); color: var(--bg-primary); }
.pc-confidence.medium { background: var(--conf-medium); color: white; }
.pc-confidence.low { background: var(--conf-low); color: var(--bg-primary); }
.pc-confidence.speculative { background: var(--conf-specul); color: white; }
```

API CONTRACTS:
  SSE block event:
  { "block_type": "PAPER_CARD", "data": { "paper_id": "...", "title": "...", ... } }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM emits <PAPER_CARD paper_id="abc123"/>
  -> Block system detects PAPER_CARD marker
  -> resolve_paper_card() looks up paper in graph data
  -> Full metadata assembled: title, authors, metrics, sparkline data
  -> PaperCardRenderer.render() creates DOM element
  -> Card inserted into response flow
  -> User hover on metric -> tooltip with explanation
  -> User click on title -> graph.focusNode(paper_id)

DATA STORED: NONE (card rendered from live graph data)
DATA CACHED: Paper resolution cached per graph session

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (LLM decides WHEN to show a card, data comes from graph)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for deciding when to embed a card)
STAGE: Generation (LLM embeds <PAPER_CARD/> markers in response text)

PROMPT SECTION: Part of "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" (see Feature #055)

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
Use <PAPER_CARD paper_id="..."/> when:
- Introducing a specific paper as important to the answer
- The user asks about a paper's influence or impact
- Comparing papers (show cards for each)
Do NOT use paper cards for passing mentions or lists of many papers.
Limit to 3 paper cards per response maximum.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"The most influential paper is <PAPER_CARD paper_id="204e3073870fae..."/>"

FALLBACK: If paper_id not found in graph, render as plain text mention.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~20 tokens per card marker

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Paper title          | Click       | Focus node in graph, open right panel       |
| Paper title          | Hover       | Gold color highlight                        |
| PageRank value       | Hover       | Tooltip: "Top N% in this graph"             |
| Pruning impact       | Hover       | Tooltip: cascade details                    |
| Sparkline            | Hover       | Tooltip: year and count at mouse position   |
| Confidence badge     | Hover       | Tooltip: confidence tier explanation         |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Card fade-in 200ms. Title hover color 150ms. Sparkline tooltip follows mouse.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Card role="article", title is a link, metrics have aria-labels.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Paper has no citation history data (new or obscure paper)
   Expected: Sparkline area hidden. Other metrics still shown.
   Implementation: Conditional rendering based on citation_history array.

2. Scenario: Paper has zero pruning impact (leaf node, no dependents)
   Expected: Pruning impact shows "0.0%" with tooltip "No dependent papers."
   Implementation: Zero is a valid value, displayed normally.

3. Scenario: Paper has 20+ authors
   Expected: Shows first 3 + "+17 more". Full list in tooltip.
   Implementation: formatAuthors() truncates with count.

4. Scenario: Paper fields_of_study is empty
   Expected: Field border defaults to field-other color. Fields row hidden.
   Implementation: Default color fallback.

5. Scenario: Graph data not yet loaded when card renders
   Expected: Card shows paper_id and "Loading..." for metrics.
   Implementation: Deferred resolution with placeholder state.
