FEATURE #059: INLINE MINI-GRAPH BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 20
OVERLAPS WITH:   #055 (Multi-Modal Responses), #034 (Chat-Driven Graph Nav)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- requires block infrastructure)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Inline Mini-Graph Block renders a small, focused subgraph visualization
directly within Athena's response. When Athena discusses a specific path,
cluster, or relationship in the lineage, the mini-graph shows exactly those
nodes and edges highlighted against a simplified background of the full graph.
This bridges the gap between textual explanation and visual understanding
without forcing the researcher to leave the chat and manually navigate the
main graph.

Consider the scenario: the researcher asks "How did attention mechanisms
evolve from Bahdanau to Vaswani?" Athena explains the path through three
intermediate papers, each representing a generalization or specialization
mutation. The mini-graph renders a force-directed layout of these five nodes
(Bahdanau, three intermediaries, Vaswani) with edges colored by mutation_type
(adoption=blue, generalization=gold, specialization=teal). Surrounding nodes
from the full graph appear as faded dots, providing spatial context without
cluttering the focused view.

The mini-graph is not a full D3.js force simulation. It uses a simplified
static layout computed from the node positions in the main graph, scaled down
to fit a 320x200px container. Nodes are 8px circles (vs 12px in the main
graph), edges are 1px lines (vs 2px), and labels are shown only for
highlighted nodes. The graph is interactive at a basic level: hovering a node
shows a tooltip with paper title and year, and clicking a node navigates to
that node in the main graph.

The LLM produces <MINI_GRAPH nodes="id1,id2,id3" highlight="id1,id3"
focus="path"/> markers. The nodes attribute lists which papers to include.
The highlight attribute marks which nodes to visually emphasize (full opacity,
colored border). The focus attribute hints at the layout strategy: "path"
arranges nodes in a horizontal chain, "cluster" uses a circular layout, and
"neighborhood" uses the original force positions.

The rendering uses a lightweight SVG generator rather than full D3 force
simulation. Node positions are computed deterministically: path layouts use
equidistant horizontal spacing, cluster layouts use circular positioning, and
neighborhood layouts extract positions from the main graph's simulation. This
ensures the mini-graph renders instantly (no simulation settling time) and
produces consistent layouts for the same input.

Edge colors in the mini-graph follow the mutation_type palette: adoption uses
accent-blue, generalization uses accent-gold, specialization uses accent-teal,
contradiction uses danger red, revival uses success green, hybridization uses
conf-specul purple, and incidental uses text-muted. This creates an immediate
visual legend that matches the main graph's conventions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena produces a response that discusses specific paths, clusters, or
neighborhoods in the graph. The LLM embeds a <MINI_GRAPH/> marker.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Trace the path from Word2Vec to BERT." Athena streams prose
   explaining the evolutionary chain.
2. A mini-graph block renders showing 6 nodes in a horizontal path layout:
   Word2Vec -> [intermediate papers] -> BERT. Edges colored by mutation_type.
3. Highlighted nodes (Word2Vec, BERT) show full opacity with gold borders.
   Intermediate nodes show at 70% opacity. Surrounding context nodes at 20%.
4. User hovers an intermediate node. Tooltip: "Pennington et al. (2014) -
   GloVe - generalization of Word2Vec."
5. User clicks the BERT node. The main graph pans to BERT and highlights it.
6. Prose continues below the mini-graph with further analysis.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Mini-graph rendered inline. Interactive hover/click. Prose context above and
below the visualization.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/blocks/mini-graph.js: MiniGraphRenderer class, SVG generation.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Registers MiniGraphRenderer for MINI_GRAPH type.
- static/css/blocks/mini-graph.css: Mini-graph container, node/edge styles.
- No backend changes (data resolved from in-memory graph).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class MiniGraphRenderer {
    constructor(data) {
      this.nodes = data.nodes;
      this.highlight = data.highlight || [];
      this.focus = data.focus || 'neighborhood';
      this.width = 320;
      this.height = 200;
    }

    render() {
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'athena-block mini-graph-block';
      const svg = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'svg');
      svg.setAttribute('viewBox', `0 0 ${this.width} ${this.height}`);
      svg.setAttribute('role', 'img');
      svg.setAttribute('aria-label', `Subgraph showing ${this.nodes.length} papers`);

      const positions = this.computeLayout();
      this.renderEdges(svg, positions);
      this.renderNodes(svg, positions);

      container.innerHTML = '<div class="block-label">SUBGRAPH</div>';
      container.appendChild(svg);
      return container;
    }

    computeLayout() {
      if (this.focus === 'path') return this.pathLayout();
      if (this.focus === 'cluster') return this.circularLayout();
      return this.extractPositions();
    }

    pathLayout() {
      const positions = {};
      const spacing = this.width / (this.nodes.length + 1);
      this.nodes.forEach((id, i) => {
        positions[id] = { x: spacing * (i + 1), y: this.height / 2 };
      });
      return positions;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Mini-graph data is resolved entirely on the frontend from
  the in-memory graph data. The LLM provides paper_ids; the frontend looks
  up node positions, edges, and metadata from the cached graph JSON.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.mini-graph-block {
  padding: 8px; text-align: center;
}
.mini-graph-block svg {
  width: 100%; max-width: 320px; height: auto;
  background: var(--bg-primary); border-radius: 4px;
}
.mg-node { cursor: pointer; }
.mg-node.highlighted { stroke: var(--accent-gold); stroke-width: 2; }
.mg-node.context { opacity: 0.2; }
.mg-edge { stroke-width: 1; fill: none; }
.mg-edge.adoption { stroke: var(--accent-blue); }
.mg-edge.generalization { stroke: var(--accent-gold); }
.mg-edge.specialization { stroke: var(--accent-teal); }
.mg-edge.contradiction { stroke: var(--danger); }
.mg-edge.revival { stroke: var(--success); }
.mg-edge.hybridization { stroke: var(--conf-specul); }
.mg-edge.incidental { stroke: var(--text-muted); opacity: 0.3; }
```

API CONTRACTS: No API calls. Frontend-only rendering from cached graph data.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Path Layout Mini-Graph

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  SUBGRAPH                                 |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  |                                       ||
  |  |  [W2V]--->[GloVe]--->[ELMo]-->[BERT] ||
  |  |   ●        ○          ○         ●     ||
  |  |   gold     blue       gold      gold  ||
  |  |   edge     edge       edge            ||
  |  |                                       ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  +-------------------------------------------+

  ● = highlighted node (full opacity, gold border)
  ○ = non-highlighted node (70% opacity)

STATE: Cluster Layout Mini-Graph

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  SUBGRAPH                                 |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  |       ○                               ||
  |  |     /   \                             ||
  |  |   ●       ○                           ||
  |  |     \   /                             ||
  |  |       ●                               ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  +-------------------------------------------+

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM emits <MINI_GRAPH nodes="id1,id2,id3" highlight="id1,id3" focus="path"/>
  -> Block system detects MINI_GRAPH marker
  -> MiniGraphRenderer receives node list, highlight list, focus type
  -> computeLayout() positions nodes based on focus type
  -> Edges looked up from graph data between listed nodes
  -> SVG generated with nodes, edges, labels for highlighted nodes
  -> Container inserted into response flow
  -> User hover on node -> tooltip with paper info
  -> User click on node -> main graph navigates to that node

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (LLM decides which nodes to show, rendering is deterministic)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for deciding when to embed a mini-graph)
STAGE: Generation (LLM embeds <MINI_GRAPH/> markers in response text)

PROMPT SECTION: Part of "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" (see Feature #055)

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
Use <MINI_GRAPH nodes="id1,id2,..." highlight="id1,id2" focus="path|cluster|neighborhood"/>
when:
- Explaining a specific path between two papers
- Discussing a cluster of related papers
- Highlighting a paper's immediate neighborhood
Use focus="path" for linear evolution chains.
Use focus="cluster" for groups of related papers.
Use focus="neighborhood" for a paper and its connections.
Maximum 15 nodes per mini-graph. Highlight no more than 5.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"The evolution path: <MINI_GRAPH nodes="330da625...,abc123...,df2b0e26..."
highlight="330da625...,df2b0e26..." focus="path"/>"

FALLBACK: If node IDs not in graph, render text list of paper titles instead.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~50 tokens per mini-graph marker

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Mini-graph node      | Hover       | Tooltip: paper title, year, type            |
| Mini-graph node      | Click       | Navigate to node in main graph              |
| Mini-graph edge      | Hover       | Tooltip: mutation_type, confidence tier     |
| Mini-graph container | Scroll      | Does not zoom (fixed scale)                 |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Nodes fade-in 200ms. Hover tooltip 150ms. Click triggers main graph pan 500ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: SVG role="img" with aria-label. Node tooltips via aria-describedby.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Specified nodes have no edges between them
   Expected: Nodes rendered without connecting edges. Still useful for spatial context.
   Implementation: Edge rendering skips missing edges.

2. Scenario: More than 15 nodes specified
   Expected: Truncated to top 15 by PageRank. Warning in tooltip.
   Implementation: Sort by PageRank, take top 15.

3. Scenario: Main graph positions not available (graph not loaded)
   Expected: Use simple circular layout as fallback.
   Implementation: extractPositions() returns circular layout on failure.

4. Scenario: All specified nodes are in the same position (overlapping)
   Expected: Jitter applied to separate nodes.
   Implementation: Collision detection with 15px minimum separation.

5. Scenario: Mobile screen (320px viewport width)
   Expected: Mini-graph scales to 100% width. Touch targets adequate.
   Implementation: SVG viewBox scales responsively.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- block infrastructure)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #034 (Chat-Driven Graph Navigation -- visual bridge to main graph)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (pure SVG, no D3 dependency)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Mini-graph renders from <MINI_GRAPH/> marker
[ ] Path layout arranges nodes horizontally
[ ] Cluster layout arranges nodes circularly
[ ] Neighborhood layout uses main graph positions
[ ] Highlighted nodes have gold border and full opacity
[ ] Edges colored by mutation_type
[ ] Hover shows paper tooltip
[ ] Click navigates to main graph

VISUAL:
[ ] 320x200px SVG container
[ ] Dark bg-primary background
[ ] Mutation-type edge colors match main graph
[ ] Node labels only on highlighted nodes

PERFORMANCE: [ ] SVG generation < 20ms for 15 nodes
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] SVG role="img" with descriptive aria-label
EDGE CASES: [ ] No edges, too many nodes, missing positions, mobile scaling

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ blocks/mini-graph.js, mini-graph.css. 2. COMPARE rendered layout against specified focus type. 3. TEST all three layout types with real graph data. 4. CHECK edge colors match mutation_type classifications. 5. VERIFY click navigation works for all nodes. 6. BROKEN until all layout types and interactions work. 7. At least 1 fix per round. 8. Log below.

FEATURE #060: DATA TABLE BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #055 (Multi-Modal Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- requires block infrastructure)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Data Table Block renders structured tabular data within Athena's responses.
Research intelligence naturally produces tables: lists of papers sorted by
PageRank, edges grouped by mutation_type with confidence tiers, DNA cluster
membership rosters, diversity metric breakdowns, and gap analysis results.
Rendering these as prose is inefficient and hard to scan. A proper table with
sortable columns, hover highlights, and click-to-navigate-to-node interactivity
turns data from text into an actionable interface.

The table renders inside the block system established by Feature #055. The
LLM produces <DATA_TABLE columns="..." rows="..."/> markers, and the block
system parses these into a structured table component. Columns can contain
text (paper titles, mutation types), numbers (citation counts, PageRank
scores, pruning impact percentages), and confidence tier badges (HIGH, MEDIUM,
LOW, SPECULATIVE with color coding).

Column types are inferred from the data content. If all values in a column
are numeric, the column is right-aligned and sortable by numeric value. If
values match confidence tier names, they render as colored badges. If values
are paper titles, they render as clickable links that navigate to the paper
in the graph. The inference is heuristic but covers 95% of cases because Arivu
operates on a known, structured dataset.

Sorting is client-side. Clicking a column header toggles ascending, descending,
and unsorted states. The sort indicator is a small arrow next to the header
text. Sorted columns have a subtle accent-gold background on the header cell.
The table maintains sort state even as new data streams in (if the table is
part of a streaming response that updates incrementally).

The table supports a maximum of 50 rows by default (matching Arivu's
MAX_REFS_PER_PAPER limit). If the LLM produces more than 50 rows, only the
first 50 are shown with a "Show all N rows" toggle at the bottom. This
prevents massive tables from overwhelming the chat view while still allowing
access to full data.

The visual design follows the dot matrix aesthetic. The table uses no visible
borders between cells. Instead, alternating rows use bg-surface and bg-elevated
backgrounds. Header cells use JetBrains Mono uppercase text in text-muted
color. Data cells use Inter for text and JetBrains Mono for numbers. The
overall effect is clean, dense, and scannable, matching the aesthetic of a
well-designed research instrument.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena produces a response that includes tabular data. The LLM embeds a
<DATA_TABLE/> marker in the stream.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "List the top 10 papers by PageRank in this lineage." Athena
   streams a brief intro, then a Data Table block renders.
2. The table shows 10 rows with columns: Rank, Paper Title, Year, PageRank,
   Pruning Impact, Confidence.
3. PageRank column values are in JetBrains Mono, right-aligned. Confidence
   column shows colored badges (HIGH=green, MEDIUM=blue).
4. User clicks the "PageRank" column header. Rows sort descending by PageRank
   (already the default, so arrow indicates current sort).
5. User clicks "Pruning Impact" header. Rows re-sort by pruning impact. The
   order changes, showing different papers at the top.
6. User clicks a paper title. The main graph navigates to that paper node.
7. User hovers a row. The row highlights with a subtle accent-gold background.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Table rendered with sortable columns. All values from graph data. Titles
clickable. Sort state maintained until chat scrolls past.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/blocks/data-table.js: DataTableRenderer class, sorting, type inference.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Registers DataTableRenderer for DATA_TABLE type.
- static/css/blocks/data-table.css: Table layout, zebra rows, sort indicators.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class DataTableRenderer {
    constructor(data) {
      this.columns = data.columns;
      this.rows = data.rows;
      this.sortColumn = null;
      this.sortDirection = null;
      this.maxRows = 50;
    }

    render() {
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'athena-block data-table-block';
      container.innerHTML = '<div class="block-label">DATA TABLE</div>';

      const table = document.createElement('table');
      table.className = 'dt-table';
      table.setAttribute('role', 'grid');

      const thead = this.renderHeader();
      const tbody = this.renderBody();
      table.appendChild(thead);
      table.appendChild(tbody);
      container.appendChild(table);

      if (this.rows.length > this.maxRows) {
        const toggle = document.createElement('button');
        toggle.className = 'dt-show-all';
        toggle.textContent = `Show all ${this.rows.length} rows`;
        toggle.onclick = () => this.expandAll(tbody);
        container.appendChild(toggle);
      }
      return container;
    }

    inferColumnType(colIndex) {
      const values = this.rows.map(r => r[colIndex]);
      if (values.every(v => !isNaN(parseFloat(v)))) return 'number';
      if (values.every(v => ['HIGH','MEDIUM','LOW','SPECULATIVE'].includes(v))) return 'confidence';
      return 'text';
    }

    sort(colIndex) {
      if (this.sortColumn === colIndex) {
        this.sortDirection = this.sortDirection === 'asc' ? 'desc' :
                             this.sortDirection === 'desc' ? null : 'asc';
      } else {
        this.sortColumn = colIndex;
        this.sortDirection = 'asc';
      }
      this.rerender();
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Table data is embedded in the LLM output marker and
  resolved from graph data on the frontend. The backend does not have a
  dedicated table endpoint.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.dt-table {
  width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 13px;
}
.dt-table th {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px;
  text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--text-muted);
  padding: 8px 12px; text-align: left; cursor: pointer;
  user-select: none; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
}
.dt-table th.sorted { background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.05); }
.dt-table td { padding: 6px 12px; }
.dt-table tr:nth-child(odd) { background: var(--bg-surface); }
.dt-table tr:nth-child(even) { background: var(--bg-elevated); }
.dt-table tr:hover { background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.08); }
.dt-table td.number { text-align: right; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
.dt-table td.paper-link { color: var(--accent-blue); cursor: pointer; }
.dt-table td.paper-link:hover { color: var(--accent-gold); text-decoration: underline; }
```

API CONTRACTS: No API calls. Data embedded in block marker.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Data Table Rendered (Sorted by PageRank)

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  DATA TABLE                               |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  | RANK | PAPER         | YR  | PAGERANK ||
  |  |------|---------------|-----|----------||
  |  |  1   | Vaswani '17   | 2017|  0.0847  ||
  |  |  2   | Devlin '18    | 2018|  0.0623  ||
  |  |  3   | Radford '19   | 2019|  0.0451  ||
  |  |  4   | Krizhevsky'12 | 2012|  0.0398  ||
  |  |  5   | He '16        | 2016|  0.0372  ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  +-------------------------------------------+

STATE: Confidence Column with Badges

  |  CONFIDENCE |
  |-------------|
  |  [HIGH]     |  <- green badge
  |  [MEDIUM]   |  <- blue badge
  |  [LOW]      |  <- amber badge
  |  [SPECUL.]  |  <- purple badge

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM emits <DATA_TABLE columns="Rank,Paper,Year,PageRank" rows="1,Vaswani,2017,0.0847;..."/>
  -> Block system detects DATA_TABLE marker
  -> DataTableRenderer parses columns and rows from attributes
  -> inferColumnType() determines rendering for each column
  -> Table rendered with zebra rows, typed cells, sort headers
  -> User clicks header -> sort() reorders rows client-side
  -> User clicks paper title -> graph.focusNode(paper_id)
  -> User hovers row -> highlight with accent-gold

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (LLM structures data into table format)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for deciding table structure)
STAGE: Generation (LLM embeds <DATA_TABLE/> markers)

PROMPT SECTION: Part of "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" (see Feature #055)

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
Use <DATA_TABLE columns="col1,col2,..." rows="val1,val2;val3,val4"/>
when presenting lists of papers, edges, metrics, or comparisons.
Columns should be meaningful labels. Rows separated by semicolons.
Values within a row separated by commas.
Include paper_ids as hidden data attributes for clickable titles.
Maximum 20 rows in the marker. Frontend will paginate if needed.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"Here are the top papers:
<DATA_TABLE columns="Paper,Year,PageRank,Impact" rows="Vaswani,2017,0.0847,31.0;BERT,2018,0.0623,24.2"/>"

FALLBACK: If table marker malformed, render data as bulleted list.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~30 tokens per row

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Column header        | Click       | Cycle sort: asc -> desc -> unsorted         |
| Paper title cell     | Click       | Navigate to paper in graph                  |
| Row                  | Hover       | Highlight with accent-gold background       |
| "Show all" toggle    | Click       | Expand to show all rows                     |
| Confidence badge     | Hover       | Tooltip with tier explanation                |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Sort transition 200ms. Row hover 100ms. Expand animation 300ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Table role="grid", sortable headers aria-sort, confidence badges aria-label.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Table has only 1 row
   Expected: Renders as a single-row table. No sorting needed but still works.
   Implementation: Sort handles single-element arrays.

2. Scenario: Column values are mixed types (some numbers, some text)
   Expected: Column treated as text type. No numeric sorting.
   Implementation: inferColumnType() requires ALL values to be numeric.

3. Scenario: Table has empty cells (missing data)
   Expected: Empty cells shown as "--" in text-muted color.
   Implementation: Null/empty check in cell renderer.

4. Scenario: Very wide table (8+ columns) on mobile
   Expected: Horizontal scroll within the table container.
   Implementation: overflow-x: auto on table wrapper.

5. Scenario: Row contains paper_id that does not match any graph node
   Expected: Title rendered as plain text, not clickable.
   Implementation: Check graph data before adding click handler.

DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- block infrastructure)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #014 (Comparison Mode -- tables are natural comparison output)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Table renders from <DATA_TABLE/> marker
[ ] Column type inference works (number, text, confidence)
[ ] Client-side sorting by column header click
[ ] Paper titles clickable with graph navigation
[ ] Confidence badges with correct colors
[ ] Row limit with "Show all" toggle
[ ] Empty cells handled gracefully

VISUAL:
[ ] Zebra row striping (surface/elevated alternation)
[ ] JetBrains Mono for headers and numbers
[ ] Sort indicator on active column
[ ] Hover highlight on rows

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Table rendering < 30ms for 50 rows
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Grid role, sortable headers, badge labels
EDGE CASES: [ ] Single row, mixed types, empty cells, wide tables, unknown paper_ids

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

VERIFY confidence badge colors match tier spec. 6. BROKEN until sorting, navigation, and badges work. 7. At least 1 fix per round. 8. Log below.

FEATURE #061: COMPARISON CARD BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #014 (Comparison Mode), #055 (Multi-Modal Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- requires block infrastructure)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Comparison Card Block renders a side-by-side comparison of two papers
within Athena's response. When the researcher asks "How does BERT compare to
GPT-2?" or Athena determines that a comparison would clarify a point, this
block shows both papers' metrics in parallel columns with visual indicators
highlighting which paper "wins" on each dimension.

Research comparison is not simple. BERT has more citations but GPT-2 may have
higher pruning impact in certain lineages. BERT's primary mutation_type from
Vaswani is "specialization" while GPT-2's is "generalization." BERT's DNA
cluster is "Representation Learning" while GPT-2's is "Language Generation."
The Comparison Card surfaces all these dimensions simultaneously, turning a
complex multi-dimensional comparison into a scannable visual.

The card layout uses two columns (left and right) with metric rows spanning
both. Each row shows a metric name (centered), left paper value (left-aligned),
right paper value (right-aligned), and a visual indicator. The indicator is a
horizontal bar spanning between the two values, with the fill colored to show
which side is larger. If the left paper has a higher PageRank, the bar fills
leftward with accent-blue. If the right paper wins, it fills rightward with
accent-teal. Equal values show a centered neutral bar.

Compared metrics include: citation_count, PageRank, pruning_impact, year
published, number of fields_of_study, number of outgoing edges (influence
breadth), number of incoming edges (influence depth), dominant mutation_type
of outgoing edges, DNA cluster membership, average edge confidence, and
text_tier (quality of available text for analysis). Not all metrics need to
be shown; the LLM selects the most relevant subset based on the user's
question.

The card also shows the direct relationship between the two papers if one
exists. If BERT cites Vaswani (or vice versa), the relationship section shows
the mutation_type, citation_intent, and confidence tier of that specific edge.
This grounds the comparison in Arivu's unique relationship data rather than
just showing parallel statistics.

The visual design places each paper's title at the top of its column with the
field color as a subtle background tint. The "winner" on each metric row has
the value in bold and the paper's accent color. The loser value is in
text-secondary. This creates an immediate visual scan pattern: bold values
attract the eye, showing which paper dominates on which dimensions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User asks a comparison question ("Compare X and Y") or Athena determines a
comparison would illustrate a point. LLM emits <COMPARISON/> marker.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Compare BERT and GPT-2 in this lineage." Athena streams a
   brief intro, then the Comparison Card renders.
2. Left column header: "BERT (2018)" with field-bio purple tint. Right column
   header: "GPT-2 (2019)" with field-cs blue tint.
3. Metric rows show:
   - Citations: 78,432 (bold) vs 12,891 -- BERT wins
   - PageRank: 0.0623 vs 0.0451 (bold in left) -- BERT wins
   - Pruning Impact: 24.2% vs 18.7% -- BERT wins
   - Outgoing Edges: 12 vs 19 (bold in right) -- GPT-2 wins
   - DNA Cluster: "Representation" vs "Generation" -- different, no winner
4. Relationship section shows: "BERT does not directly cite GPT-2. Both
   descend from Vaswani (2017) via different mutation paths."
5. User clicks BERT's title. Graph navigates to BERT. User clicks GPT-2's
   title. Graph navigates to GPT-2.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Comparison Card rendered with all metrics. Visual indicators show dimension
winners. Both papers clickable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/blocks/comparison.js: ComparisonRenderer class.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Registers ComparisonRenderer for COMPARISON type.
- static/css/blocks/comparison.css: Two-column layout, metric rows, winner indicators.
- app.py: Comparison data resolution from graph.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class ComparisonRenderer {
    constructor(data) {
      this.left = data.left;
      this.right = data.right;
      this.metrics = data.metrics || this.defaultMetrics();
      this.relationship = data.relationship;
    }

    render() {
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'athena-block comparison-block';
      container.innerHTML = `
        <div class="block-label">COMPARISON</div>
        <div class="comp-headers">
          <div class="comp-header comp-left" style="border-color: ${this.fieldColor(this.left)}">
            <span class="comp-title" data-paper-id="${this.left.paper_id}">${this.left.title}</span>
            <span class="comp-year">${this.left.year}</span>
          </div>
          <div class="comp-vs">VS</div>
          <div class="comp-header comp-right" style="border-color: ${this.fieldColor(this.right)}">
            <span class="comp-title" data-paper-id="${this.right.paper_id}">${this.right.title}</span>
            <span class="comp-year">${this.right.year}</span>
          </div>
        </div>
        ${this.renderMetricRows()}
        ${this.relationship ? this.renderRelationship() : ''}
      `;
      return container;
    }

    renderMetricRow(label, leftVal, rightVal) {
      const leftWins = parseFloat(leftVal) > parseFloat(rightVal);
      const rightWins = parseFloat(rightVal) > parseFloat(leftVal);
      return `
        <div class="comp-row">
          <div class="comp-val ${leftWins ? 'winner' : ''}">${leftVal}</div>
          <div class="comp-label">${label}</div>
          <div class="comp-val ${rightWins ? 'winner' : ''}">${rightVal}</div>
        </div>
      `;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  def resolve_comparison(left_id, right_id, graph_data):
      left = resolve_paper_card(left_id, graph_data)
      right = resolve_paper_card(right_id, graph_data)
      if not left or not right: return None
      edge = find_edge(left_id, right_id, graph_data) or find_edge(right_id, left_id, graph_data)
      relationship = None
      if edge:
          relationship = {
              'mutation_type': edge['mutation_type'],
              'citation_intent': edge['citation_intent'],
              'confidence_tier': get_confidence_tier(edge['mutation_confidence'])
          }
      return { 'left': left, 'right': right, 'relationship': relationship }

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.comparison-block { padding: 16px; }
.comp-headers {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr;
  gap: 12px; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 16px;
}
.comp-header {
  padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 6px;
  background: var(--bg-surface); border-left: 4px solid;
}
.comp-vs {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; color: var(--text-muted);
  font-size: 11px;
}
.comp-row {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr;
  gap: 12px; padding: 6px 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.05);
}
.comp-val { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 14px; }
.comp-val.winner { color: var(--accent-gold); font-weight: 600; }
.comp-val:not(.winner) { color: var(--text-secondary); }
.comp-label {
  font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted); text-transform: uppercase;
  text-align: center; align-self: center;
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
  SSE block event:
  { "block_type": "COMPARISON", "data": { "left": {...}, "right": {...}, "relationship": {...} } }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Comparison Card Rendered

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  COMPARISON                               |
  |  +------------------+  VS  +-------------+|
  |  | BERT (2018)      |      | GPT-2 (2019)||
  |  | |-purple border  |      | |-blue border||
  |  +------------------+      +-------------+|
  |                                           |
  |  78,432      CITATIONS          12,891    |
  |  0.0623      PAGERANK           0.0451    |
  |  24.2%       PRUNING            18.7%     |
  |  12          OUT EDGES          19        |
  |  Represent.  DNA CLUSTER     Generation   |
  |                                           |
  |  --- Direct Relationship ---              |
  |  No direct citation. Shared ancestor:     |
  |  Vaswani (2017)                           |
  +-------------------------------------------+

  Bold values = winner on that metric
  Non-bold = secondary color

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM emits <COMPARISON left="paper_id_1" right="paper_id_2"/>
  -> Block system detects COMPARISON marker
  -> resolve_comparison() fetches both papers from graph data
  -> Metrics computed for both papers from graph (PageRank, pruning, edges)
  -> Direct edge between papers checked
  -> ComparisonRenderer.render() creates two-column layout
  -> Winner determined per metric row (higher value = bold)
  -> Relationship section shows direct edge or shared ancestry
  -> User click on title -> navigate to paper in graph

DATA STORED: NONE (rendered from live graph data)
DATA CACHED: Comparison resolution cached per graph session

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (LLM selects which papers to compare)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for deciding when to compare)
STAGE: Generation (LLM embeds <COMPARISON/> markers in response)

PROMPT SECTION: Part of "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" (see Feature #055)

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
Use <COMPARISON left="paper_id_1" right="paper_id_2"/> when:
- User explicitly asks to compare two papers
- Explaining why one paper is more influential than another
- Contrasting different approaches in the lineage
The comparison block shows all relevant metrics automatically.
Maximum 1 comparison block per response.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"Let me compare these two: <COMPARISON left="df2b0e26..." right="9405cc0d..."/>"

FALLBACK: If either paper_id not in graph, render text comparison instead.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~30 tokens per comparison marker

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Paper title (left)   | Click       | Navigate to left paper in graph             |
| Paper title (right)  | Click       | Navigate to right paper in graph            |
| Metric value         | Hover       | Tooltip explaining the metric               |
| Winner value         | Visual only | Bold gold color indicates higher value      |
| Relationship section | Click link  | Navigate to shared ancestor if mentioned    |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Card fade-in 200ms. Metric rows stagger-in 50ms each. Winner values pulse once 300ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Comparison table role="table" with headers. Winner marked with aria-label "higher value."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Both papers have identical metric values
   Expected: Both shown in normal color (no winner). Tie indicator.
   Implementation: Check equality before applying winner class.

2. Scenario: One paper is missing from graph (deleted or evicted)
   Expected: Comparison shows available paper fully, missing paper as "N/A."
   Implementation: Null check per paper in resolve_comparison().

3. Scenario: Papers are from completely different fields
   Expected: Metrics still shown. DNA cluster difference highlighted.
   Implementation: Cluster membership comparison shows both labels.

4. Scenario: Direct edge exists between the two papers
   Expected: Relationship section shows mutation_type, intent, confidence.
   Implementation: find_edge() checks both directions.

5. Scenario: Mobile viewport (narrow screen)
   Expected: Comparison stacks vertically instead of side-by-side.
   Implementation: CSS media query at 480px switches to column layout.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- block infrastructure)
ENABLES: #014 (Comparison Mode -- uses this block as primary output)
ENHANCES: #058 (Paper Citation Card -- comparison extends single-paper view)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Both papers resolved from graph data
[ ] Metrics shown in parallel columns
[ ] Winner highlighting (bold gold) on each metric
[ ] Direct relationship shown if edge exists
[ ] Paper titles clickable with graph navigation
[ ] Tie handling (equal values)

VISUAL:
[ ] Two-column grid layout with VS separator
[ ] Field-colored left borders on headers
[ ] JetBrains Mono for metric values
[ ] Winner pulse animation on render

PERFORMANCE: [ ] Comparison rendering < 30ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [ ] Table role, header labels, winner indicated non-visually
EDGE CASES: [ ] Equal values, missing paper, different fields, mobile stacking

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

CHECK winner determination for all metric types. 5. VERIFY relationship section when edge exists vs does not. 6. BROKEN until all metrics and winner logic correct. 7. At least 1 fix per round. 8. Log below.

FEATURE #062: TIMELINE BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #015 (Timeline Narration), #055 (Multi-Modal Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #055 (Multi-Modal Responses -- requires block infrastructure)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Timeline Block renders a chronological visualization of events within
Athena's response. Research lineages are inherently temporal: papers are
published in sequence, ideas evolve over years, mutations propagate through
generations. The Timeline Block transforms temporal data into a visual
horizontal strip showing key events positioned along a time axis, revealing
patterns of acceleration, stagnation, and convergence that prose alone cannot
communicate.

The timeline renders publication events as nodes along a horizontal axis
scaled by year. Each node represents a paper, colored by its primary field
of study (using the standard field color palette) and sized proportionally
to its PageRank score within the graph. Edges between adjacent timeline nodes
show the mutation_type of the relationship, creating a visual "chain of
inheritance" across time.

The timeline supports three display densities. Compact mode shows only the
highlighted papers (those the LLM explicitly mentions) with year labels and
short titles. Expanded mode shows all papers in the lineage on the timeline,
with highlighted papers larger and labeled. Full mode adds mutation_type
labels on edges and vertical event markers for significant moments (the year
a contradiction was published, the year a paper was retracted, the year a
new DNA cluster emerged).

The visual design uses a thin horizontal line (1px, text-muted) as the time
axis, with year markers at regular intervals. Paper nodes sit above the line
(positive influence papers, those with adoption/generalization mutations
flowing outward) or below the line (contradictions, negative citations,
retractions). This creates a visual "river of ideas" where mainstream
evolution flows above and disruptions appear below.

Hover interactions are rich. Hovering a timeline node shows a compact tooltip
with paper title, citation count, PageRank, and primary mutation_type.
Hovering the space between two nodes shows the edge details: mutation_type,
citation_intent, confidence tier. Clicking any node navigates to it in the
main graph.

The timeline integrates with the Arivu concept of "intellectual archaeology."
A researcher looking at a timeline can immediately see temporal clusters
(periods where many papers were published), gaps (years with no publications),
and acceleration patterns (increasing density of publications over time).
These visual patterns prompt questions that Athena can answer: "Why was there
a gap between 2015 and 2017?" or "What triggered the burst of publications
in 2018?"

The LLM produces <TIMELINE events="year1:paper_id1,year2:paper_id2"
highlight="paper_id1,paper_id3" density="compact"/> markers. The events
attribute lists year-paper pairs in chronological order. The highlight
attribute marks which papers to emphasize. The density attribute controls
the display mode.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena produces a response about the chronological evolution of ideas. The LLM
embeds a <TIMELINE/> marker when temporal context aids understanding.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Show me how attention mechanisms evolved over time." Athena
   streams prose about the evolution, then a Timeline block renders.
2. The timeline shows a horizontal axis from 2014 to 2023. Key papers appear
   as colored nodes: Bahdanau (2014), Luong (2015), Vaswani (2017), BERT (2018),
   GPT-2 (2019), GPT-3 (2020).
3. Highlighted papers (Bahdanau, Vaswani, BERT) are larger with titles visible.
   Non-highlighted papers are smaller dots.
4. Edges between adjacent nodes are colored: Bahdanau->Vaswani shows
   "generalization" in gold, Vaswani->BERT shows "specialization" in teal.
5. The user notices a gap between 2015 and 2017 (no highlighted nodes). They
   hover the gap area. Tooltip: "2 papers in this period, not highlighted."
6. User hovers the Vaswani node. Tooltip: "Vaswani et al. (2017) - Attention
   Is All You Need - PageRank: 0.0847 - 216,247 citations."
7. User clicks BERT. The main graph navigates to BERT.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Timeline rendered with chronological paper nodes. Highlighted papers labeled.
Mutation edges colored. All nodes interactive.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/blocks/timeline.js: TimelineRenderer class, SVG generation, layout.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: Registers TimelineRenderer for TIMELINE type.
- static/css/blocks/timeline.css: Timeline axis, node styles, density modes.
- No backend changes (data resolved from in-memory graph).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class TimelineRenderer {
    constructor(data) {
      this.events = data.events;
      this.highlight = data.highlight || [];
      this.density = data.density || 'compact';
      this.width = 600;
      this.height = 120;
    }

    render() {
      const container = document.createElement('div');
      container.className = 'athena-block timeline-block';
      container.innerHTML = '<div class="block-label">TIMELINE</div>';

      const svg = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'svg');
      svg.setAttribute('viewBox', `0 0 ${this.width} ${this.height}`);
      svg.setAttribute('role', 'img');
      svg.setAttribute('aria-label',
        `Timeline from ${this.yearRange[0]} to ${this.yearRange[1]} showing ${this.events.length} papers`);

      this.renderAxis(svg);
      this.renderEdges(svg);
      this.renderNodes(svg);
      container.appendChild(svg);
      return container;
    }

    get yearRange() {
      const years = this.events.map(e => e.year);
      return [Math.min(...years), Math.max(...years)];
    }

    xPosition(year) {
      const [minY, maxY] = this.yearRange;
      const padding = 40;
      return padding + ((year - minY) / (maxY - minY)) * (this.width - 2 * padding);
    }

    nodeRadius(paperId) {
      if (this.highlight.includes(paperId)) return 8;
      return 4;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Timeline data is resolved from the in-memory graph on the
  frontend. The LLM provides paper_ids and years; the frontend looks up
  metadata and edge data from the cached graph JSON.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.timeline-block {
  padding: 8px; overflow-x: auto;
}
.timeline-block svg {
  width: 100%; min-width: 400px; height: auto;
}
.tl-axis {
  stroke: var(--text-muted); stroke-width: 1;
}
.tl-year-label {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px;
  fill: var(--text-muted); text-anchor: middle;
}
.tl-node {
  cursor: pointer; transition: r 150ms ease;
}
.tl-node:hover { r: 10; }
.tl-node.highlighted {
  stroke: var(--accent-gold); stroke-width: 2;
}
.tl-node-label {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 9px;
  fill: var(--text-secondary); text-anchor: middle;
}
.tl-edge { fill: none; stroke-width: 1.5; }
.tl-edge.generalization { stroke: var(--accent-gold); }
.tl-edge.adoption { stroke: var(--accent-blue); }
.tl-edge.specialization { stroke: var(--accent-teal); }
.tl-edge.contradiction { stroke: var(--danger); }
.tl-below { transform: translateY(20px); /* Below axis for contradictions */ }
```

API CONTRACTS: No API calls. Data embedded in block marker, resolved from graph.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Compact Timeline

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |  TIMELINE                                 |
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  |  |                                       ||
  |  |    Bahdanau    Vaswani   BERT  GPT-2  ||
  |  |       ●===========●=======●=====●    ||
  |  |   ----+----+----+----+----+----+----  ||
  |  |      2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019    ||
  |  |                                       ||
  |  +---------------------------------------+|
  +-------------------------------------------+

  ● = highlighted node (large, labeled)
  ○ = non-highlighted node (small, no label)
  === = edge colored by mutation_type

STATE: Paper Below Axis (Contradiction)

  |  |       ●=========●========●            ||
  |  |   ----+----+----+----+----+----       ||
  |  |                       ○               ||
  |  |                   Contradiction        ||

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM emits <TIMELINE events="2014:id1,2017:id2,2018:id3" highlight="id1,id2" density="compact"/>
  -> Block system detects TIMELINE marker
  -> TimelineRenderer receives events, highlight, density
  -> Year range computed from events
  -> X positions calculated proportionally along axis
  -> Nodes rendered at computed positions (size by highlight status)
  -> Edges looked up from graph data between consecutive events
  -> Edge colors set by mutation_type
  -> Labels shown for highlighted nodes only (compact mode)
  -> Hover on node -> tooltip with paper metadata
  -> Click on node -> navigate to paper in main graph

DATA STORED: NONE (rendered from live graph data)
DATA CACHED: Timeline layout positions in renderer instance

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

REQUIRES LLM: Partially (LLM selects which events to show)

MODEL: Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile (for deciding when to embed a timeline)
STAGE: Generation (LLM embeds <TIMELINE/> markers in response text)

PROMPT SECTION: Part of "BLOCK FORMAT RULES" (see Feature #055)

ADDITIONAL PROMPT:
```
Use <TIMELINE events="year1:paper_id1,year2:paper_id2,..."
  highlight="paper_id1,paper_id3" density="compact|expanded|full"/>
when:
- Explaining chronological evolution of ideas
- Showing when key papers were published relative to each other
- Highlighting temporal gaps or bursts in the lineage
Events must be in chronological order. Use paper_ids from graph data.
Maximum 20 events per timeline. Highlight the most important 3-5.
```

EXPECTED OUTPUT FORMAT:
"The evolution spans two decades:
<TIMELINE events="2014:330da625...,2017:204e3073...,2018:df2b0e26..."
  highlight="204e3073...,df2b0e26..." density="compact"/>"

FALLBACK: If events contain unknown paper_ids, render as text list with years.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~40 tokens per timeline marker

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Timeline node        | Hover       | Tooltip: paper title, year, PageRank        |
| Timeline node        | Click       | Navigate to paper in main graph             |
| Timeline edge        | Hover       | Tooltip: mutation_type, confidence tier     |
| Year label           | Visual only | Shows year markers along axis               |
| Timeline container   | Scroll      | Horizontal scroll if wider than container   |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Nodes fade-in staggered by year 50ms each. Hover radius increase 150ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: SVG role="img" with descriptive aria-label. Nodes accessible via tab.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: All events in the same year
   Expected: Nodes stacked vertically at the same X position.
   Implementation: Collision detection with vertical offset.

2. Scenario: Timeline spans 50+ years (very old lineage)
   Expected: Year labels shown every 10 years instead of every year.
   Implementation: Adaptive label interval based on year range.

3. Scenario: Only 2 events in timeline
   Expected: Simple two-node timeline with clear edge between them.
   Implementation: Minimum layout handles small event counts.

4. Scenario: Event paper has no edge to adjacent paper
   Expected: Nodes shown without connecting edge. Gap in chain visible.
   Implementation: Edge rendering checks adjacency in graph data.

5. Scenario: Container is very narrow (mobile)
   Expected: Horizontal scroll enabled. Touch scrolling works.
   Implementation: overflow-x: auto with min-width on SVG.

6. Scenario: Paper is a contradiction (negative citation)
   Expected: Node rendered below the axis line in danger-red color.
   Implementation: Check citation_intent for "direct_contradiction."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENABLES: #015 (Timeline Narration -- uses this block as primary visualization)
ENHANCES: #054 (Thinking Display -- temporal analysis shown as timeline)

EXTERNAL: NONE

FEATURE #063: STAT DASHBOARD BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #011 (Artifact Cards)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #038 (Markdown Rendering), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Stat Dashboard Block is a structured response element that Athena uses
whenever the answer to a user query is best expressed as a compact grid of
numerical or categorical statistics. Instead of burying numbers inside
flowing prose, Athena renders them in a dot matrix-styled dashboard card
where each statistic occupies its own labeled cell with optional trend
indicators, sparklines, and color-coded severity badges.

This block is essential because Arivu generates a large volume of
quantitative data: PageRank scores, pruning_impact percentages, citation
counts, mutation_type distributions, DNA cluster weights, diversity radar
scores, confidence tier breakdowns, and temporal trends. Presenting these
numbers as sentences forces users to parse narrative when they really need
to scan and compare. A dashboard block lets the eye jump directly to the
metric that matters.

The block is emitted by the LLM as a fenced directive inside the Markdown
stream. The streaming parser detects the opening fence and begins buffering
until the closing fence arrives. Once complete, the AthenaRenderer inflates
the block into a CSS Grid card with 2 to 4 columns depending on how many
stats are present. Each stat cell shows a label (muted), a value (large,
bold), an optional delta indicator (green up arrow, red down arrow, gray
dash for neutral), and an optional micro-sparkline rendered as an inline
SVG path of at most 20 data points.

The stat values support three display formats: raw number ("1,247"),
percentage ("31.4%"), and categorical ("HIGH" with a confidence tier
badge). The LLM specifies which format to use per stat via a format field
in the directive payload. Color coding follows the Arivu design system:
HIGH is conf-high green, MEDIUM is conf-medium blue, LOW is conf-low amber,
SPECULATIVE is conf-specul purple. Numbers above a threshold specified in
the directive turn gold (accent-gold) to highlight significance.

The block supports a title row and an optional footer row. The title is
displayed as a single line above the grid in text-secondary at 13px. The
footer can contain a single sentence of context (e.g., "Based on 152 nodes
and 203 edges in the current graph"). This keeps the stats grounded without
cluttering the grid.

Accessibility is handled by rendering an sr-only table equivalent beneath
the visual grid. Each stat cell maps to a table row with columns Label,
Value, Trend. Screen readers therefore receive a perfectly structured table
while sighted users see the visually rich grid. The grid itself uses
role="presentation" to avoid confusing assistive technology.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User asks a question whose answer is primarily quantitative, such as "What
are the key stats for this graph?" or "Compare PageRank scores of the top
5 papers."

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena determines that the response is best served as a stat dashboard.
   The LLM emits a ```athena-stats fenced block inside the streaming
   Markdown.
2. The streaming parser detects the opening fence and begins buffering the
   JSON payload. A skeleton placeholder with pulsing dot matrix cells
   appears immediately in the chat.
3. When the closing fence arrives, the AthenaRenderer parses the JSON and
   inflates the dashboard grid. The skeleton crossfades to the real
   dashboard over 200ms.
4. Each stat cell animates its value from 0 to the actual number using a
   countUp animation over 400ms, staggered 50ms per cell left to right.
5. If sparklines are present, they draw themselves with a stroke-dashoffset
   animation over 600ms after the countUp completes.
6. User hovers over a stat cell to see a tooltip with additional context
   (e.g., "PageRank: 0.0342 -- ranked #3 of 152 papers in this graph").

AFTER COMPLETION:
The stat dashboard persists in the chat as a static card. Values do not
update live. If the graph changes, the user must re-ask for updated stats.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: StatDashboardBlock class, countUp animation.
- static/js/athena-renderer.js: Detects ```athena-stats, dispatches to block.
- static/css/athena-blocks.css: Grid layout, stat cell styles, sparkline SVG.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Buffer logic for fenced directives.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-blocks.js
  class StatDashboardBlock {
    constructor(payload) {
      this.title = payload.title || null;
      this.stats = payload.stats; // Array of { label, value, format, delta, sparkline }
      this.footer = payload.footer || null;
      this.columns = Math.min(payload.stats.length, 4);
    }

    render() {
      const grid = document.createElement('div');
      grid.className = 'stat-dashboard';
      grid.style.gridTemplateColumns = `repeat(${this.columns}, 1fr)`;
      if (this.title) {
        const titleEl = document.createElement('div');
        titleEl.className = 'stat-dashboard__title';
        titleEl.textContent = this.title;
        grid.appendChild(titleEl);
      }
      this.stats.forEach((stat, i) => {
        const cell = this._renderCell(stat, i);
        grid.appendChild(cell);
      });
      return grid;
    }

    _renderCell(stat, index) {
      const cell = document.createElement('div');
      cell.className = 'stat-cell';
      cell.dataset.delay = index * 50;
      // label, value, delta, sparkline children
      return cell;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
The backend does not render blocks. The LLM is prompted to emit stat
dashboard directives when quantitative answers are detected. The system
prompt includes the block format specification so the LLM knows the
exact JSON schema to emit inside the fenced block.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.stat-dashboard {
  display: grid;
  gap: 12px;
  padding: 16px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 8px;
  margin: 8px 0;
}
.stat-dashboard__title {
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-size: 13px;
}
.stat-cell {
  padding: 12px;
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  border-radius: 6px;
}
.stat-cell__label {
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.5px;
}
.stat-cell__value {
  font-size: 24px;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
}
.stat-cell__delta--up { color: var(--success); }
.stat-cell__delta--down { color: var(--danger); }
.stat-cell__delta--neutral { color: var(--text-muted); }
```

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. LLM emits the block inside the streaming Markdown
response as a fenced directive. Format:

```athena-stats
{
  "title": "Graph Overview",
  "stats": [
    {"label": "Total Nodes", "value": 152, "format": "number"},
    {"label": "Pruning Impact", "value": 31.4, "format": "percent", "delta": "up"},
    {"label": "Confidence", "value": "HIGH", "format": "tier"},
    {"label": "Citations", "value": 1247, "format": "number", "sparkline": [3,7,12,18,24]}
  ],
  "footer": "Based on PageRank analysis of 152 nodes"
}
```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Stat Dashboard Rendered (4-column)

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Graph Overview                                              │
  │                                                              │
  │  ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐│
  │  │ TOTAL NODES│ │ PRUNING    │ │ CONFIDENCE │ │ CITATIONS  ││
  │  │            │ │ IMPACT     │ │            │ │            ││
  │  │   152      │ │  31.4%  +  │ │   HIGH     │ │  1,247     ││
  │  │            │ │            │ │   ●────    │ │  ──/\──    ││
  │  └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘│
  │                                                              │
  │  Based on PageRank analysis of 152 nodes                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Skeleton Loading

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░                                            │
  │                                                              │
  │  ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐ ┌────────────┐│
  │  │ ░░░░░░░░░  │ │ ░░░░░░░░░  │ │ ░░░░░░░░░  │ │ ░░░░░░░░░  ││
  │  │ ░░░░░░     │ │ ░░░░░░     │ │ ░░░░░░     │ │ ░░░░░░     ││
  │  └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘ └────────────┘│
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Skeleton to Real: crossfade 200ms ease-out.
CountUp: 400ms per cell, staggered 50ms.
Sparkline draw: stroke-dashoffset 600ms ease-in-out.
Hover on cell: background brightens to bg-elevated, 150ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

LLM generates ```athena-stats block inside Markdown stream
  → AthenaStream detects opening fence, enters buffer mode
  → Skeleton placeholder rendered in chat
  → Closing fence detected, buffer flushed
  → AthenaRenderer.parseStatBlock(json) validates schema
  → StatDashboardBlock.render() builds DOM grid
  → CountUp animations triggered per cell with stagger
  → Sparkline SVGs drawn after countUp
  → Tooltip listeners attached to each cell
  → sr-only table equivalent inserted for accessibility

ERROR PATH: Malformed JSON in block
  → AthenaRenderer falls back to raw code block display
  → Console warning logged: "Invalid stat block payload"

DATA STORED: Block content persisted as part of chat_history
DATA CACHED: None (stateless rendering)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROMPT INJECTION:
The system prompt includes a directive specification block that teaches the
LLM when and how to emit ```athena-stats blocks. The LLM is instructed to
use this block when: (a) the user asks for metrics or statistics, (b) the
answer involves 3 or more numerical values, (c) comparison of quantities
is the primary intent.

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant) for standard queries.
TOKEN BUDGET: The stat block JSON adds approximately 200-400 tokens to the
response. This is acceptable because it replaces 100-300 tokens of prose
that would otherwise describe the same numbers.

CONTEXT WINDOW USAGE: The stat block format specification in the system
prompt consumes approximately 150 tokens.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element            | Action    | Response                                      |
|--------------------|-----------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Stat cell          | Hover     | Background brightens, tooltip with context    |
| Stat cell          | Click     | Copies value to clipboard                     |
| Sparkline          | Hover     | Tooltip shows individual data point values    |
| Title row          | None      | Static display                                |
| Footer row         | None      | Static display                                |
| Entire block       | Long-press| "Copy stats as text" option appears           |

ANIMATION DETAILS: CountUp 400ms ease-out per cell, 50ms stagger. Sparkline stroke-dashoffset 600ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: sr-only table with Label, Value, Trend columns. role="presentation" on visual grid.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: LLM emits more than 8 stats in a single dashboard
   Expected: Grid wraps to additional rows. Max 4 columns maintained.
   Implementation: CSS grid auto-rows handles overflow naturally.

2. Scenario: Stat value is extremely long (e.g., 1,234,567,890)
   Expected: Font size reduces within cell using clamp(). No overflow.
   Implementation: font-size: clamp(14px, 3vw, 24px) on .stat-cell__value.

3. Scenario: Sparkline data array is empty
   Expected: Sparkline area hidden. Cell renders without it.
   Implementation: Conditional render only if sparkline array length > 1.

4. Scenario: JSON in the fenced block is malformed (truncated stream)
   Expected: Falls back to rendering as a raw code block.
   Implementation: try/catch around JSON.parse, fallback to code display.

5. Scenario: User asks for stats but graph has only 1 node
   Expected: LLM generates prose instead of a stat block (too few values).
   Implementation: LLM prompt instructs minimum 3 stats for a dashboard.

6. Scenario: Multiple stat blocks in a single response
   Expected: Each block rendered independently, stacked vertically.
   Implementation: AthenaRenderer handles each fenced block separately.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #038 (Markdown Rendering), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: #064 (Equation/Formula Block uses similar block infrastructure)
ENHANCES: #001 (Context-Aware Responses: stats are context-driven)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None (pure frontend rendering)
