FEATURE #069: PINNED MESSAGES
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Conversation Mgmt
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #006 (Conversation Memory)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Pinned Messages allows users to bookmark specific messages in their Athena
conversation for quick reference. In a long research session analyzing a
paper's ancestry graph, the user might discover a critical insight buried
at message 12 of a 40-message conversation: "The key bottleneck in this
lineage is the 2003 Bengio paper, which has a pruning_impact of 31.4%."
Without pinning, finding this message again requires scrolling through
the entire history. With pinning, the user clicks a pin icon on the
message, and it appears in a persistent "Pinned" sidebar panel accessible
from the chat header.

Pinned messages serve as the user's personal annotation layer over the
conversation. They mark the moments that matter: the PageRank revelation,
the retraction warning, the mutation_type insight, the DNA cluster
discovery. This transforms the chat from a linear stream into a curated
knowledge base where the most important findings are always one click
away.

The pin action is simple: hover over any message to reveal a pin icon in
the message header. Click it to pin. The icon fills in (outline to solid)
and a subtle gold left border appears on the pinned message. Clicking
again unpins. The pinned messages panel in the sidebar shows all pinned
messages as compact cards sorted by conversation order, each showing the
first 100 characters of the message content with a timestamp and a
"Jump to" link that scrolls the chat to the original message position.

Pinned messages are stored in the backend as metadata on the chat_history
rows. A pinned_at TIMESTAMP column (nullable) on chat_history marks which
messages are pinned. When pinned_at is non-null, the message is pinned.
This avoids a separate junction table and keeps the data model simple.

The pinned messages panel supports drag-and-drop reordering. While the
default order is chronological (by message index), the user can drag cards
to reorder them by personal priority. The reordered list is stored in
session storage on the frontend (not persisted to backend) since
reordering is a lightweight preference, not critical data.

Pinned messages interact with the graph. When a pinned message references
a specific paper (detected by paper_id mentions in the message text),
the pin card shows a small node indicator that, when clicked, highlights
that paper in the graph view. This creates a direct bridge between
conversation insights and graph navigation.

The feature also supports "pin with note": the user can right-click a
message and select "Pin with note" to attach a personal annotation (up
to 200 characters) that appears below the message preview in the pinned
panel. This note is stored alongside the pinned_at metadata.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User hovers over a message and clicks the pin icon, or right-clicks and
selects "Pin" or "Pin with note."

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User is reading message 12 where Athena describes a critical
   pruning_impact result. User hovers over the message; a pin icon appears
   in the top-right corner.
2. User clicks the pin icon. The icon fills in (outline to solid gold).
   A gold left border animates onto the message over 150ms. A toast
   appears: "Message pinned."
3. User continues the conversation for 28 more messages.
4. User clicks the "Pins" button in the chat header. A sidebar panel
   slides in from the right showing 1 pinned message card.
5. The card shows: "Athena: The key bottleneck in this lineage is the
   2003 Bengio paper..." with a timestamp and a "Jump to" link.
6. User clicks "Jump to." The chat scrolls to message 12 with a brief
   highlight pulse (gold border flashes twice over 400ms).

AFTER COMPLETION:
Message remains pinned with gold border. Pinned panel accessible from
header. Pin state persists across page refreshes (stored in backend).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-pins.js: PinManager class, sidebar panel.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Pin icon in message header, hover reveal.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Pin icon styles, gold border, sidebar panel.
- app.py: /api/chat/pin POST and /api/chat/pins GET endpoints.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-pins.js
  class PinManager {
    constructor() {
      this.pinnedMessages = [];
    }

    async pin(messageId, note = null) {
      const response = await fetch('/api/chat/pin', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ session_id: currentSessionId, message_id: messageId, note })
      });
      if (response.ok) {
        this.pinnedMessages.push({ messageId, note, pinnedAt: new Date() });
        this.markMessageAsPinned(messageId);
        this.updateSidebarPanel();
      }
    }

    async unpin(messageId) {
      await fetch('/api/chat/pin', {
        method: 'DELETE',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ session_id: currentSessionId, message_id: messageId })
      });
      this.pinnedMessages = this.pinnedMessages.filter(p => p.messageId !== messageId);
      this.unmarkMessageAsPinned(messageId);
      this.updateSidebarPanel();
    }

    jumpTo(messageId) {
      const el = document.querySelector(`[data-message-id="${messageId}"]`);
      if (el) {
        el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' });
        el.classList.add('pin-highlight-pulse');
        setTimeout(() => el.classList.remove('pin-highlight-pulse'), 400);
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py
  @app.route('/api/chat/pin', methods=['POST'])
  def pin_message():
      data = request.json
      session_id = data['session_id']
      message_id = data['message_id']
      note = data.get('note')
      db.execute(
          "UPDATE chat_history
             SET pinned_at = NOW(), pin_note = %s
             WHERE session_id = %s AND id = %s",
          (note, session_id, message_id)
      )
      return jsonify({"status": "pinned"})

  @app.route('/api/chat/pins', methods=['GET'])
  def get_pinned_messages():
      session_id = request.args['session_id']
      rows = db.fetchall(
          "SELECT id, role, content, pinned_at, pin_note
             FROM chat_history
             WHERE session_id = %s AND pinned_at IS NOT NULL
             ORDER BY id",
          (session_id,)
      )
      return jsonify(rows)

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.chat-message--pinned {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-gold);
}
.pin-icon {
  opacity: 0;
  transition: opacity 150ms;
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--text-muted);
}
.chat-message:hover .pin-icon { opacity: 1; }
.pin-icon--active { color: var(--accent-gold); opacity: 1; }
.pin-highlight-pulse {
  animation: pinPulse 400ms ease-out;
}
@keyframes pinPulse {
  0%, 100% { border-left-color: var(--accent-gold); }
  50% { border-left-color: var(--warning); }
}
.pinned-panel {
  width: 320px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border-left: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  overflow-y: auto;
  padding: 16px;
}
.pinned-card {
  padding: 12px;
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  border-radius: 6px;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
}
.pinned-card__preview {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-line-clamp: 3;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/chat/pin
  Request: { session_id, message_id, note (optional, max 200 chars) }
  Response: { status: "pinned" }

DELETE /api/chat/pin
  Request: { session_id, message_id }
  Response: { status: "unpinned" }

GET /api/chat/pins?session_id=...
  Response: [{ id, role, content, pinned_at, pin_note }]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Message With Pin Icon (Hover)

  │  paper, which has a pruning_impact of 31.4%. Removing    │
  │  it would collapse 47 downstream papers.                  │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Pinned Message (Gold Border)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┃ Athena:                                       [unpin]  │
  │  ┃ The key bottleneck in this lineage is the 2003 Bengio │
  │  ┃ paper, which has a pruning_impact of 31.4%.           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Pinned Messages Sidebar

  ┌──────────────────────────┐
  │  Pinned Messages (3)     │
  │                          │
  │  ┌──────────────────────┐│
  │  │ "The key bottleneck  ││
  │  │  in this lineage..." ││
  │  │  12:34 PM   [Jump]   ││
  │  └──────────────────────┘│
  │                          │
  │  ┌──────────────────────┐│
  │  │ "Retraction detected ││
  │  │  in Paper X (2015)." ││
  │  │  12:41 PM   [Jump]   ││
  │  │  Note: Critical!     ││
  │  └──────────────────────┘│
  │                          │
  │  ┌──────────────────────┐│
  │  │ "DNA cluster #2 is   ││
  │  │  primarily hybrid..." ││
  │  │  12:48 PM   [Jump]   ││
  │  └──────────────────────┘│
  └──────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Pin action: icon fills in 150ms, gold border slides in from left 150ms.
Unpin action: reverse of pin. Sidebar panel slides in from right 200ms.
Jump to: smooth scroll + highlight pulse 400ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks pin icon on message
  → PinManager.pin(messageId) called
  → POST /api/chat/pin sent to backend
  → Backend updates chat_history row: pinned_at = NOW(), pin_note = note
  → Frontend marks message with gold border and filled pin icon
  → Pinned panel sidebar updated with new card

User clicks "Jump to" in pinned panel
  → PinManager.jumpTo(messageId) called
  → Chat container scrolls to message element
  → Highlight pulse animation plays (400ms)

User opens pinned panel
  → GET /api/chat/pins fetches all pinned messages
  → Pinned cards rendered in chronological order
  → Drag-and-drop reorder stored in sessionStorage

ERROR PATH: Pin request fails (network error)
  → Toast: "Failed to pin message. Try again."
  → Pin icon reverts to outline state

DATA STORED: pinned_at and pin_note on chat_history rows
DATA CACHED: Pinned message list cached in PinManager.pinnedMessages

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. Pinning is a user-driven action with no LLM involvement.
The LLM is not aware of which messages are pinned.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Pin icon (outline)   | Click        | Pins message, icon fills gold            |
| Pin icon (filled)    | Click        | Unpins message, icon returns to outline  |
| Message              | Right-click  | Context menu: "Pin" or "Pin with note"   |
| Pinned card          | Click        | Scrolls to and highlights original msg   |
| Pinned card          | Drag         | Reorders within sidebar panel            |
| "Pins" header button | Click        | Toggles pinned sidebar panel             |
| Pin note             | Click        | Editable inline text field               |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Pin fill 150ms, gold border 150ms, highlight pulse 400ms, sidebar 200ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Pin icon aria-label="Pin this message". Sidebar panel role="complementary".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User pins more than 20 messages
   Expected: Sidebar scrolls. No hard limit on pins.
   Implementation: Pinned panel has overflow-y: auto.

2. Scenario: Pinned message is edited after pinning
   Expected: Pinned card preview updates to reflect edited content.
   Implementation: PinManager listens for message edit events.

3. Scenario: Pinned message is deleted
   Expected: Pin automatically removed. Card disappears from sidebar.
   Implementation: Delete handler calls PinManager.unpin().

4. Scenario: User tries to pin while offline
   Expected: Pin deferred. Queued for sync when connection restores.
   Implementation: PinManager queues failed pin requests in localStorage.

5. Scenario: Jump to a message that has been scrolled far above viewport
   Expected: Smooth scroll may take up to 500ms. Visual feedback during
   scroll (sidebar card briefly highlights to confirm action registered).
   Implementation: scrollIntoView with behavior: 'smooth'.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #068 (Message Grouping: pinned messages span groups)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None

FEATURE #070: MULTI-SELECT MODE
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Conversation Mgmt
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #069 (Pinned Messages), #068 (Message Grouping)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Multi-Select Mode enables the user to select multiple messages in the
Athena conversation and perform batch operations on them. In a standard
chat interface, actions apply to individual messages one at a time: copy
one message, delete one message, pin one message. Multi-Select Mode breaks
this limitation by allowing the user to check multiple messages and then
apply a single action to all of them simultaneously.

This feature addresses a real workflow in Arivu: after a long research
session analyzing a paper's ancestry graph, the user wants to extract the
5 most important Athena responses into a single document. Without
multi-select, they must copy each response individually, switch to their
document editor, paste, switch back, find the next response, copy, switch,
paste, repeat. With multi-select, they check 5 messages, click "Copy
selected," and paste a clean, formatted compilation.

Multi-Select Mode is activated by a toggle button in the chat header (an
icon showing two overlapping checkboxes). When activated, the chat enters
selection mode: each message gets a checkbox on its left edge, the
background dims slightly, and a floating action bar appears at the bottom
of the chat showing available batch actions. The available actions are:

COPY: Copies all selected messages as formatted text (Markdown) to the
clipboard. Messages are ordered by conversation position. Each message
is prefixed with its role ("You:" or "Athena:") and separated by blank
lines. If selected messages contain code blocks, stat dashboards, or
other structured blocks, these are included in their raw Markdown form.

PIN: Pins all selected messages simultaneously. Equivalent to clicking
the pin icon on each, but in one action. Useful for pinning an entire
exchange (question + answer) as a unit.

DELETE: Deletes all selected messages from the conversation. Requires
a confirmation dialog: "Delete N selected messages? This cannot be
undone." Deleted messages are removed from chat_history in the backend.

EXPORT: Exports selected messages as a standalone document. Opens the
export dialog with only the selected content pre-populated. Supports
Markdown, plain text, and PDF formats.

SUMMARIZE: Sends the selected messages to Athena with the prompt
"Summarize the following exchange in 2-3 sentences:" and the message
content. Athena generates a concise summary that the user can pin or
copy. This is valuable for distilling a long back-and-forth into a
single insight.

The selection state is purely frontend. Checkboxes are rendered via CSS
pseudo-elements and JavaScript event handlers. The selection set is
stored in a JavaScript Set object. When multi-select mode is deactivated
(toggle off or Escape key), the selection is cleared and the normal chat
view is restored.

Multi-Select Mode interacts with Message Grouping (#068). When a group
divider is checked, all messages in that group are selected. When a group
is collapsed, its checkbox reflects the aggregate state: checked if all
messages are selected, indeterminate if some are selected, unchecked if
none are selected.

The feature supports keyboard-driven selection for efficiency: Shift+Click
selects a range of messages between the last selected and the clicked
message (like file managers). Ctrl+A selects all messages. Escape exits
multi-select mode.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks the multi-select toggle button in the chat header, or
long-presses a message (mobile), or presses Ctrl+Shift+S.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks the multi-select toggle in the chat header. The chat
   enters selection mode: background dims slightly, checkboxes appear
   on the left edge of each message with a slide-in animation (150ms).
2. A floating action bar appears at the bottom: "0 selected" with
   grayed-out action buttons (Copy, Pin, Delete, Export, Summarize).
3. User clicks the checkbox on message 12. The checkbox fills. The
   action bar updates: "1 selected" and buttons become active.
4. User Shift+clicks message 18. Messages 12 through 18 are all
   selected (7 messages). Action bar: "7 selected."
5. User clicks "Copy." All 7 messages are copied to the clipboard as
   formatted Markdown. A toast confirms: "7 messages copied."
6. User presses Escape. Multi-select mode deactivates. Checkboxes
   slide out. Action bar disappears. Normal chat view restored.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Chat returns to normal view. No persistent state from multi-select mode.
Copied content is in the clipboard ready to paste.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-multiselect.js: MultiSelectManager class.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Toggle button, mode switching.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Selection mode styles, action bar.
- app.py: /api/chat/bulk-delete DELETE endpoint.
- backend/chat_guide.py: Summarize selected messages.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-multiselect.js
  class MultiSelectManager {
    constructor(chatContainer) {
      this.container = chatContainer;
      this.selected = new Set();
      this.active = false;
      this.lastSelected = null;
    }

    activate() {
      this.active = true;
      this.container.classList.add('multiselect-active');
      this.renderCheckboxes();
      this.showActionBar();
    }

    deactivate() {
      this.active = false;
      this.selected.clear();
      this.lastSelected = null;
      this.container.classList.remove('multiselect-active');
      this.removeCheckboxes();
      this.hideActionBar();
    }

    toggleMessage(messageId, shiftKey) {
      if (shiftKey && this.lastSelected !== null) {
        this.selectRange(this.lastSelected, messageId);
      } else if (this.selected.has(messageId)) {
        this.selected.delete(messageId);
      } else {
        this.selected.add(messageId);
      }
      this.lastSelected = messageId;
      this.updateActionBar();
    }

    async copySelected() {
      const messages = this.getSelectedMessages();
      const formatted = messages.map(m =>
        `${m.role === 'user' ? 'You' : 'Athena'}: ${m.content}`
      ).join('\n\n');
      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(formatted);
    }

    async deleteSelected() {
      if (!confirm(`Delete ${this.selected.size} selected messages?`)) return;
      await fetch('/api/chat/bulk-delete', {
        method: 'DELETE',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          session_id: currentSessionId,
          message_ids: Array.from(this.selected)
        })
      });
      this.selected.forEach(id => {
        document.querySelector(`[data-message-id="${id}"]`).remove();
      });
      this.deactivate();
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py
  @app.route('/api/chat/bulk-delete', methods=['DELETE'])
  def bulk_delete_messages():
      data = request.json
      session_id = data['session_id']
      message_ids = data['message_ids']
      db.execute(
          "DELETE FROM chat_history
             WHERE session_id = %s AND id = ANY(%s)",
          (session_id, message_ids)
      )
      return jsonify({"deleted": len(message_ids)})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.multiselect-active .chat-message {
  padding-left: 48px;
  position: relative;
}
.multiselect-active .chat-message::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 16px;
  top: 50%;
  transform: translateY(-50%);
  width: 18px;
  height: 18px;
  border: 2px solid var(--text-muted);
  border-radius: 4px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 150ms;
}
.multiselect-active .chat-message.selected::before {
  background: var(--accent-teal);
  border-color: var(--accent-teal);
}
.multiselect-active .chat-message.selected::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 20px;
  top: calc(50% - 3px);
  width: 5px;
  height: 10px;
  border: solid white;
  border-width: 0 2px 2px 0;
  transform: rotate(45deg);
}
.action-bar {
  position: sticky;
  bottom: 0;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border-top: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  padding: 8px 16px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
  z-index: 10;
}
.action-bar__count {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  color: var(--accent-teal);
  font-size: 14px;
}
.action-bar__btn {
  padding: 6px 14px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  color: var(--text-primary);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 13px;
}
.action-bar__btn:disabled {
  opacity: 0.4;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}
.action-bar__btn--danger { border-color: var(--danger); color: var(--danger); }
```

API CONTRACTS:
DELETE /api/chat/bulk-delete
  Request: { session_id, message_ids: [int] }
  Response: { deleted: int }
  Permanently removes messages from chat_history.

POST /api/chat/summarize-selected
  Request: { session_id, message_ids: [int] }
  Response: SSE stream with summary of selected messages
  Uses GROQ_FAST_MODEL to summarize the selected content.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Multi-Select Mode Active

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [x] Toggle Multi-Select                                  │
  │                                                           │
  │  [ ] You: Show me the graph for Attention Is All...       │
  │  [x] Athena: Building ancestry graph with 152 nodes...   │
  │  [ ] You: What are the mutation types?                    │
  │  [x] Athena: The graph contains 12 edges with...         │
  │  [ ] You: What about pruning impact?                      │
  │  [x] Athena: Removing the 2003 Bengio paper would...     │
  │                                                           │
  │  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
  │  3 selected  [Copy] [Pin] [Delete] [Export] [Summarize]  │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Action Bar With Selection Count

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  3 selected  [Copy] [Pin] [Delete] [Export] [Summarize]  │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Confirm Delete Dialog

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │           Delete 3 selected messages?                     │
  │                                                           │
  │        This action cannot be undone.                      │
  │                                                           │
  │              [Cancel]       [Delete]                      │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Enter multi-select: checkboxes slide in from left 150ms, action bar slides up 200ms.
Exit multi-select: reverse animations.
Checkbox check: fill animation 100ms.
Shift+Click range: all messages in range check simultaneously with 30ms stagger.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User activates multi-select mode
  → MultiSelectManager.activate() called
  → Chat enters selection mode (checkboxes, dimmed background)
  → Action bar appears with "0 selected" and disabled buttons

User selects messages (click, Shift+Click, Ctrl+A)
  → MultiSelectManager.toggleMessage() updates selected Set
  → Action bar count updates. Buttons enable when count > 0

User clicks "Copy"
  → MultiSelectManager.copySelected() gathers message content
  → Formatted as Markdown with role prefixes
  → navigator.clipboard.writeText() copies to clipboard
  → Toast: "N messages copied"

User clicks "Delete"
  → Confirmation dialog shown
  → If confirmed: DELETE /api/chat/bulk-delete
  → Backend deletes rows from chat_history
  → Frontend removes message elements from DOM
  → Multi-select mode deactivated

User clicks "Summarize"
  → POST /api/chat/summarize-selected with message_ids
  → Backend fetches message content, calls GROQ_FAST_MODEL
  → SSE streams summary as a new Athena message
  → Multi-select mode deactivated

ERROR PATH: Bulk delete fails
  → Toast: "Failed to delete messages. Try again."
  → Selection preserved for retry

DATA STORED: Deletion is permanent in chat_history. Summary is a new message.
DATA CACHED: Selection state in JavaScript Set (ephemeral).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PROMPT INJECTION:
The "Summarize" action sends selected messages to the LLM with the
system prompt: "You are Athena, a research intelligence assistant.
Summarize the following conversation exchange in 2-3 concise sentences,
focusing on the key findings about the research lineage."

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant).
TOKEN BUDGET: Selected messages typically 500-2000 tokens input. Summary
output approximately 100-200 tokens.
CONTEXT WINDOW USAGE: Summarize prompt adds approximately 50 tokens
overhead plus the selected message content.

CACHE: Summary results cached in llm_cache with key=SHA256("summarize:" +
sorted message_ids + ":" + message_content_hash).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Toggle button        | Click        | Enter/exit multi-select mode             |
| Message checkbox     | Click        | Toggle selection of that message          |
| Message checkbox     | Shift+Click  | Select range from last to current        |
| Ctrl+A               | Keydown      | Select all messages                      |
| Escape               | Keydown      | Exit multi-select mode, clear selection  |
| "Copy" action        | Click        | Copy all selected to clipboard           |
| "Pin" action         | Click        | Pin all selected messages                |
| "Delete" action      | Click        | Confirm dialog, then delete              |
| "Export" action      | Click        | Open export dialog with selected content |
| "Summarize" action   | Click        | LLM summarizes selected exchange         |
| Group checkbox       | Click        | Select/deselect all messages in group    |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Checkbox slide-in 150ms. Action bar slide-up 200ms. Range select stagger 30ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Checkboxes are aria-checked. Action bar role="toolbar". Keyboard shortcuts documented.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User activates multi-select with 0 messages in conversation
   Expected: Multi-select mode activates but shows "No messages to select."
   Implementation: Action bar shows disabled state with empty message.

2. Scenario: New message arrives while in multi-select mode
   Expected: New message appears with a checkbox. Selection not cleared.
   Implementation: New messages get checkboxes added dynamically.

3. Scenario: User selects 100+ messages and clicks "Summarize"
   Expected: Token budget exceeded. Only the first and last 10 messages
   sent to LLM with a note: "... (80 messages omitted for brevity) ..."
   Implementation: Summarize truncates to 20 messages with gap indicator.

4. Scenario: User clicks "Delete" and confirms, but network fails
   Expected: Error toast. Messages NOT removed from DOM (backend is source
   of truth). Selection preserved for retry.
   Implementation: Only remove DOM elements after successful API response.

5. Scenario: Shift+Click with no prior single-click (lastSelected is null)
   Expected: Behaves as a normal single-click (selects only that message).
   Implementation: Guard against null lastSelected in selectRange().

6. Scenario: User tries to delete messages from a shared/read-only session
   Expected: Delete button hidden or disabled. Only Copy and Export shown.
   Implementation: Check session permissions before rendering action bar.

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P3. Conversation Mgmt category. 14 hours estimated.
prediction engine uses three sources ranked by priority. First, it checks
the current ancestry graph for paper titles, author names, mutation types, and
field-specific terminology. If the user types "What is the impact of" and the
graph contains a paper titled "Attention Is All You Need," the suggestion
completes to "What is the impact of Attention Is All You Need?" because that
is the contextually most relevant completion. Second, it checks conversation
history for recently discussed concepts, paper IDs, and previously asked
questions to suggest continuations or refinements. Third, it falls back to a
static dictionary of common research query templates such as "How does [X]
relate to [Y]," "Explain the mutation between," and "Show me the pruning
impact of."

The suggestion computation runs entirely client-side with no backend calls.
The graph data is already loaded in the browser. The conversation history is
already in the DOM. The template dictionary is embedded in the JavaScript
module. This ensures zero-latency suggestions that appear within the same
animation frame as the user's keystroke.

A debounce of 100ms prevents excessive computation on rapid typing. The
suggestion only appears if the input has at least 3 characters, preventing
noise on very short inputs. When the input field is empty, no suggestion
appears. When the user backspaces to fewer than 3 characters, the ghost text
vanishes.

The ghost text is rendered as a separate span element positioned absolutely
within the input container, styled with the same font metrics as the input
text but with opacity 0.4 and pointer-events none. This prevents the ghost
text from interfering with cursor positioning or text selection.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User begins typing in the Athena chat input field after a graph is loaded.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has the Attention Is All You Need graph loaded with 152 nodes.
2. User types "What is" in the input field.
3. After 100ms debounce, the auto-complete engine scans graph context and finds
   that "What is the impact of" is a common pattern and the seed paper title
   matches. Ghost text appears: "the impact of Attention Is All You Need?"
   displayed in muted color after the cursor.
4. User continues typing "the muta" and the ghost text updates to "tion
   between Vaswani 2017 and Devlin 2018?" because mutation_type edges between
   those papers exist in the graph.
5. User presses Tab. The full suggestion commits to the input field, replacing
   the ghost text with real input text. Cursor moves to end.
6. User presses Enter to send. Normal message flow proceeds.

ALTERNATIVE PATH:
1. User types "Show me" and ghost text suggests "the pruning impact of
   Vaswani 2017."
2. User ignores the suggestion and types "all contradiction edges" instead.
3. Ghost text vanishes as the typed text diverges from the suggestion.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Accepted suggestion appears as the user's typed message. No visual indicator
that it was auto-completed. The message is treated identically.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-autocomplete.js: AutoCompleteEngine class. Graph-aware
  suggestion logic, debounce, ghost text rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Integration point. Passes graph data and
  conversation history references to AutoCompleteEngine on graph load.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Ghost text styles (.athena-ghost-text).
- templates/tool.html: Input container structure updated for absolute
  positioning of ghost text span.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. All auto-complete logic is client-side.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
class AutoCompleteEngine {
  constructor(graphData, chatHistory, templates) {
    this.graphData = graphData;
    this.chatHistory = chatHistory;
    this.templates = templates;
    this.debounceTimer = null;
    this.ghostEl = document.querySelector('.athena-ghost-text');
  }
  onInput(text) {
    clearTimeout(this.debounceTimer);
    if (text.length < 3) { this.ghostEl.textContent = ''; return; }
    this.debounceTimer = setTimeout(() => this.suggest(text), 100);
  }
  suggest(text) {
    const graphMatch = this.matchFromGraph(text);
    if (graphMatch) { this.showGhost(text, graphMatch); return; }
    const historyMatch = this.matchFromHistory(text);
    if (historyMatch) { this.showGhost(text, historyMatch); return; }
    const templateMatch = this.matchFromTemplates(text);
    if (templateMatch) { this.showGhost(text, templateMatch); return; }
    this.ghostEl.textContent = '';
  }
  matchFromGraph(text) {
    const lower = text.toLowerCase();
    // Check paper titles, author names, mutation types
    for (const node of this.graphData.nodes) {
      if (node.title.toLowerCase().startsWith(lower.slice(-20))) {
        return text + node.title.substring(lower.slice(-20).length);
      }
    }
    return null;
  }
  showGhost(typed, full) {
    this.ghostEl.textContent = full.substring(typed.length);
  }
  acceptSuggestion(inputEl) {
    if (!this.ghostEl.textContent) return false;
    inputEl.value += this.ghostEl.textContent;
    this.ghostEl.textContent = '';
    return true;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No backend API calls.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Ghost text visible

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ What is the muta|tion between Vaswani 2017 and Devlin  │   │
  │  │                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^     │   │
  │  │                  ghost text (opacity 0.4, muted)        │   │
  │  │                                                         │   │
  │  │  [Tab to accept]                           [Send ●]    │   │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Typed text:  var(--text-primary) #E2E8F0
  Ghost text:  var(--text-muted) #64748B, opacity 0.4
  Tab hint:    Small label, var(--text-muted), 11px

STATE: No suggestion (input too short or no match)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ Wh|                                                     │   │
  │  │                                            [Send ●]    │   │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Ghost text appears/disappears with 100ms opacity transition.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User types in input
  --> debounce 100ms
  --> AutoCompleteEngine.onInput(text)
  --> Check graph nodes/edges for title/author/mutation matches
  --> Check chatHistory for recent terms and patterns
  --> Check static templates for query pattern matches
  --> Render ghost text span after cursor position
  --> Tab key: accept suggestion, commit to input value
  --> Other keys: update or clear ghost text

ERROR PATH: Graph data not loaded yet --> auto-complete disabled, no ghost text
DATA STORED: NONE. Ephemeral client-side only.
DATA CACHED: Graph data reference (already in memory from graph load)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. All auto-complete logic is client-side pattern matching against
pre-loaded graph data, conversation history, and static templates. No LLM calls
are made for suggestions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Input field          | Typing      | Ghost text appears after 100ms debounce     |
| Tab key              | Keydown     | Accepts suggestion, commits to input        |
| Escape key           | Keydown     | Clears ghost text without accepting         |
| Arrow Right (at end) | Keydown     | Accepts suggestion (alternative to Tab)     |
| Any other key        | Keydown     | Continues typing, ghost updates or clears   |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Ghost text opacity transition 100ms ease-in-out
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Ghost text has aria-hidden="true". Screen readers skip it. Tab
acceptance is announced via aria-live region: "Suggestion accepted."

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Graph has 600 nodes with long titles
   Expected: Matching is capped at first 50 node titles sorted by citation
   count. Does not scan all 600 on every keystroke.
   Implementation: Pre-sort and cache top 50 titles on graph load.

2. Scenario: User types extremely fast (100+ WPM)
   Expected: Debounce at 100ms means max 10 computations per second. No jank.
   Implementation: clearTimeout before setTimeout on every input event.

3. Scenario: Tab key pressed when no ghost text is visible
   Expected: Normal tab behavior (focus moves to next element). No error.
   Implementation: acceptSuggestion returns false, event not preventDefault'd.

4. Scenario: Input contains special characters (LaTeX, Unicode)
   Expected: Matching still works. Ghost text preserves character encoding.
   Implementation: String comparison uses normalized Unicode (NFC).

5. Scenario: Multiple graph switches during conversation
   Expected: AutoCompleteEngine.graphData reference updates on graph switch.
   Old suggestions from previous graph do not appear.
   Implementation: Re-initialize engine on graph load event.

6. Scenario: Mobile device with on-screen keyboard
   Expected: Auto-complete disabled on touch devices. Ghost text positioning
   is unreliable with virtual keyboards.
   Implementation: Check for 'ontouchstart' in window, disable if true.

7. Scenario: User pastes a long block of text
   Expected: Auto-complete does not trigger on paste events.
   Implementation: Listen for 'input' event only, not 'paste' directly.
   The debounce handles paste since inputType will be 'insertFromPaste'.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001 (Context-Aware), #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming)
ENABLES: Faster query entry for all chat features
ENHANCES: #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups), #009 (Proactive Suggestions)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Graph data (already loaded), conversation history (already in DOM)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Ghost text appears after 100ms debounce when input has 3+ characters
[] Suggestions sourced from graph titles, authors, mutation types
[] Suggestions sourced from conversation history
[] Suggestions sourced from static template dictionary
[] Tab key accepts suggestion and commits to input
[] Escape key clears ghost text
[] Arrow Right at end of input accepts suggestion
[] Suggestions update on every keystroke (after debounce)
[] Graph switch re-initializes suggestion engine

VISUAL:
[] Ghost text in var(--text-muted) with opacity 0.4
[] Same font metrics as input text
[] "Tab to accept" hint visible when ghost text present

PERFORMANCE: [] Suggestion computation < 16ms (single frame budget)
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Ghost text aria-hidden, acceptance announced via aria-live
EDGE CASES: [] Mobile disabled, paste handled, special chars preserved

================================================================================
FEATURE #122: HANDWRITING/SKETCH INPUT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 20
OVERLAPS WITH:   #124 (Equation Input), #043 (File/Image Attachments)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #043 (File/Image Attachments), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Handwriting/Sketch Input adds a canvas drawing area to the Athena input region
where users can sketch diagrams, draw relationships, or write freehand notes
that are captured as images and sent alongside or instead of text messages.
This supports researchers who think visually and want to sketch a graph
structure, draw a hypothetical relationship, or annotate a concept before
asking Athena to interpret it. The canvas uses the HTML5 Canvas API with
pressure-sensitive pointer events for stylus support. Strokes are captured
as vector paths (not rasterized until send) allowing undo/redo per stroke.
On send, the canvas is rasterized to PNG, attached to the message, and the
image is passed to the LLM with a prompt asking for interpretation in the
context of the loaded ancestry graph.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks the sketch icon (pencil) in the Athena input toolbar, or uses
keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+D to toggle the drawing canvas.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks the pencil icon in the input toolbar.
2. A 300px tall canvas area slides open above the input field with toolbar:
   pen (black), highlighter (yellow, 50% opacity), eraser, undo, redo, clear.
3. User draws a rough diagram showing two circles labeled "A" and "B" with an
   arrow between them and a question mark above.
4. User types "Is this relationship real in the graph?" in the text input.
5. User presses Send. The canvas rasterizes to PNG, the message is sent with
   both the image attachment and the text query.
6. Athena responds: "Based on your sketch, you are asking about the relationship
   between papers A and B. In the loaded graph, paper [X] cites paper [Y] with
   a generalization mutation type..."
7. Canvas closes after send. Can be reopened for follow-up sketches.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Sketch image visible in chat as attachment thumbnail. Athena's response
references the sketch content.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-sketch.js: SketchCanvas class with stroke capture, tools,
  undo/redo stack, rasterization.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Sketch toggle integration, image attachment flow.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Canvas container, toolbar styles.
- app.py: Image attachment handling in /api/athena/chat endpoint.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  Image sent as base64 in message payload. LLM receives image description
  prompt with graph context. Uses config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL for image
  interpretation (if multimodal support available) or falls back to
  text-only description prompt.

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Request: { "message": "text", "session_id": "...", "sketch_image": "data:image/png;base64,..." }
Response: Standard Athena streaming response

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Canvas open with drawing

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ SKETCH CANVAS ──────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │  [Pen] [Highlight] [Eraser] [Undo] [Redo] [Clear]│    │
  │  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │    │
  │  │  │                                              │ │    │
  │  │  │      (A) ----?----> (B)                      │ │    │
  │  │  │                                              │ │    │
  │  │  │     User's freehand sketch here              │ │    │
  │  │  │                                              │ │    │
  │  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ Is this relationship real?          [Send ●]     │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Canvas slides open 200ms ease-out. Closes on send with 150ms slide.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User draws on canvas --> strokes stored as vector paths in memory
  --> User presses Send
  --> Canvas rasterized to PNG via canvas.toDataURL('image/png')
  --> Base64 image included in POST /api/athena/chat payload
  --> Backend extracts sketch_image, includes in LLM prompt context
  --> LLM interprets sketch alongside text message and graph data
  --> Response streamed back referencing sketch content

ERROR PATH: Canvas blank on send --> ignore sketch, send text only
DATA STORED: Sketch PNG in chat_history as message attachment metadata
DATA CACHED: Vector strokes in memory until canvas cleared or closed

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL
PROMPT: "The user has drawn a sketch alongside their message. The sketch shows [description]. Interpret this in the context of the loaded ancestry graph with {node_count} nodes. User's text: {message}"
FALLBACK: If model does not support image input, prompt includes "The user attached a hand-drawn sketch. Please respond to their text question: {message}"
TOKEN BUDGET: ~500 context + ~300 output

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Pencil icon          | Click       | Toggle canvas open/closed                   |
| Canvas area          | Pointer     | Draw stroke with active tool                |
| Pen tool             | Click       | Switch to black pen (2px)                   |
| Highlighter tool     | Click       | Switch to yellow highlight (8px, 50% alpha) |
| Eraser tool          | Click       | Switch to eraser (removes touched strokes)  |
| Undo button          | Click       | Remove last stroke from canvas              |
| Redo button          | Click       | Restore last undone stroke                  |
| Clear button         | Click       | Clear all strokes (with confirmation)       |
| Ctrl+Z               | Keydown     | Undo last stroke                            |
| Ctrl+Shift+Z         | Keydown     | Redo last undone stroke                     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Stroke drawing is immediate (no animation). Tool switch 100ms highlight.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Canvas has role="img" with aria-label. Toolbar buttons labeled. Not usable by screen readers (visual-only input method).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User sends with canvas open but empty (no strokes)
   Expected: No image attached, text-only message sent. Canvas closes.

2. Scenario: User draws on a touch device with finger (no stylus)
   Expected: Works with touch events. Pressure defaults to 0.5 for
   uniform stroke width.

3. Scenario: Canvas area too small for detailed sketches
   Expected: Pinch-to-zoom on touch devices. Scroll within canvas
   on desktop. Canvas can be resized by dragging bottom edge.

4. Scenario: User has 50+ strokes and presses clear
   Expected: Confirmation dialog: "Clear all strokes?" Undo stack also cleared.

5. Scenario: Very large sketch produces base64 over 1MB
   Expected: Compress PNG quality. If still over 1MB, resize canvas to 50%
   before rasterization. Warn user if sketch is too complex.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #043 (File/Image Attachments), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: Visual communication in research discussions
ENHANCES: #124 (Equation Input), #125 (Canvas Chat)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: HTML5 Canvas API, PointerEvent API

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Canvas opens/closes from toolbar icon and keyboard shortcut
[] Pen, highlighter, eraser tools work with pointer events
[] Undo/redo per stroke with Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z
[] Canvas rasterizes to PNG on send
[] Image attached to message and sent to backend
[] LLM receives sketch context in prompt
[] Canvas closes after send

VISUAL: [] Canvas slides open/closed, toolbar styled, strokes render smoothly
PERFORMANCE: [] Stroke rendering at 60fps, rasterization < 200ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Canvas labeled, toolbar buttons accessible
EDGE CASES: [] Empty canvas handled, touch input supported, large sketch compressed

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-sketch.js, athena-engine.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST drawing tools and send flow 4. CHECK rasterization quality 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified

================================================================================
FEATURE #123: TEMPLATE PROMPTS
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Proactive AI
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups), #121 (Auto-Complete Suggestions), #009 (Proactive Suggestions)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Template Prompts provides a library of pre-written, context-aware prompt
templates that users can select and customize before sending. Unlike auto-
complete which predicts what you are typing, Template Prompts presents full
structured queries organized by research task. The templates are categorized
into groups: Exploration ("What are the foundational papers in this lineage?"),
Analysis ("Compare the mutation patterns between [Paper A] and [Paper B]"),
Impact ("What happens if I remove [Paper X] from history?"), Writing ("Generate
a literature review paragraph about [topic]"), and Navigation ("Show me all
papers with contradiction edges").

When a graph is loaded, the template system scans the graph data and pre-fills
template variables with contextually relevant values. The seed paper title
fills [Paper X] slots. High-impact papers fill [Paper A] and [Paper B] slots.
Detected clusters fill [topic] slots. The user sees ready-to-send prompts
that require zero or minimal editing.

The template library is accessed via a button in the input toolbar (grid icon)
that opens a categorized dropdown. Each template shows a preview of the filled-
in text. Clicking a template inserts it into the input field where the user
can edit before sending. Templates with unfilled variables highlight the
variable in gold so the user knows what to customize.

The template definitions are stored as a static JSON structure in the
JavaScript module. No backend storage. New templates can be added by editing
the JSON array. Each template has an id, category, label, template string
with {variable} placeholders, and a priority score for sorting.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks the template icon (grid) in the Athena input toolbar, or types /
to open the template search.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has the Attention Is All You Need graph loaded.
2. User clicks the grid icon in the input toolbar.
3. A dropdown appears with categories: Exploration, Analysis, Impact, Writing,
   Navigation. Each category shows 3-5 templates.
4. User hovers over "What happens if I remove {seed_paper} from history?" in
   the Impact category. Preview shows the filled text: "What happens if I
   remove Attention Is All You Need from history?"
5. User clicks the template. The text inserts into the input field.
6. User optionally edits the text, then presses Enter to send.
7. Athena processes the message as a normal chat message.

ALTERNATIVE PATH (slash command):
1. User types "/" in the input field.
2. A filtered template list appears showing all templates matching typed text.
3. User types "/compare" and sees "Compare the mutation patterns between
   {paper_a} and {paper_b}" with top-impact papers pre-filled.
4. User selects the template. Text inserts into input.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Template text appears in input field. User sends as normal message.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-templates.js: TemplateEngine class. Template storage,
  variable resolution, dropdown rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Template dropdown integration, slash command
  detection.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Template dropdown styles, category headers,
  variable highlight styles.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Templates are client-side only.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
const TEMPLATES = [
  {
    id: 'explore_foundations',
    category: 'Exploration',
    label: 'Find foundations',
    template: 'What are the foundational papers in this lineage?',
    variables: [],
    priority: 10
  },
  {
    id: 'impact_remove',
    category: 'Impact',
    label: 'Remove from history',
    template: 'What happens if I remove {seed_paper} from history?',
    variables: ['seed_paper'],
    priority: 9
  },
  {
    id: 'analysis_compare',
    category: 'Analysis',
    label: 'Compare mutations',
    template: 'Compare the mutation patterns between {paper_a} and {paper_b}',
    variables: ['paper_a', 'paper_b'],
    priority: 8
  }
];

class TemplateEngine {
  constructor(graphData) {
    this.templates = TEMPLATES;
    this.graphData = graphData;
  }
  resolveVariables(template) {
    let text = template.template;
    const seed = this.graphData?.metadata?.seed_paper_title || '{seed_paper}';
    text = text.replace('{seed_paper}', seed);
    const topPapers = this.getTopPapers();
    text = text.replace('{paper_a}', topPapers[0]?.title || '{paper_a}');
    text = text.replace('{paper_b}', topPapers[1]?.title || '{paper_b}');
    return text;
  }
  getTopPapers() {
    return (this.graphData?.nodes || [])
      .sort((a, b) => (b.citation_count || 0) - (a.citation_count || 0))
      .slice(0, 5);
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No backend API calls.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Template dropdown open

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ TEMPLATES ───────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  EXPLORATION                                       │   │
  │  │  ○ What are the foundational papers?               │   │
  │  │  ○ List all mutation types in this lineage         │   │
  │  │  ○ Which papers have the highest impact?           │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  ANALYSIS                                          │   │
  │  │  ○ Compare mutations between {paper_a}, {paper_b}  │   │
  │  │  ○ What is the dominant mutation type?             │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  IMPACT                                            │   │
  │  │  ● What happens if I remove Attention Is All...    │   │
  │  │  ○ Show survival paths for the seed paper          │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ |                                    [Send ●]     │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Category headers:  var(--accent-gold) #D4A843, 12px uppercase
  Template items:    var(--text-primary), 14px
  Unfilled vars:     var(--accent-gold) highlight background
  Selected item:     var(--bg-elevated) background

TRANSITIONS: Dropdown slides down 150ms ease-out. Items fadeIn staggered 30ms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User clicks template icon or types /
  --> TemplateEngine renders categorized dropdown
  --> Each template's variables resolved against current graph data
  --> User clicks template
  --> Resolved text inserted into input field
  --> Unfilled variables highlighted in gold
  --> User edits and sends as normal message

ERROR PATH: No graph loaded --> templates shown without variable resolution (raw {variable} text)
DATA STORED: NONE. Templates are static client-side.
DATA CACHED: Resolved template text cached per graph_id in memory

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. Templates are client-side text insertion. The resulting message
is processed by the normal Athena chat pipeline.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Grid icon            | Click       | Toggle template dropdown                    |
| / key (empty input)  | Keydown     | Open template dropdown with search filter   |
| Template item        | Click       | Insert resolved text into input field       |
| Template item        | Hover       | Show full resolved preview text             |
| Category header      | Click       | Expand/collapse category group              |
| Escape key           | Keydown     | Close dropdown                              |
| Arrow keys           | Keydown     | Navigate dropdown items                     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Dropdown 150ms slide. Item hover 100ms background transition.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Dropdown role="listbox", items role="option". Arrow key navigation.
Category headers are aria-expanded toggles. Selected item announced.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Graph not loaded when templates opened
   Expected: Templates shown with raw {variable} placeholders in gold highlight.
   User can still select and manually fill in values.

2. Scenario: User types / followed by text that matches no template
   Expected: "No matching templates" message in dropdown. Dropdown stays open
   for user to browse categories.

3. Scenario: Template variable resolves to very long paper title (80+ chars)
   Expected: Title truncated to 60 chars with ellipsis in dropdown preview.
   Full title inserted into input field (not truncated).

4. Scenario: Multiple templates selected in sequence
   Expected: Each selection replaces input content (not appends). User must
   send between selections.

5. Scenario: User customizes a template and wants to save it
   Expected: Not supported in this phase. Custom templates are a future feature.
   No save UI shown.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001 (Context-Aware), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: Faster query patterns for new users
ENHANCES: #121 (Auto-Complete), #042 (Suggested Follow-Ups)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE (all client-side)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Template dropdown opens from grid icon and / key
[] Templates organized by category (Exploration, Analysis, Impact, Writing, Navigation)
[] Variables resolved from loaded graph data
[] Click inserts resolved text into input field
[] Unfilled variables highlighted in gold
[] Slash command filtering works
[] Arrow key navigation in dropdown
[] Escape closes dropdown

VISUAL:
[] Dropdown styled with category headers in gold
[] Variable highlights use var(--accent-gold) background
[] Staggered fadeIn animation on items

PERFORMANCE: [] Dropdown renders < 100ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Listbox role, arrow navigation, item announcements
EDGE CASES: [] No graph handled, long titles truncated, no-match state shown

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

================================================================================
FEATURE #124: EQUATION INPUT (LATEX)
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 18
OVERLAPS WITH:   #122 (Handwriting/Sketch Input), #140 (Equation Builder)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #038 (Markdown Rendering)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Equation Input adds LaTeX math expression support to the Athena input field.
Users can type LaTeX notation inline using $...$ for inline math and $$...$$
for display math. A live preview renders below the input as the user types,
showing the formatted equation in real time using KaTeX for client-side
rendering. This serves researchers who discuss mathematical relationships in
papers, such as the attention mechanism formula Q*K^T/sqrt(d_k) from the
Transformer paper. The input is sent as raw LaTeX text and rendered in both
the user's message bubble and Athena's response when it references equations.

KaTeX is loaded as a static dependency (no CDN) bundled in static/vendor/.
It renders LaTeX to HTML/CSS with no external calls, supporting most standard
LaTeX math commands.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User types $ in the input field, activating LaTeX mode indicator.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types: "How does the attention formula $QK^T / \sqrt{d_k}$ relate to
   the citation pattern in the graph?"
2. As user types within $...$, a live preview below the input shows the
   rendered equation updating in real time.
3. User presses Enter to send. The message renders with the inline equation
   formatted as proper math notation.
4. Athena responds with its own LaTeX if relevant: "The attention formula
   $\text{softmax}(QK^T / \sqrt{d_k})V$ was introduced in Vaswani 2017..."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Both user and assistant messages render LaTeX equations inline.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-latex.js: LaTeX detection, KaTeX rendering, live preview.
- static/vendor/katex/: KaTeX library files (CSS + JS + fonts).
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Integration for LaTeX rendering in message display.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: LaTeX preview container, equation block styles.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. LaTeX rendering is client-side via KaTeX. Raw LaTeX text
  stored in chat_history.

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Typing LaTeX with live preview

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ EQUATION PREVIEW ────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │              QK^T                                  │   │
  │  │  softmax( -------- ) V                            │   │
  │  │            sqrt(dk)                                │   │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ How does $QK^T / \sqrt{d_k}$|   [Send ●]        │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Preview slides open 100ms when first $ typed, closes when LaTeX resolved.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User types $ --> LaTeX mode activated, preview container shown
  --> Each keystroke within $...$ triggers KaTeX.renderToString() on preview
  --> User sends message --> raw LaTeX stored in chat_history
  --> Message display pipeline detects $...$ patterns
  --> KaTeX renders inline equations in message bubbles
  --> Assistant responses also rendered through same KaTeX pipeline

ERROR PATH: Invalid LaTeX --> preview shows red error text, message sent as raw text
DATA STORED: Raw LaTeX text in chat_history (not rendered HTML)
DATA CACHED: Rendered KaTeX HTML cached per message in DOM

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. LaTeX rendering is client-side. LLM receives raw LaTeX text
in messages and may produce LaTeX in responses.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| $ key in input       | Keydown     | Activates LaTeX mode indicator              |
| Typing within $...$  | Input       | Live preview updates with rendered equation |
| Closing $            | Keydown     | Preview shows final rendered equation       |
| Invalid LaTeX        | Input       | Preview shows error in red                  |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Preview container 100ms slideDown. Equation render is instant.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Rendered equations have aria-label with LaTeX source text. Screen readers read the LaTeX string rather than attempting to parse rendered math.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Unmatched $ (user types one $ but never closes it)
   Expected: Treated as literal $ character on send. No LaTeX rendering.

2. Scenario: Nested dollar signs ($$...$$) for display math
   Expected: Rendered as display (block) math with centered alignment.

3. Scenario: KaTeX does not support a LaTeX command
   Expected: Error shown in preview. Unsupported portion rendered as raw text.

4. Scenario: Very long equation wrapping multiple lines
   Expected: Display math block scrolls horizontally if too wide for container.

5. Scenario: User pastes LaTeX from an external editor
   Expected: Live preview detects $...$ patterns in pasted text, renders them.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #002 (Rich Formatting), #038 (Markdown Rendering)
ENABLES: Mathematical discussion in research context
ENHANCES: #140 (Equation Builder), #122 (Handwriting Input)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: KaTeX library (static vendor bundle)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Inline math $...$ detected and rendered via KaTeX
[] Display math $$...$$ rendered as centered block
[] Live preview updates as user types within delimiters
[] Raw LaTeX stored in chat_history
[] Both user and assistant messages render LaTeX
[] Invalid LaTeX shows error in preview, sent as raw text

VISUAL: [] Equations render with proper math typography, preview styled
PERFORMANCE: [] KaTeX render < 10ms per equation
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Equations have aria-label with source LaTeX
EDGE CASES: [] Unmatched $ handled, long equations scroll, paste supported

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-latex.js, vendor/katex, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST various LaTeX expressions 4. CHECK rendering accuracy 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
