================================================================================
FEATURE #125: CANVAS CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Special Modes
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 24
OVERLAPS WITH:   #122 (Handwriting/Sketch), #033 (Live Annotation Pad)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware), #002 (Rich Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign), #037 (Streaming)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Canvas Chat replaces the traditional linear message list with a freeform 2D
canvas where messages, artifacts, and graph snippets can be placed anywhere
on an infinite scrollable surface. Users drag message bubbles to arrange them
spatially, draw connection lines between messages, group related messages into
clusters, and pin important responses. This transforms Athena from a
sequential chat into a spatial thinking tool. Ideal for researchers mapping
out complex conceptual relationships across multiple papers and discussion
threads. Messages are created via a floating input that can be placed anywhere
on the canvas. Athena responses appear near the input location. The canvas
supports zoom (scroll wheel), pan (middle-click drag or two-finger drag), and
a minimap for navigation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User activates Canvas Chat from the mode switcher menu or via /canvas command.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /canvas. The linear chat transitions to a 2D canvas view.
2. Existing messages are arranged in a vertical column on the left (default).
3. User drags a message bubble about pruning impact to the upper-right area.
4. User clicks on empty canvas space to create a new input point. Types a
   follow-up question there. Athena's response appears nearby.
5. User draws a line from the pruning message to the new response, creating
   a visual connection.
6. User zooms out to see the full canvas layout of 8 message clusters.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Canvas layout preserved in session. Switching back to linear mode possible.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-canvas.js: CanvasChat class. Infinite canvas with message
  placement, drag, zoom, pan, connection lines, minimap.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Mode switch integration.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Canvas container, message node styles, connection
  line styles, minimap overlay.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  Canvas layout stored in sessions.graph_memory JSONB as canvas_layout: {
    nodes: [{id, x, y, message_id}], connections: [{from, to}]
  }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/canvas/save
Request: { "session_id": "...", "layout": { nodes: [...], connections: [...] } }
Response: { "saved": true }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Canvas with scattered messages

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─────────────┐                    ┌─────────────┐          │
  │  │ User msg 1  │                    │ User msg 3  │          │
  │  │ "What are   │                    │ "Compare    │          │
  │  │  the foun..." │------ line -----│  mutations" │          │
  │  └─────────────┘                    └──────┬──────┘          │
  │                                            │                  │
  │        ┌─────────────┐              ┌──────┴──────┐          │
  │        │ Athena resp │              │ Athena resp │          │
  │        │ "The founda-│              │ "Comparing  │          │
  │        │  tional..." │              │  Vaswani..."│          │
  │        └─────────────┘              └─────────────┘          │
  │                                                               │
  │  ┌──────┐  Minimap showing all message positions              │
  │  │ ○  ○ │                                                     │
  │  │○   ○ │                                                     │
  │  └──────┘                                                     │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Mode switch 300ms crossfade. Message drag follows pointer. Zoom smooth.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User activates /canvas --> CanvasChat initializes with existing messages
  --> Messages placed in default vertical layout
  --> User drags messages --> position updated in layout model
  --> User draws connection --> connection added to model
  --> Auto-save layout to session every 10s via POST /api/athena/canvas/save
  --> On session reload, layout restored from sessions.graph_memory

ERROR PATH: Layout save fails --> retry after 5s, warn user if persistent
DATA STORED: Canvas layout in sessions.graph_memory JSONB
DATA CACHED: Layout in memory, synced to server periodically

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. Canvas Chat is a layout/presentation feature. Messages are
created and processed through the normal Athena chat pipeline.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action         | Response                                 |
|----------------------|----------------|------------------------------------------|
| Message bubble       | Drag           | Move to new position on canvas           |
| Empty canvas space   | Double-click   | Create new input point at that location  |
| Two messages         | Shift+drag     | Draw connection line between them        |
| Scroll wheel         | Scroll         | Zoom in/out (0.25x to 4x range)         |
| Middle-click         | Drag           | Pan the canvas                           |
| Minimap              | Click          | Jump to that area of the canvas          |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Drag follows pointer at 60fps. Zoom 100ms ease. Pan smooth.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Canvas messages still readable in DOM order. Tab navigation follows creation order. Connection lines described via aria-describedby.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: 50+ messages on canvas causing performance issues
   Expected: Messages outside viewport are virtualized (DOM nodes removed,
   positions preserved). Re-inserted when scrolled into view.

2. Scenario: User switches from canvas back to linear mode
   Expected: Messages shown in chronological order. Canvas layout preserved
   for next canvas activation.

3. Scenario: Connection line drawn between unrelated messages
   Expected: Line is purely visual. No semantic meaning enforced.

4. Scenario: Canvas zoom at maximum (4x) with large messages
   Expected: Messages clip to their bounds. Text remains readable.

5. Scenario: Collaborative session with shared canvas
   Expected: Not supported in this phase. Canvas is per-session only.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #007, #037
ENABLES: Spatial thinking in research discussions
ENHANCES: #033 (Live Annotation), #122 (Handwriting/Sketch)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Canvas mode activates via /canvas command
[] Messages placeable anywhere on infinite 2D surface
[] Drag to reposition messages
[] Connection lines between messages
[] Zoom (0.25x to 4x) and pan navigation
[] Minimap for overview navigation
[] Layout auto-saved to session
[] Switch between canvas and linear mode

VISUAL: [] Messages as draggable cards, connection lines styled, minimap overlay
PERFORMANCE: [] 60fps drag, virtualization for 50+ messages
ACCESSIBILITY: [] DOM order maintained, tab navigation, connections described
EDGE CASES: [] Large canvases virtualized, mode switch preserves layout

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-canvas.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST drag, zoom, pan, connections 4. CHECK layout persistence 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24


================================================================================
FEATURE #126: SPLIT-BUBBLE MESSAGES
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #045 (Collapsible Long Responses), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text), #007 (Visual Redesign)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Split-Bubble Messages breaks a single Athena response into multiple visually
distinct bubble segments when the response contains logically separate parts.
Instead of one long continuous message bubble, each major section (narrative
explanation, data table, code block, citation list, follow-up suggestion)
renders in its own smaller bubble with a subtle vertical connector line between
them. This improves readability for complex multi-part responses by giving each
section visual breathing room and its own copy/bookmark affordance. The split
detection is content-aware: a response with a text paragraph followed by a
stats block followed by a citation card renders as three connected bubbles,
not one.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing two or more distinct content sections.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Give me a full analysis of the Vaswani 2017 paper's impact."
2. Athena streams a response with: narrative paragraph, stats dashboard, list
   of top 5 citing papers, and suggested follow-up questions.
3. Instead of one tall bubble, four connected bubbles appear:
   Bubble 1: Narrative text explanation.
   Bubble 2: Stats dashboard block.
   Bubble 3: Citation list with paper cards.
   Bubble 4: Follow-up suggestion chips.
4. A thin vertical line (2px, var(--bg-elevated)) connects the bubbles.
5. Each bubble has its own copy button and bookmark option.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Response visible as connected bubble chain. User can interact with each
section independently.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-split-bubble.js: SplitBubbleRenderer. Content-type detection,
  bubble segmentation, connector line rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Integration to route responses through splitter.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Split bubble styles, connector lines, per-bubble
  action buttons.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Splitting is client-side based on content structure detection.

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Split response with three bubbles

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                           │
  │  ┌─ Athena ──────────────────────────────────────┐       │
  │  │ The Attention Is All You Need paper has had    │  [Copy]│
  │  │ a transformative impact on NLP...              │       │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
  │       │ (connector line, 2px, var(--bg-elevated))         │
  │  ┌────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┐       │
  │  │ ┌─ IMPACT STATS ─────────────────────────┐    │  [Copy]│
  │  │ │ Nodes: 152  Edges: 203  Impact: 31%    │    │       │
  │  │ └────────────────────────────────────────┘    │       │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
  │       │                                                   │
  │  ┌────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┐       │
  │  │ Top citing papers:                             │  [Copy]│
  │  │ 1. BERT (Devlin 2018) - adoption              │       │
  │  │ 2. GPT-2 (Radford 2019) - generalization      │       │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
  │                                                           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Bubbles enter with 50ms stagger (reuses #071 entrance animation). Connector lines draw top-to-bottom 100ms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena response streamed --> content parsed for section boundaries
  --> Sections: paragraph, structured block, list, suggestion chips
  --> Each section wrapped in separate bubble container
  --> Connector lines drawn between adjacent bubbles
  --> Per-bubble actions (copy, bookmark) rendered independently

ERROR PATH: Single-section response --> renders as normal single bubble (no split)
DATA STORED: Response stored as single entry in chat_history (split is display-only)
DATA CACHED: Split structure cached in DOM per message

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. Splitting is a display-layer feature. LLM output is not modified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Individual bubble    | Hover       | Shows copy and bookmark buttons for that section |
| Copy button          | Click       | Copies that bubble's content only           |
| Bookmark button      | Click       | Bookmarks that specific section             |
| Connector line       | Hover       | Subtle highlight (opacity 0.4 to 0.8)      |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Bubble stagger 50ms. Connector draw 100ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Each bubble is a separate article element with aria-label "Response part N of M". Connector lines are decorative (aria-hidden).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Response has only one section
   Expected: No splitting. Normal single bubble.

2. Scenario: Response has 10+ sections
   Expected: All split. Connector line continuous. Scroll to see all.

3. Scenario: Streaming response where sections arrive incrementally
   Expected: First bubble appears immediately. Additional bubbles added as
   new sections are detected during streaming.

4. Scenario: A section is very short (just one line)
   Expected: Still gets its own bubble. Minimum bubble height 40px.

5. Scenario: User copies entire response (not per-bubble)
   Expected: Ctrl+A selects all bubbles. Copy concatenates all sections.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #002, #037, #007
ENABLES: Per-section interactions (copy, bookmark)
ENHANCES: #045 (Collapsible Responses), #071 (Entrance Animation)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Multi-section responses split into separate connected bubbles
[] Connector lines between bubbles
[] Per-bubble copy and bookmark buttons
[] Single-section responses render as normal bubble
[] Streaming responses split incrementally

VISUAL: [] Bubbles with connector lines, stagger animation, per-bubble actions
PERFORMANCE: [] Splitting detection < 5ms per response
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Each bubble is article with label, connectors aria-hidden
EDGE CASES: [] Single section, 10+ sections, streaming split, short sections

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-split-bubble.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST with multi-section responses 4. CHECK connector rendering 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created

================================================================================
FEATURE #127: LAYERED CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Conversation Mgmt
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 22
OVERLAPS WITH:   #010 (Conversation Threads), #131 (Chat About the Chat)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware), #006 (Conversation Memory), #007 (Visual Redesign)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Layered Chat introduces a depth dimension to the Athena conversation by
allowing users to nest sub-conversations within any message. Clicking a message
opens a "layer" beneath it where a focused sub-conversation happens, then the
user can surface back to the main thread. Each layer has its own message
history and context scope, inheriting the parent message's context as a
starting point. This resembles threaded comments but within a single chat
interface, not a sidebar. Layers can nest up to 3 levels deep. The visual
metaphor is a stack of translucent cards: the main conversation is the bottom
card, each layer is a card stacked above it with a slight offset. The parent
layer is visible but dimmed behind the active layer. A breadcrumb trail at
the top shows the layer path: Main > Message #5 > Sub-topic.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks the "dive deeper" button on any Athena response message.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena explains the mutation between two papers in the main conversation.
2. User clicks "dive deeper" on that response.
3. The main conversation dims (opacity 0.3). A new layer slides up with the
   parent message quoted at the top.
4. User types a follow-up question in this layer's input.
5. Athena responds within the layer context.
6. User clicks the breadcrumb "Main" to return to the top-level conversation.
7. The layer collapses. Main conversation restores to full opacity.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Layer stored in session. Indicator on parent message shows "1 sub-conversation."

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-layers.js: LayeredChatManager. Layer stack, context scoping,
  breadcrumb rendering, layer transitions.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Integration for layer message routing.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Layer stack visual, dimming, breadcrumb bar.
- app.py: Layer context included in /api/athena/chat when layer_id is present.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  Layer messages stored in chat_history with layer_id column. Layer context
  sent to LLM includes parent message + layer messages.

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Request: { "message": "...", "session_id": "...", "layer_id": "layer_abc123" }
Response: Standard streaming response scoped to layer context.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Active sub-layer

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [Main] > [Message #5] > [Current layer]   breadcrumb    │
  │                                                           │
  │  ┌─ DIMMED MAIN LAYER (opacity 0.3) ──────────────┐     │
  │  │  ... previous messages ...                       │     │
  │  │  [Message #5: "The mutation between..."]         │     │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
  │                                                           │
  │  ┌─ ACTIVE SUB-LAYER ─────────────────────────────┐      │
  │  │  Quoted: "The mutation between Vaswani and..."  │      │
  │  │                                                  │      │
  │  │  User: "Can you elaborate on the evidence?"     │      │
  │  │  Athena: "The generalization mutation is..."    │      │
  │  │                                                  │      │
  │  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐     │      │
  │  │  │ Type here...                [Send ●]  │     │      │
  │  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘     │      │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Layer slides up 250ms ease-out. Parent dims 200ms. Breadcrumb fadeIn.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User clicks "dive deeper" on message
  --> LayeredChatManager creates new layer with unique layer_id
  --> Parent message quoted at top of new layer
  --> Messages in layer sent with layer_id in API request
  --> Backend loads layer-specific chat_history for LLM context
  --> Responses scoped to layer context
  --> Breadcrumb updated with layer path
  --> On return to parent, layer collapses, main restores

ERROR PATH: Layer nesting exceeds 3 levels --> "Maximum depth reached" toast
DATA STORED: Layer messages in chat_history with layer_id, layer metadata in sessions.graph_memory
DATA CACHED: Layer state in memory per session

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
CONTEXT: Parent message + all layer messages (up to token budget)
PROMPT ADDITION: "This is a sub-conversation about: {parent_message_summary}. Focus on this specific topic."
TOKEN BUDGET: Parent message (~200 tokens) + layer history + user message

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| "Dive deeper" button | Click       | Opens new sub-layer on that message         |
| Breadcrumb item      | Click       | Returns to that layer level                 |
| Layer backdrop       | Click       | Returns to parent layer                     |
| Escape key           | Keydown     | Returns to parent layer                     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Layer slide 250ms, dim 200ms, breadcrumb fadeIn 150ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Breadcrumb is nav element with aria-label. Layer switch announced via aria-live. Focus moves to new layer input.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User creates 3 nested layers (maximum depth)
   Expected: "Dive deeper" button hidden on layer 3 messages. Toast: "Maximum layer depth reached."

2. Scenario: Session has 10 layers across different messages
   Expected: Each layer independent. Breadcrumb shows path for active layer only.

3. Scenario: Streaming response in a layer when user clicks breadcrumb to leave
   Expected: Streaming continues in background. Response preserved in layer history.

4. Scenario: Layer has no messages (user opened and immediately left)
   Expected: Empty layer cleaned up. No "1 sub-conversation" indicator.

5. Scenario: Parent message deleted or edited after layer created
   Expected: Layer quote shows original message text. Layer remains functional.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001, #006, #007
ENABLES: Deep-dive sub-conversations without cluttering main thread
ENHANCES: #010 (Threads), #131 (Chat About the Chat)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] "Dive deeper" button on assistant messages opens sub-layer
[] Sub-layer has own message list and input
[] Breadcrumb navigation between layers
[] Layer context sent to LLM with parent message
[] Maximum 3 levels of nesting enforced
[] Parent message indicator shows sub-conversation count

VISUAL: [] Layer stack with dimmed parent, breadcrumb bar, slide transitions
PERFORMANCE: [] Layer switch < 200ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Breadcrumb nav labeled, layer switch announced, focus managed
EDGE CASES: [] Max depth enforced, empty layers cleaned, streaming in layers

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-layers.js, athena-engine.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST layer creation, navigation, nesting 4. CHECK context scoping 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24


================================================================================
FEATURE #128: PAPER STACK
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #130 (Bookshelf), #011 (Artifact Cards)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Paper Stack renders collections of papers mentioned in Athena responses as a
visual stack of tilted paper cards, resembling a physical pile of research
papers on a desk. Each card shows the paper title, year, and a color-coded
edge stripe indicating the field of study. The stack is interactive: users
can fan out the stack by hovering, click individual cards to see details, or
drag cards to reorder them. When Athena mentions 3 or more papers in a grouped
context (such as "the foundational papers are..." or "these papers
contradict..."), the Paper Stack block replaces the default bulleted list.
This visual metaphor makes paper collections feel tangible and browsable.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena response mentions 3+ papers in a grouped context.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What are the foundational papers in this lineage?"
2. Athena responds with a paragraph and then lists 5 foundational papers.
3. Instead of a bullet list, the papers render as a stack of 5 tilted cards.
4. User hovers the stack. Cards fan out into a spread layout.
5. User clicks "BERT (Devlin 2018)". Card flips to show abstract preview,
   citation count, and mutation types.
6. User clicks "Discuss" on the card. Athena starts a focused discussion
   about that paper.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Paper stack visible in chat. Cards interactive for browsing and discussion.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: PaperStackBlock renderer. Stack layout, fan-out
  animation, card flip, interaction handlers.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Stack styles, card tilt transforms, field-color
  edge stripes, fan-out states.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Paper data already in graph response.

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Stacked (default)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │     ┌────────────────────────┐            │
  │    ┌┤ GPT-2 (Radford 2019)  │ ← CS blue  │
  │   ┌┤│                       │             │
  │  ┌┤││ BERT (Devlin 2018)    │             │
  │  │││└────────────────────────┘             │
  │  ││└─────────────────────────┘             │
  │  │└──────────────────────────┘             │
  │  └───────────────────────────┘             │
  │         5 papers stacked                   │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Fanned out (hover)

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌──────────────────┐                     │
  │  │ Vaswani 2017     │  ← slightly tilted  │
  │  └──────────────────┘                     │
  │    ┌──────────────────┐                   │
  │    │ Devlin 2018      │                   │
  │    └──────────────────┘                   │
  │      ┌──────────────────┐                 │
  │      │ Radford 2019     │                 │
  │      └──────────────────┘                 │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Fan-out 250ms stagger 40ms per card. Card flip 300ms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena response parsed --> 3+ papers detected in grouped mention
  --> PaperStackBlock instantiated with paper data array
  --> Cards rendered in stacked layout with random tilt (-3 to +3 degrees)
  --> Hover triggers fan-out transition
  --> Click opens card detail view with flip animation
  --> "Discuss" button sends new message about that paper

ERROR PATH: Paper data missing fields --> card shows title only with "?" for missing fields
DATA STORED: NONE (display-only, paper data from graph)
DATA CACHED: Card detail data cached from graph nodes

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE. Paper Stack is a rendering feature. Paper data sourced from graph.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Paper stack          | Hover       | Cards fan out into spread layout            |
| Paper stack          | Mouse leave | Cards re-stack with tilt                    |
| Individual card      | Click       | Card flips to show detail back              |
| "Discuss" button     | Click       | Sends message about that paper to Athena    |
| Card back            | Click       | Flips back to front view                    |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Fan-out 250ms with 40ms stagger. Card flip 300ms rotateY.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Stack announced as "Paper collection, N papers." Tab selects individual cards. Enter flips card.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Only 2 papers mentioned (below threshold)
   Expected: No stack. Normal inline paper references.

2. Scenario: 20+ papers in a stack
   Expected: Show top 10 cards. "...and 10 more" at bottom. Click expands full list.

3. Scenario: Paper has no year or no field of study
   Expected: Card shows "Year unknown" and neutral gray edge stripe.

4. Scenario: Same paper appears in multiple stacks
   Expected: Each stack instance is independent. Clicking "Discuss" always uses same paper_id.

5. Scenario: Stack renders inside a split bubble (#126)
   Expected: Stack fits within bubble width. Fan-out constrained to bubble bounds.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #002, #007
ENABLES: Visual paper browsing within chat
ENHANCES: #130 (Bookshelf), #011 (Artifact Cards)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Graph node data (already loaded)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] 3+ papers in grouped context render as Paper Stack
[] Hover fans out cards with stagger animation
[] Click flips card to show detail
[] "Discuss" button sends paper discussion message
[] 20+ papers show top 10 with expand option

VISUAL: [] Tilted cards, field-color edge stripes, fan-out and flip animations
PERFORMANCE: [] Fan-out animation 60fps, card flip 60fps
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Stack announced, tab navigation, enter to flip
EDGE CASES: [] Below threshold normal, large stacks truncated, missing data handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

================================================================================
FEATURE #129: STICKY NOTES
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Learning & Research
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #021 (Collaborative Notes), #125 (Canvas Chat)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware), #002 (Rich Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sticky Notes lets users create small colored note cards that float alongside
the chat conversation, pinned to specific messages or free-floating. Each
sticky note has a title, body text, and a color (yellow, pink, green, blue).
Users create notes to capture their own thoughts, hypotheses, and observations
while exploring the ancestry graph through Athena. Notes are persisted in the
session and can be referenced later. Athena is aware of the user's notes and
can incorporate them into responses when relevant (e.g., "You noted earlier
that the generalization pattern seemed unusual..."). Notes can be dragged to
reorder, pinned to specific messages, or grouped. A "Notes" sidebar panel
collects all notes in one view.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks the "Add note" button (sticky icon) in the Athena toolbar, or
right-clicks a message and selects "Add note."

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User right-clicks an Athena response about mutation patterns.
2. Selects "Add note" from context menu.
3. A small yellow sticky note card appears pinned to that message.
4. User types: "Interesting: generalization seems to dominate post-2015."
5. User presses Escape to close the edit mode. Note is saved.
6. Later, user asks "What patterns did I notice earlier?"
7. Athena responds: "You noted that generalization dominates post-2015.
   Looking at the data, this is consistent with..."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Sticky note visible pinned to message. Accessible from Notes sidebar.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-sticky.js: StickyNoteManager. Note creation, editing,
  pinning, drag, color selection, sidebar panel.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Note context injection into LLM prompts.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Sticky note card styles, color variants,
  pin indicator, notes sidebar.
- app.py: Notes stored/retrieved via session graph_memory.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  Notes stored in sessions.graph_memory JSONB as sticky_notes: [
    { id, message_id (nullable), title, body, color, created_at, position }
  ].
  Notes included in LLM context when relevant to user query.

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/notes/save
Request: { "session_id": "...", "notes": [...] }
Response: { "saved": true }

GET /api/athena/notes
Request: ?session_id=...
Response: { "notes": [...] }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Note pinned to message

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                           │
  │  ┌─ Athena ──────────────────────────────────────┐       │
  │  │ The mutation analysis shows generalization is  │       │
  │  │ the dominant pattern in post-2015 papers...    │       │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘       │
  │     ┌──────────────────────────┐                          │
  │     │ ● My Note               │ ← yellow sticky          │
  │     │ Generalization dominates │                          │
  │     │ post-2015. Check if this │                          │
  │     │ holds for biology too.   │                          │
  │     └──────────────────────────┘                          │
  │                                                           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Colors: Yellow #FEF3C7, Pink #FCE7F3, Green #D1FAE5, Blue #DBEAFE
  Note border: 1px solid darkened variant of background color

TRANSITIONS: Note appears 200ms scale(0.8 to 1.0) + opacity. Edit mode 150ms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User creates note --> StickyNoteManager adds to local model
  --> Note pinned to message_id (or free-floating if no context)
  --> Auto-save to session via POST /api/athena/notes/save
  --> On next LLM prompt, relevant notes injected into context:
      "User's notes: {note_bodies_summary}"
  --> Athena can reference notes in responses

ERROR PATH: Note save fails --> retry. Note remains in local memory regardless.
DATA STORED: Notes in sessions.graph_memory.sticky_notes JSONB array
DATA CACHED: Notes in memory for immediate access

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
CONTEXT INJECTION: When user's notes exist, add to system prompt:
  "The user has made the following personal notes during this session:
   Note 1: {body}. Note 2: {body}. Reference them when relevant."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~50 tokens per note, max 5 most recent notes = ~250 tokens

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| "Add note" button    | Click       | Creates new sticky note (free-floating)     |
| Message context menu | "Add note"  | Creates note pinned to that message         |
| Note body            | Click       | Enters edit mode (editable textarea)        |
| Note color swatch    | Click       | Changes note color                          |
| Note drag handle     | Drag        | Repositions note within chat area           |
| Note delete (x)      | Click       | Removes note (with confirmation)            |
| Notes sidebar button | Click       | Opens sidebar with all notes listed         |
| Escape key           | Keydown     | Exits note edit mode                        |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Note creation 200ms scale+opacity. Color change 100ms. Delete 150ms fadeOut.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Notes are aria-label'd with title and body. Edit mode announced. Sidebar navigable.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: 20+ notes created in one session
   Expected: Notes sidebar has scroll. Only 5 most recent in LLM context.

2. Scenario: Note pinned to a message that gets scrolled far up
   Expected: Note moves with its message. Visible in Notes sidebar regardless.

3. Scenario: User deletes all notes then asks "what did I note?"
   Expected: Athena: "You haven't made any notes in this session yet."

4. Scenario: Note text very long (500+ characters)
   Expected: Note card truncates with "..." after 200 chars. Full text in edit mode.

5. Scenario: Session reload
   Expected: Notes restored from sessions.graph_memory.sticky_notes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001, #002, #007
ENABLES: Personal annotation during research exploration
ENHANCES: #021 (Collaborative Notes), #125 (Canvas Chat)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Notes created from toolbar and message context menu
[] Notes pinned to messages or free-floating
[] Notes editable with title and body
[] 4 color options (yellow, pink, green, blue)
[] Notes sidebar panel shows all notes
[] Notes persisted in session
[] Athena references notes in context when relevant

VISUAL: [] Colored sticky cards, pin indicators, sidebar panel
PERFORMANCE: [] Note creation < 100ms, sidebar render < 50ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Notes labeled, edit mode announced, sidebar navigable
EDGE CASES: [] Many notes handled, long text truncated, session persistence

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-sticky.js, athena-engine.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST note lifecycle 4. CHECK LLM context injection 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24


================================================================================
FEATURE #130: BOOKSHELF
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #128 (Paper Stack), #011 (Artifact Cards)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Bookshelf renders a visual bookshelf metaphor in the Athena sidebar where
papers from the loaded ancestry graph are displayed as book spines on a shelf.
Each spine shows the paper's short title (or first author + year), color-coded
by field of study using the Arivu field color palette (--field-cs for Computer
Science, --field-bio for Biology, etc.). The shelf is interactive: clicking a
spine opens a paper detail card, dragging spines reorders them, and shelves
can be organized into user-defined collections (e.g., "Read," "To Read,"
"Key Papers"). The bookshelf provides a persistent visual reference of all
papers in the lineage, complementing the graph visualization with a more
traditional library metaphor.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User opens the Bookshelf panel from the sidebar or via /bookshelf command.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types /bookshelf. The sidebar switches to Bookshelf view.
2. 152 papers from the graph render as colored book spines on 4 shelves.
3. Spines are sorted by year by default. Color indicates field.
4. User clicks "Attention Is All You Need" spine. Detail card slides out.
5. User drags BERT's spine to a "Key Papers" collection shelf at the top.
6. User asks Athena: "Tell me about the papers on my Key Papers shelf."
7. Athena references the bookshelf collection in its response.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Bookshelf visible in sidebar. Collections persisted in session.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-bookshelf.js: BookshelfRenderer. Spine rendering, shelf
  layout, collections, drag reorder, detail card.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Bookshelf container, spine styles, shelf
  background, collection headers.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  Collections stored in sessions.graph_memory as bookshelf_collections JSONB.

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/bookshelf/save
Request: { "session_id": "...", "collections": { "key_papers": ["paper_id_1", ...] } }
Response: { "saved": true }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Bookshelf with papers

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  BOOKSHELF                    [Sort] │
  │                                      │
  │  KEY PAPERS (user collection)        │
  │  ┌──┬──┬──┬──┐                      │
  │  │B │G │  │  │                       │
  │  │E │P │  │  │                       │
  │  │R │T │  │  │ ← colored spines     │
  │  │T │2 │  │  │                       │
  │  ├──┴──┴──┴──┤ shelf edge            │
  │                                      │
  │  ALL PAPERS (by year)                │
  │  ┌──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┐   │
  │  │V │M │K │R │D │R │G │  │  │  │   │
  │  │a │i │r │e │e │a │o │  │  │  │   │
  │  │s │k │i │s │v │d │o │  │  │  │   │
  │  │w │o │z │N │l │f │d │  │  │  │   │
  │  ├──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┤   │
  │  [< Shelf 1 of 4 >]                 │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┘

  Spine colors: Field color palette (--field-cs, --field-bio, etc.)
  Shelf edge: var(--bg-elevated) with subtle wood-grain texture

TRANSITIONS: Spine click 200ms detail slide. Drag reorder 100ms snap.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Graph loaded --> All papers extracted from graph nodes
  --> Papers sorted by year and grouped into shelves (max 15 per shelf)
  --> Spine rendered with field color, short title, year
  --> User creates collections by dragging spines to collection shelves
  --> Collections saved to session via POST /api/athena/bookshelf/save
  --> Athena references collections when user mentions "my [collection] papers"

ERROR PATH: Graph not loaded --> empty bookshelf with "Load a graph to see papers"
DATA STORED: Collections in sessions.graph_memory.bookshelf_collections
DATA CACHED: Spine data in memory from graph nodes

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
CONTEXT: When user references a bookshelf collection, inject collection paper
  titles into LLM context: "User's 'Key Papers' collection contains: [titles]."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~200 tokens for collection reference

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Book spine           | Click       | Detail card slides out with paper info      |
| Book spine           | Drag        | Reorder within shelf or move to collection  |
| Sort dropdown        | Select      | Re-sort: by year, by citations, by field    |
| Collection header    | Click       | Expand/collapse collection shelf            |
| "New collection"     | Click       | Creates named collection shelf              |
| Shelf pagination     | Click       | Navigate between shelf pages                |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Detail slide 200ms. Drag snap 100ms. Sort re-render 250ms stagger.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Bookshelf is role="list". Each spine is role="listitem" with title. Detail card announced on open.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: 600 papers in graph (maximum)
   Expected: 40 shelves of 15 papers each. Pagination between shelves.

2. Scenario: Paper title too long for spine width
   Expected: Truncated to first author + year on spine. Full title in detail.

3. Scenario: User creates 10+ collections
   Expected: Collection shelves scroll vertically. Maximum 20 collections.

4. Scenario: Paper has unknown field of study
   Expected: Uses --field-other color (neutral blue).

5. Scenario: Graph switches while bookshelf open
   Expected: Bookshelf resets with new graph papers. Collections cleared.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #002, #007
ENABLES: Visual paper library browsing
ENHANCES: #128 (Paper Stack), #011 (Artifact Cards)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Graph node data (already loaded)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Papers rendered as colored book spines on shelves
[] Click opens detail card
[] Drag reorders and moves to collections
[] User-created named collections
[] Sort by year, citations, field
[] Shelf pagination for large graphs

VISUAL: [] Field-color spines, shelf edges, collection headers
PERFORMANCE: [] 600 spines renders < 500ms, drag 60fps
ACCESSIBILITY: [] List roles, spine labels, detail announced
EDGE CASES: [] Large graphs paginated, long titles truncated, unknown field handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-bookshelf.js, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST with various graph sizes 4. CHECK collections persistence 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

================================================================================
FEATURE #131: CHAT ABOUT THE CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Conversation Mgmt
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #127 (Layered Chat), #135 (Evolving Summary Bar)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware), #006 (Conversation Memory), #037 (Streaming)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Chat About the Chat enables meta-conversations where Athena can analyze,
summarize, and reflect on the conversation itself. The user can ask questions
like "What have we discussed so far?" or "Which topics did I focus on most?"
and Athena responds with a structured analysis of the conversation history.
This includes topic frequency analysis, a conversation timeline, identification
of unanswered questions, recurring themes, and suggested directions the user
has not yet explored. The feature treats the conversation log as a data source,
applying the same analytical rigor that Arivu applies to citation graphs.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User types /reflect, asks "what have we talked about?", or uses the
conversation analysis button in the toolbar.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has had 25 messages with Athena about the attention mechanism lineage.
2. User types: "What have we discussed so far?"
3. Athena analyzes the chat_history and responds with:
   - Topic breakdown: "60% mutation analysis, 25% impact assessment, 15% paper details"
   - Timeline: "You started with broad exploration, then focused on the BERT-Vaswani relationship"
   - Unanswered: "You asked about contradiction patterns but we did not finish that thread"
   - Suggestions: "We have not explored the Word2Vec connection or the ResNet branch"
4. Response rendered with a conversation map block showing topic clusters.

AFTER COMPLETION:
User has a clear picture of conversation progress and unexplored areas.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-reflect.js: ConversationAnalyzer. Topic extraction, timeline
  construction, gap detection.
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: ConversationMapBlock renderer.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: /reflect command routing.
- app.py: POST /api/athena/reflect endpoint for conversation analysis.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/reflect', methods=['POST'])
  def athena_reflect():
      history = load_chat_history(session_id)
      # Extract topics from each message
      # Compute topic frequency, timeline, unanswered questions
      # Send summary to LLM for narrative
      return streaming_response(analysis)

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/reflect
Request: { "session_id": "..." }
Response: Streaming analysis with topic breakdown, timeline, suggestions

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Conversation reflection rendered

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ CONVERSATION ANALYSIS ──────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │                                                    │    │
  │  │  TOPIC BREAKDOWN                                   │    │
  │  │  ████████████████████░░░░░  Mutation analysis 60%  │    │
  │  │  ██████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  Impact assessment 25%  │    │
  │  │  ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  Paper details     15%  │    │
  │  │                                                    │    │
  │  │  TIMELINE                                          │    │
  │  │  ○ Broad exploration (msgs 1-8)                   │    │
  │  │  ○ BERT-Vaswani focus (msgs 9-18)                 │    │
  │  │  ○ Impact analysis (msgs 19-25)                   │    │
  │  │                                                    │    │
  │  │  UNANSWERED                                        │    │
  │  │  ? Contradiction patterns (msg #12)                │    │
  │  │                                                    │    │
  │  │  UNEXPLORED                                        │    │
  │  │  ○ Word2Vec connection                             │    │
  │  │  ○ ResNet branch                                   │    │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS: Sections reveal with 100ms stagger. Bars animate width 300ms.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User types /reflect --> POST /api/athena/reflect
  --> Backend loads full chat_history for session
  --> Extracts topics from messages (keyword extraction + LLM classification)
  --> Computes frequency distribution, chronological phases, unanswered questions
  --> Cross-references with graph nodes to find unexplored areas
  --> LLM generates narrative summary of conversation patterns
  --> Response streamed with structured blocks

ERROR PATH: Fewer than 5 messages --> "Not enough conversation to analyze yet. Keep exploring!"
DATA STORED: action_log: { action: 'conversation_reflect' }
DATA CACHED: Reflection results cached per session until next message sent

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
PROMPT: "Analyze this conversation history: {messages_summary}. Identify: 1) Main topics discussed and their frequency. 2) Chronological phases of the conversation. 3) Questions asked but not fully answered. 4) Areas of the graph not yet explored. Format as structured sections."
FALLBACK: Rule-based analysis: keyword frequency from messages, unanswered question detection by ? marks without follow-up.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~1000 context (conversation summary) + ~400 output

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Topic bar            | Click       | Scrolls to messages about that topic        |
| Timeline phase       | Click       | Scrolls to that message range               |
| Unanswered question  | Click       | Resends that question as new message        |
| Unexplored area      | Click       | Asks Athena about that graph area           |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Bar width animation 300ms. Section stagger 100ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Bars have aria-valuenow/max. Timeline items are links. Unanswered items are buttons.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Very short conversation (under 5 messages)
   Expected: "Not enough conversation history for meaningful analysis."

2. Scenario: All messages on the same topic
   Expected: Topic breakdown shows 100% one topic. Suggestions emphasize unexplored areas.

3. Scenario: User has not loaded a graph (text-only conversation)
   Expected: "Unexplored" section omitted. Topic analysis based on text only.

4. Scenario: 100+ messages in history
   Expected: LLM receives summarized history (not full text). Topic extraction
   uses recent 50 messages for detail, older messages for broad themes.

5. Scenario: User asks /reflect twice in a row
   Expected: Second analysis includes the first reflection as a discussed topic.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001, #006, #037
ENABLES: Meta-awareness of research exploration patterns
ENHANCES: #127 (Layered Chat), #135 (Evolving Summary Bar)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: chat_history data, graph node data

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] /reflect command generates conversation analysis
[] Topic breakdown with frequency bars
[] Chronological timeline of conversation phases
[] Unanswered questions identified
[] Unexplored graph areas suggested
[] Interactive elements link back to messages

VISUAL: [] Structured blocks with bars, timeline, lists
PERFORMANCE: [] Analysis generation < 3s for 50-message conversation
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Bars labeled, timeline navigable, actions as buttons
EDGE CASES: [] Short conversations handled, large histories summarized

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-reflect.js, athena-blocks.js, app.py endpoint 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST with various conversation lengths 4. CHECK topic accuracy 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified

================================================================================
FEATURE #132: CONFIDENCE THERMOMETER
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Message Actions
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #023 (AI Disagreement Button)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Confidence Thermometer displays a visual confidence indicator on every Athena
response that communicates how certain the system is about its answer. The
indicator is a small vertical thermometer icon beside each response bubble,
filled to a level corresponding to the confidence score. The score is derived
from multiple signals: the quality of the underlying data (text_tier of papers
involved), the confidence of edge classifications (mutation_confidence from
edge_analysis), the proportion of LLM-classified vs rule-based conclusions,
and whether the response relies on graph data or is pure LLM generation.

A response built entirely from HIGH-confidence edges with full-text papers
shows a nearly full thermometer. A speculative response based on title-only
papers with SPECULATIVE-tier edges shows a low thermometer. Hovering the
thermometer reveals a breakdown tooltip: "Data quality: 78% (12 of 15 papers
have abstracts). Edge confidence: 65% (average mutation_confidence across
referenced edges). Source: 80% graph data, 20% LLM inference."

This feature is fundamental to Arivu's trust philosophy. Every claim should
be transparent about its evidentiary basis. Users make better research
decisions when they know how confident the system is.

The confidence score is computed server-side as part of every Athena response.
The backend examines which papers and edges the response references, computes
aggregate data quality and edge confidence, and attaches a confidence object
to the response metadata. The frontend renders the thermometer from this
metadata.

The confidence score formula:
  data_quality = count(text_tier <= 2) / total_papers_referenced
  edge_confidence = mean(mutation_confidence) for referenced edges
  source_ratio = graph_data_proportion (0.0 to 1.0)
  overall = (data_quality * 0.4) + (edge_confidence * 0.4) + (source_ratio * 0.2)

The thermometer fill level maps directly to overall (0.0 = empty, 1.0 = full).
Color follows confidence tier conventions: green (>= 0.75), blue (>= 0.55),
amber (>= 0.35), purple (< 0.35).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Every Athena response automatically includes a confidence thermometer.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What mutation type connects Vaswani 2017 to Devlin 2018?"
2. Athena responds with the mutation analysis. Beside the response bubble,
   a small thermometer icon shows ~75% filled in green.
3. User hovers the thermometer. Tooltip appears:
   "Data quality: 85% (both papers have full text).
    Edge confidence: HIGH (0.82).
    Source: 90% graph data, 10% LLM inference.
    Overall confidence: 0.78"
4. User asks a more speculative question: "What would happen if none of the
   attention papers existed?"
5. Athena responds with a counterfactual analysis. Thermometer shows ~40%
   filled in amber.
6. User hovers: "Data quality: 60%. Edge confidence: MEDIUM (0.51). Source:
   40% graph data, 60% LLM inference. Overall: 0.42"

AFTER COMPLETION:
Thermometer always visible on responses. Users calibrate trust accordingly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-confidence.js: ConfidenceThermometer class. Rendering,
  tooltip, color mapping.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Attaches thermometer to each response bubble.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Thermometer styles, fill animation, tooltip.
- app.py: Confidence computation in /api/athena/chat response metadata.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  In the Athena chat endpoint, after generating a response, compute confidence:
  def compute_response_confidence(response_text, graph_data, referenced_papers, referenced_edges):
      papers = [p for p in graph_data['nodes'] if p['paper_id'] in referenced_papers]
      data_quality = sum(1 for p in papers if p.get('text_tier', 4) <= 2) / max(len(papers), 1)
      edge_confs = [e['mutation_confidence'] for e in graph_data['edges'] if e['edge_id'] in referenced_edges]
      edge_confidence = sum(edge_confs) / max(len(edge_confs), 1) if edge_confs else 0.5
      source_ratio = len(referenced_papers) / max(len(referenced_papers) + estimated_llm_tokens, 1)
      overall = (data_quality * 0.4) + (edge_confidence * 0.4) + (source_ratio * 0.2)
      return {
          'overall': round(overall, 2),
          'data_quality': round(data_quality, 2),
          'edge_confidence': round(edge_confidence, 2),
          'source_ratio': round(source_ratio, 2),
          'tier': get_confidence_tier(overall)
      }

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```javascript
class ConfidenceThermometer {
  constructor(container, confidenceData) {
    this.container = container;
    this.data = confidenceData;
    this.render();
  }
  render() {
    const level = this.data.overall;
    const color = level >= 0.75 ? 'var(--conf-high)' :
                  level >= 0.55 ? 'var(--conf-medium)' :
                  level >= 0.35 ? 'var(--conf-low)' :
                                  'var(--conf-specul)';
    this.el = document.createElement('div');
    this.el.className = 'athena-confidence-thermo';
    this.el.innerHTML = `
      <div class="thermo-tube">
        <div class="thermo-fill" style="height:${level*100}%;background:${color}"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="thermo-bulb" style="background:${color}"></div>
    `;
    this.el.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => this.showTooltip());
    this.container.appendChild(this.el);
  }
  showTooltip() {
    // Render tooltip with breakdown data
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
Response metadata addition to /api/athena/chat SSE events:
{ "type": "meta", "confidence": { "overall": 0.78, "data_quality": 0.85, "edge_confidence": 0.82, "source_ratio": 0.90, "tier": "HIGH" } }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Thermometer beside response

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                           │
  │  ┌─ Athena ──────────────────────────────────────┐  ┌┐  │
  │  │ The mutation between Vaswani 2017 and Devlin   │  │█│  │
  │  │ 2018 is classified as "generalization" with    │  │█│  │
  │  │ HIGH confidence. BERT generalized the self-    │  │█│  │
  │  │ attention mechanism from translation to...     │  │░│  │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │░│  │
  │                                                      └●┘  │
  │         Thermometer: green, ~75% filled                    │
  │                                                           │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Tooltip on hover

  ┌─ CONFIDENCE ─────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Data quality:    85% (full text available)    │
  │ Edge confidence: HIGH (0.82)                  │
  │ Source:          90% graph, 10% LLM           │
  │ Overall:         0.78 (HIGH)                  │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Thermometer: 16px wide, 48px tall. Fill animates from bottom.
  Colors: --conf-high (green), --conf-medium (blue), --conf-low (amber), --conf-specul (purple)

TRANSITIONS: Fill animates 300ms ease-out on render. Tooltip 150ms fadeIn.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User sends message --> Backend processes with graph context
  --> Response generated --> Backend computes confidence from referenced data
  --> Confidence metadata included in SSE stream as "meta" event
  --> Frontend creates ConfidenceThermometer from metadata
  --> Thermometer rendered beside response bubble
  --> Hover shows tooltip with breakdown

ERROR PATH: No graph loaded --> confidence based on LLM-only signal (source_ratio=0). Low score.
DATA STORED: Confidence score in chat_history message metadata
DATA CACHED: Confidence data attached to DOM element

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOT APPLICABLE for confidence computation itself. The confidence is computed
from data metrics, not LLM self-assessment. The LLM's response is the input
that triggers confidence computation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Thermometer icon     | Hover       | Shows confidence breakdown tooltip          |
| Thermometer icon     | Click       | Expands inline confidence details below msg |
| Tooltip link         | Click       | Opens explanation of confidence scoring     |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Fill animation 300ms. Tooltip 150ms. Click expansion 200ms.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Thermometer has aria-label "Response confidence: {tier}, {overall}%". Tooltip content in aria-describedby. Click expansion provides same info as tooltip for keyboard users.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: Response references no specific papers or edges (general answer)
   Expected: Confidence computed from source_ratio alone. Typically low.
   Thermometer shows ~30% with purple.

2. Scenario: All referenced edges have SPECULATIVE confidence
   Expected: Edge confidence component very low. Overall likely in LOW tier.

3. Scenario: Response mixes high-confidence facts with speculative analysis
   Expected: Overall confidence is a blended average. Tooltip explains the mix.

4. Scenario: Confidence metadata missing from SSE stream (backend error)
   Expected: No thermometer shown. No error. Feature gracefully degrades.

5. Scenario: Very fast response (cached or simple)
   Expected: Confidence still computed and shown. Cached responses retain
   original confidence scores.

6. Scenario: User has AI Disagreement Button (#023) and low-confidence response
   Expected: Both features coexist. Disagreement button appears alongside
   thermometer. Clicking disagreement does not affect confidence display.

7. Scenario: Confidence exactly 0.0 (no data at all)
   Expected: Empty thermometer. Tooltip: "No data available to assess confidence."

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001 (Context-Aware), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: Trust calibration for all Athena responses
ENHANCES: #023 (AI Disagreement Button)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: edge_analysis.mutation_confidence, papers.text_tier

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Confidence thermometer appears on every Athena response
[] Confidence computed from data_quality, edge_confidence, source_ratio
[] Hover tooltip shows breakdown with percentages
[] Click expands inline details
[] Color follows confidence tier conventions (green/blue/amber/purple)
[] Fill level maps to overall confidence score

VISUAL:
[] 16x48px thermometer with fill animation
[] Tier-appropriate colors
[] Tooltip with structured breakdown

PERFORMANCE: [] Confidence computation < 50ms, thermometer render < 10ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] aria-label with tier and score, tooltip in aria-describedby
EDGE CASES: [] No data, missing metadata, all speculative, mixed confidence

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
