================================================================================
FEATURE #140: EQUATION BUILDER
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Learning & Research
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   #124 (Equation Input LaTeX), #139 (Live Diagram Builder)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #124 (Equation Input LaTeX), #002 (Rich Formatting), #037 (Streaming)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Equation Builder is an interactive equation construction tool within Athena
that lets users build mathematical expressions visually rather than typing raw
LaTeX. A GUI panel provides clickable symbol palettes (Greek letters,
operators, fractions, integrals, summations, matrices) that insert into a
WYSIWYG equation editor. The equation renders live via KaTeX as the user builds
it. The user can describe an equation in natural language ("the attention
formula with softmax of Q times K transpose divided by square root of d sub
k") and Athena generates the LaTeX, rendering it in the builder where the
user can refine it visually. The builder output can be inserted into chat
messages, exported as LaTeX, PNG, or MathML, and saved as reusable snippets.
This complements the raw LaTeX input (#124) by providing an accessible
alternative for users less familiar with LaTeX syntax.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks the equation builder icon (sigma symbol) in the input toolbar, or
types /equation.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks the sigma icon. Equation Builder panel opens above the input.
2. Panel shows: WYSIWYG editor area, symbol palette below (tabs: Greek,
   Operators, Structures, Common), and a LaTeX source toggle.
3. User clicks the fraction button. A fraction template appears in the editor.
4. User clicks the numerator and types "QK^T". User clicks denominator and
   clicks sqrt from the palette, then types "d_k".
5. Live preview shows the formatted equation.
6. User toggles LaTeX source view to see: \frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}
7. User clicks "Insert" to place the equation in their chat message.
8. Alternatively, user types: "Build me the attention formula" and Athena
   generates the LaTeX in the builder.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Equation inserted into input field as LaTeX. Renders in message when sent.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-equation-builder.js: EquationBuilder class. WYSIWYG editor,
  symbol palette, KaTeX preview, LaTeX source toggle, natural language input.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Builder toggle integration, LLM equation gen.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Builder panel, symbol palette tabs, editor area.
- static/vendor/katex/: KaTeX library (shared with #124).
- app.py: POST /api/athena/equation for natural language to LaTeX conversion.

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  @app.route('/api/athena/equation', methods=['POST'])
  def athena_equation():
      description = request.json['description']
      prompt = f"Convert this natural language description to LaTeX: {description}. Return only the LaTeX code, no explanation."
      latex = llm_client.generate(prompt, model=config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL)
      return jsonify({'latex': latex.strip()})

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/equation
Request: { "description": "the attention formula with softmax..." }
Response: { "latex": "\\frac{QK^T}{\\sqrt{d_k}}" }

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Builder panel open

  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─ EQUATION BUILDER ────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │   │
  │  │  │              QK^T                             │ │   │
  │  │  │  softmax( -------- ) V                       │ │   │
  │  │  │            sqrt(dk)                           │ │   │
  │  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  [Greek] [Operators] [Structures] [Common]         │   │
  │  │  ┌──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┬──┐                │   │
  │  │  │ a│ b│ g│ d│ e│ z│ h│ q│ i│ k│ ...            │   │
  │  │  └──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┴──┘                │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  LaTeX: \frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}    [Toggle]      │   │
  │  │                                                    │   │
  │  │  [Insert] [Export PNG] [Export LaTeX] [Clear]      │   │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
  │  │ Build me the attention formula     [Send ●]      │    │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Editor area: var(--bg-surface) background, white text
  Symbol palette: var(--bg-elevated) buttons, hover var(--accent-blue)
  Active tab: var(--accent-gold) underline

TRANSITIONS: Panel slides open 200ms. Symbol click 100ms highlight.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Symbol palette click --> LaTeX fragment inserted at cursor in editor
  --> KaTeX renders live preview immediately
  --> User can toggle to view/edit raw LaTeX source
  --> "Insert" places LaTeX ($...$) into chat input field
  --> Natural language input --> POST /api/athena/equation
  --> LLM generates LaTeX --> Populates builder editor
  --> User refines in builder --> Inserts when satisfied

ERROR PATH: Invalid LaTeX structure --> editor shows red underline, preview shows error
DATA STORED: Saved equation snippets in sessions.graph_memory.equation_snippets
DATA CACHED: Current equation in builder DOM

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
PROMPT: "Convert this natural language math description to LaTeX. Return only valid LaTeX code: {description}"
FALLBACK: If LLM returns invalid LaTeX, show error and let user build manually.
TOKEN BUDGET: ~100 context + ~50 output

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Symbol button        | Click       | Inserts LaTeX fragment at cursor            |
| Editor area          | Click       | Places cursor for typing                    |
| Fraction template    | Click       | Inserts \frac{}{} with cursor in numerator  |
| Tab header           | Click       | Switches symbol palette category            |
| "Toggle" button      | Click       | Switches between preview and LaTeX source   |
| "Insert" button      | Click       | Places equation in chat input               |
| "Export PNG" button   | Click       | Renders equation as PNG download           |
| "Export LaTeX" button | Click       | Copies LaTeX string to clipboard           |
| "Clear" button       | Click       | Clears editor (with confirmation)           |

ANIMATION DETAILS: Symbol click 100ms highlight. Panel 200ms slide. Toggle 150ms crossfade.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Symbol palette buttons have aria-label (e.g., "alpha"). Editor is contenteditable with role="textbox". LaTeX source is a textarea.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User types LaTeX directly in editor (not using palette)
   Expected: Works identically to raw LaTeX input. Preview updates live.

2. Scenario: Very complex equation (nested fractions, matrices)
   Expected: Editor scrolls horizontally. Preview may need zoom out. Export
   still produces correct LaTeX.

3. Scenario: Natural language description is ambiguous
   Expected: LLM generates best guess. User refines in builder.

4. Scenario: KaTeX does not support a generated LaTeX command
   Expected: Error shown in preview. User can edit LaTeX source to fix.

5. Scenario: User tries to insert equation with no content
   Expected: "Insert" button disabled when editor is empty.

6. Scenario: Multiple equations needed in one message
   Expected: User can insert, then reopen builder for next equation.
   Each insert appends to input field.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #124 (Equation Input LaTeX), #002, #037
ENABLES: Visual equation construction for non-LaTeX users
ENHANCES: #124 (Equation Input), #139 (Live Diagram Builder)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: KaTeX library (static vendor bundle)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Symbol palette inserts LaTeX fragments
[] WYSIWYG editor with live KaTeX preview
[] LaTeX source view toggle
[] Natural language to LaTeX via LLM
[] Insert places equation in chat input as $...$
[] Export as PNG and LaTeX
[] Saved snippets in session

VISUAL: [] Panel with editor, palette tabs, action buttons
PERFORMANCE: [] KaTeX render < 10ms, palette load < 100ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Symbol buttons labeled, editor as textbox, palette navigable
EDGE CASES: [] Direct LaTeX input, complex equations, ambiguous NL, empty insert

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-equation-builder.js, app.py endpoint, vendor/katex, CSS 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST palette insertion, NL generation, export 4. CHECK KaTeX rendering accuracy 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FEATURE #105: CLICK-AWARE CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Contextual Awareness
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #004 (Discuss Integration)
BLOCKS:          #106 (Zoom-Aware Chat), #108 (Prune-Aware Chat)
BLOCKED BY:      #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #004 (Discuss Integration), #006 (Conversation Memory)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click-Aware Chat is the contextual intelligence layer that detects when a user
clicks on nodes or edges in the Arivu graph and automatically adjusts Athena's
conversational context to reflect that selection. When a user clicks a paper
node, Athena knows which paper is now selected and can reference its title,
year, authors, mutation types, PageRank score, and position in the ancestry
tree without the user having to type any of that information.

This transforms the chat from a generic Q&A tool into a context-sensitive
research companion. Without click awareness, a user who clicks on a node and
then types "tell me about this paper" gets a confused response because Athena
has no idea what "this" refers to. With click awareness, Athena resolves "this"
to the clicked node's paper data and responds with a detailed analysis grounded
in the graph's actual ancestry structure.

The mechanism works by intercepting graph click events from the D3 force
simulation in graph.js. When a node or edge is clicked, the event handler
dispatches a custom DOM event (athena:graph:click) carrying the clicked
element's full data payload. The Athena chat engine listens for this event
and updates its internal conversational context object. This context object
is sent with every subsequent message to the LLM, prepended as a system
context block that the LLM can reference naturally.

Edge clicks carry different context than node clicks. When a user clicks an
edge, the context includes both the citing and cited paper, the mutation_type,
mutation_confidence, citation_intent, and the citing_sentence/cited_sentence
pair. This enables questions like "why is this classified as generalization?"
to be answered with the actual evidence from the edge_analysis table.

The click context has a staleness threshold. If the user has not interacted
with the graph for 5 minutes, the click context is cleared to avoid stale
references. A visual indicator in the chat input area shows which paper or
edge is currently in context (a small chip showing the paper title or edge
label) and can be dismissed by the user.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User clicks any node or edge in the Arivu ancestry graph while Athena chat
panel is open.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks on the "ResNet (2016)" node in the graph. The node highlights
   with the standard selection ring (gold border).
2. A context chip appears in the Athena chat input area: "ResNet (2016)" with
   an X dismiss button. The chip is styled with var(--accent-gold) border.
3. User types "What makes this paper important to the lineage?"
4. Athena receives the message plus the click context: paper_id, title, year,
   authors, citation_count, PageRank score, incoming/outgoing edge count,
   mutation types of connected edges.
5. Athena responds with a grounded answer referencing ResNet specifically:
   "ResNet (He et al., 2016) holds a PageRank of 0.043 in this lineage,
   making it the highest-impact node. It introduced residual connections
   that 23 subsequent papers adopted or generalized..."
6. The user then clicks an edge between ResNet and VGGNet. The context chip
   updates to show "ResNet -> VGGNet (specialization)".
7. User types "Is this classification correct?" Athena responds with the
   edge's mutation_evidence, confidence_tier, and citing_sentence.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Context chip remains until dismissed, a new element is clicked, or the 5-minute
staleness threshold is reached. Chat continues to use the most recent click
context for all subsequent messages until context changes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-context.js: Click event listener, context state manager,
  staleness timer, context chip UI, event dispatch to engine.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Receives context updates, prepends to LLM
  system prompt.
- static/js/graph.js: Emits athena:graph:click custom event on node/edge click.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Context chip styles.
- app.py: /api/athena/stream accepts optional graph_context parameter with
  clicked node/edge data.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaClickContext {
    constructor() {
      this.currentContext = null;
      this.stalenessTimer = null;
      this.STALENESS_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes
    }
    init() {
      document.addEventListener('athena:graph:click', (e) => {
        this.setContext(e.detail);
      });
    }
    setContext(detail) {
      this.currentContext = {
        type: detail.type, // 'node' or 'edge'
        data: detail.data,
        timestamp: Date.now()
      };
      this.renderChip();
      this.resetStalenessTimer();
    }
    getContextForLLM() {
      if (!this.currentContext) return '';
      if (Date.now() - this.currentContext.timestamp > this.STALENESS_MS) {
        this.clearContext();
        return '';
      }
      if (this.currentContext.type === 'node') {
        const p = this.currentContext.data;
        return `[CONTEXT] User is looking at: "${p.title}" (${p.year}) `
          + `by ${(p.authors || []).slice(0, 3).join(', ')}. `
          + `PageRank: ${p.pagerank}. Citations: ${p.citation_count}. `
          + `Connected edges: ${p.edge_count}. `
          + `Fields: ${(p.fields_of_study || []).join(', ')}.`;
      }
      if (this.currentContext.type === 'edge') {
        const e = this.currentContext.data;
        return `[CONTEXT] User is looking at edge: "${e.citing_title}" -> `
          + `"${e.cited_title}". Mutation: ${e.mutation_type} `
          + `(${e.confidence_tier}). Intent: ${e.citation_intent}. `
          + `Evidence: "${e.mutation_evidence}".`;
      }
      return '';
    }
    renderChip() { /* renders context chip in input area */ }
    clearContext() { this.currentContext = null; this.removeChip(); }
    resetStalenessTimer() {
      clearTimeout(this.stalenessTimer);
      this.stalenessTimer = setTimeout(() => this.clearContext(), this.STALENESS_MS);
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
The /api/athena/stream endpoint accepts an optional graph_context query
parameter (URL-encoded JSON). This context is prepended to the system prompt
sent to Groq. The backend does NOT store click context in the database; it is
ephemeral and session-scoped on the frontend.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```python
# In app.py stream handler
graph_context = request.args.get('graph_context', '')
if graph_context:
    try:
        ctx = json.loads(graph_context)
        system_prompt = f"{base_system_prompt}\n\n{ctx}"
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        system_prompt = base_system_prompt
```

API CONTRACTS:
GET /api/athena/stream
  Query params (additions):
    graph_context: string (URL-encoded JSON, optional)
  The graph_context is validated server-side: max 2000 chars, must be valid JSON.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Context Chip Active (Node Selected)

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
  │ │  [gold border chip]                      │ │
  │ │  ● ResNet (2016)                    [x]  │ │
  │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
  │ │  Ask about this paper...             [>] │ │
  │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Context Chip Active (Edge Selected)

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
  │ │  [blue border chip]                      │ │
  │ │  ○ ResNet -> VGGNet (specialization) [x] │ │
  │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
  │ │  Ask about this connection...        [>] │ │
  │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
- Chip appears: 200ms slide-down from top of input area
- Chip dismissed: 150ms fade-out
- Context switch (click different node): chip cross-fades (150ms)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

graph.js node/edge click
  -> dispatch CustomEvent('athena:graph:click', { detail })
  -> AthenaClickContext.setContext(detail)
  -> context chip rendered in input area
  -> User sends message
  -> AthenaEngine prepends getContextForLLM() to system prompt
  -> SSE stream request includes graph_context param
  -> Backend prepends context to Groq system prompt
  -> LLM generates context-aware response

ERROR PATH:
- Graph not loaded: no click events fire, context stays null, chat works normally
- Malformed click data: context silently ignored, no chip shown
- Context too large: truncated to 2000 chars server-side
DATA STORED: Click context is NOT persisted. Ephemeral per session.
DATA CACHED: No caching.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)
TEMPERATURE: 0.4
MAX TOKENS: 1500

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
"The user is currently examining a specific element in their research ancestry
graph. Use the [CONTEXT] block below to ground your responses in the actual
data. Reference specific numbers (PageRank, citation count, mutation type)
when relevant. If the user says 'this paper' or 'this connection', they mean
the element described in the context block."

RESPONSE FORMAT: Standard prose with inline data references.
CACHING: Context-aware responses are NOT cached (context changes frequently).
FALLBACK: If no click context, respond normally without graph-specific grounding.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element          | Action      | Response                                      |
|------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Context chip     | Click X     | Clear context, remove chip                    |
| Context chip     | Click body  | Scroll graph to center on that node/edge      |
| Node in graph    | Click       | Update context chip with node data            |
| Edge in graph    | Click       | Update context chip with edge data             |
| Input area       | Type        | Message sent with current click context       |
| 5 min idle       | Automatic   | Context cleared, chip removed                  |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Chip appear: translateY(-10px) to 0 over 200ms ease-out
- Chip dismiss: opacity 1 to 0 over 150ms
- Chip switch: cross-fade 150ms
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Chip has role="status" aria-live="polite". Dismiss button
labeled "Clear graph context". Screen reader announces context changes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User clicks node then immediately sends message before context loads
   Expected: Message sent without context. Context chip appears after.
   Implementation: Context set is synchronous; chip renders before next frame.

2. Scenario: User clicks node, types message, clicks different node, then sends
   Expected: Message uses the LATEST click context (second node).
   Implementation: setContext() overwrites previous context immediately.

3. Scenario: User dismisses context chip then types "tell me about this paper"
   Expected: Athena responds asking which paper the user means.
   Implementation: No context prepended when chip dismissed.

4. Scenario: Graph reloads (new paper analyzed) while context chip active
   Expected: Context cleared because old node IDs may be invalid.
   Implementation: Listen for graph:rebuild event, clear context on fire.

5. Scenario: User clicks edge with SPECULATIVE confidence
   Expected: Context includes confidence_tier. Athena may note the low confidence.
   Implementation: Full edge data included regardless of confidence level.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #004 (Discuss Integration), #006 (Conversation Memory)
ENABLES: #106 (Zoom-Aware Chat), #108 (Prune-Aware Chat)
ENHANCES: Every chat interaction when graph is visible
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CustomEvent API (browser-native)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Node click updates context chip within 100ms
[] Edge click updates context chip with mutation data
[] Context prepended to LLM system prompt on message send
[] 5-minute staleness timer clears context
[] Dismiss button clears context immediately
[] Context chip shows paper title (node) or edge label
[] New click replaces previous context (no stacking)
[] Graph reload clears stale context

VISUAL:
[] Gold border chip for nodes, blue border for edges
[] Chip slide-down animation on appear
[] Chip fade-out on dismiss
[] Chip fits within input area without overflow

PERFORMANCE: [] Context update < 100ms from click to chip render
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Chip has aria-live, dismiss button labeled, context announced
EDGE CASES: [] Stale context cleared, rapid clicks handled, graph rebuild handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-context.js, athena-engine.js, graph.js click handlers 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST node click, edge click, dismiss, staleness, rapid clicks 4. CHECK context reaches LLM prompt 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified

================================================================================
FEATURE #106: ZOOM-AWARE CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Contextual Awareness
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Zoom-Aware Chat detects the current zoom level and visible region of the Arivu
graph and adjusts Athena's responses to reflect the scope the user is examining.
When a user is zoomed out viewing the entire graph, Athena gives high-level
summaries about lineage structure. When zoomed into a specific cluster of 5-8
nodes, Athena focuses on those particular papers and their inter-relationships.

The graph uses D3's zoom transform. The visible viewport can be computed from
the zoom scale and translate offsets, combined with each node's x/y position.
Nodes whose positions fall within the current viewport bounding box are
"visible" nodes. This visible-node set is the zoom context.

The zoom context updates on a debounced schedule (500ms after the last zoom/pan
gesture ends) to avoid flooding the context system during active zooming. The
context distinguishes three zoom levels: OVERVIEW (> 60% of nodes visible),
CLUSTER (10-60% of nodes visible), and FOCUSED (< 10% of nodes visible). Each
level triggers a different system prompt modifier:

OVERVIEW: "The user is viewing the full ancestry graph. Provide high-level
structural observations about the overall lineage."
CLUSTER: "The user is focused on a cluster of N papers including [titles].
Focus responses on relationships within this group."
FOCUSED: "The user is zoomed into N specific papers: [titles]. Provide
detailed analysis of these specific papers and their connections."

This enables natural interaction patterns. A user zooms into a cluster of
early deep learning papers and asks "what connects these papers?" Athena
knows exactly which papers are visible and can answer precisely. Without
zoom awareness, Athena would need the user to list every paper by name.

The zoom level indicator appears as a subtle badge next to the context chip
area showing the current scope: a small globe icon for OVERVIEW, a cluster
icon for CLUSTER, or a magnifying glass for FOCUSED.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User zooms or pans the graph. Context updates 500ms after gesture ends.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User loads an ancestry graph for "Attention Is All You Need." The graph
   renders with all 150+ nodes visible. Zoom level: OVERVIEW.
2. A small globe icon appears in the chat context area indicating overview scope.
3. User asks "What are the major clusters in this lineage?" Athena responds
   with a high-level summary referencing all major paper groups.
4. User zooms into the pre-2000 cluster (6 nodes visible). After 500ms, the
   icon switches to a cluster icon. A subtle label shows "Viewing: 6 papers
   in early foundations cluster."
5. User asks "How do these papers relate to each other?" Athena responds
   specifically about the 6 visible papers and their edge relationships.
6. User zooms further into just 2 papers. Icon switches to magnifying glass.
   Label: "Viewing: Backpropagation (1986), Perceptron (1958)."
7. User asks "What changed between these two?" Athena gives a focused
   comparison of the two papers' contributions.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Zoom context persists until the next zoom/pan gesture. Scope indicator stays
visible. Chat responses remain contextually grounded to visible region.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-context.js: Zoom listener, viewport computation, visible
  node calculation, zoom level classification, debounce logic.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Merges zoom context with click context for LLM.
- static/js/graph.js: Exposes zoom transform and node positions to context system.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Zoom scope indicator styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaZoomContext {
    constructor(graphInstance) {
      this.graph = graphInstance;
      this.currentScope = 'OVERVIEW';
      this.visibleNodes = [];
      this.debounceTimer = null;
    }
    init() {
      this.graph.onZoom((transform) => {
        clearTimeout(this.debounceTimer);
        this.debounceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
          this.computeVisibleNodes(transform);
        }, 500);
      });
    }
    computeVisibleNodes(transform) {
      const viewport = this.getViewportBounds(transform);
      this.visibleNodes = this.graph.nodes.filter(n =>
        n.x >= viewport.left && n.x <= viewport.right &&
        n.y >= viewport.top && n.y <= viewport.bottom
      );
      const ratio = this.visibleNodes.length / this.graph.nodes.length;
      if (ratio > 0.6) this.currentScope = 'OVERVIEW';
      else if (ratio > 0.1) this.currentScope = 'CLUSTER';
      else this.currentScope = 'FOCUSED';
      this.updateScopeIndicator();
    }
    getContextForLLM() {
      if (this.currentScope === 'OVERVIEW') {
        return '[ZOOM] User is viewing the full ancestry graph overview.';
      }
      const titles = this.visibleNodes.slice(0, 10)
        .map(n => `"${n.title}" (${n.year})`).join(', ');
      return `[ZOOM] User is viewing ${this.visibleNodes.length} papers: ${titles}.` +
        ` Scope: ${this.currentScope}.`;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
No backend changes. Zoom context is appended to the graph_context parameter
already supported by #105 (Click-Aware Chat).

API CONTRACTS:
No new endpoints. Uses existing /api/athena/stream graph_context parameter.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Zoom Scope Indicator

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [globe icon] Overview (152 papers)          │
  │                                              │
  │  Ask about this lineage...             [>]   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [cluster icon] Cluster (6 papers)           │
  │  Early foundations: Perceptron, Backprop...   │
  │                                              │
  │  Ask about these papers...             [>]   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  [magnifier icon] Focused (2 papers)         │
  │  Backpropagation (1986), Perceptron (1958)   │
  │                                              │
  │  Ask about these papers...             [>]   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
- Scope change: icon cross-fades over 200ms
- Label text updates with 150ms fade transition

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

D3 zoom transform change
  -> debounce 500ms
  -> compute viewport bounding box
  -> filter nodes by position within viewport
  -> classify zoom level (OVERVIEW/CLUSTER/FOCUSED)
  -> update scope indicator UI
  -> on next message send, include zoom context in graph_context param

ERROR PATH:
- No nodes in viewport (user panned off-graph): scope defaults to OVERVIEW
- Graph not loaded: zoom context inactive, no indicator shown
DATA STORED: Zoom context is ephemeral, not persisted.
DATA CACHED: Visible node list cached until next zoom gesture.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)
TEMPERATURE: 0.4
MAX TOKENS: 1500

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION (varies by scope):
OVERVIEW: "The user is viewing the full ancestry graph. Give high-level
structural observations about lineage patterns, major clusters, and
overall research trajectory."
CLUSTER: "The user is focused on a cluster of N papers. Focus on
relationships, shared mutation patterns, and sub-lineage structure
within this visible group."
FOCUSED: "The user is examining N specific papers. Provide detailed
paper-level analysis including citation counts, mutation types, and
direct relationships between these papers."

RESPONSE FORMAT: Standard prose adapted to zoom scope.
CACHING: NOT cached (zoom context changes frequently).
FALLBACK: If zoom context unavailable, respond without scope grounding.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Scope indicator      | Hover       | Tooltip shows all visible paper titles       |
| Scope indicator      | Click       | Toggles expanded/collapsed visible list     |
| Graph zoom/pan       | Gesture     | Scope recalculated after 500ms debounce     |
| Graph reset zoom     | Click       | Scope returns to OVERVIEW                    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Scope icon transition: cross-fade 200ms
- Label text: opacity transition 150ms
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Scope indicator has aria-label describing current zoom level
and visible paper count. Updates announced via aria-live="polite".

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User zooms in/out rapidly (10+ gestures in 2 seconds)
   Expected: Only the final position is evaluated (debounce handles this).
   Implementation: clearTimeout on each gesture, 500ms debounce.

2. Scenario: Semantic zoom active (Phase 4 SemanticZoomRenderer clusters)
   Expected: Zoom context references cluster labels when in cluster view.
   Implementation: Check if semantic zoom is active, use cluster data if so.

3. Scenario: Graph has only 3 nodes total
   Expected: Zoom levels still work but thresholds adjust. 3 visible = OVERVIEW.
   Implementation: Ratio-based, so small graphs naturally classify correctly.

4. Scenario: User zooms to show exactly 1 node
   Expected: FOCUSED scope with single paper context.
   Implementation: visibleNodes.length = 1, treated as FOCUSED.

5. Scenario: Node positions not yet computed (graph still settling)
   Expected: Zoom context deferred until simulation stabilizes.
   Implementation: Check simulation alpha; skip computation if alpha > 0.1.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: All chat interactions by providing scope-appropriate responses
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: D3.js zoom transform API

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Visible nodes computed correctly from zoom transform
[] Three zoom levels classified (OVERVIEW/CLUSTER/FOCUSED)
[] Zoom context prepended to LLM system prompt
[] 500ms debounce prevents excessive recalculation
[] Scope indicator shows correct icon and paper count
[] Zoom context merges with click context (click takes priority)
[] Reset zoom returns to OVERVIEW

VISUAL:
[] Scope indicator icons (globe/cluster/magnifier) render correctly
[] Smooth transitions between scope levels
[] Paper titles in FOCUSED mode readable

PERFORMANCE: [] Visible node computation < 50ms for 600-node graph
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Scope indicator labeled, changes announced
EDGE CASES: [] Rapid zoom handled, empty viewport handled, small graph handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-context.js zoom section, graph.js zoom API 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST zoom levels at various scales, rapid zoom, pan off-graph 4. CHECK context in LLM prompt 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

================================================================================
FEATURE #107: FILTER-AWARE CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Contextual Awareness
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #106 (Zoom-Aware Chat)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Filter-Aware Chat detects when the user applies filters to the ancestry graph
(by field of study, year range, mutation type, confidence tier, or citation
count threshold) and adjusts Athena's conversational context to reflect the
filtered view. When a user filters the graph to show only "HIGH confidence
generalizations published after 2010," Athena knows exactly what subset of
the graph is visible and tailors responses to that specific slice.

Arivu's graph supports multiple filter dimensions. The graph toolbar exposes
filters for: field_of_study (CS, Biology, Physics, etc.), year range (slider),
mutation_type (adoption, generalization, specialization, etc.), confidence_tier
(HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, SPECULATIVE), and minimum citation_count. When any filter
is active, nodes and edges that do not match are dimmed or hidden. Filter-Aware
Chat reads the active filter state and constructs a filter context block.

The filter context is complementary to click and zoom contexts. All three can
be active simultaneously. The priority order is: click context (most specific),
then filter context (defines the visible subset), then zoom context (defines
the viewport scope). When merged, the LLM receives a layered understanding:
"User clicked ResNet, which is one of 23 papers visible after filtering for
HIGH confidence edges in Computer Science, and they are zoomed into a cluster
of 8 of those 23."

The filter context is updated immediately when a filter changes (no debounce
needed since filter changes are discrete user actions, not continuous gestures).
A filter summary badge appears below the context chip area showing active
filters in a compact format: "Filtered: CS only, 2010+, HIGH confidence."

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User activates or changes any graph filter control.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User opens the graph filter panel and selects "Computer Science" as the
   field of study filter. The graph dims non-CS nodes.
2. A filter badge appears in the chat context area: "Filtered: CS only."
3. User adds a year filter: 2010-2024. Badge updates: "Filtered: CS, 2010+."
4. User types "What are the most impactful papers in this view?"
5. Athena receives the filter context and responds about CS papers from 2010
   onward only, referencing actual nodes that pass both filters.
6. User adds mutation_type filter for "generalization" only. Badge updates:
   "Filtered: CS, 2010+, generalization."
7. User asks "Why do so many papers generalize in this period?" Athena
   discusses the trend of generalization within the filtered set.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Filter badge persists until all filters are cleared. Athena continues to
scope responses to the active filter set.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-context.js: Filter state listener, filter context builder,
  badge UI rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Merges filter context with click/zoom contexts.
- static/js/graph.js: Exposes active filter state via event or accessor.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Filter badge styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaFilterContext {
    constructor(graphInstance) {
      this.graph = graphInstance;
      this.activeFilters = {};
    }
    init() {
      document.addEventListener('athena:graph:filter', (e) => {
        this.activeFilters = e.detail.filters;
        this.updateBadge();
      });
    }
    getContextForLLM() {
      const parts = [];
      if (this.activeFilters.field)
        parts.push(`field: ${this.activeFilters.field}`);
      if (this.activeFilters.yearMin)
        parts.push(`year >= ${this.activeFilters.yearMin}`);
      if (this.activeFilters.yearMax)
        parts.push(`year <= ${this.activeFilters.yearMax}`);
      if (this.activeFilters.mutationType)
        parts.push(`mutation: ${this.activeFilters.mutationType}`);
      if (this.activeFilters.confidenceTier)
        parts.push(`confidence: ${this.activeFilters.confidenceTier}`);
      if (this.activeFilters.minCitations)
        parts.push(`citations >= ${this.activeFilters.minCitations}`);
      if (parts.length === 0) return '';
      const matchCount = this.graph.getFilteredNodeCount();
      return `[FILTER] Active filters: ${parts.join(', ')}. `
        + `${matchCount} papers match these criteria.`;
    }
    updateBadge() { /* renders compact filter summary */ }
    clearFilters() { this.activeFilters = {}; this.removeBadge(); }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
No backend changes. Filter context is appended to the graph_context parameter.

API CONTRACTS:
No new endpoints. Uses existing /api/athena/stream graph_context parameter.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Filter Badge Active

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ● ResNet (2016)                        [x]  │  <- click context chip
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │  Filtered: CS, 2010+, HIGH conf (23)  │   │  <- filter badge
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  Ask about this filtered view...        [>]   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
- Badge appears: 150ms fade-in when first filter activated
- Badge updates: text cross-fade 100ms on filter change
- Badge removed: 150ms fade-out when all filters cleared

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Graph filter control change
  -> dispatch CustomEvent('athena:graph:filter', { detail: { filters } })
  -> AthenaFilterContext updates activeFilters
  -> filter badge rendered
  -> on next message send, filter context merged into graph_context param
  -> LLM receives layered context (click + filter + zoom)

ERROR PATH:
- No filters active: empty context string, no badge shown
- Filter produces 0 matching nodes: context notes "0 papers match"
DATA STORED: Filter context is ephemeral, not persisted.
DATA CACHED: No caching.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)
TEMPERATURE: 0.4
MAX TOKENS: 1500

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
"The user has applied filters to their ancestry graph. Only papers matching
the filter criteria are visible. When discussing the graph, focus on papers
that match the active filters. Mention the filter criteria when relevant
to show you understand what subset the user is examining."

RESPONSE FORMAT: Standard prose referencing filter criteria.
CACHING: NOT cached (filter state changes frequently).
FALLBACK: If no filters active, respond without filter scoping.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element          | Action      | Response                                      |
|------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Filter badge     | Click       | Opens graph filter panel                      |
| Filter badge     | Hover       | Tooltip shows all active filter details       |
| Filter control   | Change      | Badge updates immediately                     |
| Clear all button | Click       | Badge removed, context cleared                |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Badge appear: opacity 0 to 1 over 150ms
- Badge text update: cross-fade 100ms
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Badge has role="status" aria-live="polite". Filter criteria
announced on change.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User applies filter that excludes the currently clicked node
   Expected: Click context cleared (node no longer visible). Filter badge
   remains. Chat responds within filter scope only.
   Implementation: On filter change, check if clicked node passes filters.

2. Scenario: User applies extremely restrictive filter (1 paper matches)
   Expected: Athena treats this as a de facto focused view. Responds about
   the single matching paper in detail.
   Implementation: matchCount = 1 handled gracefully in context string.

3. Scenario: Multiple filters applied then cleared one at a time
   Expected: Badge updates incrementally as each filter is removed.
   Implementation: activeFilters object updated per individual filter change.

4. Scenario: Filter state conflicts with zoom context (zoomed into area
   where no filtered papers exist)
   Expected: Athena notes the empty visible area. Suggests adjusting filters.
   Implementation: If visibleNodes intersect filteredNodes is empty, note it.

5. Scenario: User switches to a different graph while filters are active
   Expected: Filters cleared. Badge removed. Fresh context for new graph.
   Implementation: Listen for graph:rebuild event, clear filter context.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: All chat interactions by scoping to filtered graph subset
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: NONE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Filter state captured on every filter change
[] Filter context prepended to LLM system prompt
[] Filter badge shows compact summary of active filters
[] Matching paper count included in context
[] Click context cleared if clicked node excluded by filter
[] All filter dimensions supported (field, year, mutation, confidence, citations)
[] Graph switch clears filter context

VISUAL:
[] Filter badge styled consistently with context chips
[] Badge updates smoothly on filter change
[] Compact format readable at a glance

PERFORMANCE: [] Filter context update < 50ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Badge labeled, filter changes announced
EDGE CASES: [] Empty filter result handled, filter+zoom conflict handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

================================================================================
FEATURE #108: PRUNE-AWARE CHAT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Contextual Awareness
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #006 (Conversation Memory)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prune-Aware Chat detects when the user activates the cascading pruning
animation (Arivu's flagship feature) and adjusts Athena's context to reflect
the pruning state. When a user removes a foundational paper from history and
watches 31% of the lineage collapse, Athena knows exactly which paper was
pruned, which nodes collapsed, which survived, and the impact percentage.
This enables deeply insightful post-pruning conversations.

The cascading pruning animation is Arivu's core differentiator. When a user
clicks "Remove from history" on a node, dependent papers collapse in BFS-level
waves. Collapsed papers turn red and fade. Survival paths glow green. A stats
counter runs in real time showing collapsed count and impact percentage. After
the animation completes, the user naturally wants to discuss what happened:
"Why did those papers survive?" "What would the field look like without this
paper?" "Which surviving papers found alternative foundations?"

Without prune awareness, these questions are unanswerable because Athena has
no knowledge of the pruning result. With prune awareness, the full
precomputed_pruning data for the pruned node is injected into the chat context:
collapsed_nodes (with BFS levels), surviving_nodes (with survival_paths),
impact_percentage, and collapsed_count.

The pruning context is set when the pruning animation begins and persists until
the user resets the graph or starts a new pruning. A prominent context chip
shows the pruning state: "Pruned: ResNet (2016) - 31% collapsed, 47 papers
removed." This chip uses var(--danger) red styling to match the visual language
of the pruning animation itself.

The prune context also includes the survival paths: for each surviving node,
the alternative ancestry path that keeps it alive. This enables Athena to
explain WHY specific papers survived ("VGGNet survived because it has an
independent path through LeNet -> Neocognitron that does not depend on
ResNet").

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
User activates cascading pruning on any node in the graph.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User right-clicks "ResNet (2016)" and selects "Remove from history."
2. The cascading pruning animation begins. Nodes collapse in BFS waves.
3. As the animation runs, the prune context chip appears in the chat area:
   "Pruning: ResNet (2016)..." with a loading spinner.
4. Animation completes. Chip updates: "Pruned: ResNet (2016) - 31% collapsed
   (47 papers). 105 survived." Chip is styled with red border.
5. User types "Why did BERT survive without ResNet?"
6. Athena receives the pruning context including BERT's survival_path. It
   responds: "BERT survived the removal of ResNet because it has an
   independent dependency path through Word2Vec -> GloVe -> ELMo that
   provides its core embedding foundations without requiring ResNet's
   residual connection architecture."
7. User types "Which papers were most dependent on ResNet?"
8. Athena references the collapsed_nodes list sorted by BFS level 1 (direct
   dependents) and responds with the papers that collapsed first.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Prune context persists until graph reset or new pruning. All subsequent chat
messages are prune-aware. Quick actions may suggest pruning-related questions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-context.js: Prune event listener, pruning data parser,
  prune context chip UI.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Merges prune context with other contexts.
- static/js/pruning.js: Emits athena:graph:prune event with pruning result data.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Prune context chip styles (red variant).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaPruneContext {
    constructor() {
      this.pruneResult = null;
    }
    init() {
      document.addEventListener('athena:graph:prune', (e) => {
        this.setPruneContext(e.detail);
      });
      document.addEventListener('athena:graph:reset', () => {
        this.clearPruneContext();
      });
    }
    setPruneContext(detail) {
      this.pruneResult = {
        prunedPaperId: detail.paper_id,
        prunedTitle: detail.title,
        collapsedNodes: detail.collapsed_nodes,
        survivingNodes: detail.surviving_nodes,
        impactPercentage: detail.impact_percentage,
        collapsedCount: detail.collapsed_count,
        totalNodes: detail.total_nodes
      };
      this.renderPruneChip();
    }
    getContextForLLM() {
      if (!this.pruneResult) return '';
      const r = this.pruneResult;
      const collapsed = r.collapsedNodes.slice(0, 10)
        .map(n => `"${n.title}" (level ${n.bfs_level})`).join(', ');
      const survived = r.survivingNodes.slice(0, 5)
        .map(n => `"${n.title}" via [${n.survival_path.join(' -> ')}]`).join('; ');
      return `[PRUNE] "${r.prunedTitle}" removed from history. `
        + `Impact: ${r.impactPercentage}% collapsed (${r.collapsedCount}/${r.totalNodes}). `
        + `Collapsed (first 10): ${collapsed}. `
        + `Survived (first 5 with paths): ${survived}.`;
    }
    clearPruneContext() { this.pruneResult = null; this.removePruneChip(); }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
No new endpoints. Pruning data comes from the precomputed_pruning object in
graph JSON, which is already available on the frontend from the initial graph
load. The context is assembled entirely client-side.

API CONTRACTS:
No new endpoints. Uses existing /api/athena/stream graph_context parameter.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Pruning In Progress

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │  [red border] Pruning: ResNet (2016)   │   │
  │  │  ◐ Cascading...                        │   │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  Waiting for pruning to complete...    [>]    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Pruning Complete

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │  [red border] Pruned: ResNet (2016)    │   │
  │  │  31% collapsed (47 papers removed)     │   │
  │  │  105 papers survived                   │   │
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  Ask about the pruning result...       [>]    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
- Chip appears: slide-in with red border glow
- Loading to complete: spinner replaced with stats, 200ms transition
- Chip dismissed on graph reset: fade-out 200ms

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pruning.js animation completes
  -> dispatch CustomEvent('athena:graph:prune', { detail: pruneResult })
  -> AthenaPruneContext.setPruneContext(detail)
  -> prune chip rendered with stats
  -> User sends message
  -> AthenaEngine merges prune context into graph_context
  -> LLM generates pruning-aware response referencing collapsed/survived nodes

ERROR PATH:
- Pruning data missing (precomputed_pruning not in graph JSON): prune context
  unavailable, chip shows "Pruning data unavailable"
- Pruning on seed paper: 100% collapse, context reflects total collapse
DATA STORED: Prune context is ephemeral, not persisted.
DATA CACHED: Pruning result cached until graph reset.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) for post-prune
analysis (complex multi-paper reasoning).
TEMPERATURE: 0.3
MAX TOKENS: 2000

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
"The user has just run a counterfactual analysis by removing a paper from the
research lineage. Use the [PRUNE] context block to understand which paper was
removed, what percentage of the field collapsed, and which papers survived via
alternative paths. Help the user understand the structural implications:
Why did specific papers collapse? Why did others survive? What does this tell
us about the field's dependency structure?"

RESPONSE FORMAT: Analytical prose with specific paper references and survival
path explanations.
CACHING: NOT cached (pruning results vary per graph).
FALLBACK: If no prune context, respond normally.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element          | Action      | Response                                      |
|------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Prune chip       | Click body  | Scrolls to collapsed region of graph          |
| Prune chip       | Hover       | Tooltip shows top 5 collapsed + top 5 survived|
| Graph reset btn  | Click       | Prune context cleared, chip removed            |
| New prune        | Activate    | Previous prune context replaced                |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Chip appear: translateX(-10px) to 0, red border glow 300ms
- Spinner: rotating ring animation during cascade
- Stats reveal: counter animation 0 to final value over 500ms
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Chip has aria-live="assertive" for pruning start/complete.
Impact stats read by screen reader.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User prunes seed paper (root of the entire graph)
   Expected: 100% collapse (all papers depend on seed). Context reflects this.
   Athena discusses total lineage dependency.
   Implementation: Handle impact_percentage = 100 gracefully.

2. Scenario: User prunes a leaf node (no dependents)
   Expected: 0% collapse. Context notes minimal impact. Athena explains why.
   Implementation: collapsedCount = 0, impactPercentage = 0.

3. Scenario: User runs multiple prunes in sequence without resetting
   Expected: Each prune replaces previous context. Only latest prune active.
   Implementation: setPruneContext() overwrites, does not stack.

4. Scenario: Pruning animation interrupted (user navigates away)
   Expected: Partial prune context set with available data. Chip shows
   "Pruning interrupted."
   Implementation: Listen for prune:cancel event, set partial context.

5. Scenario: precomputed_pruning missing from graph JSON (graph too large)
   Expected: Prune context cannot be computed client-side. Chat informs user
   that pruning analysis is unavailable for this graph.
   Implementation: Check for precomputed_pruning before enabling prune chip.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #105 (Click-Aware Chat), #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #006 (Conversation Memory)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: Post-pruning discussion quality dramatically
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: precomputed_pruning data in graph JSON

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] Prune event captured when cascading animation completes
[] Prune context includes collapsed_nodes, surviving_nodes, impact stats
[] Prune chip shows paper name, impact percentage, collapsed count
[] Survival paths included in LLM context
[] Graph reset clears prune context
[] New prune replaces previous prune context
[] Seed paper prune (100% collapse) handled correctly
[] Leaf node prune (0% collapse) handled correctly

VISUAL:
[] Red border chip matches pruning animation color language
[] Loading spinner during cascade animation
[] Stats counter animation on completion

PERFORMANCE: [] Prune context assembly < 100ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Prune state changes announced, stats readable
EDGE CASES: [] 100% collapse, 0% collapse, interrupted prune, missing data

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-context.js prune section, pruning.js event dispatch 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST prune seed, prune leaf, prune middle node, reset, sequential prunes 4. CHECK context in LLM prompt includes survival paths 5. VERIFY no regression 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Empty -- feature not yet implemented]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
Notes:            P1. Critical for post-pruning discussion. Uses 70b model for complex reasoning about counterfactual lineage structure.


================================================================================
FEATURE #109: SESSION HISTORY AWARENESS
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Contextual Awareness
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #006 (Conversation Memory), #001 (Context-Aware Responses)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #006 (Conversation Memory), #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #105 (Click-Aware Chat)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Session History Awareness gives Athena knowledge of all actions the user has
taken during the current session, not just what they have typed in the chat.
This includes which papers they have clicked on, which edges they have
inspected, which prunings they have run, which filters they have toggled,
how long they have spent on each part of the graph, and what their navigation
trajectory has been. This accumulated session journal transforms Athena from
a stateless Q&A bot into a companion that remembers the user's research journey.

Arivu already tracks user actions in the action_log database table via the
log_action() utility function. Every significant user action (click, prune,
filter, zoom, chat message, export) is recorded with a session_id, action_type,
and action_data JSON. Session History Awareness reads this log and constructs
a chronological narrative of the user's session that is included in Athena's
system prompt.

The session journal is formatted as a compact timeline:
"Session started 12 minutes ago. User analyzed 'Attention Is All You Need.'
First explored the overview (2 min). Clicked on 'BERT' and 'GPT-2' nodes.
Filtered to CS papers only. Ran pruning on 'Word2Vec' (18% collapse). Asked
3 questions about transformer architecture. Currently viewing the post-prune
graph."

This enables Athena to say things like "Earlier you noticed that Word2Vec had
an 18% impact. The cluster you are now looking at depends on a different
embedding lineage..." without the user having to recap their session manually.

The journal has a token budget of 500 tokens maximum. Older actions are
summarized more aggressively. The most recent 5 actions get full detail.
Actions older than 10 minutes get one-line summaries. Actions older than
30 minutes get dropped entirely (the user has likely moved on).

The journal is reconstructed from the action_log table on every message send,
ensuring it is always current. It is NOT stored as a separate field; it is
a derived view of the existing action_log data.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Automatic. Session history is included in every Athena message context. No
user activation needed.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User analyzes "Attention Is All You Need" and explores the graph for 5
   minutes, clicking various nodes.
2. User types "What should I look at next?"
3. Athena receives the session journal showing the user's exploration pattern.
   It notices the user has clicked on 4 transformer-related papers but has
   not explored the pre-transformer RNN lineage.
4. Athena responds: "You have been exploring the transformer branch
   extensively. Consider examining the RNN lineage (LSTM, GRU, Seq2Seq)
   to understand what transformers replaced. The edge from 'Seq2Seq' to
   'Attention' is classified as a 'contradiction' with HIGH confidence,
   which suggests a fundamental paradigm shift worth investigating."
5. The suggestion is grounded in both the graph data AND the user's session
   history (what they have already seen vs. what they have not).

AFTER COMPLETION:
Session journal continues accumulating. Each new message to Athena includes
the latest journal state.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-context.js: Session journal builder, action_log reader,
  token budget management.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Includes session journal in system prompt.
- app.py: /api/athena/session-journal endpoint returns formatted journal
  from action_log table.
- backend/utils.py: log_action() already exists; no changes needed.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  class AthenaSessionHistory {
    constructor(sessionId) {
      this.sessionId = sessionId;
      this.journal = '';
    }
    async refresh() {
      const resp = await fetch(`/api/athena/session-journal?session_id=${this.sessionId}`);
      const data = await resp.json();
      this.journal = data.journal;
    }
    getContextForLLM() {
      if (!this.journal) return '';
      return `[SESSION] ${this.journal}`;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
```python
@app.route('/api/athena/session-journal')
def get_session_journal():
    session_id = request.args.get('session_id')
    if not session_id:
        return jsonify({"error": "session_id required"}), 400

    actions = db.fetchall(
        '''SELECT action_type, action_data, created_at
           FROM action_log
           WHERE session_id = %s
           AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 minutes'
           ORDER BY created_at DESC
           LIMIT 50''',
        (session_id,)
    )

    journal = build_session_journal(actions)
    return jsonify({"journal": journal})


def build_session_journal(actions):
    '''Build a compact narrative from action_log entries.
    Token budget: 500 tokens max (~375 words).'''
    if not actions:
        return "New session. No prior actions."

    lines = []
    for i, action in enumerate(reversed(actions)):
        age_minutes = (datetime.utcnow() - action['created_at']).total_seconds() / 60
        if i < 5:
            # Recent actions: full detail
            lines.append(format_action_detail(action))
        elif age_minutes < 10:
            # Medium-age: one-line summary
            lines.append(format_action_summary(action))
        # Older than 10 min: dropped (already filtered by 30-min SQL)

    return ' '.join(lines[:20])  # Hard cap at 20 lines
```

API CONTRACTS:
GET /api/athena/session-journal
  Query params:
    session_id: string (required)
  Response:
    { "journal": "Session started 12 min ago. User analyzed..." }
  Max response size: 500 tokens (~2000 chars)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Session History Indicator (subtle, non-intrusive)

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ● ResNet (2016)                        [x]  │  <- click context
  │  Filtered: CS, 2010+                         │  <- filter context
  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │  ○ Session: 12 min, 8 actions         │   │  <- history indicator
  │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  Ask a question...                     [>]    │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
- Indicator updates silently (no animation) on each journal refresh
- Tooltip on hover shows last 3 actions in plain text

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

User sends message
  -> AthenaSessionHistory.refresh() fetches /api/athena/session-journal
  -> Backend queries action_log for session actions (last 30 min)
  -> build_session_journal() formats compact narrative
  -> Frontend receives journal string
  -> AthenaEngine prepends [SESSION] block to system prompt
  -> LLM generates session-aware response

ERROR PATH:
- action_log empty: journal = "New session. No prior actions."
- Endpoint fails: session context omitted, chat works normally
DATA STORED: Uses existing action_log table. No new storage.
DATA CACHED: Journal cached for 30 seconds to avoid excessive DB reads.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL (llama-3.1-8b-instant)
TEMPERATURE: 0.4
MAX TOKENS: 1500

SYSTEM PROMPT ADDITION:
"You have access to the user's session history. Use the [SESSION] block to
understand what the user has already explored, which papers they have examined,
and what questions they have asked. Reference their journey when making
suggestions. Do not repeat information they have already seen. Guide them
toward unexplored areas of the graph."

RESPONSE FORMAT: Standard prose with session-aware references.
CACHING: NOT cached (session journal changes with each action).
FALLBACK: If session journal unavailable, respond without session context.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element              | Action      | Response                                    |
|----------------------|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Session indicator    | Hover       | Tooltip shows last 3 actions                |
| Session indicator    | Click       | Expands to show full action timeline         |
| Any graph action     | Perform     | action_log updated, journal refreshes       |

ANIMATION DETAILS: None. Session indicator updates silently.
SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Session indicator has aria-label describing action count and
session duration. Not announced on update (too frequent).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User has been active for > 30 minutes
   Expected: Only last 30 minutes of actions included. Older actions dropped.
   Implementation: SQL WHERE clause limits to 30-minute window.

2. Scenario: User has performed 100+ actions in a session
   Expected: Journal caps at 50 actions, prioritizing recent ones.
   Implementation: LIMIT 50 in SQL query, recent actions get full detail.

3. Scenario: Multiple tabs/sessions for same user
   Expected: Each session has its own journal. No cross-session bleed.
   Implementation: Journal keyed by session_id, not user_id.

4. Scenario: action_log table is empty (first action of session)
   Expected: Journal says "New session. No prior actions."
   Implementation: Empty list check in build_session_journal().

5. Scenario: Journal exceeds 500 token budget
   Expected: Older entries dropped until budget met.
   Implementation: Token counting with truncation in build_session_journal().

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #006 (Conversation Memory), #001 (Context-Aware Responses), #105 (Click-Aware Chat)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: All chat interactions by grounding in session exploration history
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: action_log table (exists from Phase 1 schema)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[] /api/athena/session-journal returns formatted journal
[] Journal includes click, prune, filter, zoom, and chat actions
[] Recent actions (< 5) get full detail
[] Older actions get one-line summaries
[] Actions > 30 minutes excluded
[] Token budget of 500 tokens enforced
[] Journal included in LLM system prompt

VISUAL:
[] Session indicator shows action count and duration
[] Tooltip shows last 3 actions on hover

PERFORMANCE: [] Journal endpoint responds in < 200ms
ACCESSIBILITY: [] Indicator labeled with session stats
EDGE CASES: [] Empty session, long session, high action count all handled

VERIFICATION:
[] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-context.js session section, app.py journal endpoint 2. COMPARE criteria 3. TEST empty session, 5-action session, 50-action session, 30-min cutoff 4. CHECK journal in LLM prompt 5. VERIFY token budget 6. BROKEN 7. 1+/round 8. Log below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-24
