FEATURE #071: MESSAGE ENTRANCE ANIMATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #075 (Response Reveal), #077 (Collapse Animation)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Message Entrance Animation controls how new messages visually appear in the
Athena chat panel. Without this feature, messages pop into existence with no
transition: one frame the space is empty, the next frame a full message block
occupies it. This instant appearance feels mechanical and disconnects the user
from the conversational rhythm of the interface. With entrance animation, each
message slides upward from a slight offset and fades from zero opacity to full
opacity, creating the sensation that the message is arriving rather than simply
appearing.

The entrance effect uses a CSS keyframe animation called athena-message-enter
that combines translateY with opacity. The message starts 12px below its final
resting position at opacity 0 and transitions to translateY(0) at opacity 1
over 280ms using a cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1) easing function. This
timing curve produces a slight acceleration at the start and a gentle
deceleration at the end, giving the motion a natural, physically plausible
quality that matches the dot matrix aesthetic of the Arivu interface where
elements materialize progressively rather than appearing instantly.

User messages and Athena messages share the same entrance animation but with a
directional difference. User messages enter from the right side (translateX
positive to zero) reflecting their origin from the user's input area. Athena
messages enter from the left side (translateX negative to zero) reflecting
their origin from the system. Both also use the vertical slide component
(translateY 12px to 0) so the overall motion is a diagonal sweep that
converges on the final position. The directional distinction reinforces the
conversational turn-taking pattern visually.

When the chat panel is scrolled to the bottom (auto-scroll active), new messages
trigger smooth scrolling that synchronizes with the entrance animation. The
scroll begins at the same instant as the animation so the user sees the message
slide into view within the visible area rather than appearing below the fold and
scrolling up afterwards. This synchronization prevents the jarring double-motion
of animate-then-scroll.

The animation is applied via a CSS class athena-msg-entering that is added when
the message DOM element is created and removed after the animation completes
(animationend event). This prevents the animation from replaying on layout
reflows, scroll position changes, or DOM mutations that could cause the message
to re-enter its CSS animation state.

For batch loading scenarios such as conversation history restoration, messages
receive a staggered entrance delay. The first message has 0ms delay, the second
40ms, the third 80ms, continuing in 40ms increments up to a maximum of 400ms
total. This produces a cascade effect where messages appear to flow into the
panel one after another like entries materializing on a research ledger rather
than all snapping in simultaneously.

The feature respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query. When the user has
requested reduced motion in their operating system settings, the entrance
animation is replaced with a simple opacity fade from 0 to 1 over 100ms with
no translateY or translateX component. This preserves the subtle appearance
effect without any spatial movement that could cause discomfort.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
A new message is appended to the Athena chat panel, either from user input
submission or from Athena's response stream completing a message block.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types "What are the key mutation types in this lineage?" and presses
   Enter. The user message element is created in the DOM with the class
   athena-msg-entering applied. It slides in from the bottom-right (12px down,
   8px right) and fades to full opacity over 280ms.
2. The chat panel scrolls smoothly downward in sync with the message entrance
   so the message is visible as it animates. The scroll and the slide happen
   in parallel, completing within the same 280ms window.
3. Athena begins streaming a response. The response container element enters
   from the bottom-left (12px down, 8px left) with the same 280ms timing.
   Text streams into the container while it is fully opaque and in its final
   position (the entrance animation completes before or during early tokens).
4. When the response completes and the next message block begins (if Athena
   sends a multi-block response), each subsequent block uses the stagger
   delay: first block at 0ms, second at 40ms, third at 80ms.
5. On animationend, the athena-msg-entering class is removed from each
   message element, leaving it in its static final state.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The message is statically positioned with no active animations, no transform
residuals, and no ongoing CSS animation state. It is indistinguishable from a
message that was loaded from history without animation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-animations.css: Keyframe definitions for athena-message-enter,
  athena-message-enter-user, athena-message-enter-athena, reduced-motion overrides.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Applies athena-msg-entering class on message creation.
  Manages stagger delay for batch loads. Removes class on animationend.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Coordinates entrance animation timing with scroll
  position during streaming responses.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Base message positioning that entrance transforms
  operate relative to.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-engine.js - message creation
  appendMessage(role, content, options = {}) {
    const el = this.createMessageElement(role, content);
    const staggerIndex = options.staggerIndex || 0;
    const delay = Math.min(staggerIndex * 40, 400);

    el.classList.add('athena-msg-entering');
    el.classList.add(role === 'user' ? 'athena-enter-user' : 'athena-enter-athena');
    el.style.animationDelay = `${delay}ms`;

    el.addEventListener('animationend', () => {
      el.classList.remove('athena-msg-entering', 'athena-enter-user', 'athena-enter-athena');
      el.style.animationDelay = '';
    }, { once: true });

    this.chatContainer.appendChild(el);
    if (this.isScrolledToBottom()) {
      this.smoothScrollToBottom(280);
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Message entrance animation is purely a frontend CSS/JS concern
  with no backend interaction. Message creation events originate from the existing
  streaming infrastructure.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
@keyframes athena-message-enter-from-left {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    transform: translate(-8px, 12px);
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: translate(0, 0);
  }
}

@keyframes athena-message-enter-from-right {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    transform: translate(8px, 12px);
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: translate(0, 0);
  }
}

.athena-msg-entering {
  will-change: transform, opacity;
}

.athena-enter-athena {
  animation: athena-message-enter-from-left 280ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1) both;
}

.athena-enter-user {
  animation: athena-message-enter-from-right 280ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1) both;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-enter-athena,
  .athena-enter-user {
    animation: athena-message-fade-only 100ms ease both;
  }
}

@keyframes athena-message-fade-only {
  from { opacity: 0; }
  to   { opacity: 1; }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints involved. Animation is triggered by DOM
insertion events in the existing message rendering pipeline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Message Entering (Athena response, t=0ms)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: What are the key mutation types?                │
  │                                                         │
  │                                                         │
  │         ○ Athena: [opacity: 0, y: +12px, x: -8px]      │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Message Entering (Athena response, t=140ms, midpoint)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: What are the key mutation types?                │
  │                                                         │
  │       ○ Athena: The graph contains  [opacity: 0.6]      │
  │         [y: +4px, x: -3px]                              │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Message Entered (Athena response, t=280ms, complete)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: What are the key mutation types?                │
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena: The graph contains 7 distinct  [opacity: 1]  │
  │    mutation types across 203 edges...    [y: 0, x: 0]   │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Batch Load Stagger (conversation history, 5 messages)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ● Message 1   [delay: 0ms]    -- visible first         │
  │  ○ Message 2   [delay: 40ms]   -- appears 40ms later    │
  │  ○ Message 3   [delay: 80ms]   -- appears 80ms later    │
  │  ○ Message 4   [delay: 120ms]  -- appears 120ms later   │
  │  ○ Message 5   [delay: 160ms]  -- appears 160ms later   │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Entrance: 280ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1). translateY(12px) to 0, opacity 0 to 1.
Directional: User messages from right (+8px x), Athena from left (-8px x).
Stagger: 40ms increment per message, capped at 400ms total delay.
Reduced motion: 100ms opacity fade only, no spatial transform.
Scroll sync: smooth scrollTo over 280ms parallel to animation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User submits message or Athena stream completes a block
  -> athena-engine.js appendMessage() called
  -> DOM element created with role-specific class
  -> athena-msg-entering class applied
  -> animationDelay set based on stagger index
  -> Element appended to chat container
  -> Browser triggers CSS animation (composite layer promoted via will-change)
  -> If auto-scroll active: smoothScrollToBottom(280) runs in parallel
  -> Animation completes at 280ms + staggerDelay
  -> animationend event fires
  -> athena-msg-entering class removed (prevents replay)
  -> Element in static final state

ERROR PATH: animationend never fires (browser bug or interrupted animation)
  -> Fallback: setTimeout(cleanup, 500) removes the class after 500ms
  -> Message remains visible and functional regardless

DATA STORED: No animation state is persisted. All animation is ephemeral CSS.
DATA CACHED: No data cached. Animation parameters are CSS constants.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

Message entrance animation is a pure CSS/JS visual effect with no LLM
involvement. The animation triggers on DOM insertion events that are downstream
of the existing message rendering pipeline. No prompts, no model calls, no
token budgets are involved.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| New user message     | DOM insert   | Slide-fade from bottom-right, 280ms      |
| New Athena message   | DOM insert   | Slide-fade from bottom-left, 280ms       |
| Batch load messages  | History load | Staggered entrance, 40ms increments      |
| Multi-block response | Stream end   | Each block enters with stagger delay     |
| Scroll position      | Auto-scroll  | Synced smooth scroll during animation    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Duration: 280ms per message. Easing: cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1).
Stagger: 40ms per message in batch, max 400ms total. Will-change: transform,
opacity (promotes to composite layer for GPU acceleration). Cleanup: class
removal on animationend + 500ms fallback timeout.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: prefers-reduced-motion reduces to 100ms opacity-only fade. No
spatial transforms in reduced motion mode. Screen readers unaffected because
animation is purely visual decoration on message containers.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: 50+ messages loaded from conversation history simultaneously
   Expected: Stagger delay caps at 400ms (10th message). Messages 11-50
   all animate with 400ms delay, creating a bulk appearance after the cascade.
   Implementation: Math.min(staggerIndex * 40, 400) caps the delay.

2. Scenario: User rapidly sends 5 messages before any animation completes
   Expected: All 5 messages animate independently with overlapping timings.
   Each starts its own 280ms animation from its insertion point.
   Implementation: Each message gets its own animationend listener. No shared
   state between concurrent animations.

3. Scenario: Chat panel is scrolled up (not at bottom) when new message arrives
   Expected: Message is appended but no auto-scroll occurs. Animation still
   plays but may be off-screen. User sees scroll indicator instead.
   Implementation: isScrolledToBottom() check before scrollTo call.

4. Scenario: Browser tab is hidden (document.hidden === true) when message arrives
   Expected: Animation skipped entirely. Message appears immediately in final
   state. When tab becomes visible, messages are static.
   Implementation: Check document.hidden; if true, skip adding animation class.

5. Scenario: prefers-reduced-motion is toggled while chat is open
   Expected: New messages use the updated preference. Existing messages are
   unaffected (their animation already completed).
   Implementation: CSS media query handles this automatically for new animations.

6. Scenario: Message contains very tall content (long code block, large table)
   Expected: Entrance animation still works. The entire message block slides in
   as one unit regardless of height.
   Implementation: Animation applies to the message container, not individual
   content elements within.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: #075 (Response Reveal)
ENHANCES: #072 (Typing Animation), #077 (Collapse Animation)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS Animations API, requestAnimationFrame, will-change compositor hints

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] New user messages slide-fade from bottom-right over 280ms
[ ] New Athena messages slide-fade from bottom-left over 280ms
[ ] Batch-loaded messages stagger at 40ms increments, capped at 400ms
[ ] Animation class removed on animationend (no replay on reflow)
[ ] Fallback timeout removes class after 500ms if animationend fails

VISUAL:
[ ] Entrance uses cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1) easing
[ ] translateY(12px) and translateX(8px) starting offsets correct per role
[ ] Opacity transitions from 0 to 1 smoothly without flicker
[ ] Stagger cascade visually distinct on 10+ message batch load

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Animation runs on compositor thread (will-change applied)
[ ] No layout thrashing during entrance (transform + opacity only)
[ ] 50 simultaneous animations do not drop below 60fps

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] prefers-reduced-motion collapses to 100ms opacity fade only
[ ] No translateX or translateY in reduced motion mode
[ ] Screen reader announcement unaffected by animation timing

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Hidden tab skips animation entirely
[ ] Rapid message sends produce overlapping but independent animations
[ ] Scrolled-up panel does not auto-scroll on new message

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Chrome, Firefox, Safari entrance verified
[ ] PERFORMANCE TEST: 50-message batch at 60fps confirmed
[ ] REDUCED MOTION: Verified with system preference toggled
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-animations.css keyframes, athena-engine.js appendMessage,
   athena-stream.js scroll sync logic, athena-chat.css base positioning
2. COMPARE all Done Criteria checkboxes against actual implementation
3. TEST user message entrance direction (bottom-right slide), Athena message
   entrance direction (bottom-left slide), verify 280ms duration in DevTools
4. TEST batch load with 20+ messages: confirm stagger cascade at 40ms, confirm
   delay cap at 400ms, confirm no messages skip animation
5. VERIFY animationend cleanup: inspect DOM after animation, confirm class
   removed, trigger layout reflow, confirm animation does not replay
6. CHECK prefers-reduced-motion: toggle OS setting, send new message, confirm
   only opacity fade with no spatial transform, confirm 100ms duration
7. BROKEN: Assume animation fails on Safari. Test WebKit prefix requirements.
   Assume compositor promotion fails. Verify will-change cleanup.
8. LOG findings below, one or more issues per round

FEATURE #072: TYPING ANIMATION (DOT MATRIX)
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 6
OVERLAPS WITH:   #073 (Streaming Cursor)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Typing Animation renders three gold dots that wave in sequence while Athena
is processing a request but has not yet begun streaming a response. This is the
"thinking" indicator: the visual signal that Athena has received the user's
message, is working on it, and will begin responding shortly. Without this
indicator, the user stares at a static chat panel with no feedback, uncertain
whether their message was received, whether the system is processing, or whether
something has silently failed.

The three dots are rendered as span elements inside a container div with the
class athena-typing-indicator. Each dot is a 6px circle filled with
var(--accent-gold) (#D4A843), the signature gold accent of the Arivu design
system. The dots are spaced 8px apart horizontally and centered vertically within
the indicator container.

The wave animation is a CSS keyframe called athena-dot-wave that transforms each
dot's vertical position. Each dot translates upward by 8px and back down over a
600ms cycle using ease-in-out timing. The three dots are staggered: the first dot
starts at 0ms, the second at 150ms, the third at 300ms. This produces the
classic wave pattern where dots rise and fall in sequence, never all at the same
position simultaneously.

The dot matrix aesthetic connects to Arivu's broader visual language. The ancestry
graph uses circular nodes. The confidence tier badges use dot indicators. The
loading skeleton uses pulsing dot placeholders. The typing indicator continues
this pattern: three dots communicating system state through minimal, precise
geometric forms rather than verbose text spinners or progress bars.

The indicator appears inside a message-shaped container positioned at the bottom
of the chat, styled identically to an Athena response message but with the dots
replacing text content. This maintains the conversational layout: the typing
indicator occupies the same visual position that the actual response will fill.
When the first token of the response arrives, the typing indicator is replaced
by the streaming response container in a single DOM swap with no visual gap.

The transition from typing indicator to response is critical. The indicator fades
out over 150ms (opacity 1 to 0) while the response container fades in over
200ms (opacity 0 to 1), creating a 50ms overlap where neither is fully visible.
This crossfade prevents the jarring snap of one element disappearing and another
appearing in its place.

The animation uses CSS-only rendering with no JavaScript animation loop. The
keyframes, stagger delays, and infinite iteration count are defined entirely in
CSS. JavaScript's only role is adding and removing the indicator element from
the DOM. This keeps the animation off the main thread and ensures smooth 60fps
rendering even when the main thread is busy with streaming response parsing.

If Athena's processing takes longer than 8 seconds without any streaming token,
the dots' wave speed slows from 600ms to 1200ms per cycle, signaling to the user
that the system is still working but the response is taking longer than usual.
This prevents the fast wave from feeling frenetic during long waits. The speed
change is applied by adding a modifier class athena-typing-slow that overrides
the animation-duration.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User sends a message to Athena and the system begins processing before
streaming has started.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User types "Explain the mutation types in the Vaswani graph" and presses
   Enter. The message is sent to the backend via POST /api/athena/message.
2. Within 100ms of sending, the typing indicator appears at the bottom of the
   chat panel inside an Athena-styled message container. Three gold dots begin
   their wave animation: dot 1 rises, dot 2 follows 150ms later, dot 3
   follows 150ms after that.
3. The user watches the dots wave for approximately 1-3 seconds while the
   backend processes the request, calls the Groq API, and begins streaming
   the response.
4. The first SSE token arrives. The typing indicator fades out over 150ms.
   Simultaneously, the response container fades in over 200ms. The dots are
   replaced by streaming text with no visual gap or layout shift.
5. If the response takes over 8 seconds, the dots slow to a 1200ms wave cycle,
   communicating extended processing without appearing broken.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The typing indicator is removed from the DOM entirely. The response message
occupies its position. No residual animation state or DOM elements remain.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-animations.css: @keyframes athena-dot-wave, timing,
  stagger delays, slow modifier, reduced-motion override.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: showTypingIndicator(), hideTypingIndicator(),
  slow-mode timeout logic.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Calls hideTypingIndicator on first token arrival.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Typing indicator container layout within message
  flow.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-engine.js
  showTypingIndicator() {
    if (this.typingIndicator) return; // already showing
    const container = document.createElement('div');
    container.className = 'athena-message athena-typing-indicator';
    container.setAttribute('role', 'status');
    container.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Athena is thinking');
    container.innerHTML = `
      <span class="athena-dot" style="animation-delay: 0ms"></span>
      <span class="athena-dot" style="animation-delay: 150ms"></span>
      <span class="athena-dot" style="animation-delay: 300ms"></span>
    `;
    this.chatContainer.appendChild(container);
    this.typingIndicator = container;
    this.typingSlowTimer = setTimeout(() => {
      container.classList.add('athena-typing-slow');
    }, 8000);
  }

  hideTypingIndicator() {
    if (!this.typingIndicator) return;
    clearTimeout(this.typingSlowTimer);
    const el = this.typingIndicator;
    el.classList.add('athena-typing-fadeout');
    el.addEventListener('animationend', () => el.remove(), { once: true });
    this.typingIndicator = null;
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. The typing indicator is triggered by the frontend when a
  message is sent and dismissed when the first SSE token is received. No
  backend endpoint is involved in displaying or hiding the dots.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
@keyframes athena-dot-wave {
  0%, 100% {
    transform: translateY(0);
  }
  50% {
    transform: translateY(-8px);
  }
}

.athena-typing-indicator {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 12px 16px;
  min-height: 40px;
}

.athena-dot {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 6px;
  height: 6px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background-color: var(--accent-gold);
  animation: athena-dot-wave 600ms ease-in-out infinite;
  will-change: transform;
}

.athena-typing-slow .athena-dot {
  animation-duration: 1200ms;
}

.athena-typing-fadeout {
  animation: athena-typing-fade 150ms ease forwards;
}

@keyframes athena-typing-fade {
  from { opacity: 1; }
  to   { opacity: 0; }
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-dot {
    animation: athena-dot-pulse 1200ms ease-in-out infinite;
  }
  @keyframes athena-dot-pulse {
    0%, 100% { opacity: 0.3; }
    50%      { opacity: 1.0; }
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints. The indicator is managed entirely by
frontend state transitions between message-sent and first-token-received events.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Typing Indicator Active (dots waving)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: Explain the mutation types in Vaswani graph     │
  │                                                         │
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐                │
  │  │                                     │                │
  │  │     ●       ○       ○               │                │
  │  │   [up]   [mid]   [down]             │                │
  │  │   gold    gold    gold              │                │
  │  │                                     │                │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘                │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Dot positions at t=0ms:    ●(up)  ○(rest)  ○(rest)
  Dot positions at t=150ms:  ○(down) ●(up)   ○(rest)
  Dot positions at t=300ms:  ○(rest) ○(down)  ●(up)
  Dot positions at t=450ms:  ○(rest) ○(rest)  ○(down)

STATE: Transition to Response (crossfade)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: Explain the mutation types...                   │
  │                                                         │
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐                │
  │  │  [opacity: 0.3]  ●  ○  ○           │ <- fading out  │
  │  │  [opacity: 0.2]  The graph co...    │ <- fading in   │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘                │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Slow Mode (after 8 seconds)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐                │
  │  │     ●       ○       ○               │                │
  │  │   [1200ms cycle - slower wave]      │                │
  │  └─────────────────────────────────────┘                │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Appear: Typing indicator fades in over 100ms on message send.
Wave: 600ms cycle per dot, 150ms stagger between dots, infinite loop.
Slow mode: Animation-duration changes from 600ms to 1200ms at 8s mark.
Disappear: 150ms fade-out on first token arrival.
Crossfade: 50ms overlap between indicator fade-out (150ms) and response fade-in (200ms).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User sends message via Enter key or submit button
  -> POST /api/athena/message dispatched
  -> athena-engine.js showTypingIndicator() called immediately
  -> Three gold dots inserted into DOM inside message container
  -> CSS animation begins on compositor thread (GPU-accelerated)
  -> setTimeout(8000) schedules slow-mode class addition

SSE stream begins, first token received
  -> athena-stream.js onFirstToken callback fires
  -> athena-engine.js hideTypingIndicator() called
  -> clearTimeout cancels slow-mode timer
  -> Indicator fades out (150ms CSS animation)
  -> animationend removes indicator from DOM
  -> Response container created and fades in (200ms)
  -> Streaming text begins populating response container

ERROR PATH: Request fails before any streaming begins
  -> Error handler calls hideTypingIndicator()
  -> Indicator removed, error message shown instead
  -> Dots never transition to slow mode because error clears them first

DATA STORED: No data stored. Typing indicator is ephemeral DOM state.
DATA CACHED: No data cached. Animation is CSS-only with no computed values.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

The typing indicator is a visual feedback mechanism that displays during the
interval between message submission and response streaming. It has no direct
interaction with the LLM. The indicator appears before any model call begins
and disappears when the first token arrives from the SSE stream. No prompts,
no model selection, no token budget considerations apply.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Message send         | Submit       | Typing indicator appears within 100ms    |
| First SSE token      | Stream start | Indicator crossfades to response, 150ms  |
| 8 second timeout     | Timer fires  | Wave speed slows from 600ms to 1200ms    |
| Request error        | Error event  | Indicator removed, error message shown   |
| Tab hidden           | Visibility   | Animation paused by browser (CSS default)|

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Dot wave: translateY(0) to translateY(-8px) and back, 600ms, ease-in-out,
infinite. Stagger: 0ms, 150ms, 300ms for dots 1, 2, 3. Slow mode: 1200ms
duration. Fade-out: opacity 1 to 0, 150ms, ease. Compositor: will-change:
transform on each dot element for GPU layer promotion.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Container has role="status" and aria-label="Athena is thinking".
Screen readers announce "Athena is thinking" once when indicator appears. The
dots are aria-hidden since they are decorative. prefers-reduced-motion replaces
wave with a pulsing opacity animation (no spatial movement).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User sends a second message while typing indicator is already showing
   Expected: Only one typing indicator at a time. The existing indicator remains.
   The new message is queued or the send button is disabled during processing.
   Implementation: showTypingIndicator() guards against duplicate creation with
   early return if this.typingIndicator already exists.

2. Scenario: Response arrives in under 50ms (cached response, fast local dev)
   Expected: Typing indicator appears briefly and crossfades out. The 100ms
   fade-in and 150ms fade-out may overlap. User sees a brief flicker of dots.
   Implementation: Minimum display time of 300ms enforced. hideTypingIndicator()
   delays removal if indicator has been visible for less than 300ms.

3. Scenario: Network disconnect during processing (SSE stream never starts)
   Expected: Typing indicator shows, transitions to slow mode at 8s, then at
   30s timeout the error handler removes it and shows a connection error.
   Implementation: Overall request timeout (30s) triggers error path cleanup.

4. Scenario: prefers-reduced-motion is active on the user's system
   Expected: Dots do not wave vertically. Instead, they pulse in opacity
   (0.3 to 1.0) at 1200ms cycle. No translateY transform applied.
   Implementation: CSS media query overrides keyframe to opacity-only pulse.

5. Scenario: Athena sends a multi-turn response with pauses between blocks
   Expected: Typing indicator reappears during pauses between response blocks
   only if the pause exceeds 2 seconds. Brief inter-block pauses do not trigger
   the indicator.
   Implementation: Debounce on typing indicator re-show with 2000ms threshold.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #007 (Visual Redesign)
ENABLES: #073 (Streaming Cursor)
ENHANCES: #037 (Streaming Text), #071 (Message Entrance Animation)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS Animations API, will-change compositor, role="status" ARIA

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Three gold dots appear within 100ms of message send
[ ] Dots wave in sequence with 150ms stagger between each
[ ] Indicator crossfades to response on first SSE token
[ ] Slow mode activates after 8 seconds (1200ms cycle)
[ ] Minimum 300ms display time prevents micro-flicker
[ ] Only one indicator instance exists at any time

VISUAL:
[ ] Dots are 6px circles filled with var(--accent-gold)
[ ] Wave height is 8px translateY with ease-in-out timing
[ ] Fade-out is 150ms, response fade-in is 200ms
[ ] No layout shift between indicator and response container

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Animation runs on compositor thread (GPU layer)
[ ] Zero main-thread JavaScript during dot wave cycle
[ ] No requestAnimationFrame loop; pure CSS animation

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] role="status" on indicator container
[ ] aria-label="Athena is thinking" announced once
[ ] prefers-reduced-motion replaces wave with opacity pulse

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Duplicate indicator prevented on rapid message send
[ ] Fast response (<50ms) shows minimum 300ms indicator
[ ] Network timeout (30s) removes indicator and shows error

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Chrome, Firefox, Safari dot rendering verified
[ ] PERFORMANCE TEST: No main-thread work during animation
[ ] REDUCED MOTION: Opacity pulse verified in all browsers
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-animations.css (dot-wave keyframe, reduced motion override),
   athena-engine.js (showTypingIndicator, hideTypingIndicator), athena-stream.js
   (first-token callback)
2. COMPARE all Done Criteria against actual implementation line by line
3. TEST send message, verify three gold dots appear within 100ms, measure wave
   cycle duration in DevTools Performance panel, confirm 600ms per cycle
4. TEST slow mode: delay response by 9+ seconds, confirm animation-duration
   changes to 1200ms, confirm visual difference is perceptible
5. VERIFY crossfade: capture frames during transition from dots to response text,
   confirm no flash of empty space, confirm 50ms overlap window
6. CHECK minimum display time: return cached response in <50ms, confirm dots
   show for at least 300ms before crossfading out
7. BROKEN: Assume will-change causes memory bloat on mobile Safari. Test with
   10+ rapid message sends. Assume animationend event is unreliable on Firefox.
8. LOG findings below, one or more issues per round

STATUS

FEATURE #073: STREAMING CURSOR
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 5
OVERLAPS WITH:   #072 (Typing Animation)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Streaming Cursor is a blinking vertical bar that appears at the exact
position where new text is being appended during Athena's streaming response.
As tokens arrive via SSE and are rendered into the response container, the
cursor sits immediately after the last character, blinking at a steady 530ms
interval to indicate that more content is forthcoming. When the stream ends,
the cursor fades out over 200ms and is removed from the DOM.

This is the text-editor cursor paradigm applied to streaming chat. Terminal
emulators, code editors, and word processors all use a blinking cursor to show
the insertion point. Users instinctively recognize this pattern: a blinking
cursor means "more is coming." A static end of text with no cursor means
"done." The streaming cursor brings this universal affordance to Athena's
response rendering.

The cursor is implemented as an inline span element with the class
athena-stream-cursor. It is 2px wide and 1.1em tall, colored with
var(--accent-gold) to match the dot matrix typing indicator's color palette.
The blink is a CSS keyframe called athena-cursor-blink that alternates the
cursor's opacity between 1 and 0 in discrete steps (not a smooth fade) to
match the behavior of real text cursors. The step-end timing function produces
the sharp on/off blink rather than a gradual pulse.

The cursor's position updates with every token append. When new text is inserted
into the response container via innerHTML or textContent manipulation, the
cursor span is removed, the text is appended, and the cursor span is re-inserted
after the new content. This cycle happens on every token (typically 30-50 times
per second during fast streaming), so the implementation must be allocation-free:
the same cursor span element is moved rather than destroyed and recreated.

For multi-block responses where Athena's output contains section headers, code
blocks, stat dashboards, or other structured elements, the cursor appears at
the end of whichever block is currently being populated. When a block is
completed and a new block begins, the cursor moves to the start of the new
block. The cursor always represents the frontier of content generation.

The cursor interacts with the auto-scroll mechanism. As the cursor moves
downward with accumulating text, the chat panel scrolls to keep the cursor
visible. The scroll tracking uses requestAnimationFrame to synchronize cursor
position checks with the rendering pipeline, preventing scroll jank from
competing with token insertion on the main thread.

The cursor blink pauses during rapid token insertion. When tokens arrive faster
than 50ms apart, the cursor remains solid (opacity 1) rather than blinking.
This prevents the distracting flicker of a cursor trying to blink while also
moving rapidly. The blink resumes when there is a gap of 200ms or more between
tokens, indicating a natural pause in the stream.

When the stream completes (SSE event: done or stream closes), the cursor plays
a fade-out animation: opacity transitions from 1 to 0 over 200ms with ease
timing. After the fade, the cursor span is removed from the DOM. This signals
to the user that the response is complete. No abrupt disappearance.

The feature respects prefers-reduced-motion by replacing the blink with a
solid cursor (no opacity animation) that simply appears during streaming and
fades out on completion. The presence of the cursor still communicates "in
progress" even without the blink.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena begins streaming a response via SSE and the first token is rendered in
the response container.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User sends "Show me the pruning impact of removing Vaswani 2017." The typing
   indicator (three gold dots) appears.
2. The first SSE token arrives: "Removing". The typing indicator crossfades out.
   The response container fades in with the text "Removing" and a blinking gold
   cursor positioned immediately after the "g".
3. Tokens arrive rapidly (~40ms apart): "Vaswani", "2017", "from", "the",
   "lineage". The cursor remains solid (no blink) and moves rightward with each
   token, always positioned after the last character.
4. A brief pause occurs (300ms gap between tokens). The cursor resumes blinking
   at its current position (530ms on/off cycle).
5. Tokens resume. The cursor goes solid again and continues advancing. Text
   wraps to the next line. The cursor follows to the new line. The chat panel
   scrolls to keep the cursor in view.
6. The stream ends. The cursor fades out over 200ms. The response is complete.
   No cursor visible. The user understands the response is finished.

AFTER COMPLETION:
No cursor element in the DOM. Response container contains only the completed
text content. Auto-scroll stops. The response message is in its final static
state.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-animations.css: @keyframes athena-cursor-blink, cursor
  sizing, color, fade-out animation, reduced-motion override.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: StreamCursor class managing cursor lifecycle,
  position tracking, blink-pause logic, fade-out on stream end.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Integration point passing container references
  to StreamCursor on stream start.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Cursor inline positioning within text flow.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-stream.js
  class StreamCursor {
    constructor() {
      this.el = document.createElement('span');
      this.el.className = 'athena-stream-cursor';
      this.el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
      this.lastTokenTime = 0;
      this.blinkPaused = false;
      this.container = null;
    }

    attachTo(container) {
      this.container = container;
      container.appendChild(this.el);
      this.resume();
    }

    onToken(text) {
      const now = performance.now();
      const gap = now - this.lastTokenTime;
      this.lastTokenTime = now;
      // Pause blink during rapid tokens (<50ms gap)
      if (gap < 50 && !this.blinkPaused) {
        this.el.classList.add('athena-cursor-solid');
        this.blinkPaused = true;
      }
      // Resume blink after pause (>200ms gap)
      if (gap > 200 && this.blinkPaused) {
        this.el.classList.remove('athena-cursor-solid');
        this.blinkPaused = false;
      }
      // Move cursor: remove, insert text, re-append
      if (this.el.parentNode) this.el.remove();
      // Text is appended by the stream renderer
      this.container.appendChild(this.el);
    }

    fadeOut() {
      this.el.classList.add('athena-cursor-fadeout');
      this.el.addEventListener('animationend', () => {
        this.el.remove();
      }, { once: true });
    }

    resume() {
      this.el.classList.remove('athena-cursor-solid', 'athena-cursor-fadeout');
      this.blinkPaused = false;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. The streaming cursor is a frontend visual element positioned
  by the SSE token renderer. No backend changes required. The cursor reacts to
  existing SSE events from the /api/graph/stream and /api/athena/message
  streaming endpoints.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
@keyframes athena-cursor-blink {
  0%  { opacity: 1; }
  50% { opacity: 0; }
  100% { opacity: 1; }
}

.athena-stream-cursor {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 2px;
  height: 1.1em;
  background-color: var(--accent-gold);
  vertical-align: text-bottom;
  animation: athena-cursor-blink 1060ms step-end infinite;
  will-change: opacity;
  margin-left: 1px;
}

.athena-cursor-solid {
  animation: none;
  opacity: 1;
}

.athena-cursor-fadeout {
  animation: athena-cursor-fade 200ms ease forwards;
}

@keyframes athena-cursor-fade {
  from { opacity: 1; }
  to   { opacity: 0; }
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-stream-cursor {
    animation: none;
    opacity: 1;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints. The cursor is driven by existing SSE token
events. The stream renderer calls StreamCursor.onToken() for each received
token and StreamCursor.fadeOut() on stream completion.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Cursor Blinking (waiting for next token, gap > 200ms)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena: Removing Vaswani 2017 from the lineage      │
  │    would collapse 31% of dependent papers. The          │
  │    primary impact cascades through|                     │
  │                                  ^                      │
  │                            [2px gold bar, blinking]     │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Cursor Solid (rapid tokens arriving, gap < 50ms)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena: Removing Vaswani 2017 from the lineage      │
  │    would collapse 31% of dependent papers. The          │
  │    primary impact cascades through three genera|        │
  │                                               ^        │
  │                          [2px gold bar, solid, moving]  │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Cursor Fade-Out (stream complete)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena: Removing Vaswani 2017 from the lineage      │
  │    would collapse 31% of dependent papers. The          │
  │    primary impact cascades through three generations    │
  │    of attention mechanism research.|                    │
  │                                   ^                     │
  │                       [fading: opacity 1 -> 0, 200ms]   │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Blink cycle: 1060ms total (530ms visible, 530ms hidden), step-end timing.
Solid mode: Blink paused when tokens arrive <50ms apart.
Resume blink: When gap exceeds 200ms between tokens.
Fade-out: 200ms ease from opacity 1 to 0 on stream end.
Position update: Cursor moves inline with each token append (no animation on position).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SSE stream begins, first token received
  -> StreamCursor.attachTo(responseContainer) places cursor in DOM
  -> Cursor begins blinking via CSS animation

Each SSE token received
  -> Stream renderer appends text to response container
  -> StreamCursor.onToken() called with timestamp
  -> Gap calculated: performance.now() - lastTokenTime
  -> If gap < 50ms: cursor set to solid (no blink)
  -> If gap > 200ms: cursor blink resumes
  -> Cursor span repositioned after latest text node
  -> requestAnimationFrame syncs scroll to keep cursor visible

SSE stream ends (done event or connection close)
  -> StreamCursor.fadeOut() adds athena-cursor-fadeout class
  -> CSS animation: opacity 1 to 0 over 200ms
  -> animationend removes cursor span from DOM
  -> Stream renderer finalizes response content

ERROR PATH: SSE stream errors or disconnects unexpectedly
  -> Error handler calls StreamCursor.fadeOut() to clean up cursor
  -> Error message appended to response container
  -> Cursor never left orphaned in DOM

DATA STORED: No data stored. Cursor state is ephemeral DOM.
DATA CACHED: lastTokenTime in StreamCursor instance (garbage collected on stream end).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

The streaming cursor is a visual indicator of SSE token arrival position. It
does not interact with the LLM directly. It consumes the output of the existing
streaming pipeline without modifying prompts, model selection, or token budgets.
The cursor is agnostic to which model produced the tokens.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| First SSE token      | Stream start | Gold cursor appears after first character |
| Subsequent tokens    | Token append | Cursor repositioned after latest text    |
| Token gap < 50ms     | Rapid stream | Cursor solid (blink paused)              |
| Token gap > 200ms    | Stream pause | Cursor blink resumes (530ms cycle)       |
| Stream end event     | Done         | Cursor fades out over 200ms              |
| Stream error         | Error        | Cursor fades out, error message shown    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Blink: step-end at 1060ms cycle (530ms on, 530ms off). Solid override: animation
set to none, opacity forced to 1. Fade-out: 200ms ease transition on opacity.
Cursor size: 2px wide, 1.1em tall, var(--accent-gold) background. Position:
inline-block after last text node in response container.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Cursor span is aria-hidden="true" since it is a decorative visual
indicator. Screen readers are unaffected. prefers-reduced-motion replaces blink
with a solid cursor (no opacity animation) that still communicates "in progress"
by its presence.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Response contains a code block being streamed line by line
   Expected: Cursor appears inside the code block container after the last
   streamed line. When the code block ends and prose resumes, cursor moves
   outside the code block to the new prose container.
   Implementation: Cursor positioning follows the active text insertion node,
   which the stream renderer tracks separately per block type.

2. Scenario: Stream pauses for 5+ seconds mid-response (Groq rate limit)
   Expected: Cursor continues blinking at its last position. No timeout removes
   it. The cursor patiently waits for the next token indefinitely.
   Implementation: No auto-removal timer. Only fadeOut() on explicit stream end.

3. Scenario: User scrolls up while streaming is active
   Expected: Cursor continues moving with text but is off-screen. Auto-scroll
   disabled because user manually scrolled. Cursor is invisible but functional.
   Implementation: Scroll sync only runs when isScrolledToBottom() is true.

4. Scenario: Very long token (single token containing 500+ characters)
   Expected: Cursor repositions after the entire token is rendered. No partial
   positioning within a single token.
   Implementation: onToken() called once per SSE data event, not per character.

5. Scenario: Two concurrent streams (edge case from retry logic)
   Expected: Only one cursor exists. If a new stream starts before the old one
   ends, the old cursor is removed and a new one is created.
   Implementation: attachTo() removes any existing cursor before creating new.

6. Scenario: Response is a single-block empty completion ("[done]" with no text)
   Expected: Cursor appears briefly and fades out immediately when done event
   arrives. Minimum visibility of 100ms before fade.
   Implementation: fadeOut() enforces minimum 100ms display via setTimeout guard.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #007 (Visual Redesign)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #072 (Typing Animation), #075 (Response Reveal)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS Animations API, performance.now(), requestAnimationFrame

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Gold cursor appears at stream start after first token
[ ] Cursor repositions after each token append
[ ] Blink pauses during rapid tokens (gap < 50ms)
[ ] Blink resumes during token gaps (gap > 200ms)
[ ] Cursor fades out over 200ms on stream completion
[ ] Cursor removed from DOM after fade-out completes

VISUAL:
[ ] Cursor is 2px wide, 1.1em tall, var(--accent-gold) color
[ ] Blink cycle is 1060ms with step-end timing (sharp on/off)
[ ] Fade-out uses ease timing (not step) for smooth disappearance
[ ] Cursor aligns with text baseline (vertical-align: text-bottom)

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Cursor repositioning does not cause layout thrashing
[ ] No new DOM elements created per token (cursor span reused)
[ ] requestAnimationFrame used for scroll sync, not setInterval

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] Cursor span has aria-hidden="true"
[ ] prefers-reduced-motion shows solid cursor (no blink)
[ ] Screen reader output unaffected by cursor presence

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Code block streaming: cursor inside code container
[ ] Long pause: cursor blinks indefinitely without timeout
[ ] Concurrent streams: old cursor removed before new one

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Chrome, Firefox, Safari cursor rendering
[ ] PERFORMANCE TEST: 1000-token stream without frame drops
[ ] REDUCED MOTION: Solid cursor verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-animations.css (cursor-blink keyframe, fade-out keyframe),
   athena-stream.js (StreamCursor class), athena-engine.js (integration points)
2. COMPARE Done Criteria against actual implementation, verify every checkbox
3. TEST stream a response, verify cursor appears at correct position after each
   token, measure blink cycle duration in DevTools (should be 1060ms)
4. TEST rapid streaming: send a response that generates 50 tokens/sec, verify
   cursor stays solid, verify no visual flicker from blink-pause transitions
5. VERIFY fade-out: trigger stream end, measure opacity transition duration
   (should be 200ms ease), confirm cursor span removed from DOM after fade
6. CHECK code block cursor: stream a response with a code block, verify cursor
   tracks inside the code container, then moves outside when prose resumes
7. BROKEN: Assume step-end timing is not supported in older Safari. Test with
   fallback. Assume performance.now() precision varies across browsers.
8. LOG findings below, one or more issues per round

FEATURE #074: CARD FLIP ANIMATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #011 (Artifact Cards), #075 (Response Reveal)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #011 (Artifact Cards), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Card Flip Animation adds a 3D rotation transition to Athena's artifact cards
that reveals additional content on the back face. Artifact cards in Athena
present structured information such as paper summaries, stat dashboards, and
comparison tables as discrete visual units with a front face that shows the
primary content. The card flip extends this pattern by giving each card a back
face containing supplementary information that is revealed when the user clicks
a flip trigger or hovers with intent.

The flip uses CSS 3D transforms: rotateY(180deg) applied to an inner container
that has backface-visibility: hidden on both the front and back face elements.
The outer container has perspective set to 800px to create a natural depth
effect. The rotation transition duration is 500ms with a cubic-bezier easing
that slightly overshoots at the end (cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.275)),
giving the flip a subtle bounce that makes the interaction feel physical rather
than mechanical.

The front face of a card shows the primary content: a paper's title, year,
citation count, and mutation type badge. The back face shows extended details
that would clutter the front view: the full abstract excerpt, the paper's field
of study tags, a mini citation graph showing direct ancestors and descendants,
and a "Discuss in Athena" button that initiates a focused conversation about
that specific paper.

The dual-face design is inspired by physical index cards that researchers use
to track papers. The front has the quick-reference information. The back has
the deep context. This metaphor maps naturally to the ancestry graph analysis
workflow where a user first scans many papers quickly (front faces) and then
dives deeper into specific ones (back faces).

The flip trigger is a small icon button in the card's top-right corner showing
two arrows forming a circular rotation. Clicking this button initiates the flip.
The button itself rotates 180 degrees during the card flip to maintain visual
continuity. On the back face, the same icon in the same position flips the card
back to the front.

The 3D transform requires careful handling of the card's height. The front face
and back face may have different content heights. The card container must
accommodate the taller of the two faces without layout shift. This is achieved
by measuring both faces on card creation and setting the container height to
max(frontHeight, backHeight). The shorter face is vertically centered within
the container.

When a card is in its flipped state and Athena scrolls the card out of the
viewport, the card resets to its front face. This prevents a disorienting
experience where the user scrolls back and finds cards in unexpected states.
The reset happens without animation (instant rotateY(0)) since the card is
off-screen.

For prefers-reduced-motion, the 3D flip is replaced with a crossfade. The
front face fades out (opacity 1 to 0 over 200ms) and the back face fades in
(opacity 0 to 1 over 200ms) with no rotateY transform. This preserves the
content reveal without 3D spatial movement.

Cards that are in the middle of streaming content cannot be flipped. The flip
button is disabled (opacity 0.3, cursor: not-allowed) until the card's content
is fully loaded. This prevents the disorienting scenario of flipping a
half-populated card.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks the flip icon on an artifact card, or presses Enter while the
flip icon is keyboard-focused.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena presents a paper summary card for "Attention Is All You Need" with
   the front face showing: title, year (2017), citation count (90,000+),
   mutation type (adoption), and a flip icon in the top-right corner.
2. User clicks the flip icon. The card begins rotating around its vertical
   axis. The front face rotates away from the viewer while the back face
   rotates into view. The rotation takes 500ms with a slight overshoot bounce.
3. The back face is now visible, showing: full abstract excerpt (first 200
   characters), field tags (Computer Science, Machine Learning), mini ancestor
   graph (3 key ancestors as dots with lines), and a "Discuss" button.
4. User reads the abstract and clicks "Discuss." Athena opens a focused thread
   about this specific paper. The card remains flipped during the discussion.
5. User clicks the flip icon on the back face. The card rotates back to the
   front face in 500ms. The original summary view is restored.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Card returns to front face. No persistent state change. The flip is purely
a view toggle. Card content remains unchanged.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-cards.css: 3D flip container, front/back face positioning,
  perspective, backface-visibility, flip button, reduced-motion crossfade.
- static/js/athena-cards.js: CardFlipManager class. Flip trigger handler, height
  normalization, off-screen reset via IntersectionObserver.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Card creation passes references to CardFlipManager.
- static/css/athena-animations.css: Flip transition timing function.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-cards.js
  class CardFlipManager {
    constructor() {
      this.flippedCards = new Set();
      this.observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
        entries.forEach(entry => {
          if (!entry.isIntersecting && this.flippedCards.has(entry.target)) {
            this.resetCard(entry.target);
          }
        });
      }, { threshold: 0 });
    }

    setupCard(cardEl) {
      const front = cardEl.querySelector('.athena-card-front');
      const back = cardEl.querySelector('.athena-card-back');
      const maxH = Math.max(front.offsetHeight, back.offsetHeight);
      cardEl.style.height = `${maxH}px`;
      this.observer.observe(cardEl);
      cardEl.querySelector('.athena-flip-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
        this.flipCard(cardEl);
      });
    }

    flipCard(cardEl) {
      const inner = cardEl.querySelector('.athena-card-inner');
      const isFlipped = this.flippedCards.has(cardEl);
      if (isFlipped) {
        inner.style.transform = 'rotateY(0deg)';
        this.flippedCards.delete(cardEl);
      } else {
        inner.style.transform = 'rotateY(180deg)';
        this.flippedCards.add(cardEl);
      }
    }

    resetCard(cardEl) {
      const inner = cardEl.querySelector('.athena-card-inner');
      inner.style.transition = 'none';
      inner.style.transform = 'rotateY(0deg)';
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        inner.style.transition = '';
      });
      this.flippedCards.delete(cardEl);
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Card flip is a pure frontend 3D CSS transform. The back face
  content is rendered at card creation time alongside the front face. No
  additional backend requests are made during the flip interaction.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-card-container {
  perspective: 800px;
  position: relative;
}

.athena-card-inner {
  position: relative;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  transition: transform 500ms cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.275);
  transform-style: preserve-3d;
}

.athena-card-front,
.athena-card-back {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  backface-visibility: hidden;
  border-radius: 8px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  padding: 16px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

.athena-card-back {
  transform: rotateY(180deg);
}

.athena-flip-btn {
  position: absolute;
  top: 8px;
  right: 8px;
  width: 28px;
  height: 28px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  border: 1px solid var(--text-muted);
  background: var(--bg-elevated);
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: transform 500ms cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.275);
  z-index: 2;
}

.athena-flip-btn:disabled {
  opacity: 0.3;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-card-inner {
    transition: none;
  }
  .athena-card-front,
  .athena-card-back {
    backface-visibility: visible;
    transition: opacity 200ms ease;
  }
  .athena-card-inner[style*="180deg"] .athena-card-front {
    opacity: 0;
    pointer-events: none;
  }
  .athena-card-inner:not([style*="180deg"]) .athena-card-back {
    opacity: 0;
    pointer-events: none;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API calls during flip. Back face content is pre-rendered
at card creation time from data already available in the frontend graph state.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Card Front Face (default)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Attention Is All You Need              [flip icon]     │
  │  Vaswani et al., 2017                                   │
  │  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────     │
  │  Citations: 90,247    Mutation: adoption                 │
  │  Confidence: HIGH     Tier: ●●●○                        │
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Front face visible                                   │
  │  ○ Back face hidden (rotateY 180deg, backface hidden)   │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Card Mid-Flip (t=250ms, rotateY=90deg)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                      │                                  │
  │                      │  <- card edge visible            │
  │                      │     (both faces hidden at 90deg) │
  │                      │                                  │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Card Back Face (flipped, rotateY=180deg)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  Attention Is All You Need              [flip icon]     │
  │  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────     │
  │  Abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models    │
  │  are based on complex recurrent or convolutional...     │
  │                                                         │
  │  Fields: [CS] [ML] [NLP]                                │
  │  Ancestors: ○--○--●  (mini graph)                       │
  │                                                         │
  │  [Discuss in Athena]                                    │
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Back face visible                                    │
  │  ○ Front face hidden                                    │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Flip forward: rotateY(0) to rotateY(180deg), 500ms, cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.275).
Flip back: rotateY(180deg) to rotateY(0), 500ms, same easing.
Flip button: rotates 180deg in sync with card.
Off-screen reset: instant rotateY(0) with transition: none.
Reduced motion: crossfade (opacity) over 200ms, no 3D rotation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Card created by Athena response renderer
  -> CardFlipManager.setupCard() measures front/back heights
  -> Container height set to max(front, back)
  -> IntersectionObserver begins tracking card visibility
  -> Flip button click listener attached

User clicks flip button
  -> CardFlipManager.flipCard() toggles rotateY on inner container
  -> CSS transition handles 500ms 3D rotation
  -> flippedCards Set tracks current state

Card scrolls off-screen while flipped
  -> IntersectionObserver callback fires (isIntersecting: false)
  -> CardFlipManager.resetCard() sets rotateY(0) with transition: none
  -> Card resets to front face instantly (invisible to user)

ERROR PATH: 3D transforms not supported (very old browser)
  -> CSS fallback: both faces stacked, flip button toggles display:none/block
  -> No animation, just content swap

DATA STORED: No data persisted. Flip state is ephemeral (CSS transform on DOM).
DATA CACHED: flippedCards Set in CardFlipManager (garbage collected on destroy).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

Card flip animation is a CSS 3D transform with no LLM involvement. The content
on both faces of the card is pre-rendered from graph data already available in
the frontend state. The "Discuss in Athena" button on the back face initiates
a new chat message, but that interaction belongs to the discuss feature (#004),
not to this animation feature.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Flip button (front)  | Click        | Card rotates to back face, 500ms         |
| Flip button (back)   | Click        | Card rotates to front face, 500ms        |
| Flip button          | Enter key    | Same as click (keyboard accessible)      |
| Card scrolled out    | IntersectObs | Reset to front face instantly             |
| Streaming card       | N/A          | Flip button disabled until load complete  |
| Discuss button       | Click        | Opens focused chat thread (delegates)    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Flip: rotateY 0 to 180deg (or reverse), 500ms, cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885,
0.32, 1.275) with slight overshoot bounce. Perspective: 800px on container.
Transform-style: preserve-3d on inner element. Backface-visibility: hidden
on both faces. Button spin: matches card rotation duration and easing.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Flip button has aria-label="Flip card to see details" (front)
and "Flip card to see summary" (back). Both faces have appropriate heading
levels. Back face content is in DOM but hidden via backface-visibility (not
display:none), so it remains accessible to screen readers. prefers-reduced-motion
uses crossfade instead of 3D rotation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Back face content is much taller than front face
   Expected: Container height accommodates the taller back face. Front face is
   vertically centered. No layout shift on flip.
   Implementation: max(front.offsetHeight, back.offsetHeight) measured on setup.

2. Scenario: User rapidly double-clicks the flip button
   Expected: Card flips to back, then immediately begins flipping back to front.
   The second click reverses the animation mid-rotation.
   Implementation: CSS transition handles interruption naturally; setting a new
   transform mid-transition creates a smooth reversal from the current angle.

3. Scenario: Card is flipped and new content is streamed to it (live update)
   Expected: Card resets to front face before content update to avoid stale
   back face data. Then re-renders both faces.
   Implementation: Content update triggers resetCard() then re-renders.

4. Scenario: Multiple cards visible, user flips three of them
   Expected: All three can be independently flipped. No global "only one flipped
   at a time" constraint.
   Implementation: flippedCards Set tracks multiple cards independently.

5. Scenario: Browser does not support preserve-3d or perspective CSS
   Expected: Graceful fallback to display toggle. Front hides, back shows.
   Implementation: Feature detection via CSS.supports('transform-style', 'preserve-3d').

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #011 (Artifact Cards), #002 (Rich Response Formatting)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #004 (Discuss Integration), #075 (Response Reveal)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS 3D Transforms, IntersectionObserver API, transform-style: preserve-3d

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Click flip button rotates card from front to back
[ ] Click flip button on back rotates card to front
[ ] Off-screen cards reset to front face automatically
[ ] Flip button disabled during card streaming
[ ] Multiple cards can be flipped independently

VISUAL:
[ ] 500ms rotation with cubic-bezier overshoot bounce
[ ] Perspective at 800px creates natural depth
[ ] Backface-visibility prevents see-through during rotation
[ ] Height normalized to max(front, back) with no layout shift

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] CSS transition on transform only (compositor layer)
[ ] No JavaScript animation loop during flip
[ ] IntersectionObserver efficiently tracks off-screen reset

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] Flip button has descriptive aria-label per face
[ ] Back face content accessible to screen readers
[ ] prefers-reduced-motion uses crossfade, no 3D rotation

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Rapid double-click produces smooth reversal
[ ] 3D not supported: fallback to display toggle
[ ] Back face taller than front: container accommodates

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Chrome, Firefox, Safari 3D transform verified
[ ] PERFORMANCE TEST: 10 simultaneous flips at 60fps
[ ] REDUCED MOTION: Crossfade verified in all browsers
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-cards.css (3D container, perspective, backface-visibility),
   athena-cards.js (CardFlipManager, IntersectionObserver), athena-animations.css
2. COMPARE Done Criteria checkboxes against actual implementation
3. TEST flip a card, verify 500ms duration, verify cubic-bezier overshoot bounce
   in DevTools transition panel, verify perspective depth is visible
4. TEST off-screen reset: flip a card, scroll it out of view, scroll back,
   verify it is on front face with no animation
5. VERIFY height normalization: create card with long abstract (back taller than
   front), flip, confirm no layout shift during or after rotation
6. CHECK reduced motion: toggle OS setting, flip card, confirm crossfade with
   no 3D rotation, confirm both faces accessible
7. BROKEN: Assume preserve-3d fails on older mobile browsers. Test CSS.supports
   fallback. Assume backface-visibility flickers on Safari.
8. LOG findings below, one or more issues per round
