FEATURE #075: RESPONSE REVEAL
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #071 (Message Entrance Animation), #074 (Card Flip)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text), #071 (Message Entrance)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Response Reveal is a progressive disclosure animation that staggers the
appearance of individual content blocks within a multi-block Athena response.
When Athena returns a complex answer containing a text paragraph, a stat
dashboard, a code block, and a suggestion list, Response Reveal ensures these
blocks do not all snap into view simultaneously. Instead, each block fades in
and slides up in sequence with a short delay between each, creating a top-to-
bottom cascade that guides the user's eye through the response content in
reading order.

Without Response Reveal, a multi-block response appears as a wall of content
that the user must parse all at once. With it, each block announces itself
individually, giving the user a fraction of a second to register its presence
before the next block appears. This is particularly important for responses
containing heterogeneous content types: a prose paragraph followed by a
mutation type table followed by a pruning impact stat dashboard. The staggered
reveal helps the user distinguish these as separate informational units rather
than a single continuous stream.

The reveal animation per block uses the same keyframe as the message entrance
animation (#071) but with a shorter duration of 200ms instead of 280ms. Each
block enters with translateY(8px) and opacity 0, transitioning to translateY(0)
and opacity 1. The stagger delay between consecutive blocks is 80ms, so the
first block begins at 0ms, the second at 80ms, the third at 160ms, and so on.
The maximum total stagger is capped at 640ms (8 blocks), after which all
remaining blocks share the 640ms delay and appear simultaneously.

Response Reveal only activates after the complete response has finished
streaming. During streaming, text appears token by token with the streaming
cursor (#073). Once the stream is complete and the response is finalized,
the response container is temporarily hidden, the stream content is parsed
into distinct block elements (paragraphs, code blocks, stat dashboards, card
containers, lists), and then the reveal animation plays the parsed blocks in
sequence. This two-phase approach prevents the reveal animation from
conflicting with the streaming animation.

The transition from streaming to reveal is seamless. The streaming cursor
fades out (#073). The response container is briefly invisible (opacity 0) for
one frame while blocks are separated. Then the reveal cascade begins. The
total time from stream end to first block visible is under 50ms, so the user
perceives a smooth continuation rather than a gap.

For short responses consisting of a single text block, Response Reveal
degrades gracefully: a single block with 0ms delay is identical to a simple
fade-in, so no special-casing is needed.

The reveal respects prefers-reduced-motion by replacing the translateY slide
with a simple opacity fade. Each block still fades in with the 80ms stagger
delay, but without vertical movement. This preserves the progressive
disclosure concept while eliminating spatial motion.

Blocks that contain internal animations (such as a stat dashboard with
counting number animations or a chart with draw-in effects) coordinate with
the reveal: the internal animation begins only after the block's reveal
animation completes. This prevents competing animations from creating visual
noise. The coordination uses the animationend event on the reveal keyframe
to trigger the block's internal animation start.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena finishes streaming a multi-block response and the stream end event
fires.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks "Give me a complete analysis of the Vaswani graph." Athena streams
   a long response containing a text paragraph, a mutation type breakdown table,
   a pruning impact stat dashboard, and three suggestion buttons.
2. The streaming cursor blinks as tokens arrive and text populates the response
   container. The user reads along as content streams.
3. The stream ends. The streaming cursor fades out (200ms). The response
   container goes to opacity 0 for one frame while blocks are parsed.
4. Block 1 (text paragraph) fades in and slides up over 200ms. The user sees
   the summary paragraph first.
5. At 80ms after block 1 started, block 2 (mutation table) begins its entrance.
   At 160ms, block 3 (stat dashboard) begins. At 240ms, block 4 (suggestions)
   begins.
6. Each block is fully visible within 200ms of its start. The entire cascade
   completes in 440ms (240ms stagger + 200ms final animation).
7. After block 3 (stat dashboard) finishes its reveal, its internal counter
   animation begins, numbers counting up to their final values.

AFTER COMPLETION:
All blocks are fully visible and static. No active animations. No transform
residuals. Content is identical to what was streamed, just presented with
a structured entrance.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-animations.css: @keyframes athena-block-reveal, stagger
  delays, reduced-motion override.
- static/js/athena-reveal.js: ResponseReveal class. Block parser, stagger
  orchestrator, internal animation coordinator.
- static/js/athena-stream.js: Calls ResponseReveal.trigger() on stream end.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Registers block types and their internal
  animation callbacks with ResponseReveal.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-reveal.js
  class ResponseReveal {
    constructor() {
      this.maxStagger = 640; // ms
      this.blockDelay = 80;  // ms between blocks
      this.animDuration = 200; // ms per block
    }

    trigger(responseContainer) {
      const blocks = this.parseBlocks(responseContainer);
      if (blocks.length === 0) return;

      // Hide container for one frame while applying reveal classes
      responseContainer.style.opacity = '0';
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        blocks.forEach((block, i) => {
          const delay = Math.min(i * this.blockDelay, this.maxStagger);
          block.classList.add('athena-block-hidden');
          block.style.animationDelay = `${delay}ms`;
          block.addEventListener('animationend', () => {
            block.classList.remove('athena-block-hidden', 'athena-block-revealing');
            block.style.animationDelay = '';
            this.triggerInternalAnimation(block);
          }, { once: true });
        });

        responseContainer.style.opacity = '1';
        requestAnimationFrame(() => {
          blocks.forEach(block => {
            block.classList.remove('athena-block-hidden');
            block.classList.add('athena-block-revealing');
          });
        });
      });
    }

    parseBlocks(container) {
      return Array.from(container.querySelectorAll(
        '.athena-text-block, .athena-stat-block, .athena-code-block, ' +
        '.athena-card-container, .athena-list-block, .athena-suggestion-row'
      ));
    }

    triggerInternalAnimation(block) {
      const event = new CustomEvent('athena-reveal-complete');
      block.dispatchEvent(event);
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Response Reveal is a frontend post-processing step that runs
  after the SSE stream completes. No backend changes. The backend streams tokens
  as before; the frontend decides how to present the finalized content.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
@keyframes athena-block-reveal {
  from {
    opacity: 0;
    transform: translateY(8px);
  }
  to {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: translateY(0);
  }
}

.athena-block-hidden {
  opacity: 0;
}

.athena-block-revealing {
  animation: athena-block-reveal 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1) both;
  will-change: transform, opacity;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  @keyframes athena-block-reveal {
    from { opacity: 0; }
    to   { opacity: 1; }
  }
  .athena-block-revealing {
    animation-duration: 150ms;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints involved. Reveal is triggered by the existing
SSE stream completion event in the frontend pipeline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Stream Just Ended (pre-reveal, opacity 0 for one frame)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● You: Give me a complete analysis of the graph        │
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena:                                              │
  │    [all blocks invisible, opacity: 0]                   │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Reveal Cascade In Progress (t=120ms)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ● Athena:                                              │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ The Vaswani graph contains 152 nodes...          │   │
  │  │ [opacity: 0.8, y: +1px] -- block 1 almost done  │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ Mutation Breakdown:                              │   │
  │  │ [opacity: 0.3, y: +5px] -- block 2 mid-reveal   │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ○ [block 3: stat dashboard, still hidden]              │
  │  ○ [block 4: suggestions, still hidden]                 │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Reveal Complete (all blocks visible)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  ● Athena:                                              │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ The Vaswani graph contains 152 nodes...          │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ Mutation Breakdown: adoption(45%), gen(22%)...   │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ Pruning Impact: 31% collapse   [counter anim]   │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │  [Explore ancestors] [Compare] [Export]                 │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

TRANSITIONS:
Per-block: 200ms, cubic-bezier(0.22, 0.61, 0.36, 1), translateY(8px to 0) + opacity.
Stagger: 80ms between consecutive blocks, capped at 640ms.
Internal animations: triggered on athena-reveal-complete CustomEvent after block reveal.
Reduced motion: opacity-only fade, 150ms, no translateY.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SSE stream completes (done event)
  -> Streaming cursor fades out (#073)
  -> athena-stream.js calls ResponseReveal.trigger(responseContainer)
  -> ResponseReveal.parseBlocks() identifies discrete content blocks
  -> Container set to opacity 0 for one frame
  -> Each block gets athena-block-hidden class + animationDelay
  -> Next frame: container opacity restored, blocks get athena-block-revealing
  -> CSS animations cascade: block 1 at 0ms, block 2 at 80ms, etc.
  -> Each block's animationend removes classes, triggers internal animation
  -> All blocks static and visible after final animation completes

ERROR PATH: Response has no parseable blocks (plain text with no structure)
  -> parseBlocks() returns empty array
  -> trigger() returns immediately, no reveal animation
  -> Content remains visible as-is from streaming

DATA STORED: No data persisted. Reveal state is ephemeral CSS animation.
DATA CACHED: No data cached. Block list parsed fresh on each trigger.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

Response Reveal operates on the rendered output of Athena's responses after
streaming is complete. It does not modify prompts, select models, or consume
tokens. The animation is purely a presentation layer concern that transforms
how already-generated content is displayed to the user.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Stream completion    | Done event   | Reveal cascade begins after cursor fade  |
| Block 1              | Reveal       | Fade-slide at 0ms delay                  |
| Block 2              | Reveal       | Fade-slide at 80ms delay                 |
| Block N              | Reveal       | Fade-slide at min(N*80, 640)ms delay     |
| Internal animation   | Reveal end   | Triggered on athena-reveal-complete event |
| Single-block response| Reveal       | Simple fade-in, no stagger needed        |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Per-block: translateY(8px) to 0, opacity 0 to 1, 200ms, cubic-bezier(0.22,
0.61, 0.36, 1). Stagger: 80ms increment, max 640ms. Internal animation
coordination: CustomEvent dispatch on animationend. Will-change: transform,
opacity applied during reveal, removed after.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: prefers-reduced-motion uses opacity-only animation at 150ms.
Blocks are in DOM and accessible to screen readers regardless of animation
state. aria-live on response container ensures screen readers announce content
when it becomes available.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Response contains 20+ blocks (very long detailed analysis)
   Expected: Blocks 1-8 stagger normally (0ms to 560ms). Blocks 9-20 all
   share the 640ms delay and appear simultaneously.
   Implementation: Math.min(i * 80, 640) caps stagger at 640ms.

2. Scenario: User scrolls during reveal cascade
   Expected: Reveal continues for all blocks regardless of scroll position.
   Off-screen blocks still animate (they will be visible when scrolled to).
   Implementation: No scroll-position gating on reveal. Animation runs to
   completion.

3. Scenario: Response is a single-character "." (minimal response)
   Expected: parseBlocks() returns one block. Reveal is a simple 200ms
   fade-in with no stagger.
   Implementation: Single block at delay 0ms. Graceful degradation.

4. Scenario: User sends a new message before reveal cascade completes
   Expected: Reveal completes on the current response. The new message
   triggers the typing indicator. No conflict.
   Implementation: ResponseReveal operates on a specific container reference.
   New messages do not interfere with ongoing reveals.

5. Scenario: Stream reconnection causes the same response to re-render
   Expected: Previous reveal state is cleaned up. New reveal cascade starts
   fresh on the re-rendered content.
   Implementation: trigger() removes all reveal classes before starting.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #037 (Streaming Text), #071 (Message Entrance)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #073 (Streaming Cursor), #074 (Card Flip Animation)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS Animations API, requestAnimationFrame, CustomEvent API

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Each block slides up 8px and fades in over 200ms
[ ] Stagger capped at 640ms (blocks 9+ appear simultaneously)
[ ] Single-block response degrades to simple fade-in

VISUAL:

[ ] No layout shift during or after reveal cascade
[ ] Stat dashboard counters start counting after their block is revealed

PERFORMANCE:

[ ] No layout thrashing (only transform + opacity animated)

ACCESSIBILITY:

[ ] Screen reader announces content via aria-live on response container
[ ] Tab order correct after reveal (blocks focusable in order)

EDGE CASES:

[ ] User scroll during reveal does not interrupt cascade
[ ] Stream reconnection cleans up previous reveal state

VERIFICATION:

FEATURE #076: CONNECTION ANIMATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           C
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #004 (Discuss Integration), #012 (AI Annotations)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #004 (Discuss Integration), #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Connection Animation draws animated lines between nodes in the Arivu ancestry
graph and corresponding messages or elements in the Athena chat panel, creating
a visual bridge between the two primary interface areas. When Athena discusses
a specific paper, the animation renders a curved line from the paper's node in
the D3 force-directed graph to the relevant message in the chat panel, showing
the user exactly which graph element the conversation is referencing.

The connection line is an SVG path element rendered in a dedicated SVG overlay
layer that sits above both the graph canvas and the chat panel. The SVG overlay
spans the full viewport and uses pointer-events: none so it does not intercept
mouse events intended for the graph or chat underneath. The path is drawn as
a cubic bezier curve that arcs gently upward before connecting to its target,
giving the line a natural "bridge" appearance rather than a rigid straight line.

The line animation uses SVG stroke-dasharray and stroke-dashoffset to produce
a "draw-in" effect. The path starts fully hidden (dashoffset equal to total
path length) and animates to dashoffset 0 over 400ms, creating the illusion
that the line is being drawn from the graph node toward the chat message. The
stroke color is var(--accent-gold) with 60% opacity, and the line has a 2px
stroke width that pulses gently (1.5px to 2.5px) while active to indicate
a live connection.

Connection animations are triggered when Athena mentions a specific paper in
its response. The stream parser identifies paper references by matching
Semantic Scholar corpus IDs or paper titles against the loaded graph data.
When a match is found, the connection animation draws a line from the matched
graph node to the chat message element containing the reference. If the graph
node is off-screen (the graph is zoomed/panned away from it), the graph
smoothly pans to center the referenced node before the connection line draws.

Multiple connections can be active simultaneously when Athena discusses several
papers in one response. Each connection line is independently animated with a
50ms stagger between lines. Connection lines for different papers use slightly
different vertical arc heights to prevent overlap, creating a fan pattern from
the chat panel to distributed graph nodes.

Connections are ephemeral by default. When the user scrolls the chat past the
referenced message, the connection line fades out over 300ms. When the user
clicks elsewhere in the graph, active connections fade. When a new Athena
response begins streaming, previous connections clear. This prevents visual
clutter from accumulating.

The user can pin a connection by clicking on the referenced paper name in the
chat message while the connection is active. A pinned connection persists until
explicitly dismissed (click the pin icon that appears on the connection) or
until the graph is rebuilt. Pinned connections use a dashed stroke pattern to
distinguish them from active connections.

For the responsive case where the graph and chat are in a vertical stacked
layout (mobile), connections draw vertically instead of horizontally. The line
arcs from the graph section down to the chat section with the same draw-in
animation but a vertical bezier curve.

Connection Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion by drawing the line
instantly (no stroke-dashoffset animation) and using a static 2px stroke
(no pulse). The line still appears and disappears but without the motion of
being drawn or breathing.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena mentions a specific paper in its response that corresponds to a node
in the currently loaded ancestry graph.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has the Attention Is All You Need graph loaded with 152 nodes. The
   graph shows paper nodes as circles in the left panel, Athena chat is in
   the right panel.
2. User asks "What is the relationship between Vaswani 2017 and Bahdanau 2014?"
3. Athena streams a response: "The Vaswani 2017 paper generalized the attention
   mechanism introduced by Bahdanau 2014..." The stream parser identifies both
   paper references.
4. A gold SVG line draws in from the Vaswani node in the graph toward the chat
   message (400ms draw-in animation). Simultaneously, the graph pans slightly
   to ensure the Vaswani node is visible.
5. 50ms later, a second gold line draws from the Bahdanau node to the same
   chat message. This line arcs higher to avoid overlapping the first.
6. Both lines pulse gently (stroke width 1.5px to 2.5px) while the response
   is active. The user sees two golden bridges linking the graph to the text.
7. User scrolls the chat to read more. As the referenced message scrolls
   off-screen, both connection lines fade out over 300ms.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Connection lines removed from SVG overlay. No visual residue. Graph and chat
are independent panels again with no connecting elements.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-connections.js: ConnectionAnimator class. SVG overlay
  management, bezier path calculation, draw-in animation, pulse loop, fade-out,
  pin logic, IntersectionObserver for off-screen cleanup.
- static/css/athena-animations.css: Connection line styles, pulse keyframe,
  reduced-motion overrides.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Reference parser identifies paper mentions in
  stream content and triggers ConnectionAnimator.
- static/js/graph.js: Provides node screen coordinates to ConnectionAnimator
  for endpoint calculation. Pan-to-node method.
- templates/tool.html: SVG overlay element in the tool layout DOM.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-connections.js
  class ConnectionAnimator {
    constructor(svgOverlay, graphApi) {
      this.svg = svgOverlay;
      this.graphApi = graphApi;
      this.activeConnections = new Map();
      this.pinnedConnections = new Set();
    }

    connect(paperId, chatMessageEl) {
      const nodePos = this.graphApi.getNodeScreenPosition(paperId);
      if (!nodePos) return;
      const msgRect = chatMessageEl.getBoundingClientRect();
      const path = this.createBezierPath(nodePos, msgRect);
      const pathEl = this.createSVGPath(path);
      this.animateDrawIn(pathEl, 400);
      this.startPulse(pathEl);
      this.activeConnections.set(paperId, { pathEl, chatMessageEl });
    }

    createBezierPath(from, toRect) {
      const startX = from.x;
      const startY = from.y;
      const endX = toRect.left;
      const endY = toRect.top + toRect.height / 2;
      const midX = (startX + endX) / 2;
      const arcHeight = -40 - (this.activeConnections.size * 15);
      const midY = Math.min(startY, endY) + arcHeight;
      return `M${startX},${startY} Q${midX},${midY} ${endX},${endY}`;
    }

    animateDrawIn(pathEl, duration) {
      const length = pathEl.getTotalLength();
      pathEl.style.strokeDasharray = length;
      pathEl.style.strokeDashoffset = length;
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        pathEl.style.transition = `stroke-dashoffset ${duration}ms ease`;
        pathEl.style.strokeDashoffset = '0';
      });
    }

    fadeOut(paperId, duration = 300) {
      const conn = this.activeConnections.get(paperId);
      if (!conn || this.pinnedConnections.has(paperId)) return;
      conn.pathEl.style.transition = `opacity ${duration}ms ease`;
      conn.pathEl.style.opacity = '0';
      setTimeout(() => {
        conn.pathEl.remove();
        this.activeConnections.delete(paperId);
      }, duration);
    }

    clearAll() {
      for (const [id] of this.activeConnections) {
        if (!this.pinnedConnections.has(id)) {
          this.fadeOut(id, 200);
        }
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Connection animation is a frontend visual effect linking
  graph nodes to chat messages. The paper reference identification uses
  client-side graph data. No backend endpoints involved.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-connection-overlay {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  width: 100vw;
  height: 100vh;
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: 50;
}

.athena-connection-line {
  stroke: var(--accent-gold);
  stroke-opacity: 0.6;
  stroke-width: 2px;
  fill: none;
  stroke-linecap: round;
}

@keyframes athena-connection-pulse {
  0%, 100% { stroke-width: 1.5px; }
  50%      { stroke-width: 2.5px; }
}

.athena-connection-line.active {
  animation: athena-connection-pulse 2000ms ease-in-out infinite;
}

.athena-connection-line.pinned {
  stroke-dasharray: 6 4;
  animation: none;
  stroke-width: 2px;
  stroke-opacity: 0.8;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-connection-line {
    animation: none;
    stroke-width: 2px;
  }
  .athena-connection-line.active {
    animation: none;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints. ConnectionAnimator reads node positions from
the graph.js API (getNodeScreenPosition) and chat message positions from the
DOM (getBoundingClientRect). All data is client-side.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Connection Drawing In (t=200ms, midpoint)

  ┌──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
  │       GRAPH PANEL        │        CHAT PANEL            │
  │                          │                              │
  │    ○ Bahdanau 2014       │  ● You: What is the          │
  │     \                    │    relationship...            │
  │      \  ............     │                              │
  │       ● Vaswani---/------│---> Athena: The Vaswani      │
  │         2017   /         │      paper generalized...    │
  │          [gold arc]      │                              │
  │                          │                              │
  └──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
   Gold bezier curve drawing in via stroke-dashoffset animation

STATE: Two Connections Active (pulsing)

  ┌──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
  │       GRAPH PANEL        │        CHAT PANEL            │
  │                          │                              │
  │    ● Bahdanau 2014       │  ● Athena: The Vaswani      │
  │     \                    │    2017 paper generalized    │
  │      \_____[high arc]____│____> the attention mech-     │
  │       ● Vaswani          │      anism introduced by     │
  │         2017             │      Bahdanau 2014...        │
  │          \___[low arc]___│____>                         │
  │                          │                              │
  └──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
   Both lines pulse 1.5px-2.5px, var(--accent-gold) at 60% opacity

STATE: Pinned Connection (dashed)

  ┌──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
  │                          │                              │
  │       ● Vaswani          │  ● Athena: Vaswani 2017     │
  │         2017             │    [pin icon]                │
  │          \- - - - - - - -│- - -> connection pinned      │
  │                          │                              │
  └──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘
   Dashed stroke, no pulse, 80% opacity, persists until dismissed

TRANSITIONS:
Draw-in: stroke-dashoffset from pathLength to 0, 400ms, ease.
Pulse: stroke-width 1.5px to 2.5px, 2000ms cycle, ease-in-out.
Fade-out: opacity 1 to 0, 300ms, ease.
Pan-to-node: graph smoothly pans to center referenced node, 500ms.
Reduced motion: no draw-in (instant), no pulse (static 2px).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena response stream references a paper (ID or title match)
  -> athena-engine.js reference parser identifies paper_id
  -> graphApi.getNodeScreenPosition(paper_id) returns {x, y}
  -> If node off-screen: graphApi.panToNode(paper_id) runs (500ms)
  -> ConnectionAnimator.connect(paper_id, chatMessageEl) called
  -> SVG bezier path calculated from node position to message rect
  -> Path element appended to SVG overlay
  -> stroke-dashoffset animation draws line in (400ms)
  -> Pulse animation begins on active class

User scrolls chat message off-screen
  -> IntersectionObserver detects chatMessageEl not intersecting
  -> ConnectionAnimator.fadeOut(paper_id) called
  -> Line fades to opacity 0 over 300ms, then removed from SVG

User clicks paper name in chat (pin action)
  -> ConnectionAnimator.pin(paper_id) adds to pinnedConnections Set
  -> Line style changes to dashed, pulse stops
  -> Line persists until user clicks dismiss icon

ERROR PATH: Graph node not found (paper not in current graph)
  -> getNodeScreenPosition returns null
  -> connect() returns immediately, no line drawn
  -> Chat reference is still functional, just not visually linked

DATA STORED: Pinned connections stored in frontend state only (not persisted).
DATA CACHED: Node positions cached per frame via getNodeScreenPosition.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

Connection animation is a visual mapping between graph nodes and chat content.
The paper reference identification uses string matching against the loaded
graph's paper_id and title fields. No LLM call is made to identify references.
The LLM generates the response text, but the connection animation layer parses
that text using deterministic pattern matching, not LLM inference.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Paper reference      | Stream parse | Connection line draws from node to chat  |
| Chat scroll          | Scroll event | Lines fade when reference scrolls away   |
| Graph pan/zoom       | Transform    | Lines update endpoints dynamically       |
| Paper name in chat   | Click        | Pin connection (dashed, persistent)      |
| Pin dismiss icon     | Click        | Remove pinned connection                 |
| New Athena response  | Stream start | Previous unpinned connections cleared    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Draw-in: SVG stroke-dashoffset animation, 400ms, ease. Pulse: stroke-width
oscillation 1.5px to 2.5px, 2000ms, ease-in-out, infinite. Fade-out: opacity
transition, 300ms, ease. Path shape: quadratic bezier curve with upward arc.
Multi-connection stagger: 50ms between consecutive lines. Arc height varies
by -40px base minus 15px per additional connection to prevent overlap.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: SVG overlay has aria-hidden="true" since connection lines are
decorative. Paper references in chat are the accessible equivalent (clickable
text). prefers-reduced-motion: no draw-in animation (instant), no pulse
(static), lines still appear and disappear.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Referenced paper node is off-screen in the graph
   Expected: Graph pans to center the referenced node (500ms), then connection
   line draws in after pan completes.
   Implementation: panToNode() returns a promise; connect() awaits it before
   drawing.

2. Scenario: Graph is zoomed in very far, node is at extreme coordinates
   Expected: Connection line bezier curve adapts to the large coordinate gap.
   Arc height scales proportionally. Line does not go off-viewport.
   Implementation: Bezier control points clamped to viewport bounds.

3. Scenario: User resizes the browser window while connections are active
   Expected: Connection endpoints update to new node and message positions.
   Lines smoothly reposition on resize.
   Implementation: ResizeObserver on SVG overlay triggers path recalculation.

4. Scenario: Chat panel is collapsed (mobile layout)
   Expected: Connection lines are hidden entirely. No attempt to draw to a
   hidden panel. Lines reappear if panel is expanded while reference is visible.
   Implementation: Check chat panel visibility before drawing.

5. Scenario: Five papers referenced in a single response
   Expected: Five connection lines with staggered draw-in (0, 50, 100, 150,
   200ms). Arc heights vary to prevent overlap: -40, -55, -70, -85, -100px.
   Implementation: arcHeight = -40 - (connectionIndex * 15).

6. Scenario: Paper referenced but not in the current graph (different lineage)
   Expected: No connection line drawn. Chat message still shows paper name as
   text. No error visible to user.
   Implementation: getNodeScreenPosition returns null; connect() returns early.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FEATURE #077: COLLAPSE ANIMATION
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Visual Features
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           H
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #075 (Response Reveal), #068 (Message Grouping)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Collapse Animation provides smooth expand and collapse transitions for
collapsible sections within Athena responses. Many Athena response types
contain sections that can be expanded or collapsed: detailed methodology
breakdowns, extended citation lists, full abstract excerpts, step-by-step
analysis walkthroughs, and grouped message clusters. Without animation, these
sections toggle between visible and hidden states with an instantaneous jump
that causes a jarring layout shift. With Collapse Animation, sections slide
open and closed with fluid height transitions that maintain the user's spatial
context.

The animation uses a height transition from 0 to the section's natural
scrollHeight (for expand) or from scrollHeight to 0 (for collapse). The
transition duration is dynamically calculated based on the section's content
height: 200ms base plus 1ms per pixel of content height, capped at 400ms
maximum. This produces fast transitions for short sections and slightly slower
transitions for tall sections, matching the user's expectation that larger
content areas take proportionally longer to open or close.

The overflow property is set to hidden during the transition to prevent content
from spilling outside the collapsing boundary. After the expand transition
completes, overflow is set to visible to allow tooltips, dropdowns, and
other overflow content within the section to render correctly. This two-phase
overflow management (hidden during animation, visible after) is critical for
content that contains interactive elements.

The collapse trigger is a clickable header element with a chevron icon that
rotates 180 degrees during the transition. The chevron points downward when
collapsed (indicating "click to expand") and upward when expanded (indicating
"click to collapse"). The rotation animation runs in sync with the height
transition using the same duration and easing.

The height transition uses the CSS easing function cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)
which is the Material Design standard ease-in-out. This produces a smooth
acceleration at the start and deceleration at the end, avoiding the linear
motion that feels mechanical.

For nested collapsible sections (a collapsed section containing another
collapsible section inside), the outer section's height must account for the
inner section's state. When the outer section expands, it measures scrollHeight
which includes the inner section in whatever state it was left in (collapsed
or expanded). If the user then expands the inner section, the outer section's
height must update to accommodate the newly expanded inner content. This is
handled by dispatching a custom event (athena-content-resize) that propagates
up to parent collapsible sections, triggering a height recalculation.

The animation interacts with the chat scroll position. When a section expands
and pushes content below it downward, the chat panel scrolls to keep the
user's current reading position stable. This is calculated by measuring the
section's position relative to the viewport before and after expansion and
adjusting scrollTop to compensate for the height delta.

Collapsible sections start in their default state based on content type.
Long analysis sections (over 500 characters) start collapsed with a preview
snippet showing the first 100 characters and a "Show more" label. Short
sections start expanded. The initial state is set without animation (no
transition on first render) to prevent a cascade of expand animations when a
response first loads.

The feature respects prefers-reduced-motion by replacing the height transition
with an instant toggle (display: none/block) that has no animated height
change. The chevron rotation is also replaced with an instant 180-degree
flip. The overflow management and scroll position compensation still apply.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks on a collapsible section header (chevron or title) in an Athena
response message.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena responds to "Give me a detailed analysis" with a response containing
   three sections: Summary (expanded), Methodology Detail (collapsed, 800
   characters), and Citation List (collapsed, 1200 characters). Each collapsed
   section shows a preview snippet and a chevron pointing down.
2. User clicks the "Methodology Detail" header. The chevron begins rotating
   from down to up. The section's height transitions from 0 to its natural
   scrollHeight (estimated 280px) over 280ms (200ms base + 80ms for 80px
   above threshold).
3. The content smoothly slides into view. The chat panel scrolls slightly to
   compensate for the height change, keeping the "Citation List" header at the
   same screen position it was before the expand.
4. User reads the methodology and clicks the header again to collapse it. The
   chevron rotates back to down. The height transitions from 280px to 0 over
   280ms. Content slides upward and disappears.
5. User clicks "Citation List" (1200 characters, approx 400px height). The
   expand transition takes 400ms (capped maximum). The list slides open with
   the same smooth easing.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Section is in its new state (expanded or collapsed). No active transition.
Overflow set to visible (if expanded). Content accessible and interactive.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/css/athena-animations.css: Collapse/expand transition rules, chevron
  rotation, overflow management, reduced-motion overrides.
- static/js/athena-collapse.js: CollapseManager class. Height measurement,
  dynamic duration calculation, scroll compensation, nested section events.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Attaches CollapseManager to collapsible sections
  during response rendering.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Collapsible section header and preview styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-collapse.js
  class CollapseManager {
    constructor() {
      this.sections = new Map();
    }

    register(sectionEl) {
      const header = sectionEl.querySelector('.athena-collapse-header');
      const body = sectionEl.querySelector('.athena-collapse-body');
      const chevron = header.querySelector('.athena-chevron');

      const state = { expanded: !sectionEl.classList.contains('collapsed') };
      this.sections.set(sectionEl, state);

      header.addEventListener('click', () => this.toggle(sectionEl));
    }

    toggle(sectionEl) {
      const state = this.sections.get(sectionEl);
      const body = sectionEl.querySelector('.athena-collapse-body');
      const chevron = sectionEl.querySelector('.athena-chevron');

      if (state.expanded) {
        this.collapse(sectionEl, body, chevron);
      } else {
        this.expand(sectionEl, body, chevron);
      }
      state.expanded = !state.expanded;
    }

    expand(sectionEl, body, chevron) {
      const targetHeight = body.scrollHeight;
      const duration = this.calcDuration(targetHeight);
      body.style.overflow = 'hidden';
      body.style.transition = `height ${duration}ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)`;
      body.style.height = '0px';
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        body.style.height = `${targetHeight}px`;
        chevron.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
      });
      body.addEventListener('transitionend', () => {
        body.style.height = 'auto';
        body.style.overflow = 'visible';
        body.style.transition = '';
        sectionEl.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('athena-content-resize', {
          bubbles: true
        }));
      }, { once: true });
      sectionEl.classList.remove('collapsed');
    }

    collapse(sectionEl, body, chevron) {
      const currentHeight = body.scrollHeight;
      const duration = this.calcDuration(currentHeight);
      body.style.height = `${currentHeight}px`;
      body.style.overflow = 'hidden';
      body.style.transition = `height ${duration}ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)`;
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        body.style.height = '0px';
        chevron.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
      });
      body.addEventListener('transitionend', () => {
        body.style.transition = '';
        sectionEl.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('athena-content-resize', {
          bubbles: true
        }));
      }, { once: true });
      sectionEl.classList.add('collapsed');
    }

    calcDuration(heightPx) {
      return Math.min(200 + heightPx, 400);
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  NOT APPLICABLE. Collapse animation is a frontend layout transition. The
  backend delivers the full response content. Whether sections are expanded
  or collapsed is a purely frontend rendering decision.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-collapse-body {
  overflow: hidden;
  height: 0;
}

.athena-collapse-body.expanded {
  height: auto;
  overflow: visible;
}

.athena-chevron {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  transition: transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
  color: var(--text-muted);
}

.athena-chevron.open {
  transform: rotate(180deg);
}

.athena-collapse-header {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 8px 0;
  cursor: pointer;
  user-select: none;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  font-size: 14px;
}

.athena-collapse-header:hover {
  color: var(--text-primary);
}

.athena-collapse-header:hover .athena-chevron {
  color: var(--accent-gold);
}

.athena-collapse-preview {
  color: var(--text-muted);
  font-size: 13px;
  white-space: nowrap;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  max-width: 80%;
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .athena-collapse-body {
    transition: none !important;
  }
  .athena-chevron {
    transition: none !important;
  }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
NOT APPLICABLE. No API endpoints. Section collapse state is frontend-only and
not persisted to the backend. The full content is always in the DOM regardless
of collapse state.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Section Collapsed (default for long content)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  v  Methodology Detail                                  │
  │     The analysis uses three-stage NLP pipeline w...     │
  │     [preview snippet, var(--text-muted)]                │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   v = chevron pointing down (rotate 0deg)

STATE: Section Expanding (mid-transition, t=140ms)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  >  Methodology Detail   [chevron rotating]             │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ The analysis uses three-stage NLP pipeline...    │   │
  │  │ Stage 1: Semantic similarity via cosine...       │   │
  │  │ [height: 140px, target: 280px, overflow:hidden]  │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

STATE: Section Expanded (transition complete)

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                         │
  │  ^  Methodology Detail                                  │
  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
  │  │ The analysis uses three-stage NLP pipeline with  │   │
  │  │ semantic similarity, LLM classification, and     │   │
  │  │ structural validation.                           │   │
  │  │                                                  │   │
  │  │ Stage 1: Semantic similarity via cosine distance │   │
  │  │ Stage 2: Groq LLM classification of mutation     │   │
  │  │ Stage 3: PageRank structural validation          │   │
  │  │ [height: auto, overflow: visible]                │   │
  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
  │                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   ^ = chevron pointing up (rotate 180deg)

TRANSITIONS:
Expand: height from 0 to scrollHeight, dynamic duration (200ms + 1ms/px, max 400ms),
cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1). Collapse: reverse. Chevron: rotate(0) to rotate(180deg),
300ms, same easing. Scroll compensation: scrollTop adjusted by height delta.
Reduced motion: instant toggle (no height transition), instant chevron rotation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks collapse header
  -> CollapseManager.toggle() checks current state
  -> If collapsed: expand path
     -> body.scrollHeight measured for target height
     -> Duration calculated: min(200 + height, 400)
     -> overflow set to hidden
     -> height transitions from 0 to scrollHeight
     -> chevron rotates to 180deg
     -> transitionend: height set to auto, overflow to visible
     -> athena-content-resize event dispatched (bubbles to parent sections)
  -> If expanded: collapse path
     -> body.scrollHeight measured for current height
     -> height set explicitly to current value
     -> height transitions from current to 0
     -> chevron rotates to 0deg
     -> transitionend: transition property cleared

Nested section expands
  -> Inner section dispatches athena-content-resize (bubbles)
  -> Parent collapsible section catches event
  -> Parent recalculates height if currently in expanded state
  -> Parent smoothly adjusts to accommodate new inner height

ERROR PATH: scrollHeight returns 0 (empty section)
  -> Expand animation has no visible effect (0 to 0 transition)
  -> Section marked as expanded, chevron rotates, but no content shown
  -> Graceful: no error, just empty expanded state

DATA STORED: No data persisted. Collapse state is ephemeral DOM state.
DATA CACHED: scrollHeight measured on each toggle (not cached between toggles).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE.

Collapse animation is a layout transition with no LLM involvement. The decision
about which sections start collapsed versus expanded is made by the frontend
renderer based on content length thresholds (over 500 characters starts
collapsed). This is a deterministic rule, not an LLM inference.

FALLBACK: Not applicable (no LLM dependency).
TOKEN BUDGET: Not applicable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                 |
|----------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| Collapse header      | Click        | Toggle section expand/collapse            |
| Chevron icon         | Click        | Same as header click (part of header)    |
| Header               | Hover        | Text color brightens, chevron turns gold |
| Header               | Enter key    | Toggle (keyboard accessible)             |
| Nested inner section | Expand       | Outer section height recalculates        |
| Long content (500+)  | Initial load | Starts collapsed with preview snippet    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Height transition: 200ms base + 1ms/px content height, max 400ms. Easing:
cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1). Chevron: transform rotate, 300ms, same easing.
Overflow: hidden during transition, visible after expand completes. Scroll
compensation: scrollTop adjusted to maintain reading position.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE
ACCESSIBILITY: Section header has role="button" and aria-expanded="true/false".
Content body has aria-hidden matching collapsed state. Chevron rotation is
decorative (aria-hidden). Focus outline visible on header. prefers-reduced-motion
removes all transitions (instant toggle).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User rapidly clicks collapse header (toggle spam)
   Expected: Each click reverses the previous animation mid-transition. The
   height smoothly changes direction without completing the previous animation.
   Implementation: Setting a new height target mid-transition causes CSS to
   interpolate from the current interpolated value, producing smooth reversal.

2. Scenario: Section contains images that load after collapse measurement
   Expected: After images load, scrollHeight changes. The section may overflow.
   Implementation: Listen for load events on images within collapsible sections.
   Dispatch athena-content-resize to trigger parent recalculation.

3. Scenario: Three levels of nested collapsible sections
   Expected: Expanding the innermost section cascades height updates through
   two parent sections. Each parent smoothly accommodates the new content.
   Implementation: athena-content-resize event bubbles through all ancestor
   collapsible sections via DOM event propagation.

4. Scenario: Section is collapsed and new content is dynamically added to it
   Expected: New content does not appear (section is collapsed). When expanded,
   the new content is included in the scrollHeight measurement.
   Implementation: scrollHeight measured fresh on each toggle, not cached.

5. Scenario: prefers-reduced-motion is enabled
   Expected: No height transition. Section toggles instantly between collapsed
   and expanded. Chevron flips instantly. Content appears/disappears with no
   intermediate frames.
   Implementation: CSS transition: none !important applied via media query.

6. Scenario: Section content exactly 500 characters (threshold boundary)
   Expected: Section starts expanded (threshold is "over 500", not "500 or
   more"). The 500-character section does not collapse on initial render.
   Implementation: Comparison uses > 500, not >= 500.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #002 (Rich Response Formatting), #007 (Visual Redesign)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #068 (Message Grouping), #075 (Response Reveal), #067 (Collapsible Responses)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: CSS Transitions API, transitionend event, scrollHeight measurement

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Click header expands collapsed section with smooth height transition
[ ] Click header collapses expanded section with smooth height transition
[ ] Dynamic duration: 200ms base + 1ms/px, capped at 400ms
[ ] Nested section expand triggers parent height recalculation
[ ] Long content (>500 chars) starts collapsed with preview

VISUAL:
[ ] Height transition uses cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) easing
[ ] Chevron rotates 180deg in sync with expand/collapse
[ ] No content overflow visible during transition (overflow: hidden)
[ ] Header hover brightens text and turns chevron gold

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Height transition triggers on compositor where possible
[ ] No layout thrashing from repeated scrollHeight measurements
[ ] Scroll compensation runs in same frame as height change

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] Header has role="button" and aria-expanded attribute
[ ] Body has aria-hidden matching collapsed state
[ ] prefers-reduced-motion: instant toggle, no transitions

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Rapid toggle: smooth reversal mid-transition
[ ] Image load in collapsed section: no overflow
[ ] 3-level nesting: height cascades correctly

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Chrome, Firefox, Safari transitions verified
[ ] PERFORMANCE TEST: 10 sections toggled at 60fps
[ ] REDUCED MOTION: Instant toggle verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-collapse.js (CollapseManager class), athena-animations.css
   (transition rules, chevron rotation), athena-chat.css (header styles)
2. COMPARE Done Criteria against implementation, verify duration calculation
   formula and cap
3. TEST expand a section, measure transition duration in DevTools, verify it
   matches 200 + height formula, verify cubic-bezier easing in Computed styles
4. TEST rapid toggle: click 5 times rapidly, verify no stuck state, verify each
   click reverses smoothly, verify final state matches click count parity
5. VERIFY nested sections: expand outer, expand inner, verify outer height
   accommodates inner, collapse inner, verify outer shrinks back
6. CHECK scroll compensation: expand a section above the viewport fold, verify
   content below does not jump, verify scrollTop adjusts correctly
7. BROKEN: Assume transitionend does not fire on rapid toggles. Test fallback
   cleanup. Assume scrollHeight returns incorrect value during transition.
8. LOG findings below, one or more issues per round

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Implementation:   Not started

FEATURE #042: SUGGESTED FOLLOW-UPS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Proactive AI
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #005 (Athena Chat Panel)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #044 (Multi-Turn Context), #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Suggested Follow-Ups provides proactive conversation continuation by
generating contextually relevant follow-up questions and actions that appear
as clickable chips below each Athena response. When Athena finishes answering
a question about a paper's citation graph, the system analyzes the response
content, the current graph state, and the conversation history to produce
three to five follow-up suggestions. These suggestions are not generic
prompts; they are deeply informed by the specific research context.

For example, after Athena explains that a paper has a PageRank centrality
score of 0.087 and serves as a methodological bottleneck, the suggested
follow-ups might include: "Show papers that would collapse if this node
were pruned," "Compare mutation types across all edges from this paper,"
"Find the closest DNA cluster to this methodology," and "What is the
pruning impact percentage for the entire subgraph?" Each suggestion maps
to a real analytical operation that Pathfinder can execute.

The suggestion engine runs after every Athena response during the streaming
completion phase. As the final token arrives and the response block renders,
a brief shimmer animation plays on the suggestion area (300ms), then the
chips fade in sequentially (100ms stagger per chip). Each chip shows a
short label (under 60 characters) styled in the dot matrix aesthetic with
a subtle left-border color coded by type: gold for graph operations, teal
for analytical queries, blue for comparative questions.

The system uses the GROQ fast model (llama-3.1-8b-instant) to generate
suggestions. The prompt includes the last assistant message, a compact
graph summary (top 15 nodes by PageRank, top 10 edges by mutation
confidence), and the user's recent conversation trajectory. The model
returns a JSON array of suggestion objects, each with a label and a
category. Token budget is kept under 400 output tokens.

Suggestions are ephemeral: they disappear when the user sends a new
message. If the user clicks a suggestion chip, the chip text is inserted
into the input area and automatically submitted, triggering a new Athena
response with its own follow-up suggestions. This creates a fluid
exploration flow where the user can drill into a research lineage by
clicking through chains of suggested questions without typing.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena finishes streaming a response. The response block is fully rendered.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What is the most critical ancestor of this paper?" Athena
   streams its response, identifying the 2003 Bengio paper with a PageRank
   of 0.087 and pruning impact of 31.4%.
2. As the final token arrives, a suggestion area appears below the response.
   A brief shimmer animation (300ms) plays across the area, then three to
   five suggestion chips fade in sequentially (100ms stagger).
3. The chips read: "Show pruning cascade for Bengio 2003," "List all
   adoption edges from this paper," "Compare with second-highest PageRank
   node," "Find DNA cluster containing Bengio 2003," "What year did this
   methodology peak?"
4. User clicks "Show pruning cascade for Bengio 2003." The chip text
   appears in the input area and is automatically submitted.
5. Athena streams a new response about the pruning cascade. A new set of
   follow-up suggestions appears below this response.
6. The previous set of suggestions disappears (fades out over 150ms) as
   the new message is submitted.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Suggestions remain visible below the most recent Athena response until the
user sends a new message (typed or clicked). Clicking a suggestion triggers
the same flow as typing and submitting that text manually.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-suggestions.js: SuggestionEngine class, chip rendering,
  click handlers, suggestion generation trigger.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Hook into onStreamComplete to trigger
  suggestion generation; pass response content to SuggestionEngine.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Chip styles, shimmer animation, fade-in
  transitions, color-coded left borders.
- app.py: /api/chat/suggestions POST endpoint for generating suggestions.
- backend/chat_guide.py: generate_follow_ups() method using GROQ fast model.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-suggestions.js
  class SuggestionEngine {
    constructor(chatEngine) {
      this.chatEngine = chatEngine;
      this.container = null;
      this.active = false;
    }

    async generateSuggestions(responseText, graphSummary) {
      const response = await fetch('/api/chat/suggestions', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({
          session_id: this.chatEngine.sessionId,
          response_text: responseText,
          graph_summary: graphSummary
        })
      });
      if (!response.ok) return;
      const data = await response.json();
      this.renderChips(data.suggestions);
    }

    renderChips(suggestions) {
      this.clearPrevious();
      this.container = document.createElement('div');
      this.container.className = 'athena-suggestions';
      this.container.classList.add('athena-suggestions--shimmer');
      setTimeout(() => {
        this.container.classList.remove('athena-suggestions--shimmer');
        suggestions.forEach((s, i) => {
          const chip = this.createChip(s.label, s.category);
          chip.style.animationDelay = `${i * 100}ms`;
          this.container.appendChild(chip);
        });
      }, 300);
      this.chatEngine.messagesContainer.appendChild(this.container);
    }

    createChip(label, category) {
      const chip = document.createElement('button');
      chip.className = 'athena-suggestion-chip';
      chip.dataset.category = category;
      chip.textContent = label;
      chip.addEventListener('click', () => this.onChipClick(label));
      return chip;
    }

    onChipClick(label) {
      this.chatEngine.submitMessage(label);
    }

    clearPrevious() {
      if (this.container) {
        this.container.classList.add('athena-suggestions--fading');
        setTimeout(() => this.container.remove(), 150);
      }
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py
  @app.route('/api/chat/suggestions', methods=['POST'])
  def generate_suggestions():
      data = request.json
      session_id = data['session_id']
      response_text = data['response_text']
      graph_summary = data.get('graph_summary', '')
      from backend.chat_guide import ChatGuide
      guide = ChatGuide(session_id)
      suggestions = guide.generate_follow_ups(response_text, graph_summary)
      return jsonify({'suggestions': suggestions})

  # In backend/chat_guide.py
  def generate_follow_ups(self, response_text, graph_summary):
      from backend.llm_client import ArivuLLMClient
      llm = ArivuLLMClient()
      prompt = (
          'Given this Athena response and graph context, generate 3-5 '
          'follow-up questions. Return JSON array of objects with '
          'label (under 60 chars) and category (graph/analytical/comparative).'
      )
      result = llm.query(
          model='fast',
          system_prompt=prompt,
          user_prompt=f'Response: {response_text[:1000]}\nGraph: {graph_summary[:500]}',
          max_tokens=400
      )
      import json
      try:
          return json.loads(result)
      except json.JSONDecodeError:
          return []

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-suggestions {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 12px 0;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.athena-suggestions--shimmer {
  background: linear-gradient(
    90deg,
    transparent 0%,
    rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.06) 50%,
    transparent 100%
  );
  background-size: 200% 100%;
  animation: suggestionShimmer 300ms ease-out;
}
@keyframes suggestionShimmer {
  from { background-position: -200% 0; }
  to { background-position: 200% 0; }
}
.athena-suggestion-chip {
  padding: 6px 14px;
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 16px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  color: var(--text-primary);
  font-size: 13px;
  cursor: pointer;
  animation: chipFadeIn 200ms ease-out forwards;
  opacity: 0;
  transition: background 150ms, border-color 150ms;
}
.athena-suggestion-chip:hover {
  background: var(--bg-elevated);
  border-color: var(--accent-gold);
}
.athena-suggestion-chip[data-category="graph"] {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-gold);
}
.athena-suggestion-chip[data-category="analytical"] {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-teal);
}
.athena-suggestion-chip[data-category="comparative"] {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-blue);
}
@keyframes chipFadeIn {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(4px); }
  to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/chat/suggestions
  Request: { session_id, response_text, graph_summary }
  Response: { suggestions: [{ label, category }] }
  Errors: 500 if LLM fails (returns empty array as fallback)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Suggestions Loading (Shimmer)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena response text ends here.                      |
  |                                                       |
  |  [~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ shimmer bar ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~]        |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Suggestions Visible (Chips Rendered)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena response text ends here.                      |
  |                                                       |
  |  [Show pruning cascade...]  [List adoption edges...]  |
  |  [Compare PageRank...]  [Find DNA cluster...]         |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+

  Chip anatomy:
  +-------------------------------------------+
  | gold |  Show pruning cascade for Bengio    |
  | bar  |  2003                               |
  +-------------------------------------------+
    3px left border = category color
    Background: var(--bg-surface)
    Text: var(--text-primary), 13px

STATE: Suggestions Fading Out (User Sends New Message)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  [chips fade out over 150ms, opacity 1 to 0]          |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Shimmer animation: 300ms linear gradient sweep from left to right.
Chip fade-in: 200ms ease-out per chip, staggered by 100ms.
Chip fade-out: 150ms opacity transition on new message submission.
Chip hover: background darkens over 150ms, border-color becomes accent-gold.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena finishes streaming response
  -> athena-engine.js calls onStreamComplete callback
  -> SuggestionEngine.generateSuggestions(responseText, graphSummary)
  -> POST /api/chat/suggestions with session_id, response_text, graph_summary
  -> Backend ChatGuide.generate_follow_ups() calls GROQ fast model
  -> GROQ returns JSON array of suggestion objects
  -> Backend returns suggestions to frontend
  -> SuggestionEngine.renderChips() displays shimmer then chips
  -> User clicks chip -> SuggestionEngine.onChipClick() -> chatEngine.submitMessage()
  -> New message submitted, previous suggestions cleared

ERROR PATH: GROQ call fails or returns invalid JSON
  -> generate_follow_ups() returns empty array
  -> Frontend receives empty suggestions list
  -> No chips rendered; no error shown to user (silent degradation)

DATA STORED: None. Suggestions are ephemeral and not persisted.
DATA CACHED: Last suggestion set cached in SuggestionEngine.container DOM reference.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: GROQ fast model (llama-3.1-8b-instant via config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL)
STAGE: Post-response suggestion generation
PROMPT:
  System: "You are a research intelligence assistant. Given the last response
  from Athena and a compact graph summary, generate 3-5 follow-up questions
  that a researcher would naturally ask next. Each question should be specific
  to the graph data, referencing paper names, mutation types, PageRank scores,
  or pruning impacts where relevant. Return a JSON array of objects, each with
  'label' (string, max 60 chars) and 'category' (one of: graph, analytical,
  comparative)."

  User: "Response: [last assistant message, truncated to 1000 chars]
  Graph: [compact summary: top 15 nodes, top 10 edges, truncated to 500 chars]"

OUTPUT FORMAT: JSON array of 3-5 objects: [{ "label": "...", "category": "..." }]
FALLBACK: If GROQ unavailable or returns invalid JSON, return empty array. No
  suggestions are shown. User can still type manually.
TOKEN BUDGET: 400 output tokens maximum. Input tokens: ~800 (response + summary).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element                | Action    | Response                                    |
|------------------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------|
| Suggestion chip        | Click     | Text inserted into input and auto-submitted |
| Suggestion chip        | Hover     | Background darkens, border becomes gold     |
| Suggestion chip        | Focus     | Outline ring for keyboard navigation        |
| Suggestion area        | Load      | Shimmer 300ms then chips fade in staggered  |
| User sends new message | Submit    | Previous suggestions fade out (150ms)       |
| Keyboard Tab           | Navigate  | Moves focus between chips in order          |
| Keyboard Enter         | On chip   | Same as click: submits chip text            |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
  Shimmer: 300ms linear gradient sweep (left to right) on suggestion container.
  Chip fade-in: 200ms ease-out, opacity 0 to 1, translateY(4px) to 0, staggered
    by 100ms per chip (first chip at 0ms, second at 100ms, etc.).
  Chip fade-out: 150ms opacity transition to 0 on container removal.
  Chip hover: 150ms background-color and border-color transition.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
  Suggestion chips are <button> elements with role="button".
  Container has aria-label="Suggested follow-up questions."
  Each chip has aria-label matching its text content.
  Tab navigation moves between chips. Enter activates.
  Screen reader announces: "Suggested follow-up: [chip text]."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: GROQ returns fewer than 3 suggestions
   Expected: Display whatever is returned (even 1 or 2 chips). No error.
   Implementation: renderChips() works with any array length.

2. Scenario: GROQ returns more than 5 suggestions
   Expected: Truncate to first 5. Ignore extras.
   Implementation: suggestions.slice(0, 5) before rendering.

3. Scenario: Suggestion text exceeds 60 characters
   Expected: Truncate with ellipsis at 60 chars.
   Implementation: CSS text-overflow: ellipsis; max-width on chip.

4. Scenario: User clicks chip while previous response is still streaming
   Expected: Chip click is ignored during active streaming state.
   Implementation: onChipClick checks chatEngine.isStreaming flag.

5. Scenario: Network timeout on suggestion generation
   Expected: No suggestions shown. Silent failure. User types manually.
   Implementation: fetch has 5-second timeout; catch block returns empty.

6. Scenario: Graph has no data yet (suggestions requested before graph loads)
   Expected: Suggestions generated from response text only (no graph context).
   Implementation: graph_summary defaults to empty string if unavailable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #044 (Multi-Turn Context), #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #005 (Athena Chat Panel), #034 (Chat-Driven Graph Navigation)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: GROQ API (llama-3.1-8b-instant)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] 3-5 suggestion chips appear below each Athena response
[ ] Clicking a chip submits that text as a new user message
[ ] Chips disappear when user sends a new message
[ ] Chips are color-coded by category (gold/teal/blue left border)
[ ] Suggestions are contextually relevant to the response and graph

PERFORMANCE:

VERIFICATION:

4. TEST chip click submits message and clears previous suggestions
