```css
}
@keyframes chatFadeOut {
  from { opacity: 1; }
  to { opacity: 0; }
}
.athena-confirm-dialog {
  position: fixed;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 24px;
  max-width: 360px;
  z-index: 1000;
  box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
}
.athena-confirm-dialog__message {
  color: var(--text-primary);
  font-size: 14px;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
  line-height: 1.5;
}
.athena-confirm-dialog__actions {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: flex-end;
  gap: 8px;
}
.athena-confirm-dialog__btn--primary {
  background: var(--accent-gold);
  color: var(--bg-primary);
  border: none;
  padding: 8px 16px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-weight: 500;
}
.athena-confirm-dialog__btn--secondary {
  background: transparent;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  padding: 8px 16px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/chat/new
  Request: { previous_session_id (optional) }
  Response: { session_id, greeting }

POST /api/chat/clear
  Request: { session_id }
  Response: { status: "cleared" }

POST /api/chat/archive
  Request: { session_id }
  Response: { status: "archived" }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Chat Header with New Chat Button

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena                                    [+] [...]  |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    [+] = New Chat button (plus-in-speech-bubble icon)
    [...] = overflow menu

STATE: Confirmation Dialog (New Chat)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |                                                       |
  |   +-------------------------------------------+      |
  |   | Start a new conversation? Current chat    |      |
  |   | will be saved to history.                 |      |
  |   |                                           |      |
  |   |                  [Cancel]  [New Chat]     |      |
  |   +-------------------------------------------+      |
  |                                                       |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Dialog: bg-surface, 1px border bg-elevated, 8px radius
    "New Chat" button: accent-gold background
    "Cancel" button: transparent, text-secondary

STATE: Chat Cleared (Greeting Message)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena                                    [+] [...]  |
  +-----------+-------------------------------------------+
  |           |                                           |
  |           |  Athena: New conversation started. I can  |
  |           |  see the ancestry graph for Attention Is  |
  |           |  All You Need. What would you like to     |
  |           |  explore?                                 |
  |           |                                           |
  +-----------+-------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Confirmation dialog: fades in 150ms, fades out 150ms on dismiss.
Chat clear: messages fade out 200ms, greeting fades in 200ms.
Backdrop overlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.5) behind dialog, fades in 150ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks "New Chat"
  -> Confirmation dialog shown
  -> User confirms
  -> POST /api/chat/archive archives current session metadata
  -> POST /api/chat/new creates new session, returns session_id + greeting
  -> Frontend clears message container (200ms fade-out)
  -> Frontend updates sessionId reference
  -> Greeting message rendered in empty chat
  -> Context window reset (no history for new session)

User types /clear
  -> Confirmation dialog: "Clear all messages? This cannot be undone."
  -> User confirms
  -> POST /api/chat/clear deletes all chat_history rows for session
  -> Frontend clears message container (200ms fade-out)
  -> No greeting shown (session continues, just empty)

ERROR PATH: Archive or new session creation fails
  -> Toast: "Failed to start new chat. Please try again."
  -> Current conversation preserved unchanged.

DATA STORED: Archived sessions stored with summary in sessions table.
  New session created in sessions table. Old chat_history preserved.
DATA CACHED: sessionId updated in AthenaEngine instance.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The New Chat / Clear feature does not invoke the LLM.
It resets the context window that the LLM will use for subsequent
messages, but the reset itself requires no LLM call. The greeting
message is a template string, not LLM-generated.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action         | Response                               |
|----------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------|
| New Chat button      | Click          | Opens confirmation dialog              |
| Ctrl+Shift+N         | Keyboard       | Opens confirmation dialog              |
| "New Chat" confirm   | Click          | Archives + resets + greeting           |
| "Cancel" button      | Click          | Dismisses dialog, no action            |
| /clear command       | Type + Enter   | Opens clear confirmation dialog        |
| "Clear" confirm      | Click          | Deletes all messages, no archive       |
| Escape key           | Press          | Dismisses open dialog                  |
| Dialog backdrop      | Click          | Dismisses dialog                       |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
  Dialog fade-in: 150ms opacity transition.
  Dialog fade-out: 150ms opacity transition.
  Chat messages fade-out: 200ms opacity animation.
  Greeting message fade-in: 200ms opacity animation.
  Backdrop overlay: 150ms opacity fade-in.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
  Confirmation dialog has role="dialog" and aria-modal="true."
  Dialog has aria-labelledby pointing to the message text.
  Focus trapped inside dialog while open.
  Escape key dismisses dialog.
  New Chat button has aria-label="Start new conversation."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User clicks New Chat with no messages in current conversation
   Expected: No archive needed. Just create new session and show greeting.
   Implementation: Archive step checks message count; skips if zero.

2. Scenario: User clicks New Chat during active streaming
   Expected: Stop streaming first, then show confirmation dialog.
   Implementation: newChat() checks isStreaming and calls stopGeneration() first.

3. Scenario: Network fails during session creation
   Expected: Error toast. Current conversation preserved unchanged.
   Implementation: resetSession() catches fetch errors and reverts.

4. Scenario: User presses Ctrl+Shift+N in rapid succession
   Expected: Only one dialog appears. Subsequent presses ignored while open.
   Implementation: newChat() checks if dialog is already open.

5. Scenario: /clear with hundreds of messages
   Expected: DELETE query may take a moment. Show brief loading indicator.
   Implementation: Clear button shows spinner while DELETE executes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #048 (Conversation History Sidebar), #005 (Athena Chat Panel)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None

[ ] Confirming creates new session and clears messages

[ ] Greeting message reflects current graph state

VERIFICATION:

FEATURE #048: CONVERSATION HISTORY SIDEBAR
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Conversation Mgmt
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #047 (New Chat / Clear Conversation)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #044 (Multi-Turn Context), #047 (New Chat / Clear Conversation)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Conversation History Sidebar provides a navigable list of all past Athena
conversations for the current user session, allowing the user to switch
between archived and active conversations. When a researcher uses the
"New Chat" button (#047) to start a fresh conversation, the previous
conversation is archived but not deleted. The History Sidebar makes those
archived conversations accessible again.

The sidebar is a slide-out panel that appears on the left side of the
Athena chat panel when the user clicks the history icon (a clock with a
counterclockwise arrow) in the chat header. The panel overlays the graph
visualization area and has a width of 280px with a semi-transparent
backdrop. It lists conversations in reverse chronological order (newest
first), each displayed as a card showing:
- A title derived from the first user message (truncated to 50 chars)
- The message count (e.g., "24 messages")
- The timestamp of the last message (relative: "2 hours ago")
- A small dot indicator: green (active), gray (archived)

Clicking a conversation card loads that conversation into the chat panel.
The current conversation is automatically saved to the history before the
switch. This creates a seamless multi-conversation workflow: start a chat
about Paper A, switch to explore Paper B, then return to the Paper A
conversation with full context preserved.

The sidebar also provides a search bar at the top that filters conversations
by keyword. The search matches against the conversation title (first user
message) and a summary of the conversation content. The search is performed
client-side using a simple substring match on cached conversation metadata.

Each conversation card has a context menu (right-click or long-press on
mobile) with options: "Rename," "Delete," and "Export as text." The Rename
option allows the user to set a custom title instead of the auto-derived
first-message title. Delete removes the conversation permanently after
confirmation. Export generates a plain-text file of all messages in the
conversation.

The conversation metadata is stored in a new conversations JSONB column
on the sessions table, containing an array of conversation summaries.
Each summary includes: conversation_id (the session_id of that
conversation), title, message_count, last_message_at, and custom_title
(if renamed). The backend endpoint /api/chat/history returns this list.

The sidebar state (open/closed) persists across page refreshes via
localStorage. The active conversation is indicated by a gold left border
on its card, matching the accent-gold color of the dot matrix design
language. Switching conversations triggers a 200ms crossfade animation
on the chat messages container.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks the history icon in the chat header.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User has been analyzing "Attention Is All You Need" for 20 messages.
   They used "New Chat" to start a conversation about "BERT." They now
   want to return to the Attention conversation.
2. User clicks the clock icon in the chat header. The History Sidebar
   slides in from the left (200ms).
3. The sidebar shows two conversation cards:
   - "What are the mutation types in BERT's..." (active, green dot, 8 msgs)
   - "Show me the PageRank distribution for..." (archived, gray dot, 20 msgs)
4. User clicks the "Show me the PageRank distribution for..." card.
5. The current BERT conversation is auto-saved. The chat container
   crossfades (200ms) to the Attention conversation.
6. All 20 messages are loaded, and the context window is restored.
   The user can continue from where they left off.
7. User closes the sidebar by clicking the X button or clicking outside.

AFTER COMPLETION:
The previous conversation is fully loaded with all messages and context.
The user can continue the conversation or switch back to any other
conversation in the history.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-history.js: HistorySidebar class, conversation list
  rendering, search, card actions, switch logic.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Hook into HistorySidebar for conversation
  switching; provide save/load session methods.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Sidebar panel, conversation cards, search
  bar, context menu, active indicator styles.
- app.py: /api/chat/history GET, /api/chat/rename POST, /api/chat/delete
  POST endpoints.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-history.js
  class HistorySidebar {
    constructor(chatEngine) {
      this.chatEngine = chatEngine;
      this.conversations = [];
      this.isOpen = localStorage.getItem('athena-history-open') === 'true';
      this.panel = this._createPanel();
    }

    async open() {
      this.isOpen = true;
      localStorage.setItem('athena-history-open', 'true');
      await this.loadConversations();
      this.panel.classList.add('athena-history--open');
    }

    close() {
      this.isOpen = false;
      localStorage.setItem('athena-history-open', 'false');
      this.panel.classList.remove('athena-history--open');
    }

    async loadConversations() {
      const resp = await fetch(
        `/api/chat/history?session_id=${this.chatEngine.sessionId}`
      );
      const data = await resp.json();
      this.conversations = data.conversations;
      this.renderList();
    }

    renderList(filter) {
      const list = this.panel.querySelector('.history-list');
      list.innerHTML = '';
      let filtered = this.conversations;
      if (filter) {
        filtered = filtered.filter(c =>
          c.title.toLowerCase().includes(filter.toLowerCase())
        );
      }
      filtered.forEach(conv => {
        const card = this._createCard(conv);
        list.appendChild(card);
      });
    }

    async switchTo(conversationId) {
      await this.chatEngine.saveCurrentSession();
      await this.chatEngine.loadSession(conversationId);
      this.renderList();
    }

    _createCard(conv) {
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.className = 'history-card';
      if (conv.conversation_id === this.chatEngine.sessionId) {
        card.classList.add('history-card--active');
      }
      card.innerHTML = `
        <div class="history-card__title">${conv.custom_title || conv.title}</div>
        <div class="history-card__meta">
          ${conv.message_count} messages - ${this._relativeTime(conv.last_message_at)}
        </div>
      `;
      card.addEventListener('click', () => this.switchTo(conv.conversation_id));
      return card;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py
  @app.route('/api/chat/history', methods=['GET'])
  def get_chat_history():
      session_id = request.args['session_id']
      row = db.fetchone(
          'SELECT conversations FROM sessions WHERE session_id = %s',
          (session_id,)
      )
      conversations = (row or {}).get('conversations', [])
      return jsonify({'conversations': conversations})

  @app.route('/api/chat/rename', methods=['POST'])
  def rename_conversation():
      data = request.json
      session_id = data['session_id']
      conversation_id = data['conversation_id']
      new_title = data['title'][:100]
      db.execute(
          """UPDATE sessions
             SET conversations = (
               SELECT jsonb_agg(
                 CASE WHEN elem->>'conversation_id' = %s
                 THEN elem || jsonb_build_object('custom_title', %s)
                 ELSE elem END
               )
               FROM jsonb_array_elements(conversations) elem
             )
             WHERE session_id = %s""",
          (conversation_id, new_title, session_id)
      )
      return jsonify({'status': 'renamed'})

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-history {
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
  bottom: 0;
  width: 280px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border-right: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  transform: translateX(-100%);
  transition: transform 200ms ease-out;
  z-index: 100;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.athena-history--open {
  transform: translateX(0);
}
.history-card {

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 150ms;
}

  background: var(--bg-elevated);
}
.history-card--active {
  border-left: 3px solid var(--accent-gold);
  background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.05);
}
.history-card__title {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  white-space: nowrap;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
}
.history-card__meta {
  font-size: 12px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
  margin-top: 4px;
}
.history-search {
  padding: 12px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
}
.history-search input {
  width: 100%;
  padding: 8px 12px;
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 6px;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  font-size: 13px;
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
GET /api/chat/history?session_id=...
  Response: { conversations: [{ conversation_id, title, message_count,
    last_message_at, custom_title }] }

POST /api/chat/rename
  Request: { session_id, conversation_id, title }
  Response: { status: "renamed" }

POST /api/chat/delete
  Request: { session_id, conversation_id }
  Response: { status: "deleted" }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Sidebar Closed (Default)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena                            [clock] [+] [...]  |
  +-----------+-------------------------------------------+
  |           |  (chat messages)                          |
  +-----------+-------------------------------------------+
    [clock] icon: history toggle, var(--text-secondary)

STATE: Sidebar Open

  +------------------------+------------------------------+
  | Conversation History   |                              |
  |  [search...........] X |  (chat messages,             |
  |                        |   partially obscured)        |
  |  +------------------+  |                              |
  |  | gold | What are   |  |                              |
  |  | bar  | the mutati |  |                              |
  |  |      | 8 msgs     |  |                              |
  |  +------------------+  |                              |
  |                        |                              |
  |  +------------------+  |                              |
  |  |      | Show me    |  |                              |
  |  |      | the PageR  |  |                              |
  |  |      | 20 msgs    |  |                              |
  |  +------------------+  |                              |
  +------------------------+------------------------------+
    Width: 280px. Active card: gold left border.
    Search bar at top. Cards in reverse chronological order.

TRANSITIONS:
Sidebar open: translateX(-100% to 0) over 200ms ease-out.
Sidebar close: translateX(0 to -100%) over 200ms ease-in.
Card hover: background darkens over 150ms.
Conversation switch: chat messages crossfade (200ms).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User clicks history icon
  -> HistorySidebar.open() called
  -> GET /api/chat/history?session_id=... fetches conversation list
  -> Conversations rendered as cards in sidebar
  -> User clicks a conversation card
  -> HistorySidebar.switchTo(conversationId) called
  -> Current session saved (auto-archive)
  -> AthenaEngine.loadSession(conversationId) loads messages from DB
  -> Chat container crossfades to loaded conversation
  -> Context window rebuilt from loaded history

User searches in sidebar
  -> Input event on search field
  -> HistorySidebar.renderList(filterText) filters cached list
  -> Client-side substring match, no backend call

ERROR PATH: Conversation load fails
  -> Toast: "Failed to load conversation."
  -> Current conversation remains active unchanged.

DATA STORED: Conversation metadata in sessions.conversations JSONB.
  Individual messages in chat_history keyed by session_id.
DATA CACHED: Conversation list cached in HistorySidebar.conversations.
  Sidebar open state in localStorage.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The Conversation History Sidebar is a navigation and
management feature. It does not invoke the LLM. When the user switches
to a loaded conversation, the multi-turn context (#044) rebuilds the
context window from the loaded chat_history, which will be used by the
LLM on the next user message.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------|
| History icon         | Click        | Toggles sidebar open/closed             |
| Conversation card    | Click        | Switches to that conversation           |
| Conversation card    | Right-click  | Context menu: Rename, Delete, Export    |
| Search input         | Type         | Filters conversation list in real time  |
| Close button (X)     | Click        | Closes sidebar                          |
| Backdrop             | Click        | Closes sidebar (mobile only)            |
| "Rename" menu item   | Click        | Inline edit for conversation title      |
| "Delete" menu item   | Click        | Confirmation then permanent delete      |
| "Export" menu item    | Click        | Downloads conversation as .txt file     |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
  Sidebar slide-in: 200ms ease-out translateX transition.
  Sidebar slide-out: 200ms ease-in translateX transition.
  Card hover: 150ms background transition.
  Conversation switch: 200ms crossfade on chat container.
  Context menu: 100ms fade-in popup.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
  Sidebar has role="complementary" and aria-label="Conversation history."
  Search input has aria-label="Search conversations."
  Conversation cards are role="button" with aria-label showing title.
  Close button has aria-label="Close history sidebar."
  Focus management: sidebar traps focus when open (Tab cycles within).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: No previous conversations (first-time user)
   Expected: Sidebar shows empty state: "No previous conversations."
   Implementation: Empty conversations array renders placeholder text.

2. Scenario: User has 100+ conversations
   Expected: List is scrollable. No pagination needed for reasonable counts.
   Implementation: overflow-y: auto on list container.

3. Scenario: Conversation card title is empty (user sent only whitespace)
   Expected: Title defaults to "Untitled conversation."
   Implementation: Fallback title in _createCard if title is empty.

4. Scenario: User deletes the active conversation
   Expected: After deletion, start a new empty conversation automatically.
   Implementation: If deleted === active, call newChat() internally.

5. Scenario: Two tabs open with the same session
   Expected: Each tab maintains its own sidebar state independently.
   Implementation: localStorage key includes tab-specific suffix.

DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #005 (Athena Chat Panel), #006 (Conversation Memory)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None

[ ] Search input labeled

[ ] Focus trapped in sidebar when open

[ ] 100+ conversations scrollable

FEATURE #049: MODEL/MODE SELECTOR
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Chat Controls
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #005 (Athena Chat Panel)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #044 (Multi-Turn Context)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Model/Mode Selector allows the user to switch between different Athena
response modes and underlying GROQ models directly from the chat interface.
Arivu uses two GROQ models: the fast model (llama-3.1-8b-instant, accessed
via config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL) for quick analytical queries and edge
classification, and the smart model (llama-3.3-70b-versatile, accessed via
config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL) for complex multi-paper reasoning and genealogy
storytelling. By default, Athena uses the fast model for most queries and
automatically escalates to the smart model for complex requests. The
Model/Mode Selector gives the user explicit control over this selection.

The selector appears as a compact dropdown in the chat header, next to the
"New Chat" button. It shows the currently active mode as a small label:
"Fast" (with a lightning bolt icon) or "Deep" (with a brain icon). Clicking
the label opens a dropdown with two mode options:

- Fast Mode (default): Uses llama-3.1-8b-instant. Optimized for quick
  answers about specific nodes, edges, mutation types, and PageRank scores.
  Response latency: 1-3 seconds. Best for: "What is the PageRank of this
  paper?" "List all adoption edges." "How many papers are in cluster 3?"

- Deep Mode: Uses llama-3.3-70b-versatile. Optimized for complex analytical
  reasoning, genealogy narratives, cross-paper comparisons, and pruning
  impact analysis. Response latency: 5-15 seconds. Best for: "Explain the
  intellectual lineage from Bengio to Vaswani." "What would this field look
  like without the 2014 GAN paper?"

The mode selection is stored in the session state and persists across page
refreshes. It is sent with each chat request so the backend knows which
model to invoke. The mode also affects the system prompt: Fast mode uses a
concise analytical prompt, while Deep mode uses an expansive reasoning
prompt that encourages multi-step analysis and cross-referencing.

The dropdown also shows a brief description of each mode and an estimated
response time indicator. A subtle color difference distinguishes the modes:
Fast mode label uses var(--accent-teal) and Deep mode uses var(--accent-blue).
The active mode's color is reflected in the typing indicator dots (#046).

When the user switches modes mid-conversation, a context anchor (#044) is
injected noting the mode change. This prevents the LLM from being confused
by a sudden shift in response style within the same conversation thread.

The selector also supports a third option: "Auto" (with a gear icon), which
is the default behavior where Athena automatically selects the appropriate
model based on query complexity. In Auto mode, the backend analyzes the
user's message and selects fast for simple queries and smart for complex
ones. The auto-selection logic examines message length, presence of
comparison keywords, and the number of papers referenced.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks the mode selector dropdown in the chat header.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User sees "Auto" mode label in the chat header (default). They want
   a detailed genealogy narrative and know it requires the smart model.
2. User clicks the "Auto" label. A dropdown opens showing three options:
   Auto (gear icon), Fast (lightning bolt), Deep (brain icon).
3. Each option shows a brief description: Auto: "Athena selects the best
   model." Fast: "Quick answers, 1-3s." Deep: "Complex reasoning, 5-15s."
4. User selects "Deep." The label changes to "Deep" with a brain icon.
   The label color changes to var(--accent-blue).
5. User types: "Explain the intellectual lineage from the Attention paper
   to BERT." Athena uses the smart model and provides a rich narrative.
6. The response is notably more detailed than the fast model would produce.
7. After the analysis, user switches back to "Fast" for quick follow-ups.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Mode persists for all subsequent messages in this session. Typing indicator
dots reflect the active mode color. Context anchor marks the mode change.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-mode-selector.js: ModeSelector class, dropdown
  rendering, mode switching, session persistence.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Include selected mode in chat request
  payload; apply mode-specific system prompts.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Mode selector dropdown styles, mode labels,
  active state colors.
- app.py: /api/chat route reads mode from request payload and selects
  the appropriate GROQ model.
- backend/chat_guide.py: Mode-aware system prompt selection.
- backend/config.py: GROQ_FAST_MODEL and GROQ_SMART_MODEL config values.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-mode-selector.js
  class ModeSelector {
    constructor() {
      this.modes = [
        { id: 'auto', label: 'Auto', icon: 'gear', color: 'var(--text-secondary)',
          desc: 'Athena selects the best model' },
        { id: 'fast', label: 'Fast', icon: 'lightning', color: 'var(--accent-teal)',
          desc: 'Quick answers, 1-3s' },
        { id: 'deep', label: 'Deep', icon: 'brain', color: 'var(--accent-blue)',
          desc: 'Complex reasoning, 5-15s' },
      ];
      this.activeMode = localStorage.getItem('athena-mode') || 'auto';
      this.element = this._createSelector();
    }

    _createSelector() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-mode-selector';
      this._renderLabel(el);
      return el;
    }

    _renderLabel(el) {
      const mode = this.modes.find(m => m.id === this.activeMode);
      el.innerHTML = `
        <button class="mode-label" style="color: ${mode.color}">
          <span class="mode-icon mode-icon--${mode.icon}"></span>
          ${mode.label}
          <span class="mode-chevron"></span>
        </button>
      `;
      el.querySelector('.mode-label').addEventListener('click', () => this.toggleDropdown());
    }

    setMode(modeId) {
      this.activeMode = modeId;
      localStorage.setItem('athena-mode', modeId);
      this._renderLabel(this.element);
      this.closeDropdown();
      document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('athena-mode-change', { detail: { mode: modeId } }));
    }

    getMode() {
      return this.activeMode;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py (within /api/chat handler)
  mode = data.get('mode', 'auto')
  if mode == 'fast':
      model = config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL
  elif mode == 'deep':
      model = config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL
  else:
      model = guide.auto_select_model(user_message)

  # In backend/chat_guide.py
  def auto_select_model(self, message):
      complex_indicators = ['explain', 'compare', 'lineage', 'narrative',
                            'genealogy', 'what would', 'without', 'impact']
      if any(kw in message.lower() for kw in complex_indicators):
          return config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL
      if len(message) > 200:
          return config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL
      return config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-mode-selector {
  position: relative;
}
.mode-label {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 4px;
  padding: 4px 10px;
  background: transparent;
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 12px;
  font-size: 12px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-color 150ms;
}
.mode-label:hover {
  border-color: var(--text-muted);
}
.mode-dropdown {
  position: absolute;
  top: calc(100% + 4px);
  right: 0;
  width: 220px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
  z-index: 200;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.mode-option {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 10px 14px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 100ms;
}
.mode-option:hover {
  background: var(--bg-elevated);
}
.mode-option--active {
  background: rgba(212, 168, 67, 0.08);
}
.mode-option__label {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--text-primary);
}
.mode-option__desc {
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/chat
  Request: { session_id, message, mode (optional: 'auto'|'fast'|'deep') }
  Response: SSE stream (unchanged)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Mode Selector Closed (Header Label)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena              [Auto v]    [clock] [+] [...]    |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    "Auto" label with gear icon and chevron. Teal border.

STATE: Mode Selector Dropdown Open

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena              [Auto v]    [clock] [+] [...]    |
  |                      +------------------+              |
  |                      | * Auto           |              |
  |                      |   Athena selects  |              |
  |                      |                  |              |
  |                      | > Fast           |              |
  |                      |   Quick, 1-3s    |              |
  |                      |                  |              |
  |                      | @ Deep           |              |
  |                      |   Complex, 5-15s |              |
  |                      +------------------+              |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Active mode: subtle gold background highlight
    Icons: * = gear, > = lightning, @ = brain

STATE: Deep Mode Active

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Athena              [Deep v]    [clock] [+] [...]    |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    "Deep" label: var(--accent-blue) text, brain icon

TRANSITIONS:
Dropdown open: 150ms fade-in + translateY(-4px to 0).
Dropdown close: 100ms fade-out.
Mode switch: label text and color change immediately.
Typing indicator dots change color to match active mode.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User selects a mode from dropdown
  -> ModeSelector.setMode(modeId) updates activeMode
  -> localStorage updated with new mode
  -> Label re-rendered with new text, icon, and color
  -> Custom event 'athena-mode-change' dispatched
  -> AthenaEngine listens and updates internal mode reference

User sends message with mode selected
  -> AthenaEngine includes mode in POST /api/chat payload
  -> Backend reads mode parameter
  -> If 'auto': ChatGuide.auto_select_model() analyzes message
  -> If 'fast': uses config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL directly
  -> If 'deep': uses config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL directly
  -> Selected model passed to ArivuLLMClient.query()

ERROR PATH: Selected model is unavailable (GROQ rate limit)
  -> Fallback to other model with warning in response
  -> "Note: Switched to [model] due to rate limits."

DATA STORED: Mode preference in localStorage (frontend).
  Mode change context anchor in chat_history (backend).
DATA CACHED: activeMode in ModeSelector instance.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: Depends on selected mode:
  - Fast: llama-3.1-8b-instant (config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL)
  - Deep: llama-3.3-70b-versatile (config.GROQ_SMART_MODEL)
  - Auto: selected per-message by auto_select_model()
STAGE: Model selection happens before every LLM call
PROMPT:
  Fast mode system prompt: "You are Athena, a concise research analyst.
  Answer using specific data from the citation graph. Be brief and
  precise. Reference PageRank scores, mutation types, and edge counts."

  Deep mode system prompt: "You are Athena, a thorough research
  intelligence guide. Provide detailed analysis with cross-paper
  comparisons, genealogy narratives, and multi-step reasoning.
  Reference specific papers, explain intellectual lineages, and
  identify patterns across the citation ancestry graph."

OUTPUT FORMAT: Free-form text (both modes).
FALLBACK: If selected model unavailable, fall back to the other model.
TOKEN BUDGET: Fast: 1500 output tokens. Deep: 4000 output tokens.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Mode label           | Click        | Opens dropdown                          |
| Mode option          | Click        | Selects mode, closes dropdown           |
| Mode option          | Hover        | Background darkens                      |
| Outside dropdown     | Click        | Closes dropdown                         |
| Escape key           | Press        | Closes dropdown                         |
| Mode change          | Automatic    | Context anchor injected in chat history |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
  Dropdown open: 150ms opacity + translateY transition.
  Dropdown close: 100ms opacity transition.
  Mode label change: immediate text/color update.
  Active mode highlight: 100ms background transition.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
  Mode selector has role="listbox" on dropdown.
  Each option has role="option" with aria-selected.
  Label button has aria-haspopup="listbox" and aria-expanded.
  Keyboard: Arrow keys navigate options, Enter selects, Escape closes.
  Screen reader announces: "Mode: Auto selected. Options available."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User switches mode mid-stream (while Athena is responding)
   Expected: Mode change takes effect on the next message, not current.
   Implementation: Mode read at message send time, not during stream.

2. Scenario: GROQ_API_KEY is not configured
   Expected: Mode selector is hidden entirely. No model selection possible.
   Implementation: ModeSelector checks config.GROQ_ENABLED before rendering.

3. Scenario: Fast model is rate-limited but deep model is available
   Expected: Auto-select falls back to deep model. User notified in response.
   Implementation: Model fallback logic in ChatGuide with inline notice.

4. Scenario: User selects Deep mode for a trivial question
   Expected: Works fine, just slower. No prevention of "misuse."
   Implementation: No validation; user has full control.

5. Scenario: localStorage is unavailable (private browsing)
   Expected: Mode defaults to 'auto' on every page load.
   Implementation: try/catch around localStorage access.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #044 (Multi-Turn Context)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #005 (Athena Chat Panel), #046 (Typing Indicator)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: GROQ API (both models)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Mode selector dropdown with Auto, Fast, Deep options
[ ] Selected mode sent with each chat request
[ ] Fast mode uses llama-3.1-8b-instant
[ ] Deep mode uses llama-3.3-70b-versatile
[ ] Auto mode selects based on query complexity
[ ] Mode persists across page refreshes (localStorage)
[ ] Context anchor injected on mode switch

VISUAL:

[ ] Dropdown with mode descriptions and active highlight
[ ] Fast: teal accent, Deep: blue accent
[ ] Active mode color reflected in typing indicator

PERFORMANCE:

[ ] Mode switch is instant (no delay)
[ ] No performance impact on chat send flow

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] Dropdown has role="listbox"
[ ] Options have role="option" with aria-selected
[ ] Keyboard navigation: arrows, enter, escape

VERIFICATION:

FEATURE #050: RICH INPUT AREA
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Core Infrastructure
PRIORITY:        P0
PHASE:           A
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #005 (Athena Chat Panel)
BLOCKS:          #043 (File/Image Attachments)
BLOCKED BY:      NONE

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Rich Input Area replaces the basic single-line text input with a full-
featured, auto-expanding textarea that supports multi-line input, keyboard
shortcuts, slash commands with autocomplete, and visual integration with
the Athena chat panel's dot matrix aesthetic. The input area is the primary
interaction point for the user's communication with Athena and must feel
responsive, polished, and purpose-built for research conversations.

The input area is a contenteditable div (not a native textarea) to enable
future rich content support such as inline paper references and formatted
text. It starts at a single line height (40px) and auto-expands up to a
maximum of 6 lines (approximately 144px) as the user types. When content
exceeds 6 lines, the input area becomes scrollable internally. The auto-
expansion uses a hidden mirror element technique: an invisible div with
identical styling mirrors the input content, and its scrollHeight drives
the visible input's height via a smooth CSS transition (100ms ease-out).

The input area supports the following keyboard shortcuts:
- Enter: Send message (when not holding Shift)
- Shift+Enter: Insert a new line (does not send)
- Ctrl+Enter (or Cmd+Enter): Send message (always, even with Shift held)
- Up Arrow (when input is empty): Edit the last user message (#040)
- Escape: Clear input and dismiss any autocomplete popups

Slash commands are triggered by typing "/" at the beginning of the input
or after a space. When "/" is detected, an autocomplete popup appears
above the input showing available commands filtered by what the user has
typed so far. The available commands include: /clear, /export, /help,
/prune [paper_id], /focus [paper_id], /compare [paper_id] [paper_id],
/cluster [cluster_id], /history, and /mode [fast|deep|auto]. Each
command in the autocomplete list shows its name, a brief description,
and a keyboard shortcut (Tab to complete). The list updates in real time
as the user types, using a fuzzy match algorithm.

The input area includes a character counter that appears only when the
user has typed more than 1500 characters (approaching the 2000-character
limit defined in the ChatRequest schema). The counter is positioned at
the bottom-right corner of the input area, showing remaining characters
in var(--text-muted) that transitions to var(--danger) when under 100
characters remaining.

The right side of the input area contains the send button: a circular
button with an upward arrow icon, styled in var(--accent-gold). The
button is disabled (opacity 0.3) when the input is empty and enabled
(full opacity) when content is present. To the left of the send button
is the attachment button (paperclip icon, #043) which is rendered as a
companion button.

The input area supports paste interception: when the user pastes text
that looks like a DOI (matching the pattern 10.XXXX/...), the input
area shows a small inline suggestion: "Looks like a DOI. Search for
this paper?" Clicking the suggestion triggers a paper search for that
DOI instead of sending it as a regular message.

The entire input area is wrapped in a container that sits at the bottom
of the Athena chat panel, with a subtle top border in var(--bg-elevated)
and a background of var(--bg-surface). The container has 12px padding and
a border-radius of 12px on the inner input element.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User clicks in the input area or starts typing anywhere in the chat panel
(keyboard focus auto-activates the input).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User clicks in the input area. A subtle focus ring appears (1px
   var(--accent-gold) border).
2. User types "What are the top 5 papers by PageRank in this graph?"
   The input remains single-line (text fits in one line).
3. User decides to ask a multi-part question. They press Shift+Enter
   to create a new line: "Also, which of those papers have hybridization
   edges?" The input expands to 2 lines smoothly (100ms).
4. User presses Enter (without Shift). The message is sent. The input
   clears and collapses back to single-line height.
5. User types "/" to invoke a slash command. An autocomplete popup
   appears above the input showing all available commands.
6. User types "/pr" and the list filters to "/prune." User presses
   Tab to complete. The input now shows "/prune " with the cursor
   after the space, ready for a paper_id argument.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Input is clear and ready for the next message. The input area maintains
focus for seamless continued interaction.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-input.js: RichInputArea class, auto-expand, keyboard
  handlers, slash command autocomplete, DOI detection, character counter.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Connect RichInputArea to message submission
  pipeline; handle slash command dispatch.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Input area styles, focus ring, autocomplete
  popup, character counter, send button, expansion transition.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-input.js
  class RichInputArea {
    constructor(container, onSubmit) {
      this.container = container;
      this.onSubmit = onSubmit;
      this.maxChars = 2000;
      this.maxLines = 6;
      this.input = this._createInput();
      this.mirror = this._createMirror();
      this.sendButton = this._createSendButton();
      this.charCounter = this._createCharCounter();
      this.autocomplete = null;
      this.setupEventListeners();
    }

    _createInput() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-input';
      el.contentEditable = 'true';
      el.setAttribute('role', 'textbox');
      el.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Message Athena');
      el.setAttribute('aria-multiline', 'true');
      el.dataset.placeholder = 'Ask Athena about the graph...';
      return el;
    }

    setupEventListeners() {
      this.input.addEventListener('input', () => {
        this.autoExpand();
        this.checkSlashCommand();
        this.updateCharCounter();
        this.updateSendButton();
        this.checkDOIPaste();
      });

      this.input.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
        if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
          e.preventDefault();
          this.submit();
        }
        if (e.key === 'Enter' && (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey)) {
          e.preventDefault();
          this.submit();
        }
        if (e.key === 'Escape') {
          this.clear();
          this.closeAutocomplete();
        }
        if (e.key === 'ArrowUp' && this.isEmpty()) {
          document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('athena-edit-last'));
        }
      });
    }

    autoExpand() {
      this.mirror.textContent = this.input.textContent;
      const newHeight = Math.min(
        this.mirror.scrollHeight,
        this.maxLines * 24
      );
      this.input.style.height = newHeight + 'px';
    }

    submit() {
      const text = this.input.textContent.trim();
      if (!text) return;
      if (text.startsWith('/')) {
        this.dispatchSlashCommand(text);
      } else {
        this.onSubmit(text);
      }
      this.clear();
    }

    clear() {
      this.input.textContent = '';
      this.input.style.height = '40px';
      this.updateCharCounter();
      this.updateSendButton();
    }

    isEmpty() {
      return this.input.textContent.trim().length === 0;
    }

    getText() {
      return this.input.textContent.trim();
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  No backend changes required for the rich input area itself. The input
  sends plain text to the existing /api/chat endpoint. Slash commands
  are dispatched client-side to their respective handlers.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
```css
.athena-input-container {
  display: flex;
  align-items: flex-end;
  gap: 8px;
  padding: 12px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border-top: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
}
.athena-input {
  flex: 1;
  min-height: 40px;
  max-height: 144px;
  padding: 8px 12px;
  background: var(--bg-primary);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 12px;
  color: var(--text-primary);
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  font-size: 14px;
  line-height: 24px;
  overflow-y: auto;
  transition: height 100ms ease-out, border-color 150ms;
  outline: none;
}
.athena-input:focus {
  border-color: var(--accent-gold);
}
.athena-input:empty::before {
  content: attr(data-placeholder);
  color: var(--text-muted);
  pointer-events: none;
}
.athena-input-mirror {
  position: absolute;
  visibility: hidden;
  white-space: pre-wrap;
  word-wrap: break-word;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.athena-send-btn {
  width: 36px;
  height: 36px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--accent-gold);
  border: none;
  cursor: pointer;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  transition: opacity 150ms;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.athena-send-btn--disabled {
  opacity: 0.3;
  cursor: default;
  pointer-events: none;
}
.athena-char-counter {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 4px;
  right: 48px;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
  transition: color 200ms;
}
.athena-char-counter--warning {
  color: var(--danger);
}
.athena-autocomplete {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: calc(100% + 4px);
  left: 12px;
  right: 12px;
  background: var(--bg-surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
  border-radius: 8px;
  max-height: 240px;
  overflow-y: auto;
  box-shadow: 0 -4px 16px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3);
  z-index: 200;
}
.athena-autocomplete-item {
  padding: 8px 14px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: background 100ms;
}
.athena-autocomplete-item:hover,
.athena-autocomplete-item--active {
  background: var(--bg-elevated);
}
.athena-autocomplete-item__name {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--accent-gold);
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
}
.athena-autocomplete-item__desc {
  font-size: 12px;
  color: var(--text-muted);
}
```

API CONTRACTS:
No new API endpoints. Uses existing POST /api/chat.
Slash commands are dispatched client-side to existing feature handlers.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Input Empty (Placeholder Visible)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | Ask Athena about the graph...            [O]   |   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Placeholder: var(--text-muted)
    Send button [O]: disabled, opacity 0.3
    Height: 40px (single line)

STATE: Input With Text (Send Enabled)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | What are the top 5 papers by PageRank? [Send]  |   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Send button: accent-gold, full opacity
    Height: 40px (text fits one line)

STATE: Multi-Line Input (Expanded)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | What are the top 5 papers by PageRank?         |   |
  |  | Also, which of those have hybridization  [Send]|   |
  |  | edges?                                         |   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Height: ~72px (3 lines), max 144px (6 lines)

STATE: Slash Command Autocomplete

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | /prune   Focus on pruning cascade        [Tab] |   |
  |  | /compare Compare two papers              [Tab] |   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | /pr                                      [Send]|   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Autocomplete appears above input, filtered by typed text

STATE: Character Counter (Near Limit)

  +-------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  |  | (long text.............................)        |   |
  |  |                                    87 left     |   |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+   |
  +-------------------------------------------------------+
    Counter: var(--danger) when under 100 chars remaining

TRANSITIONS:
Auto-expand: height transition 100ms ease-out.
Focus ring: border-color transition 150ms.
Send button enable/disable: opacity transition 150ms.
Autocomplete popup: 150ms fade-in from below.
Character counter: color transition 200ms when crossing 100-char threshold.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User types in input area
  -> 'input' event fires
  -> autoExpand() adjusts height via mirror element
  -> checkSlashCommand() shows/hides autocomplete
  -> updateCharCounter() shows counter if > 1500 chars
  -> updateSendButton() enables/disables send

User presses Enter (no Shift)
  -> keydown handler calls submit()
  -> If text starts with '/': dispatchSlashCommand()
  -> Else: onSubmit(text) passed to AthenaEngine.sendMessage()
  -> Input cleared and collapsed to single-line height

User pastes a DOI
  -> 'input' event fires
  -> checkDOIPaste() detects DOI pattern (10.XXXX/...)
  -> Inline suggestion appears: "Looks like a DOI. Search for this paper?"
  -> User clicks suggestion -> paper search triggered

ERROR PATH: contenteditable produces HTML on paste
  -> 'paste' event interceptor strips HTML, inserts plain text only
  -> Implementation: e.preventDefault() + insertText(e.clipboardData.getData('text/plain'))

DATA STORED: None. Input content is ephemeral.
DATA CACHED: Slash command list cached in RichInputArea.commands array.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The Rich Input Area is a frontend input component that
does not interact with the LLM directly. It passes user text to the
existing chat submission pipeline which handles LLM communication.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                                |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Input area           | Click/Focus  | Focus ring appears (gold border)        |
| Input area           | Type         | Text appears, auto-expand, counters     |
| Enter key            | Press        | Sends message                           |
| Shift+Enter          | Press        | Inserts new line                        |
| Ctrl+Enter           | Press        | Sends message (force)                   |
| Up Arrow (empty)     | Press        | Edit last message event dispatched      |
| Escape               | Press        | Clears input, closes autocomplete       |
| "/" character         | Type         | Autocomplete popup appears              |
| Tab (autocomplete)   | Press        | Completes selected command              |
| Send button          | Click        | Sends message (same as Enter)           |
| Paste DOI            | Paste        | Inline DOI suggestion appears           |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
  Auto-expand: 100ms ease-out height transition via mirror element.
  Focus ring: 150ms border-color transition.
  Send button: 150ms opacity transition on enable/disable.
  Autocomplete: 150ms fade-in + translateY(-4px to 0).
  Character counter: 200ms color transition at threshold.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
  Input has role="textbox" and aria-multiline="true."
  aria-label="Message Athena" on the input element.
  Placeholder text uses CSS ::before (not visible to screen readers;
    aria-label provides the accessible name instead).
  Send button has aria-label="Send message."
  Autocomplete has role="listbox" with role="option" on items.
  Character counter has aria-live="polite" (announces when near limit).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User pastes 5000 characters of text
   Expected: Text truncated to 2000 characters. Counter shows "0 left"
   in danger color. Excess text not inserted.
   Implementation: Input handler truncates content on paste.

2. Scenario: User types only whitespace and presses Enter
   Expected: Message not sent. Input remains (send is no-op on empty).
   Implementation: submit() checks trim().length > 0.

3. Scenario: User types "/" in the middle of a sentence (not a command)
   Expected: No autocomplete popup. Slash commands only at start of input.
   Implementation: checkSlashCommand() verifies "/" is at position 0.

4. Scenario: contenteditable inserts <br> tags on newline
   Expected: Stripped to plain text. Only textContent used for submission.
   Implementation: getText() returns textContent, not innerHTML.

5. Scenario: Input area is focused but browser window is in background
   Expected: No functional issue. Input retains focus on return.
   Implementation: Standard browser focus behavior.

6. Scenario: Mobile keyboard covers input area
   Expected: Chat scrolls to keep input visible above keyboard.
   Implementation: visualViewport resize listener adjusts scroll position.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: NONE
ENABLES: #043 (File/Image Attachments)
ENHANCES: #005 (Athena Chat Panel), #040 (User Message Editing)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Auto-expanding textarea from 1 to 6 lines
[ ] Enter sends, Shift+Enter inserts newline
[ ] Ctrl/Cmd+Enter sends (force send)
[ ] Up Arrow (empty input) triggers edit-last event
[ ] Slash command autocomplete with fuzzy filtering
[ ] Character counter appears at 1500+ chars

VISUAL:

PERFORMANCE:

ACCESSIBILITY:

[ ] aria-label="Message Athena"
[ ] Send button labeled
[ ] Autocomplete has role="listbox"

EDGE CASES:

[ ] Whitespace-only input not submitted
[ ] "/" mid-sentence does not trigger autocomplete

VERIFICATION:

[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec
[ ] KEYBOARD: All shortcuts verified on Windows and macOS

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-input.js, athena-engine.js (submit pipeline), athena-chat.css
2. COMPARE against all Done Criteria line by line
3. TEST: type, expand, send, clear, multi-line, slash commands
4. TEST: Enter, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+Enter, Escape, Up Arrow shortcuts
5. VERIFY autocomplete: type "/", filter by typing, Tab to complete
6. CHECK character counter at 1500, 1900, and 2000 characters
7. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN until every criterion is verified
8. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round; records ALL findings below

FEATURE #078: PDF UPLOAD
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P1
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 18
OVERLAPS WITH:   #079 (Abstract Paste Detection)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #006 (Conversation Memory)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PDF Upload enables users to drag-and-drop or browse-select an academic
PDF directly into the Athena chat input area. The system extracts full
text using PyMuPDF (import fitz), runs it through section_parser to
identify Methods, Introduction, Abstract, Related Work, and Results
sections, and feeds the structured text into Athena's context window
for deep analysis against the loaded citation graph.

This is the primary pathway for users who have a paper on disk but not
yet loaded into Arivu. Rather than searching by DOI or title, they drop
the PDF and Athena resolves the paper identity via metadata extraction
(title, authors, DOI from the PDF header), cross-references against
Semantic Scholar, and if the paper exists in the graph, links it. If
the paper is not in the graph, Athena treats the extracted text as
supplemental context for the conversation.

The upload pipeline validates file type (application/pdf only), size
(max 10MB per MAX_UPLOAD_MB config), and page count (max 200 pages).
It uses python-magic for MIME sniffing to prevent disguised file
uploads. The extracted text is stored in R2 under the key format
full_text/{paper_id}.txt when a paper_id match is found, or in a
temporary session-scoped key when no match exists. Text tier is set
based on extraction depth: tier 1 if Methods section found, tier 2 if
only Introduction, tier 3 if only Abstract, tier 4 if extraction fails.

The upload progress is shown as an inline progress ring in the chat
input area. During extraction, the ring fills from 0 to 100 with
discrete dot-matrix segments (not a smooth arc). The dot-matrix
aesthetic matches Arivu's design language: small circles at regular
intervals around the ring perimeter, filled circles representing
completed progress and unfilled circles representing remaining work.

Section extraction relies on heading detection heuristics from
section_parser: font size changes, bold formatting, known heading
keywords (Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion,
References, Conclusion). The parser returns a dict mapping section
names to their text content. PageRank analysis can then reference
specific sections when explaining how a paper contributed to the
lineage.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
User drags a PDF file over the chat input area, or clicks the attachment
icon and selects a PDF from the file browser.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User drags a PDF over the chat input. A dashed border appears around
   the input area with the text "Drop PDF to analyze" in --text-muted.
   The border uses --accent-teal color with 2px dashed style.
2. User drops the file. The dashed border disappears. A compact file
   card appears in the input area showing filename, file size, and a
   dot-matrix progress ring at 0%.
3. The frontend sends the file via multipart/form-data to
   POST /api/chat/upload-pdf. The progress ring advances as bytes
   upload (0-50%) then as extraction proceeds (50-100%).
4. Backend validates MIME type via python-magic, checks file size
   against MAX_UPLOAD_MB, extracts text with PyMuPDF (import fitz),
   runs section_parser to identify sections, and attempts paper
   identity resolution via title/DOI lookup on Semantic Scholar.
5. Backend returns extracted sections, paper metadata (if matched),
   and text tier. The file card updates to show the paper title (if
   resolved) or "Unidentified PDF" with section count.
6. The extracted text is injected into Athena's context. User can now
   ask questions referencing specific sections: "What does the Methods
   section say about their approach?"
7. If the paper matches a node in the loaded graph, Athena highlights
   that node and says: "This paper is node X in your graph with
   PageRank 0.034 and 12 downstream dependents."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Extracted text persists in session context. File card remains in chat
as a collapsible reference. User can upload additional PDFs (max 3
per session to prevent context overflow).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-upload.js: FileUploader class, drag-and-drop, card.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Upload icon in input bar, context inject.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Drop zone, file card, progress ring.
- app.py: POST /api/chat/upload-pdf endpoint.
- backend/section_parser.py: PDF section extraction (existing module).

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-upload.js
  class FileUploader {
    constructor(inputArea) {
      this.inputArea = inputArea;
      this.maxSizeMB = 10;
      this.setupDragDrop();
    }

    setupDragDrop() {
      this.inputArea.addEventListener('dragover', (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        this.inputArea.classList.add('drop-zone-active');
      });
      this.inputArea.addEventListener('dragleave', () => {
        this.inputArea.classList.remove('drop-zone-active');
      });
      this.inputArea.addEventListener('drop', (e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        this.inputArea.classList.remove('drop-zone-active');
        const file = e.dataTransfer.files[0];
        if (file && file.type === 'application/pdf') {
          this.uploadPDF(file);
        }
      });
    }

    async uploadPDF(file) {
      if (file.size > this.maxSizeMB * 1024 * 1024) {
        showToast('File exceeds 10MB limit', 'error');
        return;
      }
      const card = this.createFileCard(file.name, file.size);
      const formData = new FormData();
      formData.append('pdf', file);
      formData.append('session_id', currentSessionId);
      try {
        const resp = await fetch('/api/chat/upload-pdf', {
          method: 'POST',
          body: formData
        });
        const data = await resp.json();
        this.updateFileCard(card, data);
        athenaEngine.injectContext(data.sections, data.paper);
      } catch (err) {
        this.markCardError(card, 'Upload failed');
      }
    }

    createFileCard(name, size) {
      const card = document.createElement('div');
      card.className = 'upload-file-card';
      card.innerHTML = `
        <span class="upload-file-name">${name}</span>
        <span class="upload-file-size">
          ${(size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)}MB
        </span>
        <svg class="upload-progress-ring" width="28" height="28">
          <circle cx="14" cy="14" r="11" fill="none"
            stroke="var(--bg-elevated)" stroke-width="2"/>
        </svg>`;
      this.inputArea.prepend(card);
      return card;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  # In app.py
  @app.route('/api/chat/upload-pdf', methods=['POST'])
  def upload_pdf():
      import fitz
      from backend.section_parser import extract_sections
      file = request.files.get('pdf')
      if not file:
          return jsonify({"error": "No file provided"}), 400
      content = file.read()
      # MIME validation via python-magic
      import magic
      mime = magic.from_buffer(content, mime=True)
      if mime != 'application/pdf':
          return jsonify({"error": "Invalid file type"}), 400
      if len(content) > config.MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024 * 1024:
          return jsonify({"error": "File too large"}), 413
      doc = fitz.open(stream=content, filetype="pdf")
      if doc.page_count > 200:
          return jsonify({"error": "PDF exceeds 200 pages"}), 422
      full_text = ""
      for page in doc:
          full_text += page.get_text()
      sections = extract_sections(full_text)
      text_tier = 1 if 'methods' in sections else (
          2 if 'introduction' in sections else (
          3 if 'abstract' in sections else 4))
      # Attempt paper resolution via title
      title = sections.get('title', '')
      paper = None
      if title:
          paper = resolve_paper_by_title(title)
      return jsonify({
          "sections": list(sections.keys()),
          "text_tier": text_tier,
          "page_count": doc.page_count,
          "paper": paper.to_dict() if paper else None,
          "char_count": len(full_text)
      })

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  /* Drop zone styling */
  .drop-zone-active {
    border: 2px dashed var(--accent-teal);
    background: rgba(6, 182, 212, 0.05);
  }
  .upload-file-card {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 8px;
    padding: 8px 12px;
    background: var(--bg-elevated);
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
    font-size: 12px;
    color: var(--text-secondary);
  }
  .upload-progress-ring circle {
    stroke-dasharray: 69;  /* 2 * pi * 11 */
    stroke-dashoffset: 69;
    transition: stroke-dashoffset 300ms;
    stroke: var(--accent-teal);
  }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/chat/upload-pdf
  Content-Type: multipart/form-data
  Body: pdf (file), session_id (string)
  Success 200: { sections, text_tier, page_count, paper, char_count }
  Error 400: { error: "No file provided" }
  Error 400: { error: "Invalid file type" }
  Error 413: { error: "File too large" }
  Error 422: { error: "PDF exceeds 200 pages" }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Drop Zone Active (Dragging Over)

  +-----------------------------------------------------------+
  |                                                           |
  |  +-  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -+ |
  |  |                                                      | |
  |  |           Drop PDF to analyze                        | |
  |  |           (--text-muted, centered)                   | |
  |  |                                                      | |
  |  +-  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -  -+ |
  |                                                           |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: File Card Uploading

  +-----------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+        |
  |  | paper_v3_final.pdf  2.4MB  [O]  Extracting... |        |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+        |
  |  Type your message...                           [Send]    |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------+

  O = dot-matrix progress ring, partially filled

STATE: File Card Complete (Paper Matched)

  +-----------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+        |
  |  | "Attention Is All You Need"  T1  5 sections   |        |
  |  | Matched: node in graph (PageRank 0.089)       |        |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------+        |
  |  Type your message...                           [Send]    |
  +-----------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Drop zone border appears 100ms on dragover. File card fades in 150ms on
drop. Progress ring animates via stroke-dashoffset. Card expands to show
paper title on completion (200ms height transition).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

User drops PDF on chat input area
  -> Frontend validates file type and size client-side
  -> FormData constructed with pdf file + session_id
  -> POST /api/chat/upload-pdf sent to backend
  -> Backend: python-magic MIME check
  -> Backend: fitz.open() extracts full text
  -> Backend: section_parser.extract_sections() identifies sections
  -> Backend: title extracted, Semantic Scholar lookup attempted
  -> Backend returns sections, text_tier, paper match
  -> Frontend injects extracted text into Athena context
  -> User can now query against extracted content

ERROR PATH: MIME check fails
  -> Return 400, toast "Invalid file type. Only PDF accepted."
ERROR PATH: File too large
  -> Return 413, toast "File exceeds 10MB limit."
ERROR PATH: PyMuPDF extraction fails (corrupted PDF)
  -> Return 500, toast "Could not read PDF. File may be corrupted."
ERROR PATH: Network timeout during upload
  -> File card shows error state, "Retry" button appears.

DATA STORED: Extracted text in R2 (full_text/{paper_id}.txt) if match
DATA CACHED: Sections dict in frontend session context

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MODEL: Not directly. Extracted text is added to Athena's context window.
Subsequent LLM calls (via GROQ llama-3.1-8b-instant or
llama-3.3-70b-versatile) will include the extracted sections as
system-level context. The LLM does not perform the extraction itself.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Chat input area      | Drag over    | Dashed teal border, dim bg        |
| Chat input area      | Drop PDF     | File card appears, upload starts  |
| Chat input area      | Drop non-PDF | Toast: "Only PDF files accepted"  |
| Attachment icon      | Click        | File browser opens (accept=.pdf)  |
| File card            | Click        | Expands to show section list      |
| File card X button   | Click        | Removes file from context         |
| Progress ring        | Animates     | Dot segments fill clockwise       |
| Retry button         | Click        | Re-attempts upload                |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Progress ring: 12 dot segments around perimeter. Each segment is a 3px
circle. Filled segments use --accent-teal, unfilled use --bg-elevated.
Segments fill clockwise at upload progress rate. On completion, all
segments briefly flash --success then settle to --accent-teal.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
Drop zone: aria-label="Drop PDF file to analyze". File card: role="status"
with aria-live="polite" for progress updates. Attachment icon: labeled
"Upload PDF". Progress ring: role="progressbar" with aria-valuenow.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: User drops a non-PDF file (e.g., .docx, .jpg)
   Expected: Toast error, no file card created.
   Implementation: Check file.type client-side + MIME check server-side.

2. Scenario: PDF is password-protected
   Expected: Backend returns error "PDF is encrypted."
   Implementation: fitz.open() raises exception; catch and return 422.

3. Scenario: PDF has no extractable text (scanned image PDF)
   Expected: text_tier=4, Athena warns "This PDF appears to be a
   scanned image. Text extraction yielded no content."
   Implementation: Check if full_text.strip() is empty after extraction.

4. Scenario: PDF exceeds 200 pages
   Expected: Return 422 before attempting full extraction.
   Implementation: Check doc.page_count immediately after fitz.open().

5. Scenario: User uploads 4th PDF in same session
   Expected: Toast "Maximum 3 PDFs per session." Upload blocked.
   Implementation: Frontend tracks upload count per session.

6. Scenario: Paper matches a retracted paper in retraction_watch
   Expected: Athena warns with retraction notice after extraction.
   Implementation: Check is_retracted flag on resolved paper.

7. Scenario: Upload interrupted by network drop
   Expected: File card shows "Upload failed" with retry button.
   Implementation: fetch() catch block triggers error state.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), #006 (Conversation Memory)
ENABLES: #079 (Abstract Paste Detection uses similar extraction logic)
ENHANCES: #001 (Graph Build: uploaded PDFs provide full text for NLP)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: PyMuPDF (fitz), python-magic, section_parser.py

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Browse-select via attachment icon works
[ ] MIME validation rejects non-PDF files
[ ] Size validation rejects files > MAX_UPLOAD_MB

VISUAL:

[ ] File card with --bg-elevated background
[ ] JetBrains Mono for file metadata
[ ] Smooth transitions on card expand/collapse

PERFORMANCE:
