FEATURE #085: HEATMAP BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #083 (Mini Donut), #084 (Mini Bar Chart)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Heatmap Block renders a circle-based heatmap in Athena's response
blocks where each cell is a filled circle whose opacity encodes the
data value. Unlike traditional rectangular heatmaps, this version uses
the dot-matrix aesthetic: a grid of evenly-spaced circles where opacity
varies from 0.1 (lowest value) to 1.0 (highest value). The base fill
color is configurable per the data semantics.

This chart visualizes matrix data in citation graph analysis:
paper-to-paper similarity matrices (showing which papers in the graph
are most similar to each other), mutation type co-occurrence matrices
(which mutation types frequently appear together in the same paper's
edges), temporal heatmaps (citation activity by year and field), or
cluster interaction matrices (how strongly clusters are connected).

The heatmap grid uses circles of radius 5px with 4px gaps. Row labels
appear on the left (truncated to 12 chars) and column labels on top
(rotated -45 degrees for space efficiency). Both use Inter at 10px.
The color scheme uses a single hue with opacity variation: --accent-blue
for similarity data, --accent-teal for temporal data, --danger for
contradiction frequency. A color legend bar at the bottom shows the
opacity gradient from min to max value.

The grid dimensions are limited to 12x12 for inline display. Larger
matrices are truncated to the 12 most significant rows/columns (by
sum or max value). A note below the chart indicates truncation:
"Showing top 12 of 45 papers by similarity sum."

Each circle responds to hover with a tooltip showing the exact value,
row label, and column label. The circle also scales up slightly (r: 5
to 7) to indicate focus. A crosshair effect dims all circles not in
the hovered row or column to 0.15 opacity, making patterns easier to
read.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing matrix data that the rendering
engine maps to a heatmap block.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Show me the similarity matrix for the top 8 papers in
   this graph."
2. Athena queries the NLP worker to compute pairwise similarity
   between the top 8 papers by PageRank. Returns an 8x8 matrix.
3. The response renderer detects a heatmap directive.
4. The Heatmap Block renders: 8 rows x 8 columns of circles. The
   diagonal is full opacity (self-similarity = 1.0). Off-diagonal
   cells vary from 0.1 to 0.8 opacity. Color: --accent-blue.
5. Row and column labels show paper short titles. A legend bar at
   the bottom shows the gradient from 0.0 to 1.0.
6. User hovers over the cell at row "BERT" column "GPT-2". The
   circle expands, tooltip shows "similarity: 0.72". All circles
   not in the BERT row or GPT-2 column dim to 0.15 opacity.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Heatmap is static in the message. Hover interactions available for
exploration. Crosshair effect aids pattern recognition.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-charts.js: HeatmapChart class, SVG rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Heatmap block detection.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Heatmap container and tooltip styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-charts.js
  class HeatmapChart {
    constructor(options = {}) {
      this.dotRadius = options.dotRadius || 5;
      this.gap = options.gap || 4;
      this.maxCells = options.maxCells || 12;
      this.labelWidth = 80;
      this.labelHeight = 60;
    }

    render(matrix, rowLabels, colLabels, color = 'var(--accent-blue)') {
      const n = Math.min(matrix.length, this.maxCells);
      const step = (this.dotRadius * 2) + this.gap;
      const gridW = n * step;
      const gridH = n * step;
      const w = this.labelWidth + gridW + 16;
      const h = this.labelHeight + gridH + 40;
      const maxVal = Math.max(...matrix.flat().filter(v => v !== null));
      const minVal = Math.min(...matrix.flat().filter(v => v !== null));
      let svg = `<svg class="heatmap-chart" width="${w}"
        height="${h}" viewBox="0 0 ${w} ${h}">`;
      // Column labels (rotated)
      for (let c = 0; c < n; c++) {
        const x = this.labelWidth + c * step + this.dotRadius;
        svg += `<text x="${x}" y="${this.labelHeight - 4}"
          transform="rotate(-45, ${x}, ${this.labelHeight - 4})"
          font-family="Inter" font-size="10"
          fill="var(--text-muted)"
          text-anchor="end">${colLabels[c].slice(0, 12)}</text>`;
      }
      // Rows
      for (let r = 0; r < n; r++) {
        const y = this.labelHeight + r * step + this.dotRadius;
        // Row label
        svg += `<text x="${this.labelWidth - 4}" y="${y + 3}"
          text-anchor="end" font-family="Inter" font-size="10"
          fill="var(--text-muted)">${
          rowLabels[r].slice(0, 12)}</text>`;
        // Cells
        for (let c = 0; c < n; c++) {
          const x = this.labelWidth + c * step + this.dotRadius;
          const val = matrix[r][c];
          const opacity = minVal === maxVal ? 0.5
            : 0.1 + 0.9 * ((val - minVal) / (maxVal - minVal));
          svg += `<circle cx="${x}" cy="${y}"
            r="${this.dotRadius}" fill="${color}"
            opacity="${opacity.toFixed(2)}"
            data-row="${r}" data-col="${c}"
            data-value="${val.toFixed(3)}"
            data-rlabel="${rowLabels[r]}"
            data-clabel="${colLabels[c]}"/>`;
        }
      }
      svg += '</svg>';
      return svg;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. Heatmap is a pure frontend SVG renderer. Backend
provides matrix data as part of structured response blocks.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .heatmap-chart {
    display: block;
    margin: 8px 0;
    max-width: 400px;
  }
  .heatmap-chart circle {
    transition: r 100ms, opacity 150ms;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .heatmap-chart circle.crosshair-dim {
    opacity: 0.15 !important;
  }
  .heatmap-chart circle:hover {
    r: 7;
  }
  .heatmap-legend {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 4px;
    margin-top: 8px;
    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
    font-size: 10px;
    color: var(--text-muted);
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "heatmap", matrix: [[float]], rowLabels: [string],
           colLabels: [string], color: "var(--accent-blue)",
           title: "Similarity Matrix" }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: 8x8 Heatmap with Labels

  +---------------------------------------------------+
  |               A   B   E   G   W   R   G   A       |
  |               t   E   l   A   o   e   P   l       |
  |               t   R   e   N   r   s   T   e       |
  |               n   T   x   s   d   N   2   x       |
  |                                                   |
  |  Attention    ●   ●   ●   ○   ○   ●   ●   ●      |
  |  BERT         ●   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●   ○      |
  |  AlexNet      ●   ○   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●      |
  |  GANs         ○   ○   ○   ●   ○   ○   ○   ○      |
  |  Word2Vec     ○   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●   ○      |
  |  ResNet       ●   ○   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●      |
  |  GPT-2        ●   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●   ○      |
  |  AlexNet      ●   ○   ●   ○   ○   ●   ○   ●      |
  |                                                   |
  |  0.0  [gradient bar]  1.0                         |
  +---------------------------------------------------+

  ● = high opacity (high value)
  ○ = low opacity (low value)

STATE: Hover with Crosshair

  Same grid, but only the hovered row and column circles are bright.
  All other circles dim to 0.15 opacity. Tooltip at cursor:
  "BERT x GPT-2: 0.72"

TRANSITIONS:
Grid appears row by row, staggered 30ms per row. Hover: circle scales
from r=5 to r=7, 100ms. Crosshair dim: non-row/col circles transition
opacity to 0.15 over 150ms. Tooltip fades in 100ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates structured response with matrix data
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "heatmap" } block
  -> HeatmapChart.render(matrix, rowLabels, colLabels, color) generates SVG
  -> SVG inserted into message DOM
  -> Hover event listeners attached to each circle
  -> Crosshair: dim non-row/col circles on hover
  -> Tooltip shows row label, col label, exact value

ERROR PATH: Matrix is empty or null
  -> "No matrix data available" placeholder
ERROR PATH: Matrix is non-square
  -> Rendered as rectangular grid (rows != cols supported)

DATA STORED: None
DATA CACHED: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The heatmap is a pure visualization component. Matrix
data is computed by backend analysis (e.g., /similarity_matrix from
NLP worker) and returned as structured response data.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Cell circle          | Hover        | Scale up, tooltip, crosshair dim  |
| Cell circle          | Click        | Select cell, persist highlight    |
| Row label            | Hover        | Entire row brightens              |
| Column label         | Hover        | Entire column brightens           |
| Legend bar            | Hover        | Show value at cursor position     |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Row-by-row entry: 30ms stagger per row. Circle scale: 5px to 7px,
100ms ease-out. Crosshair dim: opacity 150ms transition. Tooltip:
100ms fade-in.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="img" with aria-label describing matrix dimensions and
top values. Each cell has data attributes. Table alternative provided
via aria-describedby linking to a hidden table element.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Matrix exceeds 12x12
   Expected: Truncated to top 12 rows/cols by sum. Note shown.
   Implementation: Sort rows/cols by sum, slice to maxCells.

2. Scenario: Matrix has null/NaN values
   Expected: Null cells rendered as --bg-elevated dots (opacity 0.1).
   Implementation: Check val !== null before opacity calculation.

3. Scenario: All values are identical
   Expected: All circles at 0.5 opacity (midpoint).
   Implementation: minVal === maxVal fallback.

4. Scenario: Matrix is 1x1 (single cell)
   Expected: Single circle at full opacity with labels.
   Implementation: Degenerate case handled by loop.

5. Scenario: Labels contain special characters
   Expected: SVG text escapes &, <, > characters.
   Implementation: Escape labels before inserting into SVG.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #041 (Stat Dashboard: heatmap as sub-component)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None (pure SVG)

[ ] Matrix truncation to 12x12 with note

VISUAL:

[ ] Inter for labels (10px), JetBrains Mono for values

[ ] Circle radius 5px, gap 4px

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] SVG renders in < 20ms for 12x12 grid (144 circles)
[ ] Crosshair effect within 16ms (CSS transitions)
[ ] No reflow on hover

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] SVG has role="img" with descriptive aria-label
[ ] Hidden table alternative for screen readers
[ ] Color not sole carrier (tooltip provides exact values)

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified with similarity and temporal data
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Opacity mapping is linear and accurate
[ ] 12x12 truncation works correctly
[ ] Null values handled gracefully

FEATURE #086: NETWORK SNIPPET BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #085 (Heatmap Block)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text), #001 (Graph Build Pipeline)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Network Snippet Block renders a mini force-directed subgraph inline
within Athena's response messages. When Athena discusses a specific
cluster, pathway, or relationship between papers, it can embed a small
interactive graph showing just those 3-15 nodes and their edges. This
is not the full Arivu graph view but a compact, self-contained D3.js
force simulation scoped to the relevant subgraph.

The snippet shows nodes as circles sized by citation count (min 6px,
max 18px radius) and colored by field of study using Arivu's field
color palette (--field-cs, --field-bio, --field-physics, etc.). Edges
are drawn as lines colored by mutation type: --success for adoption,
--accent-blue for generalization, --accent-teal for specialization,
--warning for hybridization, --danger for contradiction, --text-muted
for incidental. Edge thickness encodes confidence: HIGH=2px,
MEDIUM=1.5px, LOW=1px, SPECULATIVE=0.5px dashed.

The force simulation uses D3's forceSimulation with forceLink,
forceManyBody (charge -80), and forceCenter. The simulation runs for
100 ticks then freezes to reduce CPU usage. The graph is rendered
inside a fixed-size container (280x200px) with a --bg-primary
background and a subtle 1px --bg-elevated border.

Nodes display paper titles as tooltips on hover. The seed paper (or
focal paper of the snippet) is highlighted with a --accent-gold ring.
Users can drag nodes to rearrange the layout; the simulation unfreezes
temporarily during drag and re-freezes on release.

This feature bridges the gap between Athena's textual explanations
and the full graph visualization. When Athena says "Papers A, B, and
C form a tightly connected cluster through adoption edges," the
network snippet shows exactly that cluster inline, making the
explanation immediately visual.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response referencing a specific subgraph or cluster
that benefits from inline visualization.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Which papers form the core attention mechanism cluster?"
2. Athena identifies the cluster: "Attention Is All You Need,"
   "BERT," "GPT-2," and 4 connecting papers. It generates a response
   with an embedded network snippet directive.
3. The network snippet renders: 7 nodes connected by edges. The seed
   paper (Attention) has a gold ring. Nodes are --field-cs blue.
   Adoption edges are green, generalization edges are blue.
4. The force simulation runs 100 ticks, then freezes. Nodes settle
   into a stable layout.
5. User hovers over the BERT node. Tooltip: "BERT (Devlin, 2018)
   -- PageRank: 0.067, depth 1, 14,523 citations."
6. User drags the GPT-2 node to separate it visually. Simulation
   unfreezes, nodes adjust, then re-freezes.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Network snippet is static (frozen simulation) in the message. Hover
and drag interactions remain available. Snippet persists when scrolling.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-network.js: NetworkSnippet class, D3 force layout.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Network block detection and rendering.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Network snippet container styles.
- static/js/graph.js: Reuses color mapping utilities.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-network.js
  class NetworkSnippet {
    constructor(container, data) {
      this.width = 280;
      this.height = 200;
      this.container = container;
      this.nodes = data.nodes;
      this.edges = data.edges;
      this.focalId = data.focal_paper_id;
    }

    render() {
      const svg = d3.select(this.container)
        .append('svg')
        .attr('width', this.width)
        .attr('height', this.height)
        .attr('class', 'network-snippet');
      const sim = d3.forceSimulation(this.nodes)
        .force('link', d3.forceLink(this.edges)
          .id(d => d.paper_id).distance(40))
        .force('charge', d3.forceManyBody().strength(-80))
        .force('center', d3.forceCenter(
          this.width / 2, this.height / 2));
      // Edges
      const link = svg.selectAll('line')
        .data(this.edges).enter().append('line')
        .attr('stroke', d => this.edgeColor(d.mutation_type))
        .attr('stroke-width', d => this.edgeWidth(d.confidence_tier))
        .attr('stroke-dasharray', d =>
          d.confidence_tier === 'SPECULATIVE' ? '3,3' : 'none');
      // Nodes
      const node = svg.selectAll('circle')
        .data(this.nodes).enter().append('circle')
        .attr('r', d => Math.max(6,
          Math.min(18, Math.sqrt(d.citation_count) * 0.3)))
        .attr('fill', d => this.fieldColor(d.fields_of_study[0]))
        .attr('stroke', d => d.paper_id === this.focalId
          ? 'var(--accent-gold)' : 'none')
        .attr('stroke-width', d =>
          d.paper_id === this.focalId ? 2 : 0)
        .call(this.drag(sim));
      // Run 100 ticks then freeze
      sim.tick(100);
      sim.stop();
      this.updatePositions(link, node);
    }

    edgeColor(mutationType) {
      const map = {
        'adoption': 'var(--success)',
        'generalization': 'var(--accent-blue)',
        'specialization': 'var(--accent-teal)',
        'hybridization': 'var(--warning)',
        'contradiction': 'var(--danger)',
        'incidental': 'var(--text-muted)',
        'revival': 'var(--conf-specul)'
      };
      return map[mutationType] || 'var(--text-muted)';
    }

    edgeWidth(tier) {
      return { HIGH: 2, MEDIUM: 1.5, LOW: 1, SPECULATIVE: 0.5 }[tier] || 1;
    }

    fieldColor(field) {
      const map = {
        'Computer Science': 'var(--field-cs)',
        'Biology': 'var(--field-bio)',
        'Physics': 'var(--field-physics)',
        'Chemistry': 'var(--field-chem)',
        'Economics': 'var(--field-econ)',
        'Mathematics': 'var(--field-math)'
      };
      return map[field] || 'var(--field-other)';
    }

    drag(sim) {
      return d3.drag()
        .on('start', (event, d) => {
          sim.alphaTarget(0.3).restart();
          d.fx = d.x; d.fy = d.y;
        })
        .on('drag', (event, d) => {
          d.fx = event.x; d.fy = event.y;
        })
        .on('end', (event, d) => {
          sim.alphaTarget(0);
          d.fx = null; d.fy = null;
        });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. The network snippet data (nodes + edges subset) is
extracted from the full graph JSON already loaded in the session.
Backend provides the subgraph data as part of the structured response.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .network-snippet-container {
    width: 280px;
    height: 200px;
    background: var(--bg-primary);
    border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
    border-radius: 8px;
    overflow: hidden;
    margin: 8px 0;
  }
  .network-snippet circle {
    cursor: grab;
  }
  .network-snippet circle:active {
    cursor: grabbing;
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Subgraph data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "network", nodes: [{ paper_id, title, citation_count,
           fields_of_study }], edges: [{ source, target, mutation_type,
           confidence_tier }], focal_paper_id: string }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Network Snippet (Frozen)

  +------------------------------------------+
  |                                          |
  |      ●───────●                           |
  |     /|        \                          |
  |    / |         \                         |
  |   ●  |    (●)   ●                        |
  |    \ |         /                         |
  |     \|        /                          |
  |      ●───────●                           |
  |                                          |
  +------------------------------------------+

  (●) = focal paper with --accent-gold ring
  ● = regular node, colored by field
  Lines colored by mutation type

STATE: Hover on Node

  +------------------------------------------+
  |                       +-----------------+ |
  |      ●───────●       | BERT            | |
  |     /|        \      | Devlin, 2018    | |
  |    / |         \     | PageRank: 0.067 | |
  |   ●  |    (●)   [●]  | 14,523 cites    | |
  |    \ |         /     +-----------------+ |
  |     \|        /                          |
  |      ●───────●                           |
  +------------------------------------------+

  [●] = hovered node (slightly enlarged)

TRANSITIONS:
Nodes fade in over 200ms as simulation settles. Hover: node scales
1.2x over 100ms. Tooltip appears 100ms after hover. Drag: smooth
position updates at 60fps during active simulation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates response referencing a cluster or pathway
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "network" } block
  -> NetworkSnippet instantiated with subgraph data
  -> D3 force simulation runs 100 ticks, freezes
  -> SVG rendered with nodes and edges
  -> Hover listeners attached for tooltips
  -> Drag handlers attached for node rearrangement

ERROR PATH: No nodes in subgraph data
  -> "No subgraph data available" placeholder
ERROR PATH: D3.js not loaded
  -> Fallback to text list of paper titles

DATA STORED: None (rendered from session graph data)
DATA CACHED: Subgraph extracted from loaded graph JSON

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE directly. The LLM determines which papers to include
in the snippet as part of its response generation. The actual
rendering is a frontend component. The LLM output includes paper_ids
that the frontend uses to extract the subgraph from loaded graph data.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Node circle          | Hover        | Scale 1.2x, tooltip with metadata |
| Node circle          | Drag         | Unfreeze sim, move node           |
| Node circle          | Click        | Highlight in full graph (if open) |
| Edge line            | Hover        | Tooltip: mutation type + confidence|
| Focal node           | Always       | Gold ring indicator               |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Initial render: nodes fade in from opacity 0 to 1 over 200ms as sim
settles. Drag: simulation restarts with alpha 0.3, smooth motion.
Hover: scale transform 1.0 to 1.2, 100ms ease-out.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="img" with aria-label listing papers and relationships.
Nodes have aria-label with paper title. Focusable via keyboard (Tab
through nodes). Edge descriptions in screen-reader-only spans.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Only 2 nodes and 1 edge
   Expected: Simple pair rendered, no force simulation needed.
   Implementation: Simulation still runs but settles instantly.

2. Scenario: 15 nodes (maximum)
   Expected: Dense graph fits in 280x200 container.
   Implementation: Force charge adjusted for density.

3. Scenario: Disconnected subgraph (nodes with no edges)
   Expected: Nodes float to edges of container.
   Implementation: forceCenter keeps nodes visible.

4. Scenario: All edges are same mutation type
   Expected: All edges same color. Legend indicates type.
   Implementation: Edge color map handles single-type case.

5. Scenario: Focal paper not in subgraph nodes
   Expected: No gold ring on any node. No error.
   Implementation: focalId check returns false for all nodes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), D3.js (loaded in graph.js)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #041 (Stat Dashboard: mini graph as sub-component)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: D3.js v7 (already included in Arivu)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA

[ ] Edges colored by mutation type, width by confidence

[ ] Drag-to-rearrange with simulation restart
[ ] Simulation freezes after 100 ticks

VISUAL:

[ ] Field colors match Arivu palette

PERFORMANCE:

[ ] Drag interaction at 60fps

ACCESSIBILITY:

[ ] Nodes focusable via keyboard

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed

FEATURE #087: SPARKLINE BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 6
OVERLAPS WITH:   #084 (Mini Bar Chart)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Sparkline Block renders a compact inline SVG sparkline within Athena's
response text. The sparkline is a miniature line chart at 24px height
that displays temporal or sequential data without axes, labels, or
gridlines. It sits inline with surrounding text, flowing naturally
within a paragraph like an embedded word-level visualization.

Sparklines are used for citation velocity over time (citations per year
for a paper), PageRank evolution across graph rebuilds, cluster size
changes, or any time-series data that Athena references in its
response. For example, Athena might say: "This paper's citation
velocity [sparkline] peaked in 2019 and has been declining since."

The sparkline SVG is 80px wide and 24px tall. The line uses a 1.5px
stroke in --accent-teal by default (configurable per data semantics).
The area below the line is filled with a 0.1 opacity gradient of the
same color. The last data point is marked with a small dot (3px radius)
in the line color. If the trend is upward, the dot is --success. If
downward, --danger. If flat, --text-secondary.

The rendering uses SVG polyline for the line and polygon for the fill.
Data points are mapped to the 80x24 viewport using linear scaling.
The minimum and maximum values define the Y range. No smoothing or
interpolation is applied: the line connects data points directly,
giving a precise representation of the data.

The sparkline supports hover interaction: hovering over the sparkline
pauses the inline flow and expands it to 160x48px (2x) with a tooltip
showing the value at the cursor position. A vertical crosshair line
tracks the cursor. This is implemented via CSS transform and absolute
positioning to avoid text reflow.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing time-series or sequential data
that the rendering engine maps to an inline sparkline.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "What is the citation trend for this paper?"
2. Athena retrieves citation counts by year from Semantic Scholar
   metadata and generates a response with an embedded sparkline.
3. The sparkline renders inline: a tiny line chart 80x24px showing
   citations from 2017 to 2025. The line rises steeply in 2018-2019,
   plateaus in 2020-2022, then dips slightly.
4. The last data point (2025) has a red dot indicating a downward
   trend.
5. User hovers over the sparkline. It expands to 160x48px. A vertical
   crosshair tracks the cursor. Tooltip shows: "2019: 12,456
   citations."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Sparkline is static inline element. Hover expansion available. Returns
to 80x24 on mouse leave.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-charts.js: Sparkline class, SVG rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Sparkline block detection, inline insert.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Sparkline container, hover expansion.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-charts.js
  class Sparkline {
    constructor(options = {}) {
      this.width = options.width || 80;
      this.height = options.height || 24;
      this.color = options.color || 'var(--accent-teal)';
      this.dotRadius = 3;
    }

    render(values, labels = []) {
      if (!values || values.length < 2) return '';
      const min = Math.min(...values);
      const max = Math.max(...values);
      const range = max - min || 1;
      const stepX = this.width / (values.length - 1);
      const points = values.map((v, i) => {
        const x = i * stepX;
        const y = this.height - ((v - min) / range) * (this.height - 4) - 2;
        return `${x.toFixed(1)},${y.toFixed(1)}`;
      });
      const polyline = points.join(' ');
      // Fill polygon (line + bottom edge)
      const fillPoints = polyline
        + ` ${this.width},${this.height} 0,${this.height}`;
      // Trend dot color
      const lastVal = values[values.length - 1];
      const prevVal = values[values.length - 2];
      const dotColor = lastVal > prevVal ? 'var(--success)'
        : lastVal < prevVal ? 'var(--danger)' : 'var(--text-secondary)';
      const lastPt = points[points.length - 1].split(',');
      let svg = `<svg class="sparkline" width="${this.width}"
        height="${this.height}"
        viewBox="0 0 ${this.width} ${this.height}"
        data-values="${values.join(',')}"
        data-labels="${labels.join(',')}">`;
      svg += `<polygon points="${fillPoints}"
        fill="${this.color}" opacity="0.1"/>`;
      svg += `<polyline points="${polyline}"
        fill="none" stroke="${this.color}" stroke-width="1.5"
        stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/>`;
      svg += `<circle cx="${lastPt[0]}" cy="${lastPt[1]}"
        r="${this.dotRadius}" fill="${dotColor}"/>`;
      svg += '</svg>';
      return svg;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. Sparkline is a pure frontend SVG renderer. Backend
provides time-series data as part of structured response blocks.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .sparkline {
    display: inline-block;
    vertical-align: middle;
    margin: 0 4px;
    cursor: crosshair;
    transition: transform 150ms;
  }
  .sparkline:hover {
    transform: scale(2);
    transform-origin: center;
    position: relative;
    z-index: 10;
  }
  .sparkline-tooltip {
    position: absolute;
    background: var(--bg-elevated);
    padding: 4px 8px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
    font-size: 11px;
    color: var(--text-primary);
    pointer-events: none;
    white-space: nowrap;
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "sparkline", values: [number], labels: [string],
           color: "var(--accent-teal)" }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Inline Sparkline (Normal)

  This paper's citation velocity [/\/\___/] peaked in 2019.

  (sparkline is 80x24px, inline with text)

STATE: Hover Expanded (160x48px)

  This paper's citation velocity
        +----------------------------+
        |     /\                     |
        |    /  \    /\              |
        |   /    \  /  \___         |
        |  /      \/        \  ●    |
        +------|---------------------+
               |
          2019: 12,456

  ● = trend dot (red = declining)
  | = crosshair vertical line

TRANSITIONS:
Hover expansion: CSS scale(2) over 150ms. Crosshair line follows
cursor via mousemove. Tooltip positioned above cursor. Mouse leave:
scale(1) over 150ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates structured response with sparkline data
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "sparkline" } in text
  -> Sparkline.render(values, labels) generates inline SVG
  -> SVG inserted inline with surrounding text
  -> Mousemove listener attached for hover expansion + crosshair

ERROR PATH: Values array has < 2 points
  -> No sparkline rendered, just text
ERROR PATH: All values identical
  -> Flat horizontal line at midpoint

DATA STORED: None
DATA CACHED: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The sparkline is a pure visualization component. The
LLM provides time-series values as part of its response. The sparkline
renderer is a display layer with no LLM interaction.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Sparkline SVG        | Hover        | Expands 2x, crosshair appears    |
| Sparkline SVG        | Mouse move   | Crosshair tracks, tooltip updates |
| Sparkline SVG        | Mouse leave  | Shrinks to 1x, crosshair gone    |
| Trend dot            | Visual only  | Color indicates trend direction   |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Hover expansion: CSS transform scale(2), 150ms ease-out. Crosshair:
SVG line element repositioned on mousemove (no transition). Tooltip:
positioned via JavaScript, no animation.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="img" with aria-label: "Sparkline: values from X to Y,
trend: increasing/decreasing." Data values accessible via data
attributes. Not interactive for keyboard users (decorative inline).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Only 2 data points
   Expected: Simple line from left to right. Trend dot on right.
   Implementation: polyline with 2 points works normally.

2. Scenario: Values contain negative numbers
   Expected: Line dips below center. Scaling handles min < 0.
   Implementation: min/max range calculation handles negatives.

3. Scenario: Very large value range (1 to 100,000)
   Expected: Linear scaling compresses small values near bottom.
   Implementation: Consider log scale option for extreme ranges.

4. Scenario: Sparkline in a very narrow container
   Expected: SVG scales down via viewBox. Minimum readable: 40px.
   Implementation: viewBox allows proportional scaling.

5. Scenario: Multiple sparklines in same paragraph
   Expected: Each sparkline independent, inline with text flow.
   Implementation: Each SVG has unique data attributes.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #041 (Stat Dashboard: sparklines as sub-components)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None (pure SVG)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Area fill at 0.1 opacity below line

[ ] Hover expands to 2x with crosshair and tooltip

[ ] Hover expansion via CSS transform (no reflow)
[ ] Crosshair tracks at 60fps

ACCESSIBILITY:

[ ] Data values in data attributes
[ ] Decorative role for keyboard users

VERIFICATION:

[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified inline in paragraph text
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Trend dot colors match spec
[ ] 2-point and flat-line edge cases handled

3. TEST: render with 5, 10, 20, 50 data points

5. VERIFY inline flow (no text displacement)

FEATURE #088: PROGRESS RING BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 6
OVERLAPS WITH:   #083 (Mini Donut Chart)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Progress Ring Block renders a circular progress indicator in Athena's
response blocks using the dot-matrix aesthetic. Unlike the Donut Chart
(which shows proportional segments), the Progress Ring shows a single
metric as a percentage of a whole: coverage percentage (e.g., "78% of
references analyzed"), confidence aggregate, citation completeness,
or any scalar value from 0 to 100.

The ring is composed of discrete dots arranged in a circle, identical
to the donut chart's dot-matrix approach. The total dot count is 24
(placed every 15 degrees). Filled dots represent the progress
percentage; unfilled dots represent the remainder. Filled dots use a
color that encodes the semantic meaning: --success for high progress
(>= 75%), --accent-blue for medium (40-74%), --warning for low
(15-39%), --danger for critical (< 15%).

The center of the ring displays the percentage value in JetBrains
Mono at 16px with the "%" suffix. Below the percentage, an optional
one-word label in Inter at 10px provides context (e.g., "coverage",
"confidence", "completeness"). The total diameter is 64px for inline
display.

Progress rings animate on first render: dots fill clockwise from the
12 o'clock position, staggered 30ms per dot. The center percentage
counter increments from 0 to the target value synchronized with the
dot fill animation. This creates a satisfying "loading" visual that
draws attention to the metric.

Multiple progress rings can appear side by side in a response to show
comparative metrics. In this layout, rings are arranged horizontally
with 16px gaps, each with its own label. For example: "Coverage: 78%
| Confidence: 65% | Completeness: 92%."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing a scalar metric expressed as
a percentage that the rendering engine maps to a progress ring.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "How complete is the analysis of this graph?"
2. Athena computes: 78% of references have been analyzed at text
   tier 1-2, 65% of edges have HIGH or MEDIUM confidence, 92% of
   nodes have abstracts.
3. The response renderer detects three progress ring directives.
4. Three progress rings render side by side: "78% coverage" in
   --success (green), "65% confidence" in --accent-blue, "92%
   completeness" in --success (green).
5. Dots fill clockwise: 19 of 24 dots for 78%, 16 of 24 for 65%,
   22 of 24 for 92%. Center numbers count up from 0.
6. User hovers over the "coverage" ring. Tooltip: "78% of 203
   references analyzed at tier 1-2 text depth."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Progress rings are static after animation completes. Hover tooltips
remain available. Rings persist in chat history.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-charts.js: ProgressRing class, SVG rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Progress ring block detection.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Progress ring and multi-ring layout.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-charts.js
  class ProgressRing {
    constructor(options = {}) {
      this.dotCount = options.dotCount || 24;
      this.radius = options.radius || 28;
      this.dotRadius = options.dotRadius || 3;
    }

    render(percentage, label = '', detail = '') {
      const size = this.radius * 2 + 12;
      const cx = size / 2;
      const cy = size / 2;
      const filledDots = Math.round((percentage / 100) * this.dotCount);
      const color = this.getColor(percentage);
      let svg = `<svg class="progress-ring" width="${size}"
        height="${size}" viewBox="0 0 ${size} ${size}"
        data-percentage="${percentage}"
        data-detail="${detail}">`;
      for (let i = 0; i < this.dotCount; i++) {
        const angle = (i / this.dotCount) * 2 * Math.PI - Math.PI / 2;
        const x = cx + this.radius * Math.cos(angle);
        const y = cy + this.radius * Math.sin(angle);
        const filled = i < filledDots;
        svg += `<circle cx="${x.toFixed(1)}" cy="${y.toFixed(1)}"
          r="${this.dotRadius}"
          fill="${filled ? color : 'var(--bg-elevated)'}"
          class="progress-dot${filled ? ' progress-dot--filled' : ''}"
          style="animation-delay: ${i * 30}ms"/>`;
      }
      // Center text
      svg += `<text x="${cx}" y="${cy - 4}" text-anchor="middle"
        dominant-baseline="central"
        font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="16"
        fill="var(--text-primary)"
        class="progress-value">${percentage}%</text>`;
      if (label) {
        svg += `<text x="${cx}" y="${cy + 12}" text-anchor="middle"
          font-family="Inter" font-size="10"
          fill="var(--text-muted)">${label}</text>`;
      }
      svg += '</svg>';
      return svg;
    }

    getColor(pct) {
      if (pct >= 75) return 'var(--success)';
      if (pct >= 40) return 'var(--accent-blue)';
      if (pct >= 15) return 'var(--warning)';
      return 'var(--danger)';
    }

    static renderGroup(rings) {
      let html = '<div class="progress-ring-group">';
      const renderer = new ProgressRing();
      for (const r of rings) {
        html += renderer.render(r.percentage, r.label, r.detail);
      }
      html += '</div>';
      return html;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. Progress ring is a pure frontend SVG renderer.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .progress-ring {
    display: inline-block;
    vertical-align: middle;
    margin: 4px;
  }
  .progress-dot {
    opacity: 0;
    animation: dotAppear 100ms ease-out forwards;
  }
  @keyframes dotAppear {
    from { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.5); }
    to { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
  }
  .progress-ring-group {
    display: flex;
    gap: 16px;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    margin: 8px 0;
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "progress-ring", percentage: number, label: string,
           detail: string }
Or group: { type: "progress-ring-group", rings: [{ percentage, label,
              detail }] }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Single Progress Ring (78%)

  +----------------------------------+
  |        ● ● ● ● ● ● ●           |
  |      ●                 ●         |
  |     ●                   ○        |
  |    ●       78%          ○        |
  |     ●    coverage       ○        |
  |      ●                 ○         |
  |        ● ● ● ● ● ○ ○           |
  +----------------------------------+

  ● = filled (--success, green)
  ○ = unfilled (--bg-elevated)

STATE: Three Rings Side by Side

  +---------------------------------------------------+
  |   ● ● ● ●       ● ● ● ●       ● ● ● ● ●        |
  |  ●  78%  ○      ●  65%  ○     ●  92%   ●         |
  |  ● cover ○      ● confid○     ● compl  ●         |
  |   ● ● ○ ○       ● ● ○ ○       ● ● ● ●           |
  +---------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Dots appear one by one clockwise, staggered 30ms per dot (total 720ms
for 24 dots). Center percentage counts up from 0 to target value
synchronized with dot fill. Hover: tooltip fades in 100ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates structured response with progress data
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "progress-ring" } block
  -> ProgressRing.render(percentage, label, detail) generates SVG
  -> SVG inserted into message DOM
  -> CSS animation plays dot-by-dot fill
  -> Hover listener attached for detail tooltip

ERROR PATH: Percentage outside 0-100 range
  -> Clamped to 0-100 before rendering
ERROR PATH: Percentage is NaN
  -> Renders as 0% with --danger color

DATA STORED: None
DATA CACHED: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The progress ring is a pure visualization component.
The LLM computes the percentage metrics as part of its analysis and
includes them in the structured response.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Progress ring        | Hover        | Tooltip with detail text          |
| Filled dot           | Visual only  | Color encodes threshold bracket   |
| Center percentage    | Visual only  | Animated count-up on first render |
| Ring group           | Layout       | Horizontal flex with 16px gaps    |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Dot appearance: opacity 0 to 1 + scale(0.5) to scale(1), 100ms each,
staggered 30ms. Total animation: 24 * 30 = 720ms. Center number:
counter increments over same 720ms duration.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="img" with aria-label: "Progress: 78% coverage". Center
text readable by screen readers. Not interactive for keyboard (purely
visual indicator).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Percentage is 0%
   Expected: All 24 dots unfilled. Center shows "0%". Color: --danger.
   Implementation: filledDots = 0, all dots use --bg-elevated.

2. Scenario: Percentage is 100%
   Expected: All 24 dots filled. Center shows "100%". Color: --success.
   Implementation: filledDots = 24.

3. Scenario: Percentage is 2% (rounds to < 1 dot)
   Expected: Minimum 1 filled dot for non-zero percentage.
   Implementation: Math.max(1, Math.round(...)) when percentage > 0.

4. Scenario: Multiple ring groups in same message
   Expected: Each group renders independently with own animation.
   Implementation: CSS animation-delay scoped to each ring SVG.

5. Scenario: Percentage exceeds 100% (e.g., 105%)
   Expected: Clamped to 100%. All dots filled.
   Implementation: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, percentage)).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #041 (Stat Dashboard: progress rings as sub-components)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None (pure SVG)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Color encodes threshold bracket (green/blue/yellow/red)
[ ] Center shows percentage in JetBrains Mono 16px
[ ] Optional label below percentage in Inter 10px
[ ] Hover tooltip shows detail text
[ ] Multiple rings render side by side in group layout

VISUAL:

[ ] Dot radius 3px, ring radius 28px
[ ] Staggered dot-by-dot animation on render
[ ] Center number counts up during animation

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] SVG renders in < 5ms (24 circles)
[ ] Animation smooth at 60fps (CSS only)
[ ] No reflow during animation

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] SVG has role="img" with descriptive aria-label
[ ] Center text readable by screen readers
[ ] Color + label (not color alone) conveys meaning

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified 0%, 50%, 100%, group layout
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Color thresholds match spec
[ ] Animation timing matches 30ms stagger
[ ] Clamping at 0-100% verified

FEATURE #089: SANKEY FLOW BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 16
OVERLAPS WITH:   #086 (Network Snippet Block)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Sankey Flow Block renders a dot-matrix Sankey diagram in Athena's
response blocks. Instead of smooth flowing bands between nodes, this
Sankey uses rows of discrete dots that travel along curved paths from
source nodes to target nodes. The width of each flow (number of dot
rows) encodes the magnitude of the flow value. This creates a striking
visual that shows how ideas, citations, or influence flow through the
citation graph's intellectual lineage.

The Sankey is used for mutation type flow analysis: showing how
mutation types propagate across generations (e.g., "adoption" at depth
1 becomes "generalization" at depth 2 becomes "specialization" at
depth 3). It can also show citation flow between fields (CS papers
citing Biology papers citing Chemistry papers), or cluster contribution
flow (which clusters feed into which downstream clusters).

Nodes are vertical rectangles rendered as columns of dots (same
dot-matrix aesthetic). Each node is labeled with Inter font. Flow
paths between nodes are drawn as SVG cubic bezier curves, but instead
of filled bands, the paths are composed of dots placed along the
curve at regular intervals. The dot density along the path is
proportional to the flow value: thick flows have 5 parallel dot rows,
thin flows have 1 dot row.

The chart fits within a 360x240px container. Nodes are arranged in
columns (2-4 levels). The leftmost column represents source categories
and the rightmost represents destinations. Flow colors are inherited
from the source node's semantic color (field color or mutation type
color). Dots travel along the cubic bezier path with a subtle
animation on first render: dots appear sequentially along the path
(staggered 10ms each), creating a flowing-water effect.

The total number of nodes is limited to 15 and flows to 25 to keep
the visualization readable. Larger datasets are aggregated into an
"Other" category. Hovering over a flow highlights all its dots and
dims non-related flows. Hovering over a node highlights all flows
connected to it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing multi-stage flow data that the
rendering engine maps to a Sankey block.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "How do mutation types change across citation depths?"
2. Athena computes mutation type transitions: at depth 1, 80% are
   adoption; at depth 2, adoption splits into 40% adoption + 30%
   generalization + 10% specialization; etc.
3. The response renderer detects a Sankey directive.
4. The Sankey renders: 3 columns (depth 1, 2, 3). Left column has
   nodes for each mutation type at depth 1. Flows connect to depth 2
   nodes showing how types transform. Dot flows animate left to right.
5. User hovers over the "adoption -> generalization" flow. The flow
   dots brighten, all other flows dim to 0.15 opacity. Tooltip:
   "30% of adoption edges become generalization at depth 2 (61 edges)."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Sankey is static after animation completes. Hover interactions remain.
Chart persists in message history.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-sankey.js: SankeyFlow class, dot path rendering.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Sankey block detection.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Sankey container styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-sankey.js
  class SankeyFlow {
    constructor(options = {}) {
      this.width = options.width || 360;
      this.height = options.height || 240;
      this.dotRadius = 2;
      this.dotSpacing = 6;
      this.nodePadding = 12;
    }

    render(data) {
      // data: { nodes: [{ id, label, column, value, color }],
      //          flows: [{ source, target, value }] }
      const columns = this.computeLayout(data);
      let svg = `<svg class="sankey-chart" width="${this.width}"
        height="${this.height}" viewBox="0 0 ${this.width} ${this.height}">`;
      // Render nodes as dot columns
      for (const node of data.nodes) {
        const pos = columns[node.id];
        const dotRows = Math.max(1, Math.round(
          (node.value / this.maxNodeValue(data)) * 8));
        for (let r = 0; r < dotRows; r++) {
          const y = pos.y + r * this.dotSpacing;
          svg += `<circle cx="${pos.x}" cy="${y}"
            r="${this.dotRadius + 1}"
            fill="${node.color || 'var(--accent-blue)'}"
            data-node="${node.id}"/>`;
        }
        svg += `<text x="${pos.x}" y="${pos.y - 8}"
          text-anchor="middle" font-family="Inter"
          font-size="10" fill="var(--text-muted)">${
          node.label}</text>`;
      }
      // Render flows as dot paths
      for (const flow of data.flows) {
        const src = columns[flow.source];
        const tgt = columns[flow.target];
        const dotRows = Math.max(1, Math.round(
          (flow.value / this.maxFlowValue(data)) * 5));
        for (let r = 0; r < dotRows; r++) {
          const offsetY = (r - dotRows / 2) * 4;
          const pathDots = this.computeBezierDots(
            src.x, src.y + src.height / 2 + offsetY,
            tgt.x, tgt.y + tgt.height / 2 + offsetY, 20);
          pathDots.forEach((pt, i) => {
            svg += `<circle cx="${pt.x.toFixed(1)}"
              cy="${pt.y.toFixed(1)}" r="${this.dotRadius}"
              fill="${src.color || 'var(--accent-blue)'}"
              opacity="0.6" data-flow="${flow.source}-${flow.target}"
              style="animation-delay: ${i * 10}ms"
              class="sankey-dot"/>`;
          });
        }
      }
      svg += '</svg>';
      return svg;
    }

    computeBezierDots(x1, y1, x2, y2, count) {
      const dots = [];
      const cpx1 = x1 + (x2 - x1) / 3;
      const cpx2 = x2 - (x2 - x1) / 3;
      for (let t = 0; t <= 1; t += 1 / count) {
        const mt = 1 - t;
        const x = mt*mt*mt*x1 + 3*mt*mt*t*cpx1
                  + 3*mt*t*t*cpx2 + t*t*t*x2;
        const y = mt*mt*mt*y1 + 3*mt*mt*t*y1
                  + 3*mt*t*t*y2 + t*t*t*y2;
        dots.push({ x, y });
      }
      return dots;
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. The Sankey data (nodes + flows) is computed by the
backend analysis engine and included in the structured response. The
rendering is purely frontend.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .sankey-chart {
    display: block;
    margin: 8px 0;
    background: var(--bg-primary);
    border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 12px;
  }
  .sankey-dot {
    opacity: 0;
    animation: sankeyAppear 80ms ease-out forwards;
  }
  @keyframes sankeyAppear {
    from { opacity: 0; }
    to { opacity: 0.6; }
  }
  .sankey-chart [data-flow].dimmed {
    opacity: 0.1 !important;
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "sankey", nodes: [{ id, label, column, value, color }],
           flows: [{ source, target, value }] }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Sankey Flow (Mutation Type Across Depths)

  +---------------------------------------------------+
  |                                                   |
  |  Depth 1        Depth 2        Depth 3            |
  |                                                   |
  |  adoption  ...............  adoption              |
  |  ::::::::  .              . ::::::::              |
  |            .....  general.. ::::                   |
  |                   ::::::::                        |
  |            .....  special.. ::::                   |
  |                   ::::::::                        |
  |  general.  ...............  adoption              |
  |  ::::::::                   ::::::::              |
  |                                                   |
  +---------------------------------------------------+

  :::: = dot column (node)
  .... = dot path (flow)

STATE: Hover on Flow

  Same layout, but hovered flow dots brighten to opacity 1.0.
  All other flow dots dim to 0.1. Tooltip at cursor:
  "adoption -> generalization: 30% (61 edges)"

TRANSITIONS:
Dots appear along paths left-to-right, staggered 10ms per dot.
Hover: hovered flow opacity to 1.0, others to 0.1 over 150ms.
Tooltip fades in 100ms.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates structured response with flow data
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "sankey" } block
  -> SankeyFlow.render(data) computes layout and generates SVG
  -> Dots placed along cubic bezier paths
  -> CSS animation plays dot-by-dot appearance
  -> Hover listeners highlight flows and dim others

ERROR PATH: No flows provided
  -> "No flow data available" placeholder
ERROR PATH: Node columns exceed 4
  -> Excess columns truncated, note shown

DATA STORED: None
DATA CACHED: None

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The Sankey data is computed by backend analysis
(mutation type transitions across depths, field citation flows). The
LLM may describe the flows in text, but the visualization is a pure
frontend rendering component.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Flow path dots       | Hover        | Brighten flow, dim others         |
| Node dot column      | Hover        | Highlight all connected flows     |
| Node label           | Hover        | Same as node hover                |
| Flow path            | Click        | Show detailed flow breakdown      |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Dot path entry: staggered 10ms per dot along path (total depends on
dot count, typically 200ms per flow). Hover: opacity transitions 150ms.
Node highlight: connected flows brighten over 100ms.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="img" with aria-label describing flows: "Sankey: adoption
(80%) flows to adoption (40%) and generalization (30%) at next depth."
Flow data available via data attributes. Not interactive for keyboard.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Only 2 nodes and 1 flow
   Expected: Simple left-to-right dot path.
   Implementation: Degenerate case, bezier becomes nearly straight.

2. Scenario: Flow value is very small (< 2%)
   Expected: Single dot row (minimum). Label still shows percentage.
   Implementation: Math.max(1, ...) ensures at least 1 dot row.

3. Scenario: Many crossing flows
   Expected: Dot paths may overlap. Hover highlights distinguish.
   Implementation: z-index via SVG ordering; hovered flow on top.

4. Scenario: Node labels are long
   Expected: Truncated to 10 chars with ellipsis.
   Implementation: label.slice(0, 10) in render.

5. Scenario: 25+ flows exceed limit
   Expected: Smallest flows aggregated into "Other" category.
   Implementation: Sort flows by value, aggregate tail into "Other".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #041 (Stat Dashboard: Sankey as advanced sub-component)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: None (pure SVG, no D3 sankey plugin needed)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DONE CRITERIA
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Nodes rendered as dot columns at correct positions
[ ] Flows rendered as dot paths along cubic bezier curves
[ ] Dot row count proportional to flow value
[ ] Hover highlights individual flows, dims others
[ ] Node hover highlights all connected flows
[ ] Maximum 15 nodes, 25 flows with "Other" aggregation

VISUAL:
[ ] Dot-matrix aesthetic throughout (nodes and flows)
[ ] Flow colors inherited from source node
[ ] Inter labels for nodes, JetBrains Mono for values
[ ] Dot animation left-to-right on render

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] SVG renders in < 50ms for typical Sankey (10 nodes, 20 flows)
[ ] Hover interaction at 60fps
[ ] Animation completes within 2 seconds

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] SVG has role="img" with descriptive aria-label
[ ] Flow data accessible via data attributes
[ ] Color not sole carrier (hover tooltip provides values)

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified with mutation type flow data
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Colors match Arivu mutation type palette
[ ] Small flow aggregation works
[ ] Crossing flows distinguishable via hover

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. READ athena-sankey.js, athena-chat.css
2. COMPARE against all Done Criteria line by line
3. TEST: render with 2 nodes/1 flow, 10 nodes/15 flows, 15/25
4. TEST: very small flows, crossing flows, 4-column layout
5. VERIFY bezier dot placement accuracy (visual inspection)
6. CHECK hover highlight, dim, tooltip content
7. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN until proven otherwise
8. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round; records ALL findings below

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG

STATUS

Status:           NOT_STARTED
Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-25

FEATURE #090: TREE BLOCK
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

CATEGORY:        Response Blocks
PRIORITY:        P2
PHASE:           B
ESTIMATED HOURS: 12
OVERLAPS WITH:   #086 (Network Snippet Block)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #037 (Streaming Text)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DESCRIPTION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tree Block renders a collapsible tree diagram in Athena's response
blocks using D3.js. The tree shows hierarchical citation relationships:
the seed paper as the root, its direct references as children, and
their references as grandchildren. This is the classic ancestor tree
that makes citation lineage immediately visual.

Unlike the force-directed Network Snippet (which shows any subgraph
topology), the Tree Block enforces a strict parent-child hierarchy
with no cross-edges. This makes it ideal for showing the BFS ancestry
of a specific paper or the inheritance chain of a specific idea. The
tree is rendered top-to-bottom (root at top) with D3's tree layout
algorithm (d3.tree()).

Nodes are circles colored by field of study (--field-cs, --field-bio,
etc.) with a 6px radius. The root node uses --accent-gold fill. Edges
are drawn as SVG path elements using d3.linkVertical() for smooth
curves. Edge color encodes the mutation type of the relationship.

Each node shows its paper title (truncated to 25 chars) as a label
below the circle, in Inter 11px. Year is shown in JetBrains Mono
10px below the title. Hovering a node shows a full tooltip with title,
authors, year, and PageRank.

Nodes with children have a small expand/collapse toggle (+/- icon or
filled/hollow circle indicator). Clicking the toggle collapses that
subtree with a smooth 300ms transition. Collapsed nodes show a count
badge: "(+12)" indicating 12 hidden descendants. This allows users
to explore large trees without being overwhelmed.

The tree fits in a container of 360px width, with dynamic height based
on the visible tree depth (each level adds 60px). Maximum visible depth
is 4 levels (root + 3 generations). The tree auto-centers on the root.

The tree supports zoom and pan via mouse wheel and drag on the SVG
container. The zoom range is 0.5x to 2x. Double-click centers on the
clicked node.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TRIGGER:
Athena generates a response containing hierarchical ancestry data that
the rendering engine maps to a tree block.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Show me the ancestry tree of this paper's methods
   section."
2. Athena identifies the methodological lineage: 3 direct method
   ancestors, each with 2-4 ancestors of their own. Generates a tree
   with 15 nodes across 3 levels.
3. The Tree Block renders top-to-bottom: root at top (gold circle),
   3 children below, their children below that. Edges colored by
   mutation type (green for adoption, blue for generalization).
4. Nodes fade in level by level (200ms per level). Edges draw from
   parent to child with a path-drawing animation.
5. User clicks the collapse toggle on one of the depth-1 nodes. Its
   subtree smoothly collapses upward over 300ms. A badge "(+5)"
   appears next to the collapsed node.
6. User hovers over the root node. Tooltip: "Attention Is All You
   Need (Vaswani, 2017) -- PageRank: 0.089, 204 descendants."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Tree is interactive: collapse/expand, hover tooltips, zoom/pan. Stays
in message history. Collapse state resets if page refreshed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FILES:
- static/js/athena-tree.js: TreeBlock class, D3 tree layout.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Tree block detection and rendering.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Tree container and tooltip styles.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
  // In athena-tree.js
  class TreeBlock {
    constructor(container, data) {
      this.container = container;
      this.data = data; // hierarchical: { id, title, year, children }
      this.width = 360;
      this.levelHeight = 60;
      this.nodeRadius = 6;
    }

    render() {
      const root = d3.hierarchy(this.data);
      const visibleDepth = Math.min(root.height, 4);
      const height = (visibleDepth + 1) * this.levelHeight + 40;
      const treeLayout = d3.tree().size([this.width - 40, height - 60]);
      treeLayout(root);
      const svg = d3.select(this.container)
        .append('svg')
        .attr('width', this.width)
        .attr('height', height)
        .attr('class', 'tree-block');
      const g = svg.append('g')
        .attr('transform', 'translate(20, 30)');
      // Edges
      g.selectAll('path.tree-link')
        .data(root.links())
        .enter().append('path')
        .attr('class', 'tree-link')
        .attr('d', d3.linkVertical()
          .x(d => d.x).y(d => d.y))
        .attr('fill', 'none')
        .attr('stroke', d =>
          this.mutationColor(d.target.data.mutation_type))
        .attr('stroke-width', 1.5);
      // Nodes
      const node = g.selectAll('g.tree-node')
        .data(root.descendants())
        .enter().append('g')
        .attr('class', 'tree-node')
        .attr('transform', d => `translate(${d.x},${d.y})`);
      node.append('circle')
        .attr('r', this.nodeRadius)
        .attr('fill', d => d.depth === 0
          ? 'var(--accent-gold)'
          : this.fieldColor(d.data.field))
        .attr('stroke', 'var(--bg-primary)')
        .attr('stroke-width', 1.5);
      // Labels
      node.append('text')
        .attr('dy', this.nodeRadius + 14)
        .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
        .attr('font-family', 'Inter')
        .attr('font-size', '11px')
        .attr('fill', 'var(--text-secondary)')
        .text(d => d.data.title.slice(0, 25));
      // Year
      node.append('text')
        .attr('dy', this.nodeRadius + 26)
        .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
        .attr('font-family', 'JetBrains Mono')
        .attr('font-size', '10px')
        .attr('fill', 'var(--text-muted)')
        .text(d => d.data.year || '');
      // Collapse toggles
      node.filter(d => d.children && d.children.length > 0)
        .append('circle')
        .attr('class', 'tree-toggle')
        .attr('cy', this.nodeRadius + 2)
        .attr('r', 4)
        .attr('fill', 'var(--bg-elevated)')
        .attr('stroke', 'var(--text-muted)')
        .attr('stroke-width', 1)
        .attr('cursor', 'pointer')
        .on('click', (event, d) => this.toggleCollapse(d));
    }

    toggleCollapse(d) {
      if (d.children) {
        d._children = d.children;
        d.children = null;
      } else if (d._children) {
        d.children = d._children;
        d._children = null;
      }
      this.rerender();
    }

    mutationColor(type) {
      const map = {
        'adoption': 'var(--success)',
        'generalization': 'var(--accent-blue)',
        'specialization': 'var(--accent-teal)',
        'hybridization': 'var(--warning)',
        'contradiction': 'var(--danger)',
        'incidental': 'var(--text-muted)',
        'revival': 'var(--conf-specul)'
      };
      return map[type] || 'var(--text-muted)';
    }

    fieldColor(field) {
      const map = {
        'Computer Science': 'var(--field-cs)',
        'Biology': 'var(--field-bio)',
        'Physics': 'var(--field-physics)',
        'Chemistry': 'var(--field-chem)',
        'Economics': 'var(--field-econ)',
        'Mathematics': 'var(--field-math)'
      };
      return map[field] || 'var(--field-other)';
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:
NOT APPLICABLE. The tree hierarchy data is extracted from the loaded
graph's BFS structure. Backend provides ancestry data as part of the
structured response.

KEY CODE PATTERNS:
  .tree-block {
    margin: 8px 0;
    background: var(--bg-primary);
    border: 1px solid var(--bg-elevated);
    border-radius: 8px;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  .tree-link {
    transition: opacity 300ms;
  }
  .tree-node circle {
    transition: r 100ms;
  }
  .tree-node:hover circle {
    r: 9;
  }
  .tree-toggle {
    transition: fill 100ms;
  }
  .tree-toggle:hover {
    fill: var(--accent-blue);
  }
  .tree-collapse-badge {
    font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
    font-size: 9px;
    fill: var(--text-muted);
  }

API CONTRACTS:
No dedicated API. Hierarchy data embedded in structured response blocks.
Format: { type: "tree",
           root: { id, title, year, field, mutation_type, children: [...] },
           focal_paper_id: string }

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

STATE: Tree Block (Expanded)

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |              (●)                           |
  |          Attention Is...                   |
  |              2017                          |
  |            /   |   \                       |
  |           /    |    \                      |
  |          ●     ●     ●                    |
  |        BERT  Word2V  GPT-2                |
  |        2018  2013    2019                  |
  |        / \    |      / \                   |
  |       ●   ●   ●    ●   ●                  |
  |                                           |
  +-------------------------------------------+

  (●) = root node (--accent-gold)
  ● = child node (field-colored)

STATE: Tree Block (Partially Collapsed)

  +-------------------------------------------+
  |              (●)                           |
  |          Attention Is...                   |
  |              2017                          |
  |            /   |   \                       |
  |           /    |    \                      |
  |          ● (+5)●     ●                    |
  |        BERT  Word2V  GPT-2                |
  |        2018  2013    2019                  |
  |               |      / \                   |
  |               ●    ●   ●                  |
  +-------------------------------------------+

  (+5) = collapsed subtree badge

TRANSITIONS:
Level-by-level fade in: 200ms per level. Edge path-draw animation
(stroke-dashoffset) over 300ms. Collapse: subtree nodes slide up and
fade out over 300ms. Expand: reverse animation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA FLOW
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Athena generates structured response with hierarchy data
  -> Response renderer detects { type: "tree" } block
  -> TreeBlock instantiated with root hierarchy
  -> D3 tree layout computes positions
  -> SVG rendered with nodes, edges, labels
  -> Collapse toggle listeners attached
  -> Hover tooltip listeners attached

ERROR PATH: Hierarchy data is empty
  -> "No ancestry data available" placeholder
ERROR PATH: Tree exceeds 4 levels depth
  -> Deeper levels hidden with expand toggles on level-4 nodes

DATA STORED: None (rendered from graph data)
DATA CACHED: Collapse state in TreeBlock instance (session only)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LLM INTEGRATION
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

NOT APPLICABLE. The tree hierarchy is extracted from the loaded graph's
BFS structure. The LLM determines which subtree to display as part of
its response generation, but the rendering is purely frontend.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

| Element              | Action       | Response                          |
|----------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| Node circle          | Hover        | Scale 1.5x, tooltip with metadata |
| Node circle          | Click        | Highlight in full graph (if open) |
| Collapse toggle      | Click        | Collapse/expand subtree 300ms     |
| Collapse badge       | Visual only  | Shows hidden descendant count     |
| SVG container        | Scroll wheel | Zoom 0.5x to 2x                  |
| SVG container        | Drag         | Pan tree view                     |
| SVG container        | Double-click | Center on clicked node            |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
Level entry: nodes fade in 200ms per level (total for 4 levels: 800ms).
Edge draw: stroke-dashoffset animation 300ms per edge. Collapse: nodes
translateY + opacity 0 over 300ms. Expand: reverse. Hover: circle r
from 6 to 9, 100ms ease-out.

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE

ACCESSIBILITY:
SVG has role="tree" with aria-label describing hierarchy depth and
node count. Nodes have role="treeitem". Collapse toggles have
aria-expanded attribute. Keyboard: Tab through nodes, Enter to
toggle collapse, Arrow keys to navigate tree.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
EDGE CASES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Scenario: Root has no children (leaf paper)
   Expected: Single node displayed. No edges or toggles.
   Implementation: D3 hierarchy handles leaf-only tree.

2. Scenario: Very wide tree (root has 15+ children)
   Expected: Nodes overlap horizontally. Tree scrolls horizontally.
   Implementation: Container overflow-x: auto. Or cluster layout.

3. Scenario: All nodes at same depth (flat tree)
   Expected: Single level of children below root.
   Implementation: D3 tree layout handles depth-1 trees.

4. Scenario: Node title is empty
   Expected: Shows "[Untitled]" as label.
   Implementation: Fallback text in render.

5. Scenario: User collapses all subtrees
   Expected: Only root node visible. All badges show counts.
   Implementation: toggleCollapse handles recursive collapse.

6. Scenario: Tree data has circular reference
   Expected: D3 hierarchy rejects cycles. Error caught.
   Implementation: Validate hierarchy before d3.hierarchy().

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DEPENDENCIES
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

DEPENDS ON: #037 (Streaming Text), D3.js (loaded in graph.js)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #086 (Network Snippet: tree is specialized topology view)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: D3.js v7 (already included in Arivu)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

[ ] Edges colored by mutation type
[ ] Collapse/expand toggle on parent nodes

VISUAL:

[ ] Inter for labels, JetBrains Mono for years

PERFORMANCE:

[ ] Collapse/expand animation at 60fps
[ ] Zoom/pan at 60fps

ACCESSIBILITY:
