================================================================================
FEATURE #091: SLIDER INPUT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 10
OVERLAPS WITH:   #092 (Toggle Options), #050 (Rich Input Area)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Slider Input component gives Athena a native way to request numeric
parameter adjustments from the user directly inside the chat flow. When
Athena needs the user to pick a value along a continuous or stepped range --
graph depth, pruning threshold, similarity cutoff, maximum papers to display,
or confidence filter level -- it renders an interactive slider widget inline
rather than forcing the user to type a raw number.

This is necessary because Arivu is a parameter-rich research platform. The
graph engine alone exposes MAX_GRAPH_DEPTH (1-3), MAX_REFS_PER_PAPER (10-50),
NLP_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD (0.0-1.0), pruning impact percentage (0-100%), and
PageRank centrality cutoff (0.0-1.0). When Athena proposes a "what if" scenario
("What if we only kept papers above 0.5 similarity?") the user should be able
to explore the parameter space visually, not guess at numbers in text.

The slider follows the Arivu dot-matrix aesthetic. The track is rendered as a
row of evenly spaced dots (small circles) on a horizontal line. As the user
drags the thumb, dots behind the thumb fill with --accent-gold while dots ahead
remain hollow (--text-muted). The thumb itself is a 16px gold circle with a
subtle shadow. Current value displays in JetBrains Mono above the thumb.

Each slider has a defined range (min, max), step size, default value, and an
optional label. The backend sends slider configuration as a structured block
inside the Athena response. The frontend renders it as an AthenaSliderBlock.
When the user releases the slider, the selected value is sent back to Athena
as a structured message: { type: "slider_response", param: "similarity",
value: 0.65 }. Athena then uses that value in its next computation.

Sliders support both continuous mode (any value between min and max) and
discrete mode (snaps to step increments). For graph depth, discrete steps of
1 (values 1, 2, 3). For similarity threshold, continuous from 0.0 to 1.0
with 0.01 step. The dot-matrix rendering adapts: discrete sliders show one
dot per step, continuous sliders show a fixed 20-dot track regardless of
range.

The slider also supports a "live preview" mode. When the parameter controls
a visual aspect of the graph (e.g., similarity threshold), dragging the
slider broadcasts a preview event that temporarily adjusts the graph display
without committing the value. Releasing the slider commits the value and
sends it to Athena.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena determines that the user needs to set a numeric parameter. This
happens when: (a) user asks "adjust the similarity threshold," (b) Athena
proactively offers parameter tuning ("Would you like to adjust the depth?"),
or (c) a slash command requires a numeric argument (/prune --threshold).

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena responds with explanatory text plus a slider block:
   "The current similarity threshold is 0.25. Papers below this cutoff
   are marked as incidental. Adjust the threshold to explore:"
   [Slider: Similarity Threshold, 0.0 -- 1.0, current: 0.25]

2. User sees the dot-matrix slider. 5 of 20 dots are filled gold (25%
   position). The label "Similarity Threshold" is above. Current value
   "0.25" in JetBrains Mono appears near the thumb.

3. User drags the thumb rightward. As they drag, dots fill with gold
   progressively. The value label updates in real time: 0.30, 0.35, ...
   If live preview is enabled, the graph view dims edges below the
   current drag position.

4. User releases at 0.55. The slider snaps to the nearest step (0.55).
   A confirmation pill appears below the slider: "Set to 0.55 -- Confirm".

5. User clicks Confirm (or Athena auto-confirms after 2 seconds if the
   user does not interact further). Athena processes: "Updated similarity
   threshold to 0.55. 23 edges reclassified as incidental. The graph now
   shows 130 active edges instead of 203."

AFTER COMPLETION:
The slider remains visible in the chat as a read-only display showing the
final selected value. It can be re-activated by clicking it. The parameter
change is recorded in session memory and the action_log.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: AthenaSliderBlock class. Renders dot-matrix
  slider, handles drag, emits value events.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Recognizes slider blocks in response JSON.
  Routes confirmed slider values back to /api/athena/chat.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Slider track, dots, thumb, value label styles.
- app.py: /api/athena/chat includes slider blocks in response when parameter
  tuning is requested.
- backend/chat_guide.py: Generates slider configuration JSON from context.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  class AthenaSliderBlock {
    constructor(config) {
      this.param = config.param;       // e.g. "similarity_threshold"
      this.label = config.label;       // e.g. "Similarity Threshold"
      this.min = config.min;           // e.g. 0.0
      this.max = config.max;           // e.g. 1.0
      this.step = config.step;         // e.g. 0.01
      this.value = config.default;     // e.g. 0.25
      this.dotCount = config.discrete ? Math.round((this.max - this.min) / this.step) + 1 : 20;
      this.livePreview = config.live_preview || false;
      this.confirmed = false;
    }

    render() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-slider-block';
      el.innerHTML = this._buildLabel() + this._buildTrack() + this._buildConfirm();
      this._attachDrag(el);
      return el;
    }

    _buildTrack() {
      let dots = '';
      for (let i = 0; i < this.dotCount; i++) {
        const filled = (i / (this.dotCount - 1)) <= this._normalizedValue();
        dots += `<span class="slider-dot ${filled ? 'filled' : ''}" data-index="${i}"></span>`;
      }
      return `<div class="slider-track">${dots}<div class="slider-thumb" style="left:${this._normalizedValue() * 100}%"></div></div>`;
    }

    _normalizedValue() {
      return (this.value - this.min) / (this.max - this.min);
    }

    confirm() {
      this.confirmed = true;
      this.el.classList.add('confirmed');
      window.athenaEngine.sendStructured({
        type: 'slider_response', param: this.param, value: this.value
      });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  # In chat_guide.py, when parameter tuning is detected:
  def build_slider_block(self, param_name, current_value):
      PARAM_CONFIGS = {
          'similarity_threshold': {
              'label': 'Similarity Threshold',
              'min': 0.0, 'max': 1.0, 'step': 0.01,
              'live_preview': True
          },
          'graph_depth': {
              'label': 'Graph Depth',
              'min': 1, 'max': 3, 'step': 1,
              'discrete': True, 'live_preview': False
          },
          'max_refs': {
              'label': 'Max References Per Paper',
              'min': 10, 'max': 50, 'step': 5,
              'discrete': True, 'live_preview': False
          },
          'pruning_threshold': {
              'label': 'Pruning Impact Threshold (%)',
              'min': 0, 'max': 100, 'step': 1,
              'live_preview': True
          },
      }
      cfg = PARAM_CONFIGS.get(param_name, {})
      return {
          'type': 'slider',
          'param': param_name,
          'default': current_value,
          **cfg
      }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Response (when slider needed):
{
  "response": "Adjust the similarity threshold:",
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "slider",
      "param": "similarity_threshold",
      "label": "Similarity Threshold",
      "min": 0.0,
      "max": 1.0,
      "step": 0.01,
      "default": 0.25,
      "live_preview": true
    }
  ]
}

Slider confirmation (sent as structured input):
POST /api/athena/chat
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "message": "",
  "structured_input": {
    "type": "slider_response",
    "param": "similarity_threshold",
    "value": 0.55
  }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Slider Active (default = 0.25, 5/20 dots filled)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  [Athena]                                                     |
  |  Adjust the similarity threshold:                             |
  |                                                               |
  |  Similarity Threshold                                         |
  |                  0.25                                          |
  |  +----[track]------------------------------------------+      |
  |  | [*] [*] [*] [*] [*] [ ] [ ] [ ] [ ] [ ] [ ] [ ] .. |      |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------------+      |
  |   0.0            ^                                1.0          |
  |                thumb                                           |
  |                                                               |
  |  [*] = filled gold dot (--accent-gold)                        |
  |  [ ] = hollow dot (--text-muted outline)                      |
  |  thumb = 16px gold circle with box-shadow                     |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Slider Dragged to 0.55 (11/20 dots filled)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Similarity Threshold                                         |
  |                            0.55                               |
  |  +----[track]------------------------------------------+      |
  |  | [*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][ ][ ][ ][ ][ ].. |      |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------------+      |
  |   0.0                      ^                      1.0          |
  |                          thumb                                 |
  |                                                               |
  |  [ Confirm: 0.55 ]    <-- gold pill button below track        |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Slider Confirmed (read-only)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  Similarity Threshold                                         |
  |                            0.55                               |
  |  +----[track]------------------------------------------+      |
  |  | [*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][*][ ][ ][ ][ ][ ].. |      |
  |  +-----------------------------------------------------+      |
  |  Set to 0.55  [checkmark]                                     |
  |  (entire slider dimmed to 50% opacity, not interactive)       |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Drag: dots fill/unfill in real time (no transition delay, immediate fill).
Release: confirm pill fades in (opacity 0 to 1, 200ms ease).
Confirm: slider fades to 50% opacity (300ms), checkmark appears.
Re-activate: click on confirmed slider restores full opacity (200ms).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena determines parameter tuning is needed
  -> chat_guide.py builds slider block JSON
  -> /api/athena/chat response includes blocks[{type:"slider",...}]
  -> athena-blocks.js renders AthenaSliderBlock
  -> User drags thumb
  -> Value label updates in real time (client-side)
  -> If live_preview: broadcast "athena:preview" event with param + value
  -> Graph.js listens for preview events, temporarily filters display
  -> User releases thumb
  -> Confirm pill appears
  -> User clicks Confirm (or auto-confirm after 2s timeout)
  -> athena-engine.js sends structured_input to /api/athena/chat
  -> Backend applies parameter, computes result
  -> Athena responds with outcome text

ERROR PATH:
  -> Slider value outside [min, max]: clamp to nearest bound.
  -> Step mismatch (floating point): round to nearest step.
  -> Backend rejects value (invalid param): "That parameter is not adjustable
     in this context. Try [alternative suggestion]."
  -> Live preview not supported for param: slider works without preview.

DATA STORED: Confirmed slider value in action_log and session memory.
DATA CACHED: Current slider state in AthenaSliderBlock instance.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL

STAGE: Parameter detection during response generation. When Athena detects
the user wants to adjust a numeric parameter, the LLM decides which slider
to present.

PROMPT:
```
You are Athena, the research intelligence assistant for Arivu.
The user wants to adjust a numeric parameter related to the graph.
Graph context: {graph_summary}

Identify the parameter the user wants to adjust. Return JSON:
{
  "param": "one of: similarity_threshold, graph_depth, max_refs, pruning_threshold, pagerank_cutoff",
  "explanation": "one sentence explaining what this parameter controls",
  "current_value": <number>,
  "recommended_range": "human-readable range description"
}

If the user query does not map to a known parameter, return:
{ "param": null, "reason": "explanation" }
```

OUTPUT FORMAT: JSON object identifying the parameter
FALLBACK: If LLM unavailable, parse user message for known parameter keywords
TOKEN BUDGET: ~150 tokens per parameter detection

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element           | Action     | Response                                   |
|-------------------|------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Slider thumb      | Mousedown  | Begin drag, capture pointer                |
| Slider thumb      | Drag       | Move thumb, fill/unfill dots, update label |
| Slider thumb      | Mouseup    | Snap to nearest step, show confirm pill    |
| Slider track      | Click      | Jump thumb to click position               |
| Slider dot        | Hover      | Dot scales to 1.3x, tooltip shows value    |
| Confirm pill      | Click      | Send value to Athena, lock slider          |
| Confirmed slider  | Click      | Re-activate slider for new adjustment      |
| Value label       | --         | Display only, JetBrains Mono, 14px         |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Dot fill: background-color transition 0ms (instant on drag)
- Dot hover: transform scale(1.3) 100ms ease
- Thumb drag: follows pointer at 60fps, no transition
- Confirm pill: opacity 0 to 1, 200ms ease
- Lock: opacity transition to 0.5, 300ms ease

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Slider has role="slider" with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow
- aria-label set to slider label text
- Keyboard: Left/Right arrow keys adjust value by one step
- Home/End jump to min/max
- Screen reader: "Similarity Threshold slider. Current value 0.25.
  Minimum 0. Maximum 1."
- Confirm pill has role="button" with aria-label="Confirm value 0.55"

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User drags slider past max boundary
   Expected: Thumb clamps to max position. Value stays at max.
   Implementation: Clamp normalized position to [0, 1] range.

2. Scenario: Step size causes floating point drift (0.01 steps)
   Expected: Values displayed as clean decimals (0.55, not 0.5500000001).
   Implementation: Round to step precision before display.

3. Scenario: Two sliders in same conversation (depth and threshold)
   Expected: Each operates independently. Confirming one does not affect other.
   Implementation: Each AthenaSliderBlock instance tracks own state.

4. Scenario: User confirms slider but backend returns error
   Expected: Slider reverts to previous value. Error message shown below.
   Implementation: Store previous value, restore on error response.

5. Scenario: Touch device with imprecise finger positioning
   Expected: Larger touch target (44px minimum). Thumb enlarges on touch.
   Implementation: Touch-action: none on track. Increased hit area.

6. Scenario: Screen reader user without pointer
   Expected: Full keyboard control via arrow keys. Value announced on change.
   Implementation: aria-valuenow updates on every keystroke.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #005 (Graph-Aware Commands), #108 (Prune-Aware Chat)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: No external libraries. Pure CSS/JS slider implementation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Slider renders inline in chat with dot-matrix track
[ ] Drag updates value in real time
[ ] Snap to nearest step on release
[ ] Confirm pill appears after release
[ ] Confirmed value sent to backend as structured input
[ ] Live preview mode broadcasts events during drag
[ ] Re-activation on click of confirmed slider

VISUAL:
[ ] Dot-matrix track with gold filled / muted hollow dots
[ ] 16px gold thumb with box-shadow
[ ] JetBrains Mono value label above thumb
[ ] Confirmed state at 50% opacity with checkmark

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Drag updates at 60fps with no jank
[ ] Dot fill/unfill is instant (no perceptible delay)
[ ] Confirm round-trip completes within 500ms

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] role="slider" with proper aria attributes
[ ] Keyboard: arrow keys, Home, End
[ ] Screen reader announces value changes

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Clamping at boundaries
[ ] Floating point precision
[ ] Independent multi-slider state
[ ] Error rollback to previous value
[ ] Touch device usability

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec
[ ] ACCESSIBILITY AUDIT: Slider role verified
[ ] MOBILE TEST: Touch drag verified

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-blocks.js (AthenaSliderBlock), athena-engine.js, CSS
2. COMPARE against all Done Criteria
3. TEST: render slider, drag to various values, confirm, re-activate
4. CHECK live preview events fire correctly during drag
5. VERIFY keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home, End)
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

================================================================================
FEATURE #092: TOGGLE OPTIONS
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #091 (Slider Input), #050 (Rich Input Area)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Toggle Options provide a binary on/off switch widget that Athena can embed
directly in the chat conversation. When Athena needs the user to enable or
disable a feature, filter, or analysis mode, it renders one or more toggle
switches inline rather than asking "yes or no?" as plain text.

This is critical for Arivu because the platform has numerous binary settings
that affect graph display and analysis output: show/hide incidental edges,
enable/disable retraction warnings, show/hide speculative classifications,
filter by confidence tier, enable live preview, show cluster boundaries, and
toggle accessibility mode. Each of these is a simple on/off decision, but
typing "yes" or "no" repeatedly is tedious and error-prone.

The toggle follows the Arivu dot-matrix aesthetic. The track is a rounded
rectangle (40px wide, 20px tall) with --bg-elevated background. The thumb
is a 16px circle. When off, the thumb sits left with --text-muted color and
the track has a subtle dotted border. When on, the thumb slides right with
--accent-gold color, the track fills with a translucent gold (20% opacity),
and a small dot-matrix checkmark pattern appears inside the track.

Toggles can appear individually or as a group. A toggle group has a title
("Analysis Options") and multiple labeled toggles stacked vertically, each
with its own label and current state. The group can have a "Apply All"
button at the bottom that confirms all toggle states at once, or each toggle
can auto-confirm individually after a 500ms debounce.

Athena sends toggle configuration as a structured block. The frontend renders
it via AthenaToggleBlock. When toggles are confirmed, the values are sent
back as structured input: { type: "toggle_response", toggles: { "show_incidental": false,
"show_retractions": true } }. Athena processes the settings and adjusts its
behavior accordingly.

The toggle state persists within the conversation. If the user scrolls up to
a previous toggle group, it reflects the current state (not the state when
first rendered). This ensures consistency if the user changes a toggle later
via slash command or another toggle group.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena detects the user wants to adjust binary settings. Triggered by:
(a) user asks "hide the incidental edges," (b) Athena offers a set of
analysis options before a deep analysis, (c) /settings command, or
(d) comparison mode setup requiring feature toggles.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "I want to focus on high-confidence edges only. Hide the
   speculative and incidental classifications."

2. Athena responds with text plus a toggle group block:
   "I can adjust these display filters for you:"
   [Toggle Group: "Edge Filters"]
     [ON]  Show High Confidence edges
     [ON]  Show Medium Confidence edges
     [OFF] Show Low Confidence edges
     [OFF] Show Speculative edges
     [OFF] Show Incidental edges
   [Apply Filters]

3. User clicks the "Show Medium" toggle off. The thumb slides left (200ms),
   the gold fill drains, and the dot pattern disappears.

4. User clicks "Apply Filters." All five toggle states are sent to Athena.

5. Athena processes: "Filters applied. Now showing only High confidence
   edges (47 of 203 total). The graph highlights the core inheritance
   pathways with strongest evidence."

AFTER COMPLETION:
The toggle group remains visible in chat. Toggles show current state. User
can re-toggle at any time. Previous toggle groups in the conversation update
to reflect current state via shared state object.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: AthenaToggleBlock class. Renders toggle group,
  handles click, debounced auto-confirm, manual apply.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Recognizes toggle blocks in response JSON.
  Routes confirmed toggle values back to /api/athena/chat.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Toggle track, thumb, dot-matrix pattern, group
  container styles.
- app.py: /api/athena/chat includes toggle blocks when settings adjustment
  is requested.
- backend/chat_guide.py: Builds toggle group configuration from context.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  class AthenaToggleBlock {
    constructor(config) {
      this.groupTitle = config.title;
      this.toggles = config.toggles.map(t => ({
        key: t.key, label: t.label, value: t.default
      }));
      this.autoConfirm = config.auto_confirm || false;
      this.debounceTimer = null;
    }

    render() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-toggle-group';
      el.innerHTML = `<div class="toggle-group-title">${this.groupTitle}</div>`;
      this.toggles.forEach(t => {
        el.appendChild(this._renderToggle(t));
      });
      if (!this.autoConfirm) {
        const btn = document.createElement('button');
        btn.className = 'athena-toggle-apply athena-btn-gold';
        btn.textContent = 'Apply';
        btn.addEventListener('click', () => this.confirm());
        el.appendChild(btn);
      }
      return el;
    }

    _renderToggle(toggle) {
      const row = document.createElement('div');
      row.className = 'toggle-row';
      row.innerHTML = `
        <div class="toggle-track ${toggle.value ? 'on' : ''}"
             data-key="${toggle.key}">
          <div class="toggle-thumb"></div>
        </div>
        <span class="toggle-label">${toggle.label}</span>
      `;
      row.querySelector('.toggle-track').addEventListener('click', () => {
        toggle.value = !toggle.value;
        this._updateVisual(row, toggle.value);
        if (this.autoConfirm) this._debouncedConfirm();
      });
      return row;
    }

    confirm() {
      const state = {};
      this.toggles.forEach(t => { state[t.key] = t.value; });
      window.athenaEngine.sendStructured({
        type: 'toggle_response', toggles: state
      });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  # In chat_guide.py
  def build_toggle_group(self, context):
      return {
          'type': 'toggle_group',
          'title': 'Edge Filters',
          'toggles': [
              {'key': 'show_high', 'label': 'Show High Confidence', 'default': True},
              {'key': 'show_medium', 'label': 'Show Medium Confidence', 'default': True},
              {'key': 'show_low', 'label': 'Show Low Confidence', 'default': False},
              {'key': 'show_speculative', 'label': 'Show Speculative', 'default': False},
              {'key': 'show_incidental', 'label': 'Show Incidental', 'default': False},
          ],
          'auto_confirm': False
      }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Response (when toggles needed):
{
  "response": "Adjust these display filters:",
  "blocks": [{
    "type": "toggle_group",
    "title": "Edge Filters",
    "toggles": [
      {"key": "show_high", "label": "Show High Confidence", "default": true},
      {"key": "show_low", "label": "Show Low Confidence", "default": false}
    ],
    "auto_confirm": false
  }]
}

Toggle confirmation:
POST /api/athena/chat
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "message": "",
  "structured_input": {
    "type": "toggle_response",
    "toggles": {"show_high": true, "show_low": false}
  }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Toggle Group (mixed on/off)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  [Athena]                                                     |
  |  Adjust these display filters:                                |
  |                                                               |
  |  +-- Edge Filters ----------------------------------------+  |
  |  |                                                         |  |
  |  |  [====O] Show High Confidence       (ON, gold track)   |  |
  |  |  [====O] Show Medium Confidence     (ON, gold track)   |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Low Confidence        (OFF, muted track) |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Speculative           (OFF, muted track) |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Incidental            (OFF, muted track) |  |
  |  |                                                         |  |
  |  |  [ Apply Filters ]                                      |  |
  |  +---------------------------------------------------------+  |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Toggle Transitioning (thumb sliding)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  |  [==O--] Show Medium Confidence     (transitioning off) |  |
  |  Thumb at 50% position, track partially gold                  |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Toggle Group Confirmed

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-- Edge Filters ---- [applied checkmark] ----------------+  |
  |  |  [====O] Show High Confidence                           |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Medium Confidence                         |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Low Confidence                            |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Speculative                               |  |
  |  |  [O----] Show Incidental                                |  |
  |  |  Applied. 47 edges visible.                             |  |
  |  +---------------------------------------------------------+  |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Toggle click: thumb slides left/right (200ms ease-in-out).
Track fill: background-color transition (200ms, synced with thumb).
Apply button click: button text changes to "Applied" with checkmark (150ms).
Group confirmation: subtle border pulse (gold flash, 300ms).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena decides binary options are needed
  -> chat_guide.py builds toggle_group block JSON
  -> /api/athena/chat response includes blocks[{type:"toggle_group",...}]
  -> athena-blocks.js renders AthenaToggleBlock
  -> User clicks individual toggles (thumb slides, visual updates)
  -> If auto_confirm: debounced send after 500ms of no interaction
  -> If manual: user clicks Apply button
  -> athena-engine.js sends structured_input with all toggle states
  -> Backend processes settings, adjusts graph display filters
  -> Athena responds with confirmation and impact summary

ERROR PATH:
  -> Unknown toggle key from client: ignore unknown keys, process known ones.
  -> Conflicting toggles (all off = empty graph): warn user "No edges will be
     visible with all filters off. Keep at least one enabled."
  -> Backend error applying settings: toggle group reverts to previous state.

DATA STORED: Toggle states in session memory (graph_memory JSONB).
DATA CACHED: Toggle visual state in AthenaToggleBlock instance per group.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL

STAGE: Setting detection. When Athena detects the user wants to toggle binary
options, the LLM identifies which toggles to present.

PROMPT:
```
You are Athena, the research intelligence assistant for Arivu.
The user wants to adjust display or analysis settings.
Current settings: {current_settings_json}

Identify which toggles to present. Return JSON:
{
  "title": "group title",
  "toggles": [
    {"key": "setting_name", "label": "Human label", "default": true/false}
  ]
}

Available settings: show_high, show_medium, show_low, show_speculative,
show_incidental, show_retractions, show_cluster_borders, live_preview,
accessibility_mode.

Return ONLY valid JSON.
```

OUTPUT FORMAT: JSON toggle group definition
FALLBACK: If LLM unavailable, present all available toggles with current state
TOKEN BUDGET: ~200 tokens per toggle detection

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element           | Action     | Response                                   |
|-------------------|------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Toggle track      | Click      | Toggle on/off, thumb slides, track fills   |
| Toggle label      | Click      | Same as clicking track (entire row)        |
| Apply button      | Click      | Send all toggle states, show confirmation  |
| Toggle track      | Hover      | Subtle border highlight (--accent-blue)    |
| Confirmed group   | --         | Read-only display, toggles still clickable |
| Re-toggled value  | Click      | Resets Apply button to "Apply" (unconfirmed)|

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Thumb slide: translateX 0 to 20px (or reverse), 200ms ease-in-out
- Track fill: background-color from transparent to rgba(212,168,67,0.2), 200ms
- Apply button: text fade "Apply" -> "Applied" with checkmark, 150ms
- Group border: border-color pulse to --accent-gold, 300ms ease

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Each toggle has role="switch" with aria-checked="true"/"false"
- aria-label set to toggle label text
- Keyboard: Space or Enter toggles the switch
- Tab navigates between toggles in group
- Apply button is focusable, activated by Enter
- Screen reader: "Show High Confidence, switch, on" / "off"

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: All toggles set to off (e.g., all edge filters disabled)
   Expected: Warning message: "All edge types are hidden. The graph will
   appear empty. Enable at least one edge type."
   Implementation: Validate before sending; show inline warning.

2. Scenario: Toggle group with 20+ items (long list)
   Expected: Group scrollable with max-height 300px. Scroll indicator shown.
   Implementation: overflow-y: auto on toggle group container.

3. Scenario: User confirms toggles, then scrolls up and changes one
   Expected: Apply button reappears as "Update." New confirmation sent.
   Implementation: Track confirmed state; reset on any toggle change.

4. Scenario: Auto-confirm mode with rapid toggling
   Expected: Debounce prevents multiple sends. Only final state sent.
   Implementation: 500ms debounce timer resets on each toggle action.

5. Scenario: Mobile viewport with narrow chat panel
   Expected: Toggle rows stack cleanly. Track + label fit in single row.
   Implementation: Flex-wrap with min-width on track element.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #005 (Graph-Aware Commands), #014 (Comparison Mode)
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: No external libraries. Pure CSS/JS toggle implementation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Toggle renders inline with on/off state
[ ] Click toggles between on and off with animation
[ ] Toggle group renders multiple toggles with shared title
[ ] Apply button sends all toggle states to backend
[ ] Auto-confirm mode with 500ms debounce works correctly
[ ] Toggle state persists across conversation scroll

VISUAL:
[ ] Gold track fill and thumb for "on" state
[ ] Muted track and thumb for "off" state
[ ] Smooth 200ms thumb slide animation
[ ] Applied confirmation with checkmark

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Toggle animation at 60fps
[ ] Debounced confirm prevents duplicate sends
[ ] Group renders within 100ms for 10 toggles

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] role="switch" with aria-checked
[ ] Keyboard: Space/Enter to toggle, Tab to navigate
[ ] Screen reader announces on/off state

EDGE CASES:
[ ] All-off warning displayed
[ ] Long toggle list scrollable
[ ] Re-toggle after confirm resets apply button
[ ] Rapid toggle debounce works
[ ] Mobile layout verified

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec
[ ] ACCESSIBILITY AUDIT: Switch role verified
[ ] MOBILE TEST: Touch toggle verified

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PESSIMISTIC AGENT PROTOCOL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. READ athena-blocks.js (AthenaToggleBlock), athena-engine.js, CSS
2. COMPARE against all Done Criteria
3. TEST: render toggle group, toggle each, apply, verify state persistence
4. CHECK debounce in auto-confirm mode
5. VERIFY keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

PESSIMISTIC AGENT LOG
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementation:   Not started
Files:            Not yet created
Verified:         Not yet verified
Last Updated:     2026-03-25

================================================================================
FEATURE #093: RATING INPUT
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 8
OVERLAPS WITH:   #095 (Inline Poll), #052 (Reactions/Bookmarks)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Rating Input provides a dot-matrix star rating widget that Athena can
embed in the chat to collect structured feedback from the user. Instead of
asking "On a scale of 1 to 5, how relevant was this analysis?" and parsing
a text response, Athena renders five interactive star shapes built from the
Arivu dot-matrix visual language.

This feature matters because Arivu stores user feedback in the edge_feedback
and insight_feedback tables. These feedback signals train the system over
time -- flagged_by_users on EdgeAnalysis, user ratings on insight_cache,
and feedback on chat responses. Currently these require clicking specific UI
buttons or typing structured text. The rating widget makes feedback collection
seamless within the conversational flow.

Each star is a 5-point shape composed of small dots arranged in a star
pattern (approximately 12 dots per star, forming the outline). Unrated stars
show hollow dots in --text-muted. As the user hovers, stars fill
progressively with --accent-gold dots. Clicking sets the rating. The current
rating value displays in JetBrains Mono next to the stars: "3/5".

The rating widget supports different scales: 3-point (low/medium/high),
5-point (standard), and 10-point (detailed). The 3-point scale uses three
larger stars. The 5-point is the default. The 10-point uses smaller dots
arranged in a horizontal bar of ten marker positions.

Athena uses ratings to calibrate future responses. A low rating on an
edge classification ("How accurate is this 'generalization' label?") creates
feedback in edge_feedback. A high rating on an insight ("Was this cluster
analysis useful?") reinforces the insight_cache entry. Ratings are never
anonymous within a session -- they are tied to session_id and logged in
action_log.

The rating block also supports an optional text field below the stars for
qualitative feedback. This combines numeric rating with free-text, giving
Athena both structured and unstructured signals.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena wants user feedback on: (a) an edge classification accuracy, (b) an
insight relevance, (c) overall analysis quality, (d) a specific recommendation,
or (e) post-session satisfaction. Also triggered by /rate command.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Athena completes a cluster analysis and asks for feedback:
   "I identified 4 DNA clusters in this lineage. How useful was this
   analysis for your research?"
   [Rating: 5 stars]
   [Optional: Add a note...]

2. User hovers over the third star. Stars 1-3 fill with gold dots. Stars
   4-5 remain hollow. A tooltip shows "3/5 - Moderately useful."

3. User moves to fourth star. Stars 1-4 fill. Tooltip: "4/5 - Very useful."

4. User clicks. Rating locks at 4/5. The stars animate: a brief gold pulse
   ripples outward from the clicked star. The value "4/5" displays in
   JetBrains Mono.

5. User optionally types in the note field: "Cluster boundaries were clear
   but the transformer cluster seems too broad."

6. Athena confirms: "Thank you for the feedback (4/5). I have noted that the
   transformer cluster grouping may need refinement. I will weight similar
   feedback in future analyses."

AFTER COMPLETION:
Rating block shows final state (4 filled stars, 1 hollow). Read-only but
visible. Feedback stored in insight_feedback table and action_log.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: AthenaRatingBlock class. Renders dot-matrix
  stars, hover preview, click lock, optional text input.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Recognizes rating blocks in response JSON.
  Routes confirmed ratings to /api/athena/chat.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Star dot patterns, hover fills, locked state.
- app.py: /api/athena/chat includes rating blocks; processes feedback via
  /api/athena/feedback endpoint.
- backend/chat_guide.py: Generates rating request configuration.

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  class AthenaRatingBlock {
    constructor(config) {
      this.scale = config.scale || 5;      // 3, 5, or 10
      this.context = config.context;        // what is being rated
      this.contextId = config.context_id;   // edge_id, insight_id, etc.
      this.contextType = config.context_type; // "edge", "insight", "analysis"
      this.value = 0;
      this.locked = false;
      this.note = '';
    }

    render() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-rating-block';
      el.innerHTML = this._buildStars() + this._buildNote() + this._buildValue();
      this._attachHandlers(el);
      return el;
    }

    _buildStars() {
      let html = '<div class="rating-stars">';
      for (let i = 1; i <= this.scale; i++) {
        html += `<div class="rating-star" data-value="${i}">${this._dotMatrixStar(false)}</div>`;
      }
      html += '</div>';
      return html;
    }

    _dotMatrixStar(filled) {
      // 12 dots arranged in star pattern via CSS grid
      const cls = filled ? 'dot filled' : 'dot hollow';
      return `<svg class="star-dots" viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="24" height="24">
        <circle cx="12" cy="2" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="6" cy="8" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="18" cy="8" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="4" cy="16" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="20" cy="16" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="8" cy="22" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="16" cy="22" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
        <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="2" class="${cls}"/>
      </svg>`;
    }

    submit() {
      this.locked = true;
      window.athenaEngine.sendStructured({
        type: 'rating_response',
        context_type: this.contextType,
        context_id: this.contextId,
        value: this.value,
        scale: this.scale,
        note: this.note
      });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  # In chat_guide.py
  def build_rating_block(self, context_type, context_id, prompt_text):
      return {
          'type': 'rating',
          'scale': 5,
          'context': prompt_text,
          'context_type': context_type,
          'context_id': context_id,
          'labels': {1: 'Not useful', 2: 'Slightly useful',
                     3: 'Moderately useful', 4: 'Very useful',
                     5: 'Extremely useful'}
      }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Response (when rating requested):
{
  "response": "How useful was this analysis?",
  "blocks": [{
    "type": "rating",
    "scale": 5,
    "context": "DNA cluster analysis quality",
    "context_type": "insight",
    "context_id": "insight_abc123",
    "labels": {"1": "Not useful", "5": "Extremely useful"}
  }]
}

Rating submission:
POST /api/athena/chat
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "structured_input": {
    "type": "rating_response",
    "context_type": "insight",
    "context_id": "insight_abc123",
    "value": 4,
    "scale": 5,
    "note": "Cluster boundaries were clear..."
  }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Rating Unrated (5-star scale)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  [Athena]                                                     |
  |  How useful was this cluster analysis?                        |
  |                                                               |
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  |  |  ( ) ( ) ( ) ( ) ( )    --/5                      |        |
  |  |   1   2   3   4   5                               |        |
  |  |  Each ( ) is a dot-matrix star outline             |        |
  |  |  [Add a note...]                                   |        |
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Rating Hover at 4

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  |  |  (*) (*) (*) (*) ( )    4/5                       |        |
  |  |   1   2   3   4   5    "Very useful"              |        |
  |  |  Stars 1-4 filled gold, star 5 hollow             |        |
  |  |  [Add a note...]                                   |        |
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Rating Locked at 4/5

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  |  |  (*) (*) (*) (*) ( )    4/5  [checkmark]          |        |
  |  |  "Cluster boundaries were clear..."               |        |
  |  |  (read-only, gold accent border, dimmed)          |        |
  |  +--------------------------------------------------+        |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Hover fill: dots transition from hollow to gold (100ms per star, staggered).
Click lock: pulse animation on clicked star (scale 1.0 to 1.2 to 1.0, 200ms).
Lock state: opacity dims to 0.7 (300ms).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena determines feedback is needed
  -> chat_guide.py builds rating block JSON
  -> /api/athena/chat response includes blocks[{type:"rating",...}]
  -> athena-blocks.js renders AthenaRatingBlock
  -> User hovers over stars (preview fill, client-side)
  -> User clicks star to set rating
  -> Optional note typed in text field
  -> Auto-submit after 2s of no interaction post-click, or explicit submit
  -> athena-engine.js sends structured_input to /api/athena/chat
  -> Backend stores feedback in appropriate table (edge_feedback, insight_feedback)
  -> Athena acknowledges feedback in chat

ERROR PATH:
  -> Rating submitted without value (0): reject, prompt "Please select a rating."
  -> Context ID no longer valid (deleted insight): store feedback with orphan flag.
  -> Backend storage fails: acknowledge in chat, retry silently.

DATA STORED: Rating value + note in edge_feedback or insight_feedback.
             Action logged in action_log.
DATA CACHED: Rating state in AthenaRatingBlock instance.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL

STAGE: Feedback processing. After receiving the rating, Athena generates an
appropriate acknowledgment that incorporates the feedback.

PROMPT:
```
You are Athena, the research intelligence assistant for Arivu.
The user rated a {context_type} as {value}/{scale}.
Note: "{note}"
Context: {context_description}

Generate a brief acknowledgment (1-2 sentences) that:
1. Thanks the user
2. Indicates how the feedback will improve future analysis
3. If rating <= 2, offers to provide alternative analysis

Keep response under 50 words.
```

OUTPUT FORMAT: Plain text acknowledgment
FALLBACK: Generic "Thank you for your feedback ({value}/{scale})."
TOKEN BUDGET: ~80 tokens per acknowledgment

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element           | Action     | Response                                   |
|-------------------|------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Star shape        | Hover      | Fill stars 1 through hovered (preview)     |
| Star shape        | Click      | Lock rating at clicked value               |
| Star shape        | Mouse-out  | Revert to locked value (or empty if none)  |
| Note field        | Focus      | Expand from 1 line to 3 lines             |
| Note field        | Type       | Standard text input                        |
| Locked stars      | Hover      | No change (locked state)                   |
| Rating value      | --         | Display only, JetBrains Mono, 14px         |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Hover fill: opacity transition per dot, 100ms, staggered 30ms per star
- Click pulse: transform scale 1.0 -> 1.2 -> 1.0 on clicked star, 200ms
- Lock dim: opacity 1.0 to 0.7, 300ms ease
- Note expand: height transition from 32px to 80px, 200ms ease

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Rating group has role="radiogroup" with aria-label
- Each star has role="radio" with aria-checked and aria-label "N of M"
- Keyboard: Left/Right arrow keys change selection
- Enter confirms current selection
- Screen reader: "Rating. 4 of 5 selected. Very useful."
- Note field has aria-label "Optional feedback note"

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User clicks a star, then clicks a different star to change
   Expected: Rating updates to new value. No double-submit.
   Implementation: Rating unlocks on re-click if not yet submitted.

2. Scenario: Rating on a 10-point scale on mobile (small screen)
   Expected: Markers shrink to fit. Touch targets remain 44px minimum.
   Implementation: Scale dots to fit viewport, use horizontal scroll if needed.

3. Scenario: Note text exceeds 500 characters
   Expected: Character counter shows limit. Text truncated at 500.
   Implementation: maxlength="500" on textarea, counter displayed.

4. Scenario: User rates then navigates away before submission
   Expected: Unsent rating preserved in local state. Re-rendered on scroll back.
   Implementation: AthenaRatingBlock stores state independently of DOM.

5. Scenario: Two rating blocks in same conversation
   Expected: Each operates independently with own context_id.
   Implementation: Unique instance per block, keyed by context_id.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEPENDENCIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEPENDS ON: #007 (Visual Redesign), #050 (Rich Input Area)
ENABLES: NONE
ENHANCES: #052 (Reactions/Bookmarks), edge_feedback system, insight_feedback
CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: Feedback stored in existing DB tables (edge_feedback, insight_feedback).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DONE CRITERIA
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FUNCTIONAL:
[ ] Rating block renders with correct number of stars (3, 5, or 10)
[ ] Hover preview fills stars progressively
[ ] Click locks rating value
[ ] Optional note field expands on focus
[ ] Confirmed rating sent to backend as structured input
[ ] Feedback stored in appropriate database table

VISUAL:

[ ] Gold fill color (--accent-gold) for selected stars
[ ] JetBrains Mono value display (e.g., "4/5")
[ ] Locked state at 70% opacity with checkmark

PERFORMANCE:
[ ] Hover preview renders within 1 frame (16ms)
[ ] Click lock animation completes in 200ms
[ ] Rating submission round-trip under 500ms

ACCESSIBILITY:
[ ] role="radiogroup" with radio items per star
[ ] Keyboard: arrow keys to select, Enter to confirm
[ ] Screen reader announces current selection and label

EDGE CASES:
[ ] Re-click changes rating before submit
[ ] 10-point scale usable on mobile
[ ] Note character limit enforced
[ ] Multiple rating blocks independent

VERIFICATION:
[ ] PESSIMISTIC AGENT: All rounds completed
[ ] BROWSER TEST: Verified
[ ] CROSS-REFERENCE: Matches spec
[ ] ACCESSIBILITY AUDIT: Radiogroup role verified
[ ] DATABASE: Feedback rows created correctly

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

2. COMPARE against all Done Criteria
3. TEST: render rating, hover each star, click, add note, verify submission
4. CHECK feedback stored in correct DB table (edge_feedback or insight_feedback)
5. VERIFY keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements
6. Agent assumes implementation is BROKEN
7. Must find AT LEAST 1 issue per round
8. Records ALL findings below

================================================================================
FEATURE #094: DRAG-AND-DROP PAPER SLOTS
================================================================================

CATEGORY:        Input Methods
PRIORITY:        P3
PHASE:           D
ESTIMATED HOURS: 14
OVERLAPS WITH:   #004 (Discuss Integration), #014 (Comparison Mode)
BLOCKS:          NONE
BLOCKED BY:      #007 (Visual Redesign), #004 (Discuss Integration),
                 #050 (Rich Input Area)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Drag-and-Drop Paper Slots allow the user to drag paper nodes directly from
the Arivu graph visualization into designated slots within the Athena chat
panel. This bridges the visual and conversational interfaces: instead of
typing a paper title or ID, the user grabs a node from the D3 force graph
and drops it into a "slot" that Athena has rendered in the chat.

This is fundamental to the Arivu experience because the graph is the primary
interaction surface. Users explore lineage visually, spot interesting nodes
by their size (citation count), color (field of study), or position (cluster
membership), and want to ask Athena about them. Currently, the "Discuss"
button on node hover (Feature #004) handles single-paper queries. But when
Athena asks "Which two papers would you like to compare?" the user needs to
specify multiple papers. Typing titles is slow and error-prone. Dragging
nodes into labeled slots is immediate and unambiguous.

The slot UI consists of a rectangular drop zone inside the chat message. Each
slot has a dashed border (2px dashed --text-muted), a label ("Paper A",
"Paper B", "Seed Paper"), and a placeholder icon. When the user begins
dragging a node from the graph, the slots in the chat panel glow with a
pulsing --accent-gold border to indicate they are active drop targets.

When a paper node is dropped into a slot, the slot fills with a mini paper
card: truncated title (40 chars), year, and a small citation count badge.
The slot border changes from dashed to solid --accent-gold. The node in the
graph gets a subtle highlight ring showing it is "selected" for the chat
interaction.

Once all required slots are filled, a "Submit" button becomes active. The
user clicks Submit, and Athena receives the paper IDs in order:
{ type: "paper_slots", papers: ["id_a", "id_b"] }. Athena then proceeds
with the comparison, analysis, or whatever operation required the papers.

Slots support reordering via drag-and-drop between slots and clearing via
a small "x" button on the filled card. The component uses the HTML5 Drag
and Drop API with custom drag images showing the paper title.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
USER EXPERIENCE NARRATIVE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TRIGGER:
Athena asks for one or more specific papers as input. Triggered when:
(a) user requests "compare paper A and paper B," (b) Athena proposes
a pruning what-if scenario requiring paper selection, (c) /compare command,
(d) any analysis requiring explicit paper arguments.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. User asks: "Compare the two most foundational papers in this lineage."
   Athena responds: "I can see several candidates. Please drag the two
   papers you want to compare into the slots below:"
   [Slot: Paper A (empty)] [Slot: Paper B (empty)]

2. User moves cursor to the graph panel. They hover over the "Attention Is
   All You Need" node. The cursor changes to a grab icon. They mousedown
   and begin dragging. The slot borders in the chat pulse gold.

3. User drags the node across to the chat panel and drops it onto "Paper A."
   The slot fills: "Attention Is All You... (2017) [98201 cites]". The
   graph node gets a gold ring highlight.

4. User drags "Word2Vec" into "Paper B." Both slots are now filled. The
   "Compare" button activates (gold background, no longer grayed out).

5. User clicks "Compare." Athena receives both paper IDs and generates
   a detailed comparison analysis.

AFTER COMPLETION:
Filled slots remain visible in chat showing the selected papers. The
comparison result appears below. Slots become read-only after submission.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FILES:
- static/js/athena-blocks.js: AthenaPaperSlotBlock class. Renders drop zones,
  handles drop events, manages slot state.
- static/js/athena-engine.js: Routes filled slot submissions to /api/athena/chat.
- static/js/graph.js: Adds drag-start event on nodes. Sets dataTransfer with
  paper_id. Adds visual highlight on "claimed" nodes.
- static/css/athena-chat.css: Slot drop zones, filled cards, hover glow, pulse
  animation.
- app.py: /api/athena/chat processes structured_input with type "paper_slots".

FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  class AthenaPaperSlotBlock {
    constructor(config) {
      this.slots = config.slots.map(s => ({
        key: s.key, label: s.label, paper: null
      }));
      this.submitLabel = config.submit_label || 'Submit';
      this.minRequired = config.min_required || this.slots.length;
    }

    render() {
      const el = document.createElement('div');
      el.className = 'athena-paper-slots';
      this.slots.forEach(slot => {
        const zone = document.createElement('div');
        zone.className = 'paper-slot empty';
        zone.dataset.key = slot.key;
        zone.innerHTML = `<span class="slot-label">${slot.label}</span>
          <span class="slot-placeholder">Drag a paper here</span>`;
        zone.addEventListener('dragover', e => { e.preventDefault(); zone.classList.add('drag-over'); });
        zone.addEventListener('dragleave', () => zone.classList.remove('drag-over'));
        zone.addEventListener('drop', e => this._handleDrop(e, slot));
        el.appendChild(zone);
      });
      const btn = document.createElement('button');
      btn.className = 'athena-btn-gold slot-submit';
      btn.textContent = this.submitLabel;
      btn.disabled = true;
      btn.addEventListener('click', () => this.submit());
      el.appendChild(btn);
      this.el = el;
      return el;
    }

    _handleDrop(event, slot) {
      event.preventDefault();
      const paperId = event.dataTransfer.getData('text/paper-id');
      const paperTitle = event.dataTransfer.getData('text/paper-title');
      const paperYear = event.dataTransfer.getData('text/paper-year');
      const paperCites = event.dataTransfer.getData('text/paper-cites');
      slot.paper = { paper_id: paperId, title: paperTitle, year: paperYear, cites: paperCites };
      this._updateSlotVisual(slot);
      this._checkReady();
    }

    submit() {
      const papers = this.slots.filter(s => s.paper).map(s => s.paper.paper_id);
      window.athenaEngine.sendStructured({
        type: 'paper_slots',
        papers: papers
      });
    }
  }

BACKEND IMPLEMENTATION:

  # In chat_guide.py
  def build_paper_slots(self, slot_labels, submit_label='Compare'):
      return {
          'type': 'paper_slots',
          'slots': [{'key': f'paper_{i}', 'label': lbl}
                    for i, lbl in enumerate(slot_labels)],
          'submit_label': submit_label,
          'min_required': len(slot_labels)
      }

API CONTRACTS:
POST /api/athena/chat
Response (when paper slots needed):
{
  "response": "Drag the papers to compare:",
  "blocks": [{
    "type": "paper_slots",
    "slots": [
      {"key": "paper_0", "label": "Paper A"},
      {"key": "paper_1", "label": "Paper B"}
    ],
    "submit_label": "Compare",
    "min_required": 2
  }]
}

Slot submission:
POST /api/athena/chat
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "structured_input": {
    "type": "paper_slots",
    "papers": ["204e3073870fae3d05bcbc2f6a8e263d9b72e776",
               "330da625c15427c6e42ccfa3b747fb29e5835bf0"]
  }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
VISUAL SPECIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

STATE: Empty Slots (waiting for drop)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  [Athena]                                                     |
  |  Drag the papers you want to compare:                         |
  |                                                               |
  |  +-----------------------+  +-----------------------+         |
  |  | - - - - - - - - - - - | | - - - - - - - - - - - |         |
  |  |     Paper A            | |     Paper B            |         |
  |  |  [drag icon] Drag a    | |  [drag icon] Drag a    |         |
  |  |  paper here             | |  paper here             |         |
  |  | - - - - - - - - - - - | | - - - - - - - - - - - |         |
  |  +-----------------------+  +-----------------------+         |
  |                                                               |
  |  [ Compare ]  (disabled, gray)                                |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Dragging Active (node being dragged over chat)

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +-----------------------+  +-----------------------+         |
  |  | ~~~~ GOLD PULSE ~~~~ | | ~~~~ GOLD PULSE ~~~~ |         |
  |  |     Paper A            | |     Paper B            |         |
  |  |  Drop here             | |  Drop here             |         |
  |  | ~~~~ GOLD PULSE ~~~~ | | ~~~~ GOLD PULSE ~~~~ |         |
  |  +-----------------------+  +-----------------------+         |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: One Slot Filled

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +--[FILLED]--------------+  +-----------------------+       |
  |  | Attention Is All Y..   |  | - - - - - - - - - - - |       |
  |  | 2017  [98201 cites] [x]|  |     Paper B            |       |
  |  +------------------------+  | - - - - - - - - - - - |       |
  |                               +-----------------------+       |
  |  [ Compare ]  (disabled, gray -- need both)                   |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

STATE: Both Slots Filled

  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
  |  +--[FILLED]--------------+  +--[FILLED]--------------+     |
  |  | Attention Is All Y..   |  | Efficient Estimation.. |     |
  |  | 2017  [98201 cites] [x]|  | 2013  [41892 cites] [x]|     |
  |  +------------------------+  +------------------------+     |
  |                                                               |
  |  [ Compare ]  (active, --accent-gold background)              |
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+

TRANSITIONS:
Drag enter: slot border transitions dashed -> solid gold, pulsing (400ms cycle).
Drop: slot content fades in (opacity 0 to 1, 200ms).
Clear (x click): card fades out (150ms), reverts to empty placeholder.
Submit: button text changes to "Comparing..." spinner.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA FLOW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Athena determines paper selection is needed
  -> chat_guide.py builds paper_slots block JSON
  -> /api/athena/chat response includes blocks[{type:"paper_slots",...}]
  -> athena-blocks.js renders AthenaPaperSlotBlock
  -> User drags node from graph.js (sets dataTransfer with paper metadata)
  -> User drops on slot in chat panel
  -> Slot fills with mini paper card
  -> Repeat for remaining slots
  -> User clicks Submit
  -> athena-engine.js sends structured_input with paper IDs
  -> Backend receives paper_ids, executes comparison/analysis
  -> Athena responds with results

ERROR PATH:
  -> Drop non-paper element: ignore (dataTransfer missing paper-id).
  -> Same paper dropped in two slots: warn "Paper already in slot A."
  -> Paper ID not in current graph: warn "This paper is not in the
     current lineage graph."
  -> Graph not loaded (no nodes to drag): show text fallback "Type paper
     titles instead."

DATA STORED: Selected paper IDs in action_log.
DATA CACHED: Slot state in AthenaPaperSlotBlock instance.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LLM INTEGRATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL: config.GROQ_FAST_MODEL

STAGE: Slot configuration. When Athena determines paper selection is needed,
the LLM decides how many slots and what labels to present.

PROMPT:
```
You are Athena, the research intelligence assistant for Arivu.
The user wants to perform an operation that requires selecting papers.
User query: {user_message}
Available operations: compare (2 papers), trace_path (2 papers),
  pruning_what_if (1 paper), cluster_membership (1+ papers)

Return JSON:
{
  "operation": "compare",
  "slot_count": 2,
  "slot_labels": ["Paper A", "Paper B"],
  "submit_label": "Compare"
}

Return ONLY valid JSON.
```

OUTPUT FORMAT: JSON defining slot configuration
FALLBACK: Default to 2 slots labeled "Paper A" / "Paper B"
TOKEN BUDGET: ~100 tokens per slot configuration

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERACTIVE BEHAVIORS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Element           | Action     | Response                                   |
|-------------------|------------|--------------------------------------------|
| Graph node        | Mousedown  | Begin drag, set dataTransfer               |
| Empty slot        | Dragover   | Highlight with gold pulse border           |
| Empty slot        | Drop       | Fill slot with paper card                  |
| Filled card [x]   | Click      | Clear slot, revert to empty                |
| Filled card       | Drag       | Allow reorder between slots                |
| Submit button     | Click      | Send paper IDs, show loading state         |
| Submit button     | Hover      | Gold brightness increase                   |

ANIMATION DETAILS:
- Pulse: border-color alternates gold/transparent, 400ms cycle
- Drop fill: opacity 0 to 1, 200ms ease
- Clear: opacity 1 to 0, 150ms ease, then revert to empty state
- Button activate: background-color from gray to gold, 200ms

SOUND EFFECTS: NOT APPLICABLE.

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Slots have role="listbox" with aria-label "Paper selection slots"
- Each slot has role="option" with aria-selected
- Keyboard alternative: Tab to slot, then type paper title to search
- Screen reader: "Paper A slot, empty. Drop a paper or type to search."
- Submit button has aria-label "Submit selected papers for comparison"
- Drag alternative: click node in graph, then click slot to assign

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE CASES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Scenario: User drops same paper into both slots
   Expected: Second slot rejects. Toast: "Already selected as Paper A."
   Implementation: Check paper_id against all filled slots.

2. Scenario: No graph loaded (landing page or error state)
   Expected: Slots show text input fallback. User types paper title.
   Implementation: Detect graph state; switch to search-based selection.

3. Scenario: User fills slot A, clears it, fills slot B first
   Expected: Works fine. Slot order does not matter. Submit checks fill count.
   Implementation: Submit enabled when filled count >= min_required.

4. Scenario: Graph panel not visible (mobile with chat-only view)
   Expected: Slots switch to search-based input. Drag not available.
   Implementation: Detect viewport; if no graph visible, use search mode.

5. Scenario: Drag from graph starts but user drops outside any slot
   Expected: Drop cancelled. No change to slots. No error.
   Implementation: Default drop handler does nothing.

6. Scenario: Five slots requested (e.g., "top 5 papers for review")
   Expected: Slots stack vertically in pairs. Scrollable if needed.
   Implementation: Flex-wrap with max 2 per row.

CONFLICTS: NONE
EXTERNAL: HTML5 Drag and Drop API. Requires graph.js drag event integration.
